eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma, la Saduse dõma basɛba wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa bɔɔra tɩ ba makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ, a ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Zaanõorɛ, sawara sãn mõ'ogɛ, yãma yetɩ saaga kãn ni.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: La bulika, sawara sãn mõ'ogɛ sobge, yãma yetɩ saaga n wʋn ni. Yãma bɩsrɩ la sawara mina la ãn se'em, dee ka tãna wʋn bãŋɛ bõn-sɛba n ɩtɩ lɛɛlɛ wã n pa'alɩ sɛla.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Zamãan-ẽna nẽrba n de nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ, dee ka dɔla Nawẽnnɛ la sɩra la, boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ la bõn-yãlma, la ba kãn yẽ bõn-yãlma, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azonaasɩ bõn-yãlma la.» La a basɛ ba mɛ dee fõrgɛ.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Tɩ ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la dɔɔra mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yĩm ka dɩkɛ borborɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la Farisi dõma, la Saduse dõma dãbɩllɛ la.».

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la tẽ'esra yetɩ: «Tõma n ka dɩkɛ borborɩ la ĩyã tɩ a yele bɛla.».

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, yele ba yetɩ: «Ya ka bɔ mam sɩra zo'oge. Bẽm tɩ yãma tẽ'esra yetɩ, la de la yãma n ka tarɩ borborɩ la ĩyã?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Yãma nãn ka bãŋɛ a võorɛ? Yãma ka tẽrɩ, borborɩ banuu la tɩ bʋraasɩ tʋsanuu daan di tɩgɛ deege la, yãma daan vaagɛ di-sɛba n daan deege la pɩrɛ la pɩtɔ balɛ?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: La borborɩ bayopɔɩ la, tɩ bʋraasɩ tʋsanaasɩ la daan di tɩgɛ deege la, yãma vaagɛ di-sɛba n deege la pɩrɛ la pɩtɔ balɛ?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Bẽm tɩ ya ka bãŋɛ tɩ la dagɩ borborɩ yelle tɩ mam tɔgrɩ la ya? Guuse ya mẽŋa la Farisi dõma, la Saduse dõma dãbɩllɛ la yelle.».

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Tɩ ba yãŋa bãŋɛ tɩ a ka yele ba tɩ ba guuse ba mẽŋa la dãbɩllɛ sɛba tɩ ba tarɩ gaana borborɩ zom na, la de la Farisi dõma, la Saduse dõma zãsŋɔ la tɩ a tɔgrɩ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Azezi daan paagɛ la tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Filipi Sezaare, soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Nẽrba yetɩ mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la de la ãne?».

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Sɛba yetɩ fõn de la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na. Tɩ sɛba yetɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli. Tɩ basɛba yetɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azeremi, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la nẽra ayɩla.».

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Tɩ a lebe soke ba yetɩ: «La yãma yetɩ mam de la ãne?».

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la Krisi, Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana la Dayʋa.».

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Tɩ Azezi lebe yele ẽ yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã dayʋa, fʋ tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ nẽrsaala n pa'alɛ fʋ wãna, la de la mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum na.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ fʋ de la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, a võorɛ de la tãmpĩa, la tãmpĩ-ẽna zuo tɩ mam wʋn vigle m nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'a. La kũm pãŋa kãn tã'agɛ ẽ.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Mam wʋn bɔ fɔ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kʋlɩnbiɛ. La bõn-sɛka woo tɩ fʋ wʋn lʋ dũnia wã zuo, bõn-ẽŋa wʋn lʋ Nawẽnnɛ yire. La bõn-sɛka woo tɩ fʋ wʋn lorge dũnia wã zuo, bõn-ẽŋa wʋn lorge Nawẽnnɛ yire.».

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee kã'an a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, ba da yele nẽra nẽra tɩ ẽŋa de la Krisi.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi daan pɔsɛ zãsna a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ mam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm, tɩ tẽŋa la kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma ka nãmsɛ mam halɩ zozo'e, la ba wʋn kʋ mam mɛ. La dabsa atã daarɛ mam wʋn vo'oge.».

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ tarɩ ẽ yese kẽnkɛrŋa ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, da le tɔgɛ bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn gu fo, la kãn ẽŋɛ fʋ bɛla.».

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Tɩ Azezi gelge yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Lebe mam poorʋm, Asũtãana wã. Fõn boorɩ tɩ fʋ botɩ mam lebe la poorʋm. Fʋ tẽ'esgɔ la dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ tẽ'esgɔ, la de la nẽrsaala tẽ'esgɔ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Dee yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a dɔla mam, dẽnɩ tɩ a basɛ a mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee sakɛ nãmsgɔ wʋ nẽra n buke a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n sakɛ tɩ a koŋe a vɔm mam ĩyã, a wʋn yẽ vɔm.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: La nẽra sãn yẽ dũnia wã sɛla woo, dee koŋe vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a yõorɔ de la bẽm? Sɛla ka boe tɩ nẽra wʋn dɩkɛ teege vɔm.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme wa'am mɛ la a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa, la a malɛkadõma. La wakat-ẽŋa a wʋn yɔ nẽra woo masɛ la a tõonɛ.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ze kalam wã basɛba kãn ki, sãn dagna tɩ ba wa yẽ la Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n sẽm, la a na'am.».

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Azezi n de Adavidi yɩɩŋa, la Abraham yɩɩŋa yaabdõma n wãna:.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraham n dɔgɛ A'izakɩ, tɩ A'izakɩ dɔgɛ Azakɔbɩ, tɩ Azakɔbɩ dɔgɛ Azuda, la a kẽendõma, la a yɩbsɩ.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Tɩ Azuda la a pɔga Atamaarɩ dɔgɛ Afarɛsɩ la Azara, tɩ Afarɛsɩ dɔgɛ A'ɛsrɔm, tɩ A'ɛsrɔm dɔgɛ Aram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: tɩ Aram dɔgɛ Aminadabɩ, tɩ Aminadabɩ dɔgɛ Anaasõ, tɩ Anaasõ dɔgɛ Asalmõ.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Tɩ Asalmõ la a pɔga Arahabɩ dɔgɛ Aboozɩ, tɩ Aboozɩ la a pɔga Aruti dɔgɛ A'obɛɛdɩ, tɩ A'obɛɛdɩ dɔgɛ Azese.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: Tɩ Azese dɔgɛ naba Adavidi. Tɩ Adavidi daan di A'uuri pɔkõorɛ, tɩ ba dɔgɛ Asalomõ.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Tɩ Asalomõ dɔgɛ Aroboam, tɩ Aroboam dɔgɛ Abiya, tɩ Abiya dɔgɛ Aza.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Tɩ Aza dɔgɛ Azozafatɩ, tɩ Azozafatɩ dɔgɛ Azoram, tɩ Azoram dɔgɛ A'oziasɩ.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Tɩ A'oziasɩ dɔgɛ Azɔtam, tɩ Azɔtam dɔgɛ Akaazɩ, tɩ Akaazɩ dɔgɛ A'ezekiasɩ.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Tɩ A'ezekiasɩ dɔgɛ Amanase, tɩ Amanase dɔgɛ Amõ, tɩ Amõ dɔgɛ Azoziasɩ.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Tɩ Azoziasɩ dɔgɛ Azekoniyasɩ la a yɩbsɩ. Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yõgɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma tarɩ sẽŋɛ Babilɔnɩ.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: La ban paagɛ Babilɔnɩ na poorʋm, tɩ Azekoniyasɩ dɔgɛ Asalatɩyɛlɩ, tɩ Asalatɩyɛlɩ dɔgɛ Azorobabɛlɩ.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Tɩ Azorobabɛlɩ dɔgɛ Abiyuudɩ, tɩ Abiyuudɩ dɔgɛ A'eliakim, tɩ A'eliakim dɔgɛ Azɔɔrɩ.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Tɩ Azɔɔrɩ dɔgɛ Asadɔkɩ, tɩ Asadɔkɩ dɔgɛ Akim, tɩ Akim dɔgɛ A'eliyuudɩ.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Tɩ A'eliyuudɩ dɔgɛ A'eleazaarɩ, tɩ A'eleazaarɩ dɔgɛ Amatã, tɩ Amatã dɔgɛ Azakɔbɩ.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Tɩ Azakɔbɩ dɔgɛ Azozɛfʋ n de Amaari sɩra la. Amaari ẽŋa n dɔgɛ Azezi tɩ ba wi'iri Krisi la.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Lan pĩ'ilum na Abraham wa paagɛ Adavidi, de la zamãana pia la anaasɩ. Lan sẽŋɛ la Adavidi wa paagɛ ban yõgɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sẽŋɛ Babilɔnɩ na, de la zamãana pia la anaasɩ. Ban yõgɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sẽŋɛ Babilɔnɩ wa paagɛ Krisi la, de la zamãana pia la anaasɩ.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Azezi-krisi dɔka daan ẽŋɛ la wãna: A ma Amaari daan yetɩ a ele la Azozɛfʋ, la ba daan nãn ka gã'arɛ la taaba, dee tɩ a tara pʋʋrɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ a tara pʋʋrɛ la.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: La Azozɛfʋ n daan yetɩ a di ẽ na de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, a ka boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽ yãnnɛ nẽrba nifum, a daan tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ a basɛ ẽ sĩm.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: La ẽn daan tẽ'esrɩ bɛla la, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ malɛka wa'am wa puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ ẽ zãasŋɔ pʋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azozɛfʋ, Adavidi yɩɩŋa, da zoe dabeem, di Amaari tɩ a dɛna fʋ pɔga, pʋʋrɛ la tɩ a tarɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ ẽ.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: A wʋn dɔgɛ la budibla, tɩ fʋ sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi, la de ẽŋa n wʋn fãagɛ a nẽrba la ba tõon-be'ero pʋam basɛ.».

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Bõn-bãma wuu ẽŋɛ mɛ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa la yele se'em na n ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ:.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: «Bɩsɛ-ya, pugla n nãn ka mi bʋraaga wʋn yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ dɔgɛ budibla, tɩ ba wi'ira a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ A'emanuwɛlɩ.» A võorɛ de la Nawẽnnɛ boe la tɩ lɛ.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ nẽegɛ, ẽŋɛ wʋ Zuudãana malɛka la n yele ẽ se'em na, di a pɔga la.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: La a daan ka gã'arɛ la ẽ, halɩ tɩ a wa dɔgɛ budibla la, tɩ a sɩ bia la yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Bɛla wẽnnɛ tɩ Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, wa sẽŋɛ Zude weem ka mõola.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: yetɩ: «Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɛm mɛ.».

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Galile naba A'erɔdɩ daan wõm ban tɔgrɩ Azezi yelle.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: La de la Azã yelle tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi daan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽra ayɩla n boe weem mõola ke'enke'em yetɩ: ‹Malgɛ-ya sore sõŋa gu'ura Zuudãana. Malgɛ-ya sɔa la tɩ ba tɔ tẽntẽesɩ bɔ ẽ.› ».

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: tɩ a yele a tõntõnɩba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na n vo'oge. La de la bɛla n soe tɩ a tara pãŋa tõnna tõon-yãlma.».

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Azã daan yɛ la fuugo n de yʋgnɛ kõbrɔ, dɩkɛ sagãnnɛ lʋ a sɩa. A dia daan de la saniiro la seero.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Se'ere n soe tɩ a yele bɛla la, A'erɔdɩ mẽŋa n daan botɩ ba yõkɛ Azã lʋ tarɩ ka kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem a kẽema Afilipi pɔga A'erodɩadɩ ĩyã.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Tɩ nẽrba daan ze'ele Zerizalɛm, la Zude so'olʋm wuu, la tẽn-sɛba n lɛm kʋlaaga yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Zʋrdẽ la wuu sẽnna a zẽ'am.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Se'ere n soe la, A'erɔdɩ daan fãagɛ la Afilipi pɔga di, tɩ Azã daan yele ẽ yetɩ: «La ka masɛ tɩ fʋ fãagɛ ẽ di.».

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: tɔgra ba tõon-be'ero pa'ala ẽ, tɩ a misra ba Zʋrdẽ ko'om na pʋam.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: A'erɔdɩ daan boorɩ tɩ a kʋ Azã mɛ, dee zɔta tẽŋa la nẽrba, se'ere n soe la, ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ Azã de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: La Azã yẽ la Farisi dõma, la Saduse dõma zozo'e n sẽm a zẽ'am tɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ãn wʋ kãmpẽesɩ buuri, ãne n pa'alɛ ya tɩ ya tã wʋn zoe põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ n sẽm na?.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: La A'erɔdɩ daan wa ɩta la a dɔka daarɛ kibsa, tɩ A'erodɩadɩ pɔyʋa wa nẽrba nẽŋam, tɩ A'erɔdɩ sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm halɩ zozo'e.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Tõm-ya tõon-sɛba n pa'alɩ tɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: tɩ a pɔɩ yele pugla la yetɩ, bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ a wʋn sose, ẽŋa wʋn bɔ ẽ.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: La ya da tẽ'esra tɩ Abraham n de ya yaaba la, ya wʋn põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ la mɛ. Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn dɩkɛ kuga wã botɩ ba lebge Abraham yɩɩsɩ mɛ.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Tɩ a ma botɩ a yele naba A'erɔdɩ yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ Azã n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na zuugo ẽŋɛ la-pɛsŋa pʋam bɔ mam kalam.».

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nẽra n põn tara lɩa a nu'usum tɩ a lʋbɛ tɩɩsɩ. Tɩ-sɛka woo n ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma, a wʋn lʋbɛ ẽ mɛ, lobge bugum pʋam, tɩ bugum di ẽ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Tɩ naba la sũure sã'am, la ẽn pɔɩ la ĩyã, la sãama la ĩyã, tɩ a bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ ba bɔ pugla la ẽn boorɩ sɛla la.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Mam misri ya la ko'om pʋam tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. La sɛka n boe mam poorʋm sẽna la pãŋa gãnnɩ mam mɛ. La mam põn ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra. Ẽŋa n wʋn mise ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la bugum pʋam.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Dee tõm nẽra tɩ a sẽŋɛ yʋ'a deem ka wike Azã zuugo.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ẽŋa wʋn welge nẽrba wʋ nẽra n zãlɩ pɩ'ɩgɔ a nu'usum tɩ a vaagɛ a si zɛɛŋa yeele, ẽŋɛ a bãarɛ pʋam dee yõ urgo la, la bugum n ka kĩŋri.».

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka wike zuugo la ẽŋɛ la-pɛsŋa pʋam tarɩ ka bɔ pugla la, tɩ a to'oge ka bɔ a ma.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la Galile wa'am Zʋrdẽ Azã zẽ'am tɩ a mise ẽ ko'om pʋam.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi wa'am wa dɩkɛ kũm na ka laagɛ, dee sẽŋɛ ka yele Azezi.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Tɩ Azã daan zagsɛ, dee yetɩ: «La de nɩ na mam n wʋn wa'am fõn zẽ'am tɩ fõn mise mam, tɩ fõn sẽna mam zẽ'am?».

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Azezi n daan wõm bɛla la, a kẽ la õorŋɔ fõrgɛ wɛ'ɛsa kẽnkɛrŋa, zẽ'a tɩ nẽrba ka boe. Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la bãŋɛ, ze'ele tẽnsɩ pʋam babsɛ ẽ la nãma.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ la ẽŋɛ bɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, tɩ la de la bɛla tɩ tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ lan masɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na wuu.» Azezi n yele bɛla la, tɩ Azã sakɛ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ ze'ele õorŋɔ la zuo sige, yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ ba ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a botɩ ba bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: mise ẽ, tɩ a dɔɔra. Haya, tɩ saazuum yo'oge, tɩ a yẽ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n signi a zuo wʋ na'adawenne.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Tɩ zaanõorɛ paagɛ, tɩ karẽnbiisi la lɛm Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zẽ'a wã de la mõom, dee tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ'era, bɛla botɩ nẽrba la sẽŋɛ tẽnsɩ la pʋam ka da dia di.».

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Tɩ kõa ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu, a paagɛ la mam yɛm.».

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La ka de pẽrgrɛ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ, ya mẽŋa bɔ ba dia.».

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma tarɩ la borborɩ banuu, la zũma bayi mã'a.».

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Tarɩ-ya ba wa'am wa bɔ ma.».

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Dee botɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la zĩ'ire mõorɔ zuo. Tɩ a dɩkɛ borborɩ banuu, la zũma bayi la, gorge bɩsɛ saazuum pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise borborɩ la bɔ a karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba pʋɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ ba to'oge pʋɩ ba.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Tɩ ba wuu di tɩgɛ, dee tɩ karẽnbiisi la vaagɛ bõn-gito n deege pɩrɛ pɩtɔ pia la ayi.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Sɛba n daan di dia la, bʋraasɩ daan de wʋ tʋsanuu, tɩ pɔgsɩ la kɔma kãllɛ ka pa'asɛ.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi basɛ a karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba kẽ õorŋɔ dẽŋɛ ẽ dɔɔra mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba, dee tɩ a mẽŋa weege tɩ a keese nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Ẽn daan keese nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a sẽŋɛ la kẽnkɛrŋa ka zom tãŋa zuo tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ, tɩ ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a bɔna mĩ.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: La õorŋɔ la daan boe la mõgrɛ la pʋam zãazãarɛ, tɩ ko-mĩ'isi wẽ'era ẽ, tɩ ba nãmsra, se'ere n soe la, kʋsebgo n vugri tʋ'ʋsra ba.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Tɩ beere yɔna wiire, tɩ Azezi sẽnna ko'om na zuo wɛ'ɛsa a karẽnbiisi la bɔba.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la yẽ tɩ a sẽnnɩ ko'om na zuo, tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba yetɩ: «A de la ki-isgne.» Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba kaasra tagursi.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Keŋe-ya ya sũure, la de la mam, da zoe-ya dabeem.».

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la fõn, bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m sẽŋɛ ko'om na zuo wa'am fʋ zẽ'am.».

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am.» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yese õorŋɔ la pʋam sẽnna ko'om na zuo wɛ'ɛsa Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ bɩsɛ la kʋsebgo la n vugri zo'oge la, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ẽ. Tɩ a bɔna mĩ tɩ a mise, kaasɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fãagɛ ma.».

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Tɩ Azezi darɛ tẽegɛ nu'ugo gʋrɛ ẽ dee yetɩ: «Fʋ ka bɔ mam sɩra zo'oge, bẽm tɩ fʋ yɛm yɔɔra?».

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Tɩ ba kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam, tɩ kʋsebgo la go'oge.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la n boe õorŋɔ la pʋam na ka dũma Azezi nẽŋam yetɩ: «Fõn sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la.».

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Tɩ ba pãrgɛ mõgrɛ la dooge paagɛ Zenezarɛtɩ tẽŋa.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Tɩ zẽ'e-ẽŋa nẽrba bãŋɛ Azezi, tõm nẽrba tẽn-sɛba n lɛm na wuu, tɩ ba tarɩ bã'adõma wuu wa'am a zẽ'am.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: Ba daan sose ẽ yetɩ a basɛ tɩ bã'adõma la kalʋm a fuugo nõorɛ mã'a. La sɛba wuu n daan kalʋm a fuugo la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Ba daan dɔgɛ Azezi la Bɛtelɛm n boe Zude so'olʋm, se'emwẽnnɛ tɩ A'erɔdɩ de naba la. Tɩ bãŋrɩba ze'ele dapɔa bɔba wa'am Zerizalɛm.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n ze'ele Zerizalɛm wa'am wa soke Azezi yetɩ:.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: wa soke yetɩ: «Bia la tɩ ba dɔgɛ la boe la bɛ, sɛka n wʋn dɛna Zifdõma naba la? Tõma yẽ la a wõrbiire dapɔa bɔba, bɛla ĩyã tɩ tõma wa'am tɩ tɩ nã'asɛ ẽ.».

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: «Bẽm tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka sakra tɩ yaabdõma malŋɔ la? Ba sãn yetɩ ba di, ba ka peerɩ nu'usi wʋ tɩ yaabdõma n ɩtɩ se'em na.».

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: La naba A'erɔdɩ n daan wõm bɛla la, ẽŋa la Zerizalɛm dõma wuu yɛm n yɔɔrɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Tɩ a lebe soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm ẽŋɛ tɩ yãma mẽ sɩ'ɩsra Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la, ya mẽŋa malŋɔ ĩyã?.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Tɩ A'erɔdɩ daan lagsɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wuu, soke ba yetɩ: «Tẽn-kãna tɩ ba wʋn dɔgɛ Krisi la?».

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Nanna fʋ sɔ, la fʋ ma.› Dee le yetɩ: ‹Sɛka n tɔgɛ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma be'em, dẽnɩ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.›.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: «Da zɛrgra-ya taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ da zɛrgɛ ya.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛtelɛm n boe Zude so'olʋm na, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yele la wãna:.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: La yãma yetɩ, nẽra sãn yele a sɔ bɩɩ a ma yetɩ: ‹Mam n tarɩ nɩ bõn-sɛka tɩ m sõŋɛ fɔ la, de la Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ›, la ka le dɛna tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ a sɔ.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Se'ere n soe la, yan wʋn zɛrgɛ nẽrba se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn zɛrgɛ ya la bɛla. La sɛla tɩ ya dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ nẽrba la, ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ ya.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: ‹Bɛtelɛm n de tẽn-pɩka, fʋ ka pɔ'ɔgɛ Zude so'olʋm tẽn-kãra la pʋam, se'ere n soe la, nẽra n wʋn yese fõn pʋam dɛna nẽŋadãana bɩsra Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n de mam nẽrba la yelle.› ».

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: Bɛla, ya basrɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la lebge la zãŋa, dee dɔla ya mẽŋa malŋɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Azezi n daan pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yese bilam sẽŋɛ la so'olʋm na tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam ka zãsna nẽrba, dee mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Bẽm tɩ fʋ bɩsra sɔkɔ n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum, dee ka bɩsra dɔɔgɔ n boe fʋ mẽŋa nifum na?.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: La A'erɔdɩ sʋgɛ mɛ wi bãŋrɩba la soke ba bãŋɛ wẽnse'ere tɩ wõrbiire la yese.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Pʋlibsum dõma wã, A'ezayi yʋʋn sɩrɩ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ ya yelle, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Tɩ Azã daan bɔna yʋ'a deem, wõm Krisi tõoma la yelle, tõm a karẽnbiisi a zẽ'am.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne yɛta fʋ sɔbia yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ fʋ yese sɔkɔ a nifum, dee tɩ dɔɔgɔ bɔna fõn mẽŋa nifum?.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: dee tõm ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya Bɛtelɛm ka ɛ soke bãŋɛ bia la yelle sõŋa sõŋa, la ya sãn yẽ ẽ, ya leme wa'am wa yele mam tɩ mam mẽ sẽŋɛ ka nã'asɛ ẽ.».

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: ‹Nẽr-bãna wã pẽgrɩ mam na ba nõorɛ yetɔga, la ba sũure zãagɛ la mam mɛ.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na bɩɩ, tɩ gu'ura nẽra ayẽma?».

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Pʋlibsum dãana, dẽŋɛ yese dɔɔgɔ la fʋ mẽŋa nifum, dee yãŋa tã'agɛ yẽ sõŋa yese sɔkɔ la n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum na.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: La ban wõm naba la yetɔga la, ba sẽnnɩ mɛ wɛ'ɛsa, darɛ le yẽ wõrbiire la tɩ ba daan yẽ dapɔa bɔba la n tole ba nẽŋa, tarɩ ba halɩ ka paagɛ bia la n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la ze'ele.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Ban nã'asrɩ mam na, la de la zãŋa. Se'ere n soe la, ba zãsnɩ na zãsŋɔ n de nẽrsaalba lɔɔ.› ».

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele Azã yãma n wõnnɩ se'em, la yãma n yẽtɩ se'em na:.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Da dɩkɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ bõn-sõma bɔ baasɩ, la ya da dɩkɛ sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ zozo'e lobge kurkurdõma nẽŋam, tɩ ba wʋn nẽ ba mɛ la ba nãma, dee tɩ baasɩ la wẽrgɛ dõn ya zõrzõrʋm.».

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: La ban daan yẽ wõrbiire la, ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ halɩ zozo'e.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la tãŋa la zuo sige, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e dɔla ẽ.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: La Azezi wi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ ba wa'am na a zẽ'am, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Selse-ya sõŋa sõŋa wõm a võorɛ:.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: Fɔɔsɩ nini yẽtɩ mɛ, tɩ kɔɔrnɔ sẽnna, tɩ kõnkɔma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa, tɩ wansɩ wõnna, tɩ kũm vɔ'ɔra, tɩ nasdõma wõnna Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Azezi daan wi a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la mɛ bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba dige kʋlkã'arsɩ, dee botɩ bã'adõma buuri wuu, la kobro buuri wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: «Sose-ya Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya. Ɛ-ya, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ. Wẽ-ya kʋlɩŋa, tɩ a wʋn yo'oge bɔ ya.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Tɩ ba kẽ yire la pʋam yẽ bia la, la a ma Amaari, ka dũma tẽŋa bia la nẽŋam nã'asɛ ẽ, dee lorge ba lɔgrɔ loose ban tarɩ sɛla la bɔ ẽ. Ba daan de la sãlma, la tɩdarɛ, la miiri n tarɩ yũuŋ-yẽlga.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: «Guuse-ya, da ɩta-ya ɩr-sõma nẽrba nẽŋam tɩ ba yẽta ya. Ya sãn ɩta bɛla, ya kãn to'oge yɔɔrɔ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum zẽ'am.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Tɩ kõnkonne wa'am a zẽ'am wa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fʋ sãn bɔɔra, fʋ tã wʋn basɛ tɩ m yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ le ãna sõŋa.».

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: La dagɩ sɛla n kẽ'erɩ nẽrsaala nõorʋm n botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ, la de la sɛla n ze'etɩ nẽrsaala nõorʋm yɛsra n botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ.».

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n ka lebrɩ poorʋm mam ĩyã la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: Karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la tɩ a looge tɩ a tõm na yʋ'ʋra n wãna: Pɔspɔsɩ dãana de la Asimõ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la a yɩbga A'ãndre, la Azebede dayɔɔsɩ Azakɩ la a yɩbga Azã.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, a wʋn bɔ ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n eerɩ, wʋn yẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n wẽ'erɩ kʋlɩŋa, ba wʋn yo'oge bɔ ẽ.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan kã'an ba zãasŋɔ pʋam yetɩ, ba da le doose naba A'erɔdɩ zẽ'am, bɛla tɩ ba daan doose sore ayẽma lebe kule ba tẽŋa.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Bɛla, fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ sõŋɛ nasa, da kãn mõolɛ nẽrba zẽ'am wʋ pʋlibsum dõma n ɩtɩ se'em ba wẽndeto pʋam, la sorɔɔrɔ zuto tɩ nẽrba pẽgɛ ba la. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ bãma põn to'oge ba yɔɔrɔ mɛ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, zom tãŋa zuo zĩ'ire. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lagsɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Tɩ Azezi tẽegɛ a nu'ugo kalʋm ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Mam bɔɔra, yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa.» Bilam mẽŋa tɩ kõnkɔm na basɛ ẽ, tɩ a le ãna sõŋa.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Bɛla tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lɛm ẽ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mi tɩ Farisi dõma la n wõm fõn tɔgɛ bɛla la, la dõn ba sũure mɛ?».

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Azezi daan kẽ õorŋɔ lebe dooge la mõgrɛ la ke bɔba kule Kapɛrnaʋm, ẽn kẽ'erɩ zẽ'a la.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Azã karẽnbiisi la n fõrgɛ la, tɩ Azezi yãŋa tɔgra Azã yelle bɔ'ɔra nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma daan sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ weem? Yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la mõorɔ tɩ kʋsebgo mĩini bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: la Afilipi, la Abatelemi, la Atoma, la Amatie n de lampo-tɔ'ɔsa la, la Azakɩ n de Alfe dayʋa, la Atade.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Ãne n boe yãma pʋam tɩ a bia wʋn sose ẽ borborɩ, tɩ a bɔ ẽ kugre?.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Bãŋrɩba la n fõrgɛ la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Azozɛfʋ zãasŋɔ pʋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge tarɩ bia la, la a ma la zoe sẽŋɛ Ezipti ka bɔna bilam halɩ tɩ m le wa yele fʋ se'ere. Se'ere n soe la, naba A'erɔdɩ wʋn ɛɛra bia la mɛ tɩ a kʋ.».

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ sõŋɛ nasdõma, da basɛ tɩ fʋ gɔbga nu'ugo bãŋɛ fʋ zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo n ɩtɩ sɛla.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: Tɩ a yãŋa zãsʋm ba yetɩ:.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da yele nẽra nẽra. La sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽema zẽ'am ka pa'alɛ ẽ fɔ mẽŋa tɩ a bɩsɛ, dee bɔ bõn-sɛba tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yetɩ ba bɔ la, tɩ la pa'alɛ ba tɩ fʋ sɩrɩ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa, Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan dolɩ la vatɔ pʋam, Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ tɔlla. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi kɔm dõnna, tɩ ba kɔ si wẽena ɔbra.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Sɛla woo tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ ka bʋrɛ, ba wʋn võogɛ ẽ basɛ.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Daar-ẽŋa, Azezi yese yire sẽŋɛ ka zẽa la mõgrɛ la nõorʋm.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Tɩ nẽrba basɛba zɛ bʋraa ayɩla n de kɔɔrŋɔ n gã a bõn-gã'ala pʋam wa'am a zẽ'am. Tɩ Azezi yẽ ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la, yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «M bia, keŋe fʋ sũure, fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna fʋ zuo.».

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: La bẽm tɩ yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ? Nẽra n yɛ fu-sõma? Ayɛɩ. Nẽr-sɛba n yɛ fu-sõma la boe na'adõma yiɛ.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: la Asimõ n daan de Zelɔtɩ nẽra ayɩla la, la Azudaasɩ Iskariyo, sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi la.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Bɩɩ a sãn sose zũfo, tɩ a bɔ ẽ bõnsɛla?.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ isge yʋ'ʋŋɔ tarɩ bia la, la a ma la sẽŋɛ Ezipti.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Bɛla nẽra nẽra kãn bãŋɛ tɩ fʋ sõŋɛ nasdõma, la fʋ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n yẽ fõn sʋgɛ ẽŋɛ la, a wʋn lebse fo.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: «Nẽr-sɛba n mi tɩ ba ka masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la ba bõnɔ.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Azezi daan sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ kẽ'era la Kapɛrnaʋm tẽŋa pʋam, tɩ Rom dõma sʋda-kɛ'ɛma wa'am a zẽ'am wa sose ẽ.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma yẽ, yele Azezi yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, fʋ karẽnbiisi la ɩtɩ la sɛla n sisri vo'osgo daarɛ.».

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Basɛ-ya ba, tɩ ba ãn wʋ fɔɔsɩ n ve'erɩ fɔɔsɩ. Fʋa sãn vɛ'ɛra fʋa, bãma bayi wuu wʋn lui la boko pʋam.».

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e lagsɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ a kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam zĩ'ire, dee tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la zɛ mõgrɛ la nõorʋm.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba tẽ'esra ba sũure pʋam yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna pɔ'ɔrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: La yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la bẽm? Ya sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa bɩɩ? Ẽe. La mam yetɩ ya tɩ a de la nẽra n gãnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: La de la bãna pia la ayi tɩ Azezi daan tõm, yele ba yetɩ: «Da sẽŋɛ-ya bu-zãŋsɩ la zẽ'am, la da kẽ-ya Samaari dõma tẽnsɩ pʋam.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Yãma nẽrsaalba n de tõon-be'ero dõma la sãn mina tɩ ya bɔ ya kɔma bõn-sõma, ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum kãn bɔ yãma sɛba n sosrɩ ẽ na bõn-sõma n gãnnɩ bɛla?.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: ka bɔna bilam halɩ tɩ A'erɔdɩ wa ki. La ẽŋɛ la bɛla, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Mam wi m Dayʋa la n boe Ezipti la tɩ a wa'am.».

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Ya sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, da ɩta-ya wʋ pʋlibsum dõma n ɩtɩ se'em na, bãma nõŋɛ la ba zɛ ba wẽndeto pʋam, la sorɔ-puursi zuto pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ tɩ nẽrba yẽta ba. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ba põn to'oge ba yɔɔrɔ mɛ.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Sũ-sã'aŋɔ dõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ ba sũure mã'agɛ.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam tõntõnna n bẽ'erɩ kɔɔrʋm gã yire, a ba nãmsra mɛ zozo'e.».

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka karẽŋɛ Adavidi la a nẽrba la kɔm n yʋʋn dõnnɩ tɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na?.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pa'alɛ tɔ makrɛ la võorɛ.».

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Tɩ a makɛ magsa tɔgɛ yɛla zozo'e bɔ ba yetɩ: «Kaara n daan yese tɩ a lobge a bõnbuuri.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: La Azezi mi ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na mɛ, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma tẽ'esra pʋtẽ'e-be'ero ya sũure pʋam?.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: La de la ẽŋa yelle tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam tõntõnna n wãna tɩ mam tõm tɩ a dẽŋɛ fʋ nẽŋa malgɛ sore bɔ fɔ.›.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Sẽŋɛ-ya Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n ãn wʋ piisi n bɔɩ la zẽ'am.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Yãma n boorɩ tɩ nẽrba ɩta se'em bɔ'ɔra ya la, yãma mẽ ɩta bɛla bɔ'ɔra ba. Ẽna de la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n gʋlsɛ se'em n bɛla.».

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: La A'erɔdɩ n daan bãŋɛ tɩ bãŋrɩba la pã'asɛ ẽŋa mɛ na, a sũure n yĩige halɩ, tɩ a tõm nẽrba yetɩ ba sẽŋɛ Bɛtelɛm, la tẽn-sɛba n boe a kẽnkɛrsɩ la wuu ka kʋ budim-sɛba wuu n masɛ wʋ yʋʋma ayi, la sɛba n ka paagɛ bɛla la, wʋ ẽŋa n daan soke bãŋrɩba la bãŋɛ se'emwẽnnɛ tɩ wõrbiire la yese la.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, fʋ kẽ fʋ deem yʋ kʋlɩŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ fʋ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe zẽ'a n sʋgɛ la. La fʋ Sɔ la n yẽ tɩ fʋ sʋgɛ mɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ la, a wʋn yɔ fɔ.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Sũ-bʋgsʋm dõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ba dũnia tɩ ba sɔna.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn wa'am wa botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: A yʋʋn kẽ la Nawẽnnɛ deego la pʋam dɩkɛ borborɩ sɛba tɩ ba bĩŋe bɔ Nawẽnnɛ na obe, dee bɔ a nẽrba la. La sisri mɛ, nẽra nẽra ka obrɩ ba, sãn dagna kãabgɔ kẽma la mã'a.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma yɛm mẽ ka like?.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: La ẽn lobgrɩ bõnbuuri la, basɛba lui la sore nõorʋm, tɩ niinto wa'am wa di ba.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Bẽm n de nãana gãnna? Mam sãn yele bʋraa la yetɩ, a tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna a zuo, bɩɩ mam sãn yele ẽ yetɩ, a isge sẽnna n de nãana gãnna?.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽrsaalba tɩ ba dɔgɛ la wuu pʋam, nẽra ka boe n gãnnɩ Azã n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na. La nẽr-sɛka n de pɩka Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam gãnnɩ Azã mɛ.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: ka mõola yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na lɛm mɛ.›.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: «Doose-ya zanõrɛ n ãn mika kẽ, se'ere n soe la, zanõ-yalŋa, la so-kãtɛ la we'esɩ la sã'aŋɔ zẽ'am, la nẽrba zozo'e dolɩ la bilam.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azeremi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ:.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Ya sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, ya da tɔgra yetɔga n ka tarɩ võorɛ kaana wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em na, ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma n tɔgrɩ zɔ'ɔra la ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn lerge bãma pʋ'ʋsgɔ mɛ.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Sɛba n boorɩ sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm la ba sũure wuu tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ba sɛla tɩ ba boorɩ la tɩ la seke.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la lebse yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka narɩ tɩ fõn wa'am mam yire. Wen tɔgɛ yetɔga mã'a, tɩ mam tõntõnna la wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Bɩɩ yãma ka karẽŋɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam n yetɩ, vo'osgo daarɛ, kãabgɔ kẽma la n tõnnɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam na sã'anɩ vo'osgo daarɛ sisgo la mɛ tɩ taalɛ ka bɔna?.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ bõn-sɛka woo n kẽ'erɩ nẽrsaala nõorʋm tollɩ la a pʋʋrʋm, tɩ a yẽ'era ba basra?.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Tɩ basɛba lui tãmpĩa zuo, zẽ'a tɩ tẽntõnnɔ ka zo'oge. Tẽntõnnɔ n ka zo'oge la ĩyã, ba yese la tɔtɔ.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Mam wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa tarɩ nõorɛ dũnia zuo wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ.» Azezi yele bɛla, dee yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «Isge, dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala la kule.».

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Lan pɔsɛ Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm sẽnnɩ nẽŋa la pãŋa, la pãnsɩ dõma n mõrgrɩ tɩ ba fãagɛ ẽ.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: La ya botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee botɩ kũm vo'oge, dee botɩ kõnkɔma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa, dee dige kʋlkã'arsɩ. Yãma to'oge la zãŋa, bɛla bɔ-ya zãŋa.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: La zanõ-mika, la so-bãalga we'esɩ la vɔm zẽ'am. La de la nẽrba fẽe n dolɩ ẽ.».

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: «Ba wõm tagursi Rama tẽŋa, kʋʋnɔ la fablɩgɔ zozo'e. La de la Arasɛlɩ n kellɩ a kɔma yelle, tɩ ba pã'asra ẽ tɩ a ka sakra tɩ a go'oge. Se'ere n soe la, a kɔma la ka le bɔna.».

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Bɛla, da ãna-ya wʋ bãma n ãn se'em na, se'ere n soe la, lan dẽŋɛ ya wʋn sose ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, a põn mina yãma n boorɩ sɛla mɛ.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Ninbãal-zɔtba tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zoe ba ninbãalga.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba n soe mam, la mam mẽ soe la sʋdaasɩ basɛba. Mam sãn yele sʋda-sɛka yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ›, a we'esɩ mɛ. La mam sãn yele ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Wa'am›, a sẽm mɛ. Mam sãn yele m tõntõnna yetɩ: ‹Ẽŋɛ wãna›, a ɩtɩ mɛ.».

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ nẽra n gãnnɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la n boe kalam.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: La sɛla n yesrɩ nẽrsaala nõorʋm ze'ele la a sũure pʋam, la de la bõn-bãma n botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa yẽkɛ wẽ ba, tɩ ba ki, ba yẽga n ka kẽ tẽŋa la ĩyã.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Tɩ a isge kule.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la wuu, la lɔɔ gõnnɔ la wuu dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ so'olʋm na yelle mɛ halɩ wa paagɛ Azã wakatɛ.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Da dɩkɛ-ya sãlma bɩɩ ligri sũm sẽŋɛ.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: «Guuse ya mẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n parnɩ tɩ bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la. Ba wʋn ẽŋɛ ba mẽŋa wʋ saasaadõma n dɩkɛ piisi gãna fʋrɛ ba mẽŋa wa'am ya zẽ'am wʋ ba de la piisi la, dee tɩ ba pʋam ba sã'ana wʋ saasaadõma la.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: La naba A'erɔdɩ daan wa ki mɛ, tɩ Zuudãana malɛka puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Azozɛfʋ zãasŋɔ pʋam Ezipti, yele ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Bɛla, pʋ'ʋsra-ya wãna: ‹Tõma Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum, botɩ fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ yẽta pẽka.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Pʋpeelem dõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ba wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Azezi n wõm bɛla la, la di'ige ẽ mɛ. Tɩ a yele nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ mam nãn ka yẽ nẽra n bɔ mam sɩra wʋ bʋra-ẽna wã, baa Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la pʋam mam ka yẽ.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: La gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam boorɩ tɩ nẽrsaalba zɔta la taaba ninbãalga, dee dagɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba bõnɔ kãabra bɔ'ɔra mam.› Yãma sãn bãŋɛ nɩ yetɔg-ẽna wã võorɛ, ya kãn yele nɩ sɛba n ka tõm be'em tɩ ba tuuge ya.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Se'ere n soe la, pʋtẽ'e-yooro ze'etɩ la nẽrsaala sũure pʋam botɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em: Nẽr-kʋa, la yalsɩ, la taaba pɔgba la taaba sɩrba gã'a, la nayigum, la põmpɔrŋɔ, la pɔ'ɔrɛ yetɔga.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Tɩ basɛba lui gõ'osɩ zẽ'a, tɩ gõ'osɩ la yese fʋrɛ ba.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yẽ bɛla, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ ẽn bɔ nẽrsaalba nõor-ẽŋa la ĩyã.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: La ya sãn bɔɔra tɩ ya bãŋɛ a võorɛ, Azã n de A'eli tɩ ba yʋʋn yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: La ya da dɩkɛ tãmpɔkɔ sore la sẽnnɛ ĩyã, da dɩkɛ-ya futo bayi, bɩɩ tagra, bɩɩ doore. Ba wʋn bɔ tõntõnna ẽn boorɩ sɛla.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Ba tõoma pʋam tɩ ya wʋn bãŋɛ ba. Bẽm ĩyã, zãaŋa ka tã wʋn wɔm tã'ama, la kẽkɔ ka tã wʋn wɔm kẽnkãma.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: «Isge tarɩ bia la, la a ma la lebe Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, sɛka n daan eerɩ bia la tɩ a kʋ la ki mɛ.».

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: wa'am wa di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka, botɩ fõn boorɩ se'em ẽŋɛ tẽŋa zuo, wʋ lan ɩtɩ fõn yire se'em na.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Sɛba n botɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna nẽrba tẽŋasʋka tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wi'ira ba tɩ a kɔma.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ, nẽrba zozo'e wʋn ze'ele zanõ-bɔba, la dapɔ-bɔba wa'am wa zĩ'ire la Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ di Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n soe vo'osgo daarɛ.».

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: La de la bãna wã n dẽgrɩ nẽrsaala. La nẽra sãn di dee ka peege nu'usi, bɛla ka dẽgrɩ nẽra.».

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Tɩ basɛba lui zẽ'e-sõŋɔ zẽ'a, wɔm biɛ. Basɛba wɔm na biɛ kɔbga kɔbga, tɩ basɛba wɔm pisyoob-yoobɩ, basɛba pitã-tã.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la bilam sẽnna tɔlla, yẽ bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Amatie n zĩ tɔ'ɔsra lampo. Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge dɔla mam.» Tɩ Amatie isge dɔla ẽ.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Ya sãn paagɛ tẽn-kãtɛ bɩɩ tẽn-pɩka pʋam, soke-ya bãŋɛ nẽr-sõŋɔ sãn bɔna mĩ wʋn to'oge ya tɩ ya kẽ'era a yire, bɔna-ya bilam ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn fõrgɛ tẽŋa la.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Tɩ-sõŋɔ woo wɔnnɩ na biɛ sõma, la tɩ-be'ego woo wɔnnɩ na biɛ be'ero.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ isge tarɩ bia la, la a ma la kule Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋa.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Bɔ tɔ tɩ zĩna dia.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Sɛba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, tɩ ba nãmsra ba tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la ba bõnɔ.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: La ba wʋn lobge Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n wʋn soe nɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na basɛ la yẽŋa lika pʋam, bilam ba wʋn kaasra, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Azezi fõrgɛ bilam sẽŋɛ la ba wẽndeem.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Azezi daan fõrgɛ bilam sẽŋɛ la Tiiri, la Sidõ so'olʋm.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Amatie yire dita, tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma zozo'e wa'am wa lagɩm na Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la zẽa dita.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Mam wʋn dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba? Ba ãn wʋ kɔma n zĩ da'am wi'ira taaba yetɩ:.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Ya sãn paagɛ kẽ yire pʋam, ya pʋ'ʋsɛ ba yetɩ: ‹Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la ya.›.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Tɩ-sõŋɔ ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ be'ero, la tɩ-be'ego mẽ ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ sõma.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: La a daan wõm mɛ tɩ Arkelawusi n sɔɔgɛ a sɔ A'erɔdɩ zẽ'am di Zude na'am. Tɩ a daan zoe dabeem ka sẽŋɛ Zude so'olʋm. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã'an ẽ zãasŋɔ pʋam, tɩ a sẽŋɛ Galile so'olʋm.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ, wʋ tõma n mẽ basɛ sɛba n ẽŋɛ tõma be'em taalɛ se'em na.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Yãma sɛba tɩ ba tʋʋrɩ ya, nãmsra ya, tɔgra ya tɔg-be'ero dee parna bɔ'ɔra ya, yan dolɩ mam na ĩyã, tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: A yele bɛla, dee yele sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la yetɩ: «Kule, tɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ fõn bɔ mam sɩra se'em na.» La wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ a tõntõnna la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Tɩ bʋraa ayɩla daan bɔna mĩ, tɩ a nu'ugo ki. Tɩ Farisi dõma bɔɔra tɩ ba dõrɛ Azezi, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ ba botɩ nẽra yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ?».

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Tɩ Kanaan pɔka ze'ele tẽn-bãma wa'am a zẽ'am wa tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Zuudãana, Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe m ninbãalga, mam pɔyʋa tɩ kʋlkã'arga nãmsra ẽ halɩ zozo'e.».

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lɛm ẽ soke yetɩ: «Bẽm ĩyã tɩ fõn makra magsa tɔgra bɔ'ɔra ba?».

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma yẽ bɛla, soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma karẽnsãama la lagɩm na lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma dita.».

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: ‹Tõma peebe wɩɩsɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma zagsɛ ka wa. Tɩ tõma yõom kʋʋrɛ yõoma, tɩ yãma zagsɛ ka yõom.›.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: La yire la dõma sãn to'oge ya, ya sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la ba. La ba sãn zagsɛ ka to'oge ya, ya sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn weege ya zẽ'am.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Tɩ-sɛka woo n ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma, ba wʋn lʋbɛ ẽ mɛ yõ la bugum.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: A daan sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era la tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Nazarɛtɩ. Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n daan yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. Ba daan yetɩ: «Ba wʋn wi'ira Krisi la tɩ Nazarɛtɩ nẽra.».

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Da basɛ tɩ yelle paagɛ tɔ tɩ tɩ lui. Fãagɛ tɔ be'em dãana nu'usum basɛ. Tɩ fõn soe so'olʋm, la pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina!›.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm, ya yɔɔrɔ n wʋn zo'oge Nawẽnnɛ yire la ĩyã. Ba mẽ daan nãmsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n dẽŋɛ yãma la bɛla.».

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ yire ka yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ dɛɛm-pɔka n bẽ'erɩ gã, tɩ a ĩyã tʋlgɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-kãna n boe yãma pʋam wʋn tara pesgo, tɩ a lui boko pʋam vo'osgo daarɛ, tɩ a kãn yõkɛ ẽ tɩ a dooge?.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Tɩ Azezi ka lerge ẽ se'ere. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la paagɛ a zẽ'am yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽŋɛ bɔ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ, tɩ a kaasrɩ dɔla tɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ botɩ yãma bãŋɛ a so'olʋm yelle n sʋgɛ la mɛ. La a ka botɩ bãma bãŋɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Tɩ Azezi wõm bɛla, yele ba yetɩ: «Sɛba n tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm ka eerɩ tɩba, la de bã'adõma n eerɩ tɩba.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Azã wa'am mɛ wa lʋta nõorɛ dee ka yũura dãam, tɩ ba yetɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: La yi-sɛka, bɩɩ tẽn-sɛka nẽrba sãn ka to'oge ya, bɩɩ ka selse ya yetɔga, ya yese zẽ'e-ẽŋa dee pɩɩsɛ ya nãma tẽntõnnɔ basɛ mĩ.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Bɛla, la de la nẽrba la tõoma pʋam tɩ yãma wʋn bãŋɛ ban de nẽr-sɛba.».

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Ya sãn basɛ nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ ya be'em na taalɛ, ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Azezi daan le yetɩ: «Wʋ yaarʋm n botɩ dia zã'asʋm na, yãma mẽ masɛ tɩ ya ãna wʋ yaarʋm dũnia wã pʋam. La yaarʋm sãn sabgɛ, bẽm n wʋn basɛ tɩ a le zã'asʋm? A lebge la zãŋa, a ka le tara yõorɔ, a de la ba lobe ẽ basɛ yẽŋa, tɩ nẽrba nẽ ẽ la nãma.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Tɩ Azezi kalʋm pɔka la nu'ugo, tɩ bã'aga la basɛ ẽ. Tɩ a isge ẽŋɛ Azezi sãanɛ.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Sɛka n tarɩ wʋn to'oge pa'asɛ, tɩ la zo'oge. La sɛka n ka tarɩ, baa sɛla tɩ a tarɩ fẽe la, ba wʋn to'oge ẽ.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sarɩyadia daarɛ tẽn-ẽŋa dõma wʋn yẽ toogo gãnna Sodom la Gomõorɩ tẽnsɩ nẽrba.».

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Nẽrsaala tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ gãnna pesgo zozo'e. Bɛla, sore boe mɛ tɩ nẽra ẽŋɛ sõŋa vo'osgo daarɛ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ tõm mam na Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n ãn wʋ piisi n bɔɩ la mã'a zẽ'am.».

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa'am wa dita dee yũura, tɩ ba yetɩ, a de la dita, la dã-yũura, la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma sɩrɛ. Yɛm dãana tõoma n pa'alɩ tɩ a tarɩ yɛm.».

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: La ya sãn ka basɛ nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ ya be'em na taalɛ, ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ kãn basɛ ya taalɛ.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Azezi daan le yele yetɩ: «Dagɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n wi'iri mam tɩ ‹Zuudãana, Zuudãana› la n wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam. La de la nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum n boorɩ se'em na n wʋn kẽ.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Sẽŋɛ-ya ka zãsʋm Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na võorɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam boorɩ tɩ nẽrsaalba zɔta la taaba ninbãalga, dee dagɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba bõnɔ kãabra bɔ'ɔra mam.› Bɛla, mam ka wa'am tɩ m wi sɛba dõma n yetɩ bãma masɛ mɛ na, mam wa'am na tõon-be'ero dõma ĩyã.».

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Yãma de la dũnia wã peelem. Tẽŋa n boe tãŋa zuo ka tã wʋn sʋgɛ.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Tɩ zaanõorɛ paagɛ, tɩ ba tarɩ nẽrba zozo'e tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ tarɩ ba wa'am Azezi zẽ'am. Tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔga dige kʋlkã'arsɩ la, dee botɩ sɛba wuu n de bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam magsra magsa tɔgra bɔ'ɔra ba, se'ere n soe la, ba bɩsrɩ mɛ, dee ka yẽta, wõnna, dee ka bãŋra a võorɛ.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam tõm ya wʋ piisi saasaadõma tẽŋasʋka. Bɛla ĩyã, dɛna-ya yɛm dõma wʋ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma, la ya ãna wʋ na'adawɛma n ka tarɩ yelle se'em na.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: La Azezi yele bʋraa la yetɩ: «Tẽegɛ fʋ nu'ugo la.» Tɩ a tẽegɛ, tɩ a nu'ugo la ãna sõŋa wʋ nu'ugo ayẽma la.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Tɩ pɔka la paagɛ ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, sõŋɛ ma.».

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi zɛrgɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ a tõm tõon-yãlma zozo'e ba tẽnsɩ pʋam, dee tɩ ba ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero la yetɩ:.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Ya sãn lʋ nõorɛ, da zũŋe-ya nẽŋa wʋ pʋlibsum dõma n zũŋri ba nẽnsɩ se'em, tɩ ba pa'alɛ nẽrba tɩ bãma lʋ la nõorɛ la. Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ ba põn to'oge ba yɔɔrɔ mɛ.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, nẽrba zozo'e wʋn yele mam yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, Zuudãana, tõma daan dɩkɛ la fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba. La fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ tõma daan dɩkɛ dige kʋlkã'arsɩ, dee daan tõm tõon-yãlma zozo'e la fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ.›.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ tõma la Farisi dõma lʋta nõorɛ, dee tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka lʋta?».

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: La ba ka yõ'orɩ fɩtla vugra la pɩ'ɩgɔ, ba wʋn zoole ẽ na fɩtla zoolgo zẽ'a, tɩ a nẽegɛ peelem bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe yire la pʋam na.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n to'oge tõma tooro, la tɩ bã'asɩ basɛ.».

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ɩtɩ la bãma lɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Ya wʋn wõm, la ya kãn bãŋɛ a võorɛ, ya wʋn bɩsɛ, la ya kãn yẽ.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la nẽrba la. Se'ere n soe la, ba wʋn wa yõgɛ ya mɛ tarɩ sẽŋɛ sarɩyadia zẽ'esʋm, dee wẽ ya ba wẽndeto pʋam.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma la yese yẽŋa ka bãm bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em kʋ Azezi.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La ka masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ kɔma dia bɔ baasɩ.».

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: «Korazẽ dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo! Betisayida dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo! Se'ere n soe la, tõon-yãlma sɛba n tõm ya tẽnsɩ pʋam na, daan sãn tõm nɩ na Tiiri, la Sidõ tẽnsɩ pʋam, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, yɛ bɔra futo ẽŋɛ tãmpɛglʋm ẽŋɛ ba zuto tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ ba teege yɛm.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Fʋ sãn lʋ nõorɛ, wire fʋ nẽŋa dee sãasɛ fʋ zuugo.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: La mam wʋn yele ba yetɩ: ‹Mam põn ka mina ya, sẽŋɛ-ya fõrgɛ, tõon-be'ero dõma wã.› ».

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn di pɔka ẽŋɛ kibsa wi a tadãandõma tɩ ba wa'am naagɛ la ẽ dita, ba tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-sã'aŋɔ wakat-sɛka tɩ pɔgdita la ken bɔna la ba lɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ. La dabsa sẽm mɛ, tɩ ba wʋn yese pɔgdita la ba zẽ'am. Bɛla tɩ ba yãŋa wʋn lʋ nõorɛ.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ ya peelem nẽegɛ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka, tɩ ba yẽ ya tõon-sõma nã'asɛ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum.».

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Azezi n yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n kaagɛ ẽ na, tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Tɩ dooge-ya mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba.».

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãna pʋtẽ'era ka like, ba lɩ ba tʋba mɛ, dee mũm ba nini, tɩ ba nini da yẽ, tɩ ba tʋba da wõm, tɩ ba da wõm a võorɛ. Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm leme wa'am mam zẽ'am, tɩ m bɔ ba laafɛ.›.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: La ba wʋn yõgɛ ya sẽŋɛ gɔmnadõma, la na'adõma nẽŋam, yãma n dolɩ mam na ĩyã, tɩ ya wʋn dɛna kaset-dõma tɔgɛ bɔ bãma la bu-zãŋsɩ.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ bɛla, fõrgɛ bilam. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e doose ẽ. Tɩ a botɩ bã'adõma wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la de la sɩra. La baasɩ diti la di-zõra n luti ba dãana taablɩ tilum.».

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yɛta ya tɩ sarɩyadia daarɛ, la wʋn dɛna toogo bɔ yãma gãnna Tiiri, la Sidõ dõma.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: tɩ nẽrba da bãŋɛ tɩ fʋ lʋ la nõorɛ. La fʋ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe zẽ'a n sʋgɛ la wʋn bãŋɛ. La fʋ Sɔ la n yẽ fõn sʋgɛ lʋ nõorɛ la, a wʋn yɔ fɔ.».

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n wõm mam yetɔga wã, dee ẽŋɛ wʋ yetɔga la n yele se'em na, a dãana ãn wʋ yɛm dãana n mẽ a yire tãmpĩa zuo.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Nẽra kãn dɩkɛ tãn-paalga lige fu-kɛka. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, tãn-paalga la wʋn botɩ fu-kɛka la ãagɛ mɛ gãŋɛ bɛla.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: «Da tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ mam wa'am tɩ m sã'am na Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, bɩɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la. Mam ka wa'am tɩ m sã'am ba, mam wa'am tɩ m pa'alɛ la ba võorɛ ba'asɛ, dee ẽŋɛ ban yele se'em na.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama ayɩla wa'am a zẽ'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam wʋn dɔla fʋ sẽnna zẽ'e-sɛba wuu tɩ fʋ wʋn sẽnna.».

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: La, la sãn dɛna yãma, ya tarɩ zu-yẽlga, yãma tarɩ la nini n yẽtɩ, dee tara tʋba n wõnnɩ.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: La ba sãn tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ, da fabla-ya yãma n wʋn tɔgɛ se'em, bɩɩ yãma n wʋn tɔgɛ sɛla yelle. Se'ere n soe la, wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn bɔ ya bõn-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn tɔgɛ.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: Dee kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da botɩ nẽra bãŋɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, fʋ bɔ mam sɩra zo'oge mɛ. La wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ fõn boorɩ se'em na bɔ fɔ.» La wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ a pɔyʋa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
