local: La yãma Kapɛrnaʋm dõma, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya wʋn zẽkɛ mɛ ka paagɛ saazuum bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, ya wʋn sige mɛ halɩ ka paagɛ kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa. Se'ere n soe la, tõon-yãlma sɛba tɩ mam tõm ya zẽ'am na daan sãn tõm nɩ na Sodom tẽŋam, a wʋn ken boe nɩ mɛ zĩna.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: «Da lagsrɩ-ya bõntarsõm bĩŋra dũnia wã zuo, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ bõn-mõgla la tẽŋanzẽem wʋn sã'am ba, tɩ nayigba mẽ kẽ'era zũura.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wẽ ẽ, tɩ kʋsebgo vuge wẽ ẽ, tɩ a ka lui, se'ere n soe la, a mẽ la tãmpĩa zuo.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: La nẽra kãn dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm wɔ-kɛgsɩ pʋam. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, dã-paalga la sãn kɛlla, ba wʋn botɩ wɔɔsɩ la tãsɛ mɛ, tɩ dãam na ka'agɛ, tɩ wɔɔsɩ la mẽ sã'am. La masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm na wɔ-paalsɩ pʋam, bɛla tɩ ba wuu kãn sã'am.».

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ saazuum la tẽŋa n wʋn yuuge se'em, sɛla sɛla kãn yese Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la pʋam baa fẽfẽe, halɩ tɩ sɛla woo wa ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pɩɩna tarɩ yogro mɛ, tɩ niinto tara tʋgrɔ, la Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka tarɩ zẽ'a wʋn gã'arɛ vo'ose.».

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la nẽr-sõma zozo'e yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ yãma n yẽ sɛla la mɛ, la ba ka yẽ, ba yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ ba wõm sɛla tɩ yãma wõm na mɛ, la ba ka wõm.».

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: La dagɩ yãma mẽŋa n wʋn tɔgɛ, la de la ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la n boe ya pʋam na n wʋn tɔgɛ.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: A ẽŋɛ la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi yele se'em na ẽŋɛ.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ la Galile mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, ka zom tãŋa zuo zẽa.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ sarɩyadia daarɛ, Sodom dõma kãn yẽ toogo paagɛ yãma.».

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Lagsra-ya bõntarsõm bĩŋra Nawẽnnɛ yire, bilam tɩ mõka la tẽŋanzẽem kãn sã'am ba, tɩ nayigba mẽ kãn kẽ zũ.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n wõm mam yetɔga wã, dee ka sakɛ ɩta yetɔga la n yele se'em na, a ãn wʋ yalma n mẽ a yire tẽntõnbĩ'isgo zuo.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Azezi n ken tɔgra la ba bɛla la, tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadãana ayɩla wa'am a zẽ'am, wa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam yele yetɩ: «Mam pɔyʋa n ki lɛɛlɛ wã, wa'am wa dɩkɛ fʋ nu'ugo paglɛ a zuo, tɩ a vo'oge.».

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ ayɩla, baa a sãn ãna pɩka gãnna wuu, dee zãsna nẽrba basɛba tɩ ba da sakɛ, a wʋn dɛna nẽr-pɩka Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam. La sɛka n sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, dee zãsna nẽrba tɩ ba ɩta bɛla, a wʋn dɛna nẽr-kãtɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbia ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, basɛ tɩ m dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ m sɔ dee wa'am wa dɔla fɔ.».

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: «Bɛla, selse-ya sɛka n lobge bõnbuuri la makrɛ la võorɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Sɔbia wʋn botɩ ba yõkɛ a sɔbia kʋ. La sɔdõma mẽ wʋn ẽŋɛ ba kɔma bɛla. La kɔma mẽ wʋn zɛbɛ la ba dɔgrɩba, botɩ ba kʋ ba.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Mam tõntõnna tɩ mam looge n wãna: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ mam nõŋɛ, a paagɛ la mam yɛm. Mam wʋn basɛ tɩ m Sɩa pãŋa bɔna la ẽ, tɩ a mõolɛ sɩra, la sɛla n masɛ mam yɛm bɔ buuri wuu.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e tarɩ kɔɔrnɔ, la fɔɔsɩ, la kobro, la mugro, la bã'adõma basɛba zozo'e wa'am Azezi nẽŋam. Tɩ Azezi botɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa Azezi tɔgɛ mɛ yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n de saazuum la tẽŋa wã Zuudãana, mam pẽgrɩ fɔ, se'ere n soe la, fõn sʋgɛ bõn-bãna wã mɛ tɩ yɛm dõma la bãŋrɩba ka mina, dee pa'alɛ nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔm-pɩgsɩ la.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ya bõntarsõm n boe zẽ'a na, ya pʋtẽ'era mẽ boe la bilam.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wẽ ẽ, tɩ kʋsebgo vuge wẽ ẽ, tɩ a wuu lui saam saam.».

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan isge dɔla ẽ wɛ'ɛsa.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya sãn ka ɩta Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na gãnna lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, ya kãn tã'agɛ kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dɔla mam dee basɛ sɛba n ka tarɩ vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɩ ba laagɛ ba kũm.».

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Sore la nõorʋm tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga la, dee ka bãŋɛ a võorɛ. Tɩ Asũtãana n de tõon-be'ero dãana la wa'am wa yese yetɔga la a sũure pʋam.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nẽrba wuu wʋn sise ya mam yʋ'ʋrɛ la ĩyã. La sɛka n ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: A kãn wẽ'era nõke'ene, la a kãn zɛbra. La nẽra kãn wõm a kõa sorɔɔrɔ zuto.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: La nẽrba la n daan yẽ mugro la n tɔgrɩ, tɩ kobro la yeege, tɩ kɔɔrnɔ na sẽnna, tɩ fɔɔsɩ la yẽta la, la daan di'ige ba mɛ, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma Zuudãana la.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Ẽe, mam Sɔ, la de la fõn boorɩ bɛla la ĩyã.».

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Nini n nẽerɩ peelem bɔ'ɔra ĩyã wʋ fɩtla la. Bɛla, fʋ nini sãn ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu tarɩ peelem mɛ.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: La Azezi n tɔgɛ yetɔga la kɩ'ɩlɩm na, zãsŋɔ la di'ige nẽrba la zozo'e mɛ.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ban daan we'esɩ la, tɩ pɔka ayɩla n luti sɩkẽŋa yʋʋma pia la ayi tɩ la ka gɔ'ɔra, wa'am Azezi poorʋm wa kalʋm a fuugo nõorɛ.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: «Yãma wõm tɩ ba daan yele kʋrʋm dõma yetɩ: ‹Da kʋ nẽra, la nẽra sãn kʋ nẽra, ba di a sarɩya.›.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Azezi daan kẽ la õorŋɔ, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la doose ẽ.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Tãmpĩa zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm kõ-yẽlga la, to'oge ẽ bilam mẽŋa la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Ba sãn nãmsɛ ya tẽŋa ayɩla pʋam, ya zoe sẽŋɛ tẽŋa ayẽma. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ya kãn yãŋɛ kaagɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽnsɩ ba'asɛ dee tɩ mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la leme wa'am.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: A kãn kɔ'ɔgɛ mõ-sɛka n kɔ'ɔgɛ la ba'asɛ. La a kãn kĩŋe fɩtla gõngõŋɔ la n diti fẽfẽ la ba'asɛ, halɩ tɩ la ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ a wʋn botɩ sɩra, la sɛla n masɛ to'oge zẽ'a wuu.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Azezi daan wi a karẽnbiisi la mɛ yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ wã ninbãalga n tarɩ mam, se'ere n soe la, la de la dabsa atã n wãna tɩ ba boe mam zẽ'am, dee ka tara dia wʋn di. La mam ka boorɩ tɩ m basɛ ba tɩ ba kule la kɔm, ba tã wʋn ka vibe sore mɛ.».

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: «Mam Sɔ dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ la mam nu'usum. La nẽra ka mi mam n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la n de sɛka, sãn dagna mam Sɔ la, la nẽra mẽ ka mi mam Sɔ la n de se'em, sãn dagna mam n de a Dayʋa la, la sɛba tɩ mam wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽ na.».

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: La fʋ nini sãn ka ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu boe la lika pʋam. Peelem sɛka n wʋn nẽegɛ fʋ pʋam na sãn dɛna lika, la de la lika mẽŋa mẽŋa.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a zãsŋɔ la tarɩ la pãŋa, la ka ãn wʋ ba lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n zãsnɩ se'em na.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a daan tẽ'esrɩ a sũurum yetɩ: «Mam sãn kalʋm a fuugo mã'a, mam wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo sũure n isge la a sɔbia, la masɛ tɩ ba di a sarɩya. La sɛka n yele a sɔbia yetɩ: ‹Yalma›, la masɛ tɩ sarɩyaditba nẽŋadõma di a sarɩya. La sɛka n yele a sɔbia yetɩ: ‹Fʋ ka tarɩ võorɛ›, ẽŋa dãana masɛ mɛ tɩ a kẽ bug-tẽŋa.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ wa'am wa vugra mõgrɛ la zuo. Tɩ ko-mĩ'isi botɩ õorŋɔ la zɩgra la a mise. La Azezi daan gĩsri mɛ.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: La ban ka kẽ a sũure pʋam na ĩyã, a sakɛ la wakatɛ fẽe. La toogo bɩɩ nãmsgɔ sãn paagɛ ẽ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la ĩyã, a basrɩ mɛ tɔtɔ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Karẽnbia ka gãnnɩ a karẽnsãama, la tõntõnna mẽ ka gãnnɩ a zuudãana.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Tɩ buuri la wuu wʋn te'ege ẽ.».

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn yẽ dia la bɛ, zẽ'e-ẽna n de mõom wã, bɔ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ wã wuu tɩ la seke ba?».

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: «Wa'am-ya mam zẽ'am, yãma sɛba wuu n tʋ ze-tɩbsa targɛ la, tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ya vo'osgo.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Nẽra ka boe n tã wʋn sakɛ zuudãandõma bayi, a wʋn sise ayɩla mɛ, dee nõŋɛ ayɩla, bɩɩ a wʋn nanna ayɩla la mɛ, dee pɔ'ɔra ayɩla la. Bɛla, yãma ka tã wʋn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de ya Zuudãana, dee botɩ bõntarsõm mẽ dɛna ya zuudãana.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Tɩ Azezi gelge yẽ ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «M bia, keŋe fʋ sũure! Fõn bɔ mam sɩra la n basɛ tɩ fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.» Tɩ pɔka la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm bilam mẽŋa.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la paagɛ nẽegɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fãagɛ tɔ, tõma yetɩ ki mɛ.».

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: La karẽnbia sãn wa ãna wʋ a karẽnsãama la, la tõntõnna sãn wa ãna wʋ a zuudãana la, la seke mɛ. Ban wi'iri yidãana la tɩ Abelzebuli n de Asũtãana la, ba wʋn tʋʋra a yire la dõma gãnna la bɛla.».

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ la borborɩ balɛ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Bayopɔɩ, la zũn-pɩgsɩ fẽe.».

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: La gõ'osɩ la zẽ'a tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga la, tɩ a yɛm yɔɔra la dũnia wã yɛla, tɩ bõntarsõm yɛla pã'asra ẽ, botɩ yetɔga la ka tãna wʋn wɔm biɛ a sũure pʋam.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Dɩkɛ-ya mam sɔgrɩga sɔgɛ ya mẽŋa, zãsʋm sɛla tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ ya la, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ vo'osgo. Se'ere n soe la, mam de la sũ-bʋgsʋm dãana, la sik-m-mẽŋa dãana.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Bɛla, fʋ sãn tarɩ fʋ bɔ'a wa'am kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zẽ'a tɩ fʋ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, sãn tẽegɛ tɩ fõn tarɩ la yelle la fʋ sɔbia.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ nẽra ayɩla tɩ kʋlkã'arga dolɩ ẽ, tɩ a dɛna fʋa la muko wa'am Azezi zẽ'am, tɩ Azezi botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, tɔgra dee yẽta.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam yele ya yetɩ, ya da fabla ya vɔm yelle, yãma n wʋn di sɛla, bɩɩ ya wʋn yũ sɛla, la ya da fabla ya ĩyã yelle, ya wʋn yɛ sɛla. Vɔm ka gãnnɩ dia? Bɩɩ ĩyã ka gãnnɩ futo?.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ Zifdõma nẽŋadãana la yire yẽ wɩ-pɛɛbrɩba la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n bɔgrɩ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya zɔta dabeem, ya ka bɔ mam sɩra zo'oge.» Azezi yele bɛla, dee isge tãsɛ kʋsebgo, la ko'om na mɛ, tɩ ba ãna sĩm.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: «Bɛla, da zɔta-ya nẽrsaalba, tɩ bõn-sɛka woo n lile, wʋn lilge. La bõn-sɛka woo n sʋgɛ, wʋn puke.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ, ba zĩ'ire tẽŋa.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: La zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, wõm a võorɛ, wɔnna biɛ. Tɩ basɛba wɔm kɔbga kɔbga, tɩ basɛba pisyoob-yoobɩ, tɩ basɛba pitã-tã.».

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Mam sɔgrɩga la fakɛ mɛ, la mam zeero la ka tɩbgɛ.».

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: fʋ basɛ fʋ bɔ'a la kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne la nẽŋam, dee dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka malgɛ fʋ yelle la, la fʋ sɔbia la, dee yãŋa leme wa'am wa dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ'a la bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Tɩ la di'ige nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã kãn dɛna naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa la?».

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya bõn-ẽgla, ba ka bʋtɩ, ba mẽ ka lagsrɩ bõnkɔɔla ɩta bã pʋam. La ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔrɩ ba dia mɛ. Yãma ka tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna bõn-ẽgla halɩ zozo'e?.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yese-ya, tɩ pugla la ka ki, a gĩsri mɛ.» Tɩ nẽrba la la'ara ẽ.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Tɩ ba wuu palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Bẽm nẽra n de ẽna tɩ kʋsebgo, la ko'om sakra a nõorɛ?».

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Mam n yele ya se'em lika pʋam wã, tɔgɛ-ya ẽ peelem pʋam. La sɛla tɩ mam waasʋm ya la, zom-ya deto zuto mõolɛ ẽ.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Dee dɩkɛ borborɩ bayopɔɩ la, la zũma la pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ a karẽnbiisi la, tɩ ba to'oge pʋɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Azezi le makɛ la makrɛ ayɩla bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ bʋraa n bʋrɛ si sõma a va'am.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Fʋ sãn tara yelle la nẽra, tɩ a tara fʋ wɛ'ɛsa sarɩyadia zẽ'am, malgɛ fʋ yelle la tɔtɔ, yãma n nãn boe sore la, tɩ a da dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ sarɩyadita, tɩ a tɔgɛ fʋ yetɔga la dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ gu'ura, tɩ a kẽ'esɛ fɔ yʋ'a deem.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma wõm bɛla, yele yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna tarɩ la Abelzebuli n de kʋlkã'arsɩ naba la pãŋa digra kʋlkã'arsɩ la.».

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ãne n boe yãma pʋam n tã wʋn pa'asɛ a vɔm woglʋm fẽfẽe ẽn fablɩ la ĩyã?.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Ba daan dooge mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba paagɛ la Gadara dõma tẽŋa pʋam, tɩ bʋraasɩ bayi tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ba ze'ele yɔɔrɔ zẽ'a sẽnna tʋ'ʋsra ẽ. Ba daan de la zɔl-wẽensɩ, tɩ nẽrba zɔta ka sakra dɔla bilam tɔlla.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Da zɔta-ya sɛba n kʋʋrɩ ĩyã, dee ka tãna wʋn kʋ sɩa la. Zɔta-ya Nawẽnnɛ n tã wʋn botɩ ya ĩyã, la ya sɩa sã'am bug-tẽŋa la.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Tɩ ba wuu di tɩgɛ, dee tɩ ba vaagɛ bõn-gito n deege pɩrɛ pɩtɔ bayopɔɩ.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ, tɩ nẽrba gã gĩsra, tɩ a bɛ tarɩ kanarsɩ sẽŋɛ ka bʋrɛ lagɩm mĩ.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: La ẽn botɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yese la, a kẽ la deem gʋrɛ pugla la nu'ugo, tɩ a isge.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Mam yetɩ fʋ la asɩra, tɩ fʋ kãn yẽ sore yese yʋ'a deem na, halɩ tɩ fʋ wʋn wa yɔ fʋ sãnnɛ na wuu ba'asɛ.».

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, yele ba yetɩ: «So'olʋm sɛka woo nẽrba n welge wẽrgɛ zɛbra la taaba, so'olʋm na wʋn sã'am mɛ. La tẽn-sɛka woo, bɩɩ yi-sɛka woo nẽrba n welge zɛbra la taaba, a wʋn wõrgɛ mɛ.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: La bẽm tɩ ya fabla futo yelle? Bɩsɛ-ya mõ-puuro n ãn sõŋa se'em, ba ka tõnnɩ, ba mẽ ka wʋgrɩ futo.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Tɩ ba kaasra tagursi yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, bẽm boe tõma la fõn tẽŋasʋka? Fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ nãmsɛ tõma mɛ dee tɩ wakatɛ la nãn ka paagɛ?».

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Ba ka koosrɩ mõom niinto bayi la lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla mã'a? La baa niila ayɩla kãn ki, tɩ la sãn dagna ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n sakɛ.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Sɛba n daan di dia la, bʋraasɩ de la tʋsa anaasɩ, tɩ pɔgsɩ la kɔma kãllɛ ka pa'asɛ.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Tɩ si la yese bɩ waagɛ. Tɩ kanarsɩ la mẽ waagɛ, tɩ ba yẽ tɩ ba dagɩ si.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Tɩ yel-ẽna saagɛ tẽŋa la wuu.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: «Yãma wõm tɩ ba daan yetɩ: ‹Da gã'arɛ la fʋ tadãana pɔga.›.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: La Asũtãana sãn digra Asũtãana, a welge mɛ zɛbra la a mẽŋa. Bɛla, a so'olʋm na kãn tã'agɛ ze'ele.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, baa naba Asalomõ na'am n yʋʋn tarɩ pãŋa la, a yʋʋn ka tã'agɛ yɛ fuugo n ãn sõŋa wʋ mõ-puugo ayɩla.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: La kurkurdõma n daan zo'oge lɛm bilam ɔbra.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: La baa yãma zuugo zomto, Nawẽnnɛ mi ba wuu kãllɛ mɛ.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi basɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tɩ ba kule mɛ, dee kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Magdala.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Tɩ tõntõnɩba la sẽŋɛ va'am na dãana zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, dagɩ si sõma tɩ fõn daan bʋrɛ fʋ va'am na? La bẽm tɩ kanarsɩ bɔna mĩ zozo'e?›.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Azezi daan yese zẽ'e-ẽŋa mɛ fõrgra, tɩ fɔɔsɩ bayi dɔla ẽ tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe tɩ ninbãalga!».

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ, sɛka n bɩsɛ pɔka tɩ a yɛm bɔɔra ẽ, a põn gã'arɛ la ẽ mɛ a sũure pʋam.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Yãma n yetɩ mam digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la Abelzebuli pãŋa la, yãma nẽrba tarɩ la ãne pãŋa digra kʋlkã'arsɩ? Bɛla, ya nẽrba la mẽŋa n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ sãn botɩ mõorɔ n boe zĩna dee tɩ ba wʋn yõ ba bugum beere ãna sõŋa bɛla, a kãn bɩsɛ yãma yelle gãnna bɛla? Bẽm tɩ yãma ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra zo'oge?.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la sose Azezi yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ dige to tɩ tɩ yese, fʋ basɛ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la.».

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, da zɔta-ya dabeem, yãma tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna niinto zozo'e mɛ.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Tɩ a yele yetɩ: ‹La de la mam bɛ n ẽŋɛ bɛla.› Tɩ tõntõnɩba la soke ẽ yetɩ: ‹Fõn boorɩ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ ka võ kanarsɩ la mɛ bɩɩ?›.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ kẽ yire, tɩ fɔɔsɩ la paagɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma bɔ sɩra tɩ mam wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ yan boorɩ se'em na?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe! Zuudãana.».

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Fʋ zʋʋgɔ nifo sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, fʋ kɩrgɛ ẽ lobe basɛ zãazãarɛ, se'ere n soe la, fʋ ĩyã sɛla ayɩla sãn ka bɔna mĩ sõɩ ba wʋn lobe fʋ ĩyã wuu bug-tẽŋa.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Da fabla-ya yɛta tɩ: ‹Bẽm tɩ wʋn di?› bɩɩ ‹tɩ wʋn yũ la bẽm?› bɩɩ ‹tɩ wʋn yɛ la bẽm?›.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya.» Tɩ ba yese sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la. Tɩ kurkurdõma la ze'ele tãŋa zuo zoe sige ka lui mõgrɛ la pʋam ki.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n wʋn ze'ele nẽrba nẽŋam yetɩ ẽŋa de la mam nẽra, mam mẽ wʋn ze'ele mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nẽŋam yetɩ a de la mam nẽra.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa tɩ mam tarɩ digra kʋlkã'arsɩ, bɛla pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n wa'am wa dita na'am ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ayɛɩ, ya sãn võ kanarsɩ la, ya wʋn po võ la si.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Tɩ a kalʋm ba nini dee yetɩ: «La wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ yan bɔ sɩra se'em na.».

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: La fʋ zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, fʋ wike ẽ lobe basɛ zãazãarɛ, se'ere n soe la, fʋ ĩyã sɛla ayɩla sãn ka bɔna mĩ sõɩ ban wʋn lobe fʋ ĩyã wuu bug-tẽŋa.».

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: La de la nẽr-sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n eerɩ bõn-bãma wuu. La yãma Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mi tɩ yãma boorɩ bõn-bãma wuu mɛ.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Tɩ sɛba n gu'uri kurkurdõma la zoe sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam ka yele lan ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu, la bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la daan dolɩ la.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n sɩ'ɩsɛ nẽrba nẽŋam yetɩ ẽŋa ka mi mam, mam mẽ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nẽŋam tɩ mam ka mi ẽ.».

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Nẽra ka tã wʋn kẽ pãŋa dãana yire vaagɛ a lɔgrɔ, sãn dagna tɩ a dẽŋɛ lʋ pãŋa dãana la mɛ dee yãŋa vaagɛ a lɔgrɔ la.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ ba wuu bɩ ka paagɛ bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ wakatɛ, tɩ mam wʋn yele sɛba n lagsrɩ bõnkɔɔla la tɩ ba dẽŋɛ võ kanarsɩ la, bobe ba yõ bugum, dee yãŋa lagsɛ si la dɩgɛ m bãarɛ pʋam.› ».

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Tɩ ba nini na pike yẽta. Tɩ a kã'an ba yetɩ: «Da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽra bãŋɛ.».

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: «Ba yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-sɛka n lebse a pɔga, a bɔ ẽ gõŋɔ n pa'alɛ tɩ a lebse ẽ mɛ.›.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Dẽŋɛ-ya ɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm, la sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya bõn-bãma wuu pa'asɛ.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la dõma wuu yese tʋ'ʋsra Azezi, la ban yẽ ẽ na, ba sose ẽ yetɩ, a fõrgɛ dee basɛ ba tẽŋa.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: «Da tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ mam tarɩ la nõ-yẽnnɛ wa'am dũnia zuo. Mam ka wa'am tɩ m botɩ nẽrba dɛna nõorɛ ayɩla dũnia zuo, la de la welgre tɩ mam tarɩ wa'am.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Sɛka n ka boe la mam, sisri mam mɛ. La sɛka n ka lagɩm na mam lagsra, a wɩrgrɩ mɛ.

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Azezi le makɛ la makrɛ ayɩla bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ sɔ'ɔrɔ biire tɩ bʋraa bʋrɛ a va'am.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: La ban fõrgɛ la, ba mõolɛ bõn-ẽŋa mɛ tẽŋa la zẽ'a woo.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, nẽr-sɛka woo n lebse a pɔga, tɩ la sãn dagna tɩ a gã la bʋraasɩ, a wʋn botɩ pɔka la sã'am mɛ, a sãn ele sɩra ayẽma. La nẽr-sɛka n di pɔg-sɛka tɩ ba lebse la, ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ nẽra n gã'arɛ la a tadãana pɔga.».

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Da fabla-ya beere yelle, tɩ beere ya wʋn bãŋɛ ya wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, daarɛ woo n bɩsrɩ a mẽŋa yelle.».

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam wa'am tɩ m welge la dayɔɔsɩ la ba sɔdõma, la pugunto la ba madõma, la dayɔɔsɩ pɔgba la ba sɩrba madõma.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam yele ya yetɩ, tõon-be'ero wuu, la pɔ'ɔrɛ yetɔga wuu tɩ nẽrsaalba ɩtɩ la, Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn basɛ ba taalɛ. La sɛka n tɔgɛ pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, a kãn basɛ ẽ taalɛ.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: A ãna pɩka pɩka gãnna bõnbuuri la wuu biɛ, la ba sãn bʋrɛ ẽ, a bɩtɩ mɛ gãnna zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu, ãna wʋ tɩa la, tɩ niinto sẽna tɛ'ɛra ba tʋgrɔ a wila zuo.».

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: «Ya ken wõm tɩ ba yʋʋn yele kʋrʋm dõma yetɩ: ‹Da viige ya pɔrɛ. Fʋ sãn bĩŋe nõorɛ pɔɩ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ bɛla.›.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Ban daan yesrɩ la, tɩ ba tarɩ nẽra tɩ kʋlkã'arga dolɩ ẽ, tɩ a dɛna muko, wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Tɩ nẽra mẽŋa yir-dõma wʋn dɛna a bɛ'ɛba.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n tɔgrɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la tɔg-be'ero, a wʋn basɛ ẽ taalɛ. La sɛka n tɔgrɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɔg-be'ero, Nawẽnnɛ kãn malɩn basɛ ẽ taalɛ dũnia ẽna wã pʋam, la dũni-sɛka n sẽm na pʋam.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: La a le makɛ la makrɛ ayɩla bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ dãbɩllɛ tɩ pɔka dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ borborɩ zom la-kãra batã pʋam wẽ, tɩ dãbɩllɛ la botɩ ba wuu uke.».

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Tɩ Azezi dige kʋlkã'arga la, tɩ a yese. Tɩ muko la tɔgra. Tɩ la di'ige nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Nẽra nẽra nãn ka yẽ wãna buuri Ɩsrayɛlɩ pʋam.».

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ, ya põn da pɔɩ. Da pɔɩ saazuum, bẽm ĩyã, a de la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma gãnna mam, a ka masɛ tɩ a dɛna mam nẽra. La nẽr-sɛka woo n nõŋɛ a dayʋa bɩɩ a pɔyʋa gãnna mam, a ka masɛ tɩ a dɛna mam nẽra.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Ya sãn yetɩ, tɩa ãn sõŋa, ya yetɩ a biɛ ãn sõŋa. Bɩɩ ya sãn yetɩ, tɩa de tɩ-be'ego, ya yetɩ a biɛ mẽ de la biɛ be'ero. Se'ere n soe la, tɩa biɛ n botɩ ba bãŋra ẽn de tɩ-sɛka.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Bɛla wuu Azezi magsrɩ la magsa tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, a ka tɔgɛ yetɔga bɔ ba, tɩ la sãn dagna magsa.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: La Farisi dõma daan yetɩ: «La de la kʋlkã'arsɩ naba n bɔ ẽ pãŋa, tɩ a tara digra kʋlkã'arsɩ la.».

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: Da pɔɩ tẽŋa, bẽm ĩyã, a de la a nãma dɔglgɔ zẽ'a. Da pɔɩ Zerizalɛm, bẽm ĩyã, a de la Nawẽnnɛ n de na-kãtɛ la tẽŋa.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n ka sakɛ nãmsgɔ wʋ nẽra n zɛɛrɩ a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam, a ka masɛ tɩ a dɛna mam nẽra.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Yãma ãn wʋ kãmpẽesɩ buuri, yãma n de nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ wãna wã, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɔgɛ tɔg-sõma? Se'ere n soe la, sɛla n zo'oge sũure pʋam na tɩ nõorɛ tɔgrɩ.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa n yʋʋn gʋlsɛ se'em na n ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Mam wʋn makɛ la magsa tɔgɛ bɔ ba, mam wʋn pa'alɛ yel-sɛba n sʋgɛ halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã.».

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Azezi daan kaarɩ la tẽn-kãra la tẽn-pɩgsɩ wuu, zãsna nẽrba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga, dee botɩ bã'adõma, la kobro buuri wuu yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Da pɔɩ fʋ zuugo, bẽm ĩyã, fʋ ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ fʋ zomto baa ayɩla lebge pɛɛlga bɩɩ sabga.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n basɛ a vɔm mam ĩyã, a wʋn yẽ ẽ.».

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Nẽr-sõŋɔ tɔgrɩ la bõn-sõma tɩ a tarɩ a sũure pʋam. La nẽr-be'ego tɔgrɩ la bõn-be'ero tɩ a tarɩ a sũure pʋam.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Azezi daan basɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mɛ, dee kule yire. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pa'alɛ tɔ kanarsɩ la makrɛ võorɛ.».

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: La Azezi n yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, ba ninbãalga n yõkɛ ẽ. Se'ere n soe la, ba targɛ mɛ bãlɛ, ãna wʋ piisi n ka tarɩ dũnsĩina.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya ‹ẽe› dɛna ‹ẽe›, tɩ ya ‹ayɛɩ› dɛna ‹ayɛɩ›. La sãn gãŋɛ bɛla, sɛla n pa'asɛ la de la be'em dãana bõnɔ.».

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge ya, a to'oge la mam. La nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge mam, a to'oge la sɛka n tõm mam na.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ sarɩyadia daarɛ, nẽrsaalba wʋn ka tɔgɛ yetɔg-yaalsɩ tɩ ba tɔgɛ la wuu yelle Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Sɛka n bʋrɛ si sõma la, de la mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Va'am na bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ zo'oge mɛ, la tõntõnɩba n wʋn lagsɛ bõnkɔɔla la pɔ'ɔrɩ mɛ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: «Ya wõm tɩ ba daan yetɩ: ‹Nẽra sãn pusge a tadãana nifo, ba mẽ pusge a nifo, nẽra sãn vike a tadãana yẽnnɛ, ba mẽ vike a yẽnnɛ.›.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ẽn de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la ĩyã, a wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa yɔɔrɔ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge sɩra dãana ẽn de sɩra dãana la ĩyã, a wʋn to'oge sɩra dãana yɔɔrɔ.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: La de la fʋ yetɔga pʋam tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ bʋʋrɔ, la, la de la fʋ yetɔga pʋam tɩ fʋ wʋn koŋe bʋʋrɔ.».

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: La va'am na de la dũnia, tɩ si sõma la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm nẽrba. Tɩ kanarsɩ la dɛna Asũtãana nẽrba.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, sose-ya va'am na dãana tɩ a tõm tõntõnɩba pa'asɛ tɩ ba lagsɛ bõnkɔɔla la.».

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ya be'em, ya da ẽŋɛ be'em lebse. La nẽra sãn wẽ fʋ zʋʋgɔ pɛka, fʋ basɛ tɩ a wẽ fʋ gɔbga pɛka pa'asɛ.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ mam nẽrba wã ayɩla ko-mã'asga tɩ a yũ, ẽn de mam karẽnbia la ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ a kãn koŋe yɔɔrɔ.».

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la, tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma boorɩ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ la bõn-yãlma n pa'alɛ tɩ fõn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɩ tɩ yẽ.».

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Tɩ bɛ la n bʋrɛ kanarsɩ la dɛna Asũtãana. Tɩ bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ wakatɛ la dɛna dũnia ba'asgɔ. Tɩ sɛba n lagsrɩ bõnkɔɔla la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: La nẽra sãn sãm fɔ tɩ a to'oge fʋ fu-pɩka, fʋ basɛ tɩ a to'oge fʋ fu-kãtɛ pa'asɛ.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba n de nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ, dee ka dɔla Nawẽnnɛ la sɩra la, boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ la bõn-yãlma n wʋn pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, la ba kãn yẽ bõn-yãlma, sãn dagna bõn-yãlm-sɛka n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azonaasɩ la.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Ban lagsɛ kanarsɩ la lobe basɛ bugum pʋam se'em na, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la bɛla mẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: La nẽra sãn pẽrgɛ fɔ tɩ fʋ kɔlgɛ ẽ kilomẽtrɩ ayɩla, fʋ kɔlgɛ ẽ kilomẽtrɩ bayi.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn tõm na a malɛkadõma tɩ ba lagsɛ sɛba n pã'asɛ nẽrba tɩ ba basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, la tõon-be'ero dõma wuu, yese ba Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Wʋ Azonaasɩ n yʋʋn boe zũn-kãtɛ la pʋʋrʋm dabsa atã wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ la, bɛla Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn bɔna yɔɔgɔ pʋam na dabsa atã, wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Nẽra sãn sose fʋ sɛla, fʋ bɔ ẽ. La nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a peŋe sɛla fʋ zẽ'am, da mõŋɛ ẽ.».

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, Niniivi dõma wʋn isge zɛrgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma mɛ ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, Azonaasɩ n yʋʋn mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ ba la, ba yʋʋn teege yɛm mɛ basɛ tõon-be'ero. La nẽra n gãnnɩ Azonaasɩ n boe kalam, dee tɩ yãma ka teege yɛm.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: lobe ba basɛ bugum pʋam. Zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn kɛlla, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: «Ya wõm tɩ ba daan yetɩ: ‹Fʋ nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana, dee sise fʋ bɛ.›.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: La sarɩyadia daarɛ, na-pɔka la n yʋʋn yese wẽntulle gɔbga bɔba la, wʋn isge mɛ zɛrgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, a daan ze'ele la zẽ'a n zãagɛ wa'am wa selse naba Asalomõ yɛm yetɔga. La nẽra n gãnnɩ Asalomõ n boe kalam, dee tɩ yãma ka sɛlsra ẽ.».

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ wʋn yẽgra ba Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa la. Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, ya nõŋɛ ya bɛ'ɛba, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n nãmsrɩ ya la.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga sãn yese dee basɛ nẽra, a sẽŋɛ la pɔgẽntɩɩsɩ pʋam ka ɛɛra zẽ'a tɩ a vo'ose, la a sãn ka yẽ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ tɩ ba dɩkɛ sʋgɛ va'am pʋam, tɩ nẽra yẽ ẽ. Tɩ a sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm, tɩ a le sʋgɛ ẽ pa'asɛ, dee sẽŋɛ ka koose ẽn tarɩ sɛla wuu, sẽŋɛ ka da va'am na.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya wʋn dɛna ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum na kɔma, se'ere n soe la, ẽŋa n botɩ wʋntɛɛŋa yẽkra bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sõma la nẽr-be'ero, dee botɩ saaga niira bɔ'ɔra sɛba ɩrgɔ n masɛ, la sɛba ɩrgɔ n ka masɛ.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm le ãna wʋ lɛɛba n eerɩ kug-sõma n de tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ tɩ a da.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: a yetɩ mɛ tɩ: ‹Mam wʋn lebe la m yi-sɛka tɩ m yese dee basɛ la pʋam.› La a sãn lebe ka paagɛ yẽ tɩ deego la de la voore, pɩɩsɛ malgɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Yãma sãn nõŋɛ sɛba n nõŋɛ ya la mã'a, bẽm yɔɔrɔ tɩ ya wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am? Lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba mẽ ɩtɩ la bɛla.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: La a yẽ la ayɩla n ba ãna sõŋa tɩ a ligri zo'oge, tɩ a lebe kule ka koose ẽn tarɩ sɛla wuu, sẽŋɛ ka da ẽ.».

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: a wʋn lebe mɛ ka tarɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ bayopɔɩ n de wẽensɩ gãnna ẽŋa, wa'am wa kẽ bɔna mĩ. La nẽra la toogo wʋn gãnna kʋrʋm toogo la. La mẽ wʋn dɛna la bɛla bɔ zamãan-ẽna wã nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ la.».

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: La yãma sãn pʋ'ʋsra la ya sɔbiisi mã'a, bẽm tɩ yãma ɩtɩ gãnna ya taaba la? Nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na mẽ ɩtɩ la bɛla.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ken ãna wʋ yʋka tɩ ba lobge ko-kãtɛ pʋam yõgɛ zũma buuri wuu.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Bɛla, dɛna-ya nẽrba tɩ ya ɩrgɔ wuu wʋn masɛ, wʋ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum ɩrgɔ wuu n masɛ se'em na.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Azezi daan ken tɔgra la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ a ma, la a yɩbsɩ daan zɛ yẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ ba tɔgɛ la ẽ.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: tɩ yʋka la pɩrɛ. Tɩ ba ve'ege ẽ dooge doone, gãsɛ zũn-sɛba n ãn sõŋa ẽŋɛ pɩtɔ pʋam, dee lobe sɛba n ka ãn sõŋa la basɛ.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: La wʋn ãna la bɛla dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ. Malɛkadõma n wʋn wa'am wa gãsɛ nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ, nẽr-sõma la pʋam.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Tɩ nẽra ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ ma, la fʋ yɩbsɩ n wãna n ze yẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ ba tɔgɛ la fɔ.».

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: lobe ba basɛ bugum pʋam, zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ ba wʋn kɛlla, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.».

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele sɛka n tɔgrɩ la ẽ na yetɩ: «Ãne n de mam ma, la ãndõma n de mam yɩbsɩ?».

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Azezi tɔgɛ bɛla, dee soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma wõm bɛla wuu võorɛ?» Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe.».

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Dee tẽegɛ a nu'ugo pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Mam ma, la mam yɩbsɩ n wãna.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n de lɔɔ karẽnsãama dee zãsʋm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle pa'asɛ, ãn wʋ yidãana n loorɩ bõn-kɛgsɩ, la bõn-paalsɩ a bõntarsõm pʋam bɔ'ɔra nẽrba.».

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum n boorɩ se'em, ẽŋa n de mam yɩbga, la mam tã, la mam ma.».

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Azezi daan magsɛ magsa la kɩ'ɩlɩm, dee fõrgɛ bilam sẽŋɛ la a mẽŋa tẽŋa.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: La ẽn paagɛ a mẽŋa tẽŋa la, a kẽ la ba wẽndeego pʋam zãsna nẽrba, tɩ a zãsŋɔ la di'ige nẽrba la, tɩ ba yetɩ: «A yẽ yɛm ẽna la bɛ? A ẽŋɛ la wãne tõnna tõon-yãlma wã?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Dagɩ ẽna n de dɔ-wãta la dayʋa la? Dagɩ ẽna ma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Amaari la, tɩ a yɩbsɩ dɛna Azakɩ, la Azozɛfʋ, la Asimõ, la Azudi la?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Dagɩ a tãpa wuu n boe tɩ zẽ'am wã? A yẽ bõn-bãma wuu la bɛ?».

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Bɛla tɩ ba daan ka bɔ ẽ sɩra. Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa yẽtɩ pẽka mɛ, sãn dagna a mẽŋa tẽŋa, la a yir-dõma tẽŋasʋka mã'a tɩ a ka yẽtɩ pẽka.».

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: A daan ka tõm tõon-yãlma zozo'e bilam, ban ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la daan tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ la weem tɩ Asũtãana makɛ ẽ.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Azezi daan lʋ la nõorɛ dabsa pinaasɩ, wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ. Tɩ a kɔm dõnna.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana lɛm ẽ, yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ botɩ kuga wã lebge dia.».

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nẽrsaala kãn vɔa dia mã'a ĩyã, la de la yetɔg-sɛba wuu n yesrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorʋm ĩyã.› ».

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm, ka zom Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zuo ze'ele.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ ẽkɛ bisge tẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a malɛkadõma kɔkɛ fʋ la ba nu'usi, tɩ fʋ kãn wẽ fʋ nã'arɛ la kugre.› ».

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Da makɛ fʋ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ.› ».

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana le tarɩ Azezi zom tãŋa n ba wɔgɛ zuo, ka pa'alɛ ẽ dũnia so'olʋm wuu la a na'am pãŋa.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asɛ mam, mam wʋn bɔ fʋ bãna wã wuu.».

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ fõrgɛ, Asũtãana! La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nã'asra Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, dee sakra ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a.› ».

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana basɛ Azezi dee fõrgɛ. Tɩ malɛkadõma wa'am wa sõŋra ẽ.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Tɩ Azezi wõm tɩ ba yõkɛ Azã yʋ yʋ'a deego pʋam, tɩ a lebe Galile so'olʋm.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: La a daan basɛ Nazarɛtɩ tẽŋa, dee sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era la Kapɛrnaʋm n lɛm Galile mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, Zabulõ la Nɛftali so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ:.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: «Zabulõ la Nɛftali tẽnsɩ n tɔglɛ ko-kãtɛ bɔba, Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba, Galile tẽnsɩ tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ boe a pʋam na.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: nẽr-sɛba n boe lika pʋam na yẽ peelem zozo'e. La nẽr-sɛba n zĩ kũm mã'asʋm pʋam na, peelem nẽegɛ mɛ bɔ ba.».

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Bɛla wẽnnɛ tɩ Azezi daan pɔsɛ mõola yetɩ: «Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɛm mɛ.».

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Azezi daan eerɩ la Galile mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, yẽ bʋraasɩ bayi, Asimõ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la a yɩbga A'ãndre, tɩ ba lobrɩ yʋka mõgrɛ la pʋam, ba daan de la zũn-yõgrɩba.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Dɔla-ya mam, tɩ mam wʋn basɛ tɩ ya ɛɛra nẽrba tɩ ba dɔla mam wʋ yãma n yõgrɩ zũma se'em na.».

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Tɩ ba basɛ ba yʋgsɩ la bilam mẽŋa, dee dɔla ẽ.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽŋa le yẽ bʋraasɩ bayi n de Azebede kɔma, Azakɩ la a yɩbga Azã. Tɩ bãma, la ba sɔ Azebede bɔna õorŋɔ pʋam malgra ba yʋgsɩ. Tɩ Azezi wi ba.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: tɩ ba basɛ õorŋɔ la, la ba sɔ la tɔtɔ, dee dɔla ẽ.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Azezi daan kaarɩ la Galile so'olʋm wuu zãsna nẽrba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga dee botɩ bã'adõma, la kobro wuu n boe nẽrba la pʋam yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ daan yese Siiri so'olʋm wuu, tɩ ba tara bã'adõma wuu tɩ bã'asɩ buuri to'oto'ore tarɩ ba, nẽrba ĩisi n dõnnɩ tɩ ba nãmsra, la kʋlkã'arsɩ n dolɩ sɛba, la nẽrba n luti kinki'ire, la kɔɔrnɔ wa'am a zẽ'am, tɩ a botɩ ba wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e ze'ele Galile, la Dekapɔlɩ, la Zerizalɛm, la Zude, la tẽnsɩ n boe Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba doose ẽ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Dabsa ayoobɩ n daan tole, tɩ Azezi botɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la a yɩbga Azã doose ẽ sẽŋɛ kẽnkɛrŋa ka zom tãn-kãtɛ zuo.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Tɩ a nẽŋa teege ba nẽŋam, yẽgra wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa la. Tɩ a futo pelge halɩ wʋ peelem na.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Tɩ Amoyisi la A'eli puke ba zẽ'am tɔgra la Azezi.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, tõma n boe kalam wã, la ãn sõŋa mɛ. Fʋ sãn bɔɔra, mam wʋn tĩ põnsɩ batã, bɔ fõn ayɩla, bɔ Amoyisi ayɩla, bɔ A'eli ayɩla.».

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: La ẽn nãn ken bɔna mĩ tɔgra la, tɩ sawatɛ n yẽgrɩ wa'am wa lile ba. Tɩ kõa ze'ele sawatɛ la pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu, a paagɛ la mam yɛm. Selse-ya a yetɔga.».

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: La a karẽnbiisi la n wõm bɛla la, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba zozo'e, tɩ ba lui dɩkɛ nẽnsɩ võglɛ tẽŋa.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Tɩ Azezi lɛm kalʋm ba yetɩ: «Isge-ya, da zoe-ya dabeem.».

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Tɩ ba gorge bɩsɛ, ka le yẽ nẽra, sãn dagna Azezi mã'a.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: La ban daan ze'ele tãŋa la zuo sigra la, Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ: «Da yele-ya nẽra sɛla tɩ ya yẽ wã, sãn dagna tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa ki mɛ dee vo'oge.».

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Wãne tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ A'eli wʋn dẽŋɛ Krisi wa'am?».

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «La de la sɩra tɩ A'eli n wʋn dẽŋɛ wa'am wa le malgɛ sɛla woo.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ A'eli põn wa'am mɛ, tɩ ba ka bãŋɛ ẽ, dee ẽŋɛ ẽ ban boorɩ se'em. Ba mẽ wʋn nãmsɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la bɛla.».

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yãŋa bãŋɛ tɩ la de la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na yelle tɩ a tɔgɛ.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Ban daan lebe ka paara nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la zẽ'am na, tɩ bʋraa wa'am wa yigle Azezi nẽŋam.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, zoe m dayʋa ninbãalga, a luti la kinki'ire tɩ la nãmsra ẽ zozo'e. La sãn ẽn girge ẽ, a luti la bugum pʋam, bɩɩ ko'om pʋam.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam tarɩ ẽ wa'am fʋ karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am, la ba ka yãŋɛ botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Zamãan-ẽna nẽrba, ya ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra, la ya ka ɩtɩ sõŋa. Mam wʋn bɔna la yãma, mã'agɛ m mẽŋa halɩ ka paagɛ la wakat-kãna? Tarɩ-ya bia la wa'am.».

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: La Azezi daan tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la mɛ, tɩ a yese dee basɛ bia la, tɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm bilam mẽŋa.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am bãma mã'a, ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ tõma ka yãŋɛ dige kʋlkã'arga la tɩ a yese?».

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Yan ka bɔ sɩra zo'oge la ĩyã. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ yãma n bɔ mam sɩra la, sãn ãna baa pɩka wʋ sɔ'ɔrɔ biire la, ya tã wʋn yele tãn-ẽna yetɩ: ‹Vurge sẽŋɛ ke›, la a wʋn ẽŋɛ la bɛla. La sɛla ka boe tɩ ya kãn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ. .

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la lagɩm bɔna Galile, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Ba wʋn dɩkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ẽŋɛ la nẽrba nu'usum.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: tɩ ba kʋ ẽ. La dabsa atã daarɛ a wʋn vo'oge mɛ.» Ẽn yele bɛla la, tɩ ba sũure sã'am zozo'e.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan paagɛ la Kapɛrnaʋm tẽŋa, tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba sẽŋɛ ka soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Ya karẽnsãama la yɔɔrɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la lampo mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe, a yɔɔra.» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ kule yire, tɩ Azezi dẽŋɛ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Asimõ, fõn tẽ'esrɩ tɩ dũnia wã na'adõma to'osrɩ ba lampo la ãndõma zẽ'am? Tẽŋa dõma zẽ'am bɩɩ, sɛba n de sãama la zẽ'am?».

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Sãama zẽ'am.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛla, tẽŋa la dõma nẽŋa ka boe mĩ.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: La tõma n ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ sã'am ba sũure la ĩyã, sẽŋɛ ka lobge zũmbãŋa mõgrɛ pʋam, fõn yõkɛ zũn-sɛka yiɛn na, fʋ ya'agɛ a nõorɛ, tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ lag-kõbrɛ, dɩkɛ sẽŋɛ ka yɔ mam, la fõn ĩyã.».

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ karẽnbiisi la daan lɛm Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Ãne n de nẽr-kãtɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam?».

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Tɩ Azezi wi bi-pɩka ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: dee yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya sãn ka teege yɛm ãna wʋ kɔma la, ya kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Bɛla, sɛka woo n sike a mẽŋa ãna wʋ bi-pɩka la, a wʋn dɛna nẽr-kãtɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: La sɛka n to'oge bi-pɩka n ãn wʋ ẽna, mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, a to'oge la mam mẽŋa.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: La nẽra sãn botɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ bãna wã n bɔ mam sɩra la ayɩla basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ a wʋn yẽ la, ba sãn dɩkɛ nẽerɛ kug-kãtɛ lʋ yulse a yugla lobe ẽ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam n sõɩ.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Toogo boe dũnia zuo, nẽrba n pã'asrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba basrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la ĩyã. Dẽnɩ tɩ pã'asgɔ wa'am, la nẽr-sɛka n pã'asrɩ la wʋn yẽ toogo.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Fʋ nu'ugo bɩɩ fʋ nã'arɛ sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, wige ba basɛ. Fʋ sãn tara nu'u-yẽŋɔ bɩɩ nã'a-yẽŋɔ dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire vɔa, n sõɩ fõn tarɩ nu'usi bayi, la nãma bayi, dee tɩ ba lobe fʋ basɛ bugum n ka kĩŋri pʋam.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: La fʋ nifo sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, kɩrgɛ ẽ basɛ. Fʋ sãn tara nin-yẽŋɔ dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire vɔa, n sõɩ fõn tarɩ nini bayi, dee tɩ ba lobe fʋ basɛ bug-tẽŋa.».

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Guuse-ya da pɔ'ɔra kɔm-bãna wã baa ayɩla. Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ba malɛkadõma n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, boe mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam mɛ wakatɛ woo. .

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Yãma tẽ'esrɩ la bẽm? Nẽra sãn tara piisi kɔbga, tɩ ayɩla bɔɩ, a kãn basɛ piswɛɩ la awɛɩ la kũnkũnne zuo tɩ ba ɔbra, dee sẽŋɛ ka ɛ ayɩla n bɔɩ la?.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: La mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ a sãn yẽ ẽ, a sũure wʋn ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ la ayɩla la gãnna piswɛɩ la awɛɩ n ka bɔɩ la.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ ãn na bɛla, a ka boorɩ tɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ bãna ayɩla bɔɩ.».

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yetɩ: «Fʋ sɔbia sãn ẽŋɛ be'em, sẽŋɛ ka yẽ ẽ fõn na ẽŋa mã'a, pa'alɛ ẽ a tuure la. A sãn sakɛ fʋ nõorɛ, fʋ to'oge ẽ basɛ.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: La a sãn zagsɛ fʋ nõorɛ la, fʋ doose nẽra ayɩla bɩɩ bayi sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am. Tɩ nẽrba bayi bɩɩ batã dɛna kaset-dõma yelle la mãalgɔ ĩyã, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: La a sãn zagsɛ ka selse ba, fʋ yele wẽndeego la nẽrba. La a sãn zagsɛ ka selse wẽndeego la nẽrba, ya bɩsra ẽ wʋ nẽra n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ, bɩɩ tõon-be'ero dãana.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: La mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ bõn-sɛka woo tɩ ya wʋn lʋ dũnia wã zuo, wʋn lʋ Nawẽnnɛ yire. La sɛla tɩ ya wʋn lorge dũnia wã zuo, wʋn lorge Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Mam ken yɛta ya tɩ nẽrba bayi sãn naagɛ nõorɛ dũnia wã zuo sose mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɛla, a wʋn ẽŋɛ bɔ ba.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: La nẽrba bayi bɩɩ batã sãn lagsɛ taaba mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, mam boe la ba lɛ mɛ.».

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lɛm Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, mam sɔbia sãn ẽŋɛ mam be'em, nõorɛ wãne tɩ mam wʋn basɛ ẽ taalɛ? Nõorɛ ayopɔɩ bɩɩ?».

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka yetɩ nõorɛ ayopɔɩ mã'a, mam yetɩ nõorɛ pisyopɔɩ nõorɛ bʋyopɔɩ.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: La de la bɛla, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãna wʋ naba n boorɩ tɩ a tõntõnɩba geele a tõoma pa'alɛ ẽ.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: La ẽn pɔsɛ bɔna mĩ gɛɛla la, tɩ ba tarɩ nẽra ayɩla n di a sãnnɛ lig-kõba yʋ'ʋrɛ n de talã tʋspia wa'am.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: La a daan ka tarɩ sɛla wʋn yɔ, tɩ a zuudãana la bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba koose ẽ, la a pɔga la a kɔma, la a lɔgrɔ wuu yɔ sãnnɛ na.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Tɩ tõntõnna la ka dũma tẽŋa a zuudãana la nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ, a ẽŋɛ sugri tɩ ẽŋa wʋn yɔ sãnnɛ na wuu.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Tɩ a zuudãana la zoe a ninbãalga, basɛ sãnnɛ na, dee basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: La tõntõn-ẽŋa n yesrɩ la, a tʋ'ʋsɛ la a tõntõn-tadãana n di a sãnnɛ lig-kõba yʋ'ʋrɛ n de deniye kɔbga, yõkɛ ẽ kẽkɛ a kõnkɔa, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Yɔ ma m sãnnɛ na.›.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Tɩ a tõntõn-tadãana la ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ, a ẽŋɛ sugri tɩ ẽŋa wʋn yɔ a sãnnɛ na.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Tɩ a zagsɛ, dee tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem yetɩ, a sãn ka yɔ ẽŋa a sãnnɛ na, a kãn yese.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: La a tõntõn-taaba la n yẽ bɛla la, tɩ ba sũure sã'am zozo'e. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ba zuudãana la zẽ'am ka yele ẽ sɛla n ẽŋɛ la wuu.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Tɩ zuudãana la botɩ ba wi ẽ, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Tõntõn-be'ego wã, sãn-sɛka wuu tɩ fõn di la, mam basɛ mɛ tɩ la ba'asɛ, fõn sose mam na ĩyã.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: La masɛ nɩ mɛ tɩ fʋ mẽ zoe fʋ tadãana la ninbãalga, wʋ mam n zoe fõn ninbãalga se'em na.›.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: La a zuudãana la sũure yĩige mɛ, tɩ a basɛ tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem tɩ ba nãmsɛ ẽ halɩ tɩ a yɔ sãnnɛ na wuu.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn ẽŋɛ ya la bɛla, ya sãn ka basɛ ya sɔbiisi tõon-be'ero taalɛ la ya sũure wuu.».

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Azezi n daan tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yese Galile sẽŋɛ la Zude so'olʋm Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e doose ẽ, tɩ a botɩ ba bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba wa'am a zẽ'am tɩ ba di a yɛm bɩsɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ bʋraa lebse a pɔga n wen ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛka woo mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Yãma ka karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la? Pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ, Nawẽnnɛ n nãam nẽrsaalba la, a nãam na bʋraa la pɔka.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ a yetɩ, bʋraaga basɛ a sɔ, la a ma, dee lagɩm na a pɔga, tɩ bãma bayi lebge ayɩla.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Bɛla, ba ka le dɛna nẽrba bayi, ba yãŋa lebge la ayɩla. Bɛla, sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lagɩm na taaba, nẽrsaala da welge ba.».

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma la soke ẽ yetɩ: «La bẽm ĩyã tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, bʋraa sãn yetɩ a lebse a pɔga, a bɔ ẽ pɔgdire lebsgo gõŋɔ, dee tarɩ ẽ ka lebse?».

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La de la ya sũure n kɛ tɩ ya ka sakɛ pa'algɔ la ĩyã, tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn sakɛ tɩ ya lebse ya pɔgba. Pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ, la yʋʋn ka ãn wãna.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ, bʋraaga sãn lebse a pɔga tɩ la sãn dagna tɩ a gã la bʋraasɩ, a sãn le di pɔka ayẽma, a ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ nẽra n gã'arɛ la a tadãana pɔga.».

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraaga la a pɔga yelle sãn dɛna bɛla, bʋraaga sãn ka di pɔka n sõɩ.».

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «La dagɩ nẽrba wuu n wʋn yãŋɛ to'oge yetɔg-ẽna, sãn dagna sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba to'oge.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Nẽrba basɛba n boe tɩ ba dɔgɛ ba tɩ ba ãna wʋ ba fɔ ba mɛ na, ka dita pɔgsɩ. Tɩ nẽrba mẽ fɔ bʋraasɩ basɛba. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ĩyã, tɩ sɛba mẽ ka dita pɔgsɩ. Sɛka n tã wʋn yãŋɛ to'oge yetɔg-ẽna, a to'oge.».

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Tɩ nẽrba tarɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ wa'am Azezi zẽ'am tɩ a dɩkɛ nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la zɛbɛ nẽrba la.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya kɔm-pɩgsɩ la tɩ ba wa'am mam zẽ'am, da gu'ura-ya ba. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔma wã bõnɔ.».

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: La a dɩkɛ la a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba, dee fõrgɛ.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Tɩ bʋraaga wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam wʋn tõm na tõon-sõŋɔ kãna yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ?».
