eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fʋ sɔkra ma sɛla n ãn sõŋa yelle? Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n ãn sõŋa. Fʋ sãn bɔɔra tɩ fʋ yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, fʋ sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ a nõorɛ se'em na.».

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Tɩ a soke yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ yetɔg-bãna?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Da kʋ nẽra, da gã'arɛ la fʋ tadãana pɔga, da zũ, da tɔgɛ põmpɔrŋɔ kaseto.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: nanna fʋ sɔ, la fʋ ma, la fʋ nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa.».

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Tɩ budib-polle la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam põn sakra bõn-bãna wuu mɛ, bẽm n deege?».

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn bɔɔra tɩ fʋ masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, sẽŋɛ ka koose fõn tarɩ sɛla la wuu dɩkɛ bɔ nasdõma, tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ bõntarsõm Nawẽnnɛ yire, dee wa'am wa dɔla mam.».

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Budib-polle la n wõm bɛla la, a fõrgɛ mɛ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, se'ere n soe la, a tarɩ la bõntarsõm zo'oge.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ la tɔ mɛ bɔ bõntarsa tɩ a kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: La mam le yɛta ya tɩ, la de la nãana tɩ yʋgnɛ doose fupĩim yoko pʋam yese, gãnna bõntarsa n wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: La a karẽnbiisi la n wõm bɛla la, tɩ la di'ige ba halɩ zozo'e, tɩ ba yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ?».

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩsra ba, dee yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽna, la Nawẽnnɛ tã sɛla woo ɩta mɛ.».

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «Tõma basɛ sɛla woo mɛ, dee dɔla fõn. Bẽm tɩ tõma wʋn yẽ?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ wakat-sɛka tɩ sɛla woo wʋn lebge paalsɩ la, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n wʋn zĩ'ire a na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la, yãma sɛba n dolɩ mam na mẽ wʋn zĩ'ire na'am bĩmbĩna pia la ayi zuto, di Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri pia la ayi la sarɩya.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: La sɛba wuu n basɛ ba yiɛ, bɩɩ ba kẽendõma, la ba yɩbsɩ, bɩɩ ba sɔdõma, bɩɩ ba madõma, bɩɩ ba kɔma, bɩɩ ba vatɔ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, ba wʋn to'oge bãma n basɛ sɛla la nõorɛ kɔbga, dee yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: La nẽŋam dõma zozo'e wʋn lebge poorʋm dõma, tɩ poorʋm dõma zozo'e lebge nẽŋam dõma.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ yidãana n yese bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ tõntõnɩba tɩ ba tõm a va'am.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: La a tɔgɛ kʋ la tõntõnɩba la tɩ ẽŋa wʋn yɔ ba lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla daarɛ pʋam, dee tõm ba a va'am na tɩ ba ka tõm.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa bɩ fẽe, tɩ a le yese yẽ nẽrba basɛba n ze da'am ka tõnna.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ya mẽ sẽŋɛ mam va'am na ka tõm, tɩ mam wʋn yɔ ya lan masɛ se'em.›.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Tɩ ba isge sẽŋɛ va'am na. Tɩ a le yese wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka, la wẽngɛlŋa le ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Tɩ a le yese wẽnnɛ n we'esɩ lua, le yẽ nẽrba basɛba n ze, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: ‹Bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma zɛ kalam daarɛ la wuu pʋam ka ɩta sɛla sɛla?›.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: ‹Nẽra nẽra ka dɩkɛ tõma tõoma.› Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ya mẽ sẽŋɛ m va'am na ka tõm.›.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ, tɩ va'am na dãana yele sɛka n bɩsrɩ a tõoma la yelle yetɩ: ‹Wi tõntõnɩba la tɩ ba wa'am tɩ fʋ yɔ ba. Dẽŋɛ yɔ sɛba n sẽŋɛ poorʋm na ka paagɛ sɛba n dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ la.›.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Tɩ sɛba n sẽŋɛ wẽnnɛ n we'esɩ lua la wuu to'ose lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla ayɩla.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Tɩ sɛba n dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ la tẽ'esra tɩ bãma wʋn to'ose gãnna sɛba n wa'am poorʋm na. La ba wuu to'ose la lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla ayɩla wʋ sɛba n sẽŋɛ poorʋm na n to'ose se'em na.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: La sɛba n dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ la n to'ose lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla ayɩla la, ba yõnnɩ yidãana la mɛ.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: yetɩ: ‹Sɛba n wa'am poorʋm na tõm na karf-ayɩla mã'a, la fõn basɛ tɩ ba to'ose zõna la tõma sɛba n tõm halɩ daarɛ la wuu pʋam nãmsɛ la wʋntɛɛŋa la.›.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Tɩ a lebe yele ba nẽra ayɩla yetɩ: ‹M sɩrɛ, mam ka ẽŋɛ fʋ sɛla n ka masɛ. Tɩ tɔgɛ kʋ, tɩ fʋ sakɛ tɩ fõn wʋn to'oge la lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: To'oge fʋ yɔɔrɔ sẽŋɛ fõrgɛ. La de la mam n boorɩ tɩ m yɔ sɛba n wa'am poorʋm na zõna la fõn.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: Mam ka tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ m dɩkɛ m bõnɔ ẽŋɛ sɛla tɩ mam boorɩ? Bɩɩ mam n ẽŋɛ sõŋa la ĩyã tɩ fõn ɩta sũure?›.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Bɛla, nẽŋam dõma wʋn lebge poorʋm dõma, tɩ poorʋm dõma lebge nẽŋam dõma.».

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Azezi daan we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm welge a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la mã'a, yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: «Ya yẽ ya, tɩ we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm, bilam ba wʋn dɩkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mɛ ka ẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma nu'usum, tɩ ba wʋn tɔgɛ a yelle kʋ yetɩ, a masɛ la kũm.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: La ba wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ la bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum, tɩ ba la ẽ, wẽ ẽ na ka'asa, dee ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ. La dabsa atã daarɛ a wʋn vo'oge.».

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Tɩ Azebede pɔga daan doose la a dayɔɔsɩ paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam tɩ a sose ẽ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ ẽ.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la bẽm bɔ fɔ?» Tɩ pɔka la lerge yetɩ: «Bɔ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ sãn wa dita fʋ na'am na, tɩ m dayɔɔsɩ bayi wã ayɩla zĩ'ire fʋ zʋʋgɔ, tɩ ayɩla zĩ'ire fʋ gɔbga.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya ka mi yãma n sosrɩ sɛla. Ya wʋn tã'agɛ nãmsɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam wʋn nãmsɛ wã?» Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla.».

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ya wʋn sɩrɩ nãmsɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam wʋn nãmsɛ la. La, la dagɩ mam n wʋn looge sɛka n wʋn zĩ'ire mam zʋʋgɔ bɩɩ mam gɔbga. Mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm zẽ'e-bãma tɩ a bɔ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ la.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: La karẽnbiisi pia la n wõm bɛla la, ba sũure n isge la ba tadãandõma bayi la.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Tɩ Azezi wi ba wuu yetɩ: «Ya mi tɩ dũnia wã na'adõma soe ba nẽrba la pãŋa, dee tɩ kɛ'ɛndõma pẽrgra nẽrba tɩ ba ɩta bãma n boorɩ se'em.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: La yãma pʋam, la kãn dɛna bɛla. Nẽr-sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a lebge nẽr-keko ya pʋam, la masɛ mɛ tɩ a dɛna ya tõntõnna.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n mẽ boorɩ tɩ a dɛna nẽŋadãana ya pʋam, la masɛ mɛ tɩ a dɛna ya yamŋa wʋ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka wa'am tɩ nẽrba tõm bɔ ẽ, a wa'am tɩ a tõm mɛ bɔ, dee dɩkɛ a vɔm bɔ tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yẽ fãarɛ.».

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la Zeriko tẽŋa pʋam yɛsra, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e dɔla ẽ.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Tɩ fɔɔsɩ bayi zẽa sore nõorʋm wõm tɩ Azezi n tollɩ, tɩ ba tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Zuudãana, Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe tɩ ninbãalga.».

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tãsɛ ba yetɩ, ba sĩna. Tɩ ba malɩn tĩi tɔgra gãnna bɛla yetɩ: «Zuudãana, Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe tɩ ninbãalga.».

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele dee wi ba, soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la bẽm bɔ ya?».

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma boorɩ la tɩ nini yẽta.».

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Tɩ Azezi zoe ba ninbãalga, kalʋm ba nini, tɩ ba nini na yẽta bilam mẽŋa, tɩ ba dɔla ẽ.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Azezi n daan tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãma wuu kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ:.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: «Yãma mi tɩ la deege la dabsa ayi tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa paagɛ, tɩ ba wʋn yõkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka bɔ tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.».

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma daan sẽŋɛ ka lagsɛ la taaba Akayifu yire. Akayifu daan de la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la nẽŋadãana.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: Ba daan tɔgɛ la taaba kʋ yetɩ, ba ẽŋɛ yɛm yõkɛ Azezi kʋ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: La ba yetɩ: «Tɩ da kãn ẽŋɛ-ya bɛla kibsa la pʋam, tɩ nẽrba la wʋn gire mɛ.».

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Azezi daan boe la Betani tẽŋa, Asimõ n wa dɛna kõnkonne na yire.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: Tɩ ba daan bɔna mĩ dita, tɩ pɔka tarɩ albatrɩ dugla n pɩrɛ la tɩdarɛ ka-sõma ligri n zo'oge, wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa ka'agɛ kaam na ẽŋɛ Azezi zuugo.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yẽ bɛla, tɩ ba sũure sã'am. Tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ a sã'am kaam na wãna?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Ba tã nɩ wʋn koose kaam wã ligri zo'oge mɛ, dɩkɛ bɔ nasdõma.».

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ba tẽ'esgɔ la, yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm ẽŋɛ tɩ ya tɔgra pɔka la tɩ a sũure sã'am? Ẽn ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ mam na de la tõon-sõŋɔ.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Nasdõma boe la ya lɛ mɛ wakatɛ wuu, la mam kãn bɔna la ya wakatɛ wuu.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Ẽŋa n ka'agɛ kaam na ẽŋɛ mam zuo la, a ẽŋɛ la ba wʋn ẽŋɛ mam se'em mam laarɛ daarɛ.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ dũnia wã zẽ'e-sɛka wuu tɩ ba wʋn mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, ba mẽ wʋn tɔgɛ pɔka wã n ẽŋɛ mam se'em wã, tɩ ba tẽra a yelle.».

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azudaasɩ Iskariyo, daan sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: ka yele ba yetɩ: «Ya wʋn bɔ mam na bẽm, tɩ m pa'alɛ ya sore tɩ ya yõkɛ Azezi?» Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ lig-kõba pitã.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: La pɔsɛ la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a ɛɛra sore tɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la pɔspɔsɩ daarɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ la bɛ ka mãasʋm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la tɩ fʋ di?».

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya tẽŋa la pʋam Abõnɔ yire ka yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Karẽnsãama la yetɩ, ẽŋa wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, la de la fõn yire tɩ ẽŋa wʋn di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, la a karẽnbiisi.› ».

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la ẽŋɛ Azezi n yele ba se'em na, mãasʋm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan mã'agɛ, tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la sẽŋɛ yire la pʋam ka zẽa dita.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Ban daan dita la, tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya nẽra ayɩla n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.».

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la sũure sã'am halɩ, tɩ ba sɔkra ẽ ayɩla ayɩla yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la de la mam bɩɩ?».

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sɛka n wʋn yɛglɛ la mam tũm a nu'ugo laaga pʋam na, la de la ẽŋa n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn ki wʋ la yʋʋn põn gʋlsɛ a yelle se'em Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na. La nẽr-sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn yẽ toogo. Nẽr-ẽŋa ma daan sãn ka dɔgɛ nɩ ẽ n sõɩ.».

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Tɩ Azudaasɩ n de nẽr-sɛka n daan wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, la de la mam bɩɩ?» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yele la bɛla.».

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Ban daan boe mĩ dita la, tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «To'oge-ya di. Ẽna de la mam ĩyã.».

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Dee dɩkɛ bõn-yũula pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee bɔ ba yetɩ: «To'oge-ya ya wuu yũ.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Bãna wã de la mam zɩɩm n wʋn yese saagɛ nẽrba zozo'e ĩyã, pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la ba tɩ a basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ, mam kãn le yũ viinye biɛ bõn-yũula wã, halɩ tɩ la ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn le naagɛ la ya yũ ba mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Tɩ ba daan yõom yõoma pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee yese wɛ'ɛsa Oliivi tãŋam.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Yãma wuu wʋn lebe la poorʋm dee basɛ mam zĩna yʋ'ʋŋɔ, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn kʋ dũnsĩina, dee tɩ piisi la wɩrgɛ.› ».

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: La Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «La mam sãn wa vo'oge, mam wʋn dẽŋɛ ya nẽŋa sẽŋɛ Galile.».

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele yetɩ: «Baa bãna wuu sãn lebe poorʋm dee basɛ fõn, mam kãn malɩn basɛ fõn.».

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra, tɩ zĩna yʋ'ʋŋɔ wã, lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam.».

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele yetɩ: «Baa la sãn dɛna tɩ mam lagɩm na fõn ki, mam kãn malɩn sɩ'ɩsɛ fõn.» Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la wuu lagɩm yele bɛla.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Getsemane, tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Zĩ'ire-ya kalam, dee tɩ m sẽŋɛ nẽŋa ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: La Azezi tarɩ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azebede dayɔɔsɩ bayi la sẽŋɛ. Tɩ a sũure sã'am halɩ zozo'e, tɩ a palẽŋa ẽgra.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ mam zozo'e tɩ la ãna wʋ mam yetɩ m ki mɛ na. Weege-ya kalam gu'ura mam, da gĩse-ya.».

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: La a sẽŋɛ la nẽŋa fẽe ka võglɛ a nẽŋa tẽŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «M Sɔ, la sãn tãna, fʋ basɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ ẽna tole dee basɛ ma. La, la da kãn dɛna mam n boorɩ se'em, la dɛna fõn boorɩ se'em.».

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: La a lebe a karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am ka yẽ tɩ ba gĩsri mɛ. Tɩ a soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Yãma ka tã'agɛ naagɛ la mam gu karf-ayɩla mã'a ka gĩse?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Basɛ-ya gẽem dee pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ yelle da paagɛ ya tɩ ya lui. Ya sɩa boorɩ mɛ, la ya ĩyã la ka tarɩ pãŋa.».

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: La a daan le lebe mɛ ka pʋ'ʋsɛ yetɩ: «M Sɔ, nãmsgɔ wã sãn ka tãna wʋn tole dee basɛ mam, la sãn dɛna tɩ mam nãmsɛ, basɛ tɩ fõn boorɩ se'em ẽŋɛ.».

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: La a le lebe a karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am ka yẽ tɩ ba le gĩsra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, gẽem n daan ba tara ba.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Tɩ a basɛ ba, dee le lebe nõorɛ atã pʋam ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãma mẽŋa.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Dee leme wa'am a karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am wa soke ba yetɩ: «Ya gĩsri mɛ dee vɔ'ɔsra? Wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, tɩ ba wʋn dɩkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ẽŋɛ tõon-be'ero dõma nu'usum.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Isge-ya tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ. Sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam na n wãna n sẽm.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Azezi n daan ken tɔgra la, tɩ Azudaasɩ n de a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla doose la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e sẽŋɛ paam. Ba daan tarɩ la sʋkɔbrɔ la dɔa. La de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma n daan tõm ba.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: La Azudaasɩ n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na, põn pa'alɛ ba mɛ yetɩ: «Sɛka tɩ mam wʋn kɔkɛ ẽ na, la de la ẽŋa, yõkɛ-ya ẽ.».

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: La ẽn paam na, a sẽŋɛ mɛ tɔtɔ lɛm Azezi, pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, zaanõorɛ», dee kɔkɛ ẽ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «M sɩrɛ, ẽŋɛ fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ se'em na.» Tɩ nẽrba la paagɛ yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan boe la Azezi la, nẽra ayɩla fooge a sʋkɔbgɔ sɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna tʋbrɛ fiige.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Lebse sũm fʋ sʋ'a la a fʋʋgɔ pʋam, se'ere n soe la, sɛba n zɛbrɩ la sʋ'ʋsɩ, sʋ'ʋsɩ n wʋn kʋ ba.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Fõn ka mi tɩ mam tã wʋn sose m Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a tõm malɛkadõma n gãnnɩ tʋsa tʋsa pia la ayi?.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: La mam sãn ẽŋɛ nɩ bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yetɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em na, wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne ẽŋɛ?».

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: A tɔgɛ bɛla, dee soke nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Yãma sẽm na sʋkɔbrɔ, la dɔa tɩ ya yõkɛ mam wʋ mam de la fãara la? Mam daan boe la yãma mɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na daarɛ woo zãsna nẽrba, tɩ yãma ka yõkɛ mam.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: La bõn-bãna wuu ẽŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yʋʋn yele se'em Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na n ẽŋɛ.» Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la wuu zoe dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Sɛba n daan yõkɛ Azezi la, tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Akayifu yire. Bilam tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma daan lagsɛ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan bɔna poorʋm zãazãarɛ dɔla ka paagɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la yire, kẽ naagɛ la tõntõnɩba zẽa tɩ a bɩsɛ yelle la n wʋn ba'asɛ se'em.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la sarɩyaditba la wuu daan eerɩ la yelle tɩ ba parɩm bɔ Azezi botɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: La ba ka yẽ, baa la nẽrba zozo'e n parɩm põmpɔrŋɔ kaseto bɔ ẽ na. Tɩ nẽrba bayi yãŋa isge.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: yetɩ: «Bʋraa wã daan yetɩ, ẽŋa tã wʋn wõrgɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, dee le mẽ ẽ dabsa atã pʋam.».

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la isge soke Azezi yetɩ: «Ban tɔgɛ fõn se'em na, fõn kãn lerge se'ere?».

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Tɩ Azezi ka lerge se'ere. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana la yʋ'ʋrɛ, fʋ sãn dɛna la Krisi, Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, yele to.».

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La de la fõn yele se'em na. La mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya wʋn yẽ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la, tɩ a zĩ Nawẽnnɛ n de pãŋa dãana la zʋʋgɔ bɔba, ya wʋn yẽ tɩ a boe sawara pʋam sẽna.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la ãasʋm a mẽŋa futo, dee yetɩ: «A tʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ ka le ɛɛra tɩ nẽra le bɔ kaseto. Ya wõm ẽn tʋʋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ se'em.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Yãma tẽ'esɛ la bẽm?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «A masɛ la kũm.».

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Tɩ ba tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm mĩ a nẽŋa, dee wẽ ẽ na kũnkũra. Tɩ basɛba wẽ a pɛgsɩ.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Krisi, bãŋɛ sɛka n wẽ fõn tɔgɛ pa'alɛ tɔ.».

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan zĩ la yire la zẽnzakam, tɩ tõntõn-pɔka lɛm ẽ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽ daan boe la Azezi n de Galile nẽra la lɛ.».

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Tɩ a sɩ'ɩsɛ ba wuu nẽŋam yetɩ: «Mam ka bãŋɛ fõn boorɩ tɩ fʋ yele se'em.».

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yese yaŋa nõorʋm, tɩ tõntõn-pɔka ayẽma yẽ ẽ, yele sɛba n boe mĩ na yetɩ: «Ẽna mẽ daan boe la Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ la lɛ.».

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ le sɩ'ɩsɛ pɔɩ yetɩ: «Mam ka mi bʋraaga wã.».

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ sɛba n ze mĩ na, lɛm Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asɩra, fõn de la ba nẽra ayɩla, se'ere n soe la, fʋ yetɔga n pa'alɛ fõn ze'ele zẽ'a.».

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ pɔɩ yetɩ: «Mam ka mi bʋraaga wã, mam sãn parɩm mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgɛ ma.» Wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kaasɛ.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yãŋa tẽegɛ Azezi n daan yele ẽ yetɩ: «Lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam» na. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yese yẽŋa ka kɛlla la sũ-sã'aŋɔ zozo'e.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan sẽŋɛ ka lɛm na Zerizalɛm, paagɛ tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Betɩfaazɩ, Oliivi tãŋa sɩa. Tɩ Azezi tõm a karẽnbiisi bayi.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya tẽŋa la n boe ya nẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ bõŋ-yã'aŋa n lʋ zɛ la a biire, lorge-ya ẽ tarɩ ba wa'am wa bɔ ma.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Nẽra sãn yele ya se'ere, ya yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana la n boorɩ ba›, tɩ a wʋn basɛ tɩ ya tarɩ ẽ wa'am tɔtɔ.».

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: La ẽŋɛ la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la n yʋʋn tɔgɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Yele-ya Siyõ tẽŋa nẽrba yetɩ: ‹Ya naba la n wãna n sẽm. A sike a mẽŋa bã la bõŋa, la a biire sẽna.› ».

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ Azezi n yele ba se'em na.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: tarɩ bõŋ-yã'aŋa la a biire wa'am, wa dɩkɛ ba futo paglɛ ba zuto tɩ Azezi bã.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e yɛrgɛ ba futo sore zuo, tɩ sɛba kɔ tɩɩsɩ võorɔ yɛrgɛ sore zuo.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan boe a nẽŋa, la sɛba n boe a poorʋm na wuu tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Ozaana, naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa. Pẽgɛ-ya nẽr-sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la. Ozaana! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum.».

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Ban daan sẽnnɩ kẽ'era Zerizalɛm na, tẽŋa la wuu dãm mɛ, tɩ ba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ãne n de ẽna?».

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lerge ba yetɩ: «La de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm na.».

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Azezi daan kẽ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam digra sɛba wuu n koosrɩ, la sɛba n da'arɩ la, tɩ ba yɛsra, dee zã'agɛ sɛba n teerɩ ligri taablɩdõma, la sɛba n koosrɩ na'adawɛma kʋgsɩ lʋbɛ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Dee yele ba yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Ba wʋn wi'ira mam deego la tɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego.› La yãma dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ la nayigba lagsgɔ zẽ'a.».

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Tɩ fɔɔsɩ, la wɔbsɩ wa'am a zẽ'am Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ a botɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n yẽ tõon-yãlma la tɩ Azezi tõnnɩ, la kɔma n malɩn tĩi tɔgra Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yɛta tɩ: «Ozaana! Pẽgɛ-ya naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa» la, ba sũure n daan yĩige.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Tɩ ba yele Azezi yetɩ: «Fõn wõm ban yetɩ se'em na?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe, mam wõm ya. Yãma nãn ka karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɔg-ẽna n yetɩ: ‹Fõn botɩ kɔma, la bilɛɛsɩ n nã'asra fõn.› ».

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: La Azezi basɛ ba bilam, dee sẽŋɛ la Betani ka gã'arɛ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Beere n daan wiige bulika, tɩ Azezi lɛbra Zerizalɛm, tɩ a kɔm dõnna.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Tɩ a yẽ kẽnkãŋa n ze sore nõorʋm, sẽŋɛ tɩ a ka bɩsɛ, la a paagɛ yẽ la võorɔ mã'a. Tɩ a yele kẽnkãŋa la yetɩ: «Fʋ kãn le malɩn wɔm biɛ.» La bilam mẽŋa tɩ kẽnkãŋa la kʋɩ.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: La a karẽnbiisi la n yẽ bɛla la, la di'ige ba mɛ, tɩ ba yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ kẽnkãŋa wã darɛ kʋɩ wãna?».

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ ya sãn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra dee ka sẽ'ena, ya mẽ wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ wʋ mam n ẽŋɛ tɩ-ẽna se'em wã, la, la dagɩ bɛla mã'a, ya wʋn tã'agɛ yele tãn-ẽna yetɩ: ‹Võogɛ ka lui mõgrɛ pʋam›, la a wʋn ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: La ya sãn sose Nawẽnnɛ sɛla dee bɔ ẽ sɩra, ya wʋn to'oge.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Azezi daan kẽ la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma wa'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽ nõorɛ la bɛ ɩta bõn-bãna? La ãne n bɔ fõn nõorɛ tɩ fõn ɩta?».

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam wʋn soke ya sokre ayɩla, tɩ ya sãn lerge mam, mam mẽ wʋn yele ya nõor-sɛka tɩ mam tarɩ ɩta bõn-bãna.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Yele-ya ma, Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm ẽ bɩɩ, la de la nẽrsaalba?» Tɩ ba bãm na taaba yetɩ: «Tɩ sãn yetɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm ẽ, a wʋn yetɩ, bẽm ĩyã tɩ tõma ka sakɛ ẽ?.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Tɩ mẽ ka tã wʋn yetɩ, la de la nẽrsaalba n tõm ẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ zɔta nẽrba la, se'ere n soe la, ba wuu sakɛ tɩ Azã de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.».

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Tɩ ba koŋe lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka mi.» Tɩ a mẽ yele ba yetɩ: «Mam mẽ kãn yele ya mam n yẽ nõorɛ zẽ'e-sɛka ɩta bõn-bãna.».

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Azezi daan le yele ba yetɩ: «Pa'alɛ-ya ma yãma n tẽ'esrɩ se'em: Bʋraa n daan boe tarɩ a dayɔɔsɩ bayi, yele pɔspɔsɩ dãana la yetɩ: ‹M dayʋa, sẽŋɛ ka tõm m va'am na zĩna.›.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Tɩ a dayʋa la lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Mam kãn sẽŋɛ.› Tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ a teege yɛm sẽŋɛ.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ a dayʋa bʋyi dãana la zẽ'am ka yele ẽŋa mẽ yetɩ, a sẽŋɛ ka tõm a va'am na. Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M sɔ, mam wʋn sẽŋɛ.› La a yele bɛla dee ka sẽŋɛ.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Bãma bayi, ãne n ẽŋɛ a sɔ la n boorɩ se'em na?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Pɔspɔsɩ dãana la.» Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la pɔg-yalsɩ dõma wʋn dẽŋɛ ya nẽŋa kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Azã wa'am wa pa'alɛ ya la sore tɩ ya wʋn doose masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la ya ka bɔ ẽ sɩra. La lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la pɔg-yalsɩ dõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra. La baa yãma n yẽ bɛla la, ya ken ka teege yɛm bɔ ẽ sɩra.».

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Azezi daan le yele ba yetɩ: «Selse-ya makrɛ ayɩla. Yidãana ayɩla n daan kɔ a va'am sele tɩɩsɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de viinye, mẽ lalga kaagɛ ẽ, tũ boko tɩ ba wẽkra viinye la biɛ, dee mẽ de-woko tɩ ba zɔnna bɩsra a va'am na, yãŋa dɩkɛ nẽrba tɩ ba bɩsra va'am na, dee sẽŋɛ sore.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Tɩ viinye la biɛ go'osgo wakatɛ paagɛ, tɩ a tõm a tõntõnɩba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na zẽ'am, ka to'oge a viinye la biɛ wa bɔ ẽ.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Tɩ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na yõgɛ tõntõnɩba la wẽ ayɩla, kʋ ayɩla, dee lobe ayẽma la kuga kʋ.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Tɩ a le tõm tõntõnɩba basɛba n zo'oge gãnna pɔspɔsɩ dõma la. Tɩ ba le ẽŋɛ bãma mẽ bɛla.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Tɩ a yãŋa tõm a mẽŋa dayʋa sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na zẽ'am, dee tẽ'esra yetɩ: ‹Ẽna n de mam mẽŋa dayʋa la, ba wʋn nan ẽ.›.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: La sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na n yẽ ẽ na, ba yele taaba yetɩ: ‹Ẽna n wʋn wa sɔɔgɛ sɔna a sɔ bõntarsõm na. Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ kʋ ẽ, dee tɩ bõntarsõm na lebge tõma bõnɔ.›.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ ve'ege yese va'am na kẽnkɛrŋa ka kʋ ẽ.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Bɛla, va'am na dãana sãn wa'am, a wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na la wãne?».

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «A wʋn nãmsɛ nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ bãna mɛ halɩ kʋ ba, dee dɩkɛ va'am na bɔ nẽr-sɛba n wʋn bɔ ẽ a viinye la biɛ ba go'osgo wakatɛ, tɩ ba bɩsra.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma nãn ka karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la n yetɩ: ‹Kug-sɛka tɩ tõnmẽtba zagsɛ la n lebge kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa. La de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽna, tɩ la dɛna yel-di'iŋa bɔ tõma.›.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Bɛla, mam yetɩ ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn to'oge a so'olʋm ya zẽ'am dɩkɛ bɔ buur-sɛba n wʋn tõm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm tõoma. .

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: La kug-sɛka tɩ tõnmẽtba zagsɛ la, nẽr-sɛka woo n lui kug-ẽŋa zuo, a wʋn kɔ kɔ mɛ. La kugre la sãn lui sɛka zuo, a wʋn mẽrgɛ ẽ.».

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma n wõm makrɛ la, ba bãŋɛ tɩ a tɔgrɩ la bãma yelle.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, dee zɔta nẽrba la, se'ere n soe la, ba sakɛ tɩ Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Azezi daan le makɛ la makrɛ yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ naba n ẽŋɛ a dayʋa pɔgdire kibsa.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: tõm a tõntõnɩba yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka wi nẽr-sɛba tɩ ẽŋa daan wi la. Tɩ nẽrba la ka bɔɔra tɩ ba wa'am.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Tɩ a le tõm tõntõnɩba basɛba yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele sɛba tɩ mam daan wi la yetɩ, mam mãasʋm sɛla woo mɛ, mam kʋ la lɔlsɩ, la dũnsi n nõbɛ, mãasʋm sɛla woo ze'ele, ba wa'am kibsa la.›.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka wi ba, tɩ ba zagsɛ, dee sẽŋɛ ba sẽnnɛ. Tɩ ayɩla sẽŋɛ a va'am, tɩ ayẽma sẽŋɛ a koosgo zẽ'am.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Tɩ basɛba la yõgɛ tõntõnɩba la nãmsɛ ba dee kʋ ba.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Tɩ naba la sũure yĩige, tɩ a tõm a zɛbzɛbrɩba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka kʋ nẽr-kʋʋrba la, dee yõ ba tẽŋa la.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: La a yele a tõntõnɩba la yetɩ: ‹Kibsa la sɛla woo mãasʋm ba'asɛ mɛ, la mam n daan wi sɛba la ka masɛ tɩ ba di kibsa la.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Bɛla, sẽŋɛ-ya sorɔ-puursi zuto ka wi nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ ya wʋn yẽ tɩ ba wa'am pɔgdire kibsa la zẽ'am.›.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Tɩ tõntõnɩba la sẽŋɛ sorɔ-puursi zuto ka wi nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ ba yẽ, nẽr-sõma la nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ. Tɩ nẽrba wa'am wa pɩrɛ kibsa la zẽ'am.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Tɩ naba la sẽŋɛ tɩ a bɩsɛ nẽrba la, yẽ tɩ nẽra n boe mĩ ka yɛ pɔgdire kibsa la fuugo.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M sɩrɛ, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn wa'am kibsa la, dee ka yɛ a fuugo?› Tɩ a ka yãŋɛ lerge se'ere.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Tɩ naba la yele a tõntõnɩba yetɩ, ba lʋ a nu'usi, la a nãma lobe ẽ basɛ yẽŋa lika pʋam. Bilam tɩ nẽrba wʋn ka kɛlla, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: La de la nẽrba pãɩ tɩ ba wi, la fẽe tɩ ba tũ'use.».

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma sẽŋɛ ka bãm bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em botɩ Azezi tɔgɛ tuuge tɩ ba yẽ sore yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Bɛla tɩ ba daan tõm ba karẽnbiisi, la naba A'erɔdɩ nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma mi tɩ fõn de la sɩra dãana, dee zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla la sɩra. Fõn ka fablɩ nẽrba n tɔgrɩ fõn yelle se'em na, la fõn ka tũ'usri nẽrba.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Yele to fõn tẽ'esrɩ se'em. Sore boe tɩ tɩ yɔ lampo bɔ Rom na-keko bɩɩ, sore ka boe?».

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ba pʋtẽ'e-be'ero la, yele ba yetɩ: «Pʋlibsum dõma wã, bẽm tɩ ya bẽta mam?.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Pa'alɛ-ya ma lag-sɛka tɩ ba tarɩ yɔɔra lampo la.» Tɩ ba dɩkɛ lag-kõbrɛ pa'alɛ ẽ.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne yinyisŋa, la ãne yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a zuo?».

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «La de la Rom na-keko la.» Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, bɔ-ya na-keko la ẽŋa n soe bõn-sɛka, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ ẽn soe bõn-sɛka.».

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Ban wõm bɛla la, tɩ la di'ige ba, tɩ ba basɛ ẽ dee fõrgɛ.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Daar-ẽŋa tɩ Saduse dõma n yetɩ nẽra sãn ki a kãn vo'oge la, bãma nẽrba basɛba n wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: «Karẽnsãama, Amoyisi daan gʋlsɛ yetɩ, bʋraaga sãn ki dee ka tara bia, a sɔbia di pɔkõorɛ la dɔgɛ kɔma bɔ sɛka n ki la.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Bʋraasɩ bayopɔɩ n daan boe tõma pʋam dɛna sɔbiisi, tɩ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la di pɔka ki dee ka dɔgɛ bia.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Tɩ bʋyi dãana la di pɔkõorɛ la mẽ ki dee ka dɔgɛ bia. Tɩ bʋtã dãana la mẽ ẽŋɛ bɛla, la bãma bayopɔɩ la wuu ẽŋɛ la bɛla.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Tɩ pɔka la mẽ yãŋa ki.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Bɛla, ba sãn wa vo'oge kũm vo'ore daarɛ, bãma bayopɔɩ la pʋam, ãne n wʋn sɔna pɔka la, bãma wuu n lagɩm di ẽ na?».

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya tuuge mɛ, yãma n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em võorɛ, la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa n ãn se'em na ĩyã.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Kũm vo'ore daarɛ, bʋraasɩ ka diti pɔgsɩ, la pɔgsɩ mẽ ka ellɩ sɩrba. Ba wʋn ãna wʋ malɛkadõma n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: La sãn dɛna kũm vo'ore yelle, yãma nãn ka karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n yele ya a gõŋɔ la pʋam se'em yetɩ:.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: ‹Mam de la Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ Nawẽnnɛ› na? La pa'alɛ tɩ bãma wuu vɔa mɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ dagɩ sɛba n ki Nawẽnnɛ, a de la nẽr-sɛba n vɔa Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n daan wõm a zãsŋɔ la, la di'ige ba mɛ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Farisi dõma n daan wõm tɩ Azezi lerge Saduse dõma, tɩ ba ka le tã'agɛ ya'agɛ nõorɛ la, tɩ ba lagsɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla n de lɔɔ karẽnsãama bɔɔra tɩ a di a yɛm bɩsɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: «Karẽnsãama, Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba ɩta se'em na, bõn-kãna n gãnnɩ wuu?».

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: « ‹Nõŋɛ fʋ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ la fʋ sũure wuu, la fʋ sɩa wuu, la fʋ yɛm wuu.›.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Ẽna n de Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la pɔspɔsɩ dãana, ẽŋa n gãnnɩ wuu.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: La bʋyi dãana n ãn wʋ ẽŋa n wãna n yetɩ: ‹Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fõn nõŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa se'em na.›.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Amoyisi lɔɔ la wuu, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n gʋlsɛ sɛla la wuu boe la nõ-bãma bayi pʋam.».

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farisi dõma n lagsɛ taaba la, tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ:.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: «Yãma tẽ'esrɩ la bẽm la Krisi yelle: Ãne yɩɩŋa n de ẽ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «A de la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa.».

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa daan botɩ Adavidi wi ẽ tɩ Zuudãana yetɩ:.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele mam Zuudãana yetɩ, zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ bɔba halɩ tɩ m wa ẽŋɛ fʋ bɛ'ɛba tɩ ba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.›.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Adavidi sãn wi ẽ yetɩ, Zuudãana, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ a dɛna Adavidi yɩɩŋa?».

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka yãŋɛ lerge ẽ se'ere. Lan pɔsɛ daar-ẽŋa, nẽra ka le saagɛ soke ẽ sokre.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ, la a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ:.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: «Lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma n sɔɔgɛ Amoyisi zẽ'a.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Bɛla, ban zãsnɩ ya se'em na, ya ɩta bɛla, dee da ɩta bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na. Se'ere n soe la, bãma n zãsnɩ se'em na, ba ka ɩtɩ bɛla.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Ba zãsŋɔ la ãn wʋ ze-tɩbsa tɩ ba lʋ zelge nẽrba, la ba mẽŋa ka boorɩ tɩ ba kalʋm ba la ba nu'ubiire.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Ba tõnnɩ ba tõoma la wuu tɩ nẽrba n bɩsra ba, bɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ gãna gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga ba pʋam dɩkɛ lʋ ba dĩa bɩɩ ba kẽnkãnsɩ. Dee pɛ fu-wogro ẽŋɛ bisa tɩ ba wɔgɛ tɩ nẽrba nã'asra ba.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam yese fõrgra. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lɛm ẽ pa'alɛ ẽ tɩ a bɩsɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la n mẽ sõŋa se'em.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Ba nõŋɛ la nã'asgɔ zẽ'esɩ dia zẽ'esʋm, la ba wẽndeto pʋam.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma yẽ bõn-bãna wã wuu? Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ba wʋn lʋbɛ ba wuu mɛ saam saam, tɩ kugre baa ayɩla kãn weege a tadãana zuo.».

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Ba nõŋɛ tɩ nẽrba n pʋ'ʋsra ba da'asɩ pʋam, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba wi'ira ba yetɩ: ‹Karẽnsãama.›.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: La a daan sẽŋɛ ka zẽa la Oliivi tãŋa zuo, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la mã'a sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yele to wakat-sɛka tɩ bõn-bãma wuu wʋn ẽŋɛ, la sɛla n wʋn pa'alɛ fõn lemŋo, la dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ.».

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: La yãma da basɛ tɩ ba wi'ira ya tɩ karẽnsãama. Se'ere n soe la, yãma tarɩ la karẽnsãama ayɩla mã'a, la ya wuu de la sɔbiisi la taaba.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Guuse ya mẽŋa tɩ nẽra da pã'asɛ ya tɩ ya tuuge.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Da wi'ira-ya nẽra tɩ sɔ dũnia wã zuo Nawẽnnɛ sore la doosgo ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, ya tarɩ la Sɔ ayɩla mã'a n boe saazuum.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Nẽrba zozo'e n wʋn dɩkɛ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ wa'am wa pã'asra ya, tɩ ba nẽra woo yetɩ: ‹Mam n de Krisi la.› La ba wʋn botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tuuge mɛ.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: La ya da basɛ tɩ nẽra wi'ira ya tɩ nẽŋadãana. Se'ere n soe la, ya tarɩ la nẽŋadãana ayɩla mã'a n de Krisi.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ya wʋn wa wõm zɛbrɛ yelle, la zɛbrɛ dãmnɛ. La da basɛ-ya tɩ dabeem tarɩ ya. Dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-bãma ẽŋɛ, la, la nãn dagɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Sɛka n de nẽr-keko ya pʋam, a dɛna ya tõntõnna.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Buuri ayɩla nẽrba wʋn isge zɛbra la buuri ayẽma nẽrba, tɩ so'olʋm ayɩla isge zɛbra la so'olʋm ayẽma, la kɔm, la tẽn-mĩiŋo wʋn bɔna zẽ'esɩ zozo'e.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Sɛka n zẽkɛ a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sike ẽ. La sɛka n sike a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zẽkɛ ẽ.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: La bõn-bãma nãn de la toogo la sẽŋrɛ, la ãn wʋ pɔka virgre toogo n nãn sẽŋrɩ la.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Yãma Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma gu'uri la nẽrba tɩ ba da kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam. Ya ka kẽ'erɩ ya mẽŋa, dee mẽ ka basra nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba kẽ la sore tɩ ba kẽ. .

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Ba wʋn yõgɛ ya mɛ tarɩ ka bɔ tɩ ba nãmsɛ ya, kʋ ya, la buuri wuu wʋn sisra ya mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya kaarɩ la dũnia zẽ'esɩ wuu ɛɛra tɩ ya yẽ nẽra ayɩla tɩ a sakɛ dɔla ya sore la. La ya sãn yẽ nẽra ayɩla, ya basɛ tɩ a lebge la tõon-be'ero dãana gãnna ya mẽŋa nõorɛ ayi, wʋn sẽŋɛ bug-tẽŋa.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Nẽrba zozo'e wʋn basɛ sɩra sore, dee ɩta taaba zãmɔ, dee sisra taaba.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Yãma sɛba n de nẽŋadõma de la fɔɔsɩ, ya wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, yãma yetɩ, nẽra sãn pɔɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, la de la zãŋa. La sɛka sãn pɔɩ sãlma la n boe Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam na, dẽnɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽn pɔɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: La nẽrba zozo'e wʋn isge parɩm yetɩ bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba pã'asɛ nẽrba zozo'e tɩ ba tuuge.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Yãma ka wõnnɩ sɛla võorɛ, dee ãna wʋ fɔɔsɩ la. Bẽm n gãnnɩ? Sãlma la bɩɩ, Wẽnde-kãtɛ la n botɩ sãlma la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ na n gãnnɩ?.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Tõon-be'ero wʋn wa pa'asɛ mɛ halɩ tɩ nẽrba zozo'e nõŋrɛ ba'asɛ.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ya ken yetɩ, nẽra sãn pɔɩ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na, la de la zãŋa. La nẽra sãn pɔɩ sɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ bĩŋe bĩmbĩnne na zuo la, dẽnɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽn pɔɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n wʋn mõrgɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Fɔɔsɩ, bẽm n gãnnɩ? Sɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ la bɩɩ, kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na n botɩ sɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ na n gãnnɩ?.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga wʋn mõolɛ dũnia wuu mɛ, tɩ buuri wuu wõm, dee tɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ yãŋa paagɛ.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Bɛla, sɛka n pɔɩ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na, a po pɔɩ la sɛla n boe a zuo la wuu.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Ya wʋn wa yẽ sɛka n de bõn-be'ego n sã'anɩ sɛla woo la, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Adanɩyɛlɩ yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yelle la, tɩ a boe Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la.» Sɛka n karẽŋrɩ mõrgɛ wõm a võorɛ.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: La sɛka n pɔɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, a po pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ n boe mĩ na.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: «Ya sãn wa yẽ bɛla, nẽr-sɛba n boe Zude zoe zom tãnsɩ zuto.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: La sɛka n pɔɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire, a pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne na, la sɛka n zĩ a zuo la.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n boe gɔsgɔ da sige bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ deem dɩkɛ sɛla.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma bɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu pia pʋrɛ mɛ, baa ba sãn ãna pɩka pɩka, dee basra yel-sɛba n de kãŋkãŋɛ lɔɔ la pʋam na, ka ɩta. La de la sɛla n masɛ, la ninbãal-zʋa, la sakrɛ. La de la bõn-bãna tɩ ya wʋn dẽŋɛ ɩta, dee yãŋa ɩta sɛla tɩ ya ɩtɩ la pa'asɛ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n boe va'am da kãn lebe kule yire tɩ a dɩkɛ a fuugo.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Yãma nẽŋadõma de la fɔɔsɩ, yãma yoorɩ ya bõn-yũula mɛ yɛsra lislima basra, dee vɔlla yʋgma.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Dabs-bãma, la wʋn dɛna la toogo bɔ pɔg-sɛba n tarɩ pʋa, la sɛba n tarɩ kɔma tɩ ba mõgra la.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Yãma peerɩ ya laasɩ, la ya wama pɔa mɛ tɩ ba ãna sõŋa, dee tɩ ba pʋam pɩrɛ la nayigum, la be'ero bõnɔ.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Sose-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ ya zʋa la wakatɛ da dɛna ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ, bɩɩ vo'osgo daarɛ.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Yãma Farisi dõma, ya de la fɔɔsɩ! Peege-ya ya laasɩ la pʋam pɔspɔsɩ, tɩ ba poore la mẽ wʋn ãna sõŋa.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Se'ere n soe la, wakat-ẽŋa, la wʋn dɛna la toogo halɩ zozo'e tɩ ba nãn ka yẽ halɩ dũnia n pĩ'ilum wa paam wakat-ẽŋa, la toog-ẽŋa buuri kãn le malɩn wa'am.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, yãma ãn wʋ yɔɔrɔ tɩ ba takɛ tɩ ba zuum pelge ãna sõŋa sõŋa, dee tɩ ba pʋam pɩrɛ la kõba, la põ'osgɔ wuu.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka bo'oge nɩ dabs-bãma sõorɛ, nẽra nẽra ka boe n wʋn põsɛ. La nẽr-sɛba tɩ a looge la ĩyã tɩ a bo'oge dabs-bãma sõorɛ.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Yãma mẽ ãn na bɛla. Yãma botɩ nẽrba bɩsra ya tɩ ya ãna wʋ nẽrba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, dee tɩ ya pʋam pɩrɛ la pʋlibsum, la tõon-be'ero.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: La nẽr-sɛka sãn yele ya yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ-ya, Krisi la boe la kalam›, bɩɩ ‹bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la ke›, ya da sakɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Ya mẽtɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yɔɔrɔ, dee malgra nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ yɔɔrɔ tɩ ba ãna sõŋa.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Nẽrba wʋn wa'am wa parɩm tɩ bãma n de Krisi, la nẽrba wʋn wa'am wa parɩm tɩ bãma n de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tõnna tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ, bɔɔra tɩ ba sãn tã'agɛ, ba botɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la tuuge.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: Dee yetɩ, yãma daan sãn boe nɩ yãma yaabdõma wakatɛ la, yãma daan kãn po lagɩm na ba kʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Mam dẽŋɛ yele ya mɛ tɩ la nãn ka paam.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Ya mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ ya mẽŋa kaseto tɩ yãma de la sɛba n yʋʋn kʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la yɩɩsɩ.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Bɛla, ba sãn yele ya yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la weem›, da sẽŋɛ-ya. Bɩɩ, ba sãn yetɩ: ‹A boe la deem›, ya da sakɛ.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Tɔ. Ken sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa ẽŋɛ ya yaabdõma la n daan ẽŋɛ sɛla la ba'asɛ.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo wʋn ãna wʋ saaga n ẽn yãkɛ dapoore bɔba nẽegɛ ka paagɛ zanõa bɔba la.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Bõnsɛ'ɛdõma wã! Kãmpẽesɩ buuri! Yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne põsɛ sarɩyadia tɩ ba kãn kẽ'esɛ ya bug-tẽŋa?.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Bõn-kɩ'ɩsɩ n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, zõa lagsrɩ taaba la bilam.».

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam yɛta ya tɩ mam wʋn tõm m nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la yɛm dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma. La ya wʋn kʋ sɛba, dee ka basɛba dɔ-puursi zuto kʋ, wẽ sɛba la ka'asa ya wẽndeto pʋam, dee dɔla ba nãma tẽŋa tẽŋa nãmsra ba.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: «Toogo dabsa la poorʋm, wẽntulle wʋn sobge mɛ, tɩ wõrga kãn le nẽera a peelem, tɩ wõrbiɛ wʋn ze'ele saazuum lui. La bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum wʋn mĩim mɛ.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Bɛla tɩ pʋpeelem dõma la wuu tɩ ba kʋ dũnia zuo la taalɛ wʋn bɔna ya zuto, lan pɔsɛ la Abɛlɩ kʋa halɩ wa paagɛ Abarakɩ dayʋa Azakari tɩ ya kʋ Wẽnde-kãtɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ, la kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na tẽŋasʋka la.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: La sɛla n pa'alɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo la wʋn puke saazuum. Tɩ dũnia nẽrba buuri wuu wʋn kaasra fabla. La ba wʋn yẽ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n boe sawara pʋam sẽna la pãŋa, tɩ a na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ zozo'e.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ bõn-bãna taalɛ wʋn bɔna zamãan-ẽna nẽrba wuu zuto.».

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: La a wʋn tõm na a malɛkadõma, tɩ benkonne peebe, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ dũnia zʋʋgɔ, la gɔbga, la dapoore, la zanõrɛ ka lagsɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la, halɩ ka paagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Azezi daan yetɩ: «Zerizalɛm nẽrba, Zerizalɛm nẽrba, yãma kʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, dee lɔbra sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm ba ya zẽ'am na kuga kʋʋra. Nõorɛ faa tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m lagsɛ ya wʋ nõ-yã'aŋa n lagsrɩ a kɔma ũna a kempagsɩ pʋam se'em na, tɩ ya ka bɔɔra.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Dɩkɛ-ya makrɛ la kẽnkãŋa bãŋɛ zãsŋɔ la võorɛ. Kẽnkãŋa wila sãn wa pusra võ-paala, ya mi tɩ sigre lɛm mɛ.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ ya tẽŋa la tɩ a lebge la daboto.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Bɛla, ya mẽ sãn wa yẽ tɩ bõn-bãna wuu ɩtɩ, ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lɛm mɛ, a boe la zanõrʋm.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya kãn le malɩn yẽ mam, halɩ tɩ ya wʋn wa yetɩ: ‹Pẽgɛ-ya sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la.› ».

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ bõn-bãna wuu wʋn ɩta mɛ, dee tɩ zamãan-ẽna yãŋa tole.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazuum la tẽŋa wʋn tole mɛ, la mam yetɔga la kãn malɩn tole.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Nẽra nẽra ka mi daar-sɛka la wakat-sɛka tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme wa'am, baa malɛkadõma n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, bɩɩ Dayʋa la mẽŋa ka mi, sãn dagna Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mã'a.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo, la wʋn ãna wʋ Anoe wakatɛ n yʋʋn ãn se'em na.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Azezi ken yetɩ: «Wakat-ẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm wʋn ãna wʋ pug-sarsɩ pia n dɩkɛ ba fɩtladõma yese sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka tʋ'ʋsɛ pɔgdita.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Se'ere n soe la, lan dẽŋɛ sã'aŋɔ sa-kãtɛ la, nẽrba yʋʋn diti mɛ dee yũura, tɩ bʋraasɩ dita pɔgsɩ, tɩ pɔgsɩ ɛlla sɩrba, dee tara ba pɔyɔɔsɩ sɩɩra, halɩ wa paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ Anoe kẽ õorŋ-kãtɛ la pʋam na.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Banuu ka tarɩ yɛm, dee tɩ banuu dɛna yɛm dõma.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Ba yʋʋn ka bãŋɛ tɩ yelle boe, halɩ tɩ saaga wa ni, tɩ ko'om pe'ege nẽrba la wuu. La mẽ wʋn ãna la bɛla Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo wakatɛ.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Tɩ sɛba n ka tarɩ yɛm na dɩkɛ ba fɩtladõma, dee ka dɩkɛ kaam pa'asɛ.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Daar-ẽŋa bʋraasɩ bayi wʋn bɔna va'am, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ayɩla, dee basɛ ayẽma.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: La sɛba n tarɩ yɛm na dɩkɛ ba fɩtladõma mɛ, dee dɩkɛ kaam pa'asɛ.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Tɩ pɔgsɩ bayi wʋn bɔna nẽerʋm, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ayɩla, dee basɛ ayẽma.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Tɩ pɔgdita la yuuge ka sẽna, tɩ gẽem yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba wuu gĩse.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Bɛla, guuse ya mẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, ya ka mi daar-sɛka tɩ ya Zuudãana la wʋn leme wa'am.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Tɩ sõnsʋa, tɩ ba kaasɛ tagurga yetɩ: ‹Pɔgdita la sẽm mɛ, isge-ya yese tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.›.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ yidãana sãn mina nayiga n wʋn wa'am yʋ'ʋŋɔ wakat-sɛka wa zũ ẽ, a wʋn gu'ura a yire mɛ, a kãn basɛ tɩ nayiga la kẽ zũ ẽ.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Tɩ pug-sarsɩ la wuu nẽegɛ malgɛ ba fɩtladõma.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Bɛla, mãasʋm-ya gu'ura, tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sẽm na wakat-sɛka tɩ ya ka tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a wʋn wa'am na.».

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Tɩ sɛba n ka tarɩ yɛm na, yele yɛm dõma la yetɩ: ‹Pʋrgɛ-ya ya kaam na bɔ tɔ, tɩ tõma fɩtladõma la kĩŋri mɛ.›.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Ãne n de tõntõn-sõŋɔ n tarɩ yɛm? La de la ẽŋa tɩ a zuudãana wʋn yetɩ a bɩsra a yire yelle, bɔ'ɔra a tõntõnɩba dia wʋ lan masɛ, dee sẽŋɛ sore.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Tɩ yɛm dõma la lerge yetɩ: ‹Ayɛɩ, tõma sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, kaam na kãn seke tõma, la yãma. Sẽŋɛ-ya ka-kɔɔsrɩba la zẽ'am ka da ya mẽŋa bõnɔ.›.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Zuudãana la sãn leme wa yẽ tɩ tõntõnna la tõm wʋ ẽŋa n yele ẽ se'em na, tõntõnna la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka da, dee tɩ pɔgdita la paam, tɩ banuu la n mãasʋm kɩ'ɩlɩm na doose pɔgdita la kẽ pɔgdire la zẽ'am, dee tɩ ba yʋ zanõrɛ.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ a zuudãana la wʋn dɩkɛ ẽŋa n tarɩ sɛla la wuu bɔ ẽ mɛ tɩ a bɩsra.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ pug-sarsɩ banuu la n weege la wa'am wa wi'ira yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, zuudãana, yo'oge bɔ tɔ.›.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: La a sãn dɛna tõntõn-be'ego, tẽ'esɛ yetɩ ẽŋa zuudãana la yuuri mɛ ka kulna.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Tɩ pɔgdita la lerge yetɩ: ‹Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ mam ka mi ya.›.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: yãŋa wẽ'era a tõntõn-taaba la, dita dee yũura la dã-yũuriba.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Bɛla, guuse ya mẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, ya ka mi daar-sɛka bɩɩ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme wa'am.».

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: a zuudãana la wʋn kulum na daar-sɛka tɩ a ka tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a wʋn wa'am, la wakat-sɛka tɩ a ka mi, wa di'ige ẽ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ken ãna wʋ bʋraaga n boorɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ sore, wi a tõntõnɩba dɩkɛ a bõntarsõm pʋɩ ba tɩ ba bɩsra.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: La a wʋn dige ẽ mɛ botɩ a lagɩm na pʋlibsum dõma yẽ sɩbgrɛ. Zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ ba wʋn kɛlla, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.».

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: A bɔ tõntõnna ayɩla la talã banuu, bɔ ayɩla talã bayi, dee bɔ ayɩla talã ayɩla. A bɔ nẽra woo masɛ la ẽn wʋn tã'agɛ se'em tõm, dee sẽŋɛ.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛka n to'oge talã banuu la darɛ tõm yẽ yõorɔ talã banuu pa'asɛ.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ n daan tole, tɩ beere wiige Lasrɩ daarɛ bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Amaari ayẽma isge sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka bɩsɛ yɔɔgɔ la.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Tɩ sɛka n to'oge talã bayi la mẽ tõm yẽ talã bayi pa'asɛ.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa daan mĩim zozo'e, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ malɛka ze'ele saazuum sigum wa bilge kugre la bĩŋe kẽnkɛrŋa zẽa a zuo.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: La sɛka n to'oge talã ayɩla la sẽŋɛ ka tũ la boko pĩ a zuudãana ligri la.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: A nẽŋa daan yẽgrɩ wʋ sa-yãka. Tɩ a futo pelge ãna yɩlɩ yɩlɩ.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Tɩ la yuuge, tɩ tõntõnɩba la zuudãana la leme kulum, wa yele ba tɩ ba geele bãma n tõm se'em.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ sɛba n gu'uri yɔɔgɔ la. Tɩ ba gõgra dee ãna wʋ ba ki mɛ na.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Tɩ sɛka n daan to'oge talã banuu la, tarɩ talã banuu la tɩ a yẽ pa'asɛ la wa'am wa yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, fõn daan bɔ mam na talã banuu, mam tõm yẽ la talã banuu pa'asɛ, ban wãna.›.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele pɔgsɩ la yetɩ: «Da zoe-ya dabeem, mam mi tɩ ya eerɩ la Azezi tɩ ba ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la.
