eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Tɩ a zuudãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹La ãn sõŋa. Fʋ de la tõntõn-sõŋɔ n de sɩra dãana. Fõn ẽŋɛ sɩra la sɛla fẽe tɩ mam bɔ fʋ la ĩyã, mam wʋn basɛ tɩ fʋ sɔna bõnɔ zozo'e bɩsra. Wa'am wa naagɛ la mam ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.›.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: A ka boe kalam. A vo'oge mɛ wʋ ẽn daan yele se'em na. Wa'am-ya wa bɩsɛ ban daan bĩŋe ẽ zẽ'e-sɛka la.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Tɩ sɛka n to'oge talã bayi la mẽ wa'am wa yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, fõn daan bɔ mam na talã bayi, mam tõm yẽ la talã bayi pa'asɛ, ban wãna.›.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: dee sẽŋɛ-ya tɔtɔ ka yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, a vo'oge mɛ. La a wʋn dẽŋɛ ya nẽŋa sẽŋɛ Galile. Bilam tɩ ya wʋn ka yẽ ẽ. Sɛla tɩ mam tarɩ tɩ m yele ya n bɛla.».

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Tɩ a zuudãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹La ãn sõŋa. Fʋ de la tõntõn-sõŋɔ n de sɩra dãana. Fõn ẽŋɛ sɩra la sɛla fẽe tɩ mam bɔ fʋ la ĩyã, mam wʋn basɛ tɩ fʋ sɔna bõnɔ zozo'e bɩsra. Wa'am wa naagɛ la mam ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.›.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Tɩ pɔgsɩ la fõrgɛ yɔɔm na tɔtɔ la dabeem, dee tara sũ-yẽlga zozo'e, zɔta wɛ'ɛsa tɩ ba ka yele Azezi karẽnbiisi la sɛla n ẽŋɛ la.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Tɩ sɛka n to'oge talã ayɩla la wa'am wa yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, mam mi tɩ fõn de la nẽra yelle n tɔɩ. Fõn lagsrɩ la bõnkɔɔla sɛba tɩ fõn ka bʋrɛ, dee vaara bõn-sɛba tɩ fõn ka yɛrgɛ.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Tɩ Azezi darɛ tʋ'ʋsɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ ba. Tɩ ba lɛm ẽ ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam gʋrɛ a nãma nã'asɛ ẽ.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam zoe dabeem dɩkɛ fʋ ligri la sẽŋɛ ka tũ boko pĩ. Fʋ bõnɔ n wãna, to'oge.›.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Da zoe-ya dabeem, sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele m sɔbiisi la yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ Galile, tɩ la de la bilam tɩ ba wʋn yẽ mam.».

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Tɩ a zuudãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Tõntõn-be'ego wã, fʋ de la zẽyãka. Fõn mi tɩ mam lagsrɩ la bõnkɔɔla sɛba tɩ mam ka bʋrɛ, dee vaara bõn-sɛba tɩ mam ka yɛrgɛ la.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Pɔgsɩ la n daan boe sore wɛ'ɛsa la, tɩ sɛba n gu'uri yɔɔgɔ la nẽrba basɛba sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma bõn-sɛba wuu n ẽŋɛ la.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Beere n daan wiige bulika, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma la wuu lagsɛ tɔgɛ kʋ yetɩ ba kʋ Azezi.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: bɛla, la de nɩ na fʋ dɩkɛ m ligri la bĩŋe bãnkɩ pʋam, tɩ mam sãn leme wa'am, m to'oge m ligri la, la yõorɔ.›.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma la lagsɛ kʋ nõorɛ ayɩla, bɔ sʋdaasɩ la ligri zozo'e.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Tɩ ba lʋ ẽ tarɩ sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ n de gɔmna zẽ'am ka bɔ ẽ.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: A yele bɛla dee yetɩ: ‹To'oge-ya a talã la bɔ sɛka n tarɩ talã pia la.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: dee yele ba yetɩ: «Yele-ya nẽrba la yetɩ, yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ yãma gã gĩsra dee tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la wa'am wa zũ kũm na.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Azudaasɩ n de sɛka n daan pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi la, n yẽ tɩ ba tɔgɛ Azezi yelle kʋ yetɩ ba kʋ ẽ na, a sũure sã'am mɛ. Tɩ a tarɩ lig-kõba pitã la sẽŋɛ tɩ a ka lebse kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma la.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ, a wʋn to'oge pa'asɛ tɩ la zo'oge. La sɛka n ka tarɩ, baa sɛla fẽe tɩ a tarɩ la, ba wʋn to'oge ẽ.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: La gɔmna la sãn wõm bɛla, tõma wʋn tɔgɛ la ẽ lɛ, yese ya yelle la pʋam.».

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: dee yetɩ: «Mam ẽŋɛ la be'em, mam n pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ sɛka n ka ẽŋɛ be'em tɩ ba kʋ ẽ na.» Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «La ka pakɛ tõma. La de la fõn yelle.».

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: La tõntõn-sɛka n ka tarɩ yõorɔ la, lobe-ya ẽ basɛ yẽŋa lika pʋam. Zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ ba wʋn kɛlla, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.› ».

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la to'oge ligri la, dee ẽŋɛ wʋ ban yele ba se'em na. Tɩ yetɔg-ẽŋa saagɛ Zifdõma la tẽŋasʋka halɩ wa paam zĩna.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Tɩ Azudaasɩ dɩkɛ ligri la lobge Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam, dee sẽŋɛ ka pire mĩ'isi ki.

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: «Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sãn leme wa'am la a na'am pãŋa, la a malɛkadõma wuu, a wʋn zĩ'ire la a na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Karẽnbiisi pia la ayɩla la daan sẽŋɛ la Galile, ka zom tãn-sɛka tɩ Azezi bɔ ba nõorɛ yetɩ ba sẽŋɛ la zuo.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma la dɩkɛ ligri la yetɩ: «Ba de la zɩɩm ligri, la ka masɛ tɩ tɩ dɩkɛ ba naagɛ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la ligri.».

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Tɩ dũnia so'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn lagsɛ a nẽŋam, tɩ a welge nẽrba la yẽma yẽma wʋ dũnsĩina n welgrɩ a piisi, la a bʋʋsɩ se'em na.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: La ba yẽ ẽ mɛ, ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam nã'asɛ ẽ, la basɛba daan sẽ'enɩ mɛ.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Tɩ ba bãm, dɩkɛ ligri la da yagba va'am tɩ a dɛna sãama laarɛ zẽ'am.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: La a wʋn ze'ele piisi la a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, dee ze'ele bʋʋsɩ a gɔbga bɔba.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Tɩ Azezi lɛm ba, yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ bɔ mam pãŋa wuu n boe saazuum, la tẽŋa wã zuo mɛ.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ ba ken wi'ira zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ zɩɩm va'am, halɩ wa paam zĩna.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Bɛla, sẽŋɛ-ya dũnia wuu ka botɩ nẽrba buuri wuu lebge mam karẽnbiisi. Mise-ya ba ko'om pʋam, la Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa, la a Sɩa yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Tɩ naba la wʋn yele sɛba n boe a zʋʋgɔ la yetɩ: ‹Wa'am-ya, yãma sɛba n tarɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na. To'oge-ya so'olʋm na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan põn mãasʋm tɩ a bɔ ya halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azeremi n yʋʋn gʋlsɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Ba dɩkɛ la lig-kõba pitã, lig-sɛba tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sakɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ da ẽ na.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: La ya zãsʋm ba tɩ ba sakɛ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam bɔ ya nõorɛ tɩ ya ɩta la. La bãŋɛ-ya tɩ mam boe la ya mɛ wakatɛ wuu, halɩ tɩ la ka paagɛ dũnia ba'asgɔ.».

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam kɔm daan dõnnɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma bɔ mam dia. Koyũuro daan tarɩ mam mɛ, tɩ yãma bɔ mam ko'om tɩ mam yũ. Mam daan de la sãana, tɩ yãma to'oge mam.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: ba dɩkɛ la bãma da yagba va'am wʋ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yele mam se'em na.».

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: Mam daan de la beele, tɩ yãma bɔ mam futo tɩ mam yɛ. Mam daan bẽ'erɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma wa'am wa bɩsɛ mam, la mam daan boe la yʋ'a deem, tɩ yãma wa'am mam zẽ'am.›.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ban daan tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ gɔmna la zẽ'am na, tɩ a soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la Zifdõma naba?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La de la fõn yele se'em na.».

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ la wʋn lerge yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, la de la wakat-kãna tɩ tõma yẽ fõn, tɩ fõn kɔm dõnnɩ, tɩ tõma bɔ fõn dia? Bɩɩ koyũuro tarɩ fõn, tɩ tõma bɔ fõn ko'om?.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma daan dõrɛ ẽ mɛ, tɩ a ka lerge se'ere.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: La wakat-kãna tɩ tõma yẽ fõn, tɩ fõn dɛna sãana, tɩ tõma to'oge fõn? Bɩɩ fõn de beele, tɩ tõma bɔ fõn futo tɩ fõn yɛ?.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn ka wõm ban tɔgrɩ se'em wuu dõta fɔ?».

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: La wakat-kãna tɩ fõn bẽ'erɩ, tɩ tõma wa'am wa bɩsɛ fõn, bɩɩ fõn boe yʋ'a deem, tɩ tõma wa'am fõn zẽ'am?›.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Tɩ Azezi ka lerge yetɔga la baa ayɩla, tɩ la di'ige gɔmna la halɩ zozo'e.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Tɩ naba la wʋn lerge ba yetɩ: ‹Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ yãma n daan ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu bɔ mam nẽrba la, baa la sãn dɛna la nẽr-pɩka ayɩla tɩ ya ẽŋɛ bɔ, la de la mam tɩ ya bɔ.›.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: La Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa wakatɛ woo gɔmna la ẽn yese la nẽra ayɩla yʋ'a deem basɛ, nẽr-sɛka tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la boorɩ.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: La a wʋn yele sɛba n boe a gɔbga bɔba la yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ-ya fõrgɛ, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego boe ya zuo. Sẽŋɛ-ya ka kẽ bugum n ka kĩŋri tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm tɩ a kẽ'esɛ Asũtãana, la a malɛkadõma la pʋam.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa bibɛ'a ayɩla n daan boe yʋ'a deem, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Abarabasɩ, tɩ nẽrba wuu mina a yelle.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam kɔm daan dõnnɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma ka bɔ mam dia. Tɩ koyũuro daan tara mam, tɩ yãma ka bɔ mam ko'om tɩ mam yũ.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n lagsɛ la, tɩ Apilatɩ soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne tɩ yãma boorɩ tɩ m yese basɛ bɔ ya, Abarabasɩ bɩɩ Azezi tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Krisi la?».

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: Mam daan de la sãana, tɩ yãma ka to'oge mam. Mam daan de la beele, tɩ yãma ka bɔ mam futo tɩ mam yɛ. Mam daan bẽ'erɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma ka wa'am wa bɩsɛ mam. Mam daan boe la yʋ'a deem, tɩ yãma ka wa'am mam zẽ'am.›.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Apilatɩ daan mi tɩ la de la sũure ĩyã tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi wa bɔ ẽŋa.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: La ba wʋn lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, la de la wakat-kãna tɩ tõma daan yẽ fõn, tɩ fõn kɔm dõnnɩ, bɩɩ koyũuro tarɩ fõn, bɩɩ fõn de sãana, bɩɩ beele, bɩɩ fõn bẽ'erɩ, bɩɩ fõn boe yʋ'a deem, dee tɩ tõma ka sõŋɛ fõn?›.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: La Apilatɩ n daan zĩ sarɩyadia zẽ'am na, tɩ a pɔga tõm tɩ ba wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yese fʋ nu'ugo pʋpeelem dãana la yelle pʋam, se'ere n soe la, mam zãasʋm mɛ a yelle yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ la nãmsɛ mam zozo'e.».

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Tɩ a wʋn lerge ba yetɩ: ‹Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ yãma n daan ka ɩtɩ bɛla bɔ'ɔra mam nẽrba la, baa la sãn dɛna la nẽr-pɩka ayɩla tɩ ya ka ẽŋɛ bɔ ẽ, la de la mam tɩ ya ka ẽŋɛ bɔ.›.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma daan yele nẽrba la mɛ tɩ ba sose Apilatɩ tɩ a basɛ Abarabasɩ bɔ ba, dee kʋ Azezi.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: La bãma wʋn sẽŋɛ ka kẽ nãmsgɔ n ka ba'asrɩ pʋam. La sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ la wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Tɩ gɔmna la le soke ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna bayi wã, yãma boorɩ tɩ m basɛ la ãne bɔ ya?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Abarabasɩ.».

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Bɛla, yãma boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ Azezi tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Krisi la, la wãne?» Tɩ ba wuu lerge yetɩ: «Ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.».

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «A ẽŋɛ la bẽm n de be'em?» Tɩ ba malɩn tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.».

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: La Apilatɩ n yẽ tɩ ẽŋa kãn yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ sɛla, dee tɩ nẽrba la gita pa'asra la, tɩ a dɩkɛ ko'om peege a nu'usi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu nẽŋam, dee yele yetɩ: «Mam nẽŋa ka boe ẽna wã kʋa pʋam. La de la yãma yelle.».

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu lerge yetɩ: «A kʋa la taalɛ bɔna tõma la tɩ kɔma zuto.».

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ basɛ Abarabasɩ bɔ ba, dee botɩ ba wẽ Azezi la ka'asa, dee dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ tɩ ba ka ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Tɩ gɔmna la sʋdaasɩ tarɩ Azezi kẽ gɔmna la yire, tɩ sʋdaasɩ wuu lagsɛ kaagɛ ẽ.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Tɩ ba yɛɛsɛ Azezi futo, dee yɛ ẽ fuugo n de gomusga.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: dɩkɛ gõ'osɩ wʋgɛ mũŋa vuge ẽ, dɩkɛ dasaarɛ zãntɛ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo, yãŋa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam la'ara ẽ yetɩ: «Zifdõma naba, naa!».

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, dabsa fẽe n tole, tɩ Azezi lebe Kapɛrnaʋm. Tɩ nẽrba wõm tɩ a boe yire.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: La ba daan tʋbsrɩ la nõtõorʋm ɩta ẽ, dee tɔ'ɔsra dasaarɛ la wẽ'era a zuugo.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: lagsɛ halɩ zozo'e tɩ zẽ'a ka bɔna, baa deego la nõorʋm. Tɩ a tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: La ban la ẽ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, ba yɛɛgɛ gomusga fuugo la mɛ, dee dɩkɛ a mẽŋa fuugo la lebe yɛ ẽ, tara ẽ wɛ'ɛsa tɩ ba ka ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa Azezi-krisi kõ-yẽlga n pɔsɛ se'em n wãna:.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Tɩ nẽrba banaasɩ zɛ kɔɔrŋɔ wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ban daan ze'ele tẽŋa pʋam yɛsra la, ba tʋ'ʋsɛ la bʋraaga ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asimõ n ze'ele Sirɛnɩ, tɩ ba pẽrgɛ ẽ tɩ a to'oge Azezi kũm dɔ-puurŋa la buke.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: La ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn põn gʋlsɛ se'em, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, mam wʋn tõm m tõntõnna tɩ a dẽŋɛ fʋ nẽŋa ka malgɛ sore bɔ fɔ.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: La nẽrba la n zo'oge la, ba ka tã wʋn zɛ ẽ paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am, tɩ ba zom gɔsgɔ ka lakɛ gɔsgɔ la bɔbrɛ ayɩla, doose bilam sike kɔɔrŋɔ la, la a bõn-gã'ala deego la pʋam Azezi nẽŋam.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: La de la sɛka n mõolɩ weem tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: ‹Malgɛ-ya sore sõŋa gu'ura Zuudãana. Malgɛ-ya sɔa la tɩ ba tɔ tẽntẽesɩ bɔ ẽ.› ».

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Tɩ ba paagɛ zẽ'a tɩ ba wi'iri a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Golgota, a võorɛ de la zuwaŋrɛ zẽ'a.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ ban bɔ ẽŋa sɩra se'em na, yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «M bia, fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna fʋ zuo.».

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Bɛla tɩ Azã daan bɔna weem misra nẽrba ko'om pʋam, dee mõola yetɩ: «Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee botɩ ba mise ya ko'om pʋam, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ.».

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: dɩkɛ dãam n gaam na tɩɩm n ãn toogo bɔ Azezi tɩ a yũ. Tɩ a leme ba, zagsɛ ka yũ.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: La lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba n daan boe mĩ, daan zẽa tẽ'esra ba sũurum yetɩ:.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e ze'ele Zude so'olʋm wuu, la Zerizalɛm wuu sẽnna Azã zẽ'am tɔgra ba tõon-be'ero pa'ala ẽ. Tɩ Azã misra ba kʋlaa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Zʋrdẽ pʋam.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Tɩ ba daan ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, dee dɩkɛ a futo tɩ ba pʋɩ la taaba, pɔsɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ nẽra woo n wʋn to'oge sɛka.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: «Bẽm tɩ ẽna tɔgra bɛla? A tɔgrɩ pɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ. Ãn tã wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ mã'a?».

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Azã daan yɛ la fuugo n de yʋgma kõbrɔ, dee dɩkɛ sagãnnɛ lʋ a sɩa. A daan diti la seero la saniiro.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Dee yãŋa zẽa gu'ura ẽ.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ a pʋtẽ'erʋm ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em ba sũurum na, yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya tẽ'esra wãna ya sũurum?.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: A daan mõolɩ yetɩ: «Nẽra n boe mam poorʋm sẽna, ẽŋa tarɩ pãŋa gãnna mam mɛ. Mam ka masɛ tɩ m dõbrɛ a nẽŋam pirge a tagra.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Ba daan gʋlsɛ la gʋlsgɔ pa'alɛ se'ere n soe tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, lablɛ a zuum dɔ-puurŋa la zuo. Ba daan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Ẽna de la Azezi n de Zifdõma naba la.».

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Bẽm n de nãana gãnna, mam sãn yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ, a tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna a zuo, bɩɩ mam sãn yele ẽ yetɩ, a isge dɩkɛ a bõn-gã'ala sẽnna n de nãana?.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mam misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la ẽŋa wʋn mise ya la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.».

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Ba daan tarɩ la fãarba bayi wa po ka dɔ-puursi zuto, ayɩla Azezi zʋʋgɔ bɔba, ayẽma a gɔbga bɔba.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Bɛla m wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la tarɩ pãŋa dũnia wã zuo mɛ, wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ.» Azezi yele bɛla, dee yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ:.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa Azezi ze'ele la Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm wa'am Azã zẽ'am, tɩ a mise ẽ Zʋrdẽ ko'om na pʋam.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Tɩ sɛba n dolɩ bilam tɔlla la, lɔbra ba zuto dee tʋʋra ẽ.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: «Isge dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala kule.».

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele ko'om na pʋam dɔɔra, yẽ tɩ saazuum darɛ yo'oge, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ãna wʋ na'adawenne na sige a zuo.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: yetɩ: «Dagɩ fõn daan yetɩ fõn wʋn wõrgɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, dee le mẽ ẽ dabsa atã pʋam? Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fãagɛ fʋ mẽŋa ze'ele dɔ-puurŋa la zuo sige.».

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Tɩ kɔɔrŋɔ la isge kalam dɩkɛ a bõn-gã'ala la ba wuu nifum yese. Tɩ ba wuu zɛ yɛrɩ, pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Tõma nãn ka yẽ wãna.».

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: tɩ kõa ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Fõn de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu, fʋ paagɛ la mam yɛm.».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma mẽ daan tʋʋrɩ ẽ mɛ yetɩ:.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi le kẽ la Zifdõma wẽndeem. Tɩ bʋraa bɔna mĩ tɩ a nu'ugo ki.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Azezi le sẽŋɛ la mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e sẽna a zẽ'am, tɩ a zãsna ba.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ weem.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: «A daan fãagrɩ nẽrba basɛba mɛ, dee ka tãna wʋn fãagɛ a mẽŋa. A sãn dɛna Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma naba, a ze'ele dɔ-puurŋa la zuo sige nãnannɛ wã, tɩ tõma wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Tɩ ba bɩsra Azezi wʋn botɩ bʋraa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a kãn botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Ba daan eerɩ la ba doose bilam dõrɛ ẽ.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: La Azezi sẽnnɩ tɔlla mɛ yẽ Alfe dayʋa Alevi tɩ a zĩ lampo to'osgo deem tɔ'ɔsra lampo, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dɔla mam.» Tɩ a isge dɔla ẽ.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: A daan boe weem na dabsa pinaasɩ, tɩ Asũtãana wa'am wa makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ. A daan boe la we-dũnsi lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma sõŋra ẽ.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: A te'ege Nawẽnnɛ mɛ yetɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, Nawẽnnɛ sãn nõŋɛ ẽ, a fãagɛ ẽ lɛɛlɛ wã.».

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele bʋraa la nu'ugo n ki la yetɩ: «Isge ze'ele tẽŋasʋka.».

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Alevi yire dita, tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba la tõon-be'ero dõma zozo'e lagɩm na Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la dita, se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãma buuri n daan zo'oge dɔla ẽ.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Ba daan yõkɛ Azã yʋ la yʋ'a deego pʋam. Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Galile ka mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga yetɩ:.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Tɩ fãarba la tɩ ba ka dɔ-puursi zuto lɛm ẽ na mẽ tɔgra ẽ be'em.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Dee soke sɛba n bɩsrɩ ẽ na yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ nẽra ẽŋɛ sõŋa vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a ẽŋɛ be'em? Sore boe tɩ a fãagɛ nẽra vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a kʋ ẽ?» Tɩ ba sĩna.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n de Farisi dõma la yẽ Azezi n diti la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma la, tɩ ba soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ a lagɩm na lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma dita?».

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: «Wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɛm mɛ. Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero dee sakɛ kõ-yẽlga la.».

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: La daan pɔsɛ la wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka, tɩ tẽŋa la wuu lebge lika tarɩ ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ karf-atã.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Tɩ Azezi sũure yĩige, tɩ a bɩsɛ nẽrba la kaagɛ, tɩ a sũure sã'am, ba sũure n kɛ la ĩyã. La a yele bʋraa la yetɩ: «Tẽegɛ fʋ nu'ugo la.» Tɩ a tẽegɛ, tɩ nu'ugo la yãŋa tẽegɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Tɩ Azezi wõm bɛla, yele ba yetɩ: «Sɛba n tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm ka eerɩ tɩba, sɛba n bẽ'erɩ n eerɩ tɩba. Mam ka wa'am tɩ m wi sɛba dõma n yetɩ bãma masɛ mɛ na, mam wa'am na tõon-be'ero dõma ĩyã.».

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Azezi daan dolɩ la Galile mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, yẽ Asimõ la a yɩbga A'ãndre n boe mĩ lɔbra yʋka mõgrɛ la pʋam, ba daan de la zũn-yõgrɩba.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Karf-atã la wakatɛ tɩ Azezi tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani.» La võorɛ de la: «Mam Wẽnnaam, mam Wẽnnaam, bẽm tɩ fõn basɛ mam?».

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma yese tɔtɔ ka bãm la naba A'erɔdɩ nẽrba bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em kʋ Azezi.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Azã karẽnbiisi, la Farisi dõma daan lʋtɩ la nõorɛ. Tɩ ba wa'am wa soke Azezi yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi, la Farisi dõma karẽnbiisi la lʋta nõorɛ dee tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka lʋta nõorɛ?».

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Dɔla-ya mam, tɩ mam wʋn botɩ ya ɛɛra nẽrba tɩ ba dɔla mam wʋ yãma n yõgrɩ zũma se'em na.».

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Tɩ sɛba n ze lɛm bilam na basɛba wõm bɛla, yetɩ: «A wi'iri la A'eli.».

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la yese sẽŋɛ la Galile mõgrʋm bɔba. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e ze'ele Galile, la Zude.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ pɔgdire kibsa wi a sɩrdõma tɩ ba wa'am, ba tã wʋn lʋ nõorɛ mɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ pɔgdita la ken bɔna ba zẽ'am na bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, pɔgdita la sãn ken bɔna ba zẽ'am, ba kãn tã'agɛ lʋ nõorɛ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Tɩ ba basɛ ba yʋgsɩ la bilam mẽŋa, dee dɔla ẽ.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla zoe sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ nẽtɩŋa lʋsɛ dã-mĩ'isgo pʋam zẽkɛ la dɔɔgɔ bɔ ẽ tɩ a yũ.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: la Zerizalɛm, la Idume, la Zʋrdẽ kʋlaa doone, la tẽn-sɛba n lɛm Tiiri la Sidõ doose ẽ. Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la daan wõm na ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ ba wʋn yese pɔgdita la ba zẽ'am, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yãŋa wʋn lʋ nõorɛ.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ fẽe yẽ Azebede dayɔɔsɩ Azakɩ la a yɩbga Azã n boe õorŋɔ pʋam malgra ba yʋgsɩ.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ gu bɩsɛ A'eli la sãn wʋn wa'am wa fãagɛ ẽ.».

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, ba vuta õorŋɔ lɛm na tɩ nẽrba la da wa mike ẽ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Nẽra kãn dɩkɛ tãn-paalɛ lige fu-kɛka. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, tãn-paalga la wʋn botɩ fu-kɛka la ãagɛ mɛ gãnna kʋrʋm na.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Tɩ a darɛ wi ba, tɩ ba basɛ ba sɔ Azebede la a tõntõnɩba õorŋɔ la pʋam, dee dɔla ẽ.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Tɩ Azezi le kaasɛ ke'enke'em, dee ki.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: A daan botɩ bã'adõma zozo'e n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, bɛla tɩ bã'adõma wuu daan mikra taaba bɔɔra tɩ ba paagɛ kalʋm ẽ.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: La nẽra mẽ kãn dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm wɔ-kɛgsɩ pʋam. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, dã-paalga la sãn kɛlla, ba wʋn botɩ wɔɔsɩ la tãsɛ mɛ, tɩ dãam na, la wɔɔsɩ la wuu sã'am. La masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm na wɔ-paalsɩ pʋam.».

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi sẽŋɛ Kapɛrnaʋm. Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ paagɛ, tɩ Azezi kẽ ba wẽndeem zãsna nẽrba.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Tɩ fuugo la n yuli Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam beŋe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la ãagɛ pʋsʋka saazuum halɩ ka paagɛ tẽŋa. Tɩ tẽŋa mĩim, tɩ tãmpĩisi wõrɛ.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: La kʋlkã'arsɩ n dolɩ nẽr-sɛba la sãn ẽn yẽ ẽ, ba luti la a nẽŋam kaasra tagursi yɛta tɩ: «Fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.».

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi daan dɔla vatɔ pʋam tɔlla, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la kɔ si wẽena ɔbra.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: La zãsŋɔ la di'ige nẽrba la mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a zãsŋɔ la daan tarɩ la pãŋa, la ka ãn wʋ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n zãsnɩ se'em na.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Tɩ yɔɔrɔ be'ese, tɩ pʋpeelem dõma zozo'e n yʋʋn ki vo'oge.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Tɩ Azezi daan zɛbɛ kʋlkã'arsɩ la yetɩ, ba da botɩ nẽra bãŋɛ ẽŋa n de nẽr-sɛka.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, bẽm tɩ ba ɩta sɛla n sisri vo'osgo daarɛ?».

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Tɩ bʋraa daan bɔna wẽndeem na tɩ kʋlkã'arga tara ẽ, tɩ bʋraa la kaasɛ yetɩ:.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Tɩ Azezi n wa vo'oge la, tɩ ba ze'ele yɔɔrɔ pʋam yese kẽ Zerizalɛm, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yẽ ba.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: La bɛla poorʋm Azezi daan sẽŋɛ ka zom na tãŋa zuo, dee wi nẽr-sɛba tɩ a boorɩ la tɩ ba wa'am a zẽ'am.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma nãn ka karẽŋɛ Adavidi la a nẽrba la kɔm n yʋʋn dõnnɩ tɩ ba bɔɔra dia tɩ ba di, tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: «Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra, fõn bẽm yelle n boe la tõma? Fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ sã'am na tõma? Mam mi fõn de sɛka. Fõn de la pʋpeelem dãana n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.».

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: La Rom sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛma, la sʋdaasɩ la n ze gu'ura Azezi la n yẽ tẽŋa la n mĩim, la bõn-sɛba n ẽŋɛ la, dabeem n yõkɛ ba zozo'e, tɩ ba yetɩ: «A sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.».

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Tɩ a looge nẽrba pia la ayi tɩ ba bɔna la ẽ, tɩ a tõnna ba tɩ ba sẽnna mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: wakat-sɛka tɩ Abiata de Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la? A yʋʋn kẽ la Nawẽnnɛ deego la pʋam dɩkɛ borborɩ sɛba tɩ ba bĩŋe bɔ Nawẽnnɛ na obe. La sisri mɛ, nẽra nẽra ka obrɩ ba, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma mã'a. La Adavidi yʋʋn obe ba mɛ, dee bɔ nẽr-sɛba n boe a zẽ'am na.».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Tɩ Azezi tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Go'oge, yese dee basɛ ẽ.».

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: La pɔgsɩ zozo'e n daan ze zãazãarɛ dee bɩsra, ba daan ze'ele la Galile doose Azezi tɩ ba sõŋra ẽ.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: dee bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba digra kʋlkã'arsɩ tɩ ba yɛsra.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee le yele ba yetɩ: «La de la nẽrsaala ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ vo'osgo daarɛ, a ka ẽŋɛ nẽrsaala vo'osgo daarɛ ĩyã.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la bigse bʋraa la tɩ a gõgra, dee kaasɛ tagurga yese dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Amaari n de Azakɩ la Azozɛfʋ ma, la Azebede dayɔɔsɩ la ma n daan boe pɔg-bãma pʋam.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Sɛba tɩ a looge la daan de la Asimõ tɩ Azezi sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Bɛla, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n soe sɛla woo, baa la vo'osgo daarɛ.».

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Tɩ ba wuu zɛ yɛrɩ, sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Wãna de la bẽm? La de la zãsŋɔ paalga n tarɩ pãŋa. A tɔgrɩ la kʋlkã'arsɩ, tɩ ba sakra a nõorɛ.».

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Bʋraaga ayɩla n daan de bõntarsa, n ze'ele Arimate, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azozɛfʋ. A de la Azezi karẽnbia.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: la Azebede dayɔɔsɩ Azakɩ la a yɩbga Azã, tɩ a sɩ ba yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Bʋanɛrgɛsɩ. A võorɛ de la nẽrba n ãn wʋ sa-dãmŋɔ.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Wẽnnɛ n daan mã'agɛ, tɩ a sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am ka sose ẽ tɩ a basɛ tɩ a dɩkɛ Azezi kũm na sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ. Tɩ Apilatɩ bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba bɔ ẽ.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Tɩ Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ darɛ saagɛ Galile so'olʋm zẽ'a woo.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: La A'ãndre, la Afilipi, la Abatelemi, la Amatie, la Atoma, la Alfe dayʋa Azakɩ, la Atade, la Asimõ sɛka n de Zelɔtɩ nẽra ayɩla la.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ dɩkɛ kũm na, dɩkɛ tãn-paalɛ n ãn sõŋa vile ẽ.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Tɩ Azezi la a nẽrba daan yese Zifdõma wẽndeem na, tɩ Azakɩ la Azã doose ba, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Asimõ la A'ãndre dõma yire.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: la Azudaasɩ Iskariyo, sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi la.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: tarɩ ẽ ka laagɛ yɔ-paalɛ pʋam tɩ a botɩ ba tũ tãmpĩa ĩyã, a mẽŋa ĩyã. Tɩ ba bilge kug-kãtɛ lige yɔɔgɔ la nõorɛ dee fõrgɛ.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Tɩ Asimõ dɛɛm-pɔka ĩyã ba tʋlgɛ, tɩ a gã. Tɩ Azezi paagɛ, tɩ ba darɛ tɔgɛ pɔka la yelle bɔ ẽ.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: La Azezi daan kule la yire, tɩ nẽrba le lagsɛ zozo'e. Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la ka yẽ folle wʋn di.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Amaari ayẽma la daan zĩ mɛ tɩ ba nẽŋa tɔ yɔɔgɔ la.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ lɛm ẽ, gʋrɛ a nu'ugo botɩ a isge. Tɩ bã'a la basɛ ẽ, tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba sãanɔ.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Tɩ ba daan yetɩ Azezi yirsri mɛ, tɩ a yir-dõma wõm bɛla sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Daar-sɛka tɩ Zifdõma mãasnɩ ba vo'osgo daarɛ la n tole tɩ beere wiige, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma lagsɛ taaba sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ wa lui sobe, tɩ ba tarɩ bã'adõma wuu, la sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ tarɩ ba wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n ze'ele Zerizalɛm wa'am wa yetɩ: «Abelzebuli n dolɩ ẽ, la de la ẽŋa n de kʋlkã'arsɩ naba la pãŋa tɩ a tarɩ digra kʋlkã'arsɩ tɩ ba yɛsra.».

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma tẽrɩ tɩ põmpã'asʋm dãana wã, ẽn daan nãn vɔa la, a daan yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn vo'oge dabsa atã daarɛ.›.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba wuu lagsɛ taaba yire la zanõrʋm.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Tɩ Azezi wi ba, makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Asũtãana wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne dige Asũtãana?.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Bɛla, bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba gu'ura yɔɔgɔ la sõŋa sõŋa ka paagɛ dabsa atã daarɛ, la sãn dagna bɛla, a karẽnbiisi la wʋn wa zũ kũm na mɛ dee yele nẽrba tɩ a vo'oge mɛ. Pã'asgɔ ẽna wã yãŋa wʋn dɛna be'em gãnna pɔspɔsɩ la.».

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Tɩ Azezi botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tɩ bã'asɩ to'oto'ore tarɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee dige kʋlkã'arsɩ zozo'e, tɩ ba yese. La a ka sakɛ tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la tɔgɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba põn mina ẽ mɛ.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: So'olʋm ayɩla nẽrba sãn welge zɛbra taaba, so'olʋm ẽŋa wʋn wõrgɛ mɛ.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Tarɩ-ya gu'urba sẽŋɛ ka basɛ tɩ ba gu'ura yɔɔgɔ la wʋ yãma n boorɩ se'em na.».

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Azezi daan isge la bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ zẽ'a tɩ nẽrba ka boe, tɩ a ka pʋ'ʋsɛ.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: La yire ayɩla dõma sãn welge zɛbra taaba, yi-ẽŋa wʋn wõrgɛ mɛ.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka bɩ yɔɔgɔ la nõorɛ dee basɛ tɩ gu'urba gu'ura ẽ.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Tɩ Asimõ la nẽr-sɛba n daan boe la ẽ na yese ɛɛra ẽ.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: La Asũtãana sãn isge zɛbɛ la a mẽŋa welge, a pãŋa la ba'asɛ mɛ, a kãn tã'agɛ ze'ele.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: La ba daan yẽ ẽ mɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽrba wuu eerɩ fʋ mɛ.».

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: La nẽra ka tã wʋn kẽ pãŋa dãana yire ka vaagɛ a lɔgrɔ, sãn dagna tɩ a dẽŋɛ lʋ pãŋa dãana la mɛ, dee vaagɛ a lɔgrɔ la.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛba n lɛm na tɩ m ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bilam mẽ, tɩ la de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam wa'am.».

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn basɛ nẽrsaalba tõon-be'ero la wuu tɩ ba ɩtɩ la, la ba pɔ'ɔrɛ yetɔga la wuu taalɛ.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Tɩ a kaagɛ Galile so'olʋm wuu mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, dee dige kʋlkã'arsɩ tɩ ba yese dee basɛ nẽrba.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: La nẽra sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, Nawẽnnɛ kãn malɩn basɛ ẽ taalɛ. Tõon-be'e-ẽna taalɛ boe la a dãana zuo wakatɛ wuu.».

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Bʋraa ayɩla n de kõnkonne n wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn bɔɔra, fʋ tã wʋn basɛ tɩ m yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ le ãna sõŋa.».

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Ban yetɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ na ĩyã, tɩ a yele bɛla.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ Azezi, tɩ a tẽegɛ a nu'ugo kalʋm ẽ yetɩ: «Mam bɔɔra, yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa.».

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Tɩ Azezi ma, la a yɩbsɩ wa'am wa zɛ yẽŋa dee tõm yetɩ, ba wi ẽ tɩ a wa'am.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Tɩ bã'a la basɛ ẽ bilam mẽŋa, tɩ a le ãna sõŋa.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n daan zĩ kaagɛ ẽ, tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ ma, la fʋ yɩbsɩ n wãna n boe yẽŋa ɛɛra fɔ.».

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ a fõrgɛ, dee kã'an ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ãne n de mam ma, la ãndõma n de mam yɩbsɩ?».

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: «Da kãn yele nẽra nẽra, la sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽema zẽ'am ka pa'alɛ ẽ fɔ mẽŋa tɩ a bɩsɛ, dee bɔ ẽ bõn-sɛba tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yetɩ ba bɔ la, tɩ la pa'alɛ ba tɩ fʋ sɩrɩ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Dee bɩsɛ nẽr-sɛba n zĩ kaagɛ ẽ na, yetɩ: «Mam ma, la mam yɩbsɩ n wãna.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: La bʋraa la fõrgɛ mɛ ka dɩkɛ yelle la mõolɛ pa'alɛ la ẽŋɛ se'em. Bɛla tɩ Azezi daan ka le tãna wʋn kẽ tẽŋa pʋam, sãn dagna tɩ a li'ise mɛ kẽ. A daan boe la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ nẽrba ka boe mĩ na, tɩ nẽrba daan zɛ'ɛta zẽ'a woo sẽnna a zẽ'am.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na n de mam yɩbga, la mam tã, la mam ma.».

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Azezi le bɔna la mõgrɛ la nõorʋm zãsna nẽrba. Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e lagsɛ a zẽ'am. Bɛla tɩ a kẽ õorŋɔ la n boe mõgrɛ la pʋam na zĩ'ire, dee tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu bɔna mõgrɛ la nõorʋm.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Tɩ a makɛ magsa zãsʋm ba yɛla zozo'e. A zãsŋɔ la pʋam a yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: «Selse-ya, kaara n daan yese tɩ a lobge a bõnbuuri.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: La ẽn sẽŋɛ ka lɔbgra bõnbuuri la, basɛba lui la sore zuo, tɩ niinto wa'am wa di ba.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Tɩ basɛba lui zẽ'a n de tãmpĩisi tɩ tẽntõnnɔ ka zo'oge mĩ. Tẽntõnnɔ n ka zo'oge mĩ na ĩyã, tɩ ba bule tɔtɔ yese.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa yẽkɛ wẽ ba tɩ ba ki, ba yẽga n ka kẽ tẽŋa la ĩyã.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Tɩ bõnbuuri basɛba lui gõ'osɩ zẽ'a. Tɩ gõ'osɩ la yese fʋrɛ ba, tɩ ba ka wɔm biɛ.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Tɩ bõnbuuri basɛba lui zẽ'e-sõŋɔ pʋam bule yese, bɩ wɔm biɛ. Basɛba wɔm na biɛ pitã-tã, tɩ basɛba wɔm pisyoob-yoobɩ, tɩ basɛba wɔm kɔbga kɔbga.».

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: A yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: La Azezi yese la kẽnkɛrŋa tɩ sɛba n kaagɛ ẽ na, la a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la soke ẽ magsa la võorɛ.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ botɩ yãma bãŋɛ a so'olʋm yelle n sʋgɛ la mɛ. La sɛba n ka boe tõma pʋam na, yɛla wuu de la magsa bɔ ba.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: ‹Bɛla tɩ ba wʋn bɩsɛ yẽ dee kãn bãŋɛ. Ba wʋn wõm dee kãn bãŋɛ a võorɛ. Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ba wʋn leme wa'am Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, tɩ a basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.› ».

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: La Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma sãn ka bãŋɛ makrɛ ẽna wã võorɛ, yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ magsa la wuu võorɛ?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Bõnbuuri la tɩ kaara la lobge la, de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Sore la zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la, tɩ Asũtãana wa'am tɔtɔ wa yese yetɔga la ba sũure pʋam.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: La tãmpĩa la zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la dee sakɛ to'oge ba tɔtɔ la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: La yetɔga la n ka kẽ ba sũure pʋam na ĩyã, ba sakɛ la wakatɛ fẽe, la toogo bɩɩ nãmsgɔ sãn paagɛ ba yetɔga la ĩyã, ba basrɩ mɛ tɔtɔ.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: La gõ'osɩ la zẽ'a tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: tɩ ba yɛm yɔɔra la dũnia wã yɛla, tɩ bõntarsõm yɛla pã'asra ba, tɩ yɛla basɛba yɛmleego tara ba, botɩ yetɔga la ka tãna wʋn wɔm biɛ ba sũure pʋam.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: La zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõnnɩ kõ-yẽlga la sakra, wɔm biɛ. Tɩ basɛba wɔm pitã-tã, tɩ basɛba pisyoob-yoobɩ, tɩ basɛba kɔbga kɔbga.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Ba yõ'orɩ fɩtla dɩkɛ pɩ'ɩgɔ vuge ẽ, bɩɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ ze'ele gɔrgɔ tilum? Ayɛɩ, ba wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ zoole la sɛla zuo.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Bɛla, bõn-sɛka n sʋgɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ a puke. La bõn-sɛka wuu n lile wʋn lilge.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: A le yele ba yetɩ: «Mõrgɛ-ya wõm yãma n selsrɩ se'em na võorɛ. Bõn-sɛka tɩ ya dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ nẽrba la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn makɛ la bɛla lebse ya, le makɛ pa'asɛ.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sɛka n tarɩ, a wʋn bɔ ẽ pa'asɛ. La sɛka n ka tarɩ, bõn-sɛka tɩ a tarɩ la, a wʋn to'oge ẽ.».

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ nẽra n bʋrɛ a bõnbuuri va'am pʋam.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: A sãn gĩsra mɛ bɩɩ a sãn piga mɛ, wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ, bõnbuuri la bulli mɛ yɛsra bɩta, tɩ a ka mina lan ɩtɩ se'em.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Tẽntõnnɔ n botɩ bõnbuuri la yɛsra ba mẽŋa, dẽŋɛ puse võorɔ, yãŋa yõgɛ pʋa, wɔm biɛ, tɩ ba bɩ.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: La biɛ la sãn bɩ, kaara la sɛ'ɛrɩ ba mɛ, se'ere n soe la, bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ paagɛ mɛ.».

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm, bɩɩ tɩ wʋn yetɩ a wõn na bẽm?.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: A ãn wʋ sɔ'ɔrɔ biire n ãn pɩka pɩka gãnna bõnbuuri wuu n boe dũnia zuo.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: La ba sãn bʋrɛ ẽ, a bɩtɩ mɛ puse wil-kãra gãnna zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu. Tɩ niinto tãna wʋn tɛ ba tʋgrɔ a zuo a mã'asʋm pʋam.».

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Azezi daan dɩkɛ la magsa zozo'e n ãn wʋ bãna tɔgɛ bɔ ba, masɛ wʋ ban wʋn tã'agɛ wõm se'em na.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: A ka tɔgɛ bɔ ba tɩ la sãn dagna magsa. La a sãn yese la a karẽnbiisi la mã'a kẽnkɛrŋa, tɩ a pa'alɩ ba yɛla la wuu võorɛ.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Daar-ẽŋa mẽŋa zaanõorɛ, tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Tɩ dooge-ya mõgrɛ la ke bɔba.».

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la basɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, dee sẽŋɛ ka kẽ õorŋɔ la tɩ Azezi boe mĩ na pʋam, dɩkɛ ẽ tɩ ba wɛ'ɛsa. La õornɔ basɛba n daan mẽ boe ba zẽ'am.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ wa vugra ke'enke'em, tɩ ko-mĩ'isi wẽ'era kẽ'era õorŋɔ la pʋam deege la ba pɩrɛ ẽ.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: La Azezi gã la õorŋɔ la poorʋm kʋkɛ dafeere gĩsra. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la nẽegɛ ẽ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma n yetɩ ki wã, la ka pakɛ fõn?».

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Tɩ a isge tãsɛ kʋsebgo la, dee yele ko-mĩ'isi la yetɩ: «Go'oge-ya, ãna-ya sĩm.» Tɩ kʋsebgo, la ko-mĩ'isi la go'oge ãna sĩm.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya zɔta dabeem bɛla? Bẽm tɩ ya ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra?».

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Tɩ dabeem ba kẽ ba. Tɩ ba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ãne n de ẽna tɩ kʋsebgo la ko'om sakra a nõorɛ?».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: La Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la dooge paagɛ la mõgrɛ la doone Geraza dõma tẽŋa pʋam.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele õorŋɔ la pʋam sige. Tɩ bʋraa tɩ kʋlkã'arga dolɩ ẽ darɛ ze'ele yɔɔrʋm sẽnna tʋ'ʋsra ẽ.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: Bʋraa la daan kẽ'erɩ la yɔɔrɔ la tẽŋasʋka. Tɩ ba daan ka le tãna wʋn ba ẽ na bãnsɩ.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba daan ẽn ba la a nãma la a nu'usi la bãnsɩ, tɩ a ẽn kɔ ba basɛ. Tɩ nẽra nẽra daan ka tãna wʋn yãŋɛ ẽ.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ a ẽn ɛɛra la yɔɔrʋm, la tãnsɩ tẽŋasʋka kaasra tagursi, dee wẽ'era a mẽŋa la kuga pɔgna.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Tɩ a yẽ Azezi zãazãarɛ, zoe sẽŋɛ ka ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: dee tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Azezi, Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu Dayʋa, bẽm n boe mam na fõn tẽŋasʋka? Mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã, da nãmsɛ ma.».

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Azezi dẽŋɛ yele kʋlkã'arga la mɛ yetɩ a yese dee basɛ ẽ. Bɛla n soe tɩ kʋlkã'arga la yele bɛla.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Mam yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Atʋsa, se'ere n soe la, tõma zo'oge mɛ.».

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Tɩ a belem Azezi yetɩ, a da dige bãma tɩ ba yese tẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: La kurkurdõma zozo'e n daan boe tãŋa zuo ɔbra.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la belem Azezi yetɩ: «Bɔ tɩ nõorɛ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la.».

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Tɩ a sakɛ ban yele se'em na. Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la basɛ bʋraa la dee sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la. Tɩ ba wuu zoe sige ka lui mõgrɛ pʋam ki. Ba kãllɛ daan de wʋ tʋsayi.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Tɩ sɛba n daan gu'uri kurkurdõma la zoe sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam, la kẽnkɛrsɩ la wuu ka yele sɛla n ẽŋɛ la. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka bɩsɛ lan ẽŋɛ se'em.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: La ban paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am na, ba yẽ bʋraa la tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la daan dolɩ ẽ na n zĩ mĩ, yɛ futo, tɩ a yɛm bɔna sõŋa sõŋa. Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Tɩ sɛba n yẽ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na, tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ bʋraa la, la bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ kurkurdõma la.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Tɩ ba belem Azezi yetɩ a fõrgɛ bãma tẽŋa.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Tɩ Azezi daan kẽ'era õorŋɔ la pʋam, tɩ bʋraa la tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ daan dolɩ ẽ na belem Azezi yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ a dɔla ẽ.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Tɩ Azezi ka sakɛ, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Kule ya yire ka yele fʋ yir-dõma Zuudãana n zoe fʋ ninbãalga se'em, la ẽn ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu bɔ fʋ la.».

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la sẽŋɛ Dekapɔlɩ so'olʋm ka mõola Azezi n ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛba wuu bɔ ẽŋa. Tɩ la daan di'ige ba wuu.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Azezi daan lebe dooge la mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba la õorŋɔ la, daan bɔna mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e lagsɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadõma nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azayirusi wa'am mĩ wa yẽ Azezi, ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: belem ẽ na a sũure wuu yetɩ: «Mam pɔyʋa n bẽ'erɩ deege la a ki, wa'am wa dɩkɛ fʋ nu'usi paglɛ a zuo tɩ a põsɛ da ki.».

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Tɩ Azezi dɔla ẽ wɛ'ɛsa, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ dɔla ẽ mikra ẽ.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: La pɔka n daan boe nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka luta sɩkẽŋa yʋʋma pia la ayi, tɩ la ka gɔ'ɔra.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: A daan nãmsɛ mɛ zozo'e tɩbdõma zẽ'am, dɩkɛ ẽn tarɩ sɛla wuu sã'am, dee tɩ la ka sõna, la malɩn gãnna la bɛla.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Tɩ pɔka la daan wõm Azezi yelle, wa'am wa fɩrgɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tẽŋasʋka paagɛ Azezi poorʋm kalʋm a fuugo.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: A daan yele a pʋtẽ'erʋm yetɩ: «Mam sãn kalʋm a fuugo mã'a, mam wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: La ẽn kalʋm fuugo la, a zɩɩm na n yesrɩ la ze'ele mɛ ka le yɛsra, tɩ a bãŋɛ a ĩyã pʋam tɩ ẽŋa yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: La bilam mẽŋa tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ tɩ pãŋa n yese ẽŋa pʋam ẽŋɛ sɛla, tɩ a gelge soke nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Ãn kalʋm mam fuugo?».

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n mikri fõn wãna, dee sɔkra tɩ ãn kalʋm fõn.».

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩsɛ kaagɛ tɩ a bãŋɛ nẽr-sɛka n ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Tɩ dabeem tara pɔka la, tɩ a gõgra. La ẽn bãŋɛ sɛla n ẽŋɛ ẽ na, a wa'am mɛ wa ka dũma tẽŋa Azezi nẽŋam tɔgɛ ẽ a sɩra wuu.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «M bia, fõn bɔ mam sɩra la n botɩ fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Sẽŋɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm, fʋ bã'a la basɛ ya.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: La Azezi n ken bɔna mĩ tɔgra la, tɩ nẽrba ze'ele Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana la yire wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ pɔyʋa la ki mɛ. Da le nãmsɛ karẽnsãama.».

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Tɩ Azezi wõm ban yele se'em na, dee yele Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana la yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, wen bɔ mam sɩra.».

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: La Azezi daan ka basɛ tɩ nẽra nẽra kɔlgɛ ẽ, sãn dagna Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la a yɩbga Azã mã'a.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: La ban sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana yire la, tɩ Azezi yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n voolɩ, kaasra dee fabla zozo'e.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Tɩ a kẽ yire la yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya vɔɔla dee kɛlla? Bia la ka ki, a gĩsri mɛ.».

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Tɩ ba la'ara ẽ. Tɩ a botɩ nẽrba la wuu yese yẽŋa, dee doose bia la sɔ, la a ma, la a karẽnbiisi batã la kẽ bia la n boe zẽ'a na.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: gʋrɛ a nu'ugo dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Talita kumi.» A võorɛ de la: «Pugla, mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ fʋ isge.».

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Tɩ pugla la daan darɛ isge sẽnna. Tɩ la di'ige nẽrba la, tɩ ba zɛ yɛrɩ. Pugla la daan tarɩ la yʋʋma pia la ayi.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Tɩ Azezi daan kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da kãn botɩ nẽra nẽra bãŋɛ bõn-ẽŋa. Dee yetɩ, ba bɔ pugla la dia tɩ a di.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Azezi daan basɛ tẽn-ẽŋa dee sẽŋɛ la a mẽŋa tẽŋam, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la doose ẽ.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ a kẽ ba wẽndeem zãsna nẽrba. Tɩ nẽrba pãɩ wõm a zãsŋɔ la, tɩ la di'ige ba, tɩ ba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna yẽ zãsŋɔ ẽna la bɛ? Ãne bɔ ẽ yɛm ẽna? A ẽŋɛ la wãne ɩta bõn-yãlma bɛla?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Dagɩ ẽna n de dɔ-wãta la? Dagɩ ẽna n de Amaari dayʋa, tɩ a yɩbsɩ dɛna Azakɩ, la Azoze, la Azudi, la Asimõ la? Dagɩ a tãpa n boe tõma zẽ'am kalam wã?» Bɛla tɩ ba zagsɛ ka bɔ Azezi sɩra.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa yẽtɩ pẽka mɛ, sãn dagna a mẽŋa tẽŋa, la a buuri, la a yir-dõma tẽŋasʋka mã'a tɩ a ka yẽtɩ pẽka.».

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: La a daan ka yãŋɛ tõm tõon-yãlma bilam, sãn dagna bã'adõma fẽe tɩ a daan dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto, tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: La ban daan ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la, la di'ige ẽ mɛ. Azezi daan kaarɩ la tẽnsɩ la n boe bilam na zãsna nẽrba.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: daan wi a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la, tɩ ba wa'am a zẽ'am tɩ a tõm ba bayi bayi, dee bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba dige kʋlkã'arsɩ.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Dee daan yele ba yetɩ: «Da dɩkɛ-ya sɛla sɛla sore la sẽnnɛ ĩyã, dɩkɛ-ya dasaarɛ mã'a, da tara-ya dia, bɩɩ tãmpɔkɔ, bɩɩ ligri sũm sẽŋɛ.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: La pire-ya tagra, dee da yɛ-ya futo bayi bayi.».

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: A le yele ba yetɩ: «Yan sẽŋɛ ka sige yi-sɛka, kẽ'era-ya bilam ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn yese fõrgɛ tẽŋa la.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: La tẽn-sɛka nẽrba n ka to'oge ya, bɩɩ ka selse ya yetɔga, ya yese zẽ'e-ẽŋa dee pɩɩsɛ ya nãma tẽntõnnɔ basɛ mĩ. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, la wʋn pa'alɛ ba tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa.».

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ ka mõola yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Dee dige kʋlkã'arsɩ zozo'e tɩ ba yese, dee ẽŋɛ kaam ẽŋɛ bã'adõma zozo'e zuto, tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Tɩ naba A'erɔdɩ wõm Azezi yelle, se'ere n soe la, Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ daan yese la zẽ'a woo. Tɩ nẽrba basɛba daan yɛta tɩ: «A de la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na n vo'oge, bɛla n soe tɩ a tara pãŋa tõnna tõon-yãlma.».

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «A de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli n leme wa'am.» Tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «A de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ayẽma wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba basɛba n daan boe la.».

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Tɩ naba A'erɔdɩ daan wõm bɛla, yetɩ: «A de la Azã sɛka tɩ mam daan botɩ ba wike a zuugo la n vo'oge.».

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Se'ere n soe la, A'erɔdɩ mẽŋa n daan botɩ ba yõkɛ Azã lʋ tarɩ ka kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem, a kẽema Afilipi pɔga A'erodɩadɩ ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, a daan fãagɛ la a kẽema pɔga di.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Tɩ Azã daan yele ẽ yetɩ, la ka masɛ tɩ a fãagɛ a kẽema pɔga di.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Tɩ A'erodɩadɩ sũure yĩige la Azã, tɩ a zɩgra tɩ a kʋ ẽ, la A'erɔdɩ ĩyã a ka tã'agɛ kʋ ẽ.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: A'erɔdɩ daan zotɩ Azã mɛ, a daan mi tɩ a de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, dee dɛna pʋpeelem dãana, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a gu'ura ẽ tɩ sɛla da ẽŋɛ ẽ. A daan sãn ẽn sɛlsra Azã yetɔga, la dãanɩ ẽ mɛ zozo'e, la baa bɛla, a ẽn sɛlsra ẽ mɛ la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: La daarɛ ayɩla tɩ A'erɔdɩ ẽŋɛ a dɔka daarɛ kibsa, wi a tẽŋa nẽrzuto, la sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma, la Galile nẽŋadõma. Tɩ A'erodɩadɩ yẽ sore ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: A'erodɩadɩ pɔyʋa n daan kẽ ka wa, tɩ A'erɔdɩ, la sãama la sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm. Tɩ naba la yele pugla la yetɩ: «Sose mam fõn boorɩ sɛla tɩ m bɔ fɔ.».

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Dee pɔɩ yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ fʋ wʋn sose mam, baa la sãn dɛna mam so'olʋm pʋsʋka, mam wʋn bɔ fɔ.».

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Tɩ pugla la yese ka soke a ma yetɩ: «M sose bẽm?» Tɩ a ma lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Sose Azã n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na zuugo.».

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Tɩ pugla la lebe naba la zẽ'am tɔtɔ ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam boorɩ tɩ fʋ dɩkɛ la Azã n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na zuugo ẽŋɛ la-pɛsŋa pʋam bɔ ma lɛɛlɛ wã.».

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Tɩ naba la sũure sã'am halɩ, la ẽn pɔɩ nẽrba la zẽ'am na ĩyã, a ka boorɩ tɩ a mõŋɛ ẽ.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Tɩ a tõm a sʋdaaga tɔtɔ yetɩ, a sẽŋɛ ka wike Azã zuugo tarɩ wa'am. Tɩ a yese sẽŋɛ yʋ'a deem na ka wike Azã zuugo.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ la-pɛsŋa pʋam tarɩ wa'am wa bɔ pugla la. Tɩ pugla la dɩkɛ zuugo la bɔ a ma.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi wõm, sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ kũm na laagɛ.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Azezi n tõm sɛba la leme mɛ wa lagsɛ taaba a zẽ'am tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ẽ bãma n ẽŋɛ sɛla, la bãma n zãsʋm se'em wuu.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: La nẽrba zozo'e n daan sẽnnɩ mĩ dee fõrgra, tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan ka yẽ folle wʋn di. Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya tɩ tɩ yese kẽnkɛrŋa zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ka boe mĩ na, tɩ ya ka vo'ose fẽe.».

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Tɩ ba kẽ õorŋɔ wɛ'ɛsa kẽnkɛrŋa zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ka boe.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan yẽ ban we'esɩ la bãŋɛ ba, daan yese tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam lagɩm sẽŋɛ tɔtɔ dẽŋɛ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba we'esɩ la.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ ze'ele õorŋɔ la zuo sige, yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ ba ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, ba ãn wʋ piisi n ka tarɩ dũnsĩina. Tɩ a daan zãsna ba yɛla zozo'e.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
