local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan kẽ'era, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Kalam zẽ'a de la mõom dee tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ'era.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: basɛ nẽrba la tɩ ba sẽŋɛ yiɛ, la tẽn-pɩgsɩ la ka da dia di.».

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma mẽŋa bɔ ba dia tɩ ba di.» Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn sẽŋɛ ka da borborɩ lig-kõba kɔbsyi tarɩ wa'am wa bɔ ba tɩ ba di?».

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ la borborɩ balɛ? Sẽŋɛ-ya ka bɩsɛ.» Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ, dee leme wa yetɩ: «Tõma tarɩ la borborɩ banuu la zũma bayi.».

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ, ba basɛ tɩ nẽrba la welse sill-sille zĩ'ire mõ-mã'asa la zuo.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Tɩ ba daan zĩ'ire kɔbga kɔbga, tɩ basɛba zĩ'ire pinu-nu.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ borborɩ banuu, la zũma bayi la gorge bɩsɛ saazuo pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise borborɩ la bɔ a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, ba pʋɩ nẽrba la, dee dɩkɛ zũma bayi la fiise bɔ ba wuu.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Tɩ ba wuu di tɩgɛ.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la vaagɛ borbor-gito, la zũn-gito n deege pɩrɛ pɩtɔ pia la ayi.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Nẽrba la n daan di dia la, bʋraasɩ de la tʋsanuu.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi darɛ basɛ tɩ a karẽnbiisi la kẽ õorŋɔ dẽŋɛ ẽ dɔɔra mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba, Betisayida tẽŋa bɔba. Dee tɩ a mẽŋa weege tɩ a keese nẽrba la.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: La ẽn keese ba la, a sẽŋɛ la tãŋa zuo tɩ a ka pʋ'ʋsɛ.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ tɩ õorŋɔ la bɔna ko-kãtɛ la tẽŋasʋka, dee tɩ Azezi bɔna doone ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ tɩ a karẽnbiisi la n mõnnɩ õorŋɔ la nãmsrɩ mɛ zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, kʋsebgo n vugri tʋ'ʋsra ba. Tɩ beere yɔna wiire, tɩ a sẽnna ko'om na zuo wɛ'ɛsa ba bɔba, bɔɔra tɩ a gãŋɛ ba tole.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: La ban yẽ ẽn sẽnnɩ ko'om na zuo la, ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a de la ki-isgne, tɩ ba kaasɛ tagursi.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Ba wuu yẽ ẽ mɛ, tɩ dabeem kẽ ba. Tɩ a darɛ yele ba yetɩ: «La de la mam, keŋe-ya ya sũure, da zoe-ya dabeem!».

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Dee zom ba zẽ'am õorŋɔ la pʋam, tɩ kʋsebgo la go'oge. Tɩ karẽnbiisi la palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba zɛ yɛrɩ, se'ere n soe la.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: ba ka bãŋɛ borborɩ bõn-yãlma la võorɛ, ba pʋtẽ'era ka like.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Ba pãrgɛ mõgrɛ la dooge paagɛ la Zenezarɛtɩ, ze'ele mõgrɛ la nõorʋm.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: La ban daan ze'ele õorŋɔ la pʋam dooge la, tɩ nẽrba la bãŋɛ Azezi tɔtɔ.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Tɩ ba zoe kẽ so'olʋm na wuu, tɩ ba zɛ bã'adõma ba bõn-gã'ala pʋam wa'am, bãma n wõm tɩ Azezi boe zẽ'e-sɛka la.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: La Azezi daan sãn ẽn sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka woo, tẽn-kãra, la tẽn-pɩgsɩ, la yiɛ n boe kẽnkɛrsɩ la, ba tarɩ la bã'adõma sẽnna gã'ata da'asɩ pʋam, dee bɛlna ẽ yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ bã'adõma la kalʋm a fuugo nõorɛ mã'a. La sɛba wuu n daan kalʋm a fuugo la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Dabs-bãma pʋam nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n daan le lagsɛ, tɩ dia ka bɔna tɩ ba wʋn di. Tɩ Azezi wi a karẽnbiisi la yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: «Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ wã ninbãalga n tarɩ mam, se'ere n soe la, dabsa atã tɩ ba boe mam zẽ'am dee ka tara dia wʋn di.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: Mam sãn basɛ ba tɩ ba kule ba yiɛ la kɔm, ba wʋn ka vibe mɛ sore, se'ere n soe la, basɛba ze'ele la zẽ'a n zãagɛ wa'am.».

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tã'agɛ yẽ dia tɩ la seke nẽr-bãna wuu, zẽ'e-ẽna n de mõom wã?».

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ la borborɩ balɛ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Bayopɔɩ.».

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Tɩ a botɩ nẽrba la zĩ'ire tẽŋa. Dee dɩkɛ borborɩ bayopɔɩ la pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ a karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba pʋɩ nẽrba la. Tɩ ba pʋɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: La ba daan tarɩ la zũn-pɩgsɩ mẽ fẽe. Tɩ Azezi pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ zũma la ĩyã, dee botɩ a karẽnbiisi la dɩkɛ pʋɩ nẽrba la.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la daan di tɩgɛ. Dee tɩ a karẽnbiisi la vaagɛ dia n weege pɩrɛ pɩtɔ bayopɔɩ.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Nẽrba la n daan di dia la, bʋraasɩ de wʋ tʋsanaasɩ. Tɩ Azezi daan botɩ ba kule.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: dee tɩ ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Dalmanuta so'olʋm.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma daan wa'am wa wẽ'era nõke'ene la Azezi, yetɩ ba makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ, a botɩ bõn-yãlma ze'ele saazuo ẽŋɛ n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Tɩ Azezi sĩ'im vo'ose fõo, dee soke yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ zamãana ẽna nẽrba ɛɛra bõn-yãlma? Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ mam kãn botɩ bõn-yãlma n pa'alɛ bɛla ẽŋɛ tɩ zamãana ẽna yẽ.».

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: La Azezi basɛ ba mɛ dee le kẽ õorŋɔ dɔɔra mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la daan yĩm ka dɩkɛ borborɩ, sãn dagna borborɩ ayɩla mã'a tɩ ba tarɩ õorŋɔ la pʋam.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ: «Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la Farisi dõma, la naba A'erɔdɩ dãbɩllɛ la.».

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la tɔgra la taaba yetɩ: «Tõma n ka tarɩ borborɩ la ĩyã tɩ a yele bɛla.».

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Tɩ Azezi daan bãŋɛ, soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya tɔgra la taaba tɩ la de la yãma n ka tarɩ borborɩ la ĩyã? Yãma ka wõm a võorɛ? Yãma yɛm ka like?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Yãma tarɩ la nini dee ka yẽta? Ya tarɩ la tʋba dee ka wõnna? Yãma ka tẽrɩ?.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Mam n daan fiise borborɩ banuu la bɔ bʋraasɩ tʋsanuu tɩ ba di la, borborɩ gito n deege la, pɩtɔ balɛ tɩ yãma daan vaagɛ?» Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Pɩtɔ pia la ayi.» Tɩ a le soke ba yetɩ:.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: «La mam n daan fiise borborɩ bayopɔɩ la pʋɩ bʋraasɩ tʋsanaasɩ la, yãma daan vaagɛ sɛba n deege la pɩtɔ balɛ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Pɩtɔ bayopɔɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma nãn ka wõm a võorɛ?».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan sẽŋɛ la Betisayida. Tɩ ba tarɩ fʋa wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa belem ẽ yetɩ a kalʋm ẽ.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Tɩ Azezi gʋrɛ fʋa la nu'ugo ve'ege ẽ tẽŋa la pʋam yese kẽnkɛrŋa, ka tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm ẽŋɛ a nini, dee dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ a zuo, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽtɩ sɛla mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la dɔ'ɔgɛ zuugo bɩsɛ, yetɩ: «Mam yẽtɩ nẽrba tɩ ba ãna wʋ tɩɩsɩ la, la ba sẽnnɩ mɛ.».

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Tɩ Azezi le dɩkɛ a nu'usi kalʋm fʋa la nini. Tɩ a nini pike yɩlɩŋɛ, tɩ a yẽta sɛla woo sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Tɩ Azezi basɛ ẽ yetɩ a kule, a da le kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: La Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan we'esɩ la tẽnsɩ n lɛm tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Filipi Sezaare. La ban boe sore wɛ'ɛsa la, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Nẽrba yetɩ mam de la ãne?».

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Sɛba yetɩ fõn de la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na. Tɩ basɛba yetɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli. Tɩ sɛba yetɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yʋʋn boe la nẽra ayɩla.».

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «La yãma yetɩ mam de la ãne?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge yetɩ: «Fõn de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na.».

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da tɔgɛ ẽŋa yelle bɔ nẽra nẽra.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Tɩ Azezi yãŋa zãsna a karẽnbiisi la, yele ba yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la nãmsɛ zozo'e. Tẽŋa wã kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma nifo wʋn kẽ mam mɛ, tɩ ba kʋ mam, la dabsa atã daarɛ mam wʋn vo'oge mɛ.».

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: A daan tɔgɛ ba la vẽelga vẽelga. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ wi ẽ yese kẽnkɛrŋa ka yele ẽ yetɩ, a da le tɔgɛ bɛla.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Tɩ Azezi gelge bɩsɛ a karẽnbiisi la, dee tãsɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asũtãana, sẽŋɛ lebe mam poorʋm, se'ere n soe la, fʋ tẽ'esgɔ la dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ tẽ'esgɔ, la de la nẽrsaala tẽ'esgɔ.».

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Dee wi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, la a karẽnbiisi la a zẽ'am, yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a doose mam, dẽnɩ tɩ a basɛ a mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee sakɛ nãmsgɔ daarɛ woo wʋ nẽra n buke a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n sakɛ tɩ a koŋe a vɔm mam ĩyã, la kõ-yẽlga la ĩyã, a wʋn yẽ vɔm.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Nẽra sãn yẽ dũnia sɛla woo dee koŋe vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a yõorɔ de la bẽm?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Bẽm tɩ nẽrsaala wʋn dɩkɛ teege vɔm? Sɛla kaɩ.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n zoe yãnnɛ mam ĩyã, la mam yetɔga la ĩyã, zamãana ẽna wã n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ la sɩra, dee dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma wã tẽŋasʋka, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn zoe yãnnɛ na ẽ, wakat-sɛka tɩ a wʋn leme wa'am la a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa, la a malɛkadõma la.».

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba n ze'ele Zerizalɛm daan wa'am wa lagsɛ Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: yẽ tɩ a karẽnbiisi la basɛba diti la nu'usi n ka ãn sõŋa, la boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ ba ka peege nu'usi wʋ Zifdõma sisgo n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Farisi dõma, la Zifdõma wuu sãn ka wobse nu'usi wʋ ba yaabdõma malŋɔ la, ba ka diti.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Ba sãn ze'ele da'am wa'am, ba wʋn peege ba mẽŋa mɛ dee di. La malŋɔ basɛba zozo'e tɩ ba ɩtɩ, la de la wama, la yɔa, la kuto laasɩ peere.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la soke Azezi yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka sakra tɩ yaabdõma malŋɔ la, dita dia la nu'usi n ka ãn sõŋa?».

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «A'ezayi n yʋʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la tɔgɛ ya yelle la, a tarɩ bʋʋrɔ, yãma de la pʋlibsum dõma wʋ lan gʋlsɛ se'em na. La gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-bãna pẽgrɩ mam na nõorɛ yetɔga, la ba sũure la zãagɛ la mam mɛ.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Ban nã'asrɩ mam na, la de la zãŋa. Se'ere n soe la, ba zãsnɩ la zãsŋɔ n de nẽrsaalba nõorɛ.›.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Ya basɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ mɛ dee dɔla nẽrsaalba malŋɔ.».

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sɩrɩ zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ mɛ, dee tɩ ya dɔla ya mẽŋa malŋɔ.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Amoyisi yʋʋn yetɩ: Nanna fʋ sɔ, la fʋ ma. Dee le yetɩ: Nẽr-sɛka sãn tɔgɛ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma be'em, dẽnɩ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: La yãma yetɩ: Nẽra sãn yele a sɔ, bɩɩ a ma yetɩ: Mam n tarɩ bõn-sɛba tɩ m sõŋɛ fʋ la de la ‹Kɔrbã›, .

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: la ka le masɛ tɩ a ɩta sɛla bɔ'ɔra a sɔ, la a ma.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Yãma mẽŋa malŋɔ la tɩ ya zãsnɩ nẽrba la, botɩ ya basɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la dɛna la zãŋa. La bõnɔ zozo'e n wõm na bɛla tɩ ya ɩtɩ.».

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: La Azezi le wi la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ, tɩ ba wa'am a zẽ'am, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma wuu, selse-ya mam sõŋa wõm a võorɛ.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Sɛla n ze'etɩ yẽŋa kẽ'erɩ nẽrsaala pʋʋrʋm ka tã wʋn botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ. Bõn-sɛka n ze'etɩ nẽrsaala pʋam yɛsra n botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ. .

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: La a daan ze'ele nẽrba la zẽ'am kẽ la deem. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la soke ẽ makrɛ la võorɛ.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma yɛm mẽ ka like? Yãma ka bãŋɛ tɩ sɛla n ze'etɩ yẽŋa kẽ'era nẽrsaala pʋʋrʋm ka tã wʋn botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ?.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Se'ere n soe la, bõn-bãma ka kẽ'erɩ sũure pʋam, ba kẽ'erɩ la pʋʋrɛ pʋam tɩ ba yẽ'era ba basra.».

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Dee daan le yetɩ: «La de la bõn-sɛka n ze'etɩ nẽrsaala pʋam yɛsra n botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Se'ere n soe la, pʋtẽ'e-yooro ze'etɩ la nẽrsaala sũure pʋam botɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em: yalsɩ, la nayigum, la nẽr-kʋa.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: la taaba pɔgba la taaba sɩrba gã'a, la nẽra n nõŋɛ bõnɔ, la pʋtʋʋlgɔ, la yɛmkẽgsɩga, la tõon-dẽgrɔ, la nẽra n ɩtɩ sũure la a tadãana bõnɔ, la pɔ'ɔrɛ yetɔga, la pa'al-m-mẽŋa, la yalm-tõoma.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Bõn-be'ero bãna wuu ze'etɩ la nẽrsaala sũure pʋam botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele zẽ'e-ẽŋa sẽŋɛ la Tiiri so'olʋm ka kẽ yire pʋam, dee ka bɔɔra tɩ nẽra bãŋɛ tɩ ẽŋa boe bilam. La a daan ka tã'agɛ sʋgɛ.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Tɩ pɔka ayɩla tɩ kʋlkã'arga daan dolɩ a pɔyʋa, wõm Azezi yelle, sẽŋɛ ka ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: sose ẽ yetɩ, a dige kʋlkã'arga la tɩ a yese dee basɛ a pɔyʋa la. Pɔka la daan de la Grɛkɩ, ba dɔgɛ ẽ na Fenisi tẽŋa n boe Siiri so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ kɔma dẽŋɛ di tɩgɛ, se'ere n soe la, la ka masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ kɔma dia bɔ baasɩ.».

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, la de la sɩra, la baasɩ n boe ba dãana taablɩ tilum diti la kɔma di-zõra n luti la.».

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn lerge se'em wã ĩyã, kule, tɩ kʋlkã'arga la basɛ fʋ pɔyʋa la.».

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Tɩ pɔka la daan lebe kule, la ẽn paagɛ yire la, a yẽ tɩ a pɔyʋa la gã la bõn-gã'ala pʋam, la kʋlkã'arga la sɩrɩ yese dee basɛ ẽ mɛ.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Azezi le ze'ele Tiiri so'olʋm doose la Sidõ, doose Dekapɔlɩ tẽnsɩ pʋam lebe Galile mõgrɛ la bɔba.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ bʋraa n de waŋa, dee ka tãna tɔgra sõŋa wa'am a zẽ'am, wa belem ẽ yetɩ, a dɩkɛ a nu'ugo paglɛ waŋa la zuo.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Tɩ Azezi tarɩ ẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam yese kẽnkɛrŋa dɩkɛ a nu'ubiɛ tũm a tʋba pʋam, dee tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm kalʋm bʋraa la zɩlɩŋa.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Dee gorge saazuo sĩ'im vo'ose fõo, dee yele bʋraa la yetɩ: «Efata», a võorɛ de la «yo'oge!».

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ bʋraa la tʋba yo'oge, tɩ a zɩlɩŋa lakɛ, tɩ a tɔgra sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da tɔgɛ bɔ nẽra nẽra. La ẽn daan kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da tɔgɛ bɔ nẽra nẽra la, ba daan tõn tɔgra mɛ zozo'e.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Tɩ la di'ige ba zozo'e halɩ tɩ ba yɛta tɩ: «A ẽŋɛ sɛla woo la sõŋa, botɩ wansɩ wõnna, tɩ mugro tɔgra.».

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: La a le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ yãma nẽr-sɛba n boe kalam wã, basɛba kãn ki, sãn dagna tɩ ba wa yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm n sẽm na pãŋa.».

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Tɩ dabsa ayoobɩ daan tole, tɩ Azezi botɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la Azã mã'a doose ẽ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ kẽnkɛrŋa ka zom tãn-kãtɛ zuo. Tɩ a nẽŋa teege ba nẽŋam.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: Tɩ a futo pelge yɩlɩ yɩlɩ yẽgra halɩ, tɩ nẽra ka bɔna dũnia wã zuo tãna wʋn peege tɩ ba pelge bɛla.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Tɩ A'eli, la Amoyisi puke ba zẽ'am tɔgra la Azezi.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma n boe kalam wã, la ãn sõŋa mɛ. Basɛ tɩ tɩ tĩ põnsɩ batã, bɔ fõn ayɩla, bɔ Amoyisi ayɩla, bɔ A'eli ayɩla.».

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: A daan ka mi ẽŋa n tɔgɛ se'em, se'ere n soe la, dabeem n daan kẽ ba.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Tɩ sawatɛ wa'am wa lile ba la a mã'asʋm. Tɩ kõa ze'ele sawatɛ la pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ. Selse-ya a yetɔga.».

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ ba bɩsɛ kaagɛ ka le yẽ nẽra, sãn dagna Azezi mã'a n boe ba zẽ'am.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Tɩ ba ze'ele tãŋa la zuo sigra, tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ: «Da tɔgɛ-ya pa'alɛ nẽra nẽra yãma n yẽ sɛla la, sãn dagna tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa ki mɛ, dee vo'oge.».

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Tɩ ba to'oge ẽn yele se'em na, dee sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Kũm vo'ore võorɛ de la bẽm?».

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Tɩ ba soke Azezi yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ A'eli dẽŋɛ wa'am, tɩ Krisi yãŋa wa'am poorʋm?».

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Asɩra, A'eli n wʋn dẽŋɛ wa'am wa malgɛ sɛla woo. La bẽm tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la yelle yetɩ, a wʋn nãmsɛ mɛ zozo'e dee yẽ pɔ'ɔrɛ?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ A'eli põn wa'am mɛ, tɩ nẽrba ẽŋɛ ẽ ban boorɩ se'em, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ a yelle Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.».

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Ban lebe ka lɛm karẽnbiisi basɛba la zẽ'am na, ba yẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e n kaagɛ ba, tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba wẽ'era nõkɛ'ɛma la ba.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: La nẽrba la n daan yẽ Azezi la, la di'ige ba mɛ, tɩ ba zoe ka pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm yelle tɩ yãma wẽ'era nõke'ene la ba?».

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Tɩ nẽra ayɩla ze'ele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tẽŋasʋka yele yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam tarɩ la m dayʋa wa'am fõn zẽ'am, kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ botɩ a dɛna muko.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: La kʋlkã'arga la sãn ẽn girge ẽ, a lʋbrɩ ẽ mɛ tɩ nõtõn-fulunfuka yɛsra, tɩ a ɔbra yẽna dee gorŋe. Bɛla tɩ mam tarɩ ẽ wa'am wa sose fʋ karẽnbiisi la yetɩ ba dige kʋlkã'arga la tɩ a yese, tɩ ba ka yãŋɛ.».

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Zamãan-ẽna nẽrba, ya ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra, mam wʋn bɔna la yãma mã'agɛ m mẽŋa halɩ ka paagɛ la wakat-kãna? Tarɩ-ya bia la wa'am.».

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am a zẽ'am. La kʋlkã'arga la n yẽ Azezi la, bilam mẽŋa a dãan bia la mɛ, tɩ a lui tẽŋa bilna, tɩ nõtõn-fulunfuka yɛsra a nõorʋm.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke bia la sɔ yetɩ: «Wakat-kãna tɩ bõn-ẽna ẽŋɛ ẽ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «A bõnbɩ'ɩlʋm.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: La kʋlkã'arga la ẽn lʋbɛ ẽ na bugum, bɩɩ ko'om pʋam, bɔɔra tɩ a kʋ ẽ. Fʋ sãn tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla, zoe tɩ ninbãalga sõŋɛ tɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fʋ yetɩ, fõn sãn tãna? Nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, a tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla woo bɔ ẽ mɛ.».

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ bia la sɔ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Mam bɔ sɩra, la sõŋɛ mam tɩ m bɔ sɩra pa'asɛ.».

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: La Azezi daan yẽ tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n zotɩ sẽna a zẽ'am, tɩ a tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga n botɩ nẽra dɛna muko la waŋa, mam yetɩ fʋ yese dee basɛ ẽ, da le doose ẽ.».

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la kaasɛ tagurga, dee bigse bia la ke'enke'em, dee yese. Tɩ bia la ãna wʋ kũm na. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yetɩ a ki mɛ.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Tɩ Azezi gʋrɛ a nu'ugo zẽkɛ ẽ, tɩ a isge ze'ele saazuo.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: La Azezi daan kẽ la deem, ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la mã'a, tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ tõma ka tã'agɛ dige kʋlkã'ar-ẽna?».

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sãn dagna pʋ'ʋsgɔ, sɛla sɛla ka boe n tã wʋn dige kʋlkã'ar-ẽna buuri.».

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: La ba daan yese bilam doose la Galile tẽŋa tole. La Azezi daan ka boorɩ tɩ nẽra bãŋɛ ẽŋa n boe zẽ'a.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: A daan zãsʋm a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Ba wʋn dɩkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ẽŋɛ la nẽrba nu'usum, tɩ ba kʋ ẽ. La ba sãn kʋ ẽ, dabsa atã daarɛ a wʋn vo'oge mɛ.».

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Tɩ ba daan ka bãŋɛ yetɔga la võorɛ, dee zɔta ka soke ẽ.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Kapɛrnaʋm kẽ deem. Tɩ Azezi soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm yelle tɩ yãma wẽ'erɩ nɩ nõke'ene sore zuo la?».

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Tɩ ba sĩna, se'ere n soe la, ba wẽ'erɩ la nõke'ene la taaba bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ sɛka n de nẽr-kãtɛ gãnna.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Tɩ Azezi zĩ'ire, wi a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a dɛna nẽŋadãana, dẽnɩ tɩ a dɛna poorʋm dãana, la nẽrba wuu tõntõnna.».

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee tarɩ la bi-pɩka wa ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka, dɩkɛ a nu'usi kɔkɛ bia la, dee yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: «Nẽra sãn to'oge bi-pɩka ẽna tadãana mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, a to'oge la mam mẽŋa, la nẽra sãn to'oge mam mẽŋa, la dagɩ mam tɩ a to'oge, a to'oge la sɛka n tõm mam na.».

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Tɩ Azã yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma daan yẽ la bʋraa n tarɩ fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ digra kʋlkã'arsɩ, la ẽŋa n ka po dɛna tõma nẽra la ĩyã tɩ tõma gu ẽ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Da gu-ya ẽ, se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka boe wʋn tõm tõon-yãlma la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, dee tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe tɩ a le tã'agɛ tɔgɛ mam be'em.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ka sisri tõma, a de la tõma nẽra.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn bɔ ya ko'om tɩ ya yũ, yãma n de mam n de Krisi nẽrba la ĩyã, ẽŋa dãana kãn koŋe a yɔɔrɔ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: La nẽra sãn botɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ bãna wã n bɔ mam sɩra la ayɩla basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ a wʋn yẽ la, ba sãn dɩkɛ nẽerɛ kug-kãtɛ lʋ yulse a yugla lobe ẽ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam n sõɩ.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: La fʋ nu'ugo sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, fʋ wike ẽ basɛ, se'ere n soe la, fʋ sãn tara nu'u-yẽŋɔ dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire vɔa, n sõɩ fõn wʋn tara nu'usi bayi dee kẽ bug-tẽŋa, .

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: La fʋ nã'arɛ sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, fʋ wike ẽ basɛ, se'ere n soe la, fʋ sãn tara nã'a-yẽŋɔ dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire vɔa, n sõɩ fõn wʋn tara nãma bayi dee tɩ ba dɩkɛ fʋ lobe basɛ bug-tẽŋa, .

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: La fʋ nifo sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, fʋ kɩrgɛ ẽ basɛ, se'ere n soe la, fʋ sãn tara nin-yẽŋɔ dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm, n sõɩ fõn wʋn tara nini bayi dee tɩ ba lobe fʋ basɛ bug-tẽŋa.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ zũnzũa boe mĩ dita nẽrba ĩyã ka kiira, dee tɩ bugum na ka kĩŋra.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Ba wʋn dɩkɛ la bugum makɛ nẽrba wuu wʋ ban dɩkɛ yaarʋm yugse sɛla.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Yaarʋm ãn sõŋa mɛ, la yaarʋm sãn sabgɛ, ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne botɩ a le ãna yẽlʋm? Ãna-ya wʋ yaarʋm n tarɩ yẽlʋm se'em na, dee tara sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba.».

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Beere n daan wiige bulika, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, bãma wuu n de sarɩyaditba la daan lagsɛ mɛ tɔgɛ kʋ, botɩ ba lʋ Azezi tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke Azezi yetɩ: «Fõn de la Zifdõma naba?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «La de la fõn yele se'em na.».

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la dõrɛ ẽ yɛla zozo'e.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ le soke Azezi yetɩ: «Fõn wõm yetɔg-sɛba wuu tɩ ba tɔgɛ bɔ fʋ wã, fõn kãn lerge se'ere?».

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Tɩ Azezi ka le lerge se'ere, tɩ la di'ige Apilatɩ.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa woo Apilatɩ daan ẽn botɩ ba yese la nẽr-sɛka tɩ nẽrba la boorɩ la yʋ'a deem basɛ.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: La bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Abarabasɩ, ẽŋa la nẽrba basɛba n daan dãmɛ tẽŋa, kʋ nẽra tɩ ba yõgɛ ba yʋ.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am ka sose ẽ yetɩ, a ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n ɩtɩ se'em na bɔ ba.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Ya boorɩ tɩ m yese la Zifdõma naba la basɛ bɔ ya?».

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: La daan deege la dabsa ayi tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa tɩ ba obrɩ borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ dabsa paagɛ. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma ɛɛra sore tɩ ba sʋgɛ yõkɛ Azezi kʋ.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a bãŋɛ tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la ẽŋɛ la sũure botɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi wa bɔ ẽ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Tɩ da kãn ẽŋɛ-ya bɛla kibsa wã pʋam, tɩ nẽrba la wʋn gire mɛ.».

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la daan sa'asɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mɛ, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka sose Apilatɩ tɩ a yese Abarabasɩ basɛ bɔ ba.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Azezi daan boe la Betani, Asimõ n wa dɛna kõnkonne yire, tɩ ba daan bɔna mĩ dita. Tɩ pɔka tarɩ albatrɩ dugla n pɩrɛ la tɩdarɛ ka-sõma ligri n zo'oge tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ naarɩ, wa'am wa vuke dugla la, ka'agɛ kaam na ẽŋɛ Azezi zuugo.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ le soke ba yetɩ: «Bɛla, sɛka tɩ ya wi'iri tɩ Zifdõma naba la, yãma boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ ẽ na wãne?».

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Tɩ nẽrba basɛba n boe bilam na sũure sã'am, tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ pɔka la sã'am ka-sõma la wãna?.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.».

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Ba tã nɩ wʋn koose ba gãŋɛ lig-kõba kɔbstã mɛ, dɩkɛ ligri la bɔ nasdõma.» Tɩ ba zɛrgɛ pɔka la.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ba yetɩ: «A ẽŋɛ la bẽm n de be'em?» Tɩ ba malɩn tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.».

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya pɔka la bãalam, bẽm tɩ ya tɔgra ẽ tɩ a sũure sã'am? Ẽn ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ mam na de la tõon-sõŋɔ.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: La Apilatɩ daan boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrba la sũure n ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm, tɩ a yese Abarabasɩ basɛ bɔ ba. Dee botɩ ba wẽ Azezi la ka'asa, dee dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Nasdõma boe la ya lɛ mɛ wakatɛ wuu, ya tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ba sõŋa mɛ yan boorɩ wakat-sɛka, la mam kãn bɔna la ya lɛ wakatɛ wuu.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ tarɩ Azezi kẽ gɔmna la yire zẽnzakam, dee wi sʋdaasɩ la wuu, tɩ ba lagsɛ.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Ẽŋa n tarɩ sɛla la, tɩ a ẽŋɛ. Ẽn ka'agɛ ka-sõma la ẽŋɛ mam ĩyã la, a dẽŋɛ ẽŋɛ la ban wʋn ẽŋɛ mam se'em mam laarɛ daarɛ.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ fuugo n de gomusga yɛ Azezi, dee dɩkɛ gõ'osɩ wʋgɛ mũŋa vuge ẽ.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ dũnia wã zẽ'e-sɛka wuu tɩ ba wʋn wa mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, ba mẽ wʋn tɔgɛ pɔka wã n ẽŋɛ mam se'em wã, tɩ ba tẽra a yelle.».

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Dee yãŋa pʋ'ʋsra ẽ yetɩ: «Zifdõma naba, naa!».

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Tɩ Azudaasɩ Iskariyo n de Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am, ka yele ba tɩ ẽŋa wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Dee dɩkɛ dasaarɛ wẽ a zuugo, tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm mĩ ẽ, dee ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam nã'asra ẽ.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: La ban daan wõm ẽn yele se'em na, tɩ ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm, tɩ ba yetɩ bãma wʋn bɔ ẽ ligri. Tɩ Azudaasɩ yãŋa ɛɛra so-sɛka tɩ a wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yãŋɛ yõkɛ ẽ na.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Ban yaalɛ ẽ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, ba yɛɛgɛ gomusga fuugo la mɛ, dee le yɛ ẽ a futo, tarɩ ẽ yese tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la pɔspɔsɩ daarɛ tɩ ba kʋʋrɩ pebilsi dita Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la n daan paagɛ. Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ la bɛ ka mãasʋm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la tɩ fʋ di?».

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ban daan tarɩ Azezi wɛ'ɛsa la, ba daan tʋ'ʋsɛ la bʋraaga ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asimõ n ze'ele Sirɛnɩ, a de la Alɛgsãndrɩ la Arufusi sɔ. A daan ze'ele la va'am lɛmna. Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la pẽrgɛ ẽ, tɩ a to'oge Azezi kũm dɔ-puurŋa la buke.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Tɩ Azezi tõm a karẽnbiisi bayi yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ ya wʋn tʋ'ʋsɛ bʋraaga n tʋ yoore la ko'om, ya doose ẽ.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba daan tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ la zẽ'a tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Golgota, a võorɛ de la zuwaŋrɛ zẽ'a.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: sẽŋɛ yi-sɛka tɩ a wʋn kẽ la, ka yele yidãana la yetɩ: ‹Karẽnsãama la yetɩ, deego boe la bɛ, tɩ ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi wʋn di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la?›.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Ba daan dɩkɛ la dãam n gaam na tɩɩm yʋ'ʋrɛ n de miiri bɔ ẽ tɩ a yũ, tɩ a zagsɛ ka yũ.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: A wʋn pa'alɛ ya de-kãtɛ n boe ayẽma zuo, tɩ sɛla woo mãasʋm bɔna mĩ. Bilam tɩ ya wʋn mãasʋm tɩ tɩ di kibsa la.».

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Tɩ ba daan ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, dee dɩkɛ a futo tɩ ba pʋɩ taaba, pɔsɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ nẽra woo n wʋn to'oge se'em.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yese sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la pʋam ka yẽ sɛla woo wʋ Azezi n yele ba se'em na. Tɩ ba mãasʋm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la bilam.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: La daan de la bulika karf-awɛɩ wakatɛ tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan mã'agɛ, tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la wa'am.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Ba daan gʋlsɛ la gʋlsgɔ pa'alɛ bõn-sɛka n botɩ ba ka ẽ lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa la zuo. Ba daan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Zifdõma naba.».

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: wa zẽa dita, tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ ya nẽra ayɩla n diti la mam kalam n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.».

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Ba daan tarɩ la fãarba bayi wa po ka dɔ-puursi zuto, ayɩla a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, ayẽma a gɔbga bɔba. .

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Tɩ la daan sã'am ba sũure. Tɩ ba sɔkra ẽ ayɩla ayɩla yetɩ: «La de la mam?».

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ bilam tɔlla la lɔbra ba zuto, dee tʋʋra ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽhẽe, faa! Dagɩ fõn yetɩ fõn wʋn wõrgɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la dee le mẽ ẽ dabsa atã pʋam?.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «La de la ya nẽrba pia la ayi la nẽr-sɛka n wʋn yɛglɛ la mam tũm a nu'ugo laaga pʋam na.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: Fãagɛ fʋ mẽŋa dɔ-puurŋa la zuo sige.».

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn ki wʋ lan põn gʋlsɛ a yelle Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na. La sɛka n yetɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn yẽ toogo. Nẽr-ẽŋa ma sãn ka dɔgɛ nɩ ẽ n sõɩ.».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la mẽ la'ara ẽ yɛta taaba yetɩ: «A daan fãagrɩ nẽrba basɛba mɛ, dee ka tãna wʋn fãagɛ a mẽŋa.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: La ban daan diti la, tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «To'oge-ya di, ẽna wã de la mam ĩyã.».

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: A sãn dɛna Krisi, Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma naba la, a ze'ele dɔ-puurŋa la zuo sige nãnannɛ wã tɩ tɩ yẽ dee sakɛ.» Tɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba ka dɔ-puursi zuto lɛm ẽ na mẽ tɔgɛ ẽ be'em.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Dee dɩkɛ bõn-yũula pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee bɔ ba, tɩ ba wuu yũ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka n daan paagɛ, tɩ tẽŋa la wuu lebge lika halɩ ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ karf-atã.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Bãna wã de la mam zɩɩm n wʋn yese saagɛ nẽrba zozo'e ĩyã, pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la ba.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Karf-atã la wakatɛ tɩ Azezi tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Eloi, Eloi, lama sabaktani.» La võorɛ de la: «Mam Wẽnnaam, mam Wẽnnaam, bẽm n soe tɩ fõn basɛ mam?».

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ mam kãn le yũ viinye biɛ bõn-yũula wã, halɩ tɩ la ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn le yũ ba Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Tɩ sɛba n ze bilam na basɛba wõm bɛla, yetɩ: «Selse-ya, a wi'iri la A'eli.».

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Tɩ ba yõom yõoma pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee yese wɛ'ɛsa Oliivi tãŋam.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Tɩ nẽra ayẽma zoe ka dɩkɛ nẽtɩŋa lʋsɛ dã-mĩ'isgo pʋam zẽkɛ la dɔɔgɔ bɔ ẽ tɩ a yũ, dee yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ gu bɩsɛ A'eli la sãn wʋn wa sike ẽ dɔ-puurŋa la zuo.».

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Yãma wuu wʋn lebe la poorʋm dee basɛ mam, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn kʋ dũnsĩina dee tɩ piisi la wɩrgɛ.› ».

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Tɩ Azezi le kaasɛ ke'enke'em dee yãŋa ki.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: La Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «La mam sãn wa vo'oge, mam wʋn dẽŋɛ ya sẽŋɛ Galile.».

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa, tɩ fuugo la n yuli Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam beŋe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la ãagɛ pʋsʋka saazuum ka paagɛ tẽŋa.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Baa ba wuu sãn lebe poorʋm dee basɛ fõn, mam kãn basɛ fõn.».

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Tɩ Rom sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛma daan zɛ Azezi nẽŋam yẽ ẽn ki se'em, tɩ a yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.».

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra, tɩ zĩna, yʋ'ʋŋɔ ẽna wã, lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ nõorɛ ayi, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam.».

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: La pɔgsɩ basɛba n mẽ daan boe mĩ zɛ kẽnkɛrŋa bɩsra. Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Amaari n de Azakɩ pɩka la Azoze ma, la Asalome n daan boe ba pʋam.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ le tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Baa la sãn dɛna tɩ mam lagɩm na fõn ki, mam kãn malɩn sɩ'ɩsɛ fõn.» Tɩ karẽnbiisi la wuu lagɩm yele bɛla.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Azezi n daan boe Galile la, ba daan dolɩ ẽ mɛ dee sõŋra ẽ. La pɔgsɩ basɛba zozo'e n daan lagɩm doose ẽ wa'am Zerizalɛm n boe mĩ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Tɩ ba paagɛ zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Getsemane la. Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Zĩ'ire-ya kalam dee tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan bɔna mĩ kẽ'era, la daan de la beere n wʋn wiige Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ, tɩ ba daan bɔna mĩ mãasna.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: La a tarɩ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la Azã sẽŋɛ. Tɩ a sũure sã'am zozo'e, tɩ la dãan ẽ.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: Tɩ bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azozɛfʋ, a ze'ele la Arimate, a de la sarɩyaditba la nẽra ayɩla tɩ ba nannɩ. A mẽ daan gu'uri la Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn wa'am wa di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka. Lan daan de beere n wʋn wiige vo'osgo daarɛ la ĩyã, tɩ a kɛ'ɛm sũure sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am ka sose Azezi kũm na.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ mam zozo'e tɩ mam ãna wʋ m yetɩ m ki mɛ na. Weege-ya kalam gu'ura, da gĩse-ya.».

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: La Apilatɩ n wõm tɩ Azezi darɛ ki mɛ na, la di'ige ẽ mɛ. Tɩ a botɩ ba wi sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la, tɩ a soke bãŋɛ Azezi ki yuuge mɛ?.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: La a sẽŋɛ la nẽŋa fẽe, ka võglɛ tẽŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, la sãn tãna, toogo la fõrgɛ dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: La Apilatɩ n wõm sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la lergre la, tɩ a bɔ Azozɛfʋ sore tɩ a sike kũm na.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Dee yetɩ: «M Sɔ, fõn tã sɛla woo ɩta, basɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ ẽna fõrgɛ dee basɛ ma. La, la da kãn dɛna mam n boorɩ se'em, la dɛna fõn boorɩ se'em.».

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ da tãnnɛ sike kũm na dɔ-puurŋa la zuo, dɩkɛ tãnnɛ la vile ẽ, tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ yɔɔgɔ tɩ ba daan tũ tãmpĩa ĩyã, yãŋa bilge kug-kãtɛ lige yɔɔgɔ la nõorɛ.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: La a leme a karẽnbiisi batã la zẽ'am wa yẽ tɩ ba gĩsri mɛ, tɩ a soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asimõ, fõn gĩsri mɛ? Fõn ka tã wʋn gu karf-ayɩla ka gĩse?».

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Azoze ma Amaari daan ze mɛ bɩsra zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba bĩŋe ẽ na.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya gẽem dee pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ yelle da paagɛ ya tɩ ya lui. Ya sɩa la boorɩ mɛ, la ya ĩyã ka tara pãŋa.».

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: La a daan le lebe ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ wʋ ẽn pʋ'ʋsɛ se'em na.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Dee le leme wa yẽ tɩ ba gĩsri mɛ, se'ere n soe la, gẽem n ba tarɩ ba. Tɩ ba ka mina ban wʋn yele ẽ se'em.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: A daan le sẽŋɛ mɛ, dee leme wa'am nõorɛ atã pʋam wa soke ba yetɩ: «Ya ken vɔ'ɔsra mɛ dee gĩsra? La ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ mɛ, wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ ba wʋn dɩkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ẽŋɛ tõon-be'ero dõma nu'usum.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Isge-ya tɩ sẽŋɛ, tɩ sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam na n wãna sẽm.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: La Azezi daan ze'ele zẽ'e-ẽŋa sẽŋɛ la Zude so'olʋm, Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e le lagsɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ a zãsna ba wʋ ẽŋa n ẽn ɩta se'em na.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Azezi n daan ken tɔgra la, tɩ Azudaasɩ n de a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla, doose la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ sẽŋɛ paam. La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la daan tarɩ la sʋkɔbrɔ, la dɔa dɔla ẽ. La de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma n tõm ba.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba wa'am tɩ ba di a yɛm bɩsɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraa tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ a lebse a pɔga mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: La Azudaasɩ n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na, põn tɔgɛ mɛ pa'alɛ ba yetɩ: «Sɛka tɩ mam wʋn kɔkɛ ẽ na, ẽŋa n bɛla, yõkɛ-ya ẽ, tarɩ ẽ sõŋa fõrgɛ.».

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Tɩ a lebe soke ba yetɩ: «Amoyisi yʋʋn bɔ ya nõorɛ yetɩ bẽm?».

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: La Azudaasɩ n paam na, a sẽŋɛ lɛm na Azezi, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama.» Dee kɔkɛ ẽ.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Amoyisi yʋʋn sakɛ tɩ tɩ gʋlsɛ la pɔgdire lebsgo gõŋɔ, dee tarɩ ẽ ka lebse.».

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la paagɛ yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La de la ya sũure n kɛ tɩ ya ka sakɛ pa'algɔ la ĩyã tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ nõor-ẽŋa bɔ ya.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan boe mĩ na nẽra ayɩla fooge a sʋkɔbgɔ sɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna tʋbrɛ fiige.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: La halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo, Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn nãam nẽrsaalba la, a nãam la bʋraa la pɔka.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ la sʋkɔbrɔ, la dɔa sẽna tɩ ya yõkɛ mam wʋ mam de la fãara la?.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ bʋraa wʋn basɛ a sɔ, la a ma, dee lagɩm na a pɔga.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Mam daan boe la yãma lɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na daarɛ woo zãsna nẽrba, tɩ ya ka yõkɛ mam. La, la de la tɩ bõn-sɛka n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ.».

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: La ba ka le dɛna bayi, ba lebge la ayɩla.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la wuu zoe dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Bɛla, sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lagɩm na taaba, nẽrsaala da welge ba.».

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: La budib-polle ayɩla n daan lobe fugɔbga mã'a dɔla Azezi. Tɩ ba daan zɛ ẽ tɩ ba yõkɛ.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: La Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la kẽ la deem, tɩ ba le soke ẽ yelle ẽŋa võorɛ.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: tɩ a basɛ a fugɔbga la dee zoe beele fõrgɛ.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bʋraa sãn lebse a pɔga dee di pɔka ayẽma, a ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ nẽra n gã'arɩ la a tadãana pɔga.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Ba daan tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la yire, bilam tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wuu tigse.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: La pɔka mẽ sãn basɛ a sɩra dee ele bʋraa ayẽma, a mẽ ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ a gã'arɛ la bʋraa n dagɩ a sɩra.».

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan bɔna poorʋm zãazãarɛ dɔla, ka paagɛ kẽ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la yire zẽnzakam, zẽa la tõntõnɩba ɔɔsra bugum.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e tarɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ wa'am Azezi zẽ'am tɩ a dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la zɛbɛ nẽrba la.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la sarɩyaditba la wuu daan eerɩ la yelle tɩ ba dõrɛ Azezi botɩ ba kʋ ẽ, la ba ka yẽ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: La Azezi n yẽ bɛla la, la ka ẽŋɛ a sũure yẽlʋm, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya kɔm-pɩgsɩ la tɩ ba wa'am mam zẽ'am, da gu'ura-ya ba, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔma wã bõnɔ.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan parɩm bɔ Azezi, la ba yetɔga la ka doose taaba.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n ka sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɔna ẽ wʋ bia la, a kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Tɩ nẽrba basɛba daan isge parɩm bɔ ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: A yele bɛla, dee to'ose kɔma la mɛ, dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ba.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: «Tõma wõm tɩ a yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn wõrgɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la tɩ nẽrsaalba mẽ la, dee le mẽ ayẽma dabsa atã pʋam n dagɩ nẽrsaalba mẽa.› ».

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Azezi daan basɛ bilam dee le dɩkɛ la sore sẽnna. Tɩ bʋraa zoe wa'am a zẽ'am, wa ka dũma a nẽŋam soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fõn de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ?».

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Baa bõn-ẽŋa pʋam ba yetɔga la ka doose taaba.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fõn wi'ira mam tɩ nẽr-sõŋɔ? Nẽra ka boe n ãn sõŋa sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ mã'a.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la isge ze'ele ba wuu nẽŋam soke Azezi yetɩ: «Ban tɔgɛ fõn se'em na, fõn kãn lerge se'ere?».

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Fõn mi Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba ɩta se'em: ‹Da kʋ nẽra, da gã'arɛ la fʋ tadãana pɔga, da zũ, da tɔgɛ põmpɔrŋɔ kaseto, da wẽgɛ nẽra, nanna fʋ sɔ, la fʋ ma.› ».

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Tɩ Azezi daan sĩna ka lerge se'ere. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la Krisi, Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma pẽgrɩ la Dayʋa la?».

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, halɩ mam bõnbɩ'ɩlʋm tɩ mam põn sakra bõn-bãma wuu.».

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe, mam de la ẽŋa. La ya wʋn yẽ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la tɩ a zĩ Nawẽnnɛ n de pãŋa dãana la zʋʋgɔ bɔba, ya wʋn yẽ tɩ a boe sawara pʋam sẽna.».

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩsɛ ẽ nõŋɛ ẽ, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛla ayɩla n deege dɛna fʋ ẽŋɛ: Sẽŋɛ ka koose fõn tarɩ sɛla la wuu dɩkɛ ligri la bɔ nasdõma, tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ bõntarsõm Nawẽnnɛ yire, dee wa'am wa dɔla mam.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la ãasʋm a mẽŋa futo, dee yetɩ: «Tɩ le ɛɛra la bẽm kaset-dõma?.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: La ẽn wõm wãna la, tɩ a nẽŋa zũŋe tɩ a fõrgɛ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, se'ere n soe la, a tarɩ la bõntarsõm zo'oge.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Ya wõm ẽn sã'am Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ. Yãma tẽ'esɛ la wãne?» Tɩ ba wuu yetɩ: «A masɛ la kũm.».

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩsɛ a karẽnbiisi la kaagɛ, dee yele ba yetɩ: «La tɔ mɛ bɔ bõntatba tɩ ba kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Tɩ ba nẽrba basɛba daan sẽŋɛ tʋbsra nõtõorʋm ɩta Azezi, dɩkɛ tãnnɛ pĩ a nini, mũm kũnkũra wẽ ẽ, dee yɛta ẽ tɩ: «Bãŋɛ pa'alɛ tɔ nẽr-sɛka n wẽ fɔ?» Tɩ gu'urba la to'oge ẽ, wẽ a pɛgsɩ.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Tɩ a yetɔga la di'ige a karẽnbiisi la. Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «M kɔma, la tɔ mɛ tɩ nẽra kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan ken bɔna la zẽnzakam na, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõn-pɔka wa'am mĩ.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: La de la nãana tɩ yʋgnɛ doose fupĩim yoko pʋam yese, gãnna bõntarsa n wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: wa yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ n ɔɔsrɩ bugum na, tɩ a bɩsra ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Fõn mẽ daan po bɔna la Nazarɛtɩ bʋraa Azezi lɛ.».

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Tɩ la di'ige ba halɩ zozo'e, tɩ ba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ?».

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sɩ'ɩsɛ yetɩ: «Mam ka mi, mam ka bãŋɛ fõn yele se'em na võorɛ.» Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele bɛla dee yese ka zɛ la zanõrʋm, tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kaasɛ.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩsra ba dee yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽna, la Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn ẽŋɛ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ tã sɛla woo ɩta mɛ.».

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Tɩ tõntõn-pɔka la le yẽ ẽ, yele sɛba n boe mĩ na yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna de la Azezi nẽra ayɩla.».

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma basɛ sɛla woo mɛ dee dɔla fõn.».

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ le sɩ'ɩsɛ. Tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ze mĩ na le yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asɩra, fõn de la ba nẽra ayɩla. Fõn de la Galile nẽra.».

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n basɛ a yire, bɩɩ a kẽendõma, la a yɩbsɩ, bɩɩ a ma, bɩɩ a sɔ, bɩɩ a kɔma, bɩɩ a vatɔ mam ĩyã, la Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ĩyã.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ pɔɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam ka mi bʋra-sɛka tɩ ya tɔgrɩ a yelle wã.».

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: dũnia wã zuo a wʋn to'oge yiɛ, la kẽendõma, la yɩbsɩ, la madõma, la kɔma, la vatɔ nõor-kɔbga, la a wʋn yẽ nãmsgɔ. La wakatɛ n sẽm, a wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ le kaasɛ nõorɛ ayi pʋam. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ tẽegɛ Azezi n daan yele ẽ yetɩ: «Lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ nõorɛ ayi, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam» na. Tɩ a sũure sã'am, tɩ a kɛlla.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: La nẽŋam dõma zozo'e wʋn lebge poorʋm dõma, tɩ poorʋm dõma lebge nẽŋam dõma.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Ba daan boe la sore wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm, tɩ Azezi bɔna ba nẽŋa. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la palẽŋa ẽgra, dee tɩ dabeem tara nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ ba la. Tɩ Azezi le tarɩ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la sẽŋɛ ka tɔgra pa'ala ba bõn-sɛba n wʋn wa ẽŋɛ ẽŋa.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: yetɩ: «Ya yẽ ya, tɩ we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm, bilam tɩ ba wʋn dɩkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma nu'usum. Tɩ ba wʋn tɔgɛ a yelle kʋ yetɩ a masɛ la kũm, dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: tɩ ba la ẽ, dee tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm mĩ ẽ, wẽ ẽ na ka'asa dee kʋ ẽ. La a wʋn vo'oge dabsa atã daarɛ.».

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Tɩ Azebede kɔma Azakɩ la Azã sẽŋɛ lɛm Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma boorɩ la tõma n wʋn sose fõn bõn-sɛka la, fʋ ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ.».

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la bẽm bɔ ya?».

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn wa dita fʋ na'am na, botɩ tõma nẽra ayɩla zĩ'ire fʋ zʋʋgɔ, tɩ ayɩla zĩ'ire fʋ gɔbga.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya ka mi yãma n sosrɩ sɛla. Ya wʋn tã'agɛ nãmsɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam nãmsrɩ wã, bɩɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ nãmsɛ wʋ mam n sakɛ tɩ m nãmsɛ se'em na halɩ ka ki?».

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla.» Tɩ Azezi lebe yele ba yetɩ: «Asɩra, ya wʋn nãmsɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam nãmsrɩ wã halɩ ka ki.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: La, la dagɩ mam n wʋn looge sɛka n wʋn zĩ'ire mam zʋʋgɔ bɩɩ mam gɔbga. Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm zẽ'e-bãma tɩ a bɔ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ la.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: La karẽnbiisi pia la n wõm bɛla la, ba sũure n isge la Azakɩ la Azã.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Tɩ Azezi wi ba wuu, yele ba yetɩ: «Ya mi tɩ dũnia wã na'adõma soe ba nẽrba la pãŋa, dee tɩ kɛ'ɛndõma pẽrgra nẽrba tɩ ba ɩta bãma n boorɩ se'em.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: La yãma pʋam, la kãn dɛna bɛla. Nẽr-sɛka n boe ya pʋam bɔɔra tɩ a lebge nẽr-keko, la masɛ mɛ tɩ a dɛna ya tõntõnna.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a dɛna nẽŋadãana ya pʋam, la masɛ mɛ tɩ a dɛna ya wuu yamŋa.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka wa'am tɩ nẽrba tõm bɔ ẽ, a wa'am tɩ a tõm mɛ bɔ, dee dɩkɛ a vɔm bɔ tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yẽ fãarɛ.».

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ dɔla la Zeriko tẽŋa pʋam tɔlla, la ban daan ze'ele tẽŋa la pʋam yɛsra la, nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n dolɩ ba. Tɩ fʋa zẽa sore nõorʋm sɔsra, a yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Abartime, a de la Atime dayʋa.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: La ẽn wõm tɩ ba tɔgrɩ tɩ la de la Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ la, tɩ a tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Azezi, Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe m ninbãalga.».

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e tãsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Go'oge fʋ yetɔga la.» Tɩ a malɩn tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe m ninbãalga.».

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele dee yetɩ: «Wi-ya ẽ tɩ a wa'am.» Tɩ ba wi ẽ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Keŋe fʋ sũure, isge tɩ a wi'iri fo.».

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Tɩ a lobe a fugɔbga basɛ, dee vaa isge sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la bẽm bɔ fɔ?» Tɩ a yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, mam boorɩ tɩ m le yẽta mɛ.».

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ, fõn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã tɩ fʋ yẽ laafɛ.» Tɩ fʋa la yẽta bilam mẽŋa, dɔla Azezi sore la zuo.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan sẽŋɛ ka yɔna la Zerizalɛm, lɛm Betɩfaazɩ la Betani, Oliivi tãŋa la zẽ'am. Tɩ a tõm a karẽnbiisi bayi.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya tẽn-sɛka n boe ya nẽŋa la pʋam. Ya sãn paagɛ mĩ, ya wʋn yẽ bõn-biire tɩ ba lʋ ze'ele, tɩ nẽra nãn ka bã ẽ. Ya lorge ẽ tarɩ wa'am.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: La nẽra sãn soke ya yetɩ: ‹Wãne tɩ ya lɔrgra ẽ?›, ya lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana la n boorɩ ẽ›, tɩ a wʋn basɛ tɩ ya tarɩ ẽ leme wa'am tɔtɔ.».

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yẽ bõn-biire la n lʋ zanõrʋm sore nõorʋm, tɩ ba lorge ẽ.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: La nẽrba basɛba n daan ze bilam soke ba yetɩ: «Wãne tɩ yãma lɔrgra bõn-biire la?».

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Tɩ ba lerge wʋ Azezi n yele ba se'em na, tɩ nẽrba la basɛ ba.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ bõn-biire la sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am, ka dɩkɛ ba futo paglɛ a zuo, tɩ Azezi bã.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yɛrgɛ ba futo sore zuo, tɩ basɛba sẽŋɛ va'am ka kɔ võorɔ wa yɛrgɛ sore zuo.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n boe a nẽŋa, la sɛba n boe a poorʋm na daan tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Ozaana! Pẽgɛ-ya nẽr-sɛka n sẽm la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Pẽgɛ-ya nẽr-sɛka n sẽm tɩ a di tɩ yaaba Adavidi na'am na. Ozaana! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum.».

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ Zerizalɛm kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na bɩsɛ sɛla woo kaagɛ. Tɩ wẽnnɛ mã'agɛ, tɩ ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la yese sẽŋɛ Betani.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ba ze'ele Betani lɛbra Zerizalɛm, tɩ Azezi kɔm dõnna.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Tɩ a yẽ kẽnkãŋa n ze zãazãarɛ tara võorɔ, tɩ a sẽŋɛ tɩ a ka bɩsɛ a na'an wʋn yẽ sɛla kẽnkãŋa la zuo. La ẽn paagɛ la, a ka yẽ sɛla a zuo, a yẽ la võorɔ mã'a. Se'ere n soe la, kẽnkãma wakatɛ daan nãn ka paagɛ.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele kẽnkãŋa la yetɩ: «Nẽra kãn le malɩn di fʋ biɛ.» Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la wõm.
