eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Tɩ ba paagɛ Zerizalɛm, tɩ Azezi kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam digra nẽr-sɛba n koosrɩ, la sɛba n da'arɩ Wẽndeem na tɩ ba yɛsra. Dee zã'agɛ lig-tɛɛrba taablɩdõma, la sɛba n koosrɩ na'adawɛma la kʋgsɩ lʋbɛ.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: La a daan ka sakɛ tɩ nẽra zɛɛra sɛla dɔla Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Dee daan zãsna nẽrba la yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Ba wʋn wi'ira mam deego la tɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego buuri wuu ĩyã.› La yãma dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ la nayigba lagsgɔ zẽ'a.».

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma wõm bɛla, ɛɛra bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em kʋ ẽ, la ba zotɩ ẽ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a zãsŋɔ la daan kẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la sũure mɛ zozo'e.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Tɩ zaanõorɛ daan mã'agɛ, tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi yese tẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Tɩ bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ ba dɔla sore la, yẽ kẽnkãŋa la n kʋɩ la yẽga wuu.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ tẽegɛ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na, yele Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, bɩsɛ, kẽnkãŋa la tɩ fʋ kã be'em bɔ ẽ na kʋɩ mɛ.».

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bɔ-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɩra.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn yele tãn-ẽna yetɩ: ‹Võogɛ ka lui mõgrɛ pʋam›, dee bɔ sɩra a sũure pʋam tɩ ẽŋa n yele se'em na wʋn ẽŋɛ, dee ka sẽ'ena, la wʋn sɩrɩ ẽŋɛ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam yetɩ, ya sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ sɔsra sɛla, bɔ-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɩra tɩ ya to'oge ya, tɩ ya wʋn sɩrɩ to'oge.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: La ya sãn bɔna mĩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, ya sãn tara yelle la nẽra, basɛ-ya ẽ taalɛ, tɩ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum mẽ basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ. .

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la le sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm, tɩ Azezi kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ɛɛra. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽ nõorɛ la bɛ ɩta bõn-bãna? Bɩɩ ãne n bɔ fõn nõorɛ tɩ fõn ɩta bõn-bãna?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam wʋn soke ya sokre ayɩla, tɩ ya sãn lerge mam, mam mẽ wʋn yele ya nõor-sɛka tɩ mam tarɩ ɩta bõn-bãna.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm ẽ bɩɩ, la de la nẽrsaalba? Lerge-ya ma.».

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Tɩ ba bãm na taaba yetɩ: «Tɩ sãn lerge yetɩ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm ẽ, a wʋn yetɩ, bẽm ĩyã tɩ tõma ka sakɛ ẽ?.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Tɩ mẽ kãn tã'agɛ yetɩ la de la nẽrsaalba n tõm ẽ.» Ba daan zotɩ la nẽrba la, se'ere n soe la, nẽrba la wuu daan sakɛ tɩ Azã sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Tɩ ba koŋe lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka mi.» Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam mẽ kãn yele ya mam n yẽ nõorɛ zẽ'e-sɛka ɩta bõn-bãma.».

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi daan magsrɩ la magsa tɔgra bɔ'ɔra ba yetɩ: «Bʋraa ayɩla n daan kɔ a va'am sele tɩɩsɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de viinye, mẽ lalga kaagɛ ẽ, dee tũ boko tɩ ba wẽkra viinye la biɛ, dee mẽ de-woko tɩ ba zɔnna bɩsra a va'am na, yãŋa dɩkɛ va'am na bɔ nẽrba tɩ ba bɩsra, dee sẽŋɛ sore.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Tɩ viinye la biɛ go'osgo wakatɛ paagɛ, tɩ a tõm a tõntõnna tɩ a sẽŋɛ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na zẽ'am ka to'oge a pʋrɛ wa'am wa bɔ ẽ.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Tɩ ba yõkɛ tõntõnna la wẽ, botɩ a lebe kule zãŋa.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Tɩ va'am na dãana le tõm nẽra ayɩla ba zẽ'am, tɩ ba wẽ a zuugo ãagɛ, dee yaalɛ ẽ.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Tɩ a le malɩn tõm nẽra ayɩla, tɩ ba kʋ ẽ. Tɩ a tõm nẽrba zozo'e, tɩ ba wẽ basɛba, dee kʋ basɛba.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: La nẽra ayɩla mã'a n daan deege tɩ a tãna wʋn tõm, la de la a bi-nõŋrɛ. Tɩ a daan tõm ẽ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na zẽ'am, dee tẽ'esra yetɩ: ‹Ẽna n de mam dayʋa la, ba wʋn nan ẽ.›.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Tɩ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na yele taaba yetɩ: ‹Ẽna n wʋn wa sɔɔgɛ sɔna a sɔ bõntarsõm na, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ kʋ ẽ, tɩ bõntarsõm na lebge tõma bõnɔ.›.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ kʋ, tarɩ ẽ yese va'am na kẽnkɛrŋa ka basɛ.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Bɛla, va'am na dãana yãŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? A wʋn wa'am wa kʋ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na mɛ, dee to'oge va'am na bɔ basɛba.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Yãma põn karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ zẽ'e-sɛka n gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Kug-sɛka tɩ tõnmẽtba zagsɛ la n lebge kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: La de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽna, tɩ la dɛna yel-di'iŋa bɔ tõma.› ».

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la daan ɛɛra tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi, se'ere n soe la, ba mi tɩ la de la bãma ĩyã tɩ a makɛ makrɛ ẽŋa, la ba daan zotɩ la nẽrba la, tɩ ba basɛ ẽ dee fõrgɛ.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Tɩ ba tõm Farisi dõma basɛba, la naba A'erɔdɩ nẽrba basɛba, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka soke Azezi sokre tɩ a tɔgɛ tuuge tɩ ba yẽ sore yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Tɩ ba wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma mi tɩ fõn de la sɩra dãana, dee ka fabla nẽrba n tɔgrɩ fõn yelle se'em na, fõn ka tũ'usri nẽrba, la fõn zãsnɩ nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla la sɩra. Tõma yetɩ tɩ soke bɩsɛ, sore boe tɩ tɩ yɔ lampo bɔ Rom na-keko bɩɩ, sore ka boe? Tɩ yɔ bɩɩ tɩ da yɔ?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba bẽrɛ ẽŋa mɛ, soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya bẽta ma? Tarɩ-ya lagfɔ wa'am tɩ m bɩsɛ.».

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ lagfɔ la wa'am wa bɔ ẽ. Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne yinyisŋa, la ãne yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a zuo?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «La de la Rom na-keko la.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bɔ-ya na-keko la ẽŋa n soe bõn-sɛka, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ ẽn soe bõn-sɛka.» Tɩ Azezi yetɔga la di'ige ba.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Saduse dõma n yetɩ nẽra sãn ki a kãn vo'oge la, bãma nẽrba basɛba n wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: «Karẽnsãama, Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ bɔ tõma yetɩ: ‹Nẽra sɔbia sãn ki deege a pɔga dee ka tara bia, a di pɔkõorɛ la dɔgɛ kɔma bɔ sɛka n ki la.›.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: La bʋraasɩ bayopɔɩ n daan boe dɛna sɔbiisi. Tɩ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la di pɔga, ki dee ka dɔgɛ bia.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Tɩ bʋyi dãana la di pɔkõorɛ la, mẽ ki dee ka dɔgɛ bia. Tɩ bʋtã dãana la mẽ ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: La bãma bayopɔɩ la wuu lagɩm di pɔka la mɛ, ki dee ka dɔgɛ bia. Bɛla poorʋm tɩ pɔka la mẽ ki.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Bãma bayopɔɩ wuu n lagɩm di pɔka la dee ki la, ba sãn wa vo'oge kũm vo'ore daarɛ, ãne n wʋn sɔna pɔka la?».

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ya tuuge mɛ, yãma n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em, la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa n ãn se'em na ĩyã.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Kũm vo'ore daarɛ, bʋraasɩ ka diti pɔgba, la pɔgsɩ mẽ ka ellɩ sɩrba, ba wuu wʋn ãna wʋ malɛkadõma n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: La sãn dɛna kũm vo'ore yelle, yãma nãn ka karẽŋɛ Amoyisi gõŋɔ la pʋam Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn ze'ele tũntuugo la pʋam tɔgɛ la ẽ se'em na? A yʋʋn yele yetɩ: ‹Mam de la Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ Nawẽnnɛ.›.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: La pa'alɛ tɩ bãma wuu vɔa mɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ dagɩ sɛba n ki Nawẽnnɛ, a de la sɛba n vɔa Nawẽnnɛ. Ya tuuge mɛ zozo'e.».

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma nẽra ayɩla wõm ban tɔgrɩ la taaba, dee bãŋɛ tɩ Azezi lerge Saduse dõma la sõŋa, tɩ a lɛm ẽ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba ɩta se'em na wuu pʋam, bõn-kãna n gãnnɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛka n gãnnɩ wuu n wãna: ‹Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, selse-ya. Zuudãana n de tɩ Nawẽnnɛ na, de la Zuudãana ayɩla.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de fʋ Zuudãana la fʋ sũure wuu, la fʋ sɩa wuu, la fʋ yɛm wuu, la fʋ pãŋa wuu.›.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yɛsra, tɩ a karẽnbia ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, bɩsɛ kuga n ba ãna sõŋa tɩ ba dɩkɛ mẽ de-kãra se'em.».

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: La bʋyi dãana n wãna: ‹Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fõn nõŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa se'em na.› Nõorɛ ayẽma ka boe n gãnnɩ bãna.».

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽ de-kãra wã, ba wʋn lʋbɛ ba wuu mɛ saam saam tɩ kugre baa ayɩla kãn weege a tadãana zuo.».

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fõn yele se'em na ãn sõŋa, la de la sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ de la ayɩla, ayẽma ka boe sãn dagna ẽŋa mã'a.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: La Azezi daan sẽŋɛ ka zẽa la Oliivi tãŋa zuo tɩ a nẽŋa bɩsra Wẽnde-kãtɛ la. La Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la Azã, la A'ãndre mã'a n daan boe a zẽ'am.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Fʋ nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ la fʋ sũure wuu, la fʋ yɛm wuu, la fʋ pãŋa wuu, la fʋ nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fõn nõŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa se'em na, ãn sõŋa mɛ gãnna kãabgɔ buuri wuu.».

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: soke ẽ yetɩ: «Yele to, wakat-kãna tɩ bõn-ẽŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ, la bẽm n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ wakatɛ la paagɛ mɛ tɩ bõn-bãma wuu wʋn ẽŋɛ?».

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ ẽn lerge la yɛm na, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ ka zãagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm.» Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka le saagɛ soke ẽ sokre.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Guuse-ya tɩ nẽra da pã'asɛ ya tɩ ya tuuge.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Azezi n daan zãsnɩ nẽrba Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, a soke ba yetɩ: «Wãne tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma yetɩ Krisi de la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa?.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Nẽrba zozo'e n wʋn dɩkɛ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ wa'am wa pã'asra ya, tɩ ba nẽra woo yetɩ: ‹Mam n de Krisi la.› La ba wʋn botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tuuge mɛ.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yʋʋn botɩ Adavidi mẽŋa tɔgɛ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele mam Zuudãana yetɩ, zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ bɔba halɩ tɩ m wa ẽŋɛ fʋ bɛ'ɛba la tɩ ba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.›.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ya sãn wa wõm zɛbrɛ dãmnɛ, la zɛbrɛ yelle, da basɛ-ya tɩ dabeem kẽ ya, dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-bãma ẽŋɛ. La, la nãn ka de dũnia ba'asgɔ.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Adavidi mẽŋa sãn wi ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana›, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ a dɛna Adavidi yɩɩŋa?» Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la sɛlsra a yetɔga la sũ-yẽlʋm.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Buuri ayɩla wʋn isge zɛbra la buuri ayẽma, tɩ so'olʋm ayɩla isge zɛbra la so'olʋm ayẽma. La tẽŋa wʋn tĩi mĩim mɛ zẽ'esɩ zozo'e, la kɔm wʋn bɔna. Bɛla wuu nãn de la toogo la sẽŋrɛ, la ãn wʋ pɔka virgre toogo n nãn sẽŋrɩ la.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Azezi n zãsnɩ nẽrba la, a yele ba yetɩ: «Guuse ya mẽŋa la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n eerɩ kaara la fu-wogro bɔɔra tɩ nẽrba pʋ'ʋsra nã'asra ba da'asʋm.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ nẽrba wʋn tarɩ ya mɛ sẽŋɛ ka sãm ba sarɩyadia zẽ'esʋm, tɩ ba wẽ ya ba wẽndeto pʋam. La ba wʋn tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ gɔmnadõma, la na'adõma nẽŋam mam ĩyã. La ya wʋn tɔgɛ mam yɛla pa'alɛ ba.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: dee bɔɔra nã'asgɔ zẽ'esɩ wẽndeto pʋam, la dia zẽ'esʋm tɩ ba zẽa la.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: La dẽnɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la dẽŋɛ mõolɛ bɔ buuri wuu, dee tɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ paagɛ.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba to'osrɩ la pɔkõpa n tarɩ sɛla la wuu, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋs-wogro tɩ nẽrba bɩsra ba. Ba wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ gãnna wuu.».

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: La ba sãn wa yõgɛ ya tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka sãm sarɩyadia zẽ'am, ya da fabla yãma n wʋn tɔgɛ se'em, wakatɛ sãn wa paagɛ, tɔgɛ-ya yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya la, se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ ya mẽŋa n wʋn tɔgɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wʋn sõŋɛ ya.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Azezi daan zĩ la Wẽnde-kãtʋm ligri daka la zẽ'am dee bɩsra nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n sẽnnɩ ɩta ligri daka la pʋam. Tɩ bõntatba zozo'e ɩta ligri pãɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Sɔbia wʋn botɩ ba yõkɛ sɔbia kʋ, tɩ sɔ mẽ wʋn ẽŋɛ a bia bɛla, la kɔma wʋn zɛbra la ba sɔdõma, la ba madõma botɩ ba kʋ ba.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Tɩ pɔkõorɛ n de nasa wa'am wa dɩkɛ lig-kõb-pɩgsɩ bayi mã'a ẽŋɛ mĩ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nẽrba zozo'e wʋn sise ya mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã. La nẽr-sɛka n wʋn mõrgɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Tɩ Azezi wi a karẽnbiisi la, yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ pɔkõorɛ ẽna n de nasa la ẽŋɛ ligri daka la pʋam zo'oge gãnna ba wuu mɛ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Ya wʋn wa yẽ sɛka n de bõn-be'ego n sã'anɩ sɛla woo la, tɩ a boe zẽ'e-sɛka n ka masɛ tɩ a bɔna la.» «Ya sãn yẽ bɛla, sɛba n boe Zude zoe zom tãnsɩ zuo.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Se'ere n soe la, bãma wuu looge la bãma n tarɩ sɛla pãɩ la pʋam wa ẽŋɛ mĩ. La ẽŋa n de nasa la, dɩkɛ la a dia ligri wuu wa ẽŋɛ mĩ.».

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n boe gɔsgɔ da sige bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ deem dɩkɛ sɛla.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n boe va'am da kãn lebe kule yire tɩ a dɩkɛ a fuugo.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Dabs-bãma, la wʋn dɛna la toogo bɔ pɔg-sɛba n tarɩ pʋa la, la sɛba n tarɩ kɔma tɩ ba mõgra la.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Sose-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ yel-bãma da wa dɛna ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Se'ere n soe la, dabs-bãma wʋn dɛna la toogo zozo'e, tɩ ba nãn ka yẽ halɩ pĩ'iluŋo tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nãam dũnia wa paam zĩna, la toog-ẽŋa buuri kãn le malɩn wa'am.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka bo'oge nɩ dabs-bãma sõorɛ, nẽra ka boe nɩ n wʋn põsɛ. La Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa nẽrba tɩ a looge la ĩyã, tɩ a bo'oge dabs-bãma sõorɛ.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: La nẽra sãn yele ya yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ-ya, Krisi boe la kalam›, bɩɩ ‹bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la ke›, ya da sakɛ.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Nẽrba n wʋn wa'am wa parɩm tɩ bãma n de Krisi, la nẽrba n wʋn wa'am wa parɩm tɩ bãma n de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tõm tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ, bɔɔra tɩ ba sãn tã'agɛ, ba botɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la tuuge doose ba.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ mam dẽŋɛ yele ya sɛla woo mɛ.».

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Azezi le yele yetɩ: «Toogo la dabsa poorʋm, wẽntulle wʋn sobge mɛ, tɩ wõrga kãn le nẽera a peelem.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: La wõrbiɛ wʋn ze'ele saazuum lui. La bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum wʋn mĩim mɛ.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: La ba wʋn yẽ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n boe sawara pʋam sẽna la pãn-kãtɛ, tɩ a na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: La a wʋn tõm a malɛkadõma mɛ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ dũnia gɔbga la zʋʋgɔ, la dapoore la zanõrɛ, ka lagsɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ a looge la halɩ ka paagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu.».

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ-ya makrɛ la kẽnkãŋa bãŋɛ zãsŋɔ la võorɛ. Kẽnkãŋa wila sãn wa pusra võorɔ, ya mi tɩ sigre lɛm mɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Bɛla mẽ, ya sãn wa yẽ tɩ bõn-bãma wuu ɩtɩ, ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lɛm mɛ, a boe la zanõrʋm.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ bõn-bãna wʋn ɩta mɛ dee tɩ zamãan-ẽna yãŋa tole.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazuum la tẽŋa wʋn tole mɛ, la mam yetɔga kãn malɩn tole.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Nẽra nẽra ka mi daar-sɛka la wakat-sɛka tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme wa'am. Baa malɛkadõma n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, bɩɩ Dayʋa la mẽŋa ka mi, sãn dagna Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mã'a.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Guuse ya mẽŋa sõŋa, la ya mãasʋm, se'ere n soe la, ya ka mi wakatɛ la.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: La wʋn ãna wʋ bʋraa n we'esɩ sore dee dɩkɛ a yire gu'ule a tõntõnɩba, bɔ tõntõnna woo a tõonɛ, dee yele zanõr-gu'ura tɩ a gu'ura.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Bɛla ĩyã gu'ura-ya, se'ere n soe la, ya ka mi wakat-sɛka tɩ yidãana la wʋn leme wa'am. A tã wʋn leme wa'am zaanõorɛ mɛ, bɩɩ sõnsʋa, bɩɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kaasga wakatɛ, bɩɩ bulika.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Guuse-ya tɩ yidãana la da wa'am wa di'ige ya, tɩ ya gĩsri mɛ.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Mam n yetɩ ya se'em na, mam yetɩ nẽrba wuu la bɛla, gu'ura-ya.».

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ n daan tole, tɩ Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Azakɩ ma Amaari, la Asalome da tɩdarɛ yũuŋo n ãn sõŋa tɩ ba tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ Azezi ĩyã.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Tɩ Lasrɩ daarɛ bulika wẽnnɛ n doorɩ se'em mã'a la, tɩ ba wɛ'ɛsa yɔɔm na.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: dee tɔgra la taaba sore yetɩ: «Ãne n wʋn bilge kugre la yɔɔgɔ la nõorʋm bɔ tɔ?».

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: La ban paagɛ la, ba yẽ tɩ kugre la n ba ãna kãtɛ la põn bilge mɛ gã.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Tɩ ba kẽ yɔɔgɔ la pʋam, yẽ budib-polle n yɛ fu-pɛɛlga zẽa zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya palẽŋa ẽgra, mam mi tɩ ya eerɩ la Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ, tɩ ba ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la. A vo'oge mɛ, a ka boe kalam. Bɩsɛ-ya zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba daan bĩŋe ẽ na n wãna.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la a karẽnbiisi basɛba la yetɩ: ‹A wʋn dẽŋɛ ya nẽŋa sẽŋɛ Galile. Bilam tɩ ya wʋn yẽ ẽ, wʋ ẽn daan yele ya se'em na.› ».

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Tɩ ba yɛm bɔɩ, tɩ ba ĩyã gõgra, tɩ ba yese yɔɔgɔ la pʋam zoe, la ba daan ka yele nẽra nẽra sɛla, se'ere n soe la, dabeem n tarɩ ba. .

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Azezi n daan vo'oge Lasrɩ daarɛ bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ la, a dẽŋɛ dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ la Amaari n ze'ele Magdala. La de la ẽŋa tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ bayopɔɩ daan dolɩ ẽ tɩ Azezi dige ba, tɩ ba yese la.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka yele nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ Azezi la. Ba daan zĩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ la nintãm.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: La ban daan wõm tɩ Azezi vo'oge mɛ tɩ Amaari yẽ ẽ na, ba daan ka sakɛ.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi le teege nẽŋa ayẽma dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi bayi n daan boe sore wɛ'ɛsa tẽn-pɩka ayɩla.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Tɩ ba lebe ka yele ba taaba la, tɩ ba ka sakɛ.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: La bɛla poorʋm, tɩ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayɩla la daan zẽa dita, tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ ba. Dee zɛbɛ ba, ban daan ka bɔ sɩra, la ba sũure n kɛ la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, ba daan ka sakɛ sɛba n daan yẽ ẽ yetɩ a vo'oge mɛ na yetɔga.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: A yele bɛla, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya dũnia zẽ'a woo ka mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ nẽrba wuu.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Sɛka woo n bɔ sɩra, botɩ ba mise ẽ ko'om pʋam, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ. La sɛka woo n ka bɔ sɩra, ba wʋn di a sarɩya sɩbgɛ ẽ.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: La sɛba n wʋn bɔ mam sɩra la, wʋn ɩta la bõn-yãlma bãna pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa: Ba wʋn dɩkɛ la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ digra kʋlkã'arsɩ, dee tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: La ba sãn yõgɛ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma, bɩɩ yũ yabga, la kãn ẽŋɛ ba sɛla. La ba wʋn dɩkɛ ba nu'usi paglɛ bã'adõma zuto, tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Zuudãana Azezi n daan tɔgɛ la ba kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a zẽkɛ mɛ zom Nawẽnnɛ yire, ka zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la zẽ'esɩ wuu. Tɩ Zuudãana la sõŋɛ ba, botɩ ba tõm tõon-yãlma pa'alɛ tɩ ba kõa la mõolgɔ de la sɩra.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Mam sɩr-sõŋɔ Ateofili, nẽrba zozo'e n mõrgɛ gʋlsɛ yel-sɛba wuu n ẽŋɛ tõma zẽ'am na.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Ba gʋlsɛ yel-bãma wuu wʋ nẽrba basɛba n daan yẽ yɛla la wuu halɩ ba pĩ'iluŋo, dee mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la tɔgɛ pa'alɛ tɩ tõma wõm se'em na.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam mã'agɛ m mẽŋa doose yel-bãma nãma sõŋa sõŋa halɩ ba pĩ'iluŋo pʋam, tɩ mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ m gʋlsɛ yɛla la n doose taaba se'em na bɔ fɔ.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: Mam gʋlsrɩ wãna tɩ fʋ bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ yel-bãna wuu tɩ ba pa'alɛ fʋ la de la sɩra.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Se'emwẽnʋm tɩ A'erɔdɩ daan de Zude tẽŋa naba la, bʋraa n daan boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azakari. A daan de la Zifdõma kãabgɔ kẽema, a de la Abiya buuri nẽra. Tɩ a pɔga yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna A'elizabɛtɩ, ẽŋa mẽ daan de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Aarõ buuri.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Bãma bayi daan de la nẽrba n ɩtɩ sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ nifum, dee sakra Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, la a lɔɔ la wuu sõŋa.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: La ba daan ka tarɩ bia, se'ere n soe la, A'elizabɛtɩ daan de la kõndɔgrɛ, la bãma bayi la wuu mẽ kʋrgɛ mɛ.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: La wakatɛ n daan paagɛ tɩ Abiya buuri kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam tɩ ba tõm. Tɩ Azakari mẽ kẽ tɩ a tõm.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Kãabgɔ kẽma la malŋɔ daan de la ba pɔsɛ looge nẽr-sɛka n wʋn kẽ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ pʋam yõ tɩdarɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ ba daan pɔsɛ, tɩ la yõkɛ Azakari. Tɩ Azakari kẽ tɩ a yõ.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: La tɩdarɛ la yõ'a wakatɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu boe la yẽŋa pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka darɛ wa'am Azakari zẽ'am wa zɛ tɩdarɛ yõ'a bĩmbĩnne na zʋʋgɔ bɔba.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: La Azakari n yẽ ẽ na, a palẽŋa n ẽkɛ halɩ, tɩ dabeem kẽ ẽ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azakari, da zoe dabeem, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wõm fʋ pʋ'ʋsgɔ mɛ. Fʋ pɔga A'elizabɛtɩ wʋn dɔgɛ la budibla, tɩ fʋ sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azã.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: La fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm. La nẽrba zozo'e wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga bia la dɔka ĩyã.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: A wʋn dɛna la nẽr-keko Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ nifum, la a kãn yũ dãam bɩɩ bõn-yũula n bugri. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn bɔna la ẽ halɩ lan pɔsɛ ẽn boe a ma pʋʋrʋm, la wakatɛ wuu.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Ban daan dɔgɛ Azã la, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ na-keko A'ogisti daan bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba kãalɛ a tẽŋa la wuu nẽrba.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: A wʋn botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba zozo'e lebe sakɛ ba Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: La daan de la pɔspɔsɩ n bɛla tɩ ba kãalɛ nẽrba la, wakat-sɛka tɩ Akirinusi daan de Siiri tẽnsɩ gɔmna la.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: La a wʋn dẽŋɛ Zuudãana nẽŋam tara Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli n daan tarɩ se'em na. La a wʋn naagɛ sɔdõma la ba kɔma yɛla, dee wʋn botɩ nẽr-sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la tara yɛm ɩta wʋ sɛba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ la. A wʋn botɩ nẽrba mãasʋm sõŋa gu'ura tɩ Zuudãana.».

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Tɩ nẽrba wuu kule ba mẽnsɩ tẽnsʋm tɩ ba kãalɛ ba.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Tɩ Azakari soke malɛka la yetɩ: «Bẽm n wʋn botɩ mam bãŋɛ tɩ bõn-ẽna de la sɩra? Se'ere n soe la, mam de la bʋra-yã'aŋa dee tɩ mam pɔga mẽ kʋrgɛ.».

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ mẽ yese Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm sẽŋɛ Bɛtelɛm n boe Zude so'olʋm pʋam, zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ ba yʋʋn dɔgɛ naba Adavidi. Se'ere n soe tɩ a sẽŋɛ bilam na, a de la naba Adavidi buuri.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Tɩ malɛka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Agabrɩyɛlɩ, mam ze la Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam. Ẽŋa n tõm mam yetɩ, m wa'am yele fʋ yetɔg-yẽlga wã.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Ẽŋa la Amaari n de pɔg-sɛka n yetɩ a ele ẽ na sẽŋɛ mɛ tɩ ba kãalɛ ba, wakat-ẽŋa Amaari tarɩ la pʋʋrɛ.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: La fõn ka sakɛ mam yetɔga la ĩyã, fʋ nõorɛ n wʋn mãam. La wakatɛ sãn paagɛ, bõn-sɛba tɩ mam yele fʋ la wʋn ẽŋɛ. La fʋ kãn le tãna tɔgra, halɩ tɩ la paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ bõn-bãna wʋn ẽŋɛ la.».

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: La ban nãn boe Bɛtelɛm la, tɩ a dɔka daarɛ paagɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa, nẽrba la daan gu'uri mɛ tɩ Azakari yese. La ẽn daan boe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la pʋam yuura la, ba palẽŋa n ẽkɛ.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Tɩ a dɔgɛ a budib-kẽema, vile ẽ na fuugo gã'arɛ dũnsi yɔnnɔ daka pʋam. Se'ere n soe la, zẽ'a n ka boe sãama yire la pʋam tɩ ba wʋn gã'arɛ.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: La ẽn yãŋa wa yese la, a ka tã'agɛ tɔgɛ la nẽrba la. A nõorʋm n mãam tɩ a tara nu'usi magra pa'ala ba. Bɛla tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ a yẽ la bõn-yãlma zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la pʋam.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: La tẽn-ẽŋa dũnsĩima daan boe la sẽkam gu'ura ba dũnsi yʋ'ʋŋɔ.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Dabs-sɛba tɩ Azakari daan wʋn tõm Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam n ba'asɛ la, a daan lebe kule la a yire.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka sige ba zẽ'am. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ gilge ba. Tɩ dabeem ba kẽ ba halɩ.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Dabs-bãna poorʋm, tɩ a pɔga A'elizabɛtɩ yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ bɔna yire wõr-sɩnuu.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele ba yetɩ: «Da zoe-ya dabeem, tɩ mam tarɩ la kõ-yẽlga tɩ m yele ya, n wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba wuu sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: Dee yetɩ: «Wãna de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ tõoma. Wakat-ẽna ẽŋa n yãŋa bɩsɛ mam yelle, dee yese bõn-ẽna n de nɩ yãnnɛ bɔ mam nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.».

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: La de la yʋ'ʋŋ-ẽna tɩ ba dɔgɛ ya Fãagra la Adavidi tẽŋa pʋam, ẽŋa n de Zuudãana Krisi la.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Pugla ayɩla n daan boe Galile tẽŋa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Nazarɛtɩ, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Amaari. A daan nãn ka mi bʋraa. La bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azozɛfʋ n yetɩ a di ẽ. Azozɛfʋ de la naba Adavidi buuri nẽra. La wakat-sɛka tɩ A'elizabɛtɩ daan tarɩ pʋʋrɛ wõr-sɩyoobɩ pʋam na, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm a malɛka Agabrɩyɛlɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ Amaari zẽ'am.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: La bõn-ẽna n wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ la de la sɩra, ya wʋn yẽ bilɩa tɩ ba vile ẽ na fuugo gã'arɛ dũnsi yɔnnɔ daka pʋam.».

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Tɩ malɛka la sẽŋɛ Amaari zẽ'am ka pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tõoma. Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ boe la fʋ mɛ, a ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.».

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma kʋʋŋ-kʋʋŋɔ darɛ wa'am wa naagɛ la ayɩla la. Tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: La Amaari n daan wõm malɛka yetɔga la, a palẽŋa n ẽkɛ, tɩ a tẽ'esra malɛka la n yele se'em na võorɛ n de sɛla.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: «Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum n soe pẽka, la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a nõŋɛ la tarɩ sũ-mã'asʋm dũnia zuo.».

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Amaari, da zoe dabeem, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: La malɛkadõma la lebe la Nawẽnnɛ yire, dee tɩ dũnsĩima la yele taaba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ Bɛtelɛm ka bɩsɛ sɛla n ẽŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele tɩ la.».

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Fʋ wʋn yõkɛ la pʋʋrɛ dɔgɛ budibla, sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: La ba isge mɛ tɔtɔ sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ yẽ Amaari, la Azozɛfʋ, la bilɩa la tɩ a gã dũnsi yɔnnɔ daka pʋam.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: A wʋn dɛna la nẽr-keko. La ba wʋn wi'ira ẽ tɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu Bia. La Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ẽ a yaaba Adavidi na'am.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: La dũnsĩima la n yẽ ẽ na, ba tɔgɛ bia la yelle wʋ malɛka la n yele ba se'em na.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: tɩ a dɛna Azakɔbɩ buuri naba wakatɛ wuu, a na'am na kãn malɩn ba'asɛ.».

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Tɩ sɛba wuu n wõm dũnsĩima la n yele se'em na, la di'ige ba mɛ.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Tɩ Amaari soke malɛka la yetɩ: «Mam ka mi bʋraa, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ mam yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ?».

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: La Amaari tẽ'esrɩ yɛla wã wuu mɛ a sũure pʋam.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Tɩ malɛka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla wuu Sɩa wʋn sige fʋ zuo, la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa wʋn bɔna la fɔ, bɛla bia la tɩ fʋ wʋn dɔgɛ la wʋn welge la to'ore, ba wʋn wi'ira ẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Dee tɩ dũnsĩima la daan lɛbra dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, ba wõm dee yẽ malɛka la n yele ba se'em na.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Bãŋɛ tɩ fʋ sõogɔ A'elizabɛtɩ tɩ ba yetɩ a de la kõndɔgrɛ la, a tarɩ la pʋʋrɛ wõr-sɩyoobɩ n wãna. Baa la ẽn kʋrgɛ la, a wʋn dɔgɛ la budibla.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: La dabsa anii la daarɛ n daan paagɛ, tɩ ba botɩ ba wã bia la wʋ Amoyisi gõŋɔ n pa'alɛ tɩ ba wã ba budimto se'em na. Tɩ ba sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi, yʋ'ʋr-sɛka tɩ malɛka daan yele ba tɩ ba sɩ ẽ na, wakat-sɛka tɩ Amaari daan nãn ka yõkɛ a pʋʋrɛ la.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sɛla sɛla ka boe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka tã ɩta.».

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: La wakatɛ la n paagɛ tɩ Amaari malgɛ a mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na. Tɩ Azozɛfʋ la Amaari tarɩ bia la sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm Wẽnde-kãtʋm tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Tɩ Amaari lerge yetɩ: «Mam de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ tõntõn-pɔka. Nawẽnnɛ botɩ la ẽŋɛ wʋ fõn yele se'em na.» Tɩ malɛka la fõrgɛ dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: Se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam tɩ budib-kẽema woo wʋn dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Na-keko Atibɛɛrɩ n daan di na'am na, a yʋʋma pia la anuu yʋʋnɛ, tɩ Apõnsɩ-pilatɩ de Zude gɔmna, dee tɩ A'erɔdɩ dɛna Galile naba, tɩ a sɔbia Afilipi mẽ dɛna Itiire la Trakoniti naba. Tɩ Alisaniyasɩ dɛna Abilɛnɩ naba.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Ba daan sẽŋɛ tɩ ba kãabɛ la sɛla bɔ Nawẽnnɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na. La yetɩ, ba bɔ mõom dawɛma bayi bɩɩ na'adawen-biɛ bayi.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: La dabsa bãma pʋam, tɩ Amaari isge sẽŋɛ tɔtɔ paagɛ Zude so'olʋm tẽŋa ayɩla tɩ tãnsɩ boe mĩ.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: La A'annɩ, la Akayifu de la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma. Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azakari bia Azã daan boe mõom tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ bɔ ẽ.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ bʋraa daan boe Zerizalɛm tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Asimeyõ. A de la nẽr-sõŋɔ dee sakra Nawẽnnɛ sõŋa, dee gu'ura Ɩsrayɛlɩ Fãagra la n sẽm na. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ẽ.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: La ẽn paagɛ la, a kẽ la Azakari yire pʋ'ʋsɛ A'elizabɛtɩ.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Bɛla tɩ Azã sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛba n boe Zʋrdẽ kʋlaa nõorʋm la wuu ka mõola yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la daan põn yele ẽ tɩ a kãn ki, sãn dagna tɩ a yẽ la Krisi tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn tõm tɩ a wa'am na.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: La A'elizabɛtɩ n wõm a pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, bia la wẽrgɛ mɛ a pʋʋrʋm. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wa'am A'elizabɛtɩ zẽ'am la pãŋa.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Azã n mõolɩ se'em na ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Nẽra n mõolɩ weem tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: Malgɛ-ya sore sõŋa gu'ura Zuudãana, malgɛ-ya sɔa la tɩ ba tɔ tẽntẽesɩ bɔ ẽ.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: tɩ a tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la fõn yel-sõnnɛ gãnna pɔgsɩ la wuu. La bia la tɩ fʋ wʋn dɔgɛ la, tarɩ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la botɩ a sẽŋɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi sɔ, la a ma daan tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ n yele se'em na.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Palɛ-ya bɔgsɩ la wuu, sirge-ya tãnsɩ la kũnkũma la wuu, mãalɛ-ya so-gɔlma tɩ ba tɔ tẽntẽesɩ, palɛ-ya sorɔɔrɔ bogro tɩ ba naagɛ taaba.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Mam de la ãne tɩ mam Zuudãana ma sẽna mam zẽ'am?.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Tɩ Asimeyõ to'oge bia la a nu'usum, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Bɛla nẽrsaalba wuu wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em fãagra nẽrba basra.».

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam wõm fõn pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, bia la n boe mam pʋʋrʋm na wẽrgɛ mɛ la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: «Zuudãana, lɛɛlɛ wã, fõn bĩŋe nõorɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ mɛ. Fʋ yãŋa tã basɛ mam n de fõn tõntõnna sore tɩ m basɛ dũnia dee wa'am fõn yire la sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: La nẽrba zozo'e n sẽŋɛ ka lagsɛ Azã zẽ'am tɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam. Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ãn wʋ kãmpẽesɩ buuri, ãne n yele yãma yetɩ, ya tã wʋn zoe põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ n sẽm na?.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Fõn sakɛ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ a tɔgɛ bɔ fʋ la wʋn sɩrɩ ẽŋɛ la, fʋ yẽ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Se'ere n soe la, m nini yẽ Fãagra la tɩ fʋ tõm na.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Tõm-ya tõon-sɛba n pa'alɩ tɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Da kãn tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ Abraham n de yãma yaaba la, ya wʋn põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ mɛ. Mam yetɩ yãma mɛ tɩ, Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn dɩkɛ kuga wã botɩ ba lebge Abraham yɩɩsɩ.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Tɩ Amaari yele yetɩ: «Mam pẽgrɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ la m sũure wuu.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: ẽŋa tɩ fʋ mãasʋm bɔ tɩ dũnia nẽrba wuu yẽ.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nẽra n põn tara lɩa a nu'usum tɩ a lʋbɛ tɩɩsɩ. Tɩ-sɛka woo n ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma la, a wʋn lʋbɛ ẽ mɛ, lobge bugum pʋam tɩ bugum di ẽ.».

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: Mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ n de mam Fãagra la ĩyã.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Ẽŋa n de peelem n wʋn botɩ la nẽegɛ bɔ buuri wuu tɩ ba bãŋɛ fõn, dee wʋn botɩ tõma Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n de fõn nẽrba yẽ pẽka.».

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?».

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: se'ere n soe la, a bɩsɛ mam n de a tõntõn-pɔg-tarma la yelle mɛ. Bɛla lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã wɛ'ɛsa, zamãana wuu nẽrba wʋn wi'ira mam tɩ zu-yẽlga dãana.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: La Asimeyõ n tɔgɛ bia la yɛla la, la daan di'ige bia la sɔ, la a ma mɛ.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ, sɛka n tarɩ futo bayi, a looge bɔ sɛka n ka tarɩ. Tɩ sɛka n tarɩ dia, mẽ ẽŋɛ bɔ sɛka n ka tarɩ.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n tã wuu la n ẽŋɛ bõn-kãra bɔ mam. A de la pʋpeelem dãana.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Tɩ Asimeyõ kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ba, dee yele bia la ma Amaari tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge bi-ẽna tɩ a botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma zozo'e lui mɛ, dee tɩ zozo'e mẽ isge. Ẽŋa n wʋn pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, dee tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn zagsɛ ẽ.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba mẽ wa'am tɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?».

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n zotɩ ẽ na, a zotɩ ba ninbãalga mɛ zamãana wuu.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: Ẽŋa n wʋn nẽegɛ pa'alɛ sɛla n sʋgɛ ba pʋtẽ'era pʋam na. Dee yetɩ, Amaari mẽŋa wʋn yẽ sũ-sã'aŋɔ wʋ sʋ'a tɩ ba sɩɩsɛ lʋ nẽra sũure la.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Da to'oge-ya ligri gãŋɛ ban yele ya se'em na.».

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: A dɩkɛ la a nu'ugo tõm tõon-kãra, dee botɩ zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma la zoe wɩrgɛ la ba zẽk-m-mẽŋa pʋtẽ'era la.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-pɔka n mẽ daan boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna A'annɩ. Tɩ a sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Afanuyɛlɩ, a de la Azɛɛrɩ buuri nẽra. Ẽŋa daan de la pug-sa'arɛ dee ele sɩra paagɛ yʋʋma ayopɔɩ tɩ a sɩra la ki.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ mẽ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Dee tõma, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?» Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Da to'oge-ya nẽra bõnɔ la pãŋa bɩɩ dõrɛ nẽrba to'oge sɛla. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya yɔɔrɔ la seke ya.».

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: La a sɩra la n ki la, a yãŋa de la pɔkõorɛ kʋrgɛ halɩ ka paagɛ yʋʋma pinii la anaasɩ. A daan boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na wakatɛ wuu, tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ wuu ka basra, dee lʋta nõorɛ sɔsra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: La sãn dɛna pãnsɩ dõma, a sikri ba mɛ ba na'am pʋam, dee zẽkra sik-m-mẽŋa dõma.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Tɩ nẽrba daan zɛ gu'ura tɩ Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ ba, la ba wuu daan sokrɩ la ba mẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ, Azã na'an dɛna Krisi tɩ ba gu'ura la bɩɩ?.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a paagɛ ba zẽ'am, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, dee tɔgra bia la yelle bɔ'ɔra sɛba wuu n gu'uri wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn le fãagɛ Zerizalɛm nẽrba la basɛ.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: La sɛba n ka tarɩ sɛla la, a bɔ'ɔrɩ ba bõntarsõm zɔ'ɔra mɛ, dee botɩ bõntatba lebe fõrgɛ dee ka to'oge sɛla sɛla.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: La ban tẽ'esrɩ bɛla la, tɩ Azã yele ba yetɩ: «Mam misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la nẽra n boe tara pãŋa gãnna mam sẽna, la mam põn ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra bɔ ẽ. Ẽŋa n wʋn mise ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la bugum pʋam.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: La ban ẽŋɛ sɛla woo ba'asɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, tɩ Azozɛfʋ la Amaari lebe kule Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm na.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: A tẽerɩ zɔta tõma Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba n de a tõntõnɩba la ninbãalga mɛ, dee sõŋra tõma.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: A wʋn welge nẽrba wʋ nẽra n zãlɩ pɩ'ɩgɔ a nu'usum tɩ a vaagɛ a si zɛɛŋa yeele ẽŋɛ a bãarɛ pʋam, dee yõ urgo la, la bugum n ka kĩŋri.».

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Tɩ bia la bɩta tara pãŋa la yɛm, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔna la ẽ.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: wʋ ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tõma yaaba Abraham, la tõma sɔ-yaabdõma, la ba yɩɩsɩ la wuu tɩ a wʋn sõŋra tõma wakatɛ wuu se'em na.».

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Yetɔg-bãna, la yetɔga basɛba zozo'e tɩ Azã daan dɩkɛ zãsʋm nẽrba la pa'alɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Yʋʋnɛ woo Azezi sɔ, la a ma sẽnnɩ la Zerizalɛm dita Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: La Amaari daan weege A'elizabɛtɩ zẽ'am ẽŋɛ wʋ wõr-sɩtã, dee yãŋa lebe kule.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: La Azã daan zɛrgɛ naba A'erɔdɩ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a daan fãagɛ la a kẽema pɔga yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'erodɩadɩ di, dee tõm tõon-be'ero basɛba zozo'e pa'asɛ, tɩ Azã yele ẽ yetɩ la ka ãn sõŋa.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: La Azezi daan tarɩ la yʋʋma pia la ayi, dee tɩ ba sẽŋɛ kibsa la wʋ ban ẽn ɩta se'em na.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: A'elizabɛtɩ dɔka wakatɛ n daan paagɛ la, a dɔgɛ la budibla.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: La A'erɔdɩ daan yõkɛ Azã ka pagɛ mɛ, bɛla A'erɔdɩ malɩn tõm la tõon-be'ero pa'asɛ.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: La kibsa la dabsa n ba'asɛ la, tɩ ba lebe kulla. Dee tɩ Azezi weege Zerizalɛm, tɩ a sɔ la a ma daan ka bãŋɛ tɩ a weege ya.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Tɩ a sawiiba, la a yir-dõma wõm tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ zoe a ninbãalga zo'oge, tɩ ba wuu lagɩm ɩta sũ-yẽlga la ẽ.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Azã n daan misri nẽrba wuu ko'om pʋam na, a daan mẽ mise Azezi mɛ. La Azezi daan pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ saazuum yo'oge.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Ba daan tẽ'esɛ tɩ a dolɩ la ba tẽŋa dõma, dee sẽŋɛ dabsrɛ mõonɛ sẽnnɛ. La ban ɛ ẽ ba nẽrba la, la ba sɩrdõma zẽ'am dee ka yẽ ẽ na.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Bia la dɔka dabsa anii daarɛ tɩ ba wa'am tɩ ba wã ẽ, daan bɔɔra tɩ ba sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ a sɔ Azakari yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sige a zuo, tɩ ba yẽ ẽ tɩ a wõn na na'adawenne. Tɩ kõa ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Fõn de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu. Fʋ paagɛ la mam yɛm.».

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: ba daan lebe la Zerizalɛm ka ɛɛra ẽ.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: La a ma ka sakɛ, yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, a yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn dɛna la Azã.».

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: La Azezi n daan pĩ'ilum a tõoma la, a daan tarɩ la yʋʋma pitã, tɩ nẽrba daan tẽ'esra tɩ a de la Azozɛfʋ bia. Azozɛfʋ sɔ daan de la Aheli.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: La dabsa atã daarɛ tɩ ba yẽ ẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ a zĩ la karẽnsãandõma sɛlsra, dee sɔkra ba sokrɩsɩ.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ya yir-dõma la pʋam nẽra ka boe mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azã.».

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: tɩ Aheli sɔ dɛna Amata, tɩ Amata sɔ dɛna Alevi, tɩ Alevi sɔ dɛna Amelki, tɩ Amelki sɔ dɛna Azanayi, tɩ Azanayi sɔ dɛna Azozɛfʋ.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Azezi n daan tarɩ yɛm la ẽn lergrɩ se'em na, di'ige sɛba wuu n daan wõm na mɛ.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Tɩ ba yɩbsɛ la nu'usi soke bia la sɔ tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ ba sɩ bia la yʋ'ʋrɛ se'em.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: tɩ Azozɛfʋ sɔ dɛna Amatatɩasɩ, tɩ Amatatɩasɩ sɔ dɛna Amõsɩ, tɩ Amõsɩ sɔ dɛna Anahum, tɩ Anahum sɔ dɛna A'esli, tɩ A'esli sɔ dɛna Anagayi.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: La a dɔgrɩba la n yẽ ẽ na, la ba di'ige ba mɛ. Tɩ a ma soke ẽ yetɩ: «M bia, bẽm tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ tɩ wãna? Mam la fʋ sɔ ɛ fʋ mɛ halɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ.».

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Tɩ a botɩ ba bɔ ẽ dɔ-palaka, tɩ a to'oge gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «A yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azã.» Tɩ la di'ige nẽrba la waa wuu.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: tɩ Anagayi sɔ dɛna Amatɩ, tɩ Amatɩ sɔ dɛna Amatatɩasɩ, tɩ Amatatɩasɩ sɔ dɛna Asemeyẽ, tɩ Asemeyẽ sɔ dɛna Azozɛkɩ, tɩ Azozɛkɩ sɔ dɛna Azoda.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya ɛɛra ma? Yãma ka mi tɩ la masɛ mɛ tɩ mam bɔna m Sɔ yire wã bɩɩ?».

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: La ẽn gʋlsɛ yele bɛla la, bilam mẽŋa tɩ a zɩlɩŋa lakɛ, tɩ a tã'agɛ tɔgra pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: tɩ Azoda sɔ dɛna Azoanã, tɩ Azoanã sɔ dɛna Areza, tɩ Areza sɔ dɛna Azorobabɛlɩ, tɩ Azorobabɛlɩ sɔ dɛna Asalatɩyɛlɩ, tɩ Asalatɩyɛlɩ sɔ dɛna Aneri.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Tɩ ba ka bãŋɛ Azezi yetɔga la võorɛ.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ sɛba n lɛm bilam na wuu. Tɩ kõ-ẽŋa saagɛ Zude tẽn-sɛba tɩ tãnsɩ boe mĩ na wuu.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: tɩ Aneri sɔ dɛna Amelki, tɩ Amelki sɔ dɛna Adi, tɩ Adi sɔ dɛna Akozam, tɩ Akozam sɔ dɛna A'elmadam, tɩ A'elmadam sɔ dɛna A'eri.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Tɩ Azezi doose ba kule Nazarɛtɩ, dee sakɛ ba nõorɛ. Tɩ a ma tẽ'esra yel-bãma wuu a sũurum.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Bãma wuu n daan wõm yɛla la, ba daan tẽ'esrɩ mɛ dee sɔkra yetɩ: «Bi-ẽna yetɩ a wa ãna la wãne?» Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa boe la ẽ mɛ.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: tɩ A'eri sɔ dɛna Azoze, tɩ Azoze sɔ dɛna A'eliezɛɛrɩ, tɩ A'eliezɛɛrɩ sɔ dɛna Azorim, tɩ Azorim sɔ dɛna Amata, tɩ Amata sɔ dɛna Alevi.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩta, tɩ a yɛm pa'asra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ la nẽrba nõŋɛ ẽ.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wa'am bia la sɔ Azakari zẽ'am la pãŋa. Tɩ a to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ yetɩ:.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Azezi daan ze'ele la Zʋrdẽ kʋlaam lebe, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la kɔ'ɔn bɔna la ẽ. La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ la weem.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: tɩ Alevi sɔ dɛna Asimeyõ, tɩ Asimeyõ sɔ dɛna Azuda, tɩ Azuda sɔ dɛna Azozɛfʋ, tɩ Azozɛfʋ sɔ dɛna Azonam, tɩ Azonam sɔ dɛna A'eliakim.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: «Basɛ-ya tɩ pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ Zuudãana la, se'ere n soe la, a wa'am mɛ wa bɩsɛ a nẽrba yelle, fãagɛ ba basɛ.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: Bilam tɩ Asũtãana makɛ ẽ dabsa pinaasɩ. La dabsa pinaasɩ la pʋam a ka di sɛla sɛla, tɩ a kɔm dõnna.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: tɩ A'eliakim sɔ dɛna Ameleya, tɩ Ameleya sɔ dɛna Amena, tɩ Amena sɔ dɛna Amatata, tɩ Amatata sɔ dɛna Anatã, tɩ Anatã sɔ dɛna Adavidi.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: A bɔ tõma la Fãagra n tarɩ pãŋa n wʋn yese a tõntõnna Adavidi buuri la pʋam.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Bɛla tɩ Asũtãana yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ yele kugre wã tɩ a lebge dia.».

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: tɩ Adavidi sɔ dɛna Azese, tɩ Azese sɔ dɛna A'obɛɛdɩ, tɩ A'obɛɛdɩ sɔ dɛna Aboozɩ, tɩ Aboozɩ sɔ dɛna Asala, tɩ Asala sɔ dɛna Anaasõ.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: wʋ ẽŋa n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔgra kʋrʋm se'em na.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nẽrsaala kãn vɔa dia mã'a ĩyã.› ».

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: tɩ Anaasõ sɔ dɛna Aminadabɩ, tɩ Aminadabɩ sɔ dɛna Admẽ, tɩ Admẽ sɔ dɛna Arni, tɩ Arni sɔ dɛna A'ɛsrɔm, tɩ A'ɛsrɔm sɔ dɛna Afarɛsɩ, tɩ Afarɛsɩ sɔ dɛna Azuda.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: A yetɩ a wʋn fãagɛ tɔ tɩ bɛ'ɛba nu'usum, la nẽr-sɛba n sisri tõma la nu'usum.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka zom tãŋa zuo, pa'alɛ ẽ dũnia so'olʋm wuu nif-kabsɩgɔ pʋam.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: tɩ Azuda sɔ dɛna Azakɔbɩ, tɩ Azakɔbɩ sɔ dɛna A'izakɩ, tɩ A'izakɩ sɔ dɛna Abraham, tɩ Abraham sɔ dɛna Atara, tɩ Atara sɔ dɛna Anakɔɔrɩ.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Bɛla, a zoe tɩ yaabdõma ninbãalga mɛ, dee tẽegɛ ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la ba la yelle.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: tɩ Anakɔɔrɩ sɔ dɛna Aseruki, tɩ Aseruki sɔ dɛna Arago, tɩ Arago sɔ dɛna Afalɛkɩ, tɩ Afalɛkɩ sɔ dɛna A'ebɛrɩ, tɩ A'ebɛrɩ sɔ dɛna Asala.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bãna wuu de la mam n soe, bɛla mam tã wʋn bɔ nẽr-sɛka woo tɩ mam boorɩ mɛ. Mam wʋn bɔ fɔ dũnia wã na'am la wuu, la so'olʋm bãma wuu pẽka.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ mɛ la tɩ yaaba Abraham.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: tɩ Asala sɔ dɛna Akayinam, tɩ Akayinam sɔ dɛna Arfasadɩ, tɩ Arfasadɩ sɔ dɛna Asɛm, tɩ Asɛm sɔ dɛna Anoe, tɩ Anoe sɔ dɛna Alemɛkɩ.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Fʋ sãn ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asɛ mam, ba wuu wʋn dɛna la fõn dẽnnɛ.».

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: tɩ a wʋn fãagɛ tɔ tɩ bɛ'ɛba nu'usum, dee wʋn botɩ tɩ tõm a tõoma, tɩ dabeem kana.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nã'asra Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, dee sakra ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a.› ».

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: tɩ Alemɛkɩ sɔ dɛna Amatusala, tɩ Amatusala sɔ dɛna A'enõkɩ, tɩ A'enõkɩ sɔ dɛna Azarɛdɩ, tɩ Azarɛdɩ sɔ dɛna Amalelɛlɩ, tɩ Amalelɛlɩ sɔ dɛna Akayinam.

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana le tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm ka zom Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zuo yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ ze'ele kalam ẽkɛ bisge tẽŋa.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: Dee dɔla ẽ la pʋpeelem, dee ɩta tɩ la masra ẽn boorɩ se'em ka paagɛ tɩ vɔm tɛka.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: tɩ Akayinam sɔ dɛna A'enõsɩ, tɩ A'enõsɩ sɔ dɛna Asetɩ, tɩ Asetɩ sɔ dɛna Adam, la Nawẽnnɛ n nãam Adam.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ wʋn tõm a malɛkadõma tɩ ba gu fo.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: La fõn, m bia, ba wʋn wi'ira fʋ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu nõtɔ'ɔsa. Bɛla ĩyã, fõn wʋn dẽŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana la nẽŋam malgɛ sore bɔ ẽ.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: La ba wʋn kɔkɛ fɔ la ba nu'usi tɩ fʋ kãn wẽ fʋ nã'arɛ la kugre.› ».

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: dee yele Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ a wʋn fãagɛ ba basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yele tɩ: ‹Da makɛ fʋ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ.› ».

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n zotɩ tõma ninbãalga la ĩyã tɩ a wʋn botɩ a peelem n ãn wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa n yẽkɛ bulika la.

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: La Asũtãana n daan makɛ Azezi bɛla wuu zɩgɛ koŋe la, a basɛ ẽ dee gu'ura la a sãn le yẽ folle.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: nẽegɛ bɔ nẽr-sɛba n boe lika pʋam, la nẽr-sɛba n zotɩ kũm dabeem na, dee pa'alɛ tɩ so-sɛka tɩ tɩ wʋn doose yẽ sũ-mã'asʋm.».

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: La Azezi daan lebe Galile la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la pãŋa, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ kɔ'ɔn saagɛ zẽ'a wuu.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Tɩ bia la daan bɩta, tɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ pa'asra. A daan boe la weem ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ a wa'am Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la zẽ'am tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽ.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: La a ẽn zãsna la nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, tɩ ba wuu pẽgra ẽ.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: La Azezi daan sẽŋɛ la Nazarɛtɩ, ban daan uge ẽ tẽn-sɛka la. Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ a kẽ ba wẽndeem wʋ ẽŋa ẽn kẽ'era se'em na, yãŋa isge ze'ele tɩ a karẽŋɛ.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ gõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ la bɔ ẽ. Tɩ a lakɛ gõŋɔ la yẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a boorɩ la, yãŋa karẽŋɛ wʋ zẽ'a la n yele se'em na:.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe la mam, ẽŋa n looge mam bɔ mam pãŋa, tɩ m bʋglɛ sũ-sã'aŋɔ dõma sũure, tɩ m mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ nasdõma, dee tõm mam tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ yamsɩ tɩ ba wʋn yẽ yolsgo, dee tɔgɛ bɔ fɔɔsɩ tɩ ba nini wʋn pike, dee yese sɛba n boe nãmsgɔ pʋam la basɛ.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: dee mõolɛ tɩ wakatɛ paagɛ ya, tɩ Zuudãana wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽrba yel-sõnnɛ.».

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: La ẽn karẽŋɛ ba'asɛ la, a lebe laglɛ gõŋɔ la mɛ bɔ sɛka n gu'uri gõnnɔ la, dee zĩ'ire. Tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe wẽndeem na bɩsra ẽ zĩi.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Zĩna, lɛɛlɛ wã, sɛla tɩ ba gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ yãma wõm wã, la ẽŋɛ wʋ lan yele se'em na.».

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Tɩ ba tɔgɛ pẽgɛ ẽ, la a yetɔg-sõma la tɩ a tɔgɛ la di'ige ba mɛ. Dee tɩ ba sɔkra yetɩ, la ka de Azozɛfʋ dayʋa la n wãna?.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam põn mina tɩ ya wʋn makɛ makrɛ ẽna bɔ mam yetɩ: ‹Tɩɩm dãana, tɩbɛ fʋ mẽŋa.› La ya wʋn ken yele mam yetɩ: ‹Bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ daan ẽŋɛ Kapɛrnaʋm tẽŋam tɩ tõma wõm na, ẽŋɛ bɛla nõo fʋ mẽŋa tẽŋam kalam mẽ.› ».

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ba ka nannɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa baa ayɩla a mẽŋa tẽŋam.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: La de la sɩra tɩ mam yetɩ ya tɩ: Pɔkõpa zozo'e yʋʋn boe Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋam A'eli wakatʋm na mɛ, la saaga ka ni yʋʋma atã la wõr-sɩyoobɩ, tɩ kɔm yʋʋn ba bɔna tẽŋa la wuu pʋam.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ ka tõm A'eli yetɩ a sẽŋɛ pɔkõ-bãma zẽ'am. A tõm ẽ na pɔkõorɛ n boe Sarɛpɩta n boe Sidõ so'olʋm pʋam na zẽ'am.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: La kõnkɔma zozo'e yʋʋn boe Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋam A'elize wakatʋm na mɛ, la baa ayɩla ka yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, la Anãamã sɛka n yese Siiri so'olʋm na mã'a n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: La ban wõm bɛla la, sɛba n boe wẽndeego la pʋam wuu sũure daan yĩige mɛ.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Tɩ ba isge ve'ege Azezi tẽŋa la pʋam yese. Tẽŋa la boe la tãŋa zuo. Tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka n zãagɛ la tẽŋa la, ba daan boorɩ tɩ ba zã'agɛ ẽ basɛ mɛ tɩ a lui ki.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: La Azezi kẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tẽŋasʋka fõrgɛ.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la Galile tẽŋa ayẽma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Kapɛrnaʋm, tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ daan paagɛ, tɩ a sẽŋɛ ba wẽndeem ka zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Tɩ a zãsŋɔ la daan di'ige ba, a yetɔga la n tarɩ pãŋa la ĩyã.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Tɩ bʋraa daan bɔna wẽndeem na tɩ kʋlkã'arga tara ẽ, tɩ a tĩi kaasɛ yetɩ:.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: «A'a! Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra, fõn bẽm yelle n boe la tõma? Fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ sã'am na tõma? Mam mi fõn de sɛka, fõn de la pʋpeelem dãana n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Tɩ Azezi tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Go'oge, yese dee basɛ bʋraa la!» Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la lʋbɛ ẽ nẽrba la wuu nẽŋam, dee yese, la a ka pɔglʋm ẽ.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Tɩ ba wuu palẽŋa ẽkɛ tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Yetɔga ẽna de la bẽm? A tarɩ la pãŋa la ke'eŋo, dee tãsra kʋlkã'arsɩ, tɩ ba yɛsra.».

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Tɩ Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ saagɛ zẽ'e-bãma wuu.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Tɩ Azezi isge ba wẽndeego la pʋam yese ka kẽ Asimõ yire. Tɩ Asimõ dɛɛm-pɔka bẽ'era, tɩ a ĩyã ba tʋlgɛ. Tɩ ba tɔgɛ a yelle bɔ Azezi.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
