local: tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka suure a zuo, dee tãsɛ bã'a la yetɩ a basɛ ẽ. La ẽn yele la, bã'a la basɛ ẽ mɛ, tɩ a isge tɔtɔ ẽŋɛ ba sãanɔ.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan bɔna mĩ kẽ'era, tɩ ba tarɩ bã'adõma tɩ bã'asɩ buuri to'oto'ore tarɩ ba, sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am. Tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba nẽra woo zuo, tɩ ba wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: La kʋlkã'arsɩ daan yesrɩ mɛ dee basra nẽrba zozo'e, dee kaasra yetɩ: «Fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.» Tɩ Azezi tãsɛ ba, ka basɛ tɩ ba tɔgɛ sɛla sɛla, se'ere n soe la, ba daan bãŋɛ tɩ a de la Krisi n de Fãagra la.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azezi yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽra ka boe mĩ. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e ɛɛra ẽ. La ba daan yẽ ẽ mɛ, bɔɔra tɩ ba gu ẽ tɩ a da le yese ba zẽ'am.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: La Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ mam mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm n ãn se'em tẽnsɩ basɛba mẽ pʋam, la de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm mam.».

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: La a ẽn mõola kõ-yẽlga la Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam mɛ.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Daarɛ ayɩla Azezi ze la Zenezarɛtɩ mõgrɛ nõorʋm, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ kaagɛ ẽ mikra taaba bɔɔra tɩ ba wõm Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Tɩ a yẽ õornɔ bayi n ze mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, tɩ zũn-yõgrɩba sige pɛɛra ba yʋgsɩ dee basɛ.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: La õorŋɔ ayɩla daan de la Asimõ bõnɔ. Tɩ Azezi kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam, dee yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Mõm õorŋɔ la tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽŋa fẽe.» La Azezi n yele bɛla la, Asimõ mõm õorŋɔ la mɛ, tɩ a lebe ko'om pʋam fẽe. Tɩ Azezi zĩ'ire õorŋɔ la pʋam pa'alɛ nẽrba la.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: La ẽn pa'alɛ ba ba'asɛ la, tɩ a yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Mõm õorŋɔ la tɩ a sẽŋɛ kʋlko'om tɩ ya lobe yʋgsɩ la.».

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Tɩ Asimõ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma nãmsɛ mɛ yʋ'ʋŋɔ wã wuu dee ka yõkɛ sɛla sɛla. La fõn yele la ĩyã, tɩ wʋn lobe tɩ yʋgsɩ la.».

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ kʋlko'om ka lobe ba yʋgsɩ la, yãŋa yõgɛ zũma ba zo'oge, tɩ ba yʋgsɩ la zɩgra la ba ãasʋm.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Tɩ ba yɩbsɛ ba taaba õorŋɔ ayɩla la pʋam yetɩ, ba wa'am sõŋɛ ba. La ba taaba la sẽŋɛ mɛ ka sõŋɛ ba, tɩ ba pɩrɛ õornɔ bayi la wuu la zũma, halɩ tɩ ba zɩgra la ba mise.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: La Asimõ yʋ'ʋrɛ ayɩla n de Apɩyɛɛrɩ n yẽ bɛla la, tɩ a ka dũma tẽŋa Azezi nẽŋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõrgɛ mam zẽ'am, se'ere n soe la, mam de la tõon-be'ero dãana.».

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Se'ere n soe tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele bɛla la, ẽŋa la a taaba la n daan yẽ zũma la tɩ ba yõgɛ se'em na botɩ ba palẽŋa n ẽkɛ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: La dabeem mẽ n daan kẽ Azebede dayɔɔsɩ Azakɩ la Azã n daan ẽn dɔla la Asimõ tõnna la. Tɩ Azezi yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, tɩ lan sẽŋɛ zĩna wɛ'ɛsa wuu, fʋ wʋn ɛɛra la nẽrba tɩ ba dɔla mam wʋ yãma n yõgrɩ zũma se'em na.».

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: La Azezi n yele bɛla la, ba tarɩ õornɔ la dooge mɛ yãŋa basɛ sɛla woo dee dɔla Azezi.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: La Azezi daan boe la tẽŋa ayẽma pʋam, tɩ bʋraa mẽ bɔna mĩ dɛna kõnkonne, la bã'a la kẽ a ĩyã zo'oge mɛ. Tɩ a yẽ Azezi ka ka dũma Azezi nẽŋam dɩkɛ a nẽŋa võglɛ tẽŋa sose ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fʋ sãn bɔɔra, fʋ tã wʋn basɛ tɩ m yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ le ãna sõŋa.».

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Tɩ Azezi tẽegɛ a nu'ugo kalʋm ẽ dee yetɩ: «Mam bɔɔra, yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa.» Tɩ bã'a la basɛ ẽ bilam mẽŋa.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da kãn yele nẽra nẽra, la sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽema zẽ'am ka pa'alɛ ẽ fʋ mẽŋa tɩ a bɩsɛ, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɛla tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yetɩ ba bɔ la, tɩ la pa'alɛ ba tɩ fʋ sɩrɩ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: La bɛla wuu, Azezi yelle daan malɩn saagɛ la zẽ'esɩ zozo'e, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e lagsɛ tɩ ba selse a yetɔga la, dee tɩ a botɩ ba bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: La Azezi ẽn sẽnna la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ka boe la pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: La daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Azezi bɔna mĩ pa'ala nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, tɩ Farisi dõma, la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n ze'ele Galile, la Zude, la Zerizalɛm mẽ zẽa mĩ sɛlsra. Tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa daan bɔna la Azezi, tɩ a botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Tɩ bʋraasɩ zɛ bʋraa n de kɔɔrŋɔ gã a bõn-gã'ala pʋam wa'am. La ba boorɩ tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ kẽ la de-sɛka tɩ Azezi boe mĩ na pʋam, ka bĩŋe ẽ Azezi nẽŋam.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: La nẽrba la mike mɛ, tɩ ba ka yẽ folle tarɩ ẽ kẽ. Bɛla ĩyã tɩ ba zɛ kɔɔrŋɔ la zom gɔsgɔ, ka lakɛ gɔsgɔ la bɔbrɛ ayɩla, doose bilam sike ẽ la a bõn-gã'ala la nẽrba la sʋkam bĩŋe ẽ Azezi nẽŋam.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ ban bɔ ẽŋa sɩra se'em na, yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «M sɩrɛ, fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna fʋ zuo.».

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma la tẽ'esra yetɩ: «A tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la ãne tɔgra pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ. Ãne n tã wʋn basɛ nẽra tõon-be'ero taalɛ, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ mã'a?».

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya tẽ'esra wãna wã?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Bẽm n de nãana gãnna, mam sãn yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ, a tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna a zuo n de nãana, bɩɩ mam sãn yele ẽ yetɩ, a isge sẽnna n de nãana?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Bɛla m wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la tarɩ pãŋa dũnia zuo wʋn basɛ nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.» A yele bɛla dee yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ fʋ isge dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala la kule.».

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ kɔɔrŋɔ la isge ba wuu nẽŋam dɩkɛ a bõn-gã'ala la kulla, dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la wuu palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ dabeem kẽ ba, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Tɩ zĩna yẽ bõn-yãlma.».

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi yese sẽŋɛ ka yẽ lampo-tɔ'ɔsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Alevi, tɩ a zĩ lampo to'osgo deem. Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge dɔla mam.».

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Tɩ a isge basɛ sɛla woo dee dɔla ẽ.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Tɩ Alevi dʋgɛ dia zo'oge ẽŋɛ kibsa a yire bɔ Azezi, tɩ ẽŋa la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la nẽrba zozo'e zẽa dita.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba, la ba lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma yõnna Azezi karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma lagɩm na lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma dita?».

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm ka eerɩ tɩba. Sɛba n bẽ'erɩ n eerɩ tɩba.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Mam ka wa'am dũnia zuo sɛba dõma n yetɩ bãma masɛ ya la ĩyã, mam wa'am tɩ m yele la tõon-be'ero dõma tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.».

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la yele Azezi yetɩ: «Azã karẽnbiisi, la Farisi dõma karẽnbiisi ẽn lʋta nõorɛ mɛ dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, dee tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi dita dee yũura.».

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽra n di pɔka ẽŋɛ kibsa, tɩ a tadãandõma bɔna mĩ tɩ ba naagɛ la ẽ di kibsa la, yãma wʋn sakɛ tɩ ba lʋ nõorɛ mɛ dee tɩ pɔgdita la ken bɔna ba zẽ'am bɩɩ?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ayɛɩ. La wakatɛ wʋn wa'am tɩ ba wʋn yese pɔgdita la ba zẽ'am, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yãŋa wʋn lʋ nõorɛ.».

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Tɩ Azezi le makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nẽra kãn ãagɛ fu-paalɛ dɩkɛ lige fu-kɛka. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, a wʋn sã'am fu-paalɛ la mɛ, la tãn-paalɛ la kãn masɛ la fu-kɛka la.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: La nẽra mẽ kãn dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm wɔ-kɛgsɩ pʋam. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, dã-paalga la sãn kɛlla, ba wʋn botɩ wɔɔsɩ la tãsɛ mɛ. Bɛla dãam na wʋn ka'agɛ mɛ sã'am, tɩ wɔɔsɩ la mẽ sã'am.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: La masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm na wɔ-paalsɩ pʋam.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: La nẽra sãn yũ dã-kɛka n kele la, a ka le bɔɔra dã-paalga. A wʋn yetɩ, dã-kɛka la n ãn sõŋa.».

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi dɔla vatɔ pʋam tɔlla, tɩ a karẽnbiisi kɔ si wẽena ɔbra.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma nẽrba basɛba yẽ ban ẽŋɛ bɛla la, yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma ẽŋɛ sɛla n sisri tõma vo'osgo daarɛ?».

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka karẽŋɛ Adavidi la a nẽrba la kɔm n yʋʋn dõnnɩ tɩ Adavidi ẽŋɛ se'em na?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: A yʋʋn kẽ la Nawẽnnɛ deego pʋam dɩkɛ borborɩ sɛba tɩ ba bĩŋe bɔ Nawẽnnɛ na obe. La sisri mɛ, nẽra nẽra ka obrɩ ba, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma la mã'a. La Adavidi yʋʋn obe borborɩ bãma mɛ, dee bɔ a nẽrba la tɩ ba obe.».

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: La Azezi ken yele ba mɛ yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n soe vo'osgo daarɛ.».

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Vo'osgo daarɛ ayẽma tɩ Azezi kẽ Zifdõma wẽndeem zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. Tɩ bʋraa daan bɔna mĩ, tɩ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo ki.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma la daan bɩsra Azezi wʋn botɩ nẽra la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ la bɩɩ, a kãn botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Se'ere n soe la, ba daan eerɩ la so-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn yetɩ a sã'am bãma sisgo dõrɛ ẽ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ba tẽ'esgɔ la, yele bʋraa la nu'ugo n ki la yetɩ: «Isge ze'ele nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka.» Tɩ a isge ze'ele.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ m soke ya mɛ bɩsɛ, sore boe tɩ nẽra ẽŋɛ sõŋa vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a ẽŋɛ be'em? Sore boe tɩ a fãagɛ nẽra vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a kʋ ẽ?».

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Dee bɩsɛ ba wuu kaagɛ, dee yele bʋraa la yetɩ: «Tẽegɛ fʋ nu'ugo la.» Tɩ a tẽegɛ a nu'ugo la, tɩ a nu'ugo la yãŋa tẽegɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Tɩ ba sũnsõa yĩige halɩ tɩ ba bãnna ban wʋn ẽŋɛ Azezi se'em.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi daan sẽŋɛ ka zom tãŋa zuo tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, bɔna bilam pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋŋɔ wuu.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a wi a karẽnbiisi la, tɩ ba wa'am a zẽ'am. Tɩ a looge karẽnbiisi pia la ayi bãma pʋam. Bãma n de nẽr-sɛba tɩ a wʋn tõm na.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Ba daan de la Asimõ tɩ Azezi sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la A'ãndre n de Apɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbga, la Azakɩ, la Azã, la Afilipi, la Abatelemi.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: la Amatie, la Atoma, la Azakɩ sɛka n de Alfe bia la, la Asimõ sɛka n daan de Zelɔtɩ nẽra ayɩla la.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: la Azudaasɩ n de Azakɩ bia la, la Azudaasɩ Iskariyo sɛka n wʋn zãm Azezi la.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: La Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la ze'ele la tãŋa la zuo sige zẽ'e-sɛka n naagɛ taaba. La a karẽnbiisi basɛba zozo'e, la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e n yese Zude, la Zerizalɛm, la Tiiri la Sidõ so'olʋm n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm na n lagsɛ bilam.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Ba wa'am tɩ ba selse la Azezi yetɔga, dee tɩ a bɔ ba bã'adõma ĩmã'asʋm. La nẽrba basɛba n daan boe mĩ tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dɔla ba, tɩ ba daan yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu ɛɛra tɩ ba kalʋm ẽ, se'ere n soe la, pãŋa n yesrɩ a pʋam bɔ'ɔra ba wuu ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Tɩ Azezi gorge bɩsɛ a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Yãma sɛba n de nasdõma la tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la ya bõnɔ.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Yãma sɛba kɔm n dõnnɩ lɛɛlɛ wã tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn ka di seke. Yãma sɛba n kaasrɩ lɛɛlɛ wã tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn ka la'ara.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Yãma sɛba tɩ nẽrba sisri ya, dee bɔkra ya, tʋʋra ya, dee ka nanna ya wʋ ya de la tõon-be'ero dõma, mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ĩyã la, tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Wakat-sɛka tɩ ba ɩtɩ yãma bɛla la, ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn yẽ yɔɔrɔ zo'oge mɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire. Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ ba yaabdõma yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la bɛla.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: La yãma sɛba n de bõntatba la wʋn yẽ toogo, yãma n põn yẽ bõn-sõma la ĩyã.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: La yãma sɛba n tarɩ sɛla woo lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ la seke la, wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn ka sẽnna la kɔm. La yãma sɛba n la'arɩ lɛɛlɛ wã wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya sũure wʋn ka sã'am tɩ ya kaasɛ.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: La yãma sɛba tɩ nẽrba wuu pẽgrɩ ya la, wʋn yẽ toogo. Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ ba yaabdõma mẽ yʋʋn pẽgrɩ nõtɔ'ɔs-sɛba n de põmpɔrndõma la bɛla.».

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «La mam yetɩ yãma sɛba n selsrɩ mam wã tɩ, ya nõŋɛ ya bɛ'ɛba, la sɛba n sisri ya la, ya ɩta ba sõŋa.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: La ya kã'ara kã'a-sõma bɔ'ɔra sɛba n kã be'em bɔ'ɔra ya la, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ɩtɩ ya be'em na.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Nẽra sãn wẽ fʋ pɛka ayɩla, fʋ basɛ tɩ a wẽ ayẽma la pa'asɛ. La nẽra sãn fãagɛ fʋ fu-kãtɛ, fʋ basɛ tɩ a to'oge fʋ fu-pɩka pa'asɛ.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: La nẽra sãn sose fʋ sɛla, fʋ bɔ ẽ. La nẽra sãn dɩkɛ sɛla n de fʋ bõnɔ, fʋ da yetɩ a bɔ fɔ fʋ bõnɔ na.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: La yãma n boorɩ tɩ nẽrba ɩta bõn-sɛba bɔ'ɔra ya la, ya mẽ ɩta bɛla bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: La ya sãn nõŋɛ sɛba n nõŋɛ ya la mã'a, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya sɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Baa la tõon-be'ero dõma nõŋɛ sɛba n nõŋɛ ba mɛ.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: La ya sãn ɩta sɛba n ɩtɩ ya sõŋa la mã'a sõŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya sɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Baa la tõon-be'ero dõma ɩtɩ la bɛla.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: La ya sãn pɛŋra sɛba tɩ ya tẽ'esrɩ tɩ ba wʋn yẽ lebse ya la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya sɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Baa la tõon-be'ero dõma peŋrɩ tõon-be'ero dõma mɛ dee le tɔ'ɔsra bɛla.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: La, la sãn dɛna yãma, nõŋɛ-ya ya bɛ'ɛba, dee ẽŋɛ ba sõŋa. Peŋe-ya nẽrba dee da tẽ'esra tɩ ba le bɔ ya. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya yɔɔrɔ wʋn zo'oge mɛ, la ya wʋn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla wuu kɔma, se'ere n soe la, a ɩtɩ sɛba n ka mi yel-sõnnɛ, la pʋtʋʋl-dõma sõŋa mɛ.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Zɔta-ya nẽrba ninbãalga, wʋ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n zotɩ nẽrba ninbãalga se'em na.».

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: «Da tɔgra-ya nẽra yelle yetɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ kãn tɔgɛ ya yelle yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ be'em. Da zɛrgra-ya nẽrba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ kãn zɛrgɛ ya. Basɛ-ya taaba taalɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn basɛ ya taalɛ.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Bɔ-ya ya taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn bɔ ya. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya zo'oge wʋ ban dɩkɛ wan-kãtɛ makɛ pɩrɛ sõŋa tɩ la saara. Bõn-sɛka tɩ yãma dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ ya taaba la, bõn-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ ya.».

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Tɩ Azezi daan makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Fʋa tã wʋn ve'ege fʋa mɛ tɩ ba wuu kãn lui boko pʋam?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Karẽnbia ka gãnnɩ a karẽnsãama, la karẽnbia sãn zãsʋm sɛla woo ba'asɛ, a ãn wʋ a karẽnsãama la.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Bẽm tɩ fʋ bɩsra sɔkɔ n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum, dee ka bɩsra dɔɔgɔ n boe fʋ mẽŋa nifum na?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne yɛta fʋ sɔbia yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ fʋ yese sɔkɔ a nifum, dee ka bɩsra dɔɔgɔ n boe fʋ mẽŋa nifum? Pʋlibsum dãana, dẽŋɛ yese dɔɔgɔ la fʋ mẽŋa nifum, dee yãŋa tã'agɛ yẽ sõŋa yese sɔkɔ la n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum na.».

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: «Tɩ-sõŋɔ ka wɔnnɩ biɛ be'ero. Tɩ-be'ego mẽ ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Ba bãŋrɩ tɩɩsɩ wuu la ba biɛ pʋam. Gõ'osɩ kãn wɔm kẽnkãma, la kẽkɔ mẽ kãn wɔm tã'ama.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Nẽr-sõŋɔ sũure n ãn sõŋa botɩ a tɔgra la bõn-sõma, la nẽr-be'ego sũure n ka ãn sõŋa botɩ a tɔgra la bõn-be'ero. Se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛba n boe nẽrsaala sũurum n yesrɩ a nõorʋm.».

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: «Bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma wi'ira mam tɩ ‹Zuudãana, Zuudãana›, dee ka ɩta mam n yetɩ ya ɩta se'em na?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n sẽm mam zẽ'am, dee sɛlsra mam yetɔga, dee sakra ɩta mam n yele se'em na, mam wʋn pa'alɛ ya ẽŋa dãana n ãn se'em:.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: A ãn wʋ nẽra n boorɩ tɩ a mẽ a yire, tũ paagɛ tãmpĩa, ẽebɛ a yire tãmpĩa la zuo. Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wẽ yire la, tɩ a ka lui, ẽn mẽ sõŋa la ĩyã.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n wõnnɩ mam yetɔga, dee ka ɩta mam n yele se'em na, ãn wʋ nẽra n mẽ a yire tẽntõnnɔ zuo, dee ka tũ ẽebɛ. Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wa wẽ yire la, tɩ a kɔ'ɔn lui saam saam.».

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Azezi tɔgɛ bɛla wuu bɔ nẽrba la ba'asɛ, dee sẽŋɛ la Kapɛrnaʋm.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Tɩ Rom sʋda-kɛ'ɛma bɔna mĩ tara a tõntõnna tɩ a nõŋɛ. Tɩ tõntõnna la bẽ'era dee la a ki.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la wõm Azezi yelle, tõm Zifdõma kẽma basɛba a zẽ'am tɩ ba sose ẽ yetɩ, a wa'am wa botɩ a tõntõnna la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am ka tĩi belem ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraa la de la nẽr-sõŋɔ.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: A nõŋɛ tõma buuri mɛ, la ẽŋa n mẽ wẽndeego bɔ tõma. Bɛla ĩyã, mõrgɛ sõŋɛ ẽ.».

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Tɩ Azezi dɔla ba wɛ'ɛsa ka lɛm yire la, tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la le tõm a sɩrdõma tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, da nãmsɛ fʋ mẽŋa. Mam ka narɩ tɩ fʋ wa'am mam yire.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam tẽ'esɛ yetɩ mam mẽŋa ka narɩ tɩ m wa'am fõn zẽ'am. Wen tɔgɛ yetɔga mã'a, tɩ m tõntõnna la wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba n soe mam, la mam mẽ soe la sʋdaasɩ basɛba. Mam sãn yele sʋda-sɛka yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ›, a we'esɩ mɛ. La mam sãn yele ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Wa'am›, a sẽm mɛ. Mam sãn yele m tõntõnna yetɩ: ‹Ẽŋɛ wãna›, a ɩtɩ mɛ.».

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Azezi n wõm bɛla la, la daan di'ige ẽ mɛ, tɩ a wẽrgɛ yele nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ yãma mɛ tɩ mam nãn ka yẽ nẽra n bɔ mam sɩra wʋ bʋra-ẽna wã, baa Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la pʋam mam ka yẽ.».

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n tõm sɛba la lebe a yire ka yẽ tɩ tõntõnna la tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi we'esɩ la tẽŋa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Nayin, tɩ a karẽnbiisi, la nẽrba zozo'e dɔla ẽ.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: La Azezi paagɛ tẽŋa la nõorʋm, tʋ'ʋsɛ la nẽrba n zɛɛrɩ budibla n ki wɛ'ɛsa tɩ ba ka laagɛ. Budibla la ma de la pɔkõorɛ. La a bia de la ẽŋa mã'a. Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba zozo'e daan kɔlgɛ ẽ dɔla wɛ'ɛsa.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: La Zuudãana Azezi n yẽ pɔkõorɛ la, tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da kɛlla.».

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: A yele bɛla dee sẽŋɛ paagɛ kalʋm na sãŋandɛ la, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n zɛɛrɩ kũm na ze'ele, tɩ a yele kũm na yetɩ: «Budib-polle, mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ fʋ isge.».

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Tɩ sɛka n ki la vo'oge isge zĩ'ire, yãŋa tɔgra. Tɩ Azezi yele a ma la yetɩ: «Fʋ bia n bɛla.».

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba wuu, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-kãtɛ n wa'am tõma zẽ'am.» Dee le yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ n wa'am tɩ a sõŋɛ a nẽrba.».

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Tɩ yetɔga la saagɛ Zude so'olʋm, la tẽn-sɛba n lɛm na wuu, tɩ ba wõm Azezi n ẽŋɛ se'em.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi tɔgɛ bõn-bãma wuu pa'alɛ ẽ. Tɩ Azã wi a karẽnbiisi bayi.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: tõm ba Zuudãana Azezi zẽ'am, tɩ ba ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na bɩɩ, tɩ gu'ura nẽra ayẽma?».

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azã sɛka n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na n tõm tõma yetɩ, tɩ soke fʋ bɩsɛ, fõn de sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na bɩɩ, tɩ gu'ura nẽra ayẽma?».

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa Azezi daan botɩ bã'adõma zozo'e tɩ bã'asɩ to'oto'ore tarɩ ba, la sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ba n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee botɩ fɔɔsɩ zozo'e nini yẽta.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge sɛba tɩ Azã tõm na yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele Azã yãma n yẽ bõn-sɛba, dee wõm se'em na wuu: Fɔɔsɩ nini yẽtɩ mɛ, tɩ kɔɔrnɔ sẽnna, tɩ kõnkɔma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa, tɩ wansɩ wõnna, tɩ kũm vɔ'ɔra, tɩ nasdõma wõnna Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n ka lebrɩ poorʋm mam ĩyã tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Sɛba tɩ Azã tõm na lebe kule mɛ. Tɩ Azezi yãŋa tɔgɛ nẽrba la Azã yelle yetɩ: «Yãma daan sẽŋɛ mõom tɩ ya ka bɩsɛ la bẽm? Yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la mõogɔ tɩ kʋsebgo mĩini bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: La bẽm tɩ yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ? Yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la nẽra n yɛ fu-sõma bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ. Nẽr-sɛba n yɛɛrɩ fu-sõma dee tarɩ bõn-sõma la, kẽ'erɩ la na'adõma yiɛ pʋam.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: La yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la bẽm? Ya sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa bɩɩ? Ẽe. La mam yetɩ ya tɩ a de la nẽra n gãnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: La de la ẽŋa yelle tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam tõntõnna n wãna tɩ mam tõm tɩ a dẽŋɛ fʋ nẽŋa malgɛ sore bɔ fɔ.›.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽrsaalba tɩ ba dɔgɛ la wuu pʋam, nẽra ka boe n gãnnɩ Azã, la nẽr-sɛka n de pɩka Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam gãnnɩ Azã mɛ.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: La nẽrba la wuu, baa la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba la n wõm Azã yetɔga la, ba basɛ tɩ Azã mise ba ko'om pʋam mɛ. Bɛla pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ ba sakɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ sɩra.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: La Farisi dõma, la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n zagsɛ tɩ Azã ka mise ba ko'om pʋam na, ba zagsɛ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ ba la.».

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ mam wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ la zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba n ɩtɩ se'em na. Ba ɩrgɔ wõn na bẽm?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Ba ãn wʋ kɔma n zĩ da'am wi'ira taaba yetɩ: ‹Tõma peebe wɩɩsɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma zagsɛ ka wa. Tɩ tõma yõom kʋʋrɛ yõoma, tɩ yãma ka kɛlla.›.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na wa'am mɛ wa lʋta nõorɛ, dee ka yũura dãam, tɩ ba yetɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa'am wa dita dee yũura, tɩ ba yetɩ, a de la dita, la dã-yũura, la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba la tõon-be'ero dõma sɩrɛ.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n boorɩ se'em na n mi tɩ a tarɩ yɛm.».

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Farisi nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asimõ n daan wi Azezi tɩ a wa'am ẽŋa yire tɩ ba di. Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ yire la ka zĩ'ire bɔna mĩ tɩ a di.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: La pɔka n boe tẽŋa la pʋam tõnna yalsɩ tõoma. La pɔka la n wõm tɩ Azezi boe Farisi nẽra la yire dita la, tɩ a tarɩ albatrɩ dugla n pɩrɛ la tɩdarɛ kaam yũuŋo n ãn sõŋa wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: Yãŋa ze'ele lɛm Azezi nãma bɔba kɛlla. Tɩ a nintãm yɛsra tɔgra Azezi nãma zuo. Tɩ pɔka la suure duuse Azezi nãma la, la a zomto, dee kɔkɛ a nãma la, sõogɛ ba la tɩdarɛ kaam na.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Tɩ Farisi nẽra la n wi Azezi la yẽ bɛla, tẽ'esɛ yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, a wʋn bãŋɛ nɩ pɔg-ẽna n kalʋm ẽ wã n de nẽr-sɛka, a wʋn bãŋɛ nɩ tɩ a tõnnɩ la yalsɩ tõoma.».

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Asimõ, mam tarɩ la sɛla tɩ m yele fo.» Tɩ Asimõ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, yele!».

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra n daan boe tɩ nẽrba bayi tara a sãnnɛ. Nẽra ayɩla sãnnɛ de la lig-kõba kɔbsnuu. Tɩ ayẽma la mẽ sãnnɛ dɛna lig-kõba pinuu.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Tɩ ba daan ka tãna wʋn yɔ ẽ a ligri la. Tɩ a basɛ ligri la bɔ ba. Bãma bayi la, ãne n wʋn nõŋɛ ligri la dãana gãnna?».

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Tɩ Asimõ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ sɛka sãnnɛ n zo'oge la.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn lerge se'em na de la sɩra.».

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Dee gelge bɩsɛ pɔka la, yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽ pɔka wã? Mam n kẽ fõn yire la, fõn ka bɔ mam ko'om tɩ mam peege m nãma. La pɔka la peege mam nãma mɛ la a nintãm, dee duuse ba la a zomto.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Fõn ka kɔkɛ mam tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ fʋ to'oge mam sõŋa. La mam n kẽ'em na, pɔka la kɔkɛ mam nãma mɛ ka basɛ.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: La fõn ka nã'asɛ mam ẽŋɛ kaam mam zuugo, la a sõogɛ mam nãma mɛ la tɩdarɛ kaam.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yele fʋ yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ a tõon-be'ero n zo'oge la taalɛ mɛ. Bɛla n soe tɩ a nõŋrɛ la zo'oge. La nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ a tõon-be'ero taalɛ fẽfẽe la, a nõŋrɛ mẽ de la fẽe.».

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Azezi yele bɛla, dee yele pɔka la yetɩ: «Fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna fʋ zuo.».

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Tɩ sɛba n daan diti la ẽ na tɔgra la taaba sɔkra yetɩ: «A tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la ãne basɛ nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ?».

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele pɔka la yetɩ: «Fõn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã n fãagɛ fɔ basɛ. Sẽŋɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm.».

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Azezi daan wi a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la lagsɛ mɛ, bɔ ba pãŋa, la nõorɛ tɩ ba dige kʋlkã'arsɩ dee botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Dee tõm ba yetɩ ba mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle, dee botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: La a yele ba yetɩ: «Da dɩkɛ-ya sɛla sɛla sẽŋɛ. Da dɩkɛ-ya dasaarɛ, bɩɩ tãmpɔkɔ, bɩɩ dia, bɩɩ ligri. Da dɩkɛ ya futo bayi bayi.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: La yi-sɛka tɩ ya paagɛ tɩ ba to'oge ya, bɔna-ya yi-ẽŋa pʋam ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn fõrgɛ tẽŋa la.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ka to'oge ya, yese-ya tẽn-ẽŋa pʋam, dee pɩɩsɛ ya nãma tẽntõnnɔ basɛ. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, la wʋn pa'alɛ ba tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa.».

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Azezi n yele bɛla la, karẽnbiisi la yese mɛ sẽŋɛ tẽn-pɩgsɩ wuu pʋam ka mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga, dee botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Galile Naba A'erɔdɩ n daan wõm bõn-sɛba wuu n ɩtɩ la, a palẽŋa n ẽkɛ, se'ere n soe la, nẽrba basɛba n daan yele ẽ tɩ Azezi de la Azã sɛka n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na n vo'oge.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Tɩ nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli n le wa'am dũnia zuo, tɩ nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, kʋrʋm kʋrʋm Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ayẽma n vo'oge.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Tɩ A'erɔdɩ yetɩ: «Mam daan botɩ ba wike Azã zuugo mɛ, dee tɩ ãne n de ẽna wã tɩ mam wõm a yelle wãna wã?» La a daan boorɩ tɩ a yẽ Azezi mɛ.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la tɩ a daan tõm na, lebe mɛ ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ẽ bãma n ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu. Tɩ Azezi botɩ ba doose la ẽ yese kẽnkɛrŋa ka lɛm tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Betisayida.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la bãŋɛ ẽn sẽŋɛ zẽ'am na, babsɛ ẽ. Tɩ a to'oge ba, tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle bɔ ba, dee botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: La wẽnnɛ n daan boe mĩ tɩ a kẽ, tɩ karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la wa'am a zẽ'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Botɩ nẽrba la sẽŋɛ tẽn-pɩgsɩ, la yiɛ n lɛm na ka ɛ dia, la gã'a-zẽ'a, se'ere n soe la, tɩn boe zẽ'e-sɛka wã de la mõom.».

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma mẽŋa bɔ ba dia tɩ ba di.» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Tõma tarɩ la borborɩ banuu, la zũma bayi mã'a, sãn dagna tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ ka da dia wa'am wa bɔ nẽr-bãna wuu.».

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: La bʋraasɩ n daan boe mĩ de wʋ tʋsanuu. Tɩ Azezi yele karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ nẽrba la welse zĩ'ire pinu-nu.».

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la botɩ nẽrba la wuu zĩ'ire bɛla.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ borborɩ banuu, la zũma bayi la gorge bɩsɛ saazuum pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dia la ĩyã, dee fiise borborɩ la zũma la bɔ a karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba to'oge pʋɩ nẽrba la.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Tɩ ba wuu di tɩgɛ, dee tɩ karẽnbiisi la vaagɛ bõn-gito n deege pɩrɛ pɩtɔ pia la ayi.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Daarɛ ayɩla Azezi daan boe la kẽnkɛrŋa pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la bɔna a zẽ'am, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Nẽrba yetɩ mam de la ãne?».

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Nẽrba basɛba yetɩ fõn de la Azã sɛka n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, tɩ nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli, tɩ basɛba yetɩ, fõn de la kʋrʋm kʋrʋm Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ayẽma n vo'oge.».

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Dee yãma yetɩ mam de la ãne?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge yetɩ: «Fõn de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na.».

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da yele nẽra nẽra.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: dee yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la nãmsɛ zozo'e. Tẽŋa wã kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma nifo wʋn kẽ mam mɛ, tɩ ba kʋ mam, la dabsa atã daarɛ mam wʋn vo'oge mɛ.».

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee yele nẽrba la wuu yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a doose mam, dẽnɩ tɩ a basɛ a mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee sakɛ nãmsgɔ daarɛ woo wʋ nẽra n buke a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ, la nẽr-sɛka n sakɛ tɩ a koŋe a vɔm mam ĩyã, a wʋn yẽ vɔm.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Nẽra sãn yẽ dũnia sɛla woo dee koŋe vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a yõorɔ de la bẽm?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n zoe yãnnɛ mam ĩyã, la mam yetɔga la ĩyã, mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn zoe yãnnɛ la ẽ wakat-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn leme wa'am la m na'am pãŋa, la Nawẽnnɛ, la a malɛkadõma na'am pãŋa la.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ yãma nẽr-sɛba n boe kalam wã basɛba kãn ki, sãn dagna tɩ ba wa yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm.».

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Yetɔg-bãma poorʋm, la daan ẽŋɛ wʋ dabsa anii, tɩ Azezi botɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la Azã doose ẽ zom tãŋa zuo tɩ a ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: La ẽn boe mĩ pʋ'ʋsra la, tɩ a nẽŋa teege, tɩ a futo pelge yẽgra.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Tɩ bʋraasɩ bayi darɛ zɛ tɔgra la ẽ. Ba de la Amoyisi, la A'eli.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: n puke ba mẽŋa nẽegɛ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am peelem tɔgra la Azezi, ẽŋa n wʋn ki Zerizalɛm se'em tɩ bõn-sɛka tɩ a wa'am tɩ a ẽŋɛ la, ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ yelle.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: La gẽem n daan tarɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la a taaba la tɩ ba gĩse, yãŋa nẽegɛ yẽ Azezi n yẽgrɩ, tɩ nẽrba bayi la zɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Tɩ nẽrba bayi la daan bɔna mĩ tɩ ba fõrgɛ, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, tõma n boe kalam wã, la ãn sõŋa mɛ. Basɛ tɩ tɩ tĩ põnsɩ batã, ayɩla bɔ fõn, ayɩla bɔ Amoyisi, la ayɩla bɔ A'eli.» A daan wen tɔgɛ mɛ, a ka mi ẽn tɔgrɩ se'em na.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: La ẽn daan boe mĩ tɔgra bɛla la, tɩ sawatɛ wa'am wa lile ba la a mã'asʋm. La sawatɛ la n lile ba la, dabeem n yõkɛ karẽnbiisi la.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Tɩ kõa ze'ele sawatɛ la pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam looge. Selse-ya ẽ.».

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: La kõa la n tɔgɛ la, ba yẽ la Azezi mã'a n ze. Tɩ ba daan sĩna wakat-ẽŋa ka yele nẽra nẽra bãma n yẽ sɛla la.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Tɩ beere daan wiige, tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la ze'ele tãŋa la zuo sige, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ daan tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: La bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam belnɩ fʋ mɛ, bɩsɛ m bia wã. A de la mam bi-yẽŋa, la kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Kʋlkã'arga la sãn ẽn girge ẽ, a botɩ a kaasra mɛ, dee luta kinki'ire, tɩ nõtõorʋm yɛsra a nõorʋm. Kʋlkã'arga la ẽn nãmsɛ ẽ zo'oge mɛ, pɔglʋm ẽ dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam belem fʋ karẽnbiisi yetɩ, ba dige ẽ tɩ a yese, tɩ ba ka yãŋɛ.».

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Zamãan-ẽna nẽrba, ya ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra, la ya ka ɩtɩ sõŋa. Mam wʋn mã'agɛ m mẽŋa la yãma yuuge paagɛ la wakat-kãna?» A yele bɛla dee yele bʋraa la yetɩ: «Tarɩ bia la wa'am.».

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: La bia la n paarɩ Azezi zẽ'am na, tɩ kʋlkã'arga la lʋbɛ ẽ tɩ a gõgra. Tɩ Azezi tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la tɩ a basɛ bia la. Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la basɛ bia la, tɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ ẽ lebse a sɔ.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: La nẽrba la wuu n yẽ Nawẽnnɛ pãn-kãtɛ la, la di'ige ba mɛ. Nẽrba la wuu daan ken ze la yɛrɩ la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ Azezi ẽŋɛ la. Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ:.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: «Selse-ya yetɔg-ẽna tɩ mam yetɩ m tɔgɛ wã sõŋa: Ba wʋn yõkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ la nẽrsaalba nu'usum.».

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: La ba daan ka bãŋɛ a yetɔga la võorɛ. Yetɔga la daan sʋgɛ mɛ tɩ ba ka tã'agɛ bãŋɛ a võorɛ, dee zɔta dabeem ka soke ẽ.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Azezi karẽnbiisi la daan wẽ'erɩ la nõke'ene la taaba, bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ bãma pʋam nẽr-sɛka n wʋn dɛna nẽr-kãtɛ gãnna.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, yõkɛ bia ze'ele lɛm a mẽŋa.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: dee yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge bi-pɩka n ãn wʋ ẽna mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, a to'oge la mam, la sɛka n mẽ to'oge mam, a to'oge la sɛka n tõm mam na. Nẽr-sɛka n de pɩka ya zẽ'am na, ẽŋa n de nẽr-kãtɛ.».

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Tɩ Azã yele Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma yẽ la bʋraa n digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ ba yɛsra, la ẽŋa n ka po dɛna tõma nẽra la ĩyã, tɩ tõma gu ẽ yetɩ a basɛ.».

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Da gu-ya ẽ, nẽr-sɛka n ka sisri yãma, a de la ya nẽra.».

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Dabs-sɛba tɩ Azezi daan wʋn zẽkɛ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire la n daan lɛm, tɩ a kɔ'ɔn yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ ẽŋa sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: A daan tõm na nẽrba yetɩ ba dẽŋɛ nẽŋam. Tɩ nẽrba la daan dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Samaari tẽn-pɩka pʋam tɩ ba mãasna sɛla woo dee gu'ura ẽ.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba zagsɛ tɩ bãma kãn to'oge ẽ, ẽn we'esɩ Zerizalɛm na ĩyã.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: La a karẽnbiisi Azakɩ la Azã n bãŋɛ bɛla la, tɩ ba soke Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn boorɩ tɩ tɩ wi bugum tɩ ba ze'ele saazuum wa di ba mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Tɩ Azezi wẽrgɛ, gi ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka mi sɩ-sɛka n tarɩ ya la.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka wa'am tɩ a sã'am nẽrba vɔm, a wa'am tɩ a fãagɛ ba mɛ.» Tɩ ba wɛ'ɛsa tẽŋa ayẽma.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Ban boe sore wɛ'ɛsa la, tɩ bʋraaga yele Azezi yetɩ: «Mam wʋn dɔla fɔ sẽnna zẽ'e-sɛba wuu tɩ fʋ wʋn sẽnna.».

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Pɩɩna tarɩ yogro mɛ, tɩ niinto mẽ tara tʋgrɔ, la Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ka tarɩ zẽ'a wʋn gã'arɛ vo'ose.».

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Dee le yele bʋraa ayẽma mẽ yetɩ: «Doose mam.» Tɩ bʋraa la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ m sɔ dee wa'am wa dɔla fɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ sɛba n ka tarɩ vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na laagɛ ba kũm. Dee tɩ fõn sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga la.».

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Tɩ bʋraa ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam wʋn doose fo, la basɛ tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka keese m yir-dõma.».

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n gʋrɛ nii-kũure kɔɔra dee bɩsra poorʋm, ka tã wʋn tõm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm tõoma.».

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi looge la karẽnbiisi basɛba pisyopɔɩ la ayi tɩ a tõm ba bayi bayi tɩ ba dẽŋɛ nẽŋa sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛba, la zẽ'e-sɛba wuu tɩ ẽŋa mẽŋa wʋn sẽŋɛ la.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: A yele ba yetɩ: «Va'am na bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ zo'oge mɛ, la tõntõnɩba n wʋn lagsɛ bõnkɔɔla la pɔ'ɔrɩ mɛ. Bɛla ĩyã sose-ya va'am na dãana tɩ a tõm tõntõnɩba pa'asɛ tɩ ba lagsɛ bõnkɔɔla la.».

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Dee yãŋa yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya, yãma ãn wʋ piisi tɩ mam tõm saasaadõma tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Da dɩkɛ-ya pʋtmãanɛ, bɩɩ tãmpɔkɔ, la da dɩkɛ-ya tagra pa'asɛ, da ze'ele-ya sore bɔ nẽra gã'a.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: La ya sãn yetɩ ya kẽ yire pʋam, ya dẽŋɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ yire la dõma yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.›.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Nẽra sãn bɔna mĩ bɔɔra sũ-mã'asʋm, ya sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la ẽ. La nẽra sãn ka bɔna mĩ bɔɔra sũ-mã'asʋm, ya sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn leme ya mẽŋa zẽ'am.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Da lɔsna-ya yiɛ, bɔna-ya yire ayɩla pʋam, yũura dee dita bõn-sɛba tɩ ba bɔ ya la, se'ere n soe la, la masɛ mɛ tɩ tõntõnna to'oge a yɔɔrɔ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Ya sãn paagɛ tẽn-sɛka woo pʋam tɩ ba to'oge ya, di-ya bõn-sɛba tɩ ba bɔ ya la.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: la ya botɩ tẽn-ẽŋa bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee yele nẽrba la yetɩ: ‹Wakatɛ lɛm mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di na'am ya tẽŋasʋka.›.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: La tẽn-sɛka woo pʋam tɩ ya kẽ tɩ ba ka to'oge ya, ya yese sore dee yele tẽŋa la nẽrba wuu yetɩ:.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: ‹Tõma pɩɩsrɩ la ya tẽŋa tẽntõnnɔ n tabɛ tõma nãma la, tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa. La bãŋɛ-ya tɩ wakatɛ lɛm mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.›.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ sarɩyadia daarɛ, Sodom nẽrba kãn ba yẽ toogo wʋ tẽn-ẽŋa nẽrba n wʋn yẽ toogo se'em na.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Korazẽ dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo! Betisayida dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo! Se'ere n soe la, tõon-yãlma sɛba n tõm ya tẽnsɩ pʋam na, daan sãn tõm nɩ na Tiiri, la Sidõ tẽnsɩ pʋam, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, yɛ bɔra futo ẽŋɛ tãmpɛglʋm ẽŋɛ ba zuto tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ ba teege yɛm.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: La sarɩyadia daarɛ, la wʋn dɛna toogo bɔ yãma gãnna Tiiri, la Sidõ dõma.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: La yãma Kapɛrnaʋm dõma, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya wʋn zẽkɛ mɛ ka paagɛ saazuum bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, ya wʋn sige mɛ ka paagɛ bug-tẽŋa.».

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: La Azezi le yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Sɛka n selsrɩ ya yetɔga, a selsrɩ la mam yetɔga, la sɛka n zagsɛ ya, a zagsɛ la mam. La sɛka n zagsɛ mam, a zagsɛ la sɛka n tõm mam na.».

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: La karẽnbiisi pisyopɔɩ la ayi la sẽŋɛ mɛ dee leme wa'am la sũ-yẽlga wa yetɩ: «Zuudãana, baa la kʋlkã'arsɩ sakrɩ tõma nõorɛ mɛ, ban zotɩ fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ la ĩyã.».

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yẽ la Asũtãana n ze'ele saazuum lui tẽŋa wʋ saaga n yãkɛ la.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ mam bɔ ya pãŋa mɛ tɩ ya nẽ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma, la nãnsɩ, dee yãŋɛ Asũtãana la a pãŋa wuu, la sɛla sɛla ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ya be'em.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: La da ɩta-ya sũ-yẽlga kʋlkã'arsɩ n sakrɩ ya nõorɛ la ĩyã, ɩta-ya sũ-yẽlga ya yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire vɔm gõŋɔ pʋam na ĩyã.».

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ Azezi sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm, tɩ a yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n de saazuum la tẽŋa wã Zuudãana, mam pẽgrɩ fɔ, se'ere n soe la, fõn sʋgɛ bõn-bãna wã mɛ tɩ yɛm dõma la bãŋrɩba ka mina, dee pa'alɛ nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔm-pɩgsɩ la. Ẽe, mam Sɔ, la de la fõn boorɩ bɛla la ĩyã.».

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: «Mam Sɔ dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ la mam nu'usum. La nẽra ka mi mam n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la n de sɛka, sãn dagna mam Sɔ la, la nẽra mẽ ka mi mam Sɔ la n de se'em, sãn dagna mam n de a Dayʋa la, la sɛba tɩ mam wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽ na.».

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee wẽrgɛ la a karẽnbiisi la bɔba, yele bãma mã'a yetɩ: «Yãma sɛba n yẽtɩ sɛla tɩ ya yẽtɩ wã, tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la na'adõma zozo'e yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ yãma n yẽ sɛla la mɛ, la ba ka yẽ, ba yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ ba wõm sɛla tɩ yãma wõm na mɛ, la ba ka wõm.».

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama isge tɩ a di Azezi yɛm bɩsɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam yetɩ bẽm? Fõn karẽŋrɩ ẽ na, a yetɩ bẽm?».

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «La yetɩ: Nõŋɛ fʋ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ la fʋ sũure wuu, la fʋ sɩa wuu, la fʋ pãŋa wuu, la fʋ yɛm wuu, dee nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fõn nõŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa se'em na.».

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ yele sõŋa. Ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ vɔm na.».

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: La ẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ a bɔ a mẽŋa bʋʋrɔ la, tɩ a soke Azezi yetɩ: «Ãne n de mam tadãana?».

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra n daan yese Zerizalɛm wɛ Zeriko, tɩ fãarba yõkɛ ẽ fã a lɔgrɔ wuu, dee wẽ ẽ tɩ a dee la a ki, tɩ ba basɛ ẽ dee fõrgɛ.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽema ayɩla dɔla so-ẽŋa tɔlla, yẽ ẽ, lɔkɛ tole dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Tɩ Leviti nẽra mẽ wa'am tɔlla, yẽ ẽ, bʋrgɛ tole dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: La Samaari nẽra n mẽ daan dolɩ so-ẽŋa tɔlla yẽ ẽ, tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Tɩ a paagɛ a zẽ'am dɩkɛ dãam peege a nõtɔ la, ẽŋɛ kaam ĩmĩ vile. Yãŋa yõkɛ ẽ bã a bõŋa zuo, tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ sãama yire ka bɩsɛ a yelle.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Tɩ beere wiige tɩ a dɩkɛ lig-kõba bayi bɔ nẽr-sɛka n bɩsrɩ sãama yire yelle la, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Bɩsra a yelle, ligri la sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ, mam sãn leme, mam wʋn yɔ fɔ.› ».

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Azezi tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm dee soke ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽrba batã wã, fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ãne n de nẽra la tɩ fãarba yõkɛ la tadãana?».

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama la lerge yetɩ: «Sɛka n zoe a ninbãalga sõŋɛ ẽ na.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ mẽ ka ẽŋɛ bɛla.».

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi daan sẽnnɩ mɛ ka kẽ tẽŋa ayɩla pʋam. Tɩ pɔka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Amartɩ bɔna tẽn-ẽŋa pʋam to'oge ẽ a yire.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Amartɩ tarɩ la yɩbga tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Amaari. Tɩ Amaari zẽa Zuudãana Azezi nãma bɔba sɛlsra a yetɔga.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: La Amartɩ tõoma yelle n daan mõ'ogɛ a nini, tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka yele Azezi yetɩ: «Mam Zuudãana, mam yɩbga la n basɛ tɩ mam m yɩla mã'a tõnna la, fõn yetɩ bẽm? Yele ẽ tɩ a wa'am wa sõŋɛ ma.».

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Amartɩ, Amartɩ, fõn botɩ fʋ yɛm n yɔɔra la yɛla zozo'e yelle.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: La bõnɔ ayɩla mã'a n de yel-kãŋkãŋɛ. Amaari looge la bõn-sõŋɔ, la nẽra nẽra kãn tã'agɛ to'oge bõn-ẽŋa a zẽ'am.».

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Azezi daan boe la zẽ'a ayɩla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ. La ẽn pʋ'ʋsɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ a karẽnbia ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, pa'alɛ tɔ tõma n wʋn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ se'em, wʋ Azã n pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi se'em na.».

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn pʋ'ʋsra, ya yetɩ: ‹Tõma Sɔ, botɩ fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ yẽta pẽka, wa'am wa di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: bɔ'ɔra tɔ daarɛ woo la a dia.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ, tɩ tõma mẽ basrɩ sɛba n ẽŋɛ tõma be'em taalɛ mɛ. Da basɛ tɩ yelle paagɛ tɔ tɩ tɩ lui.› ».

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Dee le yele ba yetɩ: «Tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ, yãma nẽr-sɛka sãn tara sɩrɛ, tɩ a wa'am a zẽ'am yʋ'ʋŋɔ wa sose ẽ yetɩ: ‹M sɩrɛ, bɔ ma borborɩ batã.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: mam sɩrɛ n ze'ele sore wa'am, tɩ mam ka tara sɛla wʋn bɔ ẽ tɩ a di.›.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Tɩ a sɩrɛ la bɔna deem dee lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Da dãam mam, mam yʋ m zanõrɛ mɛ, tɩ mam la m kɔma gã, mam kãn yãŋɛ isge bɔ fɔ fõn boorɩ sɛla la.›.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, baa a sãn põn kãn isge bɔ ẽ sɛla ban de sɩtɔ la ĩyã, a wʋn isge bɔ ẽ ẽn boorɩ sɛla la mɛ, ẽn bʋlɛ ẽ na ĩyã.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Bɛla mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya sose Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya, ya ɛ, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ, ya wẽ kʋlɩŋa, tɩ a wʋn yo'oge bɔ ya.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, a wʋn bɔ ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n eerɩ, wʋn yẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n wẽ'erɩ kʋlɩŋa, ba wʋn yo'oge bɔ ẽ.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Ãne n boe yãma pʋam tɩ a bia wʋn sose ẽ zũfo, tɩ a dɩkɛ bõnsɛla bɔ ẽ?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Bɩɩ a sãn sose ẽ zelle, tɩ a dɩkɛ nãŋa bɔ ẽ?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Yãma n de tõon-be'ero dõma la sãn mina tɩ ya bɔ ya kɔma bõn-sõma, Nawẽnnɛ n de ya Sɔ n boe saazuum n ãn sõŋa la wʋn bɔ nẽr-sɛba n sosrɩ ẽ a Sɩa la gãnna.».

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: La Azezi daan dige la kʋlkã'arga n tarɩ bʋraa tɩ a dɛna muko, tɩ kʋlkã'arga la daan yese, dee tɩ bʋraa la n de muko la yãŋa tã'agɛ tɔgra, tɩ la di'ige nẽrba la.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la basɛba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽn digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la, la de la Abelzebuli n de kʋlkã'arsɩ naba la pãŋa tɩ a tarɩ digra ba.».

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Tɩ nẽrba basɛba bɔɔra tɩ ba makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ, a tõm tõon-yãlma n pa'alɛ tɩ a pãŋa la ze'etɩ la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, yele ba yetɩ: «So'olʋm sɛka woo nẽrba n welge wẽrgɛ zɛbra la taaba, so'olʋm na wʋn sã'am mɛ. La so'olʋm ẽŋa yiɛ mẽ wʋn lui mɛ.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: La Asũtãana sãn welge zɛbra la a mẽŋa, a so'olʋm na kãn tã'agɛ ze'ele. Yãma n yetɩ mam digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la Abelzebuli pãŋa la ĩyã tɩ mam yele bɛla.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Yãma n yetɩ mam digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la Abelzebuli pãŋa la, yãma nẽrba tarɩ la ãn pãŋa digri kʋlkã'arsɩ? Bɛla, ya nẽrba la mẽŋa n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa tɩ mam tarɩ digra kʋlkã'arsɩ, bɛla pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wa'am mɛ wa dita na'am ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Pãŋa dãana sãn mãasʋm sõŋa la a bʋdaan-lɔgrɔ gu'ura a yire, sɛla kãn ẽŋɛ a bõntarsõm.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: La nẽra n tarɩ pãŋa gãnna ẽ sãn wa'am wa zɛbɛ yãŋɛ ẽ, a wʋn fãagɛ la a zɛbrɛ lɔg-sɛba tɩ a te'ege la yiɛn, dee yãŋa vaagɛ a yire lɔgrɔ la pʋɩ.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ka boe la mam, sisri mam mɛ, la nẽr-sɛka n ka lagɩm na mam lagsra, a wɩrgrɩ mɛ.».

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga sãn yese dee basɛ nẽra, a sẽŋɛ la pɔgẽntɩɩsɩ pʋam ka ɛɛra zẽ'a tɩ a vo'ose, la a sãn ɛ zẽ'a koŋe, a yetɩ mɛ tɩ: ‹Mam wʋn lebe la m yi-sɛka tɩ m yese dee basɛ la pʋam.›.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: La a sãn lebe ka yẽ tɩ a pɩɩsɛ mɛ malgɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: a wʋn lebe mɛ ka tarɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ bayopɔɩ n de wẽensɩ gãnna ẽŋa mẽŋa wa'am, tɩ ba kẽ lagɩm bɔna mĩ. Bɛla, nẽr-ẽŋa toogo yãŋa wʋn gãnna a kʋrʋm toogo la mɛ.».

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ pɔka ze'ele nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Pɔg-sɛka n dɔgɛ fɔ, tɩ fʋ mõgɛ a bĩ'isum na, tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la tẽn-kãra, la tẽn-pɩgsɩ pʋam ka mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la dɔla ẽ.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n wõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, dee sakra ɩta lan yele se'em na, n tõn tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: La pɔgsɩ basɛba n mẽ daan dolɩ ẽ. La kʋlkã'arsɩ, la bã'asɩ n daan nãmsrɩ pɔg-bãma, tɩ Azezi botɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Pɔgsɩ la ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Amaari n yese Magdala, ẽŋa tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ bayopɔɩ daan yese dee basɛ ẽ na.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n lagsɛ Azezi zẽ'am zo'oge, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba de la nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ. Ba eerɩ la bõn-yãlma n wʋn pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, la ba kãn yẽ bõn-yãlma ayẽma, sãn dagna bõn-yãlma sɛka n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azonaasɩ la.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: La ayẽma yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azanɩ. Ẽŋa de la Asuza n bɩsrɩ naba A'erɔdɩ yire yelle la pɔga. La ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Asuzanɩ. Pɔg-bãna, la pɔgsɩ zozo'e n tarɩ ba mẽŋa lɔgrɔ sõŋra Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Bõn-sɛka n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Azonaasɩ la, pa'alɛ Niniivi dõma la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, bɛla Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ de la sɛka n pa'alɩ zamãan-ẽna dõma Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: La nẽrba n ze'ele tẽnsɩ to'oto'ore sẽnna Azezi zẽ'am. Nẽrba la n daan lagsɛ zo'oge pãɩ la, tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ:.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, na-pɔka la n yʋʋn yese wẽntulle gɔbga bɔba la wʋn isge mɛ zɛrgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn ze'ele la zẽ'a n zãagɛ wa'am wa selse naba Asalomõ yɛm yetɔga la, la sɛka n gãnnɩ Asalomõ n boe kalam, dee tɩ yãma ka sɛlsra ẽ.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: «Kaara n daan yese tɩ a lobge a bõnbuuri. Ẽŋa n boe mĩ lɔbgra la, tɩ bõnbuuri la basɛba lui sore zuo tɩ nẽrba nẽ ba, dee tɩ niinto mẽ wa'am wa di ba.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, Niniivi dõma mẽ wʋn isge zɛrgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma mɛ ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, Azonaasɩ n yʋʋn mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ ba la, ba yʋʋn teege yɛm mɛ basɛ tõon-be'ero. La sɛka n gãnnɩ Azonaasɩ n boe kalam, dee tɩ yãma ka teege yɛm.».

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Tɩ basɛba lui tãmpĩa zuo bule, la mã'asrɛ n ka boe mĩ na ĩyã tɩ ba zẽkɛ ki.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: «Nẽra kãn yõ fɩtla sʋgɛ, bɩɩ dɩkɛ pɩ'ɩgɔ vuge ẽ, a wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ zoole la sɛla zuo tɩ a nẽegɛ bɔ sɛba n kẽ'erɩ la.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Tɩ bõnbuuri basɛba lui gõ'osɩ zẽ'a, tɩ bãma la gõ'osɩ la wuu lagɩm yese, tɩ gõ'osɩ la fʋrɛ ba.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Fʋ nini n nẽerɩ peelem bɔ'ɔra fʋ ĩyã wʋ fɩtla la, bɛla fʋ nini sãn ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu tarɩ peelem mɛ. La fʋ nini sãn ka ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu boe la lika pʋam.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Tɩ basɛba lui zẽ'e-sõŋɔ zẽ'a yese, wɔm biɛ zo'oge paagɛ kɔbga kɔbga.» Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la, dee yetɩ: «Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, guuse tɩ bõn-sɛka n de peelem fʋ pʋam na, da dɛna lika.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la soke ẽ makrɛ la võorɛ.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: La fʋ ĩyã wuu sãn tara peelem sõŋa, tɩ baa bɔbrɛ ayɩla ka tara lika, fʋ ĩyã wuu tarɩ peelem mɛ wʋ fɩtla n tarɩ peelem nẽera bɔ'ɔra nẽrba se'em na.».

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ botɩ yãma bãŋɛ a so'olʋm yelle n sʋgɛ la mɛ, la sɛba n ka boe tõma pʋam na, mam makrɩ la magsa bɔ'ɔra ba. ‹Bɛla tɩ ba wʋn bɩsra, dee ka yẽta, ba wʋn wõnna, dee ka bãŋra ba võorɛ.›.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
