local: Azezi n daan tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ Farisi nẽra ayɩla wi ẽ tɩ a wa'am ẽŋa yire tɩ ba di. Tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka kẽ zĩ'ire bɔna mĩ tɩ a di.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Makrɛ la võorɛ n wãna: Bõnbuuri la de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Tɩ Farisi nẽra la yẽ tɩ Azezi ka peege nu'usi wʋ ban ɩtɩ ba sisgo se'em na, dee yetɩ a di, tɩ la di'ige ẽ.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Sore la zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la, tɩ Asũtãana wa'am wa yese yetɔga la ba sũure pʋam, botɩ ba kãn sakɛ yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yãma Farisi dõma peerɩ ya wama la ya laasɩ pɔa mɛ, la yãma mẽŋa pʋam pɩrɛ la dẽgrɔ n de fãaga la pʋtʋʋlgɔ.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: La tãmpĩa la zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la, dee sakɛ la sũ-yẽlga, la yetɔga la ka kẽ ba sũure pʋam sõŋa. Ba sakɛ la wakatɛ fẽe, tɩ yelle sãn paagɛ ba, tɩ ba basɛ.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Ya de la geto! La dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ poorʋm na, n ken ẽŋɛ pʋam na?.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: La gõ'osɩ la zẽ'a tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, tɩ ba yɛm yɔɔra la ba vɔm yelle, tɩ bõntarsõm la dũnia yẽlʋm yɛmleego botɩ ba ka tãna wʋn bɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la pʋam.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Dɩkɛ-ya yan tarɩ sɛla la bɔ nasdõma la ya sũure wuu, tɩ sɛla woo wʋn ãna sõŋa bɔ ya.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: La zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la, dee sakra dɩkra yetɔga la ɩta ba sũure pʋam sõŋa sõŋa, mõrgɛ zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore la pʋam wʋ bõnbuuri la n wɔm biɛ se'em na.».

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Farisi dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma bɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu pia pʋrɛ mɛ, baa ba sãn ãna pɩka pɩka, la ya ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, la ya ka nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la bõn-bãna tɩ ya wʋn dẽŋɛ ɩta, dee yãŋa ɩta sɛla tɩ ya ɩtɩ la pa'asɛ.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Nẽra ka yõ'orɩ fɩtla dɩkɛ lɔkɔ vuge ẽ, bɩɩ dɩkɛ ẽ sʋgɛ gɔrgɔ tilum. A wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ zoole la sɛla zuo, tɩ a nẽegɛ peelem tɩ sɛba n kẽ'erɩ la yẽta.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Farisi dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma eerɩ la zẽ'esɩ nẽŋa tɩ ya zĩ'ire wẽndeto pʋam, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba nã'asra ya da'asɩ pʋam.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Bõn-sɛka woo n lile la wʋn lilge mɛ, la bõn-sɛka woo n sʋgɛ wʋn wa puke mɛ tɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, ya ãn wʋ yɔɔrɔ n naagɛ la tẽŋa tɩ nẽrba nẽera ba zuto tɔlla, dee ka mina tɩ yɔɔrɔ n boe mĩ.».

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Mõrgɛ-ya bãŋɛ yãma n selsrɩ se'em na võorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ sɛla, ba wʋn bɔ ẽ pa'asɛ, la nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ sɛla, bõn-sɛba fẽe tɩ a tẽ'esɛ tɩ a tara la, ba wʋn to'oge ba.».

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fʋ sãn tɔgra bɛla fʋ po pɔ'ɔra la tõma wuu.».

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Azezi ma, la a yɩbsɩ n daan wa'am tɩ ba yẽ ẽ. La nẽrba zo'oge mɛ tɩ ba ka tã'agɛ paagɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma mẽ wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma n botɩ nẽrba la ɩta se'em na, ãn wʋ ze-tɩbsa tɩ ya zelgrɩ nẽrba la, la yãma mẽŋa ka boorɩ tɩ ya kalʋm ba la ya nu'ubiire.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Tɩ ba yele Azezi yetɩ: «Fʋ ma, la fʋ yɩbsɩ n boe yẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ ba yẽ fɔ.».

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma mẽtɩ la yɔɔrɔ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la. La yãma yaabdõma n yʋʋn kʋ ba.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba wuu n wõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, dee sakɛ ɩta lan yele se'em na, n de mam ma, la mam yɩbsɩ.».

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: La yãma n mẽtɩ sɛba tɩ ya yaabdõma kʋ la yɔɔrɔ la, la pa'alɛ tɩ ya sakɛ tɩ ban yʋʋn tõm se'em na ãn sõŋa mɛ.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi kẽ õorŋɔ, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ dooge ya mõgrɛ la ke bɔba doone.» Tɩ ba mõnna õorŋɔ la wɛ'ɛsa.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ yɛm na yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn tõm nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la Tõntõnɩba bɔ ba, la ba wʋn kʋ basɛba, dee nãmsɛ basɛba.›.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: La ban mõnnɩ wɛ'ɛsa la, tɩ gẽem yõkɛ Azezi, tɩ a gĩse. Tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ isge ko'om na zuo vugra, tɩ ko'om kẽ'era õorŋɔ la pʋam, tɩ la lebge pakrɛ bɔ ba.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Bɛla Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sɩbgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma wʋ la de la bãma n kʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo, wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Tɩ ba paagɛ nẽegɛ Azezi, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, karẽnsãama, tõma yetɩ tɩ ki mɛ.» Tɩ a isge tãsɛ kʋsebgo, la ko-mĩ'isi tɩ ba ãna sĩm. Tɩ kʋsebgo la ko-mĩ'isi la yõkɛ, tɩ la ãna sĩm.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: lan pɔsɛ la Abɛlɩ kʋa, halɩ wa paagɛ Azakari tɩ ba kʋ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne, la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ tẽŋasʋka la. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ tõon-be'ero bãma wuu taalɛ boe la zamãan-ẽna nẽrba zuo.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Tɩ a soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya ka bɔ mam sɩra?» Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba. Tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ãne n de ẽna tã'agɛ tãsra kʋsebgo la ko'om, tɩ ba sakra a nõorɛ?».

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma n gu'uri nẽrba tɩ ba ka bãŋra Nawẽnnɛ sɩra. Yãma mẽŋa ka sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la, dee mẽ gu'ura nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba sakɛ la.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: La ba paagɛ la Geraza dõma tẽŋa. Mõgrɛ la boe la tẽn-ẽŋa la Galile tẽŋasʋka.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: La Azezi n ze'ele bilam fõrgɛ la, tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la Farisi dõma sũure ba isge la ẽ. Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba bẽrɛ ẽ, sɔkra ẽ sokrɩsɩ zozo'e.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele õorŋɔ pʋam sige. Tɩ tẽŋa la bʋraa ayɩla tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ẽ sẽnna tʋ'ʋsra ẽ. A ka yɛɛrɩ fuugo, halɩ tɩ la yuuge, la a ka kẽ'erɩ yire, a boe la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ yɔɔrɔ boe mĩ na.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: bɔɔra tɩ a tɔgɛ tuuge tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Nõor-faa tɩ kʋlkã'arga la ẽn girge ẽ. Tɩ ba gu'ura ẽ, dee ba a nãma, la a nu'usi wuu la bãnsɩ. Tɩ a kɔ'ɔra bãnsɩ la. La kʋlkã'arga la ẽn tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ la mõom. La bʋraaga la n yẽ Azezi la, a kaasɛ mɛ ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam, tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Azezi, Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu Dayʋa, bẽm boe mam la fõn tẽŋasʋka? M sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ fʋ da nãmsɛ mam.» Azezi dẽŋɛ yele kʋlkã'arga la mɛ tɩ a yese dee basɛ bʋraa la, bɛla n soe tɩ kʋlkã'arga la yele bɛla.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Mam yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Atʋsa.» Se'ere n soe tɩ a yele bɛla la, kʋlkã'arsɩ la n dolɩ ẽ na zo'oge mɛ.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la sose Azezi yetɩ, a da botɩ ba sẽŋɛ bug-tẽŋa bog-ko'ogo la pʋam.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa nẽrba basɛba n wa'am wa tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Azezi, gɔmna Apilatɩ n daan botɩ ba kʋ Galile nẽrba basɛba wakat-sɛka tɩ ba boe mĩ kãabra bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ ba zɩɩm yese gaam na kãabgɔ la zɩɩm.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: La kurkurdõma n daan zo'oge bɔna tãŋa zuo ɔbra yɔnnɔ. Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la belem Azezi yetɩ, a bɔ ba nõorɛ tɩ ba kẽ kurkurdõma la. Tɩ Azezi sakɛ ban yele se'em na.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Galile nẽr-bãma n daan yẽ nãmsgɔ ẽŋa la, bãma n de tõon-be'ero dõma gãnna Galile dõma basɛba la bɩɩ?.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la basɛ bʋraa la, dee sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la. Tɩ ba wuu zoe sige ka lui mõgrɛ pʋam ki.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ayɛɩ, yãma sãn ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, yãma wuu mẽ wʋn sã'am na bɛla.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: La sɛba n gu'uri kurkurdõma la n yẽ bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ la, ba zoe sẽŋɛ la tẽŋa pʋam, la kẽnkɛrsɩ wuu ka yele sɛla n ẽŋɛ la.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: La nẽrba pia la anii tɩ de-tʋgma yʋʋn lui kʋ ba Silo tẽŋa la, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma n de tõon-be'ero dõma gãnna Zerizalɛm nẽrba basɛba la bɩɩ?.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Tɩ nẽrba sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka bɩsɛ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na. La ban paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am na, ba yẽ bʋraa la tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la yese dee basɛ ẽ na, tɩ a yɛ la futo zẽa Azezi nẽŋam, dee tarɩ yɛm sõŋa sõŋa. Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ayɛɩ, yãma sãn ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, yãma wuu mẽ wʋn sã'am na bɛla.».

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Tɩ sɛba n yẽ bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ la, yele ba lan ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la yese dee basɛ bʋraa la.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Azezi le makɛ la makrɛ ẽna wã yetɩ: «Bʋraaga n tarɩ kẽnkãŋa n sele a va'am pʋam, yãŋa wa'am tɩ a bɩsɛ a sãn tara biɛ, ka yẽ sɛla sɛla.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Tɩ Geraza tẽŋa nẽrba la wuu belem Azezi yetɩ, a fõrgɛ dee basɛ ba tẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, dabeem n ba kẽ ba. Tɩ Azezi kẽ'era õorŋɔ la tɩ a fõrgɛ.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Tɩ a yele va'am na gu'ura yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ, yʋʋma atã n wãna tɩ mam sẽnnɩ dee ka yẽta biɛ kẽnkãŋa wã zuo. Sɛ ẽ basɛ, tɩ a yẽrɛ la zẽ'a.›.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la yese dee basɛ ẽ na, sose Azezi tɩ a basɛ tɩ a doose ẽ. Tɩ Azezi zagsɛ, dee yele ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Tɩ gu'ura la lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, basɛ ẽ yʋʋn-ẽna mã'a, tɩ mam wʋn kɔ gilge a tille, ẽŋɛ bugulgo basɛ ẽ.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: «Lebe kule ya yire, ka pa'alɛ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɔ fʋ la.» Tɩ a doose tẽŋa la wuu pʋam, mõola pa'ala Azezi n ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛba wuu bɔ ẽ na.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: la yʋʋnɛ sãn kaagɛ tɩ a wɔm, la ãn sõŋa, la a sãn ken ka wɔm, fʋ yãŋa sɛ ẽ basɛ.› ».

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Azezi daan lebe dooge la mõgrɛ la ke bɔba. La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n põn lagsɛ bilam zɛ gu'ura ẽ. Azezi n daan paagɛ mĩ na, ba to'oge ẽ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Azezi daan boe la Zifdõma wẽndeem vo'osgo daarɛ zãsna nẽrba.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: La bʋraa ayɩla n boe mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azayirusi, a de la Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana, wa'am wa ka dũma tẽŋa Azezi nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am mam yire.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: tɩ pɔka ayɩla bɔna mĩ tɩ kʋlkã'arga tara ẽ botɩ a dɛna kobgo paagɛ yʋʋma pia la anii, yũre ka tãna wʋn dɔ'ɔgɛ.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: tɩ mam pɔyʋa n tarɩ yʋʋma pia la ayi n dee la a ki.» Bʋraa la bia de la ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a. Tɩ Azezi dɔla ẽ wɛ'ɛsa, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mikra ẽ.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ pɔka la, wi ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, fʋ yẽ yolsgo, fʋ bã'aga la basɛ fɔ.».

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: La pɔka n daan boe mĩ luta sɩkẽŋa yʋʋma pia la ayi tɩ la ka gɔ'ɔra. Tɩ a daan dɩkɛ ẽn tarɩ sɛla la wuu sã'am tɩbdõma zẽ'am, la baa ayɩla ka yãŋɛ botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Dee dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ pɔka la zuo, tɩ bã'aga la basɛ pɔka la bilam mẽŋa, tɩ a tã'agɛ dɔ'ɔra, yãŋa pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Tɩ a paagɛ Azezi poorʋm, kalʋm Azezi fuugo nõorɛ. La bilam mẽŋa tɩ a zɩɩm na ze'ele ka le yɛsra.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Tɩ wẽndeego la naba sũure isge la Azezi, ẽŋa n botɩ pɔka la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ la ĩyã. Tɩ a yele nẽrba la yetɩ: «Dabsa ayoobɩ n boe tɩ ya tãna wʋn tõm. Wa'am-ya dabs-bãma pʋam wa tɩbɛ ya mẽŋa, dee da sẽnna vo'osgo daarɛ tɩbra ya mẽŋa.».

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke yetɩ: «Ãne n kalʋm mam?» Tɩ nẽra woo sɩ'ɩsɛ. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, nẽrba wã wuu n lagɩm gilge fõn mikra se'em wã tɩ fõn sɔkra?».

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Pʋlibsum dõma wã! Yãma nẽra woo ka lorgrɩ a lɔlga, bɩɩ a bõŋa sẽnna yũula ko'om vo'osgo daarɛ mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Nẽra n kalʋm mam, se'ere n soe la, mam bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ pãŋa n yese mam zẽ'am.».

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Tɩ pɔg-ẽna wã n de Abraham yɩɩŋa tɩ Asũtãana lʋ ẽ yʋʋma pia la anii wã, la ka narɩ tɩ mam lorge ẽ vo'osgo daarɛ?».

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: La pɔka la n bãŋɛ tɩ a kãn yãŋɛ sʋgɛ la Azezi la, tɩ a gõgra wa'am wa ka dũma tẽŋa Azezi nẽŋam, yele ẽ nẽrba la wuu nẽŋam se'ere n soe tɩ ẽŋa kalʋm ẽ, la ẽŋa n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm bilam mẽŋa se'em.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: La ẽŋa n yele bɛla la, tɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ a bɛ'ɛba la wuu, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm la a tõon-yãlma sɛba wuu tɩ a ẽŋɛ la.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele pɔka la yetɩ: «M bia, fõn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã n botɩ fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Sẽŋɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm.».

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm wõn na bẽm? La mam wʋn dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la ẽ?.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: La Azezi n nãn boe mĩ tɔgra la, tɩ nẽra ze'ele Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana la yire wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ pɔyʋa la ki mɛ, da le nãmsɛ karẽnsãama la.».

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: A ãn wʋ sɔ'ɔrɔ biire tɩ nẽra bʋrɛ a va'am pʋam, tɩ a bɩ lebge tɩ-kãtɛ. Tɩ niinto sẽnna tɛ'ɛra ba tʋgrɔ a wila zuo.».

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: La Azezi n wõm bɛla la, tɩ a yele Azayirusi yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, wen bɔ mam sɩra, tɩ fʋ pɔyʋa la wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: La a le yetɩ: «Mam wʋn le dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm?.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ a yire, la a ka botɩ nẽra nẽra doose ẽ kẽ, sãn dagna Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azakɩ, la Azã, la bia la sɔ, la a ma mã'a.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: A ãn wʋ dãbɩllɛ tɩ pɔka dɩkɛ gaam na borborɩ zom la-kãra batã wẽ, tɩ dãbɩllɛ la botɩ ba wuu uke.».

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: La nẽrba la wuu daan kellɩ mɛ fabla bia la ĩyã. Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Da kaasra-ya, bia la ka ki, a gĩsri mɛ.».

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Azezi daan we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm dɔla tẽn-kãra la tẽn-pɩgsɩ pʋam zãsna nẽrba.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Tɩ ba la'ara Azezi, ban mi tɩ bia la ki mɛ na ĩyã.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Tɩ nẽra soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, nẽrba fẽe mã'a n wʋn yẽ fãarɛ bɩɩ?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ:.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Tɩ Azezi gʋrɛ bia la nu'ugo, tĩi tɔgɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Bia, isge.».

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: «Nargɛ-ya pãŋa doose zanõrɛ n ãn mika kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam, tɩ mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn wa ɛɛra mɛ tɩ ba kẽ, la ba kãn yãŋɛ.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Tɩ bia la vɔm leme, tɩ a isge bilam mẽŋa. Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bɔ-ya bia la dia tɩ a di.».

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: La wakatɛ sãn paagɛ, yidãana wʋn isge yʋ yaŋa la mɛ, la ya sãn weege yẽŋa, ya wʋn wẽ'era mɛ kɔkɔ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, yo'oge tɩ tɩ kẽ.› La a wʋn lerge ya yetɩ: ‹Mam ka mi yãma n ze'ele zẽ'a.›.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Tɩ la daan di'ige bia la sɔ, la a ma. Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da yele nẽra nẽra sɛla n ẽŋɛ la.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: La ya wʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Dagɩ tõma la fõn n daan lagɩm dita, dee yũura, tɩ fʋ sẽnna tõma tẽŋam zãsna nẽrba?›.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: La a wʋn le lerge ya yetɩ: ‹Mam ka mi yãma n ze'ele zẽ'a, fõrgɛ ya mam zẽ'am, yãma de la tõon-be'ero dõma.›.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: La ya wʋn ka yẽ Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba wuu, tɩ ba boe Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam, la yãma wʋn weege la yẽŋa kɛlla dee dɩkɛ ya nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: La nẽrba wʋn yese dapɔ-bɔba, la zanõ-bɔba, la zʋʋgɔ bɔba, la gɔbga bɔba wa'am wa zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam di.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽrba basɛba n de poorʋm dõma wʋn lebge nẽŋam dõma, tɩ basɛba n mẽ de nẽŋam dõma wʋn wa lebge poorʋm dõma.».

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba sẽŋɛ ka yele Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ kalam dee sẽŋɛ zẽ'a ayẽma, tɩ Naba A'erɔdɩ eerɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ a kʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele yɛm be'ero dãana la yetɩ: ‹Zĩna la beesã, mam wʋn dige kʋlkã'arsɩ mɛ tɩ ba yese, dee botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, la dabsa atã daarɛ tɩ mam wʋn ba'asɛ m tõoma la wuu.›.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: La dẽnɩ tɩ mam sẽŋɛ m sẽnnɛ na zĩna, la beesã, la dabsa atã daarɛ, se'ere n soe la, la ka masɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ki zẽ'a ayẽma, sãn dagna Zerizalɛm pʋam.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Zerizalɛm nẽrba, Zerizalɛm nẽrba, yãma kʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, dee lɔbra sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm ba ya zẽ'am la kuga kʋʋra. Nõorɛ faa tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m lagsɛ ya wʋ nõ-yã'aŋa n lagsrɩ a kɔma ũna a kempagsɩ pʋam se'em na, tɩ ya ka bɔɔra.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ ya tẽŋa bɔ ya, tɩ a lebge daboto. Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya kãn le yẽ mam, halɩ tɩ ya wʋn wa yetɩ: ‹Pẽgɛ-ya sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la.› ».

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Vo'osgo daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Azezi daan sẽŋɛ Farisi dõma naba ayɩla yire tɩ ba di. Tɩ nẽrba la n boe mĩ na bɩsra ẽn wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: La bʋraaga ayɩla n boe mĩ tɩ ĩ-fʋʋsŋɔ bã'aga tara ẽ, tɩ a zɛ Azezi nẽŋam.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma la yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ nẽra botɩ nẽrba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, sore ka boe?».

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Tɩ ba sĩna. Tɩ Azezi botɩ bʋraaga la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, basɛ ẽ tɩ a kule.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Dee soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne n boe yãma pʋam tɩ a dayʋa, bɩɩ a lɔlga wʋn lui lʋa pʋam vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ a kãn zoe tɔtɔ sẽŋɛ ka yaagɛ ẽ?».

eng: But they had no response.
local: Tɩ ba makɛ tɩ ba lerge ẽ koŋe.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba wi tɩ ba wa'am wa di la n loosrɩ zẽ'e-sõma zĩ'ita, tɩ a makɛ makrɛ yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: «Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ pɔgdire kibsa wi fo, da kãn sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire zẽ'e-sõŋɔ, tã tɩ a mẽ wi la nẽra n gãnnɩ fõn.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Tɩ sɛka n wi ya wuu la, da wa'am wa yele fʋ yetɩ: ‹Isge, dee tɩ ẽna zĩ'ire.› Tɩ fʋ yãŋa isge sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire poorʋm poorʋm la yãnnɛ.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Nẽra sãn wi fo, sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire poorʋm, tɩ sɛka n wi fʋ la sãn wa'am yẽ fɔ, a wʋn yetɩ: ‹M sɩrɛ, wa'am wa zĩ'ire nẽŋam.› Bɛla tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ nã'asgɔ nẽr-sɛba tɩ fʋ zĩ la ba la zẽ'am.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n zẽkrɩ a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sike ẽ, la nẽr-sɛka n sikri a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zẽkɛ ẽ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: La Azezi yele sɛka n wi ẽ na yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ dia wʋntɛɛŋa, bɩɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ wi nẽrba tɩ ba wa'am wa di, da wi'ira fʋ sɩrdõma, bɩɩ fʋ sɔbiisi, bɩɩ fʋ nẽrba, bɩɩ fʋ sawiiba n de bõntatba, se'ere n soe la, ba mẽ wʋn wa ẽŋɛ bɛla daar-sɛka mɛ wi fo lebse bɛla sãnnɛ.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ dia wi nẽrba tɩ ba wa'am di, wi nasdõma, la kɔɔrnɔ, la wɔbsɩ, la fɔɔsɩ.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: Fʋ sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, fʋ wʋn tara zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ba kãn yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ lebse fo. Bɛla, wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ a yɛm na vo'oge la, fʋ wʋn yẽ yɔɔrɔ.».

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: La nẽrba la n lagsɛ zẽa dia zẽ'a la Azezi la, ba wõm ẽn yele se'em na mɛ, tɩ nẽra ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n wʋn po di dia Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam na tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ bɔ ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe, ẽŋɛ dia zozo'e, wi nẽrba zozo'e tɩ ba wa'am wa di.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Tɩ dia la wakatɛ paagɛ, tɩ a tõm a tõntõnna yetɩ, a sẽŋɛ ka yele nẽrba la tɩ ẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Wa'am-ya tɩ mam mãasʋm ba'asɛ mɛ gu'ura ya.›.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra woo yɛta lan pakɛ ẽŋa se'em, tɩ ẽŋa sɔsra sugri. Pɔspɔsɩ dãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Mam da la va'am, tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ lan ãn se'em, bɛla tɩ mam sɔsra ẽ sugri.›.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Tɩ ayɩla yetɩ: ‹Mam da la nii-kɔɔrsɩ pia bɔɔra tɩ m makɛ ba bayi bayi kɔ bɩsɛ, bɛla tɩ mam sɔsra ẽ sugri.›.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Tɩ ayẽma mẽ yetɩ: ‹Mam di la pɔg-sãana, bɛla tɩ mam kãn yãŋɛ wa'am.›.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Tɩ tõntõnna la lebe ka tɔgɛ bɛla bɔ a zuudãana la. Tɩ a zuudãana la sũure yĩige, tɩ a yele a tõntõnna la yetɩ: ‹Yese tɔtɔ sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la sorɔɔrɔ zuto ka tarɩ nasdõma, la kɔɔrnɔ, la fɔɔsɩ, la wɔbsɩ wa'am tɩ ba di.›.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Tɩ tõntõnna la sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ a zuudãana la n yele se'em na, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, fõn yetɩ mam ẽŋɛ se'em na, mam ẽŋɛ mɛ, la zẽ'a ken dee mɛ.›.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Tɩ zuudãana la le yele a tõntõnna la yetɩ: ‹Yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ sorɔ-pɩgsɩ la wuu zuo, ka mõrgɛ tarɩ nẽrba la wa'am tɩ ba kẽ tɩ mam yire pɩrɛ.›.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: La mam yetɩ yãma mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ mam dẽŋɛ yele ba tɩ ba wa'am, tɩ ba zagsɛ la, ba nẽra nẽra kãn le malɩn di mam dia la.».

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Azezi daan sẽnnɩ mɛ, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ dɔla ẽ, tɩ a gelge yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: «Nẽra sãn yetɩ a doose mam dee ka nõŋɛ mam gãnna a sɔ, la a ma, la a pɔga, la a kɔma, la a sɔbiisi, la a tãpa, bɩɩ a sãn ka nõŋɛ mam gãnna a mẽŋa vɔm, a kãn tã'agɛ dɛna mam karẽnbia.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: La sɛka woo n ka sakɛ nãmsgɔ wʋ nẽra n zɛɛrɩ a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam, a kãn tã'agɛ dɛna mam karẽnbia.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Ya mi tɩ nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam bɔɔra tɩ a mẽ de-tʋgma, a wʋn dẽŋɛ zĩ'ire mɛ geele bɩsɛ, ẽn tarɩ se'em na wʋn seke tɩ a yãŋɛ mẽ ba'asɛ, bɩɩ la kãn seke?.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: La sãn dagna bɛla, a sãn ẽebɛ ẽbrɛ dee ka yãŋɛ mẽ ba'asɛ, sɛba wuu n yẽ bɛla la wʋn la ẽ mɛ.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-ẽna ẽebɛ la deego, dee ka yãŋɛ mẽ ba'asɛ.›.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Bɩɩ naba sãn wɛ'ɛsa tɩ a zɛbɛ la a na-tadãana, dẽnɩ tɩ a dẽŋɛ tẽ'esɛ bɩsɛ, ẽŋa n tarɩ nẽrba tʋspia la, a wʋn tã'agɛ zɛbɛ la na-sɛka n tarɩ nẽrba tʋspisyi, bɩɩ a kãn tã'agɛ?.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma zozo'e n daan sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am tɩ ba selse a yetɔga.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: La a sãn bãŋɛ tɩ a kãn tã'agɛ, naba ayẽma la n ken zãagɛ la, a wʋn dẽŋɛ tõm na a tõntõnɩba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ naba la zẽ'am ka soke ẽ bɩsɛ, bãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ zɛbrɛ la ba'asɛ.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la yõnna yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã to'orɩ la tõon-be'ero dõma, dee dita la ba.».

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Bɛla mẽ, yãma nẽr-sɛka woo n ka basrɩ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a tarɩ la, ka tã wʋn dɛna mam karẽnbia.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ wã soke ba yetɩ:.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Yaarʋm ãn sõŋa mɛ, la a sãn sabgɛ, ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne botɩ a le ãna yẽlʋm?.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: «Bʋra-kãna n boe ya pʋam tara piisi kɔbga, tɩ ayɩla sãn bɔɩ, tɩ a kãn lagsɛ piisi piswɛɩ la awɛɩ la basɛ dũnsẽkam, dee sẽŋɛ ka ɛ ayẽma n bɔɩ la halɩ yãŋa yẽ ẽ?.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: A ka le tara yõorɔ la tẽŋa, la a ka tã wʋn dɛna bugulgo mẽ, ba lobrɩ ẽ basra mɛ. Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: La a sãn yẽ ẽ, a wʋn yõkɛ ẽ mɛ buke la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: La a sãn kule yire, a wʋn wi a sɩrdõma, la a sawiiba mɛ yele ba yetɩ: ‹Naagɛ-ya la mam tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, mam yẽ m pesgo la n bɔɩ la mɛ.›.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Bɛla mam yetɩ ya tɩ tõon-be'ero dãana ayɩla sãn teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, sũ-yẽlga boe Nawẽnnɛ yire pʋam la bɛla gãnna nẽr-sõma piswɛɩ la awɛɩ n ka eerɩ tɩ ba teege yɛm.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Bɩɩ, pɔg-kãna n boe wʋn tara lig-kõba pia, tɩ ayɩla sãn bɔɩ, tɩ a kãn yõ fɩtla pɩɩsɛ deego la ɛ lagfɔ la sõŋa halɩ wa yẽ ẽ?.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: La a sãn yẽ ẽ, a wʋn wi a sɩrdõma, la a sawiiba mɛ yele ba yetɩ: ‹Naagɛ-ya la mam tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, mam yẽ m lagfɔ la n bɔɩ la mɛ.›.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Bɛla mam yetɩ ya tɩ tõon-be'ero dãana ayɩla sãn teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, sũ-yẽlga boe Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma zẽ'am na bɛla.».

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Bʋraa n daan boe tara a dayɔɔsɩ bayi.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Tɩ yɩbga la yele a sɔ yetɩ: ‹M sɔ, pʋɩ fʋ bõntarsõm na bɔ mam m bõnɔ.› Tɩ a sɔ pʋɩ a bõntarsõm na bɔ ba.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ dabsa fẽe, tɩ yɩbga la lagsɛ a bõntarsõm na wuu yese sẽŋɛ tẽŋa n zãagɛ, ka tõm tõon-yooro sã'am ẽn tarɩ sɛla la wuu.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: La ẽn sã'am a bõntarsõm na wuu ba'asɛ la, kɔm kãtɛ n wa'am tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ a ka le tara sɛla wʋn di.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la nẽra ayɩla zẽ'am tɩ a dɩkɛ ẽ tɩ a tõnna. Tɩ nẽra la dɩkɛ ẽ botɩ a sẽŋɛ mõom ka gu'ura a kurkurdõma.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Tɩ a kɔm dõnna tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a di kurkuri dia. Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka bɔ ẽ.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Tɩ a tẽ'esɛ a sũurum yetɩ: ‹Mam sɔ tarɩ tõntõnɩba zo'oge mɛ tɩ ba dita tɩgra dee basra, dee tɩ mam bɔna kalam kiira la kɔm.›.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: Yãŋa yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn lebe kule la m sɔ yire ka yele ẽ yetɩ, mam tõm na Nawẽnnɛ la fõn be'em.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: mam ka le masɛ tɩ ba wi mam tɩ fʋ bia. Ɩta mam wʋ fʋ tõntõnɩba la ayɩla.›.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Dee isge kulla a sɔ yire. La ẽn nãn boe zãazãarɛ la, tɩ a sɔ la yẽ ẽ, tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a zoe tʋ'ʋsɛ kɔkɛ ẽ, mõbgɛ ẽ.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Tɩ bia la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M sɔ, mam tõm na Nawẽnnɛ la fõn be'em. Mam ka masɛ tɩ ba le wi'ira mam tɩ fʋ bia.›.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Tɩ a sɔ la yele a tõntõnɩba yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ-ya tɔtɔ ka tarɩ fu-sɛka n ãn sõŋa gãnna la wa'am wa yɛ ẽ, dee dɩkɛ nutʋa pire a nu'ubiire, dee dɩkɛ tagra pire ẽ.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: La ya yõkɛ lɔl-polle n nõbɛ wa'am wa kʋ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ dia di, dee ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam bia wã daan wõn na a ki mɛ, dee le vo'oge. A daan bɔɩ mɛ, tɩ tɩ le yẽ ẽ.› Tɩ ba yãŋa ɩta sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a dayɔ-kẽema la boe la va'am. La ẽn lebe kulna wa lɛm yire la, a wõm na duursi tɩ ba wẽ'erɩ wa'ara.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Tɩ a wi a sɔ tõntõnna ayɩla soke ẽ yetɩ: ‹Bẽm n ẽŋɛ?›.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Tɩ tõntõnna la lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Fʋ yɩbga la n wa'am, tɩ fʋ sɔ kʋ lɔl-polle n nõbɛ, ẽŋa n yẽ fʋ yɩbga la tɩ a tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm na ĩyã.›.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Tɩ kẽema la sũure yĩige halɩ. Tɩ a zagsɛ tɩ ẽŋa kãn kẽ yire. Tɩ a sɔ yese belem ẽ yetɩ, a wa'am wa kẽ.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Tɩ a lerge a sɔ la yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ, yʋʋma zozo'e tɩ mam tõnnɩ fʋ tõoma, dee ka zagsɛ fʋ nõorɛ baa daarɛ ayɩla, la fõn ka bɔ mam bulla baa daarɛ ayɩla tɩ m kʋ tɩ mam la m sɩrdõma ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: La fʋ bi-ẽna wã n dɩkɛ fʋ bõntarsõm sẽŋɛ ka tõm yalsɩ tõoma sã'am ba wuu dee kulum wã, fʋ kʋ la lɔl-polle bɔ ẽ.›.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Tɩ a sɔ lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹M bia, fõn boe la mam zẽ'am daarɛ woo. La mam n tarɩ sɛla wuu la de la fõn bõnɔ.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: La, la masɛ mɛ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm, se'ere n soe la, fʋ yɩbga wã daan wõn na a ki mɛ, dee le vo'oge. A daan bɔɩ mɛ. Tɩ tɩ le yẽ ẽ.› ».

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bʋraaga n daan boe dɛna bõntarsa, tara tõntõnna tɩ a bɩsra a yire yelle. Tɩ ba wa'am wa yele bõntarsa la yetɩ, tõntõnna la sã'anɩ a bõntarsõm na mɛ.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Tɩ zuudãana la wi a tõntõnna la yetɩ: ‹Bẽm tɩ ba tɔgra fõn yelle tɩ mam wõnna wãna? Geele pa'alɛ ma fõn ẽŋɛ mam bõntarsõm na se'em. Fʋ kãn le bɩsra mam yire yelle.›.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Tɩ tõntõnna la yele a mẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Mam zuudãana la yetɩ a dige mam a tõoma la pʋam mɛ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Kʋa? Mam ka tarɩ pãŋa wʋn kɔ. La yãnnɛ n wʋn tara mam la sosgo.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: Mam mi mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ a sãn wa dige ma, m tara sɩrdõma n wʋn to'oge mam ba yiɛ sõŋa.›.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Tɩ a wi a zuudãana sãnditba ayɩla ayɩla, soke pɔspɔsɩ dãana la yetɩ: ‹Fõn tarɩ mam zuudãana sãnnɛ la wãne?›.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: ‹Mam tarɩ a sãnnɛ na kaam dugl-kãra kɔbga.› Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹To'oge fʋ sãnnɛ gõŋɔ tɔtɔ zĩ'ire gʋlsɛ yetɩ, kaam dugl-kãra pinuu.›.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Dee soke ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Fõn tarɩ mam zuudãana sãnnɛ na wãne?› Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: ‹Mam tarɩ a sãnnɛ na mui bɔra kɔbga.› Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹To'oge fʋ sãnnɛ gõŋɔ gʋlsɛ yetɩ pinii.›.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Baa la tõntõnna wã ɩrgɔ n ka doose sore la, a zuudãana pẽgɛ ẽ mɛ, ẽn tõm na yɛm na ĩyã. Dũni-ẽna wã dõma tarɩ la yɛm tõnna la taaba gãnna nẽr-sɛba n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ peelem.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya dɩkɛ dũnia bõntarsõm yõgɛ sɩrdõma, tɩ dũnia bõntarsõm na sãn wa ba'asɛ, ba wʋn to'oge ya tɩ ya bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n de sɩra dãana bõn-pɩgsɩ pʋam, mẽ de la sɩra dãana bõn-kãra pʋam. La sɛka n ka de sɩra dãana bõn-pɩgsɩ pʋam, mẽ ka de sɩra dãana bõn-kãra pʋam.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Bɛla, ya sãn ka dɛna sɩra dõma dũnia wã bõntarsõm pʋam, ãne n wʋn dɩkɛ bõntarsõm sɩra sɩra gu'ule ya?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: La ya sãn ka dɛna sɩra dõma la bõn-sɛba n dagɩ ya mẽŋa bõnɔ, ãne n wʋn dɩkɛ bõnɔ bɔ ya tɩ la dɛna ya mẽŋa bõnɔ?.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Tõntõnna ka tã wʋn sakɛ zuudãandõma bayi. A wʋn sise ayɩla mɛ, dee nõŋɛ ayɩla, bɩɩ a wʋn nanna ayɩla la mɛ, dee pɔ'ɔra ayɩla la. Bɛla, yãma ka tã wʋn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de ya Zuudãana, dee botɩ bõntarsõm mẽ dɛna ya zuudãana.».

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Farisi dõma n nõŋɛ ligri la n wõm bɛla la, ba la'arɩ Azezi mɛ.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma pa'alɩ ya mẽŋa nẽrba nẽŋam wʋ ya de la nẽr-sõma. La Nawẽnnɛ mi ya pʋtẽ'era n ãn se'em mɛ. Bõn-sɛka tɩ nẽrsaalba pẽgrɩ la, de la bõn-dẽgrɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Amoyisi lɔɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yʋʋn pa'alɩ nẽrba ban wʋn ãna se'em, ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Azã wa'am na. Lan pɔsɛ Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, ba mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga mɛ, tɩ nẽra woo nargra pãŋa tɩ a kẽ.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: La, la de la nãana tɩ saazuum la dũnia tole gãnna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la bõn-pɩka ayɩla bɔɩ.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n basɛ a pɔga, dee le di pɔka ayẽma, a ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ nẽra n gã'arɩ la a tadãana pɔga, la sɛka woo n di pɔka tɩ a sɩra basɛ mẽ ẽŋɛ la be'em.».

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Bõntarsa n daan boe, yɛɛra futo n ba ãna sõŋa, dee tara sɛla woo daarɛ woo tɩ la seke.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Tɩ nasa ayɩla mẽ daan bɔna, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Alazaarɩ, tɩ a ĩyã wuu tara nõtɔ. A daan ẽn gã la bõntarsa la zanõrʋm.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: bɔɔra tɩ a di bõntarsa la n diti tɩ di-zõra luta la. Tɩ baasɩ sẽnna lɛɛla a nõtɔ la.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Tɩ Alazaarɩ wa ki. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire Abraham zẽ'am. Tɩ bõntarsa la mẽ wa ki, tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Tɩ bõntarsa la daan bɔna bug-tẽŋa nãmsra, yãŋa gorge yẽ Abraham halɩ zẽ'a n zãagɛ, tɩ Alazaarɩ zẽa a sakʋkɔ.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Tɩ bõntarsa la wi Abraham yetɩ: ‹M sɔ Abraham, zoe m ninbãalga, dee tõm Alazaarɩ tɩ a dɩkɛ a nu'ubiire tẽ'egɛ ko'om mã'alɛ m zɩlɩŋa, se'ere n soe la, mam boe la bugum pʋam nãmsra.›.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Tɩ Abraham yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M bia, tẽegɛ tɩ fõn daan boe dũnia zuo wakat-sɛka la, fõn daan tarɩ bõn-sõma mɛ, dee tɩ Alazaarɩ yẽta toogo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, a sũure mã'agɛ mɛ, dee tɩ fõn yãŋa nãmsra.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: La bõn-sɛka n pa'asɛ, bog-ko'ogo n beŋe tõma la yãma, tɩ nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a ze'ele kalam sẽŋɛ yãma zẽ'am, a kãn tã'agɛ. La nẽra mẽ sãn bɔɔra tɩ a ze'ele yãma zẽ'am wa'am tõma zẽ'am, a kãn tã'agɛ.›.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Tɩ a lerge Abraham yetɩ: ‹M sɔ Abraham, la sãn dɛna bɛla, mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ fʋ tõm Alazaarɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ mam sɔ yire.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: tɩ mam tarɩ la sɔbiisi banuu, a sẽŋɛ ka kã'an ba, tɩ ba mẽ da wa'am nãmsgɔ zẽ'a wã.›.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Tɩ Abraham yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ba tarɩ Amoyisi la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n gʋlsɛ gõn-sɛba la mɛ. La masɛ tɩ ba sakɛ la gõn-bãma yetɔga.›.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹M sɔ, ayɛɩ, nẽra sãn vo'oge lebe sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am, ba wʋn teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.›.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Tɩ Abraham lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ba sãn kãn sakɛ Amoyisi, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la nõorɛ, baa nẽra n ki sãn vo'oge lebe, ba kãn sakɛ.› ».

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «La ka tã tɩ bõn-sɛba n pã'asrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba basra Nawẽnnɛ sore la ka wa'am. La nẽr-sɛka n wʋn botɩ bõn-bãma wa'am na wʋn yẽ toogo.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Ba sãn dɩkɛ nẽerɛ kug-kãtɛ lʋ yulse a yugla lobe ẽ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam, n sõɩ ẽŋa n wʋn botɩ bi-pɩka ayɩla basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa. Fʋ sɔbia sãn tõm be'em, fʋ kã'an ẽ. La a sãn teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero la, fʋ basɛ ẽ taalɛ.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: La a sãn ẽŋɛ fʋ be'em nõorɛ ayopɔɩ daarɛ ayɩla, dee leme fʋ zẽ'am nõorɛ ayopɔɩ wa yetɩ: ‹Mam tõm na be'em, bɔ mam sugri›, dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ basɛ ẽ taalɛ.».

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Botɩ tɩ bɔ fɔ sɩra zo'oge.».

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn bɔ mam sɩra fẽe wʋ sɔ'ɔrɔ biire la, ya tã wʋn yele kẽnkãŋa wã tɩ a võogɛ sẽŋɛ ka sele mõgrɛ pʋam ke, la a wʋn sakɛ ya nõorɛ mɛ.».

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: «Tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ, nẽra sãn bɔna yãma pʋam tara tõntõnna tɩ a kɔɔra, bɩɩ gu'ura a dũnsi, tɩ tõntõnna la sãn ze'ele va'am wa'am, a wʋn yele ẽ mɛ yetɩ, wa'am wa di bɩɩ?.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Ayɛɩ, a wʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Dʋgɛ m dia, dee teege fuugo yɛ, tarɩ dia la wa'am wa bɔ ma tɩ m di. M sãn di dee yũ ba'asɛ, tɩ fʋ mẽ yãŋa di dee yũ.›.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: La zuudãana la n yele tõntõnna la tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ a ẽŋɛ la, zuudãana la kãn pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Bɛla mẽ, ya sãn ẽŋɛ sɛla woo tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele ya tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la, ya yetɩ: ‹Tõma wen dɛna la tõntõnɩba, tõma ka narɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ, se'ere n soe la, la de la tɩ tõoma, la masɛ mɛ tɩ tɩ tõm.› ».

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Azezi daan boe la sore wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm dɔla Samaari la Galile tẽŋasʋka.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: paagɛ tẽŋa ayɩla pʋam, tɩ kõnkɔma pia tʋ'ʋsra ẽ, ze'ele kẽnkɛrŋa.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: kaasra yetɩ: «Zuudãana Azezi, zoe tɩ ninbãalga.».

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: La ẽn yẽ ba la, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya kãabgɔ kẽma la zẽ'am tɩ ba ka bɩsɛ ya ĩyã la n ãn se'em.» La ban sẽnnɩ wɛ'ɛsa la, tɩ bã'asɩ la basɛ ba, tɩ ba ãna sõŋa.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: La ba nẽra ayɩla n yẽ tɩ a kõnkɔm na basɛ ẽ na, tɩ a lɛbra Azezi zẽ'am dee tɔgra ke'enke'em pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: sẽŋɛ ka ka dũma dɩkɛ a nẽŋa võglɛ tẽŋa Azezi nẽŋam pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ. A daan de la Samaari nẽra.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «La dagɩ bãma pia n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm? Tɩ bawɛɩ la bɔna la bɛ?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Bẽm tɩ ẽna n de sãana la mã'a n leme wa'am tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ?».

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn bɔ mam sɩra la n botɩ fʋ yẽ fãarɛ, isge sẽŋɛ.».

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Farisi dõma soke Azezi mɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di na'am nẽrba sʋkam. Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa dita na'am, la ka de sɛla tɩ nẽrba tã wʋn yẽ la nini.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: La nẽra kãn yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la kalam›, bɩɩ ‹a boe la ke›. Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boe la ya tẽŋasʋka dita na'am.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: La Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ ya wʋn bɔɔra tɩ ya yẽ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la, baa la sãn dɛna daarɛ ayɩla, la ya kãn yẽ ẽ.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: La nẽrba wʋn yele ya yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la ke›, bɩɩ ‹bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la kalam.› Da kãn sẽŋɛ-ya, da doose-ya ba.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Se'ere n soe la, daar-sɛka tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme na, la wʋn ãna wʋ saaga n ẽn yãkɛ saazuum tɩ la nẽegɛ zẽ'a wuu se'em na.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: La, lan dẽŋɛ tɩ bõn-bãma wʋn ẽŋɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ a nãmsɛ zozo'e tɩ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma zagsɛ ẽ.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Wakat-sɛka tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme wa'am na, la wʋn ãna wʋ Anoe wakatɛ tɩ la yʋʋn ãn se'em na.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Nẽrba yʋʋn diti mɛ, dee yũura, tɩ bʋraasɩ dita pɔgba, tɩ pɔgsɩ mẽ ɛlla sɩrba, tɩ la wa paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ Anoe kẽ õorŋ-kãtɛ la pʋam, dee tɩ ko'om yãŋa wa'am wa pɩrɛ zẽ'a wuu libe nẽrba wuu tɩ ba ki.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: La mẽ wʋn ãna wʋ Alotɩ wakatɛ n yʋʋn ãn se'em na. Ba yʋʋn diti mɛ, dee yũura, dee da'ara, dee kɔɔsra, dee bʋta, dee mẽta.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ Alotɩ fõrgɛ Sodom tẽŋa la, dee tɩ bugum n diti kidibre ze'ele saazuum lui tẽŋa kʋ ba wuu.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo daarɛ la mẽ wʋn di'ige ba la bɛla.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Daar-ẽŋa, nẽr-sɛka n boe gɔsgɔ dee tɩ a lɔgrɔ bɔna deego pʋam, a da sige bɔɔra tɩ a ze'ele dɩkɛ ba zoe. La nẽr-sɛka n boe a va'am, da lebe kule.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya lan yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Alotɩ pɔga se'em na.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ, la nẽr-sɛka n sakɛ tɩ a koŋe a vɔm, a wʋn yẽ ẽ.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽrba bayi sãn gã sõŋɔ ayɩla pʋam yʋ'ʋŋɔ, ba wʋn dɩkɛ ayɩla, dee basɛ ayẽma.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: La pɔgsɩ bayi sãn bɔna nẽerʋm nẽena, ba wʋn dɩkɛ ayɩla, dee basɛ ayẽma.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la soke Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, bõn-ẽna wʋn ẽŋɛ la bɛ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ bõn-kɩ'ɩsɩ boe la, zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ zõa lagsrɩ taaba.».

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Azezi daan dolɩ la Zeriko tẽŋa pʋam tɔlla.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: tɩ bʋraa bɔna mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azase. A de la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba naba, dee dɛna bõntarsa.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: A daan boorɩ tɩ a bãŋɛ la Azezi n de nẽr-sɛka. A de la nẽr-gɩlga, la nẽrba la n zo'oge la ĩyã, a ka tã wʋn yẽ Azezi.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Tɩ a zoe tole nẽŋam sẽŋɛ Azezi n we zẽ'am bɔba la ka zom kẽnkãŋa tɩ a bɩsɛ Azezi.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ zẽ'e-ẽŋa, gorge bɩsɛ kẽnkãŋa la zuo yẽ ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azase, sige tɔtɔ, se'ere n soe la, dẽnɩ tɩ mam gã'arɛ fõn yire zĩna.».

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Tɩ Azase sige tɔtɔ, to'oge ẽ sẽŋɛ a yire la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: La nẽrba la n yẽ bɛla la, ba wuu yõnnɩ mɛ yetɩ: «A sẽŋɛ ka kẽ tɩ a bɔna la tõon-be'ero dãana la yire.».

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Tɩ Azase zɛ Azezi nẽŋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, mam wʋn dɩkɛ m bõntarsõm pʋsʋka pʋɩ bɔ nasdõma. La mam sãn pã'asɛ nẽrba to'oge ba sɛla, mam wʋn lebse bɔ ba nõor-anaasɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zĩna yi-ẽna dõma yẽ fãarɛ, se'ere n soe la, ẽna mẽ sɩrɩ de la Abraham yɩɩŋa.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa'am dũnia zuo tɩ a ɛ la nẽr-sɛba n bɔɩ la, fãagɛ ba.».

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Nẽrba la n daan selsrɩ Azezi yetɔga la, tɩ a makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba, se'ere n soe la, a daan lɛm na Zerizalɛm, tɩ nẽrba la tẽ'esra tɩ wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba wʋn yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ diti na'am ba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Bɛla tɩ a makɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nabia n yʋʋn yetɩ a sẽŋɛ tẽŋa n zãagɛ ka di na'am leme wa'am.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Ẽn yʋʋn yetɩ a sẽŋɛ la, a wi la a tõntõnɩba pia bɔ ba sãlma lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla ayɩla, yele ba yetɩ: ‹Dɩkɛ-ya ligri la lɛɛbra, tɩ m sẽŋɛ dee leme wa'am.›.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: La a so'olʋm na nẽrba nifo kẽ ẽ mɛ, tɩ ẽn fõrgɛ la, tɩ ba tõm tõntõnɩba tɩ ba babsɛ ẽ ka yetɩ, bãma ka boorɩ tɩ a dɛna bãma naba.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: La baa la bɛla a di na'am na mɛ, lebe kule ka basɛ tɩ ba wi tõntõn-sɛba tɩ a daan bɔ ba ligri la, tɩ ba wa'am tɩ a soke ba bãŋɛ, nẽra woo n leebe yẽ yõorɔ se'em.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Tɩ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, mam dɩkɛ fʋ sãlma lag-kõbrɛ la leebe yẽ la yõorɔ sãlma lig-kõba pia pa'asɛ.›.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Fʋ ẽŋɛ sõŋa, fʋ de la tõntõn-sõŋɔ! Fõn ẽŋɛ sɩra la bõnɔ fẽe la ĩyã, fʋ wʋn sɔna tẽnsɩ pia bɩsra.›.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Tɩ bʋyi dãana la mẽ wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, mam dɩkɛ fʋ sãlma lagfɔ la leebe yẽ la yõorɔ sãlma lig-kõba banuu pa'asɛ.›.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Tɩ a yele ẽ mẽ yetɩ: ‹Fõn wʋn sɔna tẽnsɩ banuu bɩsra.›.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Tɩ ayẽma mẽ wa'am wa yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, fʋ lagfɔ la n wãna, mam vile ẽ na tãnnɛ sʋgɛ.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: mam zotɩ fõn mɛ, se'ere n soe la, fõn de la nẽra yelle n tɔɩ. Fõn to'orɩ la bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ mẽŋa ka tõm na yõorɔ, dee lagsra bõnkɔɔl-sɛba tɩ fõn ka bʋrɛ.›.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Fʋ de la tõntõn-be'ego, mam wʋn dɩkɛ la fʋ nõorɛ yetɔga la di fʋ sarɩya. Fõn mi tɩ mam de la nẽra yelle n tɔɩ, tɔ'ɔra bõn-sɛba tɩ mam ka tõm na yõorɔ, dee lagsra bõnkɔɔl-sɛba tɩ mam ka bʋrɛ la.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: bẽm ĩyã tɩ fʋ ka tarɩ m ligri la ka bĩŋe bãnkɩ pʋam, tɩ m sãn leme wa'am tɩ m to'oge m ligri la, la yõorɔ?›.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Dee yele sɛba n boe bilam na yetɩ: ‹To'oge-ya lagfɔ la bɔ sɛka n tarɩ ligri pia la.›.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, a põn tarɩ la ligri pia.›.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: ‹Mam yetɩ ya tɩ sɛka woo n tarɩ, a wʋn to'oge pa'asɛ. La sɛka woo n ka tarɩ, a wʋn koŋe sɛla fẽe tɩ a tarɩ la.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: La sãn dɛna mam bɛ'ɛba la n ka boorɩ tɩ mam di na'am na, tarɩ-ya ba wa'am wa kʋ ba mam nẽŋam.› ».

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: La Azezi n daan tɔgɛ bɛla ba'asɛ la, a tole la nẽrba la nẽŋa tɩ ba wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: La ẽn daan sẽŋɛ ka lɛm Betɩfaazɩ, la Betani n boe Oliivi tãŋa zẽ'am na, a tõm na a karẽnbiisi bayi.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya tẽn-sɛka n boe nẽŋa la pʋam. Ya sãn paagɛ mĩ, ya wʋn yẽ bõn-biire tɩ ba lʋ ze'ele, tɩ nẽra nẽra nãn ka bã ẽ. Ya lorge ẽ tarɩ wa'am.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: La nẽra sãn soke ya yetɩ: ‹Wãne tɩ ya lɔrgra ẽ?›, ya lerge yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana la n boorɩ ẽ.› ».

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: La ẽŋa n tõm sɛba la sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ, yẽ sɛla woo wʋ ẽn yele ba se'em na.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Tɩ ba lorge bõn-biire la, tɩ bõŋa la dõma soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya lɔrgra bõŋa la?».

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ba yetɩ: «Zuudãana la n boorɩ ẽ.».

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ bõŋa la sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am, ka dɩkɛ ba futo paglɛ a zuo, tɩ Azezi bã.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: La ẽn daan we la, nẽrba la yɛrgɛ la ba futo sore la zuo.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Ẽn daan lɛm Zerizalɛm na, a dolɩ la Oliivi tãŋa sore la sigra, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu n de a karẽnbiisi la ɩta sũ-yẽlga zẽkɛ ba kõosɩ pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ ke'enke'em, a tõon-yãlma tɩ ba yẽ la ĩyã.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: yɛta tɩ: «Pẽgɛ-ya na-sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la. Sũ-mã'asʋm boe la Nawẽnnɛ yire, nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum.».

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: La Farisi dõma basɛba n boe nẽrba la pʋam yele Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tãsɛ fʋ karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba sĩna.».

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ba sãn sĩna, kuga wʋn kaasɛ tagurga.».

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Azezi n daan lɛm Zerizalɛm yẽ tẽŋa la, a kellɩ mɛ tẽŋa la nẽrba ĩyã.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: yetɩ: «Ya sãn mi ni zĩna wã bõn-sɛka n wʋn bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm, la wʋn ãn nɩ sõŋa! La, la sʋgɛ mɛ, tɩ ya nini ka yẽta ẽ.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: La wakatɛ sẽm mɛ, tɩ ya bɛ'ɛba wʋn wa'am wa mẽ lalga gilge ya tẽŋa wã wuu gu, tɩ ya nẽrba kãn tã'agɛ yese.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Ba wʋn sã'am tẽŋa la wuu mɛ, dee kʋ yãma sɛba wuu n boe a pʋam na. Ba wʋn sã'am ẽ saam saam mɛ, tɩ kugre kãn weege a tadãana zuo. Bõn-bãna n wʋn ẽŋɛ la, de la yan ka bãŋɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wa'am tɩ a fãagɛ ya la ĩyã.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla ba'asɛ la, a kẽ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam digra sɛba n koosrɩ la tɩ ba yɛsra.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: dee yele ba yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam deego wã wʋn dɛna la pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego.› La yãma dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ la nayigba lagsgɔ zẽ'a.».

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: La daarɛ woo a boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la tẽŋa la nẽrzuto daan ɛɛra sore tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: La ba ka mi ba wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, se'ere n soe la, nẽrba la wuu daan ãn na sɛlɩ sɛlsra a yetɔga la.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ pa'alɛ ba tɩ ba sɔsra Nawẽnnɛ wakatɛ wuu da basɛ.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: yele ba yetɩ: «Sarɩyadita n daan boe tẽŋa ayɩla ka zɔta Nawẽnnɛ, dee ka nanna nẽrba.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Tɩ pɔkõorɛ mẽ bɔna tẽŋa la pʋam sẽnna a zẽ'am wakatɛ wuu yɛta ẽ tɩ: ‹Sõŋɛ ma tɩ m yẽ bʋʋrɔ m bɛ zẽ'am.›.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: La pɔkõorɛ la sẽŋɛ ka sose ẽ na nõor-faa, tɩ sarɩyadita la ka sõŋɛ ẽ. La pɔkõorɛ la ẽn ken sẽnna sɔsra ẽ na, sarɩyadita la wa yele a pʋtẽ'erʋm mɛ yetɩ: ‹Baa mam ka zotɩ Nawẽnnɛ, dee ka nanna nẽrba la.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: pɔkõ-ẽna n sẽnnɩ dãana mam se'em wã, mam wʋn isge mɛ sõŋɛ ẽ. La sãn dagna bɛla, a wʋn sẽnna mɛ nãmsra mam, a kãn basɛ.› ».

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi le yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya sarɩyadita la n de nẽr-be'ego la n yele se'em.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ kãn sõŋɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ a looge la n sosrɩ ẽ wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ la, bɔ ba bʋʋrɔ gãnna bɛla? A wʋn yuuge mɛ dee sõŋɛ ba bɩɩ?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ a wʋn wa sõŋɛ ba mɛ tɔtɔ. La Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sãn wa leme dũnia zuo, a wʋn yẽ nẽrba n bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Azezi le makɛ la makrɛ ayẽma bɔ nẽr-sɛba n tẽ'esrɩ tɩ bãma de la nẽr-sõma nẽ'esra nẽrba basɛba la, yetɩ:.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: «Bʋraasɩ bayi n sẽŋɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na tɩ ba ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, ayɩla de la Farisi nẽra, tɩ ayẽma dɛna lampo-tɔ'ɔsa.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Tɩ Farisi nẽra la zɛ saazuo pʋ'ʋsra a sũurum yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fʋ, mam n ka wõn na nẽrba basɛba la n ãn se'em na, n de nayigba, bɩɩ nẽr-be'ero, bɩɩ nẽrba n tõnnɩ yalsɩ, la mam n ka wõn na lampo-tɔ'ɔs-ẽna wã la ĩyã.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Dabsa ayopɔɩ woo pʋam, mam lʋtɩ nõorɛ la bʋyi, la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam yẽtɩ la, pia pʋam woo mam loorɩ ayɩla bɔ'ɔra fõn mɛ.›.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: La lampo-tɔ'ɔsa la ze'ele la kẽnkɛrŋa põn ka gorge a nẽŋa saazuum, dee wẽ nupʋ'ʋgɔ yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ, zoe m ninbãalga, mam de la tõon-be'ero dãana.› ».

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ bʋra-ẽna wã n kule la, a yelle masɛ mɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dee tɩ Farisi nẽra la yelle ka masɛ. Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n zẽkrɩ a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sike ẽ mɛ, la nẽr-sɛka n sikri a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zẽkɛ ẽ.».

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Nẽrba basɛba n daan tarɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ wa'am Azezi zẽ'am tɩ a dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto. La a karẽnbiisi la n yẽ bɛla la, ba zɛbɛ nẽrba la mɛ.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Tɩ Azezi wi a karẽnbiisi la yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya kɔma la tɩ ba wa'am mam zẽ'am, da gu'ura-ya ba, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔma wã bõnɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n ka sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɔna ẽ wʋ bia la, a kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Zifdõma nẽrzuo ayɩla n daan soke Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fõn de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fõn wi mam tɩ nẽr-sõŋɔ? Nẽra ka boe n ãn sõŋa, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ mã'a.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Fõn mi Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba ɩta se'em na: ‹Da gã'arɛ la fʋ tadãana pɔga, da kʋ nẽra, da zũ, da tɔgɛ põmpɔrŋɔ kaseto, nanna fʋ sɔ la fʋ ma.› ».

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Halɩ mam bõnbɩ'ɩlʋm tɩ mam põn sakra bõn-bãma wuu.».

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Azezi n wõm ẽn yele bɛla la, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛla ayɩla n deege dɛna fʋ ẽŋɛ: Koose fõn tarɩ sɛla la wuu dɩkɛ ligri la pʋɩ nasdõma, tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ bõntarsõm Nawẽnnɛ yire, dee wa'am wa dɔla mam.».

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: La bʋraa la n wõm bɛla la, tɩ a sũure ba sã'am, se'ere n soe la, a daan ba dɛna la bõntarsa.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Azezi n yẽ tɩ a sũure sã'am bɛla la, tɩ a yetɩ: «La tɔ mɛ bɔ bõntatba tɩ ba kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: La de la nãana tɩ yʋgnɛ doose fupĩim yoko pʋam yese, gãnna bõntarsa n wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Tɩ sɛba n wõm bɛla la yele yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛka tɩ nẽrsaalba ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ la, Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn ẽŋɛ mɛ.».

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma basɛ tõma n tarɩ sɛla mɛ dee dɔla fõn.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
