local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n basɛ a yire, bɩɩ a pɔga, bɩɩ a sɔbiisi, bɩɩ a sɔ la a ma, bɩɩ a kɔma, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na ĩyã.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: a wʋn to'oge zo'oge gãnna ẽn basɛ sɛla la dũnia wã zuo, dee wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ wakat-sɛka n sẽm na.».

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Azezi daan tarɩ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la mɛ yese kẽnkɛrŋa, ka yele ba yetɩ: «Ya yẽ ya, tɩ we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm, la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yʋʋn gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la yelle la, wʋn sɩrɩ ẽŋɛ mɛ.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Ba wʋn yõkɛ ẽ mɛ ka ẽŋɛ bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum, tɩ ba la ẽ, yaalɛ ẽ, tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm mĩ ẽ.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: wẽ ẽ na ka'asa, dee kʋ ẽ. La dabsa atã daarɛ a wʋn vo'oge.».

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: La ba ka bãŋɛ ẽn yele se'em na võorɛ baa fẽe, yetɔga la daan sʋgɛ mɛ, tɩ ba ka tã'agɛ bãŋɛ yetɔga la võorɛ n boorɩ tɩ la yele se'em.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Azezi daan we'esɩ ka lɛm na Zeriko, tɩ fʋa zẽa sore kẽnkɛrŋa sɔsra.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: wõm nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n tollɩ la dãmnɛ, soke yetɩ: «Bẽm ẽŋɛ?».

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la n tollɩ.».

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Tɩ a tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Adavidi yɩɩŋa Azezi, zoe m ninbãalga.».

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n boe a nẽŋam na tãsɛ ẽ yetɩ a sĩna. Tɩ a malɩn tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe m ninbãalga.».

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele, yetɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am. La ẽn lɛm Azezi la, tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la bẽm bɔ fɔ?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam boorɩ tɩ m le yẽta mɛ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Le yẽta, fõn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã n botɩ fʋ yẽ laafɛ.».

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ a le yẽta dɔla Azezi pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ nẽrba la wuu n yẽ bɛla la, mẽ nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Daarɛ ayɩla Azezi boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba, dee mõola kõ-yẽlga la bɔ'ɔra ba. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yele to, fõn yẽ nõorɛ la bɛ ɩta bõn-bãna, bɩɩ ãne n bɔ fõn nõorɛ tɩ fõn ɩta?».

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam mẽ wʋn soke ya sokre ayɩla tɩ ya lerge ma:.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm ẽ bɩɩ, la de la nẽrsaalba?».

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Tɩ ba bãm na taaba yetɩ: «Tɩ sãn yetɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm ẽ, a wʋn yetɩ, bẽm ĩyã tɩ tõma ka sakɛ ẽ?.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: La tɩ sãn yetɩ la de la nẽrsaalba, nẽrba la wuu wʋn lobe tɩ la kuga kʋ, se'ere n soe la, nẽrba la sakɛ tɩ Azã daan de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.».

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Tɩ ba koŋe lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka mi sɛka n tõm ẽ.».

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bɛla, mam mẽ kãn yele ya mam n yẽ nõorɛ la zẽ'e-sɛka ɩta bõn-bãna wã.».

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ ẽna bɔ nẽrba la yetɩ: «Bʋraaga n daan kɔ a va'am sele tɩɩsɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de viinye, dɩkɛ va'am na bɔ nẽrba tɩ ba bɩsra, dee sẽŋɛ tẽŋa n zãagɛ ka yuuge.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Tɩ viinye la biɛ go'osgo wakatɛ paagɛ, tɩ a tõm a tõntõnna tɩ a sẽŋɛ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na zẽ'am ka to'oge a pʋrɛ wa'am wa bɔ ẽ. Tɩ sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na wẽ ẽ, dee botɩ a lebe kule zãŋa.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Tɩ a le tõm a tõntõnna ayɩla ba zẽ'am, tɩ ba wẽ ẽ, dee yaalɛ ẽ, botɩ a mẽ lebe kule zãŋa.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Tɩ a le malɩn tõm ayɩla bʋtã pʋam, tɩ ba wẽ ẽ pɔglʋm, dee dige ẽ va'am na pʋam.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Tɩ va'am na dãana la tẽ'esɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? M yãŋa wʋn tõm na m mẽŋa bi-nõŋrɛ, tã tɩ ba wʋn nã'asɛ ẽŋa.›.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: La sɛba n bɩsrɩ va'am na n yẽ va'am na dãana mẽŋa dayʋa la, tɩ ba bãm na taaba yetɩ: ‹Ẽna n wʋn wa sɔɔgɛ sɔna a sɔ bõntarsõm na, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ kʋ ẽ tɩ bõntarsõm na lebge tõma bõnɔ.›.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ ve'ege yese va'am na kẽnkɛrŋa ka kʋ ẽ. Bɛla, va'am na dãana yãŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽr-bãna la wãne?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: A wʋn wa'am wa kʋ nẽrba la mɛ, dee to'oge va'am na bɔ nẽrba basɛba tɩ ba bɩsra.» La nẽrba la n wõm bɛla la, ba yetɩ: «La kãn ẽŋɛ bɛla baa fẽfẽe.».

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Tɩ Azezi bɩsra ba, dee yetɩ: «La bɛla võorɛ de la bẽm tɩ ba gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Kug-sɛka tɩ tõnmẽtba zagsɛ la, lebge la kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa›?.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n lui kug-ẽŋa zuo, a wʋn kɔ kɔ mɛ, la kugre la sãn lui nẽr-sɛka zuo, a wʋn mẽrgɛ ẽ mɛ.».

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la ɛɛra sore wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, ba bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la de la bãma ĩyã tɩ a makɛ makrɛ la. La ba zotɩ la nẽrba la.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la pigra Azezi. Bɛla tɩ ba tõm nẽrba n ãn wʋ ba de la nẽr-sõma la tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka bẽrɛ Azezi la yetɔga, tɩ a sãn tɔgɛ tuuge, tɩ ba yẽ sore yõkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ gɔmna n soe nõorɛ nu'usum.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la sẽŋɛ ka soke Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma mi tɩ fõn tɔgrɩ se'em na, la fõn zãsnɩ se'em na masɛ mɛ, la fõn ka tũ'usri nẽrba, dee zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sɩra sɩra.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Bɛla, lɔɔ la bɔ sore tɩ tɩ yɔ lampo bɔ Rom na-keko bɩɩ, a ka bɔ sore?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Tɩ a bãŋɛ ba yɛmkẽgsɩga la, yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: «Tarɩ-ya lagfɔ ayɩla wa'am.» Tɩ ba tarɩ wa'am. Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne yinyisŋa, la ãne yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a zuo?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «La de la Rom na-keko la.».

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bɔ-ya na-keko la ẽŋa n soe bõn-sɛka, dee mẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋa n soe bõn-sɛka.».

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Azezi n daan tɔgɛ se'em nẽrba la nẽŋam na, ba ka tã'agɛ yõkɛ ẽ, a lergre la di'ige ba mɛ, tɩ ba sĩna.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Saduse dõma n daan yetɩ nẽra sãn ki a kãn vo'oge la, bãma nẽrba basɛba n wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Sɛka sɔbia sãn tara pɔga, ki dee tɩ a ka tara bia, a di a pɔkõorɛ la dɔgɛ kɔma bɔ sɛka n ki la.›.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: La bʋraasɩ bayopɔɩ n daan boe dɛna sɔbiisi, tɩ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la di pɔga tɩ a ka dɔgɛ, dee tɩ a ki.

eng: The second.
local: tɩ bʋyi dãana la mẽ di pɔkõorɛ la ki, dee tɩ a ka dɔgɛ.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: Tɩ bʋtã dãana la mẽ ẽŋɛ bɛla. La bãma bayopɔɩ la wuu lagɩm di pɔkõorɛ la mɛ ki, tɩ ba nẽra nẽra ka dɔgɛ bia.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ pɔka la mẽ ki.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Bɛla, bãma bayopɔɩ la wuu n di pɔka la, ba sãn wa vo'oge kũm vo'ore daarɛ, ãne n wʋn sɔna pɔka la?».

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Dũni-ẽna nẽrba diti pɔgsɩ mɛ dee ɛlla sɩrba.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: la nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ tɩ ba masɛ tɩ ba vo'oge kẽ dũnia sɛka n sẽm pʋam na ka diti pɔgba, la ba ka ellɩ sɩrba.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: La ba ka tã wʋn le ki, se'ere n soe la, ba ãn wʋ malɛkadõma la. Ba de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma, ban vo'oge dee basɛ kũm na ĩyã.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: La Amoyisi yʋʋn põn pa'alɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge mɛ, zẽ'e-sɛka n tɔgrɩ tũntuugo n diti bugum yelle la, Amoyisi yetɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Bɛla pa'alɛ tɩ bãma vɔa mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ dagɩ sɛba n ki Nawẽnnɛ, a de la nẽr-sɛba n vɔa Nawẽnnɛ. Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am nẽrba la wuu vɔa mɛ, baa la sɛba n ki la.».

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fʋ tɔgɛ sõŋa.».

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: La ba daan ka le saagɛ soke ẽ sɛla sɛla.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Wãne tɩ ba yetɩ Krisi de la Adavidi yɩɩŋa?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Dee tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ yele yõoma gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele mam Zuudãana yetɩ, zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ bɔba.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: halɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ fʋ bɛ'ɛba la tɩ ba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.›.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: La Adavidi mẽŋa sãn wi ẽ yetɩ ‹Zuudãana›, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ a dɛna Adavidi yɩɩŋa?».

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: La nẽrba la wuu n selsrɩ Azezi la, tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ:.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: «Guuse ya mẽŋa la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n eerɩ gilgra la fu-wogro, bɔɔra tɩ nẽrba pʋ'ʋsra nã'asra ba da'asʋm, dee bɔɔra nã'asgɔ zẽ'esɩ wẽndeto pʋam, la dia zẽ'esʋm tɩ ba zẽa la.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba to'osrɩ la sɛla wuu tɩ pɔkõpa tarɩ, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋs-wogro tɩ nẽrba bɩsra ba. Ba wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ gãnna wuu.».

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Azezi daan boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, bɩsɛ yẽ bõntatba n tarɩ ba bɔ'a ɩta ligri daka pʋam.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: dee yẽ pɔkõorɛ n de nasa n ẽŋɛ lig-kõb-pɩgsɩ bayi mã'a.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ pɔkõorɛ ẽna n de nasa la ẽŋɛ zo'oge gãnna ba wuu mɛ.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Bãma wuu looge la bõntarsõm sɛba tɩ ba tarɩ zo'oge la pʋam wa'am wa ẽŋɛ mĩ. La ẽŋa n de nasa la, dɩkɛ la a dia ligri wuu wa ẽŋɛ mĩ.».

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: La nẽrba basɛba n daan tɔgɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la yelle yetɩ, ba dɩkɛ la kug-sõma mẽ ẽ, dee dɩkɛ bɔ'a tɩ ba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ malgɛ ẽ, tɩ a ãna sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ ba wʋn lʋbɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ ya yẽtɩ wã wuu saam saam, tɩ kugre baa ayɩla kãn weege a tadãana zuo.».

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, bõn-bãna wʋn ẽŋɛ la dabʋrɛ? La bẽm wʋn pa'alɛ tõma tɩ tɩ bãŋɛ tɩ bõn-bãna wʋn ẽŋɛ?».

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Guuse-ya tɩ nẽra da pã'asɛ ya tɩ ya tuuge. Nẽrba zozo'e n wʋn dɩkɛ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ wa'am wa pã'asra ya, tɩ ba nẽra woo yetɩ: ‹Mam n de Krisi›, bɩɩ ba wʋn yetɩ: ‹Wakatɛ lɛm mɛ.› Da doose-ya nẽr-bãma.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: La ya sãn wõm zɛbrɛ, la tẽn-dãmŋɔ yɛla, da basɛ-ya tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ya, dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-bãma dẽŋɛ ẽŋɛ, la, la nãn ka de dũnia ba'asgɔ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Buuri ayɩla wʋn isge zɛbra la buuri ayẽma, tɩ so'olʋm ayɩla dõma isge zɛbra la so'olʋm ayẽma.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: la tẽŋa wʋn tĩi mĩim mɛ, la kɔm, la bã'a-loŋrɩsɩ n wʋn wa'am zẽ'esɩ zozo'e, la yel-kãra n kẽ'esrɩ nẽrba dabeem, la bõn-yãlma n wʋn ẽŋɛ saazuum.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: La, lan dẽŋɛ bõn-bãna wã n wʋn ẽŋɛ, ba wʋn yõgɛ ya mɛ, nãmsɛ ya, tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka sãm ba wẽndetʋm sarɩyaditba zẽ'am, tɩ ba yʋ ya, la ba wʋn botɩ ya ze'ele na'adõma, la gɔmnadõma nẽŋam mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: La wʋn doose la bɛla tɩ ya yẽ folle tɔgɛ mam yelle pa'alɛ ba.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Bɛla, ẽŋɛ ya sũurum tɩ la dagɩ ya fabla yan wʋn lerge se'em po'oge ya mẽŋa.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Mam mẽŋa wʋn bɔ ya yɛm tɩ ya tɔgɛ tɩ ya bɛ'ɛba kãn yãŋɛ lerge sɩ'ɩsɛ ya yetɔga.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Baa la ya sɔdõma, la ya madõma, la ya sɔbiisi, la ya sɩrdõma wʋn yõgɛ ya sẽŋɛ ka bɔ, tɩ ba kʋ ya nẽrba basɛba.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Nẽrba wuu wʋn sise ya mam ĩyã.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: La ya zomto baa ayɩla kãn bɔɩ.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Ya sãn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, ya wʋn yẽ vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Ya sãn wa yẽ tɩ sʋdaasɩ gilgri Zerizalɛm, ya bãŋɛ tɩ a sã'aŋɔ lɛm mɛ.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛba n boe Zude tẽŋa zoe zom tãnsɩ zuto, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n boe Zerizalɛm pʋam na yese zoe, la sɛba n boe vatʋm na da kẽ tẽŋa pʋam.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Dabsa bãma de la tõon-be'ero sɩbgrɛ dabsa tɩ bõn-sɛba wuu n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na wʋn ẽŋɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa, la wʋn dɛna la toogo bɔ pɔg-sɛba n tarɩ pʋa, la sɛba n tarɩ bilɛɛsɩ tɩ ba mõgra la. Se'ere n soe la, nãmsgɔ kãtɛ n wʋn wa'am tẽŋa wã pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ sũure wʋn yĩige mɛ la tẽŋa wã nẽrba.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Ba wʋn kʋ basɛba mɛ la sʋkɔbrɔ, la ba wʋn yõgɛ basɛba mɛ tarɩ sẽŋɛ bu-zãŋsɩ tẽnsʋm ka ẽŋɛ yamsɩ. Bu-zãŋsɩ wʋn sã'am Zerizalɛm mɛ, halɩ tɩ ba wakatɛ wʋn wa ba'asɛ.».

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: «Bõn-yãlma n wʋn ẽŋɛ wẽntulle, la wõrga, la wõrbiɛ pʋam. La dũnia nẽrba buuri wuu wʋn fabla mɛ zɛ yɛrɩ, ko-kãtɛ ko-mĩ'isi n wʋn tĩi wẽ'era ɩta voole la ĩyã.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: La nẽrba wʋn tẽ'esra la bõn-sɛka n wʋn ẽŋɛ dũnia wuu zuo la, tɩ dabeem wʋn botɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ tɩ ba yɛm bɔɩ, se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum wʋn mĩim mɛ.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa ba wʋn yẽ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la boe wara pʋam sẽna la pãn-kãtɛ, tɩ a na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: La ya sãn yẽ tɩ bõn-bãna pɔsɛ ɩta, gorge-ya bɩsɛ saazuum, tɩ ya fãarɛ lɛm mɛ.».

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Azezi makɛ la makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya kẽnkãŋa, la tɩɩsɩ wuu.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Ya sãn yẽ tɩ ba võorɔ pusra, ya mẽŋa mi tɩ sigre lɛm mɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Bɛla mẽ, ya sãn yẽ tɩ bõn-bãna ɩta, ya bãŋɛ tɩ wakatɛ lɛm mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ bõn-bãna wʋn ɩta mɛ dee tɩ zamãan-ẽna yãŋa tole.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazuum la tẽŋa wʋn tole mɛ, la mam yetɔga la kãn malɩn tole.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Guuse ya mẽŋa tɩ ya yɛm da yɔɔra la di tɩgɛ, la dã-yũure, la dũni-ẽna vɔm yɛla, tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo daarɛ da wa di'ige ya.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: wʋ bẽrgɔ la. Se'ere n soe la, daarɛ ẽŋa wʋn wa di'ige nẽrba wuu n boe tẽn-gõŋɔ wã zuo mɛ.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Bɛla, da gĩsra-ya, pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ wakatɛ wuu, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ pãŋa tã'agɛ põsɛ bõn-sɛba wuu n wʋn wa ẽŋɛ la, dee tã'agɛ ze'ele Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la nẽŋam.».

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Azezi daan ẽn zãsna la nẽrba Wẽnde-kãtʋm na wʋntɛɛŋa, tɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ a ẽn yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ ka gã'arɛ Oliivi tãŋa la zuo.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: La nẽrba wuu lagsrɩ la a zẽ'am Wẽnde-kãtʋm na bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ sɛlsra a yetɔga.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Zɔɔg-basɛ la daan dee la fẽe la a paagɛ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la ɛɛra bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em kʋ Azezi, dee zɔta nẽrba la dabeem.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Tɩ Asũtãana kẽ Azudaasɩ tɩ ba wi'iri Iskariyo n de Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla sũurum.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la gu'urba nẽŋadõma zẽ'am, ka yele ba ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em botɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Tɩ ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm, tɩ ba yetɩ bãma wʋn bɔ ẽ ligri.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: tɩ Azudaasɩ sakɛ, ɛɛra sore tɩ a botɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la da bãŋɛ.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la daarɛ tɩ ba kʋʋrɩ pebilsi dita Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la n daan paagɛ.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Tɩ Azezi tõm Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka mãasʋm kibsa la dia tɩ tɩ wa'am wa di.».

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛ tɩ tɩ wʋn sẽŋɛ ka mãasʋm?».

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Selse-ya, ya sãn ka kẽ'era tẽŋa la pʋam, ya wʋn tʋ'ʋsɛ bʋraaga n tʋ yoore la ko'om, ya doose ẽ kẽ yi-sɛka tɩ a wʋn kẽ la pʋam.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: La ya sãn kẽ, ya yele yidãana la yetɩ: ‹Karẽnsãama la yetɩ, de-sɛka tɩ ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la wʋn di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la boe la bɛ?›.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: A wʋn pa'alɛ ya de-kãtɛ n boe ayẽma zuo, tɩ lɔgrɔ wuu bɔna mĩ. Mãasʋm-ya bilam gu'ura to.».

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yẽ sɛla woo wʋ Azezi n yele ba se'em na, tɩ ba mãasʋm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Tɩ wakatɛ la paagɛ, tɩ Azezi zĩ'ire la a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la tɩ ba di.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Mam ba bɔɔra mɛ la m sũure wuu tɩ mam na yãma di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibs-ẽna wã dee tɩ m yãŋa nãmsɛ.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, mam kãn le di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa halɩ tɩ a võorɛ wa ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Dee dɩkɛ bõn-yũula pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee yetɩ: «To'oge-ya bãna wã pʋɩ taaba yũ.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ lɛɛlɛ wã kɔ'ɔn wɛ'ɛsa wuu, mam kãn le yũ viinye biɛ bõn-yũula wã halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wa'am wa dita na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.».

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Dee dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam ĩyã tɩ mam dɩkɛ bɔ yãma ĩyã. Ɩta-ya wãna tẽra mam yelle.».

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: La ban di kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a dɩkɛ la bõn-yũula yetɩ: «Bõn-yũul-bãna pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la nõor-paalga, mam zɩɩm n wʋn yese saagɛ bɔ yãma la ĩyã.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: La bɩsɛ-ya, sɛka n yetɩ a botɩ ba yõkɛ mam na lagɩm na mam kalam dita.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn ki wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ la ẽŋɛ se'em na. La nẽr-sɛka n wʋn botɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na wʋn yẽ toogo.».

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la sɔkra taaba tɩ ba bãŋɛ nẽr-sɛka n de ẽ bãma pʋam n yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Karẽnbiisi la daan wẽ'erɩ la nõke'ene bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ bãma pʋam nẽr-sɛka n masɛ tɩ a dɛna nẽr-keko gãnna.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Dũni-ẽna na'adõma soe nẽrba la pãŋa, la sɛba n tarɩ ke'eŋo la botɩ nẽrba wi'ira ba tɩ: ‹Sõm-ɩtba›.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: La yãma pʋam, la kãn dɛna bɛla. Sɛka n de nẽr-keko yãma pʋam, a ãna wʋ bõn-pɩka la, la sɛka n de nẽŋadãana ãna wʋ tõntõnna.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: La ãne n de nẽr-keko gãnna, sɛka n zĩ dita la bɩɩ, sɛka n tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra ẽ na? La dagɩ sɛka n zĩ dita la? La mam boe ya pʋam wʋ tõntõnna la.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Yãma de la nẽrba n mõrgɛ weege bɔna la mam, yel-sɛba n paagɛ mam na pʋam.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Bɛla mam wʋn bɔ ya so'olʋm, wʋ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ mam so'olʋm se'em na.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: tɩ ya wʋn wa zĩ'ire mam so'olʋm pʋam dita dee yũura la mam, dee zĩ'ire na'am bĩmbĩna zuto di Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma buuri pia la ayi la sarɩya.».

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: La Azezi yele Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asimõ, Asimõ, bãŋɛ tɩ Asũtãana sose la sore tɩ a makɛ ya bɩsɛ, wʋ nẽra n yeele si yɛsra urgo la.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: La mam pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ bɔ fɔ, tɩ fõn bɔ mam sɩra la, fʋ da basɛ. La fʋ sãn wa leme wa dɔla mam, fʋ bɔ fʋ sɔbiisi la bʋraanɛ tɩ ba ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ.».

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam sakɛ mɛ tɩ m kẽ yʋ'a deem la fõn, bɩɩ m ki la fõn.».

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Apɩyɛɛrɩ, mam yetɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ẽna wã, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ la nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam, dee tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kaasɛ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: La Azezi soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Mam n daan tõm yãma tɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, tɩ yãma ka dɩkɛ pʋtmãanɛ, la tãmpɔkɔ, la tagra la, sɛla daan pɔ'ɔgɛ ya mɛ bɩɩ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ.».

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Lɛɛlɛ wã, la ka le dɛna bɛla. Sɛka n tarɩ pʋtmãanɛ, a dɩkɛ, la sɛka n mẽ tarɩ tãmpɔkɔ, a dɩkɛ. La sɛka n ka tarɩ sʋkɔbgɔ, a koose a fuugo da sʋkɔbgɔ.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Ba dɩkɛ ẽ naagɛ la tõon-be'ero dõma›. Mam yetɩ ya tɩ la de la mam tɩ bõn-ẽŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ. Asɩra, bõn-sɛka n gʋlsɛ tɔgra mam yelle la, yãŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ mɛ.».

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Zuudãana, bɩsɛ sʋkɔbrɔ bayi n wãna.» Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ la seke ya.».

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Tɩ Azezi yese sẽŋɛ Oliivi tãŋam wʋ ẽŋa ẽn ɩta se'em na, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la doose ẽ.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: La ẽn paagɛ mĩ na, a yele ba yetɩ: «Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ yelle da paagɛ ya tɩ ya lui.».

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Dee basɛ ba sẽŋɛ zãagɛ fẽe masɛ wʋ nẽra n wʋn lobe kugre se'em, ka ka dũma tẽŋa pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: «M Sɔ, fʋ sãn bɔɔra, basɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ ẽna tole dee basɛ ma. La, da kãn ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em, ẽŋɛ fõn boorɩ se'em.» .

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Tɩ malɛka ze'ele saazuum wa'am wa pa'asɛ ẽ pãŋa.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Tɩ Azezi sũure ba sã'am halɩ, tɩ a malɩn tĩi pʋ'ʋsra tɩ a mõrgrɛ tɔgra tẽŋa wʋ zɩɩm na.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Ẽn daan pʋ'ʋsɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a lebe ka paagɛ a karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am, tɩ ba gĩsri mɛ, ba sũure n sã'am ĩyã.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma gĩsra? Isge-ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ yelle da paagɛ ya tɩ ya lui.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Azezi n daan ken tɔgra la, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e sẽnna paana. Tɩ Azudaasɩ n de a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla bɔna ba nẽŋa, sẽŋɛ lɛm Azezi tɩ a kɔkɛ ẽ, pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azudaasɩ, fõn yetɩ fʋ kɔkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mɛ botɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ?».

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la n ze a zẽ'a, yẽ lan yetɩ la ẽŋɛ se'em na yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tɩ dɩkɛ tɩ sʋkɔbrɔ la zɛbɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla dɩkɛ a sʋkɔbgɔ sɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna zʋʋgɔ tʋbrɛ fiige.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya bɛla.» Dee kalʋm bʋraaga la tʋbrɛ la, tɩ a lebe tablɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Dee yele kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la gu'urba nẽŋadõma, la kẽma n yetɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ la sʋkɔbrɔ, la dasa sẽna tɩ ya yõkɛ mam wʋ mam de la fãara la?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Mam daan boe la ya lɛ mɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na daarɛ woo, tɩ ya ka yõkɛ mam. La wakat-ẽna wã ya tã wʋn ẽŋɛ yan boorɩ se'em, se'ere n soe la, la de la lika pãŋa dãana wakatɛ.».

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: La ba yõkɛ Azezi mɛ tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yire, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ bɔna zãazãarɛ li'isra dɔla.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: La ba tʋʋrɛ la bugum yire la zẽnzaka tẽŋasʋka lagsɛ zẽa, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ mẽ lagɩm na ba zẽa.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Tɩ tõntõn-pɔka ayɩla yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ n zĩ bugum na zẽ'am, bɩsra ẽ zĩi dee yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna mẽ dolɩ la Azezi.».

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sɩ'ɩsɛ yetɩ: «Pɔka, mam ka mi ẽ.».

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Tɩ la le ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ nẽra ayẽma le yẽ ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽ de la ba nẽra ayɩla.» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Bʋraaga, mam dagɩ ba nẽra.».

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Tɩ la le ẽŋɛ fẽe masɛ wʋ karf-ayɩla, tɩ nẽra ayɩla le yetɩ: «Asɩra, bʋra-ẽna ẽn dɔla ẽ mɛ, a mẽ de la Galile nẽra.».

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Bʋraaga, mam ka bãŋɛ fõn tɔgɛ se'em na võorɛ.» La ẽn ken bɔna mĩ tɔgra la, tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kɔ'ɔn kaasɛ.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana la gelge bɩsɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ tẽegɛ Zuudãana n yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ mam nõorɛ atã mɛ zĩna, dee tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kaasɛ la.».

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yese yẽŋa ka kɛlla la sũ-sã'aŋɔ zozo'e.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Bʋra-sɛba n gu'uri Azezi la daan la ẽ mɛ, dee wẽ ẽ.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: dɩkɛ fuugo vile a nẽŋa wẽ ẽ, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bãŋɛ pa'alɛ tɔ sɛka n wẽ fɔ.».

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: La ba tʋ ẽ na tʋʋrɛ basɛba zozo'e pa'asɛ.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: La beere n daan wiige, tɩ Zifdõma kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma wuu lagsɛ tɩ ba tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am, dee yele ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a wʋn wa'am na, fʋ yele to.» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam sãn yele ya, ya kãn sakɛ.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: la mam sãn soke ya sokre, ya mẽ kãn lerge mam.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã kɔ'ɔn wɛ'ɛsa wuu, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ n de pãn-kãtɛ dãana la zʋʋgɔ.».

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Tɩ ba wuu soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛla fõn sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma n yele se'em na, mam de la bɛla.».

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Tɩ le ɛɛra la bẽm kaset-dõma? Tõma mẽŋa wõm a nõorɛ yetɔga mɛ.».

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Tɩ ba wuu tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: ka dõrɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma yẽ bʋra-ẽna tɩ a boe mĩ dãmna la tɩ tẽŋa wã dee yetɩ ba da yɔɔra lampo bɔ'ɔra Rom na-keko, dee ken yetɩ ẽŋa de la Krisi, ẽŋa de la naba.».

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la Zifdõma naba?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La de la fõn yele se'em na.».

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ yele kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la, la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Mam ka yẽ bʋraaga wã yel-sɛka n wʋn botɩ tɩ di a sarɩya.».

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Tɩ ba sĩna dee bʋla yetɩ: «A zãsŋɔ la tɩ a pɔsɛ zãsʋm Galile la Zude wa paam kalam na, tarɩ la dãmŋɔ sẽna nẽrba pʋam.».

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Apilatɩ n wõm bɛla la, a soke mɛ tɩ a bãŋɛ, bʋra-ẽna sãn dɛna Galile nẽra.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: La ẽn bãŋɛ tɩ a ze'ele la Galile n de naba A'erɔdɩ so'olʋm pʋam na, tɩ a botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka bɔ naba A'erɔdɩ. A'erɔdɩ mẽ daan boe la Zerizalɛm wakat-ẽŋa.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: La A'erɔdɩ n yẽ Azezi la, a sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ halɩ, se'ere n soe la, a wõm Azezi yelle tɩ la yuuge mɛ, tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a yẽ ẽ. A tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a wʋn tõm na tõon-yãlma tɩ a yẽ.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: A'erɔdɩ daan soke Azezi la sokrɩsɩ zozo'e, tɩ a ka lerge ẽ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n boe mĩ na dõrɛ ẽ zozo'e yetɩ, a tõm na be'em.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Tɩ A'erɔdɩ la a sʋdaasɩ yaalɛ ẽ, dɩkɛ fu-sõŋɔ yɛ ẽ, tɩ ba la ẽ, dee tarɩ ẽ lebe ka bɔ Apilatɩ.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Daar-ẽŋa, A'erɔdɩ la Apilatɩ n de bɛ'ɛba la taaba la, lebge la sɩtɔ.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ wi kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma, la nẽrba wuu.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ bʋra-ẽna wa'am wa bɔ mam yetɩ a tarɩ la dãmŋɔ sẽna nẽrba pʋam. La mam n soke ẽ yãma nẽŋam kalam wã, mam ka yẽ a yel-sɛka n botɩ yãma dõrɛ ẽ na.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: La A'erɔdɩ mẽ ka yẽ a yelle, bɛla tɩ a tarɩ ẽ leme wa'am wa bɔ tõma. Bʋraaga wã ka tõm be'em n masɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Mam wʋn botɩ ba wẽ ẽ mɛ la ka'asa dee basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ.» .

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Tɩ ba wuu kɔ'ɔn lagɩm tãsra yetɩ: «Kʋ bʋra-ẽna, dee yese Abarabasɩ bɔ tɔ.».

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Abarabasɩ daan dãmɛ na tẽŋa, kʋ nẽra, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ yʋ.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ le tɔgra la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la bɔɔra tɩ a basɛ Azezi.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Tɩ ba zagsɛ, dee kaasra yetɩ: «Ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.».

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ le yele ba nõor-atã pʋam yetɩ: «Bẽm be'em tɩ a tõm? Mam ka yẽ yel-sɛka tɩ a tõm tɩ la masɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ. Mam wʋn botɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa dee basɛ ẽ.».

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Tɩ ba ken kaasra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo.» Tɩ ba kaasga la zo'oge gãŋɛ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ sakɛ ẽŋɛ ban boorɩ se'em na.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: yese bʋra-sɛka n daan dãmɛ tẽŋa kʋ nẽra la basɛ, ẽŋa tɩ nẽrba la boorɩ tɩ a basɛ, dee dɩkɛ Azezi bɔ sʋdaasɩ tɩ ba ẽŋɛ ẽ nẽrba la n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Ban tarɩ Azezi wɛ'ɛsa la, ba tʋ'ʋsɛ la Sirɛnɩ tẽŋa bʋraaga yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asimõ n ze'ele va'am lɛmna, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ botɩ a to'oge dɔ-puurŋa la buke dɔla Azezi poore.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n dolɩ ẽ, tɩ pɔgsɩ kɛlla fabla wẽ'era nupʋ'ʋrɔ a ĩyã.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Tɩ Azezi wẽrgɛ ba bɔba yele ba yetɩ: «Zerizalɛm pɔgsɩ, da kɛlla-ya mam ĩyã, kɛlla-ya ya mẽŋa la ya kɔma ĩyã.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Se'ere n soe la, dabsa sẽna tɩ ba wʋn yetɩ: ‹Pɔg-kõndɔgrɩba, la sɛba n ka dɔgɛ, sɛba tɩ kɔma ka mõgɛ ba bĩ'isum tarɩ zu-yẽlga.›.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: La nẽrba wʋn wa yele tãnsɩ tɩ ba lui dĩ ba, dee yele kũnkũma yetɩ, ba lile ba.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba sãn ɩta dɔ-mã'asa wãna, la wʋn ẽŋɛ dɔ-kɛ'ɛsɩ la wãne?».

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: La ba tarɩ la tõon-be'ero dõma bayi sẽŋɛ tɩ ba lagɩm ba la Azezi kʋ.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: La ba paagɛ la zẽ'a tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Zuwaŋrɛ, bilam tɩ ba ka Azezi dɔ-puurŋa zuo, dee ka tõon-be'ero dõma bayi la dɔ-puursi zuto, ayɩla a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, ayɩla a gɔbga bɔba.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «M Sɔ, basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ, tɩ ba ka mi ban ɩtɩ sɛla.» Tɩ ba dɩkɛ a futo tɩ ba pʋɩ taaba, pɔsɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ nẽra woo n wʋn to'oge fu-sɛka.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: La nẽrba la ze la bilam bɩsra ẽ, tɩ Zifdõma kẽma la la'ara ẽ yetɩ: «A fãagɛ nẽrba basɛba mɛ, a sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la, a fãagɛ a mẽŋa.».

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la mẽ la'ara ẽ, lɛm dɩkɛ dã-mĩ'isgo bɔ ẽ.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn dɛna Zifdõma naba, fãagɛ fʋ mẽŋa.».

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: La ba gʋlsɛ la gʋlsgɔ lablɛ a zuum yetɩ: «Ẽna wã de la Zifdõma naba la.».

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Tɩ tõon-be'ero dõma la n boe dɔ-puursi zuto la ayɩla tʋ Azezi yetɩ: «Dagɩ fõn de Krisi la? Fãagɛ fʋ mẽŋa dee fãagɛ tõma.».

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Tɩ ayẽma la tãsɛ a tadãana la yetɩ: «Fõn ka zotɩ Nawẽnnɛ? Ba wʋn kʋ tõma wuu mɛ.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma n tõm tõon-be'ero la, la masɛ mɛ tɩ ba kʋ tõma, la nẽr-ẽna wã ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n de be'em.».

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Dee yele Azezi yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn wa dita fʋ na'am na, fʋ tẽegɛ m yelle.».

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra, tɩ zĩna nõo fʋ wʋn bɔna la mam Nawẽnnɛ yire.».

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: La daan de wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka, tɩ tẽŋa la wuu lebge lika, tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa ka le yẽkɛ halɩ ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ karf-atã. Tɩ fuugo la n yuli Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam beŋe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la ãagɛ pʋsʋka.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Tɩ Azezi tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «M Sɔ, mam basrɩ m sɩa la fõn nu'usum.» A yele la bɛla dee ki.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Tɩ Rom sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛma yẽ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na, nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Asɩra, bʋraaga wã de la nẽr-sõŋɔ.».

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu n wa'am tɩ ba bɩsɛ lan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em na, yẽ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na mɛ. Tɩ ba lebe kulla, dee wẽ'era nupʋ'ʋrɔ.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: La nẽr-sɛba wuu n mi Azezi, la pɔg-sɛba n doose ẽ halɩ Galile wa'am na, ze la kẽnkɛrŋa bɩsra lan ɩtɩ se'em na.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Bʋraaga ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n daan de Azozɛfʋ. A de la Arimate tẽŋa nẽra, dee dɛna Zifdõma sarɩyaditba nẽra ayɩla. A de la nẽr-sõŋɔ tɩ a ɩrgɔ wuu masɛ.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: la a daan gu'uri mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wa'am wa di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka. La ba sarɩyaditba la n daan looge yɛm tɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na, a daan ka sakɛ.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Azezi n ki la, ẽŋa n sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am ka sose Azezi kũm na, tɩ Apilatɩ sakɛ.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Tɩ a sike kũm na vile ẽ na tãnnɛ tarɩ ka bĩŋe yɔɔgɔ tɩ ba tũ tãmpĩa ĩyã nãn ka laagɛ nẽra mĩ pʋam.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: La de la daar-ẽŋa tɩ Zifdõma mãasnɩ ba vo'osgo daarɛ. Wẽnnɛ sãn kẽ tɩ ba vo'osgo daarɛ wʋn pɔsɛ.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Tɩ pɔg-sɛba n doose Azezi Galile wa'am na, doose Azozɛfʋ sẽŋɛ ka yẽ yɔɔgɔ la, dee yẽ ban bĩŋe Azezi kũm na se'em.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: dee lebe kule ka mãasʋm tɩdarɛ kaam buuri to'oto'ore tɩ ba tarɩ wa ẽŋɛ kũm na. La vo'osgo daarɛ la ba vo'ose wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ la n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Lasrɩ daarɛ bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ pɔgsɩ la isge dɩkɛ kaam na tɩ ba mãasʋm na tarɩ sẽŋɛ yɔɔm na.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Ban paagɛ la, ba yẽ tɩ kugre la tɩ ba dɩkɛ lige yɔɔgɔ nõorɛ la bilge mɛ gã kẽnkɛrŋa.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Tɩ ba kẽ yɔɔgɔ la pʋam bɩsɛ ka yẽ Zuudãana Azezi kũm na.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Tɩ ba zɛ yɛrɩ, bilam mẽŋa tɩ ba yẽ bʋraasɩ bayi n ze lɛm ba, yɛ futo tɩ ba yẽgra.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Tɩ pɔgsɩ la palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba suure ba zuto tẽŋa. Tɩ bʋraasɩ la soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma ɛɛra nẽr-sɛka n vɔɩ sɛba n ki zẽ'am?.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: A ka boe kalam. A vo'oge mɛ. Tẽegɛ-ya ẽŋa n daan nãn boe Galile dee yele ya se'em na.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: a yetɩ, ba wʋn yõkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mɛ tarɩ ka bɔ tõon-be'ero dõma, tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, la dabsa atã daarɛ, a wʋn vo'oge mɛ.».

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Tɩ ba yãŋa tẽegɛ a yetɔga la.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: ze'ele yɔɔm na lebe kule ka yele a karẽnbiisi pia la ayɩla la, la nẽrba basɛba la wuu lan ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Ba daan de la Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Azanɩ, la Azakɩ ma Amaari, la pɔgsɩ basɛba, bãma n daan tɔgɛ yel-bãna bɔ Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayɩla la.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: La ba daan tẽ'esɛ tɩ pɔgsɩ la n yele ba se'em na ka tarɩ võorɛ, tɩ ba ka sakɛ tɩ la de sɩra.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ isge mɛ zoe sẽŋɛ yɔɔm na ka veese bɩsɛ, yẽ tãna la mã'a, lebe kule ka zɛ yɛrɩ bõn-sɛba n ẽŋɛ la ĩyã.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Daar-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi bayi wɛ'ɛsa tẽŋa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Emayusi. Bilam na Zerizalɛm zãagɛ masɛ wʋ kilomẽtrɩ pia la ayɩla.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ba daan tɔgrɩ la taaba bõn-sɛba wuu n ẽŋɛ la yelle.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: La ban daan sõsrɩ la taaba la, tɩ Azezi lɛm ba, tɩ ba lagɩm sẽnna.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: La ba yɛm lige mɛ, tɩ ba ka bãŋɛ ẽ.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm yelle tɩ yãma sẽnna dee tɔgra la taaba?» Tɩ ba ze'ele zũŋe ba nẽnsɩ.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akleopasɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mã'a n boe Zerizalɛm ka bãŋɛ bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ dabsa wã pʋam?».

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm ẽŋɛ?» Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ la, a de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa n tarɩ pãŋa a tõoma la a yetɔga pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ la nẽrba wuu nifum.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Tɩ tõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma botɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, di a sarɩya yetɩ ba kʋ ẽ, tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, tɩ a ki.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: La tõma daan tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ la de la ẽŋa n wʋn fãagɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la. La zĩna de la dabsa atã n wãna tɩ yel-ẽna wã ẽŋɛ.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: La tõma pɔgsɩ basɛba n botɩ tõma palẽŋa ẽkɛ. Ba isge la bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ sẽŋɛ yɔɔm na.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: leme wa yele tõma yetɩ bãma ka yẽ kũm na, bãma yẽ la malɛkadõma, tɩ ba yele bãma yetɩ a vo'oge mɛ.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Tɩ tõma nẽrba basɛba mẽ sẽŋɛ yɔɔm na, ka yẽ tɩ la sɩrɩ ãn wʋ pɔgsɩ la n yele se'em na. La ba ka yẽ Azezi.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yɛm-ka-like dõma, bẽm tɩ ya ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yʋʋn yele se'em na wuu sɩra tɔtɔ?.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: La ka masɛ nɩ tɩ Krisi nãmsɛ la bõn-bãna wuu, dee yãŋa di a na'am na?».

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Tɩ a yãŋa pa'alɛ ba bõn-sɛba wuu n gʋlsɛ tɔgra ẽŋa yelle Nawẽnnɛ gõnnɔ la pʋam, a pɔsɛ pa'alɛ la Amoyisi, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la wuu n gʋlsɛ la võorɛ n de se'em.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: La ban daan sẽŋɛ ka lɛm tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba we la, a ẽŋɛ wʋ a boorɩ tɩ a tole mɛ na.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Tɩ ba sose ẽ yetɩ: «Doose tõma kẽ gã'arɛ, se'ere n soe la, wẽnnɛ kẽ mɛ, zẽ'a boe mĩ sɔbra mɛ.» Tɩ a sakɛ doose ba kẽ.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: La ba daan zĩ'ire mɛ tɩ ba di, tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ ba.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Tɩ ba yɛm yãŋa like, tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽŋa n de nẽr-sɛka. Tɩ a bɔɩ ba nẽŋam bilam mẽŋa.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Tɩ ba yɛta taaba yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n tɔgɛ yetɔga la pa'alɛ tõma, dee tɔgra yel-sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam pa'ala tõma sore zuo la, tõma sũure ka ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm zo'oge sɩra?».

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Tɩ ba isge wakat-ẽŋa lebe Zerizalɛm ka yẽ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayɩla la, la sɛba n boe ba zẽ'am na tɩ ba lagsɛ taaba mɛ.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: Tɩ ba yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ Zuudãana la sɩrɩ vo'oge mɛ, Asimõ yẽ ẽ mɛ.».

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Tɩ nẽrba bayi la mẽ yele ba bõn-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sore la zuo, la ẽŋa n fiisri borborɩ la, bãma n bãŋɛ ẽ se'em.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Ban boe mĩ tɔgra bɛla la, tɩ Azezi darɛ wa'am wa zɛ ba tẽŋasʋka, yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna la ya.».

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma yẽ la ki-isgne.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ya tẽ'esra pʋtẽ'era bʋyi bʋyi?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya mam nu'usi, la mam nãma, la de la mam. Sa'abɛ-ya mam bɩsɛ, ki-isgne ka tarɩ nẽnnɔ, la kõba, wʋ yãma n yẽ mam n tarɩ se'em wã.».

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: A tɔgɛ bɛla dee pa'alɛ ba la a nu'usi, la a nãma.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Ba sũure n ãn yẽlʋm dee tɩ la di'ige ba, tɩ ba ken ka bɔ sɩra, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ sɛla wʋn di kalam mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ zũn-sẽ'enɛ bɔ ẽ.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: Tɩ a to'oge obe ba nifum bilam.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, a yele ba yetɩ: «Mam n daan ken bɔna la yãma la, mam n yele ya bõn-sɛba la n wãna. Mam daan yele ya yetɩ: Dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-sɛba wuu n gʋlsɛ mam yelle Amoyisi lɔɔ gõnnɔ pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam, la yõoma gõnnɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ.».

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Tɩ Azezi like ba yɛm tɩ ba bãŋɛ bõn-sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ gõnnɔ pʋam na võorɛ.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: yele ba yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ yetɩ, Krisi la wʋn nãmsɛ mɛ ki, la dabsa atã daarɛ a wʋn vo'oge mɛ.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Ẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã tɩ ba wʋn pɔsɛ Zerizalɛm mõolɛ bɔ buuri wuu yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Yãma n de bõn-bãma wuu kaset-dõma wʋn pa'alɛ nẽrba wuu.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: La mam wʋn tõm sɛka tɩ mam Sɔ daan bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ ya la, tɩ a wa'am wa bɔna la ya. Bɔna-ya Zerizalɛm gu'ura halɩ tɩ a ze'ele saazuum wa'am wa bɔ ya pãŋa.».

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi tarɩ ba mɛ tẽŋa la pʋam yese sẽŋɛ ka lɛm Betani, zẽkɛ a nu'usi saazuum kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ba.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Ẽŋa n boe mĩ kã'ara kã'a-sõma bɔ'ɔra ba la, a basɛ ba mɛ dee zom Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asɛ ẽ, dee lebe Zerizalɛm la sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Ba daan boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na daarɛ woo pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: La dabsa atã daarɛ, pɔgdire kibsa n daan boe Kana, Galile so'olʋm pʋam. Tɩ Azezi ma daan bɔna mĩ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Tɩ ba mẽ wi Azezi la a karẽnbiisi, tɩ ba wa'am kibsa la.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Tɩ dãam daan pɔ'ɔgɛ, tɩ Azezi ma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dãam na pɔ'ɔgɛ mɛ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M ma, dagɩ fõn wʋn yele mam, mam n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em. Mam wakatɛ nãn ka paagɛ.».

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Tɩ Azezi ma la yele tõntõnɩba la yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ se'em wuu, ya ẽŋɛ.».

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: La kuga dʋgrɔ bayoobɩ n daan boe mĩ tɩ Zifdõma daan tara pɛɛra ba mẽŋa wʋ ba sisgo n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na. Ko'om lɩtrɩ pinii bɩɩ kɔbga n daan pɩtɩ dʋkɔ ayɩla.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ãgɛ-ya ko'om pɩrɛ dʋgrɔ la.» Tɩ ba ãgɛ pɩrɛ ba.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Kãbgɛ-ya tarɩ ka bɔ sɛka n bɩsrɩ dia yelle la.» Tɩ ba kãbgɛ tarɩ ka bɔ ẽ.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: La sɛka n bɩsrɩ dia la yelle la n leme ko'om na, ba lebge la dãam, tɩ a ka mina ban ze'ele zẽ'a. La tõntõnɩba la n ãgɛ ko'om na mi mɛ. Tɩ sɛka n bɩsrɩ dia la yelle la wi pɔgdita la.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra woo ẽn dẽŋɛ bɔ la dã-sõma, tɩ ba sãn wa yũ tɩgɛ, tɩ ba yãŋa bɔ'ɔra dã-sɛba n ka ba ãna sõŋa la. La fõn dɩkɛ la dã-sõma ze'ele bɔ'ɔra poorʋm.».

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Ẽna n de Azezi pɔspɔsɩ tõon-yãlma tɩ a ẽŋɛ. A ẽŋɛ ẽ na Kana Galile so'olʋm pʋam, pa'alɛ a na'am pãŋa, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, ẽŋa la a ma, la a yɩbsɩ, la a karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ la Kapɛrnaʋm. Ba kẽ bilam na dabsa fẽe.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: La Zifdõma Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa daarɛ n daan lɛm, tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: ka kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam yẽ nẽrba n koosrɩ niigi, la piisi, la na'adawɛma, la lig-tɛɛrba n zĩ mĩ.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Tɩ a dɩkɛ mĩ'isi ẽŋɛ ka'asrɛ dige ba, la niigi, la piisi la wuu Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam tɩ ba yese, dee zã'agɛ lig-tɛɛrba la taablɩdõma lʋbɛ, tɩ ba ligri la ka'asɛ.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Dee yele sɛba n koosrɩ na'adawɛma la yetɩ: «Tarɩ-ya ba yese. Da dɩkɛ-ya mam Sɔ deego la ẽŋɛ leebgo zẽ'a.».

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la tẽegɛ tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ, fõn deego la nõŋlʋm tɩ mam tarɩ la, wʋn di mam wʋ bugum na.».

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tõon-yãlma kãna tɩ fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ pa'alɛ tõma tɩ fõn tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ wãna?».

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Sã'am-ya Wẽnde-kãtɛ wã, tɩ mam wʋn le mẽ ẽ dabsa atã pʋam.».

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ba dɩkɛ la yʋʋma pinaasɩ la ayoobɩ mẽ Wẽnde-ẽna wã, tɩ fõn yetɩ, fõn wʋn mẽ ẽ dabsa atã pʋam?».

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: La Azezi n tɔgrɩ Wẽndeego yelle la, la de la a mẽŋa ĩyã yelle tɩ a tɔgrɩ.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: La Azezi n daan wa ki dee vo'oge la, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la tẽegɛ tɩ a daan yele la wãna, tɩ ba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yetɔga, la Azezi n daan yele se'em na sɩra.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: La Azezi n daan boe Zerizalɛm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, nẽrba zozo'e n daan yẽ tɩ a tõnnɩ tõon-yãlma, tɩ ba bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: La Azezi daan ka dɩkɛ a sũure wuu pa'alɛ ba, se'ere n soe la, a mi ba wuu mɛ.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: A ka eerɩ tɩ nẽra tɔgɛ nẽrsaalba yelle bɔ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a mi bõn-sɛka n boe nẽrsaalba sũure pʋam mɛ.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ, Yetɔga la põn bɔna mɛ. La Yetɔga la yʋʋn boe la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ de la bʋyɩla.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: A põn bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Nawẽnnɛ la ẽŋa n yʋʋn nãam sɛla woo. Sɛla sɛla ka boe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ la ẽŋa ka nãam.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Ẽŋa n de vɔm dãana. La de la ẽŋa n botɩ nẽrsaalba yẽta peelem.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Peelem na nẽegɛ lika pʋam mɛ, la lika ka yãŋɛ kĩŋe ẽ.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Nawẽnnɛ daan tõm na nẽra ayɩla, a yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azã.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: A wa'am tɩ a tɔgɛ la peelem na yelle pa'alɛ nẽrba tɩ ba wuu wʋn tã'agɛ bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Azã mẽŋa dagɩ peelem na, la a wa'am tɩ a tɔgɛ la sɛka n de peelem na yelle pa'alɛ nẽrba.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Sɛka n de peelem sɩra sɩra la, ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am dũnia zuo. Ẽŋa n nẽegɛ tɩ nẽrsaalba wuu yẽta peelem.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Sɛka n de peelem na daan wa bɔna la dũnia zuo. Nawẽnnɛ la ẽŋa n yʋʋn nãam dũnia. Baa la bɛla, nẽrsaalba ka boorɩ tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽ.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: A daan wa'am na a mẽŋa tẽŋa pʋam, la a nẽrba la ka to'oge ẽ.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: La sɛba wuu n to'oge ẽ bɔ ẽ sɩra la, a bɔ ba nõorɛ mɛ tɩ ba lebge Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Ba ka lebge Nawẽnnɛ kɔma wʋ nẽrsaalba n dɔgrɩ se'em na, la ka de ĩyã n boorɩ se'em, bɩɩ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em. La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ ba lebge a kɔma.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Sɛka n de Yetɔga la daan lebge la nẽrsaala, kẽ'era tõma tẽŋasʋka. Tõma yẽ a na'am pãŋa mɛ, na'am pãŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Bi-yẽŋa n ze'ele a Sɔ zẽ'am na'am pãŋa. La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, la a sɩra wuu boe la a zẽ'am.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: La de la ẽŋa yelle tɩ Azã daan tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ẽna n de sɛka tɩ mam põn tɔgɛ a yelle yetɩ, nẽra ayẽma n boe mam poorʋm sẽna, ẽŋa gãnnɩ mam mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a dẽŋɛ mam mɛ bɔna.».

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Ẽŋa n botɩ tõma wuu yẽta yel-sõnnɛ woo yel-sõnnɛ, ẽn ba tara Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Nawẽnnɛ bɔ a lɔɔ la Amoyisi, tɩ a bɔ tõma. La, la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, la a sɩra la, la doose la Azezi-krisi tɩ a bɔ tõma.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe Farisi dõma pʋam tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Anikodɛm. A daan de la Zifdõma nẽŋadãana.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Nẽra nẽra nãn ka yẽ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la Nawẽnnɛ Bi-yẽŋa la n boe la ẽ lɛ n botɩ tõma bãŋɛ ẽ, ẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ de la bʋyɩla.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Bʋra-ẽŋa daan wa'am na Azezi zẽ'am yʋ'ʋŋɔ wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tõm fõn tɩ fʋ dɛna karẽnsãama, se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma wʋ fõn ɩtɩ se'em wã wuu, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ n boe la a dãana.».

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Azã n daan bɔ kaseto se'em n wãna: Zifdõma nẽŋadõma sɛba n kẽ'erɩ Zerizalɛm na daan tõm na ba kãabgɔ kẽma, la Leviti dõma tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azã zẽ'am, ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne?».

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn ka yẽ dɔg-paalga, a ka tã wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm.».

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Tɩ Azã tɔgɛ ba vẽelga yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na.».

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Tɩ Anikodɛm soke ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra n kʋrgɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ ba le tã'agɛ dɔgɛ ẽ paalga? A wʋn le tã'agɛ mɛ kẽ a ma pʋʋrʋm tɩ ba le dɔgɛ ẽ bɩɩ?».

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Tɩ ba le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne? Fõn de la A'eli bɩɩ?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ A'eli.» Tɩ ba le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la n yetɩ a wa'am na bɩɩ?» Tɩ a yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ.».

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn ka dɔgɛ la ko'om, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, a ka tã wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Tɩ ba le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛla fõn de la ãne? Tɩ tɩ tarɩ fõn lerge se'em na, sẽŋɛ ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ sɛba n tõm tõma la. Fõn mẽŋa yetɩ fõn de la ãne?».

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Nẽrsaala n dɔgɛ sɛka la de la nẽrsaala, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n dɔgɛ sɛka la tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa vɔm.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Ban soke ẽ bɛla la, tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam de la sɛka n mõolɛ weem tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: ‹Malgɛ-ya sore tɩ a dɛna tẽntẽa bɔ Zuudãana la› », wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn põn yele se'em na.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Da basɛ tɩ la di'ige fo, mam n yetɩ: Dẽnɩ tɩ ba le dɔgɛ ya la ĩyã.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n daan sẽŋɛ Azã zẽ'am na, Farisi dõma n daan tõm ba.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Kʋsebgo vugri la ẽn boorɩ zẽ'a, tɩ fʋ wõnna a dãmnɛ, dee ka mina ẽn ze'ele zẽ'a sẽna, la ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'a. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n dɔgɛ sɛka la ãna bɛla.».

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Tɩ ba daan soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn sãn dagna Krisi la, bɩɩ A'eli, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la, bẽm tɩ fõn misra nẽrba ko'om pʋam?».

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Tɩ Anikodɛm le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bõn-ẽŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne ẽŋɛ?».

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna mam, mam misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la nẽra ayɩla n boe ya tẽŋasʋka tɩ ya ka mina ẽ.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de Ɩsrayɛlɩ karẽnsãam-kɛ'ɛma dee ken ka mina bõn-ẽna?.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: Ẽŋa wa bɔna la mam poorʋm, la mam ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra.».

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ tõma tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ tõma mi, dee yɛta sɛla tɩ tõma yẽ. La ya ka sakɛ tõma yetɔga la.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Bõn-bãna wuu daan ẽŋɛ la Betani, Zʋrdẽ kʋlaa la doone, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Azã daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: Mam sãn tɔgɛ dũnia wã yɛla pa'alɛ ya, tɩ ya ka sakɛ, mam sãn tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire yɛla pa'alɛ ya, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne sakɛ?.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azã yẽ Azezi n sẽm a zẽ'am. Tɩ a yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, ẽna n de Nawẽnnɛ Pebila n yesrɩ nẽrsaalba tõon-be'ero basra.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Nẽra nẽra nãn ka zom Nawẽnnɛ yire, sãn dagna Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sige.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: La de la ẽŋa yelle tɩ mam daan yele yetɩ: ‹Nẽra n wa bɔna mam poorʋm dɛna nẽr-keko gãnna mam na, se'ere n soe la, a dẽŋɛ mam mɛ bɔna.›.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn zẽkɛ bõnsɛla dɔɔgɔ zuo weego pʋam se'em na, dẽnɩ tɩ ba zẽkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ bɛla.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Mam daan ka mi ẽn de sɛka, la mam wa'am wa misra la nẽrba ko'om pʋam, tɩ m botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma bãŋɛ ẽ.».

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra wʋn tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Azã le tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Mam yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra ãna wʋ na'adawenne, wa zoole a zuo.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ nẽrsaalba mɛ halɩ, tɩ a tõm a Bi-yẽŋa dũnia zuo, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra kãn sã'am, la a wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: La mam daan ka mi ẽ, la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm mam tɩ m mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, yele mam tɩ: ‹Fʋ sãn wa yẽ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n sige zoole sɛka zuo la, la de la ẽŋa n misri nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la pʋam.›.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka tõm a Dayʋa la dũnia tɩ a di nẽrba sarɩya sɩbgɛ ba, a tõm ẽ tɩ a fãagɛ ba mɛ basɛ.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
