local: La mam yẽ ẽ mɛ, bɔ kaseto yetɩ, ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la.».

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: La sɛka n bɔ ẽ sɩra, ba kãn di a sarɩya. La sɛka n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la, a põn koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ, ẽn ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Bi-yẽŋa la sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azã daan le zɛ bilam la a karẽnbiisi bayi.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Bõn-ẽna n de sarɩyadia la: Peelem wa'am dũnia pʋam mɛ, la nẽrsaalba nõŋɛ la lika gãnna peelem na, ba tõoma n de tõon-be'ero la ĩyã.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: yẽ Azezi n sẽnnɩ, tɩ a yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Pebila la n wãna.».

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero sisri peelem mɛ, a ka sẽm peelem pʋam, a zotɩ mɛ tɩ a tõoma la wʋn puke.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi bayi la wõm ẽn yele se'em na, isge dɔla Azezi.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ sɩra sẽm na peelem zẽ'am, tɩ a tõoma la pa'alɛ tɩ a sakrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Tɩ Azezi gelge yẽ ban dolɩ ẽ, soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma eerɩ la bẽm?» Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Rabi, fõn kẽ'erɩ la bɛ?».

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi la a karẽnbiisi sẽŋɛ la Zude so'olʋm ka bɔna bilam misra nẽrba ko'om pʋam.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya tɩ ya wʋn yẽ.» Tɩ ba doose sẽŋɛ ka yẽ ẽn kẽ'erɩ zẽ'a na, daan weege bɔna a zẽ'am daar-ẽŋa, la daan de wʋ wẽngɛlŋa karf-anaasɩ.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Tɩ Azã mẽ daan bɔna Enõ tẽŋa n lɛm Salim tẽŋa la misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam, se'ere n soe la, ko'om n daan boe mĩ zo'oge. Tɩ nẽrba sẽnna, tɩ a misra ba.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Nẽrba bayi la n wõm Azã n tɔgɛ Azezi yelle se'em doose ẽ na, ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ de la A'ãndre, a de la Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbga.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa ba daan nãn ka kẽ'esɛ Azã yʋ'a deem.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Tɩ A'ãndre dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka yẽ a kẽema Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma yẽ Mesi la. La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ Krisi.».

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi basɛba, la Zifdõma nẽra daan wẽ'era nõke'ene, Zifdõma sisgo n yetɩ ba pɛɛra ba mẽŋa malgra la yelle.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Tɩ a daan tarɩ Asimõ sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am, tɩ Azezi bɩsɛ ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Fõn de la Azã bia Asimõ. Ba wʋn wi'ira fʋ tɩ Asefasɩ, bɩɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ.».

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azã zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, sɛka n daan boe la fõn Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba la, sɛka tɩ fõn daan tɔgɛ a yelle la, ẽn wãna n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam, tɩ nẽrba wuu sẽnna a zẽ'am.».

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azezi bɔɔra tɩ a sẽŋɛ Galile, yẽ Afilipi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dɔla mam.».

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽra ka tã wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ la sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Afilipi ze'ele la Betisayida tẽŋa, A'ãndre la Apɩyɛɛrɩ mẽ ze'ele la bilam.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Yãma mẽŋa tã wʋn bɔ kaseto mɛ tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Dagɩ mam n de Krisi la. La, ba tõm mam mɛ tɩ m dẽŋɛ a nẽŋa wa'am.›.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Tɩ Afilipi sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Anatanayɛlɩ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma yẽ sɛka tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ a yelle lɔɔ gõnnɔ pʋam, tɩ a yelle ken gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na. A de la Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ tẽŋa, Azozɛfʋ dayʋa.».

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: La ãn wʋ nẽra n di pɔga: Pɔgdita la n soe pɔka la, la pɔgdita la sɩrɛ n ze dee sɛlsra a yetɔga la, a tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ zozo'e. Bɛla mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ zozo'e.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Tɩ Anatanayɛlɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bõn-sõŋɔ tã wʋn ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ?» Tɩ Afilipi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Yi wa'am wa bɩsɛ.».

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ ẽŋa lebge kãtɛ dee tɩ mam lebge pɩka.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ Anatanayɛlɩ n sẽm a zẽ'am, tɩ a tɔgɛ a yelle yetɩ: «Ẽna de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽra sɩra sɩra n ka tarɩ yelle.».

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Sɛka n yese saazuum wa'am n gãnnɩ wuu, la sɛka n yese dũnia de la dũnia nẽra, la a tɔgrɩ la dũnia yelle. Sɛka n yese Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am na n gãnnɩ wuu.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Tɩ Anatanayɛlɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne mina mam?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boe kẽnkãŋa la tẽŋa, lan dẽŋɛ Afilipi n wʋn wi fʋ la, mam yẽ fʋ mɛ.».

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: la a tɔgrɩ la ẽn wõm dee yẽ sɛla la, la nẽra nẽra ka to'oge a yetɔga la.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Tɩ Anatanayɛlɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma naba.».

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n to'oge a yetɔga la sakɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n yele fõn tɩ mam yẽ fõn boe kẽnkãŋa tẽŋa la ĩyã, tɩ fʋ bɔ mam sɩra? Fʋ wʋn yẽ bõn-kãra n gãnnɩ bõn-bãna.».

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na tɔgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ a Sɩa pãŋa zozo'e la ĩyã.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire n yo'oge, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma zɔnna dee sigra Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la zuo.».

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ a Dayʋa la mɛ, tɩ a dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ a nu'usum.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Sɛka n bɔ a Dayʋa la sɩra tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. La sɛka n ka sakɛ a Dayʋa la kãn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre boe a dãana zuo.».

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi daan sẽŋɛ la Galile mõgrɛ tɩ ba ken wi'ira tɩ Tiberiadɩ mõgrɛ la doone.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ doose ẽ, ban yẽ tɩ a tõnnɩ tõon-yãlma botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm na ĩyã.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi zom tãŋa zuo ka zẽa.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: La Zifdõma kibsa n de Zɔɔg-basɛ la daan lɛm mɛ.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Tɩ Azezi zẽkɛ zuugo yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n sẽm a zẽ'am, tɩ a soke Afilipi yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn yẽ dia la bɛ da bɔ nẽr-bãna wã tɩ ba di?».

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: A daan yele wãna tɩ a di la a yɛm bɩsɛ, bẽm ĩyã, a mẽŋa daan põn mina ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em mɛ.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Tɩ Afilipi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Baa tɩ sãn da dia lig-kõba kɔbsyi, la ka tã wʋn seke tɩ nẽra woo to'oge fẽfẽe.».

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbia ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'ãndre n de Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbga yele ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: «Budibla n boe kalam tara borborɩ banuu, la zũn-pɩgsɩ bayi. La bãna de la bẽm, tɩ tɩ wʋn dɩkɛ bɔ nẽrba n zo'oge wãna?».

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ nẽrba la zĩ'ire.» La mõogɔ n daan boe zẽ'e-ẽŋa zo'oge. Tɩ ba zĩ'ire, la bʋraasɩ kãllɛ daan de wʋ tʋsanuu.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Tɩ Azezi to'oge borborɩ la pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee pʋɩ bɔ nẽrba la n zĩ la, la a daan ken ẽŋɛ zũma la mẽ na bɛla. Tɩ nẽra woo to'oge ẽn boorɩ se'em di tɩgɛ.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: La ban daan di tɩgɛ la, tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Lagsɛ-ya borbor-gito la n deege la, tɩ sɛla da sã'am.».

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Tɩ ba lagsɛ borborɩ banuu la tɩ ba di deege la gito, tɩ ba pɩrɛ pɩtɔ pia la ayi.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Nẽrba la n yẽ Azezi n ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma la, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Ẽna wã sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la tɩ ba yetɩ a sẽm dũnia zuo la.».

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: La Azezi daan bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba pẽrgɛ ẽŋa mɛ tɩ a di na'am, tɩ a lebe sẽŋɛ tãŋa la zuo ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Tɩ zaanõorɛ daan paagɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la sige mõgrʋm.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: ka kẽ õorŋɔ pãrgra ko'om na wɛ'ɛsa Kapɛrnaʋm, mõgrɛ la ke bɔba. Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan sobe, dee tɩ Azezi nãn ka sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ vugra tɩ ko'om na wẽ'era ke'enke'em.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ masɛ wʋ kilomẽtrɩ anuu bɩɩ kilomẽtrɩ ayoobɩ, yẽ tɩ Azezi sẽnnɩ ko'om na zuo lɛnna õorŋɔ la, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La de la mam. Da zoe-ya dabeem.».

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam. Tɩ õorŋɔ la darɛ paagɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba we'esɩ la.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Tɩ beere daan wiige, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n weege mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba la bãŋɛ tɩ õorŋɔ ayɩla mã'a n zaam boe. La ba yẽ tɩ Azezi ka kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam la a karẽnbiisi la, a karẽnbiisi la mã'a n fõrgɛ.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Tɩ õornɔ basɛba ze'ele Tiberiadɩ wa'am wa lɛm zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Zuudãana Azezi daan pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dia la ĩyã, dee tɩ ba di la.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n yẽ tɩ Azezi, la a karẽnbiisi la ka boe zẽ'e-ẽŋa la, tɩ ba kẽ õornɔ sẽŋɛ Kapɛrnaʋm tɩ ba ka ɛ ẽ.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: La ba daan sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Azezi la mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, wãne mã'a tɩ fõn wa'am kalam?».

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ yan eerɩ mam na, dagɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la mam bõn-yãlma la võorɛ, la de la yan di tɩgɛ la ĩyã.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Da tõnna-ya dia n wʋn sã'am ĩyã, tõnna-ya di-sɛba n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa n wʋn bɔ ya vɔm ẽŋa. Se'ere n soe la, la de la ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la bɔ ẽ a nõorɛ.».

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Tɩ ba soke Azezi yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tõm tõon-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Tõon-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ ya tõm, de la ya sakɛ sɛka tɩ a tõm na.».

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõon-yãlma kãna tɩ fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ tõma yẽ, bɔ fʋ sɩra? Tõon-kãna tɩ fõn wʋn tõm?.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn di la dia tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ mannɩ weem, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ se'em na, a yʋʋn bɔ ba la dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ ba di.».

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ la dagɩ Amoyisi n yʋʋn bɔ ya dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire la, la de la mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ ya dia sɩra sɩra n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Dia sɩra sɩra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ya la, de la sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sige dũnia bɔ'ɔra nẽrba vɔm na.».

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, bɔ'ɔra tɔ di-ẽŋa daarɛ woo.».

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam n de dia n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm na. Nẽr-sɛka woo n sẽm mam zẽ'am, kɔm kãn malɩn yõkɛ ẽ, la sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra, koyũuro kãn malɩn tara ẽ.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: La mam yele yãma mɛ, tɩ ya yẽ mam mɛ, la ya ka bɔ mam sɩra.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Sɛba wuu tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔrɩ mam, sẽm mam zẽ'am mɛ. La sɛka n sẽm mam zẽ'am, mam kãn dige nẽra baa ayɩla basɛ.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum tɩ m ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em, mam sigum tɩ m tõm na sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: La sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em de la m da botɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ a bɔ mam na baa ayɩla sã'am, la de la m vo'oge ba wuu dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em de la sɛka woo n yẽ a Dayʋa la, dee bɔ ẽ sɩra wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. Mam wʋn vo'oge ẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.».

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma yõnna Azezi, ẽn yetɩ, ẽŋa n de dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum na ĩyã.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Tɩ ba daan yɛta taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna dagɩ Azozɛfʋ bia Azezi, sɛka tɩ tõma mi a sɔ, la a ma la? La bẽm tɩ a yetɩ, ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire?».

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Da yõnna-ya.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nẽra ka tã wʋn wa'am mam zẽ'am tɩ la sãn dagna mam Sɔ n tõm mam na n tarɩ ẽ wa'am. La mam n wʋn vo'oge ẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn pa'alɛ ba wuu.› La sɛka woo n selse mam Sɔ la, dee to'oge a pa'algɔ la, sẽm mam zẽ'am mɛ.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Nẽra nãn ka yẽ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, sãn dagna sɛka n ze'ele a zẽ'am na mã'a n yẽ ẽ.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Mam n de dia n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm na.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Yãma yaabdõma yʋʋn di mannɩ mɛ weego pʋam, la ba ki mɛ.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: La di-sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum na, de la dia tɩ nẽr-sɛka n di kãn ki.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Mam n de di-sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum, tɩ nẽra sãn di di-ẽna, a wʋn vɔa wakatɛ wuu. La di-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn bɔ dũnia dõma tɩ ba yẽ vɔm na, de la mam ĩyã.».

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma la wẽ'era nõke'ene la taaba yetɩ: «Nẽr-ẽna wã wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ tõma a ĩyã tɩ tõma di?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya sãn ka di Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ĩyã, la ya sãn ka yũ a zɩɩm, ya ka tarɩ vɔm na ya pʋam.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Sɛka n diti mam ĩyã dee yũura mam zɩɩm tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, la mam wʋn vo'oge ẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam ĩyã de la dia mẽŋa mẽŋa, la mam zɩɩm de la bõn-yũula mẽŋa mẽŋa.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Sɛka n diti mam ĩyã, dee yũura mam zɩɩm boe la mam mɛ, tɩ mam bɔna la ẽ.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Mam Sɔ n tõm mam na, de la vɔm dãana, ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ mam tara vɔm, bɛla, sɛka n diti mam ĩyã mẽ wʋn vɔa mam ĩyã.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Ẽna de la dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum, a ka ãn wʋ di-sɛka tɩ ya yaabdõma yʋʋn di ki la. Sɛka n di di-ẽna wʋn vɔa wakatɛ wuu.».

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Azezi n daan zãsnɩ nẽrba Kapɛrnaʋm wẽndeego la pʋam na, tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: La a karẽnbiisi basɛba zozo'e n daan wõm bɛla la, tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Yetɔg-ẽna to'ore tɔ mɛ, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ sakɛ yetɔg-ẽna wã?».

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yõnnɩ mɛ bõn-ẽna yelle, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bõn-ẽna botɩ yãma lɛbra la poorʋm kãn le doose mam?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: La ya sãn yẽ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la zonnɩ ẽn daan boe zẽ'a na, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm na, nẽrsaala ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla. Yetɔg-sɛba tɩ mam tɔgɛ bɔ ya la, ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am bɔ'ɔra vɔm.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: La basɛba n boe ya tẽŋasʋka ka bɔ mam sɩra.» Se'ere n soe tɩ Azezi yele bɛla la, a daan dẽŋɛ bãŋɛ mɛ halɩ pɔspɔsɩ sɛba n kãn bɔ ẽŋa sɩra, la sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽŋa.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Tɩ a le yele ba yetɩ: «Bɛla n soe tɩ mam yetɩ, nẽra ka tã wʋn wa'am mam zẽ'am, tɩ la sãn dagna mam Sɔ la n bɔ ẽ sore.».

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la poorʋm, a karẽnbiisi la zozo'e basɛ ẽ mɛ dee fõrgɛ ka le doose ẽ.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la yetɩ: «Yãma mẽ boorɩ tɩ ya basɛ mam dee fõrgɛ mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma wʋn basɛ fõn dee sẽŋɛ la ãn zẽ'am? Fõn tarɩ la yetɔga n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: La tõma bɔ sɩra mɛ dee bãŋɛ tɩ fõn de la Pʋpeelem Dãana tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam n looge yãma pia la ayi la. La ya nẽra ayɩla de la Asũtãana nẽra.».

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: A daan tɔgrɩ la Asimõ bia Azudaasɩ Iskariyo yelle. Se'ere n soe la, ẽŋa n daan de a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla n wʋn wa pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi daan gilgri la Galile, a ka boorɩ tɩ a gilge Zude, Zifdõma nẽŋadõma n eerɩ ẽ tɩ ba kʋ la ĩyã.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma põnsɩ kibsa daan lɛm.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: tɩ Azezi yɩbsɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ kalam dee sẽŋɛ Zude tɩ fʋ karẽnbiisi mẽ yẽ fʋ tõoma la tɩ fʋ tõnnɩ na.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ ẽ, a ka sʋgrɩ a tõoma. Fõn tõnnɩ tõon-bãna wã, botɩ nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ fɔ.».

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: La a yɩbsɩ la daan ka bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam wakatɛ nãn ka paagɛ, la wakatɛ woo ãn sõŋa mɛ bɔ yãma.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Dũnia dõma kãn tã'agɛ sise yãma, la ba sisri mam mɛ, mam n tɔgrɩ pa'ala tɩ ba tõoma de la be'em na ĩyã.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Yãma sẽŋɛ kibsa la, tɩ mam nãn kãn sẽŋɛ, se'ere n soe la, mam wakatɛ nãn ka paagɛ.».

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: A yele ba bɛla dee weege la Galile.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: La a yɩbsɩ la n daan sẽŋɛ kibsa la, a mẽ sẽŋɛ mɛ, la a ka botɩ nẽrba yẽ ẽ.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma ɛɛra ẽ kibsa la pʋam, sɔkra yetɩ: «A boe la bɛ?».

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Tɩ nẽrba waasna tɔgra a yelle nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam zozo'e, tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «A de la nẽr-sõŋɔ», tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, a pã'asrɩ la nẽrba.».

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: La nẽra nẽra daan ka saagɛ tɔgɛ a yelle nẽrba la zẽ'am, ban zotɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la ĩyã.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: La kibsa la daan paagɛ la pʋsʋka, tɩ Azezi kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Tɩ la di'ige Zifdõma, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna ẽŋɛ la wãne mina Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ sõŋa, dee ka yẽ zãsŋɔ?».

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam zãsŋɔ la ka ze'ele mam mẽŋa zẽ'am, la ze'ele la sɛka n tõm mam na zẽ'am.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, a wʋn bãŋɛ zãsŋɔ la sãn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, bɩɩ mam sãn tɔgra la mam mẽŋa pãŋa.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n tɔgrɩ la a mẽŋa pãŋa, eerɩ la a mẽŋa pẽka. La nẽr-sɛka n eerɩ sɛka n tõm ẽ na pẽka, ẽŋa de la sɩra dãana, la sɛla n ka masɛ ka boe a pʋam.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Amoyisi n bɔ ya lɔɔ la, dagɩ bɛla? La ya nẽra nẽra ka ɩtɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na. Bẽm tɩ ya ɛɛra mam tɩ ya kʋ?».

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ fo! Ãn eerɩ fʋ tɩ a kʋ?».

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam daan tõm tõon-yãlma ayɩla mɛ, tɩ la di'ige ya.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Amoyisi n yʋʋn bɔ ya nõorɛ yetɩ, ya wãta ya budimto. La, la dagɩ Amoyisi n pɔsɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla, la de la tɩ sɔ-yaabdõma. La yãma wãtɩ mɛ baa vo'osgo daarɛ.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Yãma sãn wãta ya budimto vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ la ka sã'am Amoyisi lɔɔ la, bẽm tɩ yãma sũnsõa yĩige la mam, mam n daan botɩ bʋraa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ la ĩyã.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Da kãn dɩkɛ-ya nini n yẽ se'em mõŋɛ nẽra bʋʋrɔ, bɔ-ya bʋʋrɔ wʋ lan masɛ se'em na.».

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Tɩ Zerizalɛm nẽrba basɛba soke yetɩ: «Dagɩ bʋra-ẽna tɩ ba eerɩ tɩ ba kʋ la?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya, a tɔgrɩ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ pʋam, dee tɩ ba ka yele ẽ se'ere. Nẽŋadõma la na'an bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ a de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na bɩɩ?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Tõma mi bʋra-ẽna n ze'ele zẽ'a, la Krisi la sãn wa sẽna, nẽra kãn bãŋɛ ẽn ze'ele zẽ'a.».

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Bɛla tɩ a Azezi daan tɔgɛ ke'enke'em ẽn zãsnɩ nẽrba Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yetɩ: «Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya mi mam mɛ dee bãŋɛ mam n ze'ele zẽ'a, la mam ka wa'am na m mẽŋa nõorɛ. La sɛka n tõm mam na de la sɩra dãana, yãma ka mi ẽ.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: La mam nõo mi ẽ, se'ere n soe la, mam ze'ele la ẽŋa zẽ'am, ẽŋa n tõm mam.».

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Tɩ ba ɛɛra sore tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, la nẽra nẽra ka kalʋm ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a wakatɛ daan nãn ka paagɛ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la nẽrba zozo'e daan bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ, dee yɛta tɩ: «Krisi la sãn wa wa'am, a kãn yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma gãnna bʋra-ẽna n ẽŋɛ wã.».

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma wõm nẽrba la n waasnɩ Azezi yelle, tɩ bãma la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma tõm gu'urba tɩ ba ka yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Mam wʋn ken bɔna la ya la wakatɛ fẽe, dee lebe sɛka n tõm mam na zẽ'am.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ya wʋn ɛɛra mam, la ya kãn yẽ mam, la mam n boe zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam.».

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Bɛla tɩ Zifdõma yele taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wʋn wa sẽŋɛ la bɛ tɩ tõma kãn yẽ ẽ? A yetɩ a sẽŋɛ la Zifdõma n wɩrgɛ bɔna Grɛkɩdõma tẽŋasʋka la zẽ'am, ka zãsʋm Grɛkɩdõma bɩɩ?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Yetɔg-ẽna wã võorɛ de la bẽm tɩ a yetɩ: ‹Ya wʋn ɛɛra mam, la ya kãn yẽ mam, la mam n boe zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam?› ».

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: La kibsa la ba'asgɔ daarɛ n de a dabs-kãtɛ daarɛ, tɩ Azezi daan isge ze'ele tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Koyũuro sãn tara nẽr-sɛka, a wa'am mam zẽ'am wa yũ.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Nẽr-sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra, ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm wʋn bulla a pʋam, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na.».

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Azezi n yele bɛla la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tɩ sɛba n bɔ'ɔrɩ ẽ sɩra la wʋn wa to'oge. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ daan nãn ka bɔ a Sɩa la, Azezi n daan nãn ka yẽ a na'am pãŋa la ĩyã.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n wõm yetɔg-bãna la, tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ẽna sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la.».

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la Krisi la.» Tɩ basɛba daan yetɩ: «Krisi la wʋn ze'ele la Galile bɩɩ?.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yele tɩ Krisi la wʋn dɛna la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa, a wʋn ze'ele la Bɛtelɛm, tẽn-sɛka tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn boe la.».

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Bɛla tɩ welgre wa'am nẽrba la pʋam Azezi ĩyã.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Tɩ ba nẽrba basɛba daan bɔɔra tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, la nẽra nẽra ka kalʋm ẽ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: La gu'urba la tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma la tõm na lebe ba zẽ'am mɛ, tɩ ba soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya ka yõkɛ ẽ wa'am?».

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Nẽra nãn ka tɔgɛ wʋ bʋra-ẽna wã.».

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma la soke ba yetɩ: «A pã'asɛ yãma mẽ yãŋɛ mɛ?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Tõma nẽŋadõma nẽra ayɩla, bɩɩ Farisi nẽra bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: La de la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n ka mi lɔɔ la, ba wʋn yẽ la kã'a-be'ero.».

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Tɩ Anikodɛm n de ba nẽra ayɩla n daan sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Azezi la, yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: «Tõma lɔɔ la bɔ sore tɩ tɩ mõŋɛ nẽra bʋʋrɔ, dee ka selse ẽ bãŋɛ ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ sɛla?».

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Tɩ ba lebse yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽ de la Galile nẽra bɩɩ? Ɛ bɩsɛ, fʋ wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ka ze'etɩ Galile.» .

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Tɩ nẽra woo daan kule a yire.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: La Azezi sẽŋɛ la Oliivi tãŋa zuo.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Tɩ beere wiige bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ a le lebe Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ nẽrba wuu daan sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ a zẽa zãsna ba.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma tarɩ pɔka tɩ ba kuke tɩ a boe mĩ ɩta yalsɩ wa'am wa botɩ a ze'ele nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: dee yele Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma kuke la pɔka wã tɩ a boe mĩ ɩta yalsɩ.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Amoyisi bɔ tõma nõorɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam yetɩ, pɔka sãn ẽŋɛ wãna, tɩ lobe ẽ na kuga kʋ. La fõn yetɩ bo?».

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Ba yele bɛla tɩ ba di la a yɛm bɩsɛ, tã'agɛ dõrɛ ẽ. Tɩ Azezi suure tara a nu'ubiire gʋlsra tẽŋa.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Tɩ ba ken sɔkra ẽ, tɩ a dɔ'ɔgɛ yele ba yetɩ: «Sɛka n boe yãma tẽŋasʋka ka tõm tõon-be'ego, a dẽŋɛ dɩkɛ kugre lobe ẽ.».

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Dee le suure gʋlsra tẽŋa.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: La ban wõm bɛla la, bõnkɛgsɩ n dẽŋɛ yese ayɩla ayɩla, tɩ ba wuu yese, dee basɛ Azezi mã'a la pɔka la, tɩ a zɛ a nẽŋam.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Tɩ Azezi dɔ'ɔgɛ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, sɛba n yõkɛ fʋ wa'am na bɛɛ? Nẽra nẽra ka di fʋ sarɩya sɩbgɛ fɔ?».

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge Azezi yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, Zuudãana.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam mẽ kãn di fʋ sarɩya. Sẽŋɛ, la da le tõm tõon-be'ero.».

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Tɩ Azezi le tɔgɛ la nẽrba la yetɩ: «Mam de la dũnia peelem. Nẽr-sɛka n dolɩ mam, a kãn malɩn sẽnna lika pʋam, a wʋn yẽ peelem n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.».

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ fʋ mẽŋa kaseto, la dagɩ kaseto asɩra.».

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Baa mam sãn bɔ'ɔra m mẽŋa kaseto, mam kaseto de la sɩra. Se'ere n soe la, mam mi mam n ze'ele zẽ'a wa'am, la mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a. La yãma ka mi mam n ze'ele zẽ'a, la mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Yãma diti sarɩya la nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em. La mam ka diti nẽra nẽra sarɩya.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: La baa mam sãn dita sarɩya, mam sarɩyadia de la sɩra, se'ere n soe la, mam ka de m yɩla. La de la mam, la mam Sɔ n tõm mam na.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: La yãma lɔɔ la pʋam la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Nẽrba bayi kaseto de la sɩra.›.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Mam mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ m mẽŋa kaseto, la mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na mẽ bɔ'ɔrɩ mam kaseto mɛ.».

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn Sɔ la boe la bɛ?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka mi mam, la ya ka mi mam Sɔ la. Ya sãn mi ni mam, ya wʋn mi ni mam Sɔ la mẽ.».

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Azezi n daan zãsnɩ nẽrba Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ligri lagsgɔ dagsɩ zẽ'am na, tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna. La nẽra nẽra ka yõkɛ ẽ, a wakatɛ n nãn ka paagɛ la ĩyã.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam wʋn fõrgɛ mɛ, la ya wʋn ɛ mam koŋe, la ya wʋn ki la ya tõon-be'ero la pʋam. Mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam.».

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ẽn yetɩ: ‹Mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam› na, a yetɩ a kʋ la a mẽŋa bɩɩ?».

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ze'ele la tẽŋa, la mam ze'ele la saazuum wa'am. Yãma de la dũnia wã nẽrba, la mam dagɩ dũnia wã nẽra.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yetɩ, ya wʋn ki la ya tõon-be'ero la pʋam. Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn ka bɔ sɩra tɩ mam de la sɛka tɩ mam de la, ya wʋn ki la ya tõon-be'ero la pʋam.».

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam de la sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ ya halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: Mam tarɩ nɩ yɛla zozo'e mɛ n wʋn tɔgɛ yãma yelle, dee di ya sarɩya. La sɛka n tõm mam na de la sɩra dãana, la sɛla tɩ mam wõm a zẽ'am n bɛla tɩ mam tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra dũnia dõma.».

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: La ba ka bãŋɛ tɩ a tɔgrɩ ba la a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yelle.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Bɛla tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn wa zẽkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la, ya wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam de la sɛka tɩ mam de la. La ya wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam ka ɩtɩ sɛla sɛla la m mẽŋa nõorɛ, mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n pa'alɛ mam se'em na mã'a tɩ mam tɔgrɩ.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Azezi daan bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Farisi dõma wõm tɩ ẽŋa yẽtɩ karẽnbiisi gãnna, dee misri ba ko'om pʋam gãnna Azã.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: La sɛka n tõm mam na boe la mam mɛ, a ka basɛ mam m yɩla, se'ere n soe la, mam ɩtɩ la sɛla n ãn a sũure yẽlʋm daarɛ woo.».

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: La, la daan dagɩ Azezi mẽŋa n misri ba ko'om pʋam, la de la a karẽnbiisi la.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Ẽn tɔgɛ bɛla la, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: La Azezi n daan bãŋɛ ban tɔgrɩ se'em na, tɩ a ze'ele Zude so'olʋm lɛbra Galile.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele Zifdõma sɛba n daan bɔ ẽ sɩra la yetɩ: «Ya sãn sakra mam yetɔga la ka basɛ, ya sɩrɩ de la mam karẽnbiisi.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: La daan de la a doose Samaari so'olʋm pʋam tole.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: La ya wʋn bãŋɛ sɩra, la sɩra la wʋn to'oge ya basɛ.».

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Tɩ a daan sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Samaari tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Sisaarɩ n lɛm va-sɛka tɩ Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn bɔ a bia Azozɛfʋ la.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ, tõma ka de nẽra nẽra yamsɩ. La wãne tɩ fõn yetɩ, tɩ wʋn to'oge tɩ mẽŋa?».

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Azakɔbɩ lʋa n daan boe bilam, tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ targɛ zĩ'ire lʋa la nõorʋm. La daan de wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero de la tõon-be'ero yamŋa.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Tɩ Samaari pɔka wa'am tɩ a ãkɛ ko'om. Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽŋɛ ko'om bɔ ma tɩ m yũ.».

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Yamŋa dagɩ yire la nẽra wakatɛ wuu, la yire la dayʋa de la yire la nẽra wakatɛ wuu.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Tɩ pɔka la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fõn dɛna Zifu sɔsra mam n de Samaari pɔka yetɩ, m bɔ fʋ ko'om tɩ fʋ yũ?» Se'ere n soe la, Zifdõma la Samaari dõma daan nẽ'esrɩ la taaba.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la sãn to'oge ya basɛ, ya sɩrɩ to'oge ya mẽŋa.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn mina Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla, la sɛka n yetɩ fʋ tɩ ‹bɔ ma ko'om tɩ m yũ› la, fʋ wʋn sose nɩ ẽ, la a wʋn bɔ fɔ ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.».

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: Mam mi tɩ ya de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ. La yãma eerɩ mam mɛ tɩ ya kʋ, yãma n ka to'orɩ mam yetɔga la ĩyã.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, fõn ka tarɩ lʋwannɛ, dee tɩ lʋa la kɔ, fõn wʋn yẽ vɔm ko-bãma la bɛ?.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Mam tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ mam yẽ m Sɔ zẽ'am na. La yãma mẽ ɩtɩ la sɛla tɩ ya wõm ya sɔ zẽ'am na.».

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Tõma yaaba Azakɔbɩ n bɔ tõma lʋa la, ẽŋa la a kɔma, la a dũnsi yũ lʋa la ko'om mɛ. Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn gãnnɩ tõma yaaba la bɩɩ?».

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Abraham n de tõma sɔ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn de nɩ na Abraham kɔma, ya wʋn tõm nɩ wʋ Abraham n tõm se'em na.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛka woo n yũ lɔ-ẽna ko'om na, koyũuro wʋn le tarɩ ẽ.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: La yãma eerɩ mam mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ ya kʋ, mam n tɔgɛ yãma sɩra la tɩ mam wõm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na ĩyã. Abraham ka ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: La sɛka n wʋn yũ ko-sɛba tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ẽ na, koyũuro kãn le malɩn tarɩ ẽ. La ko-sɛba tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ẽ na wʋn lebge wʋ bulga, bulla a pʋam bɔ'ɔra ẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Ya tõnnɩ na ya sɔ n tõnnɩ se'em na.» Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma dagɩ tãmpĩiba. Tõma tarɩ la sɔ ayɩla, n de Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, bɔ ma ko-sɛba tɩ m wʋn yũ tɩ koyũuro kãn le tara mam na, tɩ m da le wa'am kalam wa ãkɛ ko'om.».

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn de nɩ ya sɔ, ya wʋn nõŋɛ nɩ mam, se'ere n soe la, mam ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am. Mam ka wa'am m mẽŋa, ẽŋa n tõm mam.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ ka wi fʋ sɩra tɩ ya wa'am.».

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Bẽm tɩ yãma ka wõnna mam yetɔga la võorɛ? La de la yãma n ka boorɩ tɩ ya sakɛ mam yetɔga la ĩyã.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka tarɩ sɩra.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tarɩ bʋʋrɔ, fõn yetɩ fõn ka tarɩ sɩra la.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Ya sɔ de la Asũtãana, la ya boorɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la ya sɔ la n boorɩ se'em na. A de la nẽr-kʋʋra halɩ pĩ'iluŋo. A ka boe sɩra pʋam, sɩra n ka boe a pʋam na ĩyã. A sãn parna, ẽn ãn se'em n bɛla, se'ere n soe la, a de la põmpɔrma, la põmpɔrŋɔ wuu sɔ.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Fʋ ele la sɩrba banuu, la fõn boe la sɛka lɛɛlɛ wã dagɩ fʋ sɩra, bɛla fʋ tɔgɛ la asɩra.».

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: La, mam n tɔgrɩ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ ya ka bɔ'ɔra mam sɩra.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Tɩ pɔka la yele Azezi yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, lɛɛlɛ wã mam bãŋɛ tɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Yãma nẽr-kãna n tã wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ mam tõm tõon-be'ego? La mam n tɔgrɩ sɩra la, bẽm tɩ ya ka bɔ'ɔra mam sɩra?.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na tãn-ẽna zuo. Tɩ yãma Zifdõma yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrba pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ Zerizalɛm.».

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽra selsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. La yãma n dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la ĩyã, bɛla tɩ ya ka sɛlsra.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, bɔ mam yetɔga wã sɩra tɩ wakatɛ n sẽm tɩ ya kãn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ la tãn-ẽna zuo, bɩɩ Zerizalɛm.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma tarɩ bʋʋrɔ tɩ tõma yetɩ, fʋ de la Samaari nẽra, kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ fo.».

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Yãma Samaari dõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la sɛka tɩ ya ka mi. La tõma Zifdõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la sɛka tɩ tõma mi, se'ere n soe la, fãarɛ ze'ele la Zifdõma zẽ'am.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga ka dolɩ mam, mam nã'asrɩ la m Sɔ, la yãma ka sakɛ tɩ ya nã'asra mam.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: La wakatɛ sẽna, la a paam mɛ, tɩ sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra sɩra la, wʋn pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ la a Sɩa pãŋa, la sɩra. Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsra ẽ se'em n bɛla.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Mam ka eerɩ m mẽŋa nã'asgɔ. Nẽra ayɩla n boe n eerɩ mam nã'asgɔ, dee dita sarɩya.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Nawẽnnɛ de la Sɩa, dẽnɩ tɩ sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ ẽ na, pʋ'ʋsra ẽ la a Sɩa pãŋa, la sɩra.».

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn sakɛ mam yetɔga, a kãn malɩn ki.».

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam mi tɩ Mesi la sẽm mɛ, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge, tɩ ba wi'ira tɩ Krisi la. Ẽŋa sãn wa'am, a wʋn pa'alɛ tɔ sɛla woo.».

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nãnannɛ wã tõma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ kʋlkã'arga n sɩrɩ dolɩ fo. Abraham la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la ki mɛ. Tɩ fõn yetɩ, nẽra sãn sakɛ fõn yetɔga, a kãn malɩn ki.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n de ẽŋa n tɔgrɩ la fõn wã.».

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Fõn gãnnɩ tõma yaaba Abraham n ki la mɛ bɩɩ? Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la mẽ ki mɛ. Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn de la ãne?».

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Bɛla mã'a tɩ a karẽnbiisi la wa'am, tɩ la di'ige ba, ẽn tɔgrɩ la pɔka la. La baa bɛla, nẽra nẽra ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ la bẽm?» bɩɩ: «Fõn tɔgrɩ la pɔka la tɩ bẽm?».

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam sãn nã'asra m mẽŋa, mam nã'asgɔ la de la zãŋa. La de la mam Sɔ la n nã'asrɩ mam, sɛka tɩ ya wi'iri ya Nawẽnnɛ na.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Tɩ pɔka la basɛ a yoore la, dee sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam ka yele nẽrba yetɩ:.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: La yãma ka mi ẽ. La mam nõo mi ẽ. Mam sãn yetɩ, mam ka mi ẽ, mam wʋn dɛna la põmpɔrma wʋ yãma la. La mam mi ẽ mɛ, dee sakra a yetɔga.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: «Wa'am-ya wa bɩsɛ bʋraa n yele mam sɛla wuu tɩ mam ẽŋɛ. A nõo kãn dɛna la Krisi la?».

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Ya sɔ Abraham yʋʋn ẽŋɛ la sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm tɩ a wʋn wa yẽ mam daarɛ la, la a yẽ mɛ, ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.».

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Tɩ ba yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma la yele Azezi yetɩ: «Fõn nãn ka paagɛ yʋʋma pinuu mẽŋa dee yẽ Abraham?».

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a karẽnbiisi la mõrgra ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, di wɛ!».

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ Abraham n yʋʋn nãn ka boe la, mam boe mɛ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam tarɩ la dia dita tɩ yãma ka mina.».

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Tɩ ba pĩ'ise kuga tɩ ba lobe ẽ. Tɩ Azezi li'ise Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yese.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la daan sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Nẽra n tarɩ dia wa'am wa bɔ ẽ bɩɩ?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam dia de la m ẽŋɛ sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em, dee tõm a tõonɛ na ba'asɛ.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm tɩ a ka di Zifdõma kibsa.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Yãma yetɩ: ‹La deege la wõr-sɩnaasɩ tɩ bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ paagɛ.› La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya gorge bɩsɛ vatɔ la, ba bɩ mɛ paagɛ lagsgɔ.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: La Azezi daan sẽnnɩ tɔlla mɛ, yẽ bʋraa tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna fʋa.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: La kʋlga n daan boe Zerizalɛm tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ la Ebre yetɔga dɛna Betɛsda, a lɛm na piisi zanõrɛ, tɩ põnsɩ banuu bɔna mĩ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Sɛka n lagsrɩ la põn tara la yɔɔrɔ. Lagsgɔ la de la nẽrba tɩ a lagsrɩ tɩ ba tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. Bɛla tɩ sɛka n bʋrɛ, la sɛka n lagsɛ la lagɩm ɩta sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, nẽr-kãna n tõm tõon-be'ero? Bʋraa wã bɩɩ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma n tõm tõon-be'ero, tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna fʋa?».

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Tɩ bã'adõma zozo'e daan gã põnsɩ la tẽŋa, fɔɔsɩ la wɔbsɩ, la kɔɔrnɔ. Ba daan gu'ura la ko'om na sãn wa dãmɛ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Yetɔg-ẽna wã de la sɩra: ‹Ayẽma n bʋtɩ, tɩ ayẽma lagsra›.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La dagɩ bʋraa wã bɩɩ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, tɩ a dɛna fʋa. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ a tõm na ẽ pa'alɛ a tõoma.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: La bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe mĩ tɩ bã'a tara ẽ yʋʋma pitã la anii.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Mam tõm ya tɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka lagsɛ la nẽrba, wʋ va'am tɩ yãma ka tõm dee lagsra a bõnkɔɔla. La de la nẽrba basɛba n tõm tɩ yãma po dita tõonɛ na yõorɔ.».

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ tõma tõm sɛka n tõm mam na tõoma, la nãn ken dɛna wʋntɛɛŋa wã. Yʋ'ʋŋɔ sẽm mɛ tɩ nẽra kãn le tã'agɛ tõm.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ ẽ gãɩ, bãŋɛ tɩ a bẽ yuuge mɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ boorɩ tɩ fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm?».

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Mam n nãn boe dũnia pʋam wã, mam de la dũnia peelem.».

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Samaari tẽn-ẽŋa nẽrba zozo'e bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ, pɔka la n yele ba yetɩ: «A yele mam na sɛla wuu tɩ mam ẽŋɛ» la ĩyã.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Tɩ bã'ara la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam ka tara nẽra tɩ ko'om na sãn wa dãmɛ tɩ a wʋn yõkɛ mam basɛ kʋlga la pʋam. Mam sãn yetɩ m sige, ayẽma n dẽŋɛ mam sige.».

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: A yele bɛla dee tʋbsɛ la nõtõorʋm tẽŋa bũn tẽntõnnɔ bɩ fʋa la nini.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Bɛla tɩ Samaari dõma la wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa sose ẽ yetɩ, a bɔna bãma zẽ'am. Tɩ a bɔna ba zẽ'am dabsa ayi.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge, dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala sẽŋɛ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ ka wire fʋ nẽŋa Silowe kʋlgam.» Tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka wire a nẽŋa, dee lɛbra, tɩ a nini na yẽta.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan le bɔ ẽ sɩra pa'asɛ, a yetɔga la ĩyã.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Bilam zẽ'am tɩ bʋraa la daan yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dɩkɛ a bõn-gã'ala sẽnna. La daar-ẽŋa daan de la Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Tɩ a sawiiba, la sɛba n mi ẽ kʋrʋm tɩ a sosrɩ mɛ na yetɩ: «Dagɩ ẽna n daan zĩ sɔsra la?».

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: La ba daan yele pɔka la yetɩ: «La ka le dɛna fõn yele la ĩyã tɩ tõma bɔ ẽ sɩra. Tõma mẽŋa wõm a yetɔga mɛ, la tõma mi tɩ ẽna sɩrɩ de la dũnia Fãagra la.».

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Bɛla tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma yele bʋraa la n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na yetɩ: «La de la vo'osgo daarɛ, la sisri mɛ tɩ fʋ tʋ bõn-gã'ala.».

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: La dabsa ayi la poorʋm, Azezi yese bilam sẽŋɛ la Galile.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «La de la ẽŋa.» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, a wõn na ẽŋa dee la dagɩ ẽ.» Tɩ a mẽŋa yetɩ: «La de la mam.».

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sɛka n botɩ mam yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na, yele mam tɩ m dɩkɛ m bõn-gã'ala sẽŋɛ.».

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Azezi mẽŋa daan yetɩ: «Ba ka nannɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa a mẽŋa tẽŋa.».

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn nini na yãŋa yẽta?».

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽr-kãna n yele fõn yetɩ, fʋ dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala sẽŋɛ?».

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: La ẽn paagɛ Galile la, ba to'oge ẽ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, se'ere n soe la, ba mẽŋa daan sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la wakatɛ ka yẽ ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu mɛ.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Bʋraa la yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azezi la n bũn tẽntõnnɔ bɩ mam nini, dee yele mam tɩ m sẽŋɛ Silowe kʋlgam ka wire. Tɩ mam sẽŋɛ ka wire, yãŋa yẽta.».

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Tɩ sɛka n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na ka mina ẽn de sɛka, se'ere n soe la, Azezi daan kẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n boe bilam na pʋam fõrgɛ.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: La Azezi daan le sẽŋɛ la Kana n boe Galile so'olʋm na, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a daan botɩ ko'om lebge dãam na. La naba tõntõn-kɛ'ɛma n daan boe Kapɛrnaʋm tɩ a dayʋa bẽ'era.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi yẽ bʋraa la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Da le tõm tõon-be'ero, tɩ sɛla n de be'em gãnna bɛla da paagɛ fɔ.».

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «A boe la bɛ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Mam ka mi.».

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Tɩ a wõm tɩ Azezi ze'ele la Zude wa'am Galile, tɩ a sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am ka sose ẽ yetɩ, ẽŋa bia n boe mĩ tɩ a ki, a wa'am wa botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la sẽŋɛ ka yele Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n botɩ ẽŋa yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na de la Azezi.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ sɛka n daan de fʋa la sẽŋɛ Farisi dõma zẽ'am.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ya sãn ka yẽ bõn-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ, ya ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra.».

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: La Azezi n daan bũn tẽntõnnɔ bɩ fʋa la nini daar-sɛka tɩ a yẽta la, daan de la Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Tɩ ba daan ɛɛra tɩ ba nãmsɛ Azezi, ẽn botɩ kɔɔrŋɔ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ la ĩyã.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Tɩ naba tõntõn-kɛ'ɛma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, wa'am tɔtɔ tɩ m bia la nãn ka ki.».

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma la mẽ le soke ẽ yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn yẽta?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «A bũn na tẽntõnnɔ bɩ mam nini, tɩ mam wire, yãŋa yẽta.».

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ tõnnɩ mɛ wa paam nãnannɛ wã, tɩ mam mẽ tõnna.».

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ, fʋ bia la yeege mɛ.» Tɩ bʋraa la daan bɔ Azezi yetɔga la sɩra, kulla.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba yetɩ: «Nẽr-ẽna wã ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am, se'ere n soe la, a ka sakrɩ vo'osgo daarɛ sisgo.» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Nẽra n de tõon-be'ero dãana wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tõm tõon-yãlma wãna?» Tɩ welgre kẽ Farisi dõma la tẽŋasʋka.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma daan malɩn ɛɛra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, la dagɩ ẽn sã'am vo'osgo daarɛ sisgo la mã'a ĩyã, la de la ẽn ken wi'ira Nawẽnnɛ tɩ ẽŋa Sɔ, ɩta a mẽŋa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: La ẽn boe sore kulla la, tɩ a tõntõnɩba tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ bia la yeege mɛ.».

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Tɩ ba le soke bʋraa la yetɩ: «Fõn wʋn yele a yelle tɩ bẽm, ẽn basɛ tɩ fõn nini yẽta la?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «A de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.».

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ Dayʋa la ka tã ɩta sɛla sɛla la a mẽŋa nõorɛ. A ɩtɩ la ẽn yẽ tɩ a Sɔ ɩtɩ se'em na. Sɔ la n ɩtɩ se'em na, a Dayʋa la mẽ ɩtɩ la bɛla mẽŋa.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Tɩ a soke ba tɩ: «Wakat-kãna tɩ la sõɩ?» Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zaam wʋntɛɛŋa karf-ayɩla tɩ a ɔɔrɔ la basɛ ẽ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la ka bɔɔra tɩ ba sakɛ tɩ a daan de la fʋa dee yãŋa yẽta, tɩ ba wi a sɔ la a ma.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Sɔ la nõŋɛ a Dayʋa la mɛ, pa'alɛ ẽ sɛla wuu tɩ ẽŋa mẽŋa ɩtɩ, la a wʋn pa'alɛ ẽ na tõon-kãra n gãnnɩ bãna wã tɩ la di'ige ya.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: soke ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la yãma dayʋa la tɩ yãma yetɩ yãma dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna la fʋa la? La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ a yãŋa yẽta?».

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Tɩ a sɔ la bãŋɛ tɩ la de la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi yele ẽŋa tɩ a bia la yeege mɛ na. Tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma wuu bɔ Azezi sɩra.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: La wʋ Sɔ la n vo'orɩ kũm bɔ'ɔra ba vɔm na, Dayʋa la mẽ bɔ'ɔra sɛba tɩ a boorɩ vɔm na bɛla.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Tɩ a sɔ la a ma lerge yetɩ: «Tõma mi tɩ a de la tõma dayʋa, la tõma daan dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna la fʋa.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Ẽna de la bõn-yãlma bʋyi dãana tɩ Azezi ẽŋɛ Galile, ẽn daan ze'ele Zude lebe Galile la.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: La Sɔ la ka diti nẽra nẽra sarɩya, a basɛ sarɩyadia la wuu bɔ la a Dayʋa la.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: La tõma ka mi lan ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ a yãŋa yẽta, bɩɩ sɛka n botɩ a nini na yẽta. Soke-ya ẽ, tɩ a ka le dɛna bia, a mẽŋa wʋn tɔgɛ.».

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: tɩ nẽrba wuu wʋn nã'asra Dayʋa la, wʋ ba nã'asrɩ a Sɔ la se'em na. La sɛka n ka nã'asrɩ Dayʋa la, a ka nã'asrɩ Sɔ la n tõm ẽ na.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: A sɔ la a ma la n zotɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la ĩyã tɩ ba yele bɛla. Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la daan dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ kʋ mɛ yetɩ, nẽra sãn sakɛ tɩ Azezi de la Krisi, bãma wʋn yese ẽ mɛ ba wẽndeego la pʋam.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛka n selse mam yetɔga dee bɔ sɛka n tõm mam na sɩra, a tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. La ba kãn di a sarɩya. La a yese la kũm pʋam tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ a sɔ la a ma la yetɩ: «Soke-ya ẽ tɩ a ka de bia.».

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ wakatɛ sẽm mɛ, la a paam mɛ, tɩ sɛba n ka tarɩ vɔm na wʋn wõm Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la kõa, la sɛba n wõm na wʋn vɔa.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Tɩ ba le wi bʋra-sɛka n daan de fʋa la nõorɛ ayi pʋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tɔgɛ sɩra Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã. Tõma mi tɩ bʋra-ẽna de la tõon-be'ero dãana.».

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ vɔm a mẽŋa bɔ'ɔra se'em na, a bɔ a Dayʋa la mẽ vɔm na bɛla, tɩ a tara bɔ'ɔra.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «A sãn dɛna tõon-be'ero dãana, bɩɩ a dagɩ tõon-be'ero dãana, mam ka mi. Mam n mi sɛla de la, mam daan de la fʋa, la lɛɛlɛ wã mam yẽtɩ mɛ.».

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: La a bɔ ẽ nõorɛ mɛ tɩ a dita sarɩya, ẽn de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ĩyã.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «A ẽŋɛ fõn na bẽm? A ẽŋɛ la wãne botɩ fõn yẽta?».

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ mam n tɔgɛ se'em na di'ige ya, se'ere n soe la, wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ sɛba wuu n boe yɔɔm wʋn wõm a kõa.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam põn dẽŋɛ yele ya mɛ, tɩ ya ka selse, bẽm tɩ ya le bɔɔra tɩ ya wõm? Yãma mẽ boorɩ tɩ ya lebge la a karẽnbiisi bɩɩ?».

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: vo'oge ze'ele yɔɔrɔ pʋam yese. Tɩ sɛba n ẽŋɛ sõŋa yẽ vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la sɛba n ẽŋɛ be'em, a wʋn di ba sarɩya.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Tɩ ba tʋ ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Fõn de a karẽnbia, tõma de la Amoyisi karẽnbiisi.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Mam ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla la m mẽŋa nõorɛ. Mam diti sarɩya wʋ mam n wõm se'em Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na. La mam sarɩyadia masɛ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka eerɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ mam mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, la de la sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn tɔgɛ bɔ la Amoyisi, la ẽna, tõma ka mi ẽn ze'ele zẽ'e-sɛka.».

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Mam sãn bɔ m mẽŋa kaseto, a kãn dɛna kaseto asɩra.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la yele ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la yel-di'iŋa, yãma ka mi ẽn ze'ele zẽ'a, la ẽŋa n botɩ mam nini yẽta.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: La nẽra ayẽma n boe bɔ'ɔra mam kaseto, la mam mi tɩ a kaseto la tɩ a bɔ'ɔrɩ mam yelle la de la sɩra.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka selsrɩ tõon-be'ero dõma sosgo, la nẽra sãn pʋ'ʋsra ẽ, dee ɩta ẽn boorɩ se'em, a selsrɩ la ẽŋa dãana sosgo.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: La yãma tõm nẽra mɛ Azã zẽ'am, tɩ a tɔgɛ sɩra kaseto bɔ ya.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Halɩ pĩ'iluŋo, ba nãn ka wõm tɩ nẽra botɩ nẽra tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna fʋa nini yẽta.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Sãn dɛna mam, mam kaseto ka ze'etɩ nẽrsaala zẽ'am, la mam yele bɛla tɩ ya tã'agɛ yẽ la fãarɛ.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Bʋra-ẽna sãn ka ze'ele nɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a kãn yãŋɛ nɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla.».

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Azã daan ãn wʋ fɩtla n laŋɛ nẽegɛ, la yãma daan sakɛ mɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga wakatɛ fẽe mã'a la a peelem na.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ba dɔgɛ fõn na tõon-be'ero pʋam, tɩ fõn yetɩ fʋ pa'alɛ tõma?» Tɩ ba dige ẽ ba wẽndeem na.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: La mam tarɩ kaseto mɛ gãnna Azã dẽnnɛ na. La de la tõon-sɛba tɩ mam Sɔ bɔ mam tɩ m tõm na, tõon-bãma tɩ mam tõnnɩ na n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam kaseto pa'ala tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n tõm mam.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Tɩ Azezi wõm tɩ ba dige ẽ mɛ, yãŋa wa yẽ ẽ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn bɔ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sɩra mɛ?».

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: La mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na bɔ'ɔrɩ mam kaseto mɛ. Yãma nãn ka wõm a kõa, la ya nãn ka yẽ ẽ.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la de la ãne? Yele ma tɩ m bɔ ẽ sɩra.».

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: La a yetɔga la ka boe ya sũure pʋam, bɛla n soe tɩ ya ka bɔ sɛka tɩ a tõm na sɩra.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ yẽ ẽ mɛ, ẽŋa n tɔgrɩ la fʋ lɛ wã.».

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Yan eerɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yetɔga la võorɛ la, ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya wʋn yẽ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ a pʋam. La de la gõn-ẽŋa n tɔgrɩ mam yelle.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Tɩ bʋraa la yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam bɔ fʋ sɩra mɛ», dee ka dũma Azezi nẽŋam.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: La ya ka boorɩ tɩ ya wa'am mam zẽ'am wa yẽ vɔm na.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Mam wa'am dũnia wã zuo tɩ welgre n bɔna, tɩ fɔɔsɩ yẽta, dee tɩ sɛba n yẽtɩ la lebge fɔɔsɩ.».

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Mam ka eerɩ nã'asgɔ nẽrsaalba zẽ'am.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Tɩ Farisi dõma sɛba n daan boe mĩ na n wõm bɛla la, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma mẽ de la fɔɔsɩ bɩɩ?».

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: La mam mi ya, mam mi tɩ ya ka nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ.
