eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn de nɩ fɔɔsɩ, ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka boe ya zuo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma yetɩ yãma yẽtɩ mɛ, bɛla tɩ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ bɔna ya zuo.».

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Mam wa'am na m Sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ, tɩ ya ka to'oge mam. La ayẽma sãn wa'am na a mẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ, ẽŋa tɩ ya to'orɩ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Yãma to'osrɩ la pẽka taaba zẽ'am, dee ka ɛɛra pẽka n ze'etɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a zẽ'am. Yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ mam sɩra?.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Da tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ mam n wʋn yetɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ mam Sɔ la zẽ'am, la de la Amoyisi n wʋn yetɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ, ẽŋa tɩ ya te'ege.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Ya sãn bɔ nɩ Amoyisi sɩra, ya wʋn bɔ mam mẽ sɩra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a gʋlsɛ mam yelle mɛ.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: La ya sãn ka bɔ ẽŋa n gʋlsɛ bõn-sɛba la sɩra, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ mam yetɔga la sɩra?».

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: La Azezi daan yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n ka dolɩ zanõrɛ kẽ'era piisi denne pʋam, dee dɔla lalga kẽ'era, ẽŋa de la nayiga la fãara.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n dolɩ zanõrɛ la kẽ'era, ẽŋa n de piisi la dũnsĩina.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: La zanõrɛ la gu'ura yo'orɩ mɛ bɔ'ɔra ẽŋa. Tɩ piisi la mina a kõa, tɩ a wi'ira ẽn soe sɛba la yʋ'ʋra, botɩ ba dɔla ẽ yɛsra.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: La a sãn tarɩ a piisi la wuu yese, a tollɩ la ba nẽŋa sẽnna, tɩ ba dɔla ẽ, ban mi a kõa la ĩyã.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: La ba kãn malɩn doose sãana, ba wʋn zoe mɛ fõrgɛ dee basɛ ẽ, ban ka mi a kõa la ĩyã.».

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Azezi makɛ la makrɛ ẽna wã bɔ ba, la ba ka bãŋɛ ẽn tɔgɛ se'em bɔ ba la võorɛ.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ mam n de piisi denne na zanõrɛ.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Sɛba wuu n dẽŋɛ mam wa'am na de la nayigba la fãarba, la piisi la ka selse ba.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Mam n de zanõrɛ la. La nẽr-sɛka n dolɩ mam zẽ'am kẽ'era, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ. La a wʋn kẽ'era dee yɛsra, yẽta a dia.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Nayiga sẽm tɩ a zũ mɛ, la a kʋ, la a sã'am mã'a, la mam wa'am tɩ nẽrba n yẽ vɔm sõŋa sõŋa.».

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam n de dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ. Dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ sakɛ kũm mɛ a piisi ĩyã.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: La nẽra n tõnnɩ tɩ ba yɔɔra ẽ, tɩ a ka sɔna piisi la, a ka bɩsrɩ ba sõŋa, la a sãn yẽ saasaa n sẽm, a basrɩ piisi la mɛ dee zɔta. La saasaa la wʋn yõgɛ piisi la mɛ, botɩ ba wɩrgɛ.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a tõnnɩ tɔ'ɔra la yɔɔrɔ, a ka fablɩ piisi la yelle.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Mam n de dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ la, la mam mi m piisi la mɛ, tɩ ba mẽ mina mam.

eng: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I give up my life for the sheep.
local: wʋ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n mi mam, tɩ mam mẽ mina ẽ se'em na. Mam sakɛ kũm mɛ m piisi la ĩyã.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: La mam tarɩ piisi basɛba mɛ n ka boe denne wã pʋam. Dẽnɩ tɩ mam tarɩ ba wa'am, tɩ ba wõm mam kõa, lagɩm na bãna tɩ ba dɛna ayɩla, tɩ dũnsĩina ayɩla bɩsra ba.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Mam Sɔ nõŋɛ mam mɛ, mam n dɩkɛ m vɔm bɔ'ɔra nẽrsaalba, dee wʋn le to'oge ẽ na ĩyã.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Nẽra nẽra ka to'orɩ mam vɔm. Mam mẽŋa n wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ. Mam tarɩ pãŋa mɛ wʋn dɩkɛ m vɔm bɔ, dee tara pãŋa wʋn le to'oge ẽ. Mam Sɔ n bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em n bɛla.».

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Yetɔg-bãna ĩyã tɩ welgre daan le wa'am Zifdõma la tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Tɩ basɛba zozo'e yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ, a de la zɔlgɔ, bẽm tɩ ya sɛlsra ẽ?».

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Yetɔg-bãna wã dagɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ nẽra yetɔga. Kʋlkã'arga tã wʋn botɩ fʋa nini yẽta?».

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa Zifdõma Wẽnde-kãtɛ la malgrɛ tẽerɛ kibsa n daan boe Zerizalɛm. La daan de la ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ka gilgra zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Asalomõ võnana la.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma lagsɛ gilge ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wʋn basɛ tɩ tõma sẽ'ena ka paagɛ la dabʋrɛ? Fʋ sãn dɛna la Krisi la, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na, fʋ yele to sɩra sɩra.».

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam põn yele ya mɛ, tɩ ya ka bɔ mam sɩra. Tõon-sɛba tɩ mam tõnnɩ na m Sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ la, pa'alɩ mam n de sɛka mɛ.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: La yãma ka bɔ mam sɩra, yãma n dagɩ mam piisi la ĩyã.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: Mam piisi la wõnnɩ mam kõa mɛ, mam mi ba mɛ, la ba dolɩ mam mɛ.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: Mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ba la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, tɩ ba kãn malɩn sã'am, la nẽra ka tã wʋn fãagɛ ba mam nu'usum.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: Mam Sɔ la n bɔ mam bãma la, a gãnnɩ sɛla woo mɛ, nẽra ka tã wʋn fãagɛ ba mam Sɔ la nu'usum.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Mam, la mam Sɔ la de la ayɩla.».

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma le pĩ'ise kuga tɩ ba lobe ẽ kʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ botɩ mam tõm tõon-sõma zozo'e mɛ pa'alɛ ya. Tõon-sõma bãma pʋam, tõon-kãna ĩyã tɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya lobe mam na kuga?».

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La dagɩ tõon-sõma yelle tɩ tõma bɔɔra tɩ tɩ lobe fʋ la kuga kʋ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋr-sã'aŋɔ la ĩyã, fõn de nẽrsaala dee ẽŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa tɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.».

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ ya lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Ya de la wẽna.›.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Tɩ mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yetɔga la de la sɩra, la a ka tã wʋn sã'am. A wi'iri sɛba tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔga bɔ ba la tɩ wẽna.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: La mam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tõm dũnia zuo wã, yãma yele mam tɩ m sã'anɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam n yele tɩ mam de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la ĩyã?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Mam sãn ka tõnna m Sɔ tõoma, ya da bɔ mam sɩra.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: La mam sãn tõnna a tõoma, baa ya sãn ka bɔ mam sɩra, ya bɔ m tõoma la sɩra, tɩ ya tã'agɛ bãŋɛ, la ya sakɛ sɩra tɩ mam Sɔ la boe la mam lɛ mɛ, tɩ mam mẽ bɔna la ẽ.».

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Tɩ ba le zɩgra tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a põsɛ.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: La ẽn põsɛ la, a le dooge la Zʋrdẽ kʋlaa la doone, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Azã daan pɔsɛ bɔna mĩ misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, ka bɔna bilam.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am. Ba daan tɔgrɩ yetɩ: «Azã ka tõm tõon-yãlma baa ayɩla, la Azã n daan tɔgɛ bʋra-ẽna yelle se'em wuu la de la sɩra.».

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: La nẽrba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ bilam.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe Betani tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Alazaarɩ, tɩ bã'a yõkɛ ẽ. A tãpa Amaari la a Amartɩ daan kẽ'erɩ la tẽn-ẽŋa pʋam.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: La de la Amaari ẽŋa n daan ka'agɛ tɩdarɛ kaam ẽŋɛ Azezi nãma, dɩkɛ a zomto duuse ba la. Ẽŋa tã n de Alazaarɩ n bẽ'erɩ la.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Tɩ a tãpa la daan tõm tɩ ba ka yele Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fʋ sɩrɛ la bẽ'erɩ mɛ.».

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: La Azezi n wõm bɛla la, tɩ a yetɩ: «Bã'a-ẽna ba'asgɔ kãn dɛna kũm. La de la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ yẽ zẽkrɛ, la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn zẽkɛ bõn-ẽŋa pʋam.».

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: La Azezi daan nõŋɛ Amartɩ la a yɩbga, la Alazaarɩ mɛ.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: La ẽn wõm tɩ Alazaarɩ bẽ'erɩ mɛ na, a ken weege ẽn daan boe zẽ'e-sɛka la dabsa ayi.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Tɩ lebe-ya Zude.».

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ Zifdõma daan eerɩ fõn tɩ ba lobe la kuga kʋ. Dee tɩ fõn le yetɩ fʋ lebe bilam?».

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bulika tɩ la ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ, la de la karf-pia la ayi tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa boe. Nẽra sãn sẽnna wʋntɛɛŋa, a ka wẽ'erɩ a nãma, se'ere n soe la, dũnia peelem boe mɛ, tɩ a yẽta.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: La nẽra sãn sẽnna yʋ'ʋŋɔ, a wẽ'erɩ a nãma mɛ, se'ere n soe la, peelem ka boe tɩ a yẽta.».

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Azezi yele bɛla dee yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ sɩrɛ Alazaarɩ gĩsri mɛ, la mam wʋn sẽŋɛ ka nẽegɛ ẽ.».

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, a sãn gĩsra, a wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Azezi tɔgɛ ba la Alazaarɩ kũm yelle. La ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a tɔgrɩ la gẽem yelle.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Bɛla tɩ Azezi tɔgɛ ba vẽelga yetɩ: «Alazaarɩ ki mɛ.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: La mam n daan ka boe mĩ na, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ yãma ĩyã, tɩ ya wʋn bɔ mam sɩra. Tɩ sẽŋɛ-ya a zẽ'am.».

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Tɩ Atoma yele a karẽnbi-taaba yetɩ: «Tɩ mẽ sẽŋɛ-ya tɩ tɩ ka lagɩm ki.».

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Azezi n daan paagɛ la, tɩ ban laagɛ Alazaarɩ de la dabsa anaasɩ.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: La Betani daan lɛm Zerizalɛm masɛ wʋ kilomẽtrɩ atã.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e wa'am tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsɛ Amartɩ, la Amaari ba tã la kũm.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: La Amartɩ n wõm tɩ Azezi sẽm mɛ na, a daan yese mɛ tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ. Dee tɩ Amaari weege zẽa yire.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Tɩ Amartɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn daan sãn boe nɩ kalam, mam tã wã kãn ki.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam ken mina tɩ bõn-sɛka woo tɩ fõn wʋn sose Nawẽnnɛ, a wʋn bɔ fɔ.».

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tã la wʋn vo'oge basɛ kũm.».

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Tɩ Amartɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam mi tɩ a wʋn vo'oge dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ, tɩ kũm wuu wʋn vo'oge la.».

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n de kũm vo'ore dãana, la vɔm dãana. Sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra, baa a sãn ki, a wʋn vo'oge.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: La sɛka woo n vɔa dee bɔ mam sɩra, kũm kãn tã'agɛ ẽ wakatɛ wuu. Fõn bɔ bõn-ẽna sɩra?».

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Tɩ Amartɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe, Zuudãana, mam bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn de la Krisi, Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la n sẽm dũnia zuo la.».

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: La ẽn yele bɛla la, a fõrgɛ mɛ, sẽŋɛ ka wi a yɩbga Amaari, sʋgɛ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama wa'am mɛ, dee wi'ira fõn.».

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: La Amaari n wõm bɛla la, a isge mɛ tɔtɔ wɛ'ɛsa a zẽ'am.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: La Azezi daan nãn ka paagɛ tẽŋa la pʋam, a ken bɔna la Amartɩ n daan tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ zẽ'e-sɛka la.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n wa'am wa pʋ'ʋsɛ kũm na, dee bɔna la Amaari lɛ yire la pʋam na, yẽ tɩ a isge tɔtɔ yɛsra, tɩ ba tẽ'esra tɩ a we'esɩ la yɔɔm tɩ a ka kaasɛ, tɩ ba dɔla ẽ.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: La Amaari n paagɛ Azezi n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, yẽ ẽ na, a yigle mɛ a nẽŋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, fõn daan sãn boe nɩ kalam, mam tã wã kãn ki.».

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: La Azezi n daan yẽ tɩ a kellɩ mɛ, la Zifdõma la n dolɩ ẽ na mẽ kellɩ mɛ na, tɩ a sũure sã'am halɩ, tɩ la dãam ẽ zozo'e.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma laagɛ ẽ na bɛ?» Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, wa'am wa bɩsɛ.».

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Tɩ Azezi kɛlla.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma la n yẽ bɛla la, yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya ẽn nõŋɛ ẽ se'em.».

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Tɩ basɛba yele yetɩ: «Dagɩ bʋra-ẽna n daan botɩ fʋa la nini yẽta la, a ka tã wʋn basɛ nɩ tɩ ẽna mẽ da ki?».

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Tɩ Azezi sũure le sã'am halɩ, tɩ a wɛ'ɛsa yɔɔm na. Yɔɔgɔ la daan de la tãmpĩa yoko, tɩ kugre lige a nõorɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Lakɛ-ya kugre la.» Tɩ sɛka n ki la tã Amartɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, a põn põ'ogɛ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, la de la dabsa anaasɩ n wãna tɩ a boe yɔɔm.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka yele fʋ yetɩ, fʋ sãn bɔ mam sɩra, fʋ wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa?».

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Tɩ ba lakɛ kugre la. Tɩ Azezi gorge saazuum yetɩ: «M Sɔ, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ, fõn selse mam pʋ'ʋsgɔ la.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: Mam mi tɩ daarɛ woo fʋ selsrɩ mam pʋ'ʋsgɔ mɛ. La mam yele bɛla la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ wã ĩyã, tɩ ba wʋn bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn tõm mam.».

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: A yele bɛla dee tɔgɛ la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Alazaarɩ, yese!».

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Tɩ sɛka n daan ki la isge yese. Tɩ tãna vile a nãma la a nu'usi, la tãnnɛ ayẽma n vile a nẽŋa. Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Lorge-ya tãna la, dee basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ.».

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: La Zifdõma sɛba n daan wa'am Amaari zẽ'am wa yẽ Azezi n ẽŋɛ se'em na, ba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Tɩ basɛba sẽŋɛ Farisi dõma zẽ'am ka yele ba Azezi n ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma lagsɛ ba sarɩyaditba kẽma yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Bʋra-ẽna wã tõnnɩ la tõon-yãlma zozo'e.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Tɩ sãn basɛ ẽ tɩ a ɩta bɛla, nẽrba wuu wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ, la Rom dõma wʋn wa'am wa sã'am tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ wã, la tɩ so'olʋm wã wuu mɛ.».

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akayifu n daan de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yʋʋn-ẽŋa, yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka mi sɛla sɛla.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Ya ka mi tɩ bʋraa ayɩla sãn ki nẽrba la ĩyã, n sõɩ tɩ buuri la wuu n wʋn sã'am?».

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: La, la dagɩ a mẽŋa yɛm tɩ a yele bɛla, la de la ẽn daan de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yʋʋn-ẽŋa la tɩ a to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Azezi wa ki bɔ bãma buuri la.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: La, la dagɩ bãma buuri la mã'a ĩyã tɩ la masɛ tɩ a ki, la de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma n wɩrgɛ zẽ'esɩ wuu la ĩyã, tɩ a yetɩ a lagsɛ ba tɩ ba lebge buuri ayɩla.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: La sẽŋɛ la daar-ẽŋa, tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Azezi ka le ɛɛra Zifdõma tẽŋasʋka tɩ ba yẽta ẽ. A fõrgɛ bilam sẽŋɛ la tẽnsɩ n lɛm mõom, ka kẽ'era tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Efrayim la a karẽnbiisi la.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: La Zifdõma Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa n daan lɛm, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e ze'ele tẽn-pɩgsɩ pʋam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm lan dẽŋɛ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, tɩ ba ka malgɛ ba mẽŋa wʋ ba sisgo n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Tɩ ba ɛɛra Azezi, sẽŋɛ ka zɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Yãma tẽ'esɛ la dẽna? A sɩrɩ kãn wa'am kibsa wã?».

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma la daan bɔ la nõorɛ tɩ nẽra sãn mina ẽn boe zẽ'a, a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n lagsɛ zo'oge mikra taaba, nẽera taaba nãma, tɩ Azezi dẽŋɛ yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Guuse ya mẽŋa la Farisi dõma pʋlibsum na n ãn wʋ dãbɩllɛ n botɩ dãam wuu kɛlla la.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Bõn-sɛka woo n lile wʋn lilge, la bõn-sɛka woo n sʋgɛ wʋn puke.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Bɛla, bõn-sɛka woo tɩ ya tɔgɛ lika pʋam, ba wʋn tɔgɛ ẽ na peelem pʋam tɩ nẽrba wuu wõm, la bõn-sɛka tɩ ya waasʋm deem na, ba wʋn zom na gɔsgɔ mõolɛ tɩ nẽrba wuu wõm.».

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: «M sɩrdõma, mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya da zɔta dabeem la sɛba n kʋʋrɩ ĩyã mã'a, dee ka tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla pa'asɛ la.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Mam wʋn pa'alɛ ya lan masɛ tɩ ya zɔta sɛka. Zɔta-ya Nawẽnnɛ n tã wʋn kʋ, dee tara pãŋa wʋn kẽ'esɛ ya bug-tẽŋa la. Asɩra, zɔta-ya Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Ba ka koosrɩ mõom niinto banuu la lig-kõba bayi mã'a? La Nawẽnnɛ ka yĩnni baa ayɩla yelle.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: La baa yãma zuugo zomto, Nawẽnnɛ mi ba wuu kãllɛ mɛ. Bɛla ĩyã, da zɔta-ya dabeem, tɩ yãma tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna niinto zozo'e mɛ.».

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: «La mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n ze'ele nẽrba nẽŋam yetɩ ẽŋa de la mam nẽra, mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma nẽŋam yetɩ a de la mam nẽra.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n sɩ'ɩsɛ nẽrba nẽŋam tɩ ẽŋa dagɩ mam nẽra, mam mẽ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma nẽŋam tɩ a dagɩ mam nẽra.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n tɔgɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la be'em, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ a tõon-be'ero taalɛ, la nẽr-sɛka woo n pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, Nawẽnnɛ kãn basɛ a tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Ba sãn yõgɛ ya sẽŋɛ ba wẽndeto pʋam, bɩɩ na'adõma nẽŋam, bɩɩ kɛ'ɛndõma nẽŋam tɩ ba di ya sarɩya, da fabla-ya yan wʋn lerge se'em, bɩɩ yan wʋn tɔgɛ se'em to'oge ya mẽŋa.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wʋn pa'alɛ ya wakat-ẽŋa yan wʋn tɔgɛ se'em.».

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ nẽra ayɩla n boe nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, yele m kẽema tɩ a dɩkɛ tõma sɔ n ki dee bõn-sɛba la, pʋɩ bɔ ma m bõnɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Ãne n bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m dɛna ya sarɩyadita, bɩɩ m pʋta ya bõntarsõm bɔ'ɔra ya?».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Dee yele nẽrba la wuu yetɩ: «Guusra-ya ya mẽŋa, da ɛɛra bõntarsõm zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ nẽra n tarɩ bõntarsõm zo'oge ĩyã tɩ a tarɩ yõvore.».

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: A yele bɛla, dee makɛ la makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Bʋraaga n daan boe dɛna bõntarsa, tɩ a vatɔ bõnkɔɔla mãalɛ sõŋa.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: tɩ a tẽ'esɛ a pʋtẽ'erʋm yetɩ: ‹Zẽ'a ka boe tɩ m wʋn sũm m bõnkɔɔla la. Bɛla ĩyã, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?›.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Yãŋa yele a mẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Mam mi mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em. Mam wʋn wõrɛ m bã la mɛ, malgɛ mẽ bã-kãra, sũm m bõnkɔɔla, la m sɛla woo.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Dee yele m sũurum yetɩ: M tarɩ bõntarsõm zo'oge, la wʋn seke ma paagɛ yʋʋma zozo'e. Bɛla m wʋn vo'ose mɛ di dee yũ, dee ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.›.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Gergo wã, yʋ'ʋŋ-ẽna wã m wʋn to'oge fʋ vɔm na. La ãne n wʋn sɔna bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ bĩŋe la?› ».

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: La Azezi le yetɩ: «La de la bɛla la nẽr-sɛka woo n lagsrɩ bõntarsõm bĩŋra a mẽŋa ĩyã, dee ka tara sɛla sɛla Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bɛla ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya da fabla ya vɔm yelle, ya wʋn di sɛla, bɩɩ ya wʋn yɛ fu-sɛba la yelle.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Vɔm yelle gãnnɩ dia mɛ, tɩ ĩyã yelle mẽ gãnna futo.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya gõorɔ, ba ka bʋtɩ, ba ka lagsrɩ bõnkɔɔla, la ba ka tarɩ bãarɛ bɩɩ dia bĩŋre zẽ'a, la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ ba dia. La yãma ka tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna bõn-ẽgla zo'oge sɩra?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ãne n boe ya pʋam n tã wʋn pa'asɛ a vɔm woglʋm fẽfẽe ẽn fablɩ la ĩyã?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: La yãma sãn ka tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ bɛla n de bõn-pɩka, bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma fabla bõn-sɛba n weege la yelle?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya mõ-puuro n ãn sõŋa se'em, ba ka tõnnɩ, ba mẽ ka wʋgrɩ futo. La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, baa naba Asalomõ na'am n yʋʋn tarɩ pãŋa la, a yʋʋn ka tã'agɛ yɛ fuugo n ãn sõŋa wʋ mõ-puugo ayɩla.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ sãn botɩ mõorɔ n boe zĩna dee tɩ ba wʋn yõ ba bugum beere ãna sõŋa bɛla, a kãn bɩsɛ ya yelle sõŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Bẽm tɩ yãma ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra zo'oge?.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ yan wʋn di sɛla, bɩɩ yan wʋn yũ sɛla yelle pakɛ ya.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: La de la nẽr-sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n eerɩ bõn-bãma wuu. La yãma Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mi tɩ yãma boorɩ bõn-bãma wuu mɛ.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Ɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na gãnna wuu, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya bõn-bãma pa'asɛ.».

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: «M nẽrba, ya ka zo'oge, la da zɔta-ya dabeem, se'ere n soe la, la paagɛ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm mɛ tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ ya a so'olʋm.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Koose-ya yãma n tarɩ sɛla la dɩkɛ ligri la bɔ nasdõma. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya eerɩ la bõntarsõm n ka sã'anɩ. Ɛ-ya bõntarsõm tɩ ba bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire wakatɛ wuu, bilam tɩ nayigba kãn tã'agɛ zũ, tɩ bõn-mõgla mẽ kãn tã'agɛ sã'am ba.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ya bõntarsõm n boe zẽ'a na, ya pʋtẽ'era mẽ boe la bilam.».

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Azezi le yetɩ: «Mãasʋm-ya lʋ ya sagãna ya sɩɩsɩ tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ tõm, dee yõ ya fɩtladõma.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: ãna wʋ nẽrba n gu'uri ba zuudãana n wʋn ze'ele pɔgdire kibsa wa'am, tɩ a sãn wa'am wa wi, tɩ ba yo'oge yaŋa tɔtɔ bɔ ẽ.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Tõntõn-sɛba tɩ ba zuudãana wʋn paam tɩ ba ze gu'ura ẽ, tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra tɩ, a wʋn basɛ tɩ ba zĩ'ire, tɩ zuudãana la mẽŋa mãasʋm wa pʋɩ ba dia.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: A sãn wa'am sõnsʋa, bɩɩ bewiine, wa yẽ tɩ ba ken gu'ura ẽ, ba tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ yidãana sãn mina wakat-sɛka tɩ nayiga wʋn wa zũ ẽ, a wʋn gu'ura a yire mɛ kãn basɛ tɩ nayiga la kẽ zũ ẽ.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Bɛla, yãma mẽ mãasʋm zɛ sõŋa, tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sẽm na wakat-sɛka tɩ yãma ka tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a wʋn wa'am na.».

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn makɛ makrɛ ẽna wã la tõma mã'a bɩɩ, nẽrba wuu?».

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ãne n de tõntõn-sõŋɔ n tarɩ yɛm? La de la ẽŋa tɩ a zuudãana wʋn yetɩ a bɩsra a yire yelle, bɔ'ɔra a tõntõnɩba dia wʋ lan masɛ, dee sẽŋɛ sore.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Zuudãana la sãn leme wa yẽ tɩ tõntõnna la tõm wʋ ẽŋa n yele se'em na, tõntõnna la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra tɩ, a zuudãana la wʋn dɩkɛ ẽŋa n tarɩ sɛla la wuu bɔ ẽ mɛ tɩ a bɩsra.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: La tõntõnna la sãn tẽ'esɛ yetɩ, ẽŋa zuudãana la yuuri mɛ ka kulna, yãŋa wẽ'era tõntõn-bʋraasɩ, la tõntõn-pɔgsɩ, dita, dee yũura bugra.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: a zuudãana la wʋn kulum na daar-sɛka tɩ a ka tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a wʋn wa'am, la wakat-sɛka tɩ a ka mi, wa di'ige ẽ. La a wʋn dige ẽ mɛ botɩ a lagɩm na sɛba n ka bɔ ẽŋa sɩra la yẽ sɩbgrɛ.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Tõntõn-sɛka n mi a zuudãana n boorɩ se'em, dee ka mãasʋm bɩɩ ka tõm ẽn boorɩ se'em na, ba wʋn wẽ ẽ mɛ zozo'e.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: La tõntõn-sɛka n ka mi a zuudãana n boorɩ se'em, dee ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ ba wẽ ẽ, ba wʋn wẽ ẽ na fẽe. Sɛka tɩ ba bɔ ẽ zo'oge, ba mẽ wʋn soke ẽ yɛla zozo'e yelle mɛ, la sɛka tɩ ba gu'ule ẽ zo'oge, ba mẽ wʋn soke ẽ halɩ yɛla zozo'e yelle gãnna mɛ.».

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: «Mam wa'am tɩ m tʋʋrɛ la bugum dũnia wã zuo, la mam boorɩ nɩ mɛ zozo'e tɩ bugum na põn darɛ dita.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: La dẽnɩ tɩ mam yẽ nãmsgɔ zozo'e wʋ mam mise la ko'om pʋam na, la a yelle la wʋn nãmsɛ mam mɛ halɩ tɩ la wa ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam wa'am tɩ m botɩ nẽrba nõorɛ n dɛna ayɩla dũnia zuo bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, mam yetɩ ya tɩ mam tarɩ la welgre wa'am.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: La, lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã wɛ'ɛsa, nẽrba banuu sãn bɔna yire ayɩla pʋam, nẽrba batã wʋn welge mɛ sisra nẽrba bayi la, tɩ nẽrba bayi la mẽ sisra nẽrba batã la.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: La sɔ wʋn sise a dayʋa, tɩ dayʋa mẽ sise a sɔ. La ma wʋn sise a pɔyʋa, tɩ pɔyʋa mẽ sise a ma. La dayʋa pɔga wʋn sise a sɩra ma, tɩ sɩra ma mẽ sise a dayʋa pɔga.».

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Ya sãn yẽ tɩ wara sobge bɔbrɛ ayẽma, ya yetɩ mɛ tɔtɔ tɩ saaga wʋn ni mɛ, tɩ a sɩrɩ ni.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: La ya sãn le yẽ tɩ kʋsebgo vugri bɔbrɛ ayẽma, ya yetɩ mɛ tɩ wɔlgɔ lɛm mɛ, tɩ wɔlgɔ wʋn sɩrɩ paagɛ.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Pʋlibsum dõma wã! Yãma bɩsrɩ mɛ mina tẽŋa, la saazuum yɛla n ãn se'em. La bẽm tɩ yãma ka mina bõn-sɛba n ɩtɩ lɛɛlɛ wã võorɛ bɔkɛ?».

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: «Bẽm tɩ ya mẽŋa ka tẽ'esɛ bãŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ ya wʋn ẽŋɛ bɔkɛ?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Fʋ sãn tara yelle la nẽra, tɩ a tarɩ fʋ wɛ'ɛsa sarɩyadia zẽ'am, mõrgɛ tɩ ya mãalɛ yelle la sore, tɩ a da dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ sarɩyadita, tɩ a tɔgɛ fʋ yetɔga la dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ gu'ura, tɩ a kẽ'esɛ fɔ yʋ'a deem.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra, tɩ fʋ kãn yẽ sore yese yʋ'a deem na, halɩ tɩ fʋ wʋn wa yɔ fʋ sãnnɛ na wuu ba'asɛ.».

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Lan dẽŋɛ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, Azezi mi mɛ tɩ ẽŋa wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ ẽŋa wʋn fõrgɛ dũnia sẽŋɛ a Sɔ zẽ'am. La a daan nõŋɛ a nẽrba la n boe dũnia mɛ, a nõŋɛ ba la halɩ nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan boe mĩ dita la zaanõorɛ dia. La Asũtãana daan põn botɩ Asimõ dayʋa Azudaasɩ Iskariyo tẽ'esɛ a sũurum tɩ ẽŋa wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: La Azezi daan mi tɩ ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am, la ẽŋa lebrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la a mi tɩ ẽŋa Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ la ẽŋa nu'usum.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Tɩ a isge dia la zẽ'am yɛɛgɛ a fu-sɛka n boe zuum na, dee dɩkɛ tãnnɛ lʋ a sɩa.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Yãŋa pʋrgɛ ko'om ẽŋɛ laaga pʋam, pɛɛra a karẽnbiisi la nãma, dɩkɛ tãnnɛ la n lʋ a sɩa la duusra ba.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Tɩ a daan paagɛ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ zẽ'am, tɩ a soke ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, fõn yetɩ fʋ peege la mam nãma?».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn ka mi mam ɩtɩ se'em wã võorɛ lɛɛlɛ, la fʋ wʋn wa bãŋɛ.».

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ! Fõn kãn peege mam nãma.» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam sãn ka peege fo, fʋ ka le tara pʋrɛ la mam.».

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, da peege m nãma la mã'a, po peege m nu'usi la m zuugo.».

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛka n sɔ ĩyã ka le bɔɔra tɩ a sɔ, sãn dagna a nãma mã'a tɩ a wʋn peege, se'ere n soe la, a ĩyã wuu dẽkɛ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa. Ya dẽkɛ mɛ ãna sõŋa sõŋa, la, la dagɩ ya wuu.».

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Azezi daan mi sɛka n yetɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na mɛ, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a yetɩ, dagɩ ya wuu n ãn sõŋa.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: La Azezi n peege ba nãma kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a le yɛ la a fuugo la, lebe ka zĩ'ire dia la zẽ'am, yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma bãŋɛ mam n ẽŋɛ se'em na võorɛ?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Yãma wi'iri mam tɩ Karẽnsãama, la Zuudãana, ya yele sõŋa. Mam de la bɛla.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Mam n de ya Zuudãana, la ya Karẽnsãama la sãn peege ya nãma, yãma mẽ masɛ mɛ tɩ ya pɛɛra taaba nãma.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: Se'ere n soe la, la de la mãmsgɔ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ bɔ ya, tɩ ya mẽ ɩta wʋ mam n ẽŋɛ ya se'em na.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ tõntõnna ka gãnnɩ a zuudãana, la sɛka tɩ ba tõm na ka gãnnɩ sɛka n tõm ẽ na.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Yan mi bõn-bãma la, ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya de la zu-yẽlʋm dõma.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: La dagɩ ya wuu yelle tɩ mam tɔgrɩ, mam mi sɛba tɩ mam looge la mɛ, la dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-sɛba n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-sɛka n diti la mam na, wẽrgɛ mɛ zɛbra la mam.›.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: Mam dẽŋɛ yɛta ya mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, tɩ la nãn ka ẽŋɛ. Tɩ la sãn wa ẽŋɛ, ya wʋn bɔ sɩra tɩ mam de la sɛka tɩ mam de la.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛka n to'orɩ sɛka tɩ mam tõm na, a to'orɩ la mam. La sɛka n to'orɩ mam na, a to'orɩ la sɛka n tõm mam na.».

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la, sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ ẽ zozo'e, tɩ a yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya nẽra ayɩla n wʋn wa pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.».

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la daan bɩsra taaba, ka mina ẽn tɔgrɩ nẽr-sɛka yelle.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la ayɩla, sɛka tɩ a nõŋɛ la daan zĩ la Azezi sakʋkɔ.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ kãbsɛ ẽ tɩ a soke Azezi bãŋɛ la de la ãn yelle tɩ a tɔgrɩ la.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Tɩ karẽnbi-ẽŋa dente Azezi, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, ãn de ẽ?».

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «A de la sɛka tɩ mam wʋn suke borborɩ girgo bɔ ẽ na.» A dɩkɛ borborɩ girgo la suke bɔ la Azudaasɩ n de Asimõ Iskariyo dayʋa.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Tɩ Azudaasɩ to'oge borborɩ la, tɩ Asũtãana kẽ a sũurum. Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ se'em na, ẽŋɛ ẽ tɔtɔ.».

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: La sɛba n daan lagɩm bɔna mĩ dita la, nẽra nẽra ka bãŋɛ se'ere n soe tɩ a yele ẽ bɛla.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: La Azudaasɩ n daan zɛɛrɩ ligri tãmpɔkɔ la, nẽrba basɛba tẽ'esɛ tɩ Azezi yele ẽ tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka da la bõn-sɛba tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ kibsa la, bɩɩ a bɔ nasdõma sɛla.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Azudaasɩ n daan to'oge borborɩ la, dee kɔ'ɔn yese. La daan de la yʋ'ʋŋɔ.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Azudaasɩ n yese la, tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Lɛɛlɛ wã Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn yẽ a na'am pãŋa, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn yẽ nã'asgɔ a ĩyã.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sãn yẽ nã'asgɔ a ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa wʋn nã'asɛ ẽ. Lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ a wʋn nã'asɛ ẽ.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: M kɔma, mam ken bɔna la ya la wakatɛ fẽe mã'a. La ya wʋn ɛ mam, la mam yetɩ ya lɛɛlɛ wã wʋ mam n daan yele Zifdõma se'em na: Ya ka tã wʋn wa'am zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam we'esɩ la.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ya la nõor-paalga: Nõŋɛ-ya taaba. Wʋ mam n nõŋɛ ya se'em na, ya mẽ nõŋɛ taaba bɛla.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Ya sãn nõŋɛ taaba, nẽrba wuu wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ ya de la mam karẽnbiisi.».

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ soke Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn we'esɩ la bɛ?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a na, fʋ ka tã wʋn doose mam sẽŋɛ lɛɛlɛ wã. La fʋ wʋn wa doose mam poore wa'am.».

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, bẽm ẽŋɛ tɩ mam kãn tã'agɛ doose fõn sẽŋɛ lɛɛlɛ wã? Mam sakɛ tɩ m ki mɛ fõn ĩyã.».

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wʋn sakɛ tɩ fʋ ki mam ĩyã? Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam.».

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa daan deege la dabsa ayoobɩ la a paagɛ, tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Betani, ẽn vo'oge Alazaarɩ tẽn-sɛka la.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Tɩ ba daan ẽŋɛ dia bilam bɔ ẽ, tɩ Amartɩ daan pʋta dia la. La Alazaarɩ daan lagɩm na nẽrba la zẽa la Azezi dita.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Tɩ Amaari dɩkɛ tɩdarɛ ka-sõma ligri n zo'oge, tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «naarɩ» n paagɛ lɩtrɩ pʋsʋka wa'am wa ka'agɛ ẽŋɛ Azezi nãma, dɩkɛ a zomto duuse ba. Tɩ yire la wuu lebge tɩdarɛ kaam na yũuŋo.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbia ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azudaasɩ Iskariyo, sɛka n wʋn wa pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na, yetɩ:.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: «Tɩ sãn koose nɩ tɩdarɛ kaam wã lig-kõba kɔbstã, tɩ kãn dɩkɛ ligri la bɔ nasdõma?».

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Ẽn yele bɛla la, dagɩ tɩ a fablɩ la nasdõma yelle, la de la ẽn de nayiga, la ẽŋa n tarɩ ba ligri tãmpɔkɔ, zũura sɛla tɩ ba sũm a pʋam na.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya pɔka la bãalam, tɩ a dẽŋɛ ẽŋɛ la ba wʋn ẽŋɛ mam se'em mam laarɛ daarɛ.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Nasdõma boe la ya lɛ mɛ daarɛ woo, la mam kãn bɔna la ya daarɛ woo.».

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e wõm tɩ Azezi boe la Betani. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ mĩ, la dagɩ Azezi mã'a ĩyã tɩ ba sẽŋɛ, ba sẽŋɛ tɩ ba mẽ ka yẽ la Alazaarɩ tɩ Azezi daan vo'oge la.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la bãm yetɩ, bãma mẽ wʋn kʋ Alazaarɩ mɛ.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: tɩ la de la ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e basɛ bãma dee bɔ Azezi sɩra.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n wa'am kibsa la wõm tɩ Azezi sẽm Zerizalɛm.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Tɩ ba kɔ karkɔnsɩ võorɔ tʋ'ʋsra ẽ, dee tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ozaana! Pẽgɛ-ya nẽr-sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la, ẽŋa n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma naba la.».

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ bõn-biire bã ẽ wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ:.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: «Siyõ nẽrba, da zɔta-ya dabeem. Bɩsɛ-ya, ya naba n bã bõn-biire sẽna la.».

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la daan nãn ka bãŋɛ yel-ẽŋa võorɛ. La Azezi n yãŋa wa yẽ na'am pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, tɩ ba tẽegɛ tɩ bõn-ẽŋa n gʋlsɛ a yelle, la ba daan ẽŋɛ la bɛla bɔ ẽ.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n wa bɔna Azezi zẽ'am, wakat-sɛka tɩ a wi Alazaarɩ tɩ a vo'oge ze'ele yɔɔgɔ pʋam yese la, daan pa'alɛ nẽrba la ẽn daan ẽŋɛ se'em mɛ.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ, ban wõm tɩ a tõm na tõon-yãlm-ẽŋa la ĩyã.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Bɛla tɩ Farisi dõma yɛta taaba yetɩ: «Ya yẽ ya, ya ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla. Bɩsɛ-ya, nẽrba wuu dolɩ ẽ mɛ.».

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: La Grɛkɩdõma n daan boe nẽr-sɛba n sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm kibsa tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ na pʋam.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Bãma n sẽŋɛ Afilipi n ze'ele Betisayida n boe Galile so'olʋm pʋam na zẽ'am, ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Kɛ'ɛma, tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ yẽ la Azezi.».

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Tɩ Afilipi sẽŋɛ ka yele A'ãndre, tɩ A'ãndre la Afilipi doose taaba sẽŋɛ ka yele Azezi.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Wakatɛ la paagɛ mɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn yẽ a na'am pãŋa.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ba sãn ka bʋrɛ ke-biire tɩ a yɛm bule yese dee tɩ a mẽŋa põ'ogɛ, ke-bi-ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a n wʋn bɔna, la ba sãn bʋrɛ ẽ tɩ a yɛm na bule yese dee tɩ a mẽŋa ki, a wʋn wɔm biɛ zozo'e.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe vɔm, la nẽr-sɛka n ka nõŋɛ a vɔm dũnia wã zuo, a wʋn tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: La nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a tõm bɔ mam, dẽnɩ tɩ a dɔla mam. La mam n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, mam tõntõnna mẽ wʋn bɔna la bilam. La nẽra sãn tõnna bɔ'ɔra mam, mam Sɔ wʋn pẽgɛ ẽ.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã mam sũure sã'am mɛ, la m wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Mam wʋn yetɩ: M Sɔ, sõŋɛ ma tɩ m põsɛ nãmsgɔ ẽna wã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la de la yel-ẽna ĩyã tɩ mam wa'am tɩ m nãmsɛ.».

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: A yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ, nã'asɛ fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ.» Tɩ kõa ze'ele saazuum yetɩ: «Mam nã'asɛ m yʋ'ʋrɛ mɛ, la mam wʋn le nã'asɛ ẽ.».

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ze bilam na wõm, yetɩ: «Saaga n dãmɛ.» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Malɛka n tɔgɛ la ẽ.».

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «La dagɩ mam ĩyã tɩ kõ-ẽŋa tɔgɛ, la de la yãma ĩyã.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã de la dũnia wã sarɩyadia wakatɛ. Lɛɛlɛ wã ba wʋn dige dũnia wã naba basɛ yẽŋa.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: La ba sãn wa zẽkɛ mam saazuum, mam wʋn tarɩ nẽrba wuu wa'am mam zẽ'am.».

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Azezi yele bɛla pa'alɛ la ẽŋa wʋn ki se'em.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ pʋam pa'alɛ tõma yetɩ, Krisi wʋn bɔna la wakatɛ wuu, la bẽm tɩ fõn yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ ba zẽkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la saazuum? Ãne n de Nẽrsaala Dayɔ-ẽŋa?».

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Peelem ken bɔna la ya lɛ mɛ wakatɛ fẽe. Peelem wã n ken bɔna la ya wakat-sɛka la, sẽnna-ya tɩ lika da wa liŋe ya. Sɛka n sẽnnɩ lika pʋam ka mi ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'a.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Wakat-sɛka tɩ peelem ken bɔna la yãma la, bɔ-ya sɛka n de peelem na sɩra, tɩ ya lebge peelem na kɔma.» Azezi yele la bɛla dee fõrgɛ ba zẽ'am, sẽŋɛ ka sʋgɛ.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Baa la Azezi n ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma zozo'e nẽrba la nẽŋam na, ba ka bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Zuudãana, ãne n bɔ tõma kõa la sɩra? La Zuudãana la pãŋa puke pa'alɛ la ãne?».

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Ba ka tã wʋn bɔ sɩra, se'ere n soe la, A'ezayi yʋʋn le yetɩ:.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: «Nawẽnnɛ pĩ ba nini mɛ, tɩ ba da yẽta la ba nini na, dee lige ba pʋtẽ'era, tɩ ba da wõm a yetɔga la võorɛ. Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm tɩ a bɔ ba laafɛ.».

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: A'ezayi yʋʋn yele la bɛla, se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn yẽ la Azezi na'am pãŋa, tɩ a yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yelle.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Baa la bɛla wuu, Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la pʋam nẽrba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ, la Farisi dõma la ĩyã ba ka sakɛ yele, ba zotɩ mɛ tɩ ba wʋn dige ba ba wẽndeego la pʋam.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Ba daan nõŋɛ la nẽrsaalba pẽka gãnna Nawẽnnɛ pẽka.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Azezi tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra, la dagɩ mam mã'a tɩ a bɔ'ɔrɩ sɩra, a bɔ'ɔrɩ la sɛka n tõm mam na sɩra.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: La sɛka n yẽ mam, yẽ la sɛka n tõm mam na.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Mam de la peelem n wa'am dũnia zuo, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra kãn bɔna lika pʋam.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: La nẽra sãn wõm mam yetɔga, dee ka sakɛ yetɔga la, la dagɩ mam n wʋn di a sarɩya, se'ere n soe la, mam ka wa'am tɩ m di dũnia dõma sarɩya, mam wa'am tɩ m fãagɛ ba mɛ.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Sɛka n zagsɛ mam, ka sakɛ mam yetɔga la, a tarɩ sɛka n wʋn di a sarɩya. La de la yetɔga la tɩ mam tɔgɛ la n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ a ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam ka tɔgrɩ la m mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ. La de la mam Sɔ n tõm mam na mẽŋa n bɔ mam nõorɛ mam wʋn yele se'em, la mam wʋn tɔgɛ sɛla yelle.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: La mam mi tɩ sɛka n sakɛ a nõorɛ la tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. La bõn-sɛba tɩ mam yetɩ la, la de la mam Sɔ n yele mam se'em na n bɛla tɩ mam yele.».

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Azezi zãsʋm ba yetɩ: «Mam de la viinye tɩa mẽŋa mẽŋa. La mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n de viinye tɩa la dãana.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Wil-sɛka n puse mam zuo dee ka wɔnna biɛ, a wãarɩ ẽ mɛ basra. La wil-sɛka woo n wɔnnɩ biɛ, a yorsrɩ ẽ mɛ tɩ a ãna sõŋa malɩn wɔnna biɛ zɔ'ɔra.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Yãma põn ãna sõŋa mɛ, yetɔg-sɛba tɩ mam tɔgɛ bɔ ya la ĩyã.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Bɔna-ya la mam lɛ, tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la ya. Wʋ tɩa wille n ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ a to'ore tɩ a sãn ka bɔna la tɩa la, bɛla, ya mẽ sãn ka bɔna la mam, ya ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Mam n de tɩa la, tɩ yãma dɛna wila la. Sɛka n boe la mam, tɩ mam bɔna la ẽ, ẽŋa dãana tõnnɩ tõon-sõma zɔ'ɔra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ya ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla, tɩ ya sãn ka bɔna la mam.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Nẽra sãn ka bɔna la mam lɛ, ba wʋn lobe ẽ basɛ wʋ tɩa wille la, tɩ a kʋɩ. La ba vaarɩ wil-sɛba n kʋɩ la ɩta la bugum pʋam, tɩ bugum dita ba.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Ya sãn bɔna la mam, tɩ mam yetɔga la bɔna ya pʋam, ya sose yan boorɩ sɛla wuu, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Ya sãn tõm tõon-sõma zo'oge pa'alɛ tɩ yãma de la mam karẽnbiisi, bɛla ya wʋn nã'asɛ mam Sɔ.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Mam nõŋɛ ya wʋ mam Sɔ n nõŋɛ mam se'em na. Tara-ya mam nõŋlʋm na ya sũure pʋam.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ya sãn sakɛ mam nõorɛ la, ya wʋn tara mam nõŋlʋm na ya sũure pʋam, wʋ mam sakɛ m Sɔ nõorɛ tara a nõŋlʋm se'em na.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: Mam tɔgɛ ya wãna tɩ ya tara la mam sũ-yẽlga, tɩ ya sũ-yẽlga wʋn zo'oge pãɩ.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Mam nõorɛ n wãna: Nõŋɛ-ya taaba, wʋ mam n nõŋɛ ya se'em na.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Nẽra ka boe n tarɩ nõŋlʋm gãnna nẽra n sakɛ tɩ a ki a sɩrdõma ĩyã.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Ya sãn ẽŋɛ mam n yele ya se'em na, ya de la mam sɩrdõma.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: Mam ka le wi'ira ya tɩ m tõntõnɩba, se'ere n soe la, tõntõnna ka mi a zuudãana n ɩtɩ sɛla. Mam wi'iri ya tɩ m sɩrdõma, se'ere n soe la, mam botɩ yãma bãŋɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ mam Sɔ yele mam na wuu mɛ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Dagɩ yãma n looge mam, mam n looge ya, dɩkɛ m tõonɛ na ẽŋɛ ya nu'usum tɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka tõm tõon-sõma wʋ tɩa n wɔnnɩ biɛ la, tɩ ya tõon-sõma la wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu. Bɛla, bõn-sɛka woo tɩ ya wʋn sose mam Sɔ la, la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, a wʋn bɔ ya.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Mam n bɔ ya nõorɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ se'em de la ya nõŋɛ taaba.».

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele yetɩ: «Dũnia nẽrba sãn sise ya, ya bãŋɛ tɩ ba dẽŋɛ sise la mam.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Ya sãn de nɩ na dũnia n soe ya, dũnia nẽrba wʋn nõŋɛ nɩ ya, se'ere n soe la, ya de la dũnia bõnɔ. La mam loose ya la dũnia pʋam yese, la dagɩ dũnia n soe ya, bɛla tɩ dũnia nẽrba sisra ya.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya yetɔg-sɛka tɩ mam yele ya tɩ: ‹Tõntõnna ka gãnna a zuudãana la.› Ba sãn nãmsɛ mam, ba mẽ wʋn nãmsɛ yãma mɛ. La ba sãn sakɛ mam yetɔga, ba wʋn sakɛ yãma mẽ yetɔga.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ ya bõn-bãma wuu mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, ba ka mi sɛka n tõm mam na.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Mam sãn ka wa'am nɩ, la mam sãn ka tɔgɛ nɩ la ba lɛ, ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka boe ba zuo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, ba ka tã wʋn yetɩ bãma tarɩ bʋʋrɔ.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Sɛka n sisri mam na, a mẽ sisri mam Sɔ la mɛ.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Mam sãn ka tõm nɩ tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla nãn ka tõm ba tẽŋasʋka, ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka boe ba zuo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, ba yẽ mam tõoma mɛ, dee sise mam na m Sɔ la.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Bɛla pʋam tɩ bõn-sɛka n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: ‹Ba sise mam na zãŋa.›.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Sõŋra la wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am, a de la sɩra Sɩa tɩ mam wʋn tõm, tɩ a ze'ele mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am ya zẽ'am. Ẽŋa wʋn tɔgɛ mam yelle bɔ ya.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: La yãma mẽ wʋn tɔgɛ mam yelle, se'ere n soe la, halɩ pĩ'iluŋo tɩ ya boe la mam lɛ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: Mam tɔgɛ bɛla bɔ ya tɩ sɛla da wa botɩ ya basɛ ka le dɔla mam.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Ba wʋn wa dige ya ba wẽndeto pʋam, la wakatɛ sẽna tɩ nẽrba wʋn kʋ ya, dee tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma tõm na Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la bɛla, se'ere n soe la, ba ka mi mam Sɔ la, la mam mẽŋa.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: La mam yele ya bɛla tɩ wakat-ẽŋa sãn wa paagɛ, ya tẽegɛ tɩ mam põn yele ya.» «Mam ka yele ya bɛla pɔspɔsɩ, se'ere n soe la, mam daan boe la ya lɛ mɛ.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: La nãnannɛ wã mam we'esɩ la sɛka n tõm mam na zẽ'am, la ya nẽra baa ayɩla ka sokrɩ mam yetɩ, mam we'esɩ la bɛ?.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: La mam n yele ya bõn-ẽna wã ĩyã, sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ ya zozo'e.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: La mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ la ãn sõŋa bɔ ya tɩ mam sẽŋɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam sãn ka sẽŋɛ, Sõŋra la kãn wa'am ya zẽ'am, la mam sãn sẽŋɛ, mam wʋn tõm ẽ bɔ ya.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: La a sãn wa'am, a wʋn botɩ dũnia nẽrba bãŋɛ tɩ ba tuuge mɛ tõon-be'ero yelle, la sɛla n masɛ yelle, la sarɩyadia yelle.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: A wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ ba tõon-be'ero la de la ban ka bɔ mam sɩra la.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: A wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ sɛla n masɛ, se'ere n soe la, mam we'esɩ la m Sɔ zẽ'am, la ya kãn le yẽ mam.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: A wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ sarɩyadia yelle, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ di dũnia wã naba sarɩya mɛ, tɩ a koŋe bʋʋrɔ.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: Mam ken tara la yɛla zozo'e tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ ya, la ya kãn tã'agɛ to'oge ba nãnannɛ wã.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n de sɩra dãana la sãn wa'am, a wʋn pa'alɛ ya sɩra wuu, se'ere n soe la, a yetɔga la ka ze'etɩ a mẽŋa zẽ'am, a wʋn tɔgɛ la sɛla tɩ a wõm na wuu bɔ ya, dee yele ya sɛla n boe nẽŋa sẽna.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: A wʋn nã'asɛ mam, se'ere n soe la, a wʋn dɩkɛ la sɛla n boe mam zẽ'am na tɔgɛ bɔ ya.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Mam Sɔ la n tarɩ sɛla wuu la de la mam bõnɔ. Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yetɩ, a wʋn dɩkɛ la sɛla n boe mam zẽ'am na tɔgɛ bɔ ya.».

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: «La sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya kãn le yẽ mam, la, la sãn le ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya wʋn le yẽ mam.».

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi basɛba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n yetɩ, la sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ kãn le yẽ ẽŋa, la, la sãn le ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ wʋn le yẽ ẽŋa, dee le yetɩ, ẽŋa we'esɩ la a Sɔ zẽ'am na, a võorɛ de la bẽm?».

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Bɛla tɩ ba daan yɛta tɩ: «Ẽn yetɩ la sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe la, a võorɛ de la bẽm? Tõma ka mi bõn-sɛka yelle tɩ a tɔgrɩ la.».

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: La Azezi n daan mi tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba soke ẽ mɛ na, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Mam n yetɩ, la sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya kãn le yẽ mam, la, la sãn le ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya wʋn le yẽ mam na yelle tɩ yãma sɔkra taaba?.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya wʋn kaasɛ fablɛ, la dũnia dõma sũure wʋn ãna yẽlʋm. Ya sũure wʋn sã'am, la ya sũ-sã'aŋɔ la wʋn wa lebge sũ-yẽlga.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Pɔka sãn bɔna mĩ virgra, sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ ẽ, a nãmsgɔ wakatɛ n paagɛ la ĩyã. La a sãn dɔgɛ bia la kɩ'ɩlɩm, a ka le tẽra a nãmsgɔ la yelle, a sũure ãn yẽlʋm mɛ, ẽn dɔgɛ bia la ĩyã.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Bɛla nãnannɛ wã yãma mẽ tarɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la mam wʋn le wa yẽ ya, la ya sũure wʋn ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm. La nẽra baa ayɩla kãn tã'agɛ sã'am ya sũ-yẽlga la.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: La daar-ẽŋa daarɛ, ya kãn le soke mam sɛla baa ayɩla yelle. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ bõn-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn sose mam Sɔ la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, a wʋn bɔ ya.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Ya nãn ka sose sɛla la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ. Sose-ya tɩ ya wʋn to'oge tɩ ya sũ-yẽlga zo'oge pãɩ.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Mam makɛ la makrɛ tɔgɛ bɔ ya. La wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ m Sɔ la yelle vẽelga vẽelga bɔ ya, kãn le makɛ makrɛ tɔgɛ.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Daar-ẽŋa daarɛ ya wʋn sose mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam ka yetɩ mam wʋn sose ẽ bɔ ya.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Mam Sɔ la mẽŋa nõŋɛ yãma mɛ, se'ere n soe la, yãma nõŋɛ mam mɛ, dee bɔ mam sɩra tɩ mam ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: Mam ze'ele la m Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am dũnia zuo, la mam fõrgrɩ dũnia zuo wɛ'ɛsa la m Sɔ la zẽ'am.».

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nãnannɛ wã, fõn yãŋa tɔgrɩ la vẽelga vẽelga, dee ka le makra makrɛ.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Tõma yãŋa mi tɩ fõn mi sɛla woo mɛ, fõn ka le ɛɛra tɩ nẽra soke fo. Bɛla tõma sakɛ tɩ fõn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am.».

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya yãŋa sakɛ ya?.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Tɔ. Wakatɛ sẽna, la a põn paam mɛ, tɩ ya wuu wʋn wɩrgɛ tɩ ya nẽra woo bɔna a yire dee basɛ mam m yɩla. La mam ka de m yɩla, se'ere n soe la, mam Sɔ la boe la mam mɛ.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: Mam yele ya bɛla tɩ ya tara la sũ-mã'asʋm mam yetɔga la ĩyã. Dũnia wã pʋam ya tarɩ la nãmsgɔ, la keŋe ya sũure, tɩ mam yãŋɛ dũnia mɛ.».

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya sũure sã'am. Bɔ-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, la ya bɔ mam mẽ sɩra.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Kẽ'a zẽ'esɩ boe mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yire zo'oge mɛ. Mam we'esɩ tɩ m ka malgɛ la zẽ'a ya ĩyã. La sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, mam kãn yele nɩ ya.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: La mam sãn sẽŋɛ ka malgɛ zẽ'a la ya ĩyã, mam wʋn leme wa'am wa tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ mam zẽ'am, tɩ mam n boe zẽ'a na, yãma mẽ bɔna bilam.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: La mam n we'esɩ zẽ'e-sɛka la, ya mi a sore la.».

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Tɩ Atoma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma ka mi fõn we'esɩ zẽ'a na, tõma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tã'agɛ bãŋɛ a sore?».

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n de sore, la sɩra, la vɔm dãana. Nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn wa'am mam Sɔ zẽ'am, tɩ la sãn ka doose la mam.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Ya sãn mi ni mam, ya wʋn mi ni mam Sɔ la mẽ. La lɛɛlɛ wã, ya mi ẽ, la ya yẽ ẽ.».

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Tɩ Afilipi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, pa'alɛ tɔ fʋ Sɔ la, tɩ bɛla seke tõma mɛ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Afilipi, mam boe la ya yuuge mɛ, tɩ fõn nãn ken ka mina mam? Sɛka n yẽ mam, yẽ mam Sɔ mɛ. La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn yetɩ, m pa'alɛ ya m Sɔ la?.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Fõn ka bɔ sɩra tɩ mam boe la mam Sɔ la lɛ, tɩ mam Sɔ la bɔna la mam lɛ? Yetɔg-sɛba tɩ mam tɔgrɩ la yãma la, ka ze'etɩ mam mẽŋa zẽ'am, la de la mam Sɔ la n boe la mam lɛ la n tõnnɩ a tõoma.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Bɔ-ya mam sɩra, mam n yetɩ mam boe la m Sɔ la lɛ, tɩ mam Sɔ la bɔna la mam lɛ la. La ya sãn ka sakɛ bɛla, ya bɔ mam sɩra m tõoma la ĩyã.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra la, a mẽ wʋn tõm tõon-sɛba tɩ mam tõnnɩ na, la a wʋn tõm tõon-kãra n gãnnɩ bɛla, se'ere n soe la, mam we'esɩ la m Sɔ zẽ'am.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: La bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ ya wʋn sose la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ bɔ ya tɩ m Sɔ la yẽ nã'asgɔ a Dayʋa la ĩyã.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Ya sãn sose sɛla la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ.».

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: «Ya sãn nõŋɛ mam, ya wʋn sakɛ mam nõorɛ la mɛ.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: La mam wʋn sose m Sɔ la, tɩ a bɔ ya Sõŋra ayẽma tɩ a bɔna la ya lɛ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: A de la sɩra Sɩa tɩ dũnia dõma ka tã wʋn to'oge ẽ, se'ere n soe la, ba ka tã wʋn yẽ ẽ, la ba ka mi ẽ. La yãma mi ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a boe la ya zẽ'am, la a wʋn bɔna la ya lɛ.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: Mam kãn basɛ ya kɩɩbsɩ, mam wʋn leme wa'am ya zẽ'am.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: La sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, dũnia nẽrba kãn le yẽ mam. La yãma nõo wʋn yẽ mam, se'ere n soe la, mam n vɔa la, yãma mẽ wʋn vɔa mɛ.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: La daar-ẽŋa daarɛ, ya wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam boe la m Sɔ la lɛ, tɩ yãma bɔna la mam, tɩ mam bɔna la yãma.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Sɛka n bãŋɛ mam nõorɛ la tɩ mam bɔ la, dee sakra ɩta bɛla, la de la ẽŋa n nõŋɛ mam. Mam Sɔ la wʋn nõŋɛ sɛka n nõŋɛ mam na mɛ. La mam wʋn nõŋɛ ẽŋa dãana, dee dɩkɛ m mẽŋa pa'alɛ ẽ.».

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Tɩ Azudaasɩ ayẽma n dagɩ Azudaasɩ Iskariyo la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn wʋn dɩkɛ fʋ mẽŋa pa'alɛ tõma, dee kãn pa'alɛ dũnia nẽrba?».

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn nõŋɛ mam, a wʋn sakɛ ɩta mam n yele se'em na, la mam Sɔ wʋn nõŋɛ a dãana. La tõma wʋn wa'am a zẽ'am wa bɔna la ẽ.
