eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Sɛka n ka nõŋɛ mam, a ka sakɛ ɩta mam yetɔga n yele se'em na. La yetɔga la tɩ ya wõnnɩ wã dagɩ mam mẽŋa yetɔga, la de la mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na yetɔga.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: Mam ken bɔna la ya zẽ'am dee tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna bɔ ya.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: La Sõŋra la, mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a wʋn tõm na mam yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ a wa'am ya zẽ'am na, ẽŋa n wʋn zãsʋm ya sɛla woo, dee tẽegɛ ya bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam yele ya la.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Mam basrɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔ ya, mam mẽŋa sũ-mã'asʋm tɩ mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ya. Mam ka bɔ'ɔrɩ ya wʋ dũnia n bɔ'ɔrɩ se'em na. Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya sũure sã'am, da zɔta-ya dabeem.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Ya wõm mam n yele ya tɩ mam we'esɩ mɛ, la mam wʋn leme wa'am ya zẽ'am. Ya sãn nõŋɛ nɩ mam, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ nɩ sũ-yẽlʋm mam n we'esɩ m Sɔ zẽ'am na ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, mam Sɔ la n gãnnɩ mam.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, mam tɔgɛ ya mɛ tɩ la nãn ka paagɛ, tɩ la sãn wa paagɛ, ya wʋn bɔ mam sɩra.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: Mam kãn le tɔgɛ la ya lɛ zo'oge, se'ere n soe la, dũnia naba sẽm mɛ. La a ka tarɩ pãŋa la mam.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: La dẽnɩ tɩ dũnia dõma bãŋɛ tɩ mam nõŋɛ m Sɔ la mɛ, la mam ɩtɩ wʋ mam Sɔ la n bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na. Isge-ya, tɩ fõrgɛ kalam.».

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla ba'asɛ la, ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la yese dooge la Sedrõ kʋlga ke bɔba, gaarɛ n daan boe mĩ, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka kẽ bilam.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: La Azudaasɩ n de sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na daan mi zẽ'a la mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan ẽn lagsra taaba bilam mɛ.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Bɛla tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma daan tõm gu'urba tɩ ba doose la sʋdaasɩ, tɩ Azudaasɩ tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ mĩ. Ba daan tarɩ la zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ, la fɩtladõma, dee lasʋm bugum zɛɛra.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: La Azezi mi bõn-sɛba wuu n wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ mɛ, bɛla tɩ a sẽŋɛ lɛm ba soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma eerɩ la ãne?».

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma eerɩ la Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la.» Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La de la mam.» La Azudaasɩ n daan yetɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na daan ze mĩ mɛ.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: La Azezi n yetɩ: «La de la mam» na, ba sẽŋɛ la abilimpoore lebe lui tẽŋa.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Tɩ Azezi le soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne tɩ yãma eerɩ?» Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la.».

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yele ya tɩ la de la mam. La sãn dɛna la mam tɩ ya eerɩ, basɛ-ya nẽr-bãna tɩ ba fõrgɛ.».

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: A yele bɛla tɩ a yetɔga tɩ a tɔgɛ la n ẽŋɛ, a yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam na, nẽra baa ayɩla ka bɔɩ.».

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: La Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ daan tarɩ la sʋkɔbgɔ, fooge sʋkɔbgɔ la sɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna zʋʋgɔ tʋbrɛ fiige. Tõntõnna la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Amalkusi.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Lebe sũm fʋ sʋ'a la a fʋʋgɔ pʋam. Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam kãn sakɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam Sɔ bɔ mam na mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la, la ba kɛ'ɛma, la Zifdõma gu'urba la yõkɛ Azezi lʋ ẽ.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: tarɩ ẽ dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ A'annɩ zẽ'am. A'annɩ daan de la Akayifu n de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yʋʋn-ẽŋa la dɛɛm-bʋraa.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: La de la Akayifu n daan yele Zifdõma tɩ nẽra ayɩla sãn ki nẽrba wuu ĩyã n sõɩ.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: La Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ la karẽnbia ayɩla n daan dolɩ Azezi. La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la daan mi karẽnbi-ẽŋa mɛ, tɩ a po doose la Azezi kẽ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la yire.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ weege zɛ la yẽŋa, yaŋa nõorʋm, tɩ karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana mi la, yese ka tɔgɛ la pɔka la n gu'uri zanõrɛ la, dee botɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ kẽ.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Tɩ tõntõn-pɔka la n gu'uri zanõrɛ la, soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Fõn dagɩ la bʋra-ẽna karẽnbia ayɩla?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ a karẽnbia.».

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: La ɔɔrɔ n daan boe, tɩ tõntõnɩba, la gu'urba la tʋʋrɛ bugum kaagɛ ɔɔsra. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan po bɔna mĩ ɔɔsra bugum na.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la soke Azezi a karẽnbiisi la, la a zãsŋɔ la yelle.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam tɔgɛ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam bɔ nẽrba, mam zãsʋm nẽrba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ Zifdõma wuu ẽn lagsrɩ taaba la mɛ, mam ka sʋgɛ tɔgɛ sɛla sɛla.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Bẽm tɩ fʋ sɔkra mam? Soke sɛba n wõm mam yetɔga la, bãma mi mam n yele se'em na mɛ.».

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la, tɩ tõntõnna n ze mĩ, wẽ a pɛka, dee yetɩ: «Fõn lerge la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la bɛla?».

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam sãn tɔgɛ la be'em, pa'alɛ sɛla tɩ mam tɔgɛ be'em na, la mam sãn tɔgɛ la sõŋa, bẽm tɩ fʋ wẽ'era ma?».

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Tɩ A'annɩ botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ ban lʋ ẽ se'em na, sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Akayifu zẽ'am.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: La Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ daan ken zɛ ɔɔsra la bugum na. Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽ dagɩ la bʋra-ẽna karẽnbia ayɩla?» Tɩ a sɩ'ɩsɛ yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ a karẽnbia.».

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna ayɩla n de nẽr-sɛka tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan sɛ a tʋbrɛ fiige la sɔbia, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Dagɩ fõn tɩ mam yẽ la bʋraa la gaarɛ la pʋam na?».

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ le sɩ'ɩsɛ. Wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ nõ-rɔɔgɔ kaasɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Tɩ ba daan tarɩ Azezi Akayifu yire sẽŋɛ gɔmna yire. La daan de la bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ. La Zifdõma la mẽŋa ka kẽ gɔmna la yire, ba ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ ba da dẽgɛ la ba mẽŋa, tã'agɛ di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ yese sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am ka soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm yelle tɩ yãma tarɩ bʋra-ẽna wã wa'am?».

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «A sãn ka ẽŋɛ nɩ be'em, tõma kãn yõkɛ ẽ wa'am wa bɔ fõn.».

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Tara-ya ẽ ya mẽŋa sẽŋɛ ka di a sarɩya wʋ ya lɔɔ la n de se'em na.» Tɩ Zifdõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ tɩ kʋ nẽra.».

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ Azezi n daan tɔgɛ pa'alɛ kũm sɛka buuri tɩ ẽŋa wʋn ki la ẽŋɛ.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ lebe kẽ deem ka wi Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de Zifdõma naba la?».

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ fõn sɔkra bɛla bɩɩ, la de la nẽrba basɛba n tɔgɛ mam yelle bɛla bɔ fõn?».

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ Zifu. Fʋ mẽŋa buuri, la ba kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma n yõkɛ fʋ tarɩ wa bɔ mam. Bẽm tɩ fõn ẽŋɛ?».

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam so'olʋm dagɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ. Mam so'olʋm sãn de nɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ, mam tõntõnɩba la wʋn zɛbɛ nɩ mɛ tɩ ba da yõkɛ mam bɔ Zifdõma la. La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam so'olʋm dagɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ.».

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛla fõn de la naba?» Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La de la fõn yele se'em na, mam de la naba. Mam dɔgɛ wa'am dũnia wã tɩ m tɔgɛ la sɩra kaseto. La sɛka woo n dolɩ sɩra selsrɩ la mam kõa.».

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Sɩra de la bẽm?» Apilatɩ n yele bɛla la, a le yese sẽŋɛ la Zifdõma la zẽ'am ka yele ba yetɩ: «Mam ka yẽ sɛla n de be'em bʋraa wã zẽ'am.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: La yãma malʋm mɛ tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa wakatɛ woo m yese yʋ'a deem nẽra ayɩla basɛ. Yãma boorɩ tɩ m yese la Zifdõma naba la basɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Da kãn basɛ ẽŋa. Tõma boorɩ tɩ fʋ basɛ la Abarabasɩ.» La Abarabasɩ daan de la fãara.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Bɛla tɩ Apilatɩ botɩ ba tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka wẽ ẽ na ka'asa.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ dɩkɛ gõ'osɩ wʋgɛ mũŋa vuge ẽ, dee yɛ ẽ fuugo n de gomusga.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: sẽŋɛ lɛm ẽ yɛta tɩ: «Zifdõma naba, naa», dee wẽ'era a pɛgsɩ.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ le yese nẽrba la zẽ'am ka yele ba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam wʋn tarɩ ẽ yesem na ya zẽ'am tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ mam ka yẽ sɛla n de be'em bʋra-ẽna zẽ'am.».

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Tɩ Azezi vuge gõ'osɩ mũŋa la, dee yɛ fuugo la n de gomusga la yese yẽŋa. Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra la n wãna.».

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la, la ba tõntõnɩba la n yẽ ẽ na, ba kaasrɩ la tagurga yetɩ: «Ka ẽ, ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.» Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ba yetɩ: «To'oge-ya ẽ ka ka ya mẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, mam ka yẽ sɛla n de be'em a zẽ'am.».

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma tarɩ lɔɔ, la lɔɔ la pʋam la masɛ mɛ tɩ a ki, se'ere n soe la, a ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa tɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.».

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Apilatɩ n wõm yetɔg-ẽna la, dabeem n malɩn yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Tɩ a le kẽ deem ka soke Azezi yetɩ: «Fõn ze'ele la bɛ?» Tɩ Azezi ka lerge ẽ.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Mam tɩ fõn sĩm ka lɛrgra? Fʋ ka mi tɩ mam tarɩ nõorɛ wʋn botɩ ba basɛ fɔ, bɩɩ ba ka fʋ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ?».

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nõorɛ la sãn ka ze'ele nɩ saazuum, fʋ ka tarɩ nɩ nõorɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ mam sɛla. Bɛla n soe tɩ sɛka n tarɩ mam wa'am wa bɔ fʋ la, tõm tõon-be'ego gãnna fõn.».

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Apilatɩ n daan wõm bɛla la, a eerɩ la sore tɩ a basɛ Azezi, tɩ Zifdõma la daan kaasra tagurga yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn basɛ bʋra-ẽna, fʋ dagɩ Rom na-keko sɩrɛ. Nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ a mẽŋa naba, a de la na-keko la bɛ.».

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: La Apilatɩ n wõm yetɔg-bãma la, a tarɩ Azezi yese la yẽŋa, dee zĩ'ire sarɩyadia la zẽ'am tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ kug-palagsɩ zẽ'a, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ la Ebre yetɔga dɛna Gabata la.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: La daan de la beere n wʋn wiige tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la di, la daan masɛ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka. Tɩ Apilatɩ yele Zifdõma la yetɩ: «Ya naba la n wãna.».

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Tɩ ba kaasra tagurga yetɩ: «Kʋ ẽ, kʋ ẽ, ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.» Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ba yetɩ: «M ka ya naba la dɔ-puurŋa zuo?» Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka tarɩ naba ayẽma, sãn dagna Rom na-keko la.».

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Bɛla tɩ Apilatɩ bɔ ba Azezi tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ ka ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo. Tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: botɩ a mẽŋa tʋ dɔ-puurŋa la yese tẽŋa la pʋam wɛ'ɛsa zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri zuwaŋrɛ zẽ'a, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ la Ebre yetɔga dɛna Golgota.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Bilam tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo. Dee mẽ ka nẽrba bayi dɔ-puursi zuto, ayɩla a gɔbga bɔba, ayẽma a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ Azezi bɔna ba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ botɩ ba gʋlsɛ dɔ-palaka zuo lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa la zuo. Ba gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ, Zifdõma naba la.».

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Zifdõma zozo'e daan karẽŋɛ gʋlsgɔ la mɛ, se'ere n soe la, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba daan ka Azezi dɔ-puurŋa zuo la lɛm na tẽŋa la. La ba daan gʋlsɛ la Ebre, la Latẽ, la Grɛkɩ yetɔga.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma yele Apilatɩ yetɩ: «Da gʋlsɛ yetɩ ‹Zifdõma naba›, gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹A yetɩ ẽŋa de la Zifdõma naba.› ».

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Tɩ Apilatɩ lerge ba yetɩ: «Sɛla tɩ mam gʋlsɛ la, mam gʋlsɛ mɛ.».

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: La sʋdaasɩ la n ka Azezi kɩ'ɩlɩm na, ba dɩkɛ la a futo pʋɩ zẽ'esɩ banaasɩ, tɩ sʋdaaga woo daan to'oge pʋrɛ ayɩla, deege fu-woko. La fu-woko la daan ka tarɩ pɛ'ɛsõm, ba daan wʋgɛ a wuu la munne.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Tɩ ba daan yele taaba yetɩ, ba da ãasʋm ẽ, ba pɔsɛ bɩsɛ sɛka n wʋn to'oge ẽ. Tɩ ba ẽŋɛ bɛla. La de la wãna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. La gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Ba pʋɩ mam futo bɔ taaba, dee pɔsɛ mam fu-woko la yelle.».

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Azezi ma, la a ma yɩbga, la Amaari n de Akleopasɩ pɔga, la Amaari n ze'ele Magdala daan ze mɛ lɛm dɔ-puurŋa la.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Tɩ Azezi yẽ a ma la, la a karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ a nõŋɛ la n ze lɛm ẽ, tɩ a yele a ma la yetɩ: «M ma, fʋ bia n wãna.».

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Dee yele a karẽnbia la yetɩ: «Fʋ ma n wãna.» Lan sẽŋɛ wakat-ẽŋa, karẽnbia la tarɩ Azezi ma la mɛ kule a yire.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi mi mɛ tɩ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ mɛ, tɩ a yetɩ: «Koyũuro n tarɩ mam.» A yele bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ yele se'em na n ẽŋɛ.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: La lɔkɔ n daan ze pɩrɛ la dã-mĩ'isgo. Tɩ ba dɩkɛ sɩpɔɔm lʋsɛ dã-mĩ'isgo la pʋam zẽkɛ la dɔɔgɔ ẽŋɛ Azezi nõorʋm.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Tɩ a yũ dã-mĩ'isgo la, dee yetɩ: «Sɛla woo ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ», dee suure a zuugo ki.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: La daan de la beere n wʋn wiige Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ, daar-ẽŋa daan de la dabs-kãtɛ, tɩ Zifdõma la ka bɔɔra tɩ ba basɛ tɩ kũm na weege bɔna dɔ-puursi la zuto daar-ẽŋa. Tɩ ba sose Apilatɩ yetɩ, a botɩ ba kɔ ba nãma dee sike ba dɔ-puursi la zuto.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la sẽŋɛ ka kɔ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la tɩ ba ka lɛm Azezi la nãma, le kɔ ayẽma la mẽ nãma.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: La ban paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am na, ba yẽ tɩ a darɛ ki mɛ, tɩ ba ka kɔ a nãma.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Tɩ sʋdaaga ayɩla lʋ a lʋgrɛ la kãnnɛ, tɩ zɩɩm la ko'om yese.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yẽ bõn-bãna la n tɔgrɩ pa'ala ya. La a kaseto la de la sɩra, la a mi tɩ ẽŋa tɔgrɩ la sɩra, tɩ yãma mẽ wʋn bɔ sɩra.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Yel-bãna ẽŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na n ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: «A kõbrɛ baa ayɩla kãn kɔ'ɔgɛ.».

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Ba wʋn bɩsɛ sɛka tɩ ba lʋ la.».

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Arimate bʋraa Azozɛfʋ n daan de Azezi karẽnbia dee sʋgra, ẽn zotɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la ĩyã, daan sẽŋɛ ka sose Apilatɩ sore tɩ a dɩkɛ Azezi kũm na, tɩ Apilatɩ sakɛ, tɩ Azozɛfʋ sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ kũm na.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Tɩ Anikodɛm, sɛka n daan sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am yʋ'ʋŋɔ la po doose Azozɛfʋ. A tarɩ la bõnɔ yʋ'ʋra n de miiri, la alowe n lagɩm taaba gãŋɛ wʋ kilo pitã tɩ ba yũuŋo ãna sõŋa wa'am.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ Azezi kũm na, ẽŋɛ bõnɔ na n tarɩ yũuŋo la ẽŋɛ, vile ẽ na tãna wʋ Zifdõma n ẽn ɩta ba kũm se'em na.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: La gaarɛ n daan boe zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba daan ka Azezi dɔ-puurŋa la zuo la, la yɔ-paalɛ n tũ tãmpĩa ĩyã n daan boe mĩ tɩ ba nãn ka laagɛ nẽra a pʋam.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: La beere n wʋn wiige vo'osgo daarɛ la, la yɔɔgɔ la n daan lɛm na ĩyã, ba daan laagɛ Azezi kũm na bilam.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Lasrɩ daarɛ bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ Amaari n ze'ele Magdala sẽŋɛ yɔɔm na. Ẽn paagɛ la, a yẽ tɩ kugre la bilge mɛ dee basɛ yɔɔgɔ la nõorɛ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Tɩ a zoe sẽŋɛ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ la karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ Azezi nõŋɛ la zẽ'am, ka yele ba yetɩ: «Ba dɩkɛ Zuudãana la yɔɔgɔ la pʋam yese mɛ, tõma ka mi ban bĩŋe ẽ zẽ'e-sɛka.».

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la karẽnbia ayɩla la yese wɛ'ɛsa yɔɔm na.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Bãma bayi daan lagɩm zɔta mɛ wɛ'ɛsa, tɩ karẽnbia ayẽma la zoe gãŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ dẽŋɛ paagɛ yɔɔm na.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: veese bɩsɛ, yẽ tãna la n gã mĩ, dee ka kẽ.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ daan paam poorʋm wa kẽ yɔɔgɔ la pʋam, yẽ tãna la n gã mĩ.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: La tãn-sɛka tɩ ba daan dɩkɛ vile Azezi zuugo la gã mĩ mɛ, la a ka naagɛ la tãna basɛba la, a kilim mɛ gã ayẽma.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Tɩ karẽnbia ayẽma la n dẽŋɛ paagɛ la, mẽ kẽ yɔɔgɔ la pʋam, yẽ bõn-sɛba n boe mĩ na, la a bɔ sɩra mɛ.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: La karẽnbiisi la daan nãn ka wõm Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Azezi ki dee vo'oge la võorɛ.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi bayi la daan lebe kule ba yiɛ.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: La Amaari daan ze la yɔɔgɔ la nõorʋm yẽŋa kɛlla. A daan kellɩ mɛ dee veese tɩ a bɩsɛ yɔɔgɔ la pʋam.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: yẽ malɛkadõma bayi n yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ zẽa zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba daan bĩŋe Azezi kũm na. Ayɩla zĩ la a zuugo bɔba, tɩ ayɩla zẽa a nãma bɔba.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Tɩ malɛkadõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ fõn kɛlla?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Ba dɩkɛ la mam Zuudãana, tɩ mam ka mina ban bĩŋe ẽ zẽ'e-sɛka.».

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Ẽn tɔgɛ bɛla la, dee gelge yẽ Azezi n ze, la a ka bãŋɛ tɩ la de la ẽŋa.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, bẽm tɩ fõn kɛlla? Ãne tɩ fõn eerɩ?» Tɩ a tẽ'esɛ tɩ a de la gaarɛ la dãana, yele ẽ yetɩ: «M kẽema, la sãn dɛna la fõn dɩkɛ ẽ, yele ma fõn bĩŋe ẽ zẽ'a tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ ẽ.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Amaari.» Tɩ Amaari gelge tɔgɛ la Ebre yetɩ: «Rabuni.» A võorɛ de la karẽnsãama.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da kalʋm ma, mam nãn ka zom m Sɔ la zẽ'am. Sẽŋɛ m sɔbiisi la zẽ'am ka yele ba yetɩ: ‹Mam zonnɩ na mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n de yãma Sɔ, la yãma Nawẽnnɛ na zẽ'am.› ».

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Tɩ Amaari n ze'ele Magdala sẽŋɛ ka yele karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, ẽŋa yẽ Zuudãana la, dee yele ba Azezi n yele ẽŋa se'em.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: La daar-ẽŋa daan de la Lasrɩ daarɛ wẽnsobre, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la bɔna deem, la ba daan yʋ de-sɛka tɩ ba boe a pʋam na kʋlɩnsɩ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba daan zotɩ la Zifdõma nẽŋadõma. Tɩ Azezi wa'am wa ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la ya.».

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Azezi n yele ba bɛla la, a pa'alɛ ba la a nu'usi, la a lʋgrɛ la. La karẽnbiisi la n daan yẽ Zuudãana Azezi la, ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ zozo'e.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la ya. Mam mẽ tõnnɩ ya, wʋ mam Sɔ la n tõm mam se'em na.».

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Ẽn yele bɛla la, dee fʋʋsɛ ẽŋɛ ba zuo, yele ba yetɩ: «To'oge ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo tɩ ya basɛ a tõon-be'ero taalɛ, a tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna a zuo. La nẽr-sɛka woo tɩ ya zagsɛ ka basɛ a tõon-be'ero taalɛ, a tõon-be'ero taalɛ ken bɔna la a zuo.».

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Atoma n de Azezi karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «Lɩɩba» la, daan ka boe ba zẽ'am Azezi n daan wa'am mĩ na.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi basɛba la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma yẽ Zuudãana la.» Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Mam sãn ka yẽ kʋʋsa la tɩ ba ka a nu'usi, la yisa dɩkɛ m nu'ubiire kalʋm ba, la mam sãn ka kalʋm a lʋgrɛ la, mam kãn malɩn bɔ sɩra.».

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: La bɛla poorʋm dabsa anii daarɛ, tɩ karẽnbiisi la daan le lagsɛ taaba deego la pʋam, tɩ Atoma bɔna la ba lɛ. La kʋlɩnsɩ la daan yʋ mɛ, tɩ Azezi wa'am wa ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la ya.».

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Dee yele Atoma yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ fʋ nu'ubiire kalʋm mam nu'usi la, dee tẽegɛ fʋ nu'ugo kalʋm mam lʋgrɛ la bɩsɛ. Da kãn sẽ'ena, bɔ sɩra.».

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Tɩ Atoma lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ de la mam Zuudãana la mam Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «La de la fõn yẽ mam na ĩyã tɩ fʋ bɔ sɩra? Sɛba n ka yẽ dee bɔ'ɔra sɩra la tarɩ zu-yẽlʋm.».

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Azezi daan tõm tõon-yãlma basɛba mɛ zozo'e a karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am, n ka gʋlsɛ ẽŋɛ gõn-ẽna wã pʋam.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: La bõn-bãna wã gʋlsɛ tɩ ya bɔ la sɩra tɩ Azezi n de Krisi, Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, tɩ ya sãn bɔ ẽ sɩra, ya wʋn yẽ yõvore a yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a gorge bɩsɛ la saazuum yetɩ: «M Sɔ, mam daarɛ paagɛ ya, nã'asɛ mam n de fʋ Dayʋa la, tɩ mam mẽ nã'asɛ fɔ.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: La de la fõn bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m sɔna nẽrsaalba wuu, dee bɔ sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam na wuu vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: La vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ la de la ba bãŋɛ fõn, fʋ yɩla mã'a n de Sɩra Dãana Nawẽnnɛ na, la ba bãŋɛ mam Azezi-krisi tɩ fʋ tõm na.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: Mam basɛ tɩ fõn yẽ nã'asgɔ tẽŋa wã zuo mɛ. Mam tõm tõon-sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam tɩ m tõm na ba'asɛ mɛ.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, m Sɔ, botɩ m yẽ nã'asgɔ fʋ zẽ'am, wʋ mam n daan tarɩ na'am pãŋa fʋ zẽ'am se'em, lan dẽŋɛ dũnia n daan nãn ka nãam na.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Mam botɩ sɛba tɩ fõn looge dũnia nẽrba pʋam bɔ mam na bãŋɛ fõn mɛ. Ba daan de la fõn nẽrba, tɩ fõn dɩkɛ ba bɔ mam, la ba sakɛ fʋ yetɔga la mɛ.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã ba bãŋɛ tɩ fõn bɔ mam sɛla la wuu ze'ele la fõn zẽ'am.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam tɔgɛ ba la yetɔg-sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam na, tɩ ba to'oge, dee bãŋɛ sɩra sɩra tɩ mam ze'ele la fõn zẽ'am. La ba bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn tõm mam.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ la bãma ĩyã, mam ka pʋ'ʋsrɩ dũnia nẽrba ĩyã, la de la nẽr-sɛba tɩ fʋ bɔ mam na ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, bãma de la fõn nẽrba.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: La mam bõnɔ de la fõn bõnɔ, tɩ fõn bõnɔ dɛna mam bõnɔ, la mam yẽ nã'asgɔ mɛ ba zẽ'am.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Mam ka le bɔna dũnia zuo, la bãma boe la dũnia zuo dee tɩ mam sẽna fõn zẽ'am. M Sɔ n de pʋpeelem dãana, gu'ura ba la fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ la tɩ fʋ bɔ mam na pãŋa, tɩ ba wʋn dɛna ayɩla wʋ mam la fõn n de ayɩla se'em na.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Mam n daan boe la ba lɛ la, mam daan gu'uri ba la yʋ'ʋr-sɛka tɩ fõn bɔ mam na pãŋa. Mam bɩsɛ ba yelle mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla ka bɔɩ, sãn dagna sɛka n de a sã'am, tɩ fõn gõŋɔ n yele se'em na ẽŋɛ la.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã mam sẽm na fõn zẽ'am, la mam tɔgrɩ la wãna mam nãn ken bɔna dũnia zuo, tɩ ba wʋn tara mam sũ-yẽlga, tɩ ba sũ-yẽlga zo'oge pãɩ.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Mam tɔgɛ fõn yetɔga la bɔ ba mɛ, la dũnia nẽrba sisri ba mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba dagɩ dũnia bõnɔ, wʋ mam mẽ dagɩ dũnia nẽra se'em na.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: Mam ka sosrɩ tɩ fʋ yese ba dũnia wã zuo, la de la tɩ fʋ gu ba la be'em dãana.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: La bãma dagɩ dũnia nẽrba, wʋ mam mẽ n dagɩ dũnia nẽra se'em na.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Botɩ fõn sɩra la wʋn basɛ tɩ ba welge to'ore tara pʋpeelem dɛna fʋ nẽrba. Fõn yetɔga la n de sɩra.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Mam mẽ tõm ba dũnia pʋam wʋ fõn tõm mam dũnia zuo se'em na.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: La bãma ĩyã tɩ mam welge m mẽŋa to'ore dɩkɛ m mẽŋa bɔ fõn, tɩ fõn sɩra la wʋn botɩ ba mẽ welge ba mẽŋa to'ore tara pʋpeelem dɛna fʋ nẽrba.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: La dagɩ bãma mã'a ĩyã tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ, la de la sɛba n wʋn wa bɔ mam sɩra ba yetɔga la ĩyã la mẽ yelle.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fõn tɩ fʋ botɩ ba lagɩm dɛna bʋyɩla, wʋ fõn de mam Sɔ n boe la mam lɛ, tɩ mam mẽ bɔna la fõn se'em na, tɩ ba mẽ wʋn lagɩm dɛna bʋyɩla la tõma, tɩ dũnia nẽrba la wʋn bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn tõm mam.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: La mam bɔ ba na'am pãn-sɛka tɩ fõn bɔ mam na mɛ tɩ ba dɛna ayɩla, wʋ tõma n de ayɩla se'em na.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la ba, tɩ fõn bɔna la mam, tɩ ba wʋn sɩrɩ dɛna ayɩla, tɩ dũnia nẽrba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ la de la fõn tõm mam, la fõn nõŋɛ ba wʋ fõn nõŋɛ mam se'em na.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: M Sɔ, mam boorɩ tɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ fʋ bɔ mam na, mẽ bɔna la mam lɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la, tɩ ba wʋn yẽ mam na'am pãŋa, na'am pãn-sɛka tɩ fõn bɔ mam na, se'ere n soe la, fõn nõŋɛ mam mɛ, tɩ dũnia yʋʋn nãn ken ka bɔna.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: M Sɔ, fõn de sɛka n masɛ. Dũnia nẽrba ka bãŋɛ fõn, la mam nõo bãŋɛ fõn mɛ, la nẽr-bãma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la de la fõn tõm mam.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: Mam botɩ ba bãŋɛ fõn mɛ, la mam wʋn ken botɩ ba bãŋɛ fɔ, tɩ nõŋlʋm sɛka tɩ fõn nõŋɛ mam na, wʋn bɔna la ba, tɩ mam mẽ bɔna la ba.».

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: La bɛla poorʋm Azezi le dɩkɛ a mẽŋa mɛ pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi la Tiberiadɩ mõgrɛ nõorʋm. Ẽn pa'alɛ ba a mẽŋa se'em n wãna:.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ, la Atoma tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ lɩɩba la, la Anatanayɛlɩ n ze'ele Kana n boe Galile so'olʋm, la Azebede kɔma, la Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba bayi n daan lagɩm bɔna la taaba.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Mam we'esɩ tɩ m ka yõgɛ la zũma.» Tɩ basɛba la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma mẽ wʋn doose la fʋ sẽŋɛ.» Tɩ ba yese ka kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ. La yʋ'ʋŋ-ẽŋa ba ka yõkɛ sɛla sɛla.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Tɩ beere bɔna mĩ yɩlɩŋra, tɩ Azezi zɛ mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la ka bãŋɛ tɩ la de la ẽŋa.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «M kɔma, yãma tarɩ zũma?» Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ.».

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Lobe-ya yʋka la õorŋɔ la zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ ya wʋn yõgɛ zũma.» Tɩ ba lobe ba yʋka la, yõgɛ zũma zo'oge ka tãna wʋn ve'ege ẽŋɛ õorŋɔ la pʋam.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Tɩ karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ Azezi nõŋɛ la yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «La de la Zuudãana la.» La Apɩyɛɛrɩ n wõm tɩ a de la Zuudãana la, tɩ a dɩkɛ a fuugo yɛ, ẽkɛ lui ko'om pʋam dɔɔra.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi basɛba n daan boe õorŋɔ la pʋam na vɛ'ɛra yʋka la n pɩrɛ la zũma la sẽna, ba daan ka zãagɛ la doone, la daan masɛ wʋ kãntɩɩsɩ kɔbsyi.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: La ban dooge doone na, ba yẽ la bugsãana n diti, tɩ zũma dɔga ba zuo, la borborɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Looge-ya zũn-sɛba tɩ ya yõgɛ lɛɛlɛ la basɛba wa'am.».

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam ve'ege yʋka la n pɩrɛ la zũma la dooge. Zũn-kãra kɔbga la pinuu la atã n daan boe a pʋam. La baa la zũma la n daan zo'oge bɛla la, yʋka la ka ãagɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya wa di.» La karẽnbiisi la nẽra baa ayɩla daan ka saagɛ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne?» Se'ere n soe la, ba daan bãŋɛ tɩ a de la Zuudãana la.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ dɩkɛ borborɩ la pʋɩ ba, dee dɩkɛ zũma la bɔ ba, tɩ ba di.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Azezi n daan vo'oge la, nõorɛ atã n bɛla tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi la.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Ban daan di kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ Azezi soke Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã bia, fõn nõŋɛ mam gãnna bãna n nõŋɛ mam se'em na?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe! Zuudãana, fõn mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ fõn mɛ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Gu'ura m pebilsi.».

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Tɩ Azezi le soke ẽ nõorɛ bʋyi yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã bia, fõn nõŋɛ mam?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe! Zuudãana, fõn mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ fõn mɛ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsra m piisi.».

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Tɩ Azezi le soke ẽ nõorɛ atã yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã bia, fõn nõŋɛ mam?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sũure daan sã'am, ẽn soke ẽ nõorɛ atã yetɩ: «Fõn nõŋɛ mam mɛ bɩɩ» la. Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn mi sɛla wuu mɛ, fõn mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ fõn mɛ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Gu'ura m piisi la.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ fõn daan de budib-paalga la, fʋ daan lʋtɩ fʋ sagãnnɛ mɛ sẽnna fõn boorɩ zẽ'a, la fʋ sãn wa kʋrgɛ, fʋ wʋn zẽkɛ fʋ nu'usi tɩ nẽra lʋ fʋ sagãnnɛ, dee tarɩ fʋ sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ fʋ ka boorɩ.».

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Azezi daan yele bɛla pa'alɛ la kũm sɛka buuri tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ wʋn ki tɩ la nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ. La ẽn yele bɛla la, a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dɔla mam!».

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ gelge mɛ yẽ karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ Azezi nõŋɛ la n dolɩ ba poore. La de la karẽnbi-ẽŋa n daan dente Azezi dia la wakatɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la de la ãne n yetɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ fõn?».

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ n yẽ ẽ na, tɩ a soke Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, dee ẽna wã, bẽm n wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ?».

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam sãn bɔɔra tɩ a vɔa, halɩ tɩ mam wa leme wa'am, bẽm n pakɛ fõn? Fõn wen dɔla mam.».

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Tɩ yetɔg-ẽna wã saagɛ sɔbiisi la zẽ'am tɩ karẽnbi-ẽŋa kãn ki. La Azezi ka yele tɩ a kãn ki, a yetɩ: «Mam sãn bɔɔra tɩ a vɔa halɩ tɩ mam wa leme wa'am, bẽm n pakɛ fõn?».

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: La de la karẽnbi-ẽŋa n bɔ bõn-bãna kaseto, la ẽŋa n gʋlsɛ ba. La tõma mi tɩ a kaseto la de la sɩra.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: La Azezi daan ẽŋɛ yɛla basɛba zozo'e mɛ. Ba sãn yetɩ ba gʋlsɛ nɩ ba wuu ayɩla-yɩla, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ zẽ'a kãn bɔna dũnia zuo n wʋn to'oge gõnnɔ na tɩ ba wʋn gʋlsɛ nɩ na.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Daarɛ ayɩla zaanõorɛ karf-atã, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã dɔla taaba wɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, wakat-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ẽn sẽnna pʋ'ʋsra la.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: La bʋraa n boe tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna kɔɔrŋɔ. La daarɛ woo ba ẽn tara ẽ sẽnna zĩ'ita la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la nõ-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ nõ-sõŋɔ la, tɩ a sɔsra nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam na.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: La kɔɔrŋɔ la n yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã n boe mĩ tɩ ba kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ a sose ba yetɩ, ba bɔ ẽ sɛla.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Tɩ ba bɩsra ẽ zĩi, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ tõma.».

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Tɩ a malɩn bɩsra ba, tẽ'esra tɩ ba wʋn bɔ ẽ sɛla.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka tarɩ ligri wʋn bɔ fɔ. La sɛla tɩ mam tarɩ la, mam wʋn bɔ fɔ. La Azezi-krisi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, isge sẽnna.».

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ gʋrɛ la a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo zẽkɛ ẽ tɩ a isge. Tɩ a nãma la zĩila keŋe bilam mẽŋa.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: tɩ a vaagɛ isge ze'ele saazuo, sẽnna dɔla ba kẽ'era Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, ẽgra sisra, dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la wuu yẽ ẽ tɩ a sẽnnɩ dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: Tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ la de la ẽŋa n ẽn zẽa Wẽnde-kãtɛ la nõ-sõŋɔm na sɔsra. La ban yẽ sɛla n ẽŋɛ ẽ na, la di'ige ba mɛ zozo'e.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: La nẽrba la n yẽ bʋra-sɛka n daan de kɔɔrŋɔ la n dolɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã la, la di'ige ba wuu mɛ, tɩ ba zoe ka lagsɛ taaba ba zẽ'am zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de «Asalomõ võnana» na.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ n yẽ bɛla la, tɩ a soke nẽrba la yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, bẽm tɩ bõn-ẽna wã di'ige ya? La bẽm tɩ ya bɩsra tõma, wʋ la de la tõma mẽŋa pãŋa, bɩɩ tõma n ba dɔla Nawẽnnɛ sõŋa la ĩyã tɩ tõma botɩ bʋra-ẽna isge sẽnna?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tɩ yaabdõma Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la, n zẽkɛ a tõntõnna Azezi la yʋ'ʋrɛ. Ẽŋa tɩ ya daan yõkɛ sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ sɛba n soe pãŋa la nu'usum, tɩ baa gɔmna Apilatɩ n daan boorɩ tɩ a basɛ ẽ na, ya daan zagsɛ mɛ a nẽŋam.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: La yan daan zagsɛ Azezi la, ya zagsɛ la pʋpeelem dãana, la sɩra dãana, dee daan bɔɔra tɩ Apilatɩ basɛ nẽr-kʋʋra la bɔ ya.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Bɛla yãma botɩ ba kʋ la yõvore dãana, ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge. Tõma daan yẽ bõn-ẽna mɛ, tɔgra a yelle bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: La de la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ n tarɩ pãŋa la ĩyã, la tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la n botɩ bʋra-ẽna tɩ yãma mi dee yẽ wã keŋe. La de la ẽn bɔ Azezi sɩra la n botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm sõŋa ya wuu nẽŋam wã.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Tɔ. M sɔbiisi, mam bãŋɛ tɩ yan ka mi la ĩyã, tɩ yãma la ya nẽŋadõma daan ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: La bɛla pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba wuu tɔgɛ yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Krisi wa nãmsɛ la, ẽŋɛ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Bɛla, teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee wa doose Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a wʋn basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm, dee tõm Krisi tɩ a daan põn dẽŋɛ looge la bɔ ya. Ẽŋa n de Azezi.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ sɛla wuu wʋn teege lebge paalsɩ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn põn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔgɛ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm se'em na.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Bɛla tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Ya Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ nẽra ayɩla ze'ele ya buuri pʋam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa bɔ yãma, wʋ ẽŋa n botɩ mam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa se'em na. Dẽnɩ tɩ ya selse bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a wʋn tɔgɛ bɔ ya la.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: la nẽr-sɛka woo n ka selse Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-ẽŋa, ba wʋn yese ẽ mɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka kʋ.›.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, lan pĩ'ilum la Asãmwɛlɩ la sɛba n tɔgɛ ẽŋa poorʋm na wuu, yʋʋn põn tɔgɛ dabs-bãna yelle mɛ.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yʋʋn tɔgɛ la de la yãma ĩyã, la ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma la ken dɛna la yãma ĩyã. A yele Abraham yetɩ: ‹Fʋ yɩɩsɩ la ĩyã tɩ mam wʋn ẽŋɛ dũnia buuri wuu yel-sõnnɛ.›.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Nawẽnnɛ looge a tõntõnna la, dẽŋɛ tõm ẽ na ya zẽ'am tɩ a ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ botɩ ya nẽra woo basɛ a tõon-be'ero.».

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã ken tɔgra la nẽrba la lɛ, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma, la Wẽnde-kãtɛ gu'urba nẽŋadãana, la Saduse dõma wa'am ba zẽ'am.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Tɩ ba sũure yĩige la Tõntõnɩba la n zãsnɩ nẽrba la, dee mõola Azezi kũm vo'ore yelle pa'ala tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge mɛ na.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Tɩ ba yõgɛ ba ka yʋ, gu'ura tɩ beere wiige, se'ere n soe la, wẽnnɛ daan sobe mɛ.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n daan wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, ba zozo'e daan bɔ yetɔga la sɩra mɛ. Ba kãllɛ daan de wʋ tʋsanuu.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: La beere n wiige, tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma lagsɛ taaba Zerizalɛm pʋam.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: La A'annɩ n de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la Akayifu, la Azã, la Alɛgsãndrɩ, la sɛba wuu n de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yir-dõma boe mĩ mɛ.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Tɩ ba botɩ ba tarɩ ba wa'am wa ze'ele ba nẽŋam, tɩ ba soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne n bɔ yãma pãŋa la nõorɛ tɩ yãma ɩta bõn-bãna?».

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ Apɩyɛɛrɩ pãŋa, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽŋadõma, la kẽma.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: yãma n sokrɩ tõma zĩna daarɛ wã tõon-sõŋ-sɛka tɩ tõma ẽŋɛ bɔ kɔɔrŋɔ la yelle, la bõn-sɛka n botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: dẽnɩ tɩ ya wuu, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma wuu bãŋɛ tɩ la de la Azezi-krisi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã tɩ nẽra wã n ze ya nẽŋam wã tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm. Ẽŋa tɩ ya daan ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɔgɛ yetɩ: ‹Kug-sɛka tɩ yãma mẽtba zagsɛ la, lebge la kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.›.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Fãarɛ ka boe nẽra ayẽma zẽ'am, sãn dagna Azezi-krisi mã'a n tã wʋn fãagɛ nẽrba dũnia zuo basɛ.».

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã n yẽ bʋraanɛ tɔgra la, la di'ige nẽrba la mɛ, ban mi tɩ ba nini ka yo'oge, dee tɩ ba dagna gõn-mitba la, tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ ba daan boe la Azezi lɛ.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: La ban yẽ bʋraa la n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm zɛ ba zẽ'am na, ba ka mi ban wʋn lerge se'em bɔ ba.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Tɩ ba yele ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am yetɩ ba yese. Tɩ ba yese. Dee tɩ ba bãm yele taaba yetɩ:.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽr-bãna la wãne? Nẽrba wuu n boe Zerizalɛm wã mi mɛ tɩ bãma n ẽŋɛ yel-kɩrga wã, tõma kãn tã'agɛ sɩ'ɩsɛ bõn-ẽna.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: La tõma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ba tõoma la da le saagɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa, la de la tɩ zɛbɛ ba yetɩ, ba da le tɔgɛ Azezi yelle bɔ nẽra nẽra.».

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Bɛla tɩ ba wi ba yetɩ, ba da le sakɛ tɩ ba tɔgɛ, dee zãsʋm nẽrba Azezi yɛla.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã lerge ba mɛ yetɩ: «Tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ, la masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tɩ tõma sakɛ yãma nõorɛ gãnna Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Tõma ka tã wʋn basɛ ka tɔgɛ sɛla tɩ tõma yẽ dee wõm na.».

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Tɩ ba le gɩ'ɩlɛ ba, dee basɛ ba tɩ ba fõrgɛ. Ba ka yẽ ba wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em botɩ ba sɩbgɛ ba, se'ere n soe la, nẽrba la wuu pẽgrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bõn-sɛka tɩ ba ẽŋɛ la ĩyã mɛ.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Bʋraa la tɩ yel-kɩrga la ẽŋɛ tɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na, gãŋɛ yʋʋma pinaasɩ mɛ.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: La ban basɛ ba la, Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã sẽŋɛ la ba taaba zẽ'am ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma la n yele bãma se'em na wuu.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: La ban wõm bɛla la, tɩ ba naagɛ taaba pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ la yɛm ayɩla yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn nãam saazuum la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la bõn-sɛba wuu n boe ba pʋam na.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Fõn yʋʋn botɩ tõma yaaba Adavidi n de fʋ tõntõnna la tɔgɛ la fʋ Sɩa pãŋa yetɩ: ‹Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ dũnia buuri wuu sũure yĩige, tɩ nẽrba tẽ'esra tẽ'es-yaalsɩ?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Tɩ dũnia na'adõma mãasʋm zɛ, tɩ tẽŋa la nẽŋadõma lagsɛ taaba tɩ ba zɛbɛ la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, la Krisi tɩ a looge la?›.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: La de la sɩra tɩ A'erɔdɩ, la Apõnsɩ-pilatɩ, la bu-zãŋsɩ, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba lagsɛ taaba tẽn-ẽna wã pʋam zɛbɛ la fʋ tõntõn-sõŋɔ Azezi tɩ fʋ looge la.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Bɛla ba ẽŋɛ la bõn-sɛka tɩ fõn yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ looge yɛm la fʋ pãŋa bɔɔra tɩ la ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana, lɛɛlɛ wã bɩsɛ ban zɛbrɩ tõma n de fʋ tõntõnɩba la se'em, bɛla ĩyã, bɔ tɩ pãŋa tɩ tɩ mõolɛ fʋ kõ-yẽlga la bʋraanɛ.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: La fʋ pãŋa, botɩ sɛba n bẽ'erɩ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee tɩ tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ ẽŋɛ, Azezi n de fʋ tõntõn-sõŋɔ la yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.».

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Ban pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ ba'asɛ la, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba lagsɛ la mĩim mɛ. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ba wuu pãŋa, tɩ ba tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bʋraanɛ.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n daan bɔ Azezi sɩra la lagɩm tara la yɛm ayɩla, la sũure ayɩla. Tɩ ba nẽra baa ayɩla ka yetɩ, ẽŋa n tarɩ sɛla la de la ẽŋa mẽŋa mã'a bõnɔ, la de la ba wuu bõnɔ.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: La Tõntõnɩba la tɔgrɩ Zuudãana Azezi kũm vo'ore yelle la pãŋa zozo'e, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔna ba wuu zuo.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: La sɛba n daan tarɩ deto bɩɩ vatɔ la, koose ba mɛ tarɩ ligri la wa'am wa ẽŋɛ Tõntõnɩba la nu'usum. Tɩ ba yãŋa ẽn pʋɩ bɔ nẽra woo ẽŋa n boorɩ sõŋrɛ masɛ se'em. Tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla ka bɔna ba pʋam tɩ sɛla pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: La Azozɛfʋ n de Leviti nẽra n yese Sipri so'olʋm tɩ Tõntõnɩba la sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Abarnabasɩ. A yʋ'ʋrɛ la võorɛ de la: «Nẽra n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽrba bʋraanɛ».

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Ẽŋa daan koose la a va'am dɩkɛ ligri la tarɩ wa'am wa ẽŋɛ Tõntõnɩba la nu'usum.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Ateofili, mam pɔspɔsɩ gõŋɔ la pʋam mam tɔgɛ pa'alɛ fõn Azezi n daan pɔsɛ tõm se'em, dee pa'alɛ nẽrba se'em wuu halɩ.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tarɩ ẽ zom a yire la mɛ. La ẽn daan nãn ka zom Nawẽnnɛ yire la, a daan tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la pãŋa pa'ala Tõntõnɩba tɩ a looge la, bãma n wʋn tõnna se'em.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: Azezi kũm na poorʋm, dabsa pinaasɩ pʋam tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a Tõntõnɩba, tõm tõon-yãlma zozo'e n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽŋa vo'oge mɛ, dee tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yɛla pa'alɛ ba.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: La ban lagsɛ bɔna la ẽ na, tɩ a bɔ ba nõorɛ yetɩ: «Da yese-ya Zerizalɛm tẽŋa pʋam, gu'ura-ya bõn-sɛka tɩ mam Sɔ daan bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ya, tɩ mam yele ya la.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Azã daan misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la, la kãn yuuge dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mise ya a Sɩa pʋam.».

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: La Tõntõnɩba la n lagsɛ taaba la, soke Azezi mɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la de la lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ fõn wʋn to'oge Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma na'am le bɔ ba bɩɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «La ka de ya bãŋɛ wakatɛ la dabs-sɛba tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ looge la a mẽŋa pãŋa.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn wa'am ya zuo, wa bɔ ya pãŋa. La ya wʋn dɛna mam kaset-dõma pa'alɛ mam yɛla Zerizalɛm, la Zude, la Samaari tẽnsʋm, halɩ ka paagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu.».

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Ẽŋa n tɔgɛ bɛla ba'asɛ la, dee zɔnna Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ ba bɩsra ẽ, tɩ wara lile ẽ.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: La ẽn zonnɩ na, ba ken bɩsra la saazuum zĩi, tɩ bʋraasɩ bayi yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ darɛ wa'am wa zɛ ba zẽ'am.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: dee soke ba yetɩ: «Galile nẽrba, bẽm tɩ yãma zɛ bɩsra saazuum? Azezi n yese ya zẽ'am wɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire wã, a wʋn le leme wʋ yãma n yẽ ẽ tɩ a wɛ'ɛsa se'em wã.».

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: La Azezi Tõntõnɩba la daan boe la Oliivi tãŋa zuo, yãŋa ze'ele bilam lɛbra Zerizalɛm. Tãŋa la, la Zerizalɛm ka zãagɛ gãŋɛ kilomẽtrɩ ayɩla.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: La ban paagɛ Zerizalɛm tẽŋam na, ba zom na saazuum deego tɩ ba ẽn bɔna mĩ na pʋam. La sɛba n daan boe mĩ na de la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azã, la Azakɩ, la A'ãndre, la Afilipi, la Atoma, la Abatelemi, la Amatie, la Azakɩ n de Alfe bia, la Asimõ sɛka n daan de Zelɔtɩ nẽra, la Azakɩ bia Azudaasɩ.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Bãma wuu la pɔgsɩ, la Azezi ma Amaari, la Azezi yɩbsɩ n daan ẽn lagsɛ taaba pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla wakatɛ wuu.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: La daarɛ ayɩla, sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la daan lagsɛ mɛ, ba kãllɛ daan de wʋ kɔbga la pisyi. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ isge ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka yetɩ:.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: «M sɔbiisi, la sãn dɛna Azudaasɩ n pa'alɛ nẽrba sore tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yʋʋn dẽŋɛ botɩ naba Adavidi tɔgɛ a yelle mɛ halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm. Bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ la masɛ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n yʋʋn tɔgɛ se'em na.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Azudaasɩ, a daan de la tõma nẽra ayɩla, Nawẽnnɛ n daan looge ẽ tɩ a tõnna a tõoma.».

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: La ẽn daan tõm be'em na tɩ ba yɔ ẽ, tɩ a da va'am la a tõon-be'ero la yɔɔrɔ, lui aliko, tɩ a pʋʋrɛ pusge, tɩ a yõorɔ wuu yurge.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Zerizalɛm tẽŋa nẽrba wuu wõm bɛla mɛ, tɩ ba wi'ira va'am na yʋ'ʋrɛ la ba tẽŋa yetɔga yetɩ «Akeldama». A võorɛ de la zɩɩm va'am.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ ken tɔgɛ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yõoma gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹A yire wʋn lebge daboogo, tɩ nẽra nẽra da le kẽ'era mĩ.› Dee ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Nẽra wʋn sɔɔgɛ a zẽ'am tõm.›.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ nẽra ayɩla pa'asɛ tõma Tõntõnɩba la pʋam pa'alɛ Azezi vo'ore la sɩra. La masɛ tɩ nẽr-ẽŋa dɛna la nẽr-sɛka n daan põn bɔna tɩ pʋam wakat-sɛka wuu tɩ Zuudãana Azezi daan boe la tõma la, lan pĩ'ilum la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, wa paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Azezi daan yese tõma sʋkam zom Nawẽnnɛ yire la.».

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Tɩ ba bãm yetɩ nẽrba bayi. Ayɩla de la Azozɛfʋ, tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Abarsabasɩ, dee tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ ken dɛna Azustusi. Tɩ ayẽma la mẽ yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Amatɩyasɩ.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Tɩ ba yãŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Tɩ Zuudãana, fõn mi nẽrsaalba wuu sũure. Pa'alɛ tɔ nẽrba bayi wã pʋam, fõn looge nẽr-sɛka.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: tɩ a wʋn sɔɔgɛ Azudaasɩ zẽ'am lagɩm na Tõntõnɩba la, tõm tõon-sɛka tɩ Azudaasɩ basɛ dee sẽŋɛ lan masɛ tɩ a sẽŋɛ zẽ'am na.».

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Ba pʋ'ʋsɛ bɛla dee pɔsɛ mɛ, tɩ la yõkɛ Amatɩyasɩ, tɩ ba botɩ a naagɛ la Tõntõnɩba pia la ayɩla la.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: La Zifdõma kibsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Pãntekɔtɩ daarɛ n daan paagɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi wuu daan bɔna zẽ'a ayɩla.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Tɩ voole darɛ ze'ele saazuum wa'am wʋ kʋseb-kãtɛ wa pɩrɛ de-sɛka tɩ ba zĩ mĩ na.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Tɩ ba yẽ sɛla n ãn wʋ bug-zɩlɩsɩ n pʋɩ ayɩla ayɩla zɔɔla ba nẽra woo zuo.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa daan wa'am ba wuu zẽ'am la pãŋa, tɩ ba tɔgra bu-zãŋsɩ yetɔga, wʋ Sɩa la n bɔ ba tɩ ba tɔgɛ se'em na.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: La Zifdõma n daan dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore sõŋa n yese dũnia so'olʋm wuu wa'am wa bɔna Zerizalɛm.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: La nẽrba la n daan wõm voole la, ba daan lagsɛ zo'oge mɛ, tɩ la daan di'ige ba, se'ere n soe la, nẽra woo daan wõm tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la a mẽŋa buuri yetɔga.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba zɛ yɛrɩ, sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, nẽr-bãna wã wuu n tɔgrɩ wã de la Galile dõma.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: La, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ tõma nẽra woo wõm tɩ ba tɔgra a buuri yetɔga?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Tõma nẽrba basɛba yese la Partɩ, la Medi, la Elamɩ, la Mezopotami tẽnsʋm, tɩ basɛba mẽ yese Zude, la Kapadɔsɩ, la Põ, la Azi tẽnsʋm.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: tɩ basɛba mẽ yese Frizi, la Pãnfili, la Ezipti, la Libi tẽnsɩ n lɛm Sirɛnɩ, tɩ basɛba yese Rom tẽŋam.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Nẽrba la basɛba de la Zifdõma, la basɛba dagɩ Zifdõma dee sakɛ dɔla lɔɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ Zifdõma. Basɛba mẽ de la Krɛtɩ dõma, la Araabɩ dõma. La baa bɛla wuu tõma wõm mɛ tɩ ba tɔgrɩ tõma buuri yetɔga pa'ala tõon-kãra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ.».

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba ka mina ba wʋn tẽ'esɛ se'em, yãŋa sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Yel-ẽna võorɛ de la bẽm?».

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Tɩ basɛba la'ara ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã yũ la dãam buge.».

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la a Tõntõn-taaba pia la ayɩla la ze'ele saazuo, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Yãma Zifdõma, la yãma sɛba n boe Zerizalɛm wã wuu, selse-ya mam n yetɩ m yele ya se'em wã, tɩ ya tã'agɛ bãŋɛ yel-ẽna wã võorɛ.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Yãma n tẽ'esrɩ tɩ nẽrba wã yũ la dãam buge la, la dagɩ bɛla, la nãn de la bulika.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: La bõn-ẽna n ɩtɩ wã de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azʋwɛlɩ n yʋʋn yele se'em na n ɩtɩ.».

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: A yʋʋn yele tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Dũnia ba'asgɔ sãn lɛm, mam wʋn basɛ tɩ m Sɩa sige nẽrsaalba buuri wuu zuto. A wʋn botɩ ya dayɔɔsɩ la ya pɔyɔɔsɩ to'oge mam yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba. Dee botɩ ya kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ bɩsɛ yẽ yɛla wʋ zãasŋɔ la, dee botɩ ya bõnkɛgsɩ zãasʋm.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Asɩra, wakat-ẽŋa mam wʋn basɛ tɩ m Sɩa la sige m tõntõn-bʋraasɩ, la m tõntõn-pɔgsɩ zuto, tɩ ba to'oge mam yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: Mam wʋn botɩ bõn-yãlma ẽŋɛ saazuum, dee botɩ yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ mam pãŋa ẽŋɛ tẽŋa zuo: Zɩɩm, la bugum, la bug-zũ'usi zozo'e n wʋn bɔna.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Wẽntulle wʋn wa lebge la lika, tɩ wõrga mõ'ogɛ ãna wʋ zɩɩm na, dee tɩ Zuudãana daarɛ n de na'am kãtɛ daarɛ paagɛ.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: La nẽr-sɛka woo n wʋn wi Zuudãana yʋ'ʋrɛ sose tɩ a fãagɛ ẽ, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.».

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, selse-ya mam n yetɩ m yele ya se'em wã. La de la Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la yelle. Nawẽnnɛ n looge ẽ bɔ ya, botɩ a tõm tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ tɩ tẽŋasʋka, wʋ yãma n põn mina se'em na, pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa n tõm ẽ tɩ zẽ'am.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn põn tẽ'esɛ bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na ĩyã, tɩ a dɩkɛ Azezi ẽŋɛ ya nu'usum, tɩ ya botɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la yõkɛ ẽ tarɩ ka ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ, fãagɛ ẽ kũm nãmsgɔ pʋam basɛ, kũm n ka tã'agɛ tara ẽ na ĩyã.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Adavidi yʋʋn põn tɔgɛ a yelle yetɩ: ‹Mam yẽtɩ mam Zuudãana la mam nẽŋam mɛ daarɛ woo. A boe la mam zʋʋgɔ bɔba sõŋra mam tɩ sɛla kãn botɩ mam bike.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Bɛla ĩyã mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ, dee tɔgra sũ-yẽlga yetɔga. La baa mam wʋn ki, mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: tɩ fõn Nawẽnnɛ kãn basɛ mam kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋam, fõn kãn basɛ tɩ fʋ pʋpeelem dãana põ'ogɛ.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Fõn botɩ mam bãŋɛ yõvore sore mɛ. Fõn wʋn wa botɩ mam yẽ sũ-yẽlga mɛ zozo'e fõn nẽŋam.› ».

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: «M sɔbiisi, basɛ-ya tɩ m tɔgɛ ya vẽelga vẽelga tɩ yaaba naba Adavidi yelle. A yʋʋn ki mɛ, tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ. Tɩ a yɔɔgɔ bɔna tɩ tẽŋasʋka kalam halɩ wa paam zĩna.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Adavidi yʋʋn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, la a bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la nõorɛ pɔɩ yetɩ, a wʋn wa botɩ a yɩɩsɩ la nẽra ayɩla le di na'am wʋ ẽn yʋʋn diti na'am se'em na.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Adavidi dẽŋɛ yẽ Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ a tõm se'em na mɛ. Tɩ a tɔgɛ Krisi kũm vo'ore yelle yetɩ: Nawẽnnɛ kãn basɛ ẽ kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋam, a kãn botɩ a ĩyã põ'ogɛ.».

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Azezi ẽŋa n wãna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge. Tɩ tõma waa wuu dɛna kaset-dõma tɔgra a yelle bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yãŋa zẽkɛ ẽ mɛ ka zĩ'ire a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ a to'oge a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a wʋn bɔ tõma wuu la. Bõn-ẽŋa n wãna tɩ ya yẽtɩ dee wõnna lɛɛlɛ wã.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: La dagɩ Adavidi n yʋʋn zom Nawẽnnɛ yire. La a mẽŋa yʋʋn yetɩ: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele mam Zuudãana yetɩ: Zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ bɔba.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: halɩ tɩ m botɩ fʋ bɛ'ɛba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.».

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: «Bɛla, Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri wuu masɛ mɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ sɩra sɩra tɩ Azezi tɩ ya daan ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la, Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ ya tɩ ẽŋa n de Krisi, dee dɛna tɩ Zuudãana.».

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Nẽrba la n daan wõm yetɔga wã la, la daan ba kẽ ba sũure mɛ halɩ tɩ ba soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Tõntõnɩba basɛba la yetɩ: «Tɩ sɔbiisi, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?».
