eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ba yetɩ: «Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ ya nẽra woo botɩ ba mise ẽ ko'om pʋam la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ dee bɔ ya a Sɩa la.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la, de la yãma la ya kɔma, la sɛba wuu n boe zẽ'a n zãagɛ tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wa wi ba a zẽ'am na ĩyã.».

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan tɔgɛ la yetɔga basɛba zozo'e pa'alɛ ba sɩra, dee sɩgɛ ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ ya zamãan-ẽna nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ la pʋam.».

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la zozo'e daan sakɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɔga la, botɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam. Nẽr-sɛba n daan pa'asɛ ba pʋam daar-ẽŋa masɛ wʋ nẽr-tʋsatã.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: La ba daan ba mõrgɛ botɩ Tõntõnɩba la zãsna ba mɛ, dee naagɛ taaba la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, lagsra dita la taaba, fiisra borborɩ dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: La dabeem daan kẽ nẽrba la wuu mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Azezi Tõntõnɩba la tõm na tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ zozo'e.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n daan bɔ Azezi sɩra la wuu daan lagɩm taaba la nõorɛ ayɩla, tɩ ba bõntarsõm lebge ba wuu bõnɔ.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: dee daan kɔɔsra ba lɔgrɔ la ba bõntarsõm, pʋta bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔɔra sõŋrɛ se'em na.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: La daarɛ woo ba ẽn lagsra Wẽnde-kãtʋm na mɛ, la taaba yiɛ, dita dee fiisra borborɩ, tara sũ-yẽlga la yɛm ayɩla.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ, dee yẽta yel-sõnnɛ nẽrba wuu zẽ'am. La daarɛ woo Zuudãana botɩ nẽrba n yẽta fãarɛ pa'asra sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la pʋam.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Bʋraa n daan boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ananiyasɩ, tɩ a pɔga yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Asafɩɩra. Tɩ ba koose ba va'am.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: tɩ a looge ligri la basɛba sʋgɛ, dee tarɩ lig-sɛba n weege la tarɩ ka bɔ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la. La ẽn ẽŋɛ bɛla la, a pɔga la mi mɛ.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Ananiyasɩ, bẽm tɩ Asũtãana kẽ fʋ sũurum, tɩ fʋ parɩm bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, looge fʋ va'am na tɩ fʋ koose la ligri basɛba sʋgɛ?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Fõn nãn ka koose nɩ ẽ na, a de la fʋ bõnɔ. Fõn koose la mẽ, fʋ tã wʋn ẽŋɛ fʋ yɛm n boorɩ se'em mɛ. Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ fõn tẽ'esɛ fʋ sũurum ẽŋɛ yel-ẽna wã? Fʋ ka parɩm bɔ nẽrsaala, fʋ parɩm bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiyasɩ n wõm yetɔg-ẽna la, a lui la tẽŋa ki. La sɛba wuu n wõm bõn-ẽna la, dabeem n kẽ ba.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Tɩ budib-paalsɩ isge dɩkɛ ẽ põbɛ, zɛ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe masɛ wʋ karf-atã, tɩ a pɔga mẽ wa'am, la a ka bãŋɛ bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ la.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Yele ma, yãma n koose ya va'am na, ligri la wuu n wãna bɩɩ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe! Ba wuu n bɛla.».

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla tɩ ya makɛ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɩsɛ? Bãŋɛ tɩ sɛba n laagɛ fʋ sɩra la boe la zanõrʋm. Ba wʋn zɛ fʋ mẽ yese.».

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Tɩ pɔka la kɔ'ɔn lui tẽŋa ki Apɩyɛɛrɩ nẽŋam. Tɩ budib-paalsɩ la kẽ'em wa yẽ tɩ a ki mɛ, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ yɛglɛ a sɩra la.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Tɩ dabeem daan kẽ Azezi nẽrba la wuu, la sɛba wuu n wõm bõn-ẽna la.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Tɩ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la tõm tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ zozo'e nẽrba la wuu sʋkam. Tɩ sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la lagsɛ taaba la yɛm ayɩla, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «Asalomõ võnana» la.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Tɩ sɛba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la ka sakɛ lagɩm na ba, la nẽrba pẽgrɩ Azezi nẽrba la mɛ zozo'e.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: La bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ zozo'e n daan bɔ'ɔrɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra pa'asra a nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Halɩ tɩ nẽrba tara bã'adõma sẽnna gã'ata sorɔɔrɔ zuto bõn-gã'ala pʋam, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sãn wa dɔla bilam zẽ'a tɔlla, a yinyisŋa lile ba tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yese tẽn-sɛba n gilge Zerizalɛm na tarɩ bã'adõma, la sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ba wa'am. La ba wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la sɛba n de Saduse dõma n boe a zẽ'am na, daan isge ɩta sũure.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: yõgɛ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deego pʋam.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka sẽŋɛ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ka yo'oge yʋ'a deego la nõorɛ, tarɩ ba yese yẽŋa ka yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: «Sẽŋɛ-ya Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ka tɔgɛ vɔm paalga wã yelle wuu bɔ nẽrba la.».

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Ban wõm bɛla la, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na bewiine zãsna nẽrba. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la, la sɛba n boe a zẽ'am na, daan wi Zifdõma sarɩyaditba, la ba kẽma wuu tɩ ba lagsɛ taaba. Tɩ ba tõm nẽrba yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ yʋ'a deem na ka tarɩ Tõntõnɩba la wa'am.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: La ban tõm sɛba la n paagɛ yʋ'a deem na, ba ka yẽ ba, leme wa yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: «Tõma n paagɛ la, tõma yẽ tɩ yʋ'a deego la kʋlɩnsɩ la pagɛ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ sɛba n gu'uri la zɛ deego la nõam, tɩ tõma yo'oge ka yẽ nẽra nẽra.».

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: La Wẽnde-kãtɛ gu'urba nẽŋadãana, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la n wõm yel-ẽna la, ba ze la yɛrɩ, tẽ'esra yel-ẽna n de yel-sɛka.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Tɩ nẽra wa'am wa yele ba yetɩ: «Selse-ya, bʋra-sɛba tɩ ya kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deego pʋam na ze la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba.».

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la gu'urba nẽŋadãana, la a nẽrba sẽŋɛ ka yõgɛ ba wa'am, dee ka le dãam ba, ba zotɩ mɛ tɩ nẽrba la wʋn lobe ba la kuga.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Ban tarɩ ba wa'am wa ze'ele ba nẽŋam na, kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la soke ba yetɩ:.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: «Tõma daan ka yele ya yetɩ, ya da le zãsʋm nẽrba la nẽr-ẽna wã yʋ'ʋrɛ? La bɩsɛ-ya yãma n ẽŋɛ se'em na, yãma zãsŋɔ saagɛ Zerizalɛm wuu mɛ, dee bɔɔra tɩ ya dɩkɛ bʋra-ẽna kũm taalɛ dɔglɛ tõma zuto.».

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la a Tõntõn-taaba la lerge ba yetɩ: «La masɛ mɛ tɩ tõma sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ gãnna nẽrsaalba nõorɛ.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la n vo'oge Azezi tɩ ya daan ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ mɛ zĩ'ire a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, botɩ a dɛna nẽŋam dãana la Fãagra bɔ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sore tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Tõma de la bõn-bãna kaset-dõma, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a tarɩ bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba n sakrɩ ẽ na, mẽ de la kaset-dãana.».

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Ban wõm yetɔg-ẽna wã la, ba sũure yĩige mɛ halɩ tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ba.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Tɩ bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agamalɩyɛlɩ, a de la Farisi nẽra, a zãsnɩ la nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ yɛla, a de la nẽra tɩ ba wuu nannɩ. Tɩ a isge ze'ele nẽrba la n lagsɛ la tẽŋasʋka, bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba yese Tõntõnɩba la yẽŋa tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe. Tɩ ba yese ba.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, guuse ya mẽŋa la yãma n yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ nẽr-bãna wã se'em na.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: La ka yuuge tɩ nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Atedasɩ daan isge zẽkɛ a mẽŋa yetɩ, ẽŋa de la nẽrzuo. Tɩ nẽrba n paagɛ wʋ nẽr-kɔbsnaasɩ daan doose ẽ. Tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ ẽ na wuu wɩrgɛ. Tɩ la lebge zãŋa.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: La bɛla poorʋm, Azudaasɩ n yese Galile mẽ daan isge mɛ nẽrba kãalgɔ wakatɛ, tɩ nẽrba basɛba dɔla ẽ. Tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ ẽ na mẽ wɩrgɛ.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, lɛɛlɛ wã mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya basɛ nẽr-bãna, da kãn ẽŋɛ ba sɛla sɛla. La sãn dɛna nẽrsaalba pʋtẽ'era, la ba tõoma, la wʋn wa lebge zãŋa.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: La, la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ, ya kãn tã'agɛ sã'am ba. Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa, tɩ ya da wa yẽ tɩ ya zɛbrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ.» Tɩ ba sakɛ a yetɔga la.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: dee wi Tõntõnɩba la, tɩ ba wa'am. Tɩ ba botɩ ba wẽ ba, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Da le tɔgɛ-ya Azezi yɛla bɔ nẽrba.» Dee yãŋa basɛ ba yetɩ, ba fõrgɛ.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Tɩ Tõntõnɩba la yese sarɩyadia zẽ'am na sũ-yẽlga, ban masɛ tɩ ba yẽ nãmsgɔ Azezi ĩyã la.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: La daarɛ woo ba boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, la ba yiɛ pʋam, ken zãsna nẽrba, dee mõola Azezi kõ-yẽlga yetɩ, ẽŋa n de Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: La dabs-bãma wakatɛ karẽnbiisi la daan pa'asrɩ mɛ zɔ'ɔra, tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n tɔgrɩ Grɛkɩ yetɔga la daan yõnna la Zifdõma nẽr-sɛba n tɔgrɩ Ebre yetɔga la yetɩ, ban sõŋrɩ nẽrba la dia daarɛ woo la, ba ka bɔ'ɔrɩ bãma pɔkõpa la wʋ lan masɛ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Tõntõnɩba pia la ayi la wi karẽnbiisi la wuu, yele ba yetɩ: «La ka masɛ tɩ tõma basɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga mõolgɔ dee pʋta dia.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Bɛla tɩ sɔbiisi, bɩsɛ ya pʋam looge bʋraasɩ bayopɔɩ n de nẽr-sõma, dee dɛna yɛm dõma, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ba lɛ zozo'e, tɩ tɩ dɩkɛ tõon-ẽna wã ẽŋɛ ba nu'usum.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Dee tɩ tõma wɩsgɛ tɩ mẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la a yetɔga la mõolgɔ.».

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Tɩ ban yele se'em na daan ẽŋɛ nẽrba la wuu sũure yẽlʋm. Tɩ ba looge A'etɩɛnɩ n bɔ Azezi sɩra zozo'e tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ẽ lɛ, la Afilipi, la Aprokɔɔrɩ, la Anikanɔɔrɩ, la Atimõ, la Aparmɛnasɩ, la Anikolasɩ n yese Antɩyɔsɩ dee dɔla Zifdõma pʋ'ʋsgɔ sore.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ Tõntõnɩba la zẽ'am, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la daan saarɩ zẽ'esɩ mɛ pa'asra, tɩ karẽnbiisi la kãllɛ mẽ pa'asra Zerizalɛm pʋam zozo'e. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma zozo'e daan bɔ'ɔra Azezi sɩra.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: La A'etɩɛnɩ n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ la a pãŋa zozo'e la, daan tõnnɩ la tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma basɛba n daan de yamsɩ dee yãŋa to'oge ba mẽŋa, tɩ ba wi'ira ba wẽndeego tɩ soe m mẽŋa dõma wẽndeego, n yese Sirɛnɩ, la Alɛgsãndri, la Zifdõma basɛba n yese Silisi, la Azi tẽnsɩ n wẽ'erɩ nõke'ene la A'etɩɛnɩ.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ A'etɩɛnɩ yɛm tɩ a tɔgra, tɩ ba ka tã'agɛ lerge ẽ.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Tɩ ba ɛ nẽrba botɩ ba dõrɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma wõm tɩ a tɔgɛ la Amoyisi la Nawẽnnɛ tɔg-be'ero.».

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ban ẽŋɛ bɛla la, ba botɩ nẽrba la, la ba kẽma, la ba lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wuu sũure isge mɛ, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ tarɩ sẽŋɛ sarɩyaditba zẽ'am.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: dee botɩ nẽrba parɩm bɔ ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã tɔgrɩ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ tɩ a bɔ Amoyisi la tɔg-be'ero ka gɔ'ɔra.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Tõma wõm tɩ a yetɩ, Azezi n yese Nazarɛtɩ la wʋn sã'am Wẽnde-kãtɛ la mɛ, dee teege ɩr-sɛba tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn pa'alɛ tɩ yaabdõma yetɩ tɩ ɩta la.».

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Tɩ sarɩyaditba la wuu n zĩ la bɩsra A'etɩɛnɩ zĩi, yẽ tɩ a nẽŋa ãn wʋ malɛka nẽŋa la.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana soke A'etɩɛnɩ yetɩ: «La de la bɛla bɩɩ?».

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi la m sɔdõma, selse-ya. Nawẽnnɛ n de na'am kãtɛ dãana la yʋʋn puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ tɩ yaaba Abraham, ẽn yʋʋn nãn boe Mezopotami nãn ka sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire Haran tẽŋam na.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: a yʋʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Yese dee basɛ fʋ tẽŋa la fʋ yir-dõma, dee sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fʋ la.›.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Tɩ Abraham yese Kalde dõma tẽŋam sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire Haran. La a sɔ kũm poorʋm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ le botɩ a yese sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛka tɩ ya boe mĩ zĩna wã pʋam.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka bɔ ẽ zẽ'a tẽŋa la pʋam baa fẽe, dee bĩŋe nõorɛ yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn bɔ ẽ tẽn-ẽŋa, la a kũm poorʋm tɩ a yɩɩsɩ wʋn wa sɔna tẽŋa la. La wakat-ẽŋa a yʋʋn nãn ka dɔgɛ bia.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele ẽ na wãna: ‹Fʋ yɩɩsɩ la wʋn wa sẽŋɛ tẽŋa ayẽma ka kẽ'era mĩ wa lebge yamsɩ bilam, la ba wʋn nãmsɛ ba mɛ yʋʋma kɔbsnaasɩ.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: La mam wʋn wa sɩbgɛ so'olʋm sɛka n botɩ ba dɛna yamsɩ la mɛ. La bɛla poorʋm tɩ ba wʋn yese bilam leme wa pʋ'ʋsɛ mam kalam.›.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la Abraham yetɩ, ẽŋa la a yɩɩsɩ wuu wãta ba budimto tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ ba de la ẽŋa nẽrba. Tɩ Abraham yʋʋn wa dɔgɛ A'izakɩ, tɩ dabsa anii daarɛ tɩ a wã ẽ. Tɩ A'izakɩ mẽ wa dɔgɛ Azakɔbɩ ẽŋɛ bɛla. Tɩ Azakɔbɩ mẽ wa dɔgɛ tɩ yaabdõma pia la ayi la mẽ ẽŋɛ bɛla.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Tɩ tɩ yaabdõma la yʋʋn yũuna la ba yɩbga Azozɛfʋ, koose ẽ bɔ Ezipti dõma. La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn boe la ẽ lɛ mɛ.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: yʋʋn yese ẽ a nãmsgɔ wuu pʋam, dee bɔ ẽ yel-sõnnɛ, la yɛm Afaarõ n de Ezipti naba la nẽŋam. Tɩ naba la botɩ Azozɛfʋ dɛna gɔmna sɔna Ezipti tẽŋa, la a yire wuu bɩsra.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Tɩ kɔm kãtɛ wa'am Ezipti, la Kanaan so'olʋm halɩ tɩ la dɛna toogo. Tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn ka tara dia.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Tɩ Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn wõm tɩ dia boe Ezipti, tɩ a tõm tɩ yaabdõma tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka da.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: La a le tõm ba mɛ nõor-ayi pʋam, tɩ Azozɛfʋ botɩ a kẽendõma la bãŋɛ ẽ. Tɩ Afaarõ mẽ bãŋɛ Azozɛfʋ sɔ yir-dõma yelle.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ tõm tɩ ba wi a sɔ Azakɔbɩ, la a yir-dõma wuu tɩ ba wa'am. Ba kãllɛ yʋʋn paagɛ nẽrba pisyopɔɩ la anuu.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Tɩ Azakɔbɩ isge sẽŋɛ Ezipti. Bilam tɩ ẽŋa la tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn ki.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Tɩ ba zɛ ba sẽŋɛ Sisɛm ka laagɛ ba yɔ-sɛka tɩ Abraham yʋʋn da la ligri A'emõorɩ kɔma zẽ'am na.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la Abraham wakatɛ n yʋʋn lɛm na, nẽrba la yʋʋn yɩrgɛ mɛ zo'oge Ezipti.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Halɩ tɩ nẽra ayɩla wa di Ezipti na'am ka mina Azozɛfʋ yelle.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Tɩ naba la yʋʋn ẽŋɛ yɛm wẽeŋa la tõma yaabdõma nãmsɛ tɩ buuri la, pẽrgɛ ba botɩ ba basɛ ba bilɛɛsɩ tɩ ba ki.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yʋʋn dɔgɛ Amoyisi tɩ a dɛna bi-sõŋɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam. Tɩ ba uge ẽ a sɔ yire wõr-sɩtã.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: dee tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka basɛ, tɩ Afaarõ bipugla yʋʋn dɩkɛ ẽ uge, tɩ a lebge wʋ a mẽŋa bia.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Tɩ Amoyisi zãsʋm Ezipti dõma yɛm wuu, yʋʋn dɛna nẽr-kãtɛ a yetɔga la a tõoma wuu pʋam.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: La ẽn yʋʋn paagɛ yʋʋma pinaasɩ la, tɩ a yʋʋn looge yɛm sẽŋɛ ka kaagɛ bɩsɛ a sɔbiisi n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ yɩɩsɩ la.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: yãŋa yẽ tɩ Ezipti nẽra nãmsrɩ ba nẽra ayɩla. Tɩ a po'oge tɩ nẽra la, wẽ Ezipti nẽra la kʋ lerge sãnnɛ.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: A tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a buuri la wʋn bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ a botɩ ẽŋa fãagɛ ba Ezipti dõma nu'usum, la ba ka bãŋɛ.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a le yẽ a buuri nẽrba bayi tɩ ba zɛbrɩ la taaba. Tɩ a pãsɛ ba, bɔɔra tɩ ba sũure mã'agɛ, soke ba yetɩ: ‹M sɩrdõma, ya de la sɔbiisi, la bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ ya zɛbra la taaba?›.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Tɩ sɛka n ɩtɩ a tadãana be'em na yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ãne n bɔ fʋ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ sɔna tɩ bɩsra, dee dɛna tɩ sarɩyadita?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Fʋ boorɩ tɩ fʋ kʋ mam wʋ fõn zaam kʋ Ezipti nẽra se'em na?›.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Yetɔg-ẽna ĩyã tɩ Amoyisi zoe sẽŋɛ Madiyã ka kẽ'era. Bilam tɩ a dɔgɛ dayɔɔsɩ bayi.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: La yʋʋma pinaasɩ poorʋm, daarɛ ayɩla a boe la weem lɛm Sinayi tãŋa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka ze'ele tũntuugo tɩ bugum boe mĩ dita pʋam puke a mẽŋa bilam, tɩ a yẽ ẽ.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Amoyisi n yẽ ẽ na, la di'ige ẽ mɛ, tɩ a lɛm tɩ a bɩsɛ. Tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele ẽ yetɩ:.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: ‹Mam de la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ fʋ yaabdõma Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la.› Tɩ Amoyisi gõgra dee ka tã'agɛ bɩsɛ.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Pirge fʋ tagra la, tɩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ fʋ ze la de la mam zẽ'e-sõŋɔ.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Mam yẽ m nẽrba la n boe Ezipti nãmsgɔ la mɛ, dee wõm ba fablɩgɔ la, tɩ mam sigum tɩ m fãagɛ ba. Bɛla ĩyã mam yetɩ m tõm fʋ la Ezipti.› ».

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Tɩ A'etɩɛnɩ ken yetɩ: «Amoyisi ẽna wã tɩ ba yʋʋn zagsɛ ẽ yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ãne n bɔ fɔ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ sɔna tɔ, dee dɛna tɩ sarɩyadita?› Ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn botɩ a malɛka tɔgɛ bɔ ẽ tũntuum na. Ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm tɩ a sɔna a nẽrba la dee yese ba nãmsgɔ la pʋam.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Ẽŋa n tõm tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa Ezipti tẽŋam yese ba bilam, dee tõm tõon-yãlma tarɩ ba pãrgɛ mõg-mõlga la, dee bɔna la ba weego la pʋam yʋʋma pinaasɩ.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Amoyisi ẽŋa n yʋʋn yele Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ nẽra ayɩla ze'ele ya buuri pʋam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa bɔ yãma wʋ ẽŋa n botɩ mam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa se'em na.›.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: La tɩ yaabdõma n yʋʋn lagsɛ weem na, la de la ẽŋa n ze'ele tɩ yaabdõma, la malɛka n tɔgɛ la ẽ Sinayi tãŋa zuo tẽŋasʋka, to'oge Nawẽnnɛ vɔm yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ tɔ.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Tɩ tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn ka bɔɔra tɩ ba sakɛ a nõorɛ, yʋʋn zagsɛ ẽ, dee tẽ'esɛ ba sũurum bɔɔra tɩ ba lebe Ezipti.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Ba yʋʋn yele Aarõ yetɩ: ‹Ẽŋɛ baga bɔ tɔ tɩ ba bɔna tɩ nẽŋam. Tõma ka mi bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ Amoyisi n yese tõma Ezipti tẽŋam wa'am na.›.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Bɛla tɩ ba yãŋa yagbɛ na-biire kãabra ẽ tɩ a dɛna ba bagrɛ, ɩta sũ-yẽlga la ba mẽŋa nu'usi n ẽŋɛ sɛla la.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bure a poore bɔ ba, dee basɛ ba tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsra wõrbiɛ n boe saazuum, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba, yãma tẽ'esrɩ tɩ la de la mam tɩ ya yʋʋn kʋʋrɩ dũnsi, la kãabgɔ basɛba weem yʋʋma pinaasɩ bɔ'ɔra la bɩɩ?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Ayɛɩ, ya yʋʋn zɛɛrɩ la ya bagrɛ Amolɔkɩ fuu-deego, la ya bagrɛ Arefã wõrbiire, bõn-bãma tɩ ya yʋʋn ẽŋɛ pʋ'ʋsra. Bɛla ĩyã mam wʋn botɩ ba pe'ege ya sẽŋɛ zãagɛ halɩ gãŋɛ Babilɔnɩ tẽŋam.›.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Tɩ sɔ-yaabdõma yʋʋn tarɩ la fuu-deego weem n pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boe ba zẽ'am. Ba yʋʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ tɩ a ãna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n tɔgɛ bɔ Amoyisi yetɩ, ba ẽŋɛ ẽ tɩ a ãna wʋ ẽn yẽ a wõnnɛ se'em na.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn to'oge fuu-deego la ba mẽ sɔdõma zẽ'am, zɛ ẽ doose la Azoze wa'am wa zɛbɛ to'oge so'olʋm sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn dige nẽrba tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ ba yese ba nẽŋam na. Tɩ fuu-deego la weege bɔna wa paagɛ naba Adavidi wakatʋm.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Adavidi yʋʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, dee sose ẽ yetɩ, a bɔ ẽ sore tɩ a mẽ pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego bɔ ẽ tɩ a bɔna la Azakɔbɩ yɩɩsɩ lɛ.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: La, la de la a bia Asalomõ n yãŋa n mẽ deego la bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla wuu ka kẽ'erɩ deto tɩ nẽrsaalba mẽ pʋam, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ayɩla n yʋʋn yele se'em yetɩ:.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, saazuum de la mam na'am bĩmbĩnne, dee tɩ dũnia dɛna mam nãma dɔglgɔ zẽ'a. Bẽm deego tɩ ya wʋn mẽ bɔ mam? La bẽm vo'osgo zẽ'a tɩ ya yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ bɔ mam?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: La dagɩ mam mẽŋa n ẽŋɛ sɛla woo bɩɩ?› ».

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: A'etɩɛnɩ ken yele ba yetɩ: «Ya de la sɩ'ɩsrɩba, ya ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ la ya sũure wuu, ya ka selsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la, dee kɔ'ɔn zagsra Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa daarɛ woo wʋ ya yaabdõma n mẽ yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-kãna tɩ ya yaabdõma la yʋʋn ka nãmsɛ ẽ? Bãma n yʋʋn kʋ sɛba n dẽŋɛ mõolɛ sɩra dãana tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn tõm tɩ a wa'am na yelle. La yãma n yãŋa dõrɛ ẽ kʋ ẽ.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Yãma yʋʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ mɛ malɛkadõma zẽ'am. La ya ka sakɛ lɔɔ la.».

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Ban wõm bɛla la, ba sũnsõa yĩige mɛ, tɩ ba ɔbra yẽna.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: La A'etɩɛnɩ daan tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa, gorge bɩsra saazuum zĩi, yẽ Nawẽnnɛ na'am n puke yẽgra, tɩ Azezi zɛ Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ bɔba.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire yo'oge mɛ, tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la zɛ Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ bɔba.».

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Tɩ ba lɩ ba tʋba kaasɛ tagurga, dee lagɩm zoe paagɛ yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: ve'ege ẽ tẽŋa la pʋam yese ka lɔbra ẽ la kuga tɩ ba kʋ. Sɛba n lobrɩ ẽ na kuga la yɛɛsɛ ba futo gu'ule la budib-polle yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asolɩ.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: La ban lobrɩ A'etɩɛnɩ la kuga la, tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana Azezi, to'oge m sɩa.».

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Dee dɩkɛ dũma ka tẽŋa tĩi kaasɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, da geele ba tõon-be'ego wã taalɛ.» A yele la wãna dee ki.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: La Asolɩ daan sakɛ tɩ A'etɩɛnɩ kũm na masɛ mɛ. La daar-ẽŋa tɩ ba pɔsɛ nãmsra Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n boe Zerizalɛm na halɩ, tɩ ba wuu zoe wɩrgɛ Zude, la Samaari so'olʋm pʋam, la Azezi Tõntõnɩba pia la ayi la mã'a n weege.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n zotɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan dɩkɛ A'etɩɛnɩ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ, dee kɛlla zozo'e a kũm na ĩyã.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: La Asolɩ daan kɔ'ɔn nãmsra Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mɛ bɔɔra tɩ a sã'am ba wuu, kẽ'era yiɛ pʋam yõgra bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ botɩ ba tara ba sẽnna yʋ'ʋra.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbi-sɛba n daan wɩrgɛ la daan sẽnna zẽ'esɩ wuu mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Afilipi daan sẽŋɛ la Samaari tẽŋa ayɩla pʋam ka mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ba.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la daan selsrɩ Afilipi n tɔgrɩ ba se'em na mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, ban wõm dee yẽ ẽn ɩtɩ bõn-yãlma la.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: La kʋlkã'arsɩ daan ẽn tĩi kaasɛ mɛ, yese dee basɛ ban tarɩ sɛba la. Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n de kɔɔrnɔ la wɔbsɩ zozo'e daan yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba tara sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: La bʋraa n daan boe tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Asimõ. A daan de la tɩɩm dãana, tõnna tɩɩm tõoma botɩ Samaari so'olʋm nẽrba palẽŋa ẽgra. Dee pa'ala a mẽŋa tɩ ẽŋa de la nẽr-kãtɛ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: tɩ nẽrba wuu sakra a yetɔga sõŋa, bõnbɩ'ɩsɩ la bõnkɛgsɩ wuu yɛta tɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna de la sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ wẽnnɛ pãn-kãtɛ la n sige.».

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Ẽn tõm a tɩɩm tõoma botɩ nẽrba la palẽŋa ẽgra yuuge la, ba sakɛ ẽ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: La Afilipi n mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle, la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ bɔ ba la, ba daan bɔ a yetɔga la sɩra mɛ, botɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam, bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Tɩ Asimõ mẽ sakɛ yetɔga la, botɩ ba mise ẽ ko'om pʋam, tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa lablɛ Afilipi, la ẽn yẽtɩ bõn-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ sɛba n ɩtɩ la, la ẽkɛ la a palẽŋa.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Azezi Tõntõnɩba la n daan boe Zerizalɛm na wõm mɛ tɩ Samaari dõma to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mɛ, tɩ ba tõm Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã ba zẽ'am.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: La ban paagɛ mĩ na, ba pʋ'ʋsɛ mɛ bɔ ba tɩ ba to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba daan põn mise ba la ko'om pʋam la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa nãn ka sige ba nẽra nẽra zuo.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã dɩkɛ ba nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto, tɩ ba yãŋa to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Asimõ n yẽ tɩ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la n dɩkɛ ba nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto, tɩ ba to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la, a daan boorɩ tɩ a bɔ ba la ligri.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: yãŋa yele ba yetɩ: «Bɔ-ya mam pãn-ẽna wã tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo tɩ mam wʋn dɩkɛ m nu'usi paglɛ a zuo, a wʋn kɔ'ɔn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.».

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn na fʋ ligri la wʋn sã'am, fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a da'arɩ la ligri la ĩyã.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Fʋ nẽŋa ka boe yel-bãna pʋam, la fʋ ka tarɩ pʋrɛ mĩ, se'ere n soe la, fʋ sũure ka masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nifum.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'e-ẽna wã, dee sose Zuudãana, tɩ tã tɩ a basɛ fʋ tõon-be'ero tɩ fʋ tẽ'esrɩ fʋ sũurum na taalɛ.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Mam yẽ tɩ fʋ tarɩ la sũ-toogo, tɩ tõon-be'ero lʋ fɔ.».

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Tɩ Asimõ lerge yetɩ: «Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Zuudãana bɔ ma tɩ bõn-sɛka tɩ ya yele mam na sɛla sɛla da paagɛ ma.».

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã daan tɔgɛ Zuudãana Azezi yetɔga la pa'alɛ nẽrba mɛ, dee daan lɛbra Zerizalɛm. La sore zuo, ba mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga Samaari tẽnsɩ zozo'e pʋam mɛ.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka tɔgɛ bɔ Afilipi yetɩ: «Isge sẽŋɛ wẽntulle gɔbga bɔba, doose sorɔ-sɛka n ze'ele Zerizalɛm wɛ Gaza la, sorɔ-ẽŋa dolɩ la weego pʋam.».

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Tɩ a isge sẽŋɛ. La Etiopi bʋraa n daan dolɩ sore la, a de la nẽr-kãtɛ, a gu'uri la Etiopi pɔg-naba Akãndase bõntarsõm. A daan sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: dee zẽa wiiri torko pʋam lɛbra kulla. A daan tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi gõŋɔ karẽŋra.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele Afilipi yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ lɛm torko wã.».

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Tɩ Afilipi zoe paagɛ, wõm tɩ a karẽŋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi gõŋɔ. Tɩ Afilipi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wõnnɩ fõn karẽŋɛ se'em wã võorɛ mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm, tɩ nẽra sãn ka pa'alɛ mam?» Dee sose Afilipi yetɩ, a zom zĩ'ire ẽŋa zẽ'am. Tɩ a zom zĩ'ire.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a daan karẽŋrɩ la gʋlsɛ yele la wãna: «A ãn wʋ pesgo tɩ ba tarɩ wɛ tɩ ba kʋ, dee ãna wʋ pebila tɩ ba sɛɛrɩ a kõbrɔ tɩ a ka kaasra, dee mẽ põn ka ya'ara nõorɛ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Ba pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ mɛ, ka di a sarɩya sõŋa. Tɩ nẽra ka bɔna wʋn tɔgɛ a yɩɩsɩ yelle, se'ere n soe la, ba yese a vɔm mɛ dũnia zuo.».

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Tɩ Etiopi bʋraa la soke Afilipi yetɩ: «Ãn yelle tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la tɔgrɩ wãna? A tɔgrɩ la a mẽŋa yelle bɩɩ, nẽra ayẽma yelle?».

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Tɩ Afilipi yãŋa dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a karẽŋɛ la pa'alɛ a võorɛ, dee doose bilam tɔgɛ Azezi kõ-yẽlga bɔ ẽ.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: La ban sẽnnɩ wɛ la, ba paagɛ la zẽ'a tɩ ko'om boe mĩ. Tɩ Etiopi bʋraa la yele Afilipi yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, ko'om n wãna n boe kalam. Bẽm n wʋn gu mam ko'om misga?» .

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Dee botɩ torko la ze'ele. Tɩ Afilipi la ẽŋa sige ko'om na pʋam, tɩ Afilipi mise ẽ ko'om pʋam.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: La ban ze'ele ko'om na pʋam dɔɔra la, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽkɛ Afilipi fõrgɛ. Tɩ Etiopi bʋraa la ka le yẽ ẽ, dee lebe dɔla a sore la wɛ'ɛsa la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Tɩ nẽrba yẽ Afilipi Azɔtɩ tẽŋam, tɩ a dɔla tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga halɩ ka paagɛ Sezaare.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Tɩ Asolɩ ken nãmsra Azezi karẽnbiisi la, yetɩ a kʋ ba, sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana zẽ'am.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: ka sose ẽ yetɩ, a gʋlsɛ gõnnɔ bɔ Zifdõma wẽndeto n boe Damaasɩ tẽŋam na yetɩ, ẽŋa sãn yẽ nẽrba n de bʋraasɩ, bɩɩ pɔgsɩ n dolɩ Azezi sore, ẽŋa wʋn yõgɛ ba mɛ, lʋ ba tarɩ sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: La ẽn to'oge nõorɛ bɔna sore wɛ ka lɛm Damaasɩ la, tɩ peelem ze'ele saazuum darɛ wa nẽegɛ gilge ẽ.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Tɩ a lui tẽŋa, wõm kõa n yele ẽ yetɩ: «Asolɩ, Asolɩ, bẽm tɩ fʋ nãmsra mam?».

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Tɩ Asolɩ lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn de la ãne?» Tɩ kõa la lerge yetɩ: «Mam de la Azezi, sɛka tɩ fʋ nãmsrɩ la.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: La isge kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ ba wʋn yele fo fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em.».

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Tɩ bʋra-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na zɛ yɛrɩ, ka yele se'ere. Ba wõm kõa la mɛ, dee ka yẽ nẽra.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Tɩ Asolɩ isge pike nini ka le yẽta. Tɩ ba ve'ege ẽ tarɩ sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: La dabsa atã tɩ a nini ka yẽtɩ. La a ka di, bɩɩ yũ sɛla.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: La Azezi karẽnbia ayɩla n daan boe Damaasɩ, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ananiyasɩ. Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ ẽ wʋ zãasŋɔ la, wi ẽ yetɩ: «Ananiyasɩ.» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, mam n wãna.».

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge doose sorɔ-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ sorɔ-tẽntẽa la, sẽŋɛ Azudaasɩ yire ka soke Tarsɩ tẽŋa bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asolɩ la yelle. A boe mĩ pʋ'ʋsra mɛ.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: La a yẽ la bʋraa wʋ zãasŋɔ la, bʋraa la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Ananiyasɩ, a yẽ ẽ tɩ a kẽ'em mɛ wa dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ẽŋa zuo tɩ ẽŋa le yẽta.».

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Tɩ Ananiyasɩ lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam põn wõm bʋraa wã yelle zozo'e mɛ, ẽŋa n tõm fʋ nẽrba be'em se'em Zerizalɛm.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: la a to'oge nõorɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am mɛ wa'am kalam tɩ a yõgɛ sɛba wuu n pʋ'ʋsrɩ la fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ la.».

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele Ananiyasɩ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ, mam looge ẽ mɛ tɩ m botɩ a tarɩ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ sẽŋɛ ka tɔgɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, la ba na'adõma, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma mẽ.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Mam wʋn pa'alɛ ẽ ẽn wʋn nãmsɛ se'em mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.».

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Tɩ Ananiyasɩ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ yire la pʋam dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ Asolɩ zuo yele ẽ yetɩ: «M sɔbia Asolɩ, fõn daan boe sore zuo sẽnna tɩ Zuudãana Azezi puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ fʋ la, ẽŋa n tõm mam fʋ zẽ'am tɩ m botɩ fʋ nini le yẽta, dee tɩ fʋ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa.».

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Tɩ Ananiyasɩ n yele bɛla la, sɛla n ãn wʋ pɔgrɔ n kɔ'ɔn ze'ele Asolɩ ninum lui, tɩ a le yẽta. Tɩ a isge, tɩ ba mise ẽ ko'om pʋam.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: dee tɩ a di dia, tɩ a pãŋa le wa'am. Asolɩ daan weege Damaasɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am na dabsa fẽe.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: darɛ kẽ Zifdõma wẽndetʋm mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga yetɩ, Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Tɩ la di'ige sɛba wuu n wõm na. Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Dagɩ bʋra-ẽna wã n daan boe Zerizalɛm kɔ'ɔn nãmsra nẽr-sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ la? A nõo ka wa'am kalam tɩ a yõgɛ ba lʋ mɛ tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am?».

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: La Asolɩ daan yẽtɩ bʋraanɛ mɛ pa'asra, tɔgɛ yãŋɛ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Damaasɩ la, pa'alɛ ba tɩ Azezi de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: La dabsa faa n pa'asɛ tɩ Zifdõma lagɩm ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla yetɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: Tɩ Asolɩ daan bãŋɛ ban bãm yetɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na. Ba daan gu'uri tẽŋa la yansɩ la wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ, bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Tɩ daarɛ ayɩla yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ a karẽnbiisi daan botɩ a sige pɩ'ɩgɔ pʋam, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ sike lalga poorʋm, tɩ a fõrgɛ.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: La Asolɩ daan sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm ka bɔɔra tɩ a lagɩm na Azezi karẽnbiisi la. La ba daan zotɩ ẽ mɛ, ba daan ka sakɛ tɩ a sɩrɩ de la Azezi karẽnbia.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Tɩ Abarnabasɩ daan tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la zẽ'am ka pa'alɛ ba Asolɩ n daan yẽ Zuudãana Azezi Damaasɩ sore zuo, la Zuudãana la n tɔgɛ ẽ se'em, la ẽn daan kãbɛ sũure mõolɛ Azezi yelle Damaasɩ se'em.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Tɩ Asolɩ yãŋa daan bɔna la ba Zerizalɛm, ɛɛra tẽŋa la pʋam wuu, kãbɛ sũure mõola Zuudãana Azezi yelle.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: La a daan tɔgrɩ mɛ wẽ'era nõke'ene la Zifdõma sɛba n tɔgrɩ Grɛkɩ yetɔga la. Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la n bãŋɛ bɛla la, ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ la Sezaare, tɩ ba botɩ a sẽŋɛ Tarsɩ.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Tɩ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n boe Zude, la Galile, la Samaari tẽnsʋm na, daan ka le yẽ nãmsgɔ. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sõŋra ba, tɩ ba kãllɛ pa'asra, tɩ ba zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ nã'asra Zuudãana la ba sũure wuu.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan eerɩ gilgra la zẽ'esɩ la wuu, tɩ daarɛ ayɩla tɩ a sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la n boe Lida la zẽ'am.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: La bilam a yẽ la bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'ene, a daan bẽ mɛ kɔɔrʋm gã yʋʋma anii.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «A'ene, Azezi-krisi bɔ fʋ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ. Isge vaagɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala la bĩŋe.» Tɩ a isge bilam zẽ'am.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Tɩ Lida, la Saarõ tẽnsɩ nẽrba wuu yẽ tɩ bʋraa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ, tɩ ba teege yɛm dɩkɛ ba mẽŋa bɔ Zuudãana.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: La pɔka ayɩla n daan boe Zope dɛna Azezi karẽnbia tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Atabita. A daan tõnnɩ la tõon-sõma sõŋra nasdõma.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa bã'a n yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a ki. Tɩ ba sɔ ẽ, tarɩ ẽ zom deego n boe deego zuum ka gã'arɛ ẽ.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Zope ka zãagɛ la Lida. La karẽnbiisi la n wõm tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ boe bilam na, tɩ ba tõm bʋraasɩ bayi yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka wi ẽ yetɩ, a wa'am, a da yuuge.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ isge doose ba sẽŋɛ. La ban paagɛ la, ba tarɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ zom na deego la n boe deego zuo la. Tɩ pɔkõpa wuu lɛm Apɩyɛɛrɩ kɛlla, dee pa'ala fu-sɛba tɩ Adɔrkasɩ daan pɛ bɔ ba, ẽn daan vɔa la.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ botɩ ba wuu yese yẽŋa, dee ka dũma tẽŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ, dee wẽrgɛ kũm na bɔba yetɩ: «Atabita, isge.» Tɩ a pike nini yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, isge zĩ'ire.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ gʋrɛ a nu'ugo, tɩ a isge ze'ele, tɩ a wi Azezi nẽrba la, la pɔkõpa la pa'alɛ ba tɩ a le vɔa mɛ.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: La Zope tẽŋa dõma wuu n daan wõm yel-ẽna wã la, ba nẽrba zozo'e n bɔ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan yuuge Zope paagɛ la dabsa se'em. A daan boe la Asimõ n de gãnzapa la yire.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Bʋraa n daan boe Sezaare, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Akɔrnɛlɩ. A daan de la sʋda-kɛ'ɛma, ba wi'iri sʋda-sɛba tɩ a de kɛ'ɛma ba pʋam na tɩ Itali dõma sʋdaasɩ.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: A daan de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma wuu daan nanna Nawẽnnɛ. La a daan sõŋrɩ la Zifdõma nasdõma zozo'e, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ daarɛ woo.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan gelge, tɩ a yẽ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka wʋ a zãasʋm mɛ na, tɩ a wa'am wa kẽ a zẽ'am yele ẽ yetɩ: «Akɔrnɛlɩ!».

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Tɩ a daan bɩsra malɛka la zĩi, tɩ dabeem tara ẽ, tɩ a yetɩ: «M dãana, fʋ yetɩ bo?» Tɩ a lerge yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, la fõn sõŋrɩ nasdõma la paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm mɛ, a tẽrɩ fʋ yelle mɛ.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: La nãnannɛ wã, tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope ka yele Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩ a wa'am.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: A de la sãana bɔna Asimõ n de gãnzapa la yire. A yire la lɛm na ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm.».

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: La malɛka la n tɔgɛ la Akɔrnɛlɩ dee fõrgɛ la, tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ wi a tõntõnɩba bayi, la a sʋda-sõŋɔ ayɩla n tõnnɩ a tõoma, dee mẽ nanna Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: yãŋa tɔgɛ sɛla n ẽŋɛ la wuu pa'alɛ ba, dee tõm ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: La beere n wiige, tɩ ba bɔna sore wɛ ka lɛm Zope, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ zom gɔsgɔ tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ, la masɛ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Tɩ a kɔm daan dõnna, tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a di. La ban nãn boe mĩ ɩta dia tɩ ba bɔ ẽ na, tɩ a bɩsɛ yẽ wʋ a zãasʋm mɛ na.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: a daan yẽ tɩ saazuum n yo'oge, tɩ sɛla n ãn wʋ fu-kãtɛ n lʋ zẽ'esɩ sɩnaasɩ signa tẽŋa.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: La dũnsi buuri wuu, la tẽŋa bõn-vuula, la bõn-ẽgla buuri wuu n boe a pʋam.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Tɩ a wõm kõa n yele ẽ yetɩ: «Apɩyɛɛrɩ, isge kʋ di.».

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, Zuudãana, mam ken ka di sɛla n de sisgo bõnɔ bɩɩ bõn-dẽgrɔ baa fẽe.».

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Tɩ kõa la le yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, a ãn sõŋa, da kãn yetɩ, a de la bõn-dẽgrɔ.».

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Bõnɔ la ẽŋɛ la nõor-atã, dee yãŋa lebe saazuum.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan zɛ yɛrɩ, tẽ'esra a pʋtẽ'erʋm bõn-ẽŋa tɩ a yẽ la võorɛ. La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ sɛba tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ daan tõm na soke Asimõ yire n boe zẽ'a, wa'am wa zɛ yaŋa nõorʋm.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: yãŋa wi soke yetɩ, Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ boe la kalam bɩɩ?.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ ken tẽ'esra la bõnɔ la tɩ a yẽ la yelle, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraasɩ batã n eerɩ fo, ban wãna.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Isge sige doose ba, da sẽ'ena, tɩ mam n tõm ba.».

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sige bʋraasɩ la zẽ'a ka yele ba yetɩ: «Mam tɩ ya eerɩ la. Bẽm yelle ĩyã tɩ yãma wa'am kalam?».

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Akɔrnɛlɩ, a de la sʋda-kɛ'ɛma, tɩ a ɩrgɔ masɛ, tɩ a nanna Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ Zifdõma wuu tɔgra a yelle sõŋa. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ, a botɩ fõn wa'am a yire wa yele ẽ fõn tarɩ yetɔg-sɛka la.».

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ botɩ ba kẽ yire la gã'arɛ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a isge dɔla ba wɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba n boe Zope la kɔlgɛ ẽ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: La dabsa ayi daarɛ tɩ ba paagɛ Sezaare. Tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ daan wi a yir-dõma la a sɩrdõma lagsɛ gu'ura ba.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ n paagɛ kẽ'era la, tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam nã'asɛ ẽ.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ gʋrɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Isge, mam mẽ de la nẽrsaala wʋ fõn na.».

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Tɩ ba sõsra la taaba kẽ'era, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yẽ nẽrba n lagsɛ zo'oge.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Ya mi tɩ Zifdõma sisri mɛ, ba ka lagnɩ la nẽra n de sãana, la ba ka kẽ'erɩ a yire. La Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ mam mɛ tɩ m da yetɩ, nẽrsaala de dẽgrɔ dãana, bɩɩ a ka narɩ m lagɩm na ẽ.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ fõn wi mam, tɩ mam wa'am ka zagsɛ. La mam boorɩ tɩ m bãŋɛ la bẽm yelle ĩyã tɩ yãma botɩ mam wa'am?».

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La de la dabsa anaasɩ n wãna wẽngɛlŋa wʋ wakat-ẽna wã, tɩ mam daan boe m yire pʋ'ʋsra, tɩ bʋraa kɔ'ɔn yɛ fuugo n yẽgrɩ zɛ mam nẽŋam.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: dee yetɩ: ‹Akɔrnɛlɩ, Nawẽnnɛ to'oge fʋ pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, la a tẽrɩ fõn sõŋrɩ nasdõma se'em na mɛ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Bɛla, tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope ka wi Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩ a wa'am. A boe la Asimõ n de gãnzapa la yire. A yire la lɛm na ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm.›.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam tõm nẽrba tɔtɔ tɩ ba wa'am wa wi fʋ tɩ fʋ wa'am. La fõn wa'am wã, fʋ ẽŋɛ sõŋa. Tɩ wuu yãŋa boe la Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam kalam tɩ tɩ selse bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele fõn yetɩ, fʋ yele tõma la.».

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yãŋa pɔsɛ yele yetɩ: «Sɩra sɩra, mam yãŋa bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka tũ'usri nẽrba.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: La buuri wuu pʋam nẽr-sɛka n nannɩ Nawẽnnɛ dee ɩta sɛla woo tɩ la masra, ẽŋa dãana n paagɛ a yɛm.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Tɔ. Ya mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm a yetɔga la bɔ la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, a de la kõ-yẽlga n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ wʋn tara sũ-mã'asʋm la Nawẽnnɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã, ẽŋa n de nẽrsaalba wuu Zuudãana.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Yãma mi yel-sɛba wuu n daan ẽŋɛ Zude la, la daan pĩ'ilum na Galile Azã n daan mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga dee mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na poorʋm.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Ya ken mina Azezi n yese Nazarɛtɩ la, la Nawẽnnɛ n daan bɔ ẽ a Sɩa pãŋa se'em, tɩ a daan sẽŋɛ zẽ'esɩ wuu ẽŋɛ ɩr-sõma, botɩ Asũtãana n daan nãmsrɩ sɛba la wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ daan boe la ẽ mɛ.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tõma yẽ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a daan tõm Zerizalɛm la Zifdõma so'olʋm na wuu mɛ, dee tɩ ba daan ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan vo'oge ẽ dabsa atã daarɛ, botɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: La a ka dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ nẽrba la wuu, a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ la tõma sɛba n de kaset-dõma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan põn dẽŋɛ looge la. A vo'ore la poorʋm, tõma daan lagɩm na ẽ mɛ di dee yũ.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: A daan bɔ tõma nõorɛ yetɩ, tɩ mõolɛ ẽŋa yetɔga, dee yele nẽrba, tɩ la de la ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tɩ a di nẽr-vɔɔsɩ la kɩ'ɩndõma sarɩya.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la wuu yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yelle mɛ yetɩ, sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ a dãana tõon-be'ero taalɛ a yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.».

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ n daan ken tɔgra yetɔg-bãna la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sige sɛba wuu n wõm yetɔga la zuto.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n daan botɩ a Sɩa la mẽ sige bu-zãŋsɩ la zuto la, la daan di'ige Zifdõma sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra n doose Apɩyɛɛrɩ wa'am na mɛ.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba daan wõm tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la bu-zãŋsɩ yetɔga pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã to'oge la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋ tõma n daan to'oge se'em na. Bɛla, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ gu ba yetɩ, ba da to'oge ko'om misga?».

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba mise ba ko'om pʋam la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ. Tɩ ba sose Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ, a weege bãma zẽ'am ẽŋɛ dabsa fẽe pa'asɛ.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: La masɛ la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ naba A'erɔdɩ daan nãmsra Azezi nẽrba basɛba.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: dee botɩ ba kʋ Azakɩ n de Azã sɔbia la sʋkɔbgɔ.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: La ẽn yẽ tɩ la ẽŋɛ Zifdõma sũure yẽlʋm mɛ na, a le botɩ ba yõkɛ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, wakat-sɛka tɩ Zifdõma diti borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: La ẽn yõkɛ ẽ kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem na, a botɩ sʋdaasɩ pia la ayoobɩ n pʋɩ bana-banaasɩ gu'ura ẽ sɔɔsna taaba. A boorɩ tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa n tole, dee tɩ a tarɩ ẽ yese nẽrba la nẽŋam ka di a sarɩya.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Bɛla tɩ ba daan pagɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ basɛ yʋ'a deego la pʋam. Tɩ Azezi nẽrba la daan pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a ĩyã halɩ ka basɛ.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Be-sɛka n wʋn wiige tɩ A'erɔdɩ yese Apɩyɛɛrɩ la, daar-ẽŋa yʋ'ʋŋɔ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan gã la sʋdaasɩ bayi tẽŋasʋka gĩsra. Ba daan ba ẽ mɛ la bãnsɩ bayi, tɩ gu'urba la bɔna zanõrʋm gu'ura yʋ'a deego la.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: La haya! Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka kɔ'ɔn wa'am. Tɩ peelem kɔ'ɔn nẽegɛ deego la pʋam. Tɩ malɛka la wẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lʋgrɛ nẽegɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Isge tɔtɔ.» Tɩ bãnsɩ la ba'agɛ lui tẽŋa dee basɛ a nu'usi la.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ga fʋ sagãnnɛ, dee pire fʋ tagra.» Tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɛla. Tɩ malɛka la le yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yɛ fʋ fuugo dee doose mam.».

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ doose ẽ yese yẽŋa, dee ka bãŋɛ tɩ malɛka la n ɩtɩ se'em na de la sɩra, a tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ãna wʋ a zãasʋm mɛ na.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: La ban gãŋɛ pɔspɔsɩ gu'urba la, la gu'urba bʋyi dõma la, ba yãŋa paagɛ la kut-yan-sɛka n yesrɩ kẽ'era tẽŋa la pʋam na, tɩ yaŋa la yo'oge a mẽŋa, tɩ ba yese yẽŋa yãŋa dɔla sore wɛ. Bilam zẽ'am tɩ malɛka la fõrgɛ dee basɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yɛm yãŋa wa'am, tɩ a yetɩ: «Lɛɛlɛ wã mam bãŋɛ sɩra sɩra, tɩ Zuudãana n tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa fãagɛ mam A'erɔdɩ nu'usum, la bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ Zifdõma eerɩ tɩ ba ẽŋɛ mam na.».

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: La ẽn bãŋɛ bõn-ẽna võorɛ la, a sẽŋɛ la Azã-markɩ ma Amaari yire. La nẽrba n lagsɛ bilam zo'oge bɔna mĩ pʋ'ʋsra.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: La ẽn paagɛ mĩ na, a wẽ la yaŋa nõorɛ, tɩ pɔsɔɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Arode wa'am tɩ a bɩsɛ.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: La ẽn bãŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ kõa la, tɩ a sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm tɩ a ka yo'oge yaŋa la, dee zoe kẽ ka yetɩ: «Apɩyɛɛrɩ ze yaŋa nõorʋm.».

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Tɩ ba yele pɔsɔɔ la yetɩ: «Fõn de la zɔlgɔ.» Tɩ a le yetɩ: «La de la ẽŋa.» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «La de la a sɩa».

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ ken wẽ'era yaŋa la. Tɩ ba yo'oge yẽ tɩ la de la ẽŋa. Tɩ la di'ige ba.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbsɛ nu'usi pa'alɛ ba yetɩ, ba ãna sĩm. Dee tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba Zuudãana n ẽŋɛ se'em tarɩ ẽŋa yʋ'a deego la pʋam yese yẽŋa. Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Yele-ya Azakɩ, la tɩ sɔbiisi la, sɛla n ẽŋɛ la.» Dee yese sẽŋɛ zẽ'a ayẽma.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: La beere n wiige la, dãmŋɔ zozo'e n wa'am sʋdaasɩ la tẽŋasʋka, tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ ka bɔna.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Tɩ A'erɔdɩ botɩ ba ɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, ka yẽ ẽ. Tɩ a di gu'urba la sarɩya, bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba kʋ ba. La bɛla poorʋm a yese Zude sẽŋɛ la Sezaare ka bɔna bilam.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: La naba A'erɔdɩ sũure daan yĩige mɛ halɩ la Tiiri la Sidõ tẽnsɩ nẽrba. Tɩ ba ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yẽ ẽ, yãŋa lagsɛ taaba dẽŋɛ yõkɛ Ablastisi n bɩsrɩ naba A'erɔdɩ yire yelle la sɩa. Tɩ bãma la ẽŋa yãŋa doose taaba sẽŋɛ ka sose naba A'erɔdɩ yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm wa'am bãma la ẽŋa tẽŋasʋka. Se'ere n soe la, ba dia daan ze'etɩ la naba la tẽŋam sẽnna.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: La A'erɔdɩ looge la daarɛ yetɩ, ba tɔgɛ yetɔga la. La daarɛ la n paagɛ la, a yɛ la a na'am futo zĩ'ire a na'am kʋka zuo tɔgra yetɔg-kãra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Tɩ ba kaasɛ yetɩ: «Kõa wã de la Nawẽnnɛ kõa, la dagɩ nẽrsaala kõa.».

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka botɩ bã'a yõkɛ A'erɔdɩ bilam zẽ'am, tɩ zũnzũa di ẽ, tɩ a ki, ẽn ka pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ dee pẽgɛ a mẽŋa la ĩyã.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la daan saagɛ zẽ'a wuu, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e sakra yetɔga la pa'asra.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: La Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ n daan tõm ba tõoma Zerizalɛm ba'asɛ la, ba daan tarɩ Azã-markɩ mɛ lebe Antɩyɔsɩ.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n boe Antɩyɔsɩ la pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la karẽnsãandõma n daan boe mĩ. Ba de la Abarnabasɩ, la Asimeyõ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ nẽr-sabga, la Alusiyusi n ze'ele Sirɛnɩ, la Amanayɛnɩ tɩ ba daan uge ẽ la naba A'erɔdɩ, la Asolɩ.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: La daarɛ ayɩla ba daan lʋ la nõorɛ pʋ'ʋsra dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele ba yetɩ: «Looge-ya Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ tɩ ba tõm tõon-sɛka tɩ mam looge ba tɩ ba tõm na.».

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Tɩ ba lʋ nõorɛ dɩkɛ nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ, dee basɛ ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n tõm ba la, ba sẽŋɛ la Selesi tẽŋam ka kẽ õorŋɔ bilam sẽŋɛ Sipri so'olʋm tɩ ko'om gilge la.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: La ban paagɛ Salamini tẽŋam na, ba daan mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mɛ Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam. Ba daan tarɩ la Azã-markɩ tɩ a sõŋra ba.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: La ba daan doose la Sipri so'olʋm la pʋam ka paagɛ Pafɔsɩ. Bilam ba yẽ la tɩɩm dãana n parɩm tɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa. A de la Zifu, a yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Abar-zezi.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: A daan boe la tẽŋa la gɔmna Asɛryusi-polusi n de yɛm dãana la zẽ'am. Tɩ gɔmna la daan botɩ ba wi Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ, a daan boorɩ tɩ a wõm na Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: La tɩɩm dãana la yʋ'ʋrɛ la Grɛkɩ de la A'elimasɩ. A daan sɩ'ɩsrɩ Asolɩ la Abarnabasɩ yetɔga la mɛ, dee daan ɛɛra tɩ a gu gɔmna la tɩ a da sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la Asolɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ ayẽma n de Apolɩ la pãŋa, tɩ a bɩsra tɩɩm dãana la zĩi.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tarɩ la yɛmkẽgsɩga la pã'asgɔ, fʋ de la Asũtãana bia, fʋ de la tõon-sõma wuu bɛ. Fõn dɩkrɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la mɛ tɩ a lɛbgra põmpɔrŋɔ.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, bãŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana pãŋa wʋn botɩ fʋ fɔ mɛ. Fʋ kãn le yẽ peelem halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka.» La bilam zẽ'am tɩ tɩɩm dãana la kɔ'ɔn fɔ, ba'abra ɛɛra nẽra tɩ a ve'ege ẽ.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: La gɔmna la n yẽ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na, a bɔ yetɔga la sɩra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana zãsŋɔ la daan kẽ a sũure mɛ.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Apolɩ la a taaba la ze'ele la Pafɔsɩ kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Pɛrzɩ n boe Pãnfili so'olʋm pʋam na. Tɩ Azã-markɩ basɛ ba bilam dee lebe Zerizalɛm.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Tɩ ba daan ze'ele Pɛrzɩ sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm pʋam na. Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ paagɛ, tɩ ba kẽ ba wẽndeem zĩ'ire.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
