local: La ban karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ wẽndeego la nẽŋadõma tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ sɔbiisi, ya sãn tara yetɔga n wʋn bɔ nẽrba la bʋraanɛ, ya tɔgɛ.».

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ isge yɩbsɛ la nu'ugo yele ba yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba, la yãma sɛba n po nanna Nawẽnnɛ, selse-ya.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Nawẽnnɛ tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ wã, ẽŋa n yʋʋn looge tɩ yaabdõma, ban yʋʋn boe Ezipti na, ẽŋa n botɩ ba yɩrgɛ zo'oge. La ẽŋa n yʋʋn tõm pãŋa tõoma tarɩ ba yese Ezipti.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: La ban boe weem yʋʋma pinaasɩ la, a ẽŋɛ sũ-mã'asʋm la ba mɛ.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Dee yʋʋn sã'am buuri bayopɔɩ Kanaan so'olʋm pʋam, dee bɔ ba tẽŋa la tɩ ba sɔna.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Bɛla wuu yuuge masɛ wʋ yʋʋma kɔbsnaasɩ la pinuu. La bɛla poorʋm a bɔ ba la nẽŋadõma, tɩ ba sɔna ba wa paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Asãmwɛlɩ wakatʋm.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Tɩ ba yãŋa sose yetɩ, bãma boorɩ la naba. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba Akisi dayʋa Asayuli n de Abẽnzamẽ buuri nẽra, tɩ a di na'am yʋʋma pinaasɩ.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn to'oge Asayuli na'am na, dee botɩ Adavidi dɛna naba. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn tɔgɛ Adavidi yelle yetɩ: ‹Mam yẽ la Azese bia Adavidi n ãn mam sũure yẽlʋm. Ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em na wuu.›.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: La de la ẽŋa buuri nẽra ayɩla n de Azezi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a dɛna Fãagra bɔ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, wʋ ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Ẽŋa n daan nãn ka wa'am na, Azã n daan mõolɩ a wa'aŋɔ yelle bɔ Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba wuu yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero tɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: La Azã n daan boe mĩ tɩ a tõm a tõoma ba'asɛ la, a soke ba yetɩ: ‹Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam de la ãne? Yãma n tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam de nẽr-sɛka la, mam dagɩ ẽŋa. La selse-ya, ẽŋa boe la mam poorʋm sẽna, mam ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra.›.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: M sɔbiisi, yãma n de Abraham yɩɩsɩ, la yãma sɛba n po nanna Nawẽnnɛ, la de la tõma ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm fãarɛ kõa wã.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: La Zerizalɛm nẽrba, la ba nẽŋadõma daan ka bãŋɛ Azezi n de nẽr-sɛka. La ba ka bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la tɩ ba karẽŋrɩ vo'osgo daarɛ wuu la võorɛ. La ban di Azezi sarɩya la, nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ mɛ.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Baa ban ka yẽ bõn-sɛka n wʋn botɩ ba kʋ ẽ na, ba sĩm mɛ dee yele Apilatɩ yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: La ban daan ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu wʋ lan põn gʋlsɛ a yelle se'em ba'asɛ la, ba sike ẽ dɔ-puurŋa la zuo mɛ laagɛ.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan botɩ a vo'oge.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: La a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa mɛ pa'alɛ sɛba n daan doose ẽ Galile sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm na dabsa faa, bãma n de a kaset-dõma Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: La tõma mõolɩ la kõ-yẽlga la bɔ yãma: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma la.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: a ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽŋa bɔ la tõma n de ba yɩɩsɩ la. La de la a vo'oge Azezi mɛ. La ãn wʋ lan gʋlsɛ yõoma gõŋɔ yõoma bʋyi dãana la pʋam yetɩ: ‹Fʋ de la mam bia. Zĩna mam n lebge fʋ sɔ.›.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn vo'oge ẽ tɩ a kãn põ'ogɛ. Bɛla tɩ a yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn bɔ ya bõn-sõma sɩra sɩra tɩ mam põn bĩŋe nõorɛ la Adavidi la.›.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ la yele yõoma gõŋɔ pʋam zẽ'a ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Fʋ kãn basɛ fʋ pʋpeelem dãana tɩ a põ'ogɛ.›.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Wakat-sɛka tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn vɔa la, a tõm Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n boorɩ se'em mɛ, dee ki tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ a yaabdõma yɔɔm, tɩ a ĩyã põ'ogɛ.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge sɛka la ka põ'ogɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, yãma masɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ la de la Azezi ĩyã tɩ tõma mõolɛ pa'alɛ yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Amoyisi lɔɔ yʋʋn ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ ya yelle masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: la nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ Azezi sɩra, a yelle masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am mɛ.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Bɛla, guuse-ya tɩ lan gʋlsɛ se'em Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na da paagɛ ya.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: La yetɩ: ‹Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ yãma sɛba n de sɩ'ɩsrɩba la, ya palẽŋa wʋn ẽkɛ mɛ tɩ ya ki. Se'ere n soe la, tõon-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn tõm ya zamãana wã pʋam na, ya kãn nan tɩ la wʋn sɩrɩ ẽŋɛ, baa nẽra sãn tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ya.› ».

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ n daan isge tɩ ba yese la, tɩ nẽrba la sose ba yetɩ, ba le wa'am vo'osgo daarɛ wa tɔgɛ yetɔg-sɛba tɩ ba tɔgɛ la.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: La ban yese tɩ ba fõrgɛ la, tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e, la nẽrba basɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma dee sakɛ dɔla Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ tɩ a bɔ Zifdõma, daan dɔla Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ. Tɩ Apolɩ dõma pa'alɛ ba yetɩ, ba mõrgɛ bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na pʋam.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Tɩ vo'osgo daarɛ le paagɛ, tɩ nẽrba lagsɛ zozo'e wʋ la de la tẽŋa la nẽrba wuu la, tɩ ba selse Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: La Zifdõma n yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n zo'oge la, tɩ ba ẽŋɛ sũure sɩ'ɩsɛ Apolɩ n tɔgɛ se'em na, dee tɔgra tɔg-be'ero.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan kãbɛ sũure tɔgɛ ba yetɩ: «Tõma wʋn dẽŋɛ nɩ tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga wã bɔ la yãma. La yãma n zagsɛ la, yãma pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka masɛ tɩ ya yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. Bɛla ĩyã tõma wʋn basɛ yãma, dee sẽŋɛ la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bɔ tõma nõorɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam looge fo tɩ fʋ ãna wʋ peelem bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, botɩ nẽrba yẽ fãarɛ dũnia zẽ'esɩ wuu.› ».

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: La bu-zãŋsɩ la n wõm bɛla la, ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ halɩ, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la. La nẽr-sɛba n masɛ tɩ ba yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ la daan sakɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana yetɔga la saagɛ tẽŋa la wuu.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma daan sa'asɛ pɔgsɩ n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, dee dɛna yʋ'ʋrɛ dõma, la tẽŋa la nẽrzuto, botɩ ba nãmsɛ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ dige ba ba tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ ba yese.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ pɩɩsɛ ba nãma tẽntõnnɔ basɛ, pa'alɛ tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, dee wɛ'ɛsa Ikonim.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la n boe Antɩyɔsɩ la daan tarɩ sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ba.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ n paagɛ Ikonim la, ba le kẽ la Zifdõma wẽndeego pʋam tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba. Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e, Zifdõma la nẽrba basɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: La Zifdõma nẽr-sɛba n ka sakɛ yetɔga la, bãma n sa'asɛ bu-zãŋsɩ botɩ ba sũure sã'am la Azezi karẽnbiisi la.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan yuuge Ikonim mɛ, te'ege Zuudãana Azezi tɔgɛ a yetɔga la bʋraanɛ. Tɩ Zuudãana daan bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba tõm tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽŋa yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la de la sɩra.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: La tẽŋa la nẽrba daan welse mɛ, tɩ basɛba bɔna Zifdõma poorʋm, tɩ basɛba bɔna Azezi Tõntõnɩba la poorʋm.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ, la Zifdõma, la ba nẽŋadõma bɔɔra tɩ ba nãmsɛ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ lobe ba la kuga.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Tɩ ba bãŋɛ, zoe sẽŋɛ Likoni tẽnsʋm, Listri, la Dɛrbɩ, la tẽn-pɩgsɩ sɛba n lɛm bilam na pʋam.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Bilam ba daan mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la mɛ.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: La bʋraa n daan boe Listri ka tãna sẽnna. A ma dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna la kɔɔrŋɔ.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Tɩ a daan zẽa sɛlsra Apolɩ n tɔgrɩ la. Tɩ Apolɩ bɩsra ẽ zĩi, bãŋɛ tɩ a bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ tãna wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge ze'ele saazuo.» Tɩ kɔɔrŋɔ la vaagɛ isge sẽnna.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n yẽ Apolɩ n ẽŋɛ se'em na, tɩ ba tĩi tɔgɛ la Likoni tẽŋa yetɔga yetɩ: «Wẽna n lebge nẽrsaalba sigum tõma zẽ'am.».

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Tɩ ba daan wi'ira Abarnabasɩ tɩ Azeyusi, dee wi'ira Apolɩ tɩ A'ɛrmẽsɩ, ẽŋa n tarɩ yetɔga la wa'am na ĩyã.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Ba daan tarɩ la deego tẽŋa la zanõ-bɔba kãabra Azeyusi. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽema la tarɩ lɔlsɩ, la tɩɩsɩ puuro wa'am zanõrʋm na, tɩ ẽŋa la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la bɔɔra tɩ ba kãabɛ Abarnabasɩ la Apolɩ.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: La Tõntõnɩba Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ n wõm ban yetɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na, ba sũure sã'am mɛ, tɩ ba ãasʋm ba futo, dee zoe kẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ:.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: «Nẽrba wã, bẽm tɩ ya yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ wãna? Tõma mẽ de la nẽrsaalba wʋ yãma n ãn se'em na, la tõma mõolɩ la kõ-yẽlga pa'ala yãma tɩ ya basɛ bõn-bãna wã n ka tarɩ yõorɔ, dee dɔla Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana. Ẽŋa n nãam saazuum la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la bõn-sɛba wuu n boe ba pʋam na.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn basɛ buuri wuu tɩ ba ɩta la ba mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: La baa bɛla wuu, Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ sõŋa mɛ pa'alɛ ẽŋa n de sɛka. Ẽŋa n botɩ saaga niira bɔ'ɔra ya, tɩ ya bõnkɔɔla bɩta, tɩ dia bɔna zo'oge, tɩ ya sũure ãna yẽlʋm.».

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Baa la Apolɩ n tɔgɛ bɛla la, la ẽŋɛ la toogo bɔ ba, dee tɩ ba yãŋɛ gu nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tɩ ba ka kãabɛ ba.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: La Zifdõma n ze'ele Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm, la Ikonim wa'am wa sa'asɛ nẽrba la, botɩ ba lobe Apolɩ la kuga, vuuge ẽ tẽŋa la pʋam yese basɛ, dee tẽ'esɛ tɩ a ki mɛ.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la lagsɛ gilge ẽ, tɩ a isge kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ẽŋa la Abarnabasɩ sẽŋɛ Dɛrbɩ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga Dɛrbɩ mɛ, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e lebge Azezi karẽnbiisi, dee tɩ ba yãŋa lebe Listri, la Ikonim, la Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: La ba daan tɔgɛ pa'alɛ karẽnbiisi la mɛ, bɔ ba bʋraanɛ yetɩ, ba mõrgɛ bɔna Azezi sɩra la pʋam da basɛ. Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn yẽ la nãmsgɔ zozo'e dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.».

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: La tẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ ba sẽŋɛ la, ba looge la kẽma Azezi nẽrba la pʋam, lʋ nõorɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee dɩkɛ ba basɛ Zuudãana tɩ ba bɔ sɩra la nu'usum.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: La ban doose Pisidi la, ba paagɛ Pãnfili so'olʋm.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: La ba mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Pɛrzɩ mɛ, dee sẽŋɛ Atali.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: La ba kẽ la õorŋɔ bilam sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ, tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba daan dɩkɛ ba ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum tɩ a sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba tõm tõon-sɛka tɩ ba tõm ba'asɛ wã.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: La ban paagɛ la, ba botɩ Azezi nẽrba la lagsɛ mɛ, tɩ ba tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ n tõm la bãma se'em, la a yo'oge sore mɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ tɩ ba tãna wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: La ba boe la karẽnbiisi la lɛ bilam yuuge mɛ.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Nẽrba basɛba n ze'ele Zude wa'am Antɩyɔsɩ wa zãsna Azezi karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Ya sãn ka wãta ya budimto wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ la n zãsnɩ se'em na, ya kãn tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ.».

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan ka sakɛ ban yele se'em na, daan tĩi wẽ'era nõke'ene la ba. Tɩ karẽnbiisi la kʋ nõ-yẽnnɛ yetɩ, Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ, la bãma nẽrba basɛba sẽŋɛ Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma n boe Zerizalɛm na zẽ'am nõke'ene ẽŋa yelle.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: La Azezi nẽrba la n tõm ba yetɩ ba sẽŋɛ la, ba doose la Fenisi la Samaari tẽnsʋm tɔgɛ pa'alɛ bu-zãŋsɩ n teege yɛm sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ se'em. Bɛla botɩ Fenisi la Samaari tẽnsɩ karẽnbiisi la sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ zozo'e.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: La ban paagɛ Zerizalɛm na, Azezi Tõntõnɩba la, la Azezi nẽrba, la ba kẽma la wuu to'oge ba mɛ sõŋa sõŋa. Tɩ ba tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ se'em wuu la bãma.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Tɩ Farisi nẽrba basɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la isge yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ ba wãta bu-zãŋsɩ la, dee zãsʋm ba tɩ ba sakɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ la wuu.».

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Tɩ Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma la lagsɛ tɩ ba bɩsɛ ba wʋn ẽŋɛ yelle la se'em.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: La ban wẽ'erɩ nõke'ene zɔ'ɔra la, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ isge yele ba yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, yãma mi mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la mam kʋrʋm kʋrʋm yãma pʋam, tɩ m tɔgɛ a kõ-yẽlga la bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ tɩ ba sakɛ.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n mi nẽrsaala pʋtẽ'era la, bɔ ba a Sɩa mɛ, wʋ ẽŋa n daan bɔ tõma se'em na, pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa fãagɛ ba mɛ.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: La a ka welge tõma la bãma. La ban bɔ Azezi sɩra la ĩyã tɩ a yese be'em ba sũure pʋam basɛ.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Bɛla, bẽm tɩ ya makra Nawẽnnɛ bɔɔra tɩ ya zelge karẽnbiisi la zeero tɩ tõma yaabdõma, la tõma ka yãŋɛ tʋ?.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: La ka ãn sõŋa, tõma mi tɩ la de la Zuudãana Azezi yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã tɩ tõma yẽ fãarɛ, wʋ bãma n mẽ yẽ fãarɛ se'em na.».

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu ãna sĩm sɛlsra Abarnabasɩ la Apolɩ n tɔgrɩ pa'ala tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la bãma bu-zãŋsɩ tẽŋasʋka la.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: La ban tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ Azakɩ yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, selse-ya.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɔgɛ pa'alɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n dẽŋɛ bɩsɛ bu-zãŋsɩ yelle se'em, looge ba nẽrba basɛba tɩ ba dɛna a mẽŋa nẽrba.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: La bõn-bãma masɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yele se'em na. Ba yʋʋn gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: ‹Bɛla poorʋm, mam wʋn leme wa'am, wa le mẽ naba Adavidi deego la n lui la mɛ. Mam wʋn le malgɛ ẽ mɛ, mẽ a daboogo la zuo.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Bɛla tɩ nẽr-sɛba n weege la wuu, la bu-zãŋsɩ wuu tɩ mam looge tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba la, wʋn ɛ mam n de ba Zuudãana.›.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em n wãna, ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ bõn-bãma tɩ ba põn mina halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm.».

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Tɩ Azakɩ ken yetɩ: «Bɛla tɩ mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ tɩ da nãmsɛ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma dee teege yɛm dɔla Nawẽnnɛ na.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Tɩ gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ bɔ ba yetɩ, ba da di di-sɛba tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ baga la, tɩ ba wʋn dẽgɛ ba, ba da tõnna yalsɩ tõoma, ba da di sɛla tɩ ba tẽgɛ kʋ, la ba da di zɩɩm.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Se'ere n soe la, halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm ba karẽŋrɩ Amoyisi gõnnɔ Zifdõma wẽndeto la pʋam ba vo'osgo dabsa wuu mɛ, dee mõola ba tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam.».

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Bɛla tɩ Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma, la Azezi nẽrba la wuu tẽ'esɛ yetɩ, la ãn sõŋa tɩ ba looge nẽrba bãma pʋam tõm ba tɩ ba doose la Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ. Tɩ ba looge Azudi sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n ken dɛna Abarsabasɩ la, la Asɩlaasɩ. Bãma tɩ ba daan nannɩ.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: La ba daan gʋlsɛ la gõŋɔ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Tõma n de Azezi Tõntõnɩba, la Azezi nẽrba la kẽma n pʋ'ʋsrɩ yãma bu-zãŋsɩ n de tɩ sɔbiisi n boe Antɩyɔsɩ, la Siiri, la Silisi so'olʋm.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Tõma wõm tɩ tõma nẽrba basɛba n wa'am wa dãana yãma la yetɔga botɩ yãma yɛm yɔɔra, tõma ka bɔ ba nõorɛ.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ tõma lagsɛ taaba kʋ nõorɛ ayɩla, tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ tõma looge nẽrba basɛba tõm ba tɩ ba doose la tɩ sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ Abarnabasɩ la Apolɩ wa'am yãma zẽ'am.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bãma n põsɛ ba yõorɛ tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi tõoma la ĩyã.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Bɛla tɩ tõma tõm Azudi la Asɩlaasɩ tɩ ba wa'am wa tɔgɛ bõn-bãma pa'alɛ ya.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la tõma tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ tɩ da zelge ya zeero basɛba pa'asɛ, sãn dagna yel-sɛba n de kãŋkãŋɛ wã.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: La de la ya da di di-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ bɔ baga, ya da di zɩɩm, la ya da di sɛla tɩ ba tẽgɛ kʋ, la ya da tõnna yalsɩ tõoma. Ya sãn guuse ya mẽŋa ka ẽŋɛ bõn-bãna, ya ẽŋɛ sõŋa. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Ba daan keese sɛba tɩ ba tõm na mɛ, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ. La ban paagɛ bilam na, ba lagsɛ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mɛ, dɩkɛ gõŋɔ la bɔ ba.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: La ban daan karẽŋɛ gõŋɔ la, la bɔ ba bʋraanɛ mɛ, botɩ ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm zozo'e.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Tɩ Azudi la Asɩlaasɩ n daan de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la tɔgɛ zozo'e keŋe ba sɔbiisi la sũure, pa'asɛ ba pãŋa.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: La ba daan yuuge mĩ na fẽe, dee tɩ ba sɔbiisi la basɛ ba tɩ ba lebe sɛba n tõm ba la zẽ'am la sũ-mã'asʋm. .

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan weege la Antɩyɔsɩ bilam, tɩ bãma la nẽrba basɛba zozo'e zãsna nẽrba Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga dee mõola a kõ-yẽlga la.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: La daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Apolɩ yele Abarnabasɩ yetɩ: «Tɩ lebe ka kaagɛ tɩ sɔbiisi la n boe tẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ tɩ daan mõolɛ Zuudãana yetɔga bɔ ba la, bɩsɛ ba ãn se'em.».

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Tɩ Abarnabasɩ daan bɔɔra tɩ Azã sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n ken dɛna Amarkɩ la po doose ba sẽŋɛ.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: La Apolɩ daan tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ka ãn sõŋa tɩ a doose bãma, se'ere n soe la, a daan basɛ ba mɛ Pãnfili, ka le doose la ba tõm.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Tɩ ba daan wẽ nõke'ene welge taaba. Tɩ Abarnabasɩ daan botɩ Amarkɩ doose ẽ, tɩ ba kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Sipri.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: La Apolɩ daan looge la Asɩlaasɩ tɩ a doose ẽ. Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la dɩkɛ ba ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Tɩ ba yãŋa sẽŋɛ Siiri la Silisi tẽnsʋm ka pa'asɛ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pãŋa.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: La Apolɩ dõma sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ la Dɛrbɩ la Listri tẽnsʋm. Tɩ Azezi karẽnbia daan bɔna mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Atimõtɩ, a ma de la Zifu n sakɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra. Dee tɩ a sɔ dɛna Grɛkɩ.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi n boe Listri la Ikonim tẽnsʋm na daan tɔgra a yelle sõŋa.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ bɔɔra tɩ a dɔla la ba lɛ tõnna, daan wã ẽ Zifdõma sɛba n boe tẽn-bãma la ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, ba wuu daan mi tɩ a sɔ de la Grɛkɩ.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: La ba daan dolɩ la tẽnsɩ pʋam bɩsra Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, dee tɔgra pa'ala ba Azezi Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma la n boe Zerizalɛm na n looge yɛm se'em, dee yɛta ba tɩ ba sakɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ ba looge la.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Bɛla tɩ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la daan yẽ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo la pʋam, tɩ ba kãllɛ pa'asra daarɛ woo.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ dõma daan bɔɔra tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azi tẽnsʋm ka mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa gu ba, tɩ ba doose Frizi la Galatɩ so'olʋm.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: La ban sẽŋɛ ka lɛm Mizi so'olʋm na, ba boorɩ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ la Bitini, tɩ Azezi Sɩa la ka bɔ ba sore.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Tɩ ba doose Mizi tole sẽŋɛ Trʋasɩ.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: La yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ Apolɩ bɩsɛ yẽ Masedʋanɩ nẽra wʋ zãasŋɔ la, tɩ bʋraa la zɛ a zẽ'am bɛlna ẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am Masedʋanɩ wa sõŋɛ tɔ.».

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: La Apolɩ n daan yẽ bɛla la, tõma mãasʋm mɛ tɔtɔ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ Masedʋanɩ, bẽm ĩyã, tõma tẽ'esɛ bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ n wi tõma yetɩ, tɩ sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ba.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Tɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ bilam kɔ'ɔn sẽŋɛ Samotrasɩ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Neapolisi.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: La tõma yãŋa ze'ele la bilam sẽŋɛ Filipi n de Masedʋanɩ tẽn-kãra la tẽŋa ayɩla. Filipi tẽŋa de la Rom dõma n soe ẽ. Tõma ẽŋɛ bilam na dabsa faa.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Tɩ vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ tõma yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ kʋlaa nõorʋm, tõma tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ zẽ'e-ẽŋa de la Zifdõma pʋ'ʋsgɔ zẽ'a. Tɩ tõma zĩ'ire bilam tɔgra bɔ'ɔra pɔgsɩ n lagsɛ.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: La pɔka ayɩla n daan boe mĩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Alidi, a ze'ele la Tiyatiiri. A daan koosrɩ la fu-sõma n de gomusga. Tɩ a sɛlsra tõma yetɔga la, tɩ Zuudãana botɩ a bãŋɛ Apolɩ yetɔga la võorɛ dee sakɛ.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Tɩ tõma daan mise ẽŋa la a yir-dõma ko'om pʋam. Tɩ a sose tõma yetɩ: «Ya sãn sakɛ tɩ mam sɩrɩ bɔ Zuudãana sɩra mɛ, ya wa'am kẽ'era mam yire.» La a pẽrgɛ tõma mɛ, tɩ tõma sakɛ.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Tɩ daarɛ ayɩla tɩ tõma wɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ zẽ'am, tɩ pugla n de yamŋa tɩ kʋlkã'arga dolɩ ẽ daan tʋ'ʋsɛ tõma. Kʋlkã'arga la daan botɩ a yẽta la yel-sɛba n boe nẽŋam sẽnna tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, la sɛba n soe ẽ na daan yẽtɩ ligri zozo'e mɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Tɩ a dɔla Apolɩ la tõma tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã de la Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla wuu la tõntõnɩba, ba mõolɩ la fãarɛ sore pa'ala ya.».

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: La a ẽŋɛ bɛla la dabsa faa. Tɩ Apolɩ sũure sã'am la ẽ, tɩ a wẽrgɛ yele kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ, tɩ fʋ yese dee basɛ pugla wã.» Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la yese bilam zẽ'am dee basɛ ẽ.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Tɩ sɛba n soe ẽ na n bãŋɛ tɩ bãma kãn le yẽ ligri a zẽ'am na, tɩ ba yõkɛ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ ve'ege ba kẽ da'am ka sãm ba tẽŋa la nẽŋadõma zẽ'am.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ ba paagɛ sɛba n diti sarɩya la zẽ'am ka yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã de la Zifdõma, bãma n dãmnɩ tɩ tẽŋa wã.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Ba pa'alɩ la bõn-sɛba n sisri, tɩ tõma n de Rom nẽrba kãn sakɛ ẽŋɛ.».

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: La Azezi Tõntõnɩba, la a karẽnbi-sɛba n boe Zude so'olʋm na wõm mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la mẽ sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mɛ.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lagɩm zɛbra Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ, tɩ sɛba n diti sarɩya la botɩ ba vɛ ba futo la yɛɛsɛ, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba wẽ ba la ka'asa.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan lebe la Zerizalɛm, tɩ Zifdõma basɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la wẽ'era nõke'ene la ẽ.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Tɩ ba wẽ ba zozo'e dee kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deem, dee yele gu'ura la tɩ a gu'ura ba sõŋa.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ fõn kẽ lagɩm na bu-zãŋsɩ n ka wãtɩ la dita?».

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Ban yele bɛla la, tɩ a kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a de-sɛka n boe pʋam pʋam na, lʋ ba nãma lablɛ la dɔɔgɔ.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yãŋa pa'alɛ ba yɛla la wuu n daan doose se'em na yetɩ:.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Tɩ sõnsʋa tɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ pʋ'ʋsra dee yõona yõoma pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ sɛba n po bɔna yʋ'a deem na sɛlsra.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: «Mam daan boe la Zope pʋ'ʋsra, yẽ sɛla wʋ zãasŋɔ la, tɩ sɛla la ze'ele saazuum sigra wʋ fu-kãtɛ n lʋ zẽ'esɩ sɩnaasɩ sige wa paam mam boe zẽ'a la.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa darɛ mĩim zozo'e, tɩ yʋ'a deego la mĩim la a ẽbrɛ waa wuu, tɩ kʋlɩnsɩ la wuu yo'ose, tɩ bãnsɩ la wuu ba'asɛ.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Tɩ mam bɩsra bõnɔ na zĩi, yẽ yire dũnsi, la we-dũnsi, la bõn-vuula, la bõn-ẽgla.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Tɩ yʋ'a deego gu'ura la nẽegɛ isge yẽ tɩ kʋlɩnsɩ la yo'ose mɛ, tɩ a tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba yese mɛ, tɩ a fooge sʋ'a tɩ a kʋ a mẽŋa.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n yetɩ: ‹Apɩyɛɛrɩ, isge kʋ di.›.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Da kãn ẽŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa sɛla, tõma wuu boe la kalam.».

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Ayɛɩ, Zuudãana, sɛla n de sisgo bõnɔ bɩɩ bõn-dẽgrɔ ken ka kẽ mam nõorʋm.›.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Tɩ yʋ'a deego gu'ura la wi yetɩ, ba tarɩ bugum wa'am. Dee ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ kẽ deego la pʋam ka lui Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ nẽŋam gõgra la dabeem.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Tɩ kõa la le ze'ele saazuum tɔgɛ bɔ mam yetɩ: ‹Bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, a ãn sõŋa, da kãn yetɩ, a de la bõn-dẽgrɔ.›.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: isge tarɩ ba yese, soke ba yetɩ: «Bʋraasɩ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne yẽ fãarɛ?».

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: La daan ẽŋɛ la nõorɛ atã, dee viise lebe saazuum.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Bɔ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra, tɩ fõn na fʋ yir-dõma wuu wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.».

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Tɩ bʋraasɩ batã kɔ'ɔn paam yi-sɛka tɩ mam boe mĩ na. Ba daan tõm ba la Sezaare tẽŋam yetɩ, ba wa'am mam zẽ'am.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Tɩ ba tɔgɛ Zuudãana yetɔga bɔ ẽŋa la sɛba wuu n boe a yire la.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele mam yetɩ, m doose ba sẽŋɛ, m da sẽ'ena. Tɩ tɩ sɔbiisi bãna bayoobɩ wã kɔlgɛ mam, tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Akɔrnɛlɩ yire.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ a tarɩ ba ka pɛ ba nõtɔ la. La bilam zẽ'am tɩ ba mise ẽŋa la a yir-dõma wuu ko'om pʋam la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Tɩ a yele tõma tɩ ẽŋa daan yẽ la malɛka n ze ẽŋa yire, yele ẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope ka wi Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩ a wa'am.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Tɩ a tarɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ sẽŋɛ a yire ka bɔ ba dia. Tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma la wuu sũure daan ãna yẽlʋm zozo'e, ban bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: Ẽŋa n wʋn tɔgɛ yetɔg-sɛba n wʋn botɩ fõn na fʋ yir-dõma wuu yẽ fãarɛ.›.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: La beere n wiige, tɩ sɛba n diti sarɩya la tõm ba tõntõnɩba yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka yele sɛka n gu'uri yʋ'a deego la yetɩ, a basɛ bʋraasɩ la.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Mam n boe mĩ tɩ m tɔgɛ la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sige ba zuto wʋ ẽn daan sige tõma zuo se'em pɔspɔsɩ la.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Tɩ yʋ'a deego gu'ura la tɔgɛ bɔ Apolɩ yetɩ: «Sarɩyaditba la bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, m basɛ tɩ ya yese. Bɛla yese-ya fõrgɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm.».

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam tẽegɛ Zuudãana n daan yetɩ: ‹Azã daan misri nẽrba la ko'om pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn mise ya a Sɩa pʋam.›.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ yele tõntõnɩba la yetɩ: «Tõma n de Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽrba, tɩ ba ka di tõma sarɩya dee wẽ tõma, kẽ'esɛ tõma yʋ'a deem, yãŋa yetɩ ba li'ise basɛ tõma? Ayɛɩ, ba mẽŋa wa'am wa yese to.».

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: La sãn dɛna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba a Sɩa, wʋ ẽn daan bɔ tõma n bɔ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi sɩra se'em na, mam de la ãne wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ?».

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Tɩ tõntõnɩba la sẽŋɛ ka yele sarɩyaditba la bɛla. Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba, ban wõm tɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽrba la ĩyã.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Ban wõm bɛla la, ba sĩm mɛ dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ mẽ yẽ sore mɛ tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero yẽ yõvore.».

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Tɩ ba mẽŋa wa'am wa belem ba, botɩ ba yese, dee sose ba yetɩ, ba fõrgɛ dee basɛ ba tẽŋa.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: La A'etɩɛnɩ kũm na poorʋm, nẽr-sɛba n yẽ nãmsgɔ la wɩrgɛ mɛ, sẽŋɛ halɩ Fenisi, la Sipri, la Antɩyɔsɩ ka mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bɔ'ɔra Zifdõma mã'a.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: La ba yese yʋ'a deem na sẽŋɛ la Alidi yire ka tɔgɛ bɔ Azezi karẽnbiisi la bʋraanɛ dee fõrgɛ.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: La ba bʋraasɩ basɛba daan ze'ele la Sipri, la Sirɛnɩ so'olʋm sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ mẽ ka mõolɛ bu-zãŋsɩ, tɔgɛ Zuudãana Azezi kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ba.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: La Zuudãana pãŋa daan boe la ba mɛ. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan bɔ Zuudãana sɩra dɔla ẽ.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: La Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n boe Zerizalɛm na n wõm bɛla la, ba tõm na Abarnabasɩ yetɩ a sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: La ẽn paagɛ yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ nẽrba la yel-sõnnɛ se'em na, a sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ. Tɩ a pa'alɛ ba yetɩ, ba keŋe ba mẽŋa dɔla Zuudãana la ba sũure wuu da basɛ.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a daan de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, dee bɔ Azezi sɩra zo'oge, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ẽ. La nẽrba zozo'e n daan pa'asɛ dɔla Zuudãana.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: La Abarnabasɩ babsɛ mɛ sẽŋɛ Tarsɩ tɩ a ka ɛ Asolɩ.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: La ẽn yẽ ẽ na, a tarɩ ẽ mɛ wa'am Antɩyɔsɩ. La yʋʋnɛ tõrɛ tɩ ba lagsɛ la Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, dee zãsʋm nẽrba zozo'e. La de la Antɩyɔsɩ tẽŋam tɩ ba daan pɔsɛ wi karẽnbiisi la tɩ Krisi nẽrba.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba daan ze'ele Zerizalɛm sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agabisi isge ze'ele tɔgɛ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa yetɩ, kɔm kãtɛ n wʋn wa'am dũnia wuu zuo. La kɔm ẽŋa sɩrɩ wa'am mɛ na-keko Akloorɩ wakatʋm.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, nẽra woo looge ẽn tarɩ sɛla bɔ tɩ ba to'oge sõŋɛ Azezi nẽr-sɛba n boe Zude la.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Tɩ ba daan ẽŋɛ bɛla dɩkɛ ligri la ẽŋɛ Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ nu'usum, tõm ba yetɩ, ba tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka bɔ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la kẽma n boe Zerizalɛm na.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ doose la Amfipoli, la Apoloni tẽnsʋm tole sẽŋɛ Tesaloniki, zẽ'e-ẽŋa Zifdõma wẽndeego n daan boe mĩ.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ daan kẽ mĩ wʋ ẽ ẽn ɩta se'em na, tɔgɛ bõn-sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na pa'alɛ ba vo'osgo dabsa atã.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: A tɔgɛ pa'ala ba yetɩ, la masɛ mɛ tɩ Krisi nãmsɛ ki dee vo'oge. Dee yetɩ: «Azezi tɩ mam mõolɩ a yelle bɔ'ɔra ya wã de la Krisi la.».

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Tɩ ba nẽrba basɛba, la Grɛkɩdõma nẽrba zozo'e n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, la pɔgsɩ zozo'e n de nẽrzuto daan sakɛ po dɔla Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma daan ẽŋɛ sũure, sẽŋɛ da'am ka ɛ nẽrba n ka tarɩ võorɛ lagsɛ tɩ ba dãm tẽŋa la. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azasõ yire ka ɛɛra Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ tɩ ba tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la nẽrba zẽ'am.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: La ba sẽŋɛ ka ɛ ka yẽ ba, yõkɛ Azasõ la Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la kɛ'ɛndõma zẽ'am ka tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã eerɩ dãmna la dũnia wuu. La ban wãna n wa'am kalam.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: tɩ Azasõ to'oge ba. Nẽr-bãna wã wuu sɩ'ɩsrɩ la Rom na-keko nõorɛ yetɩ, naba ayẽma n boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azezi.».

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: La kɛ'ɛndõma la, la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n wõm bɛla la, ba sũure isge mɛ.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Tɩ ba botɩ Azasõ la sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na bĩŋe ligri, dee tɩ ba basɛ ba.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: La wẽnnɛ n daan sobe, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la botɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ sẽŋɛ Beere. La ban paagɛ la, ba sẽŋɛ mɛ ka kẽ Zifdõma wẽndeem.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: La bãma tarɩ pʋtẽ'e-sõnnɛ mɛ gãnna Zifdõma sɛba n boe Tesaloniki la, sakɛ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la ba sũure wuu, dee daan karẽŋra Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la daarɛ woo tɩ ba bɩsɛ Apolɩ n yele se'em na de la sɩra bɩɩ la dagɩ sɩra.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: La ba nẽrba zozo'e, la Grɛkɩdõma pɔgsɩ n de yʋ'ʋrɛ dõma, la bʋraasɩ zozo'e sakɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Tesaloniki la wõm tɩ Apolɩ mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Beere, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ mĩ ka dãm nẽrba la, sa'asɛ ba la Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ botɩ nẽrba basɛba kɔlgɛ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm. Dee tɩ Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ weege Beere.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: La sɛba n kɔlgɛ Apolɩ la tarɩ ẽ paagɛ la Atɛnɩ, tɩ Apolɩ soolʋm ba yetɩ, ba sãn paagɛ, ba yele Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ yetɩ, ba wa'am tɔtɔ.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: La Apolɩ daan boe la Atɛnɩ gu'ura Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ. La a daan yẽ la baga n boe tẽŋa la pʋam zozo'e, tɩ a sũure sã'am.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ Zifdõma wẽndeem ka tɔgra la Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, dee tɔgra daarɛ woo la nẽrba n boe da'am.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Tɩ bãŋrɩba daan bɔna mĩ tɔgra la Apolɩ, basɛba dolɩ la A'epikiri zãsŋɔ, dee tɩ basɛba dɔla Astoyisi zãsŋɔ. Tɩ nẽrba basɛba soke yetɩ: «Yetɔg-tɔgsa wã boorɩ tɩ a yetɩ bẽm?» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «La ãn wʋ a mõolɩ la wẽn-paalsɩ yelle la.» Se'ere n soe la, Apolɩ mõolɩ la Azezi kõ-yẽlga, la kũm vo'ore yelle.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Arewopaazɩ zẽ'am ka yetɩ: «Tõma boorɩ la tɩ bãŋɛ zãsŋɔ paalga sɛka tɩ fõn tɔgrɩ la võorɛ.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Fõn tɔgɛ yel-sɛba tɩ tõma wõm wã de la yel-sãasɩ bɔ tõma, bɛla tɩ tõma bɔɔra tɩ bãŋɛ a võorɛ.».

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Wakatɛ wuu Atɛnɩ dõma, la nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ bilam na, tɔgrɩ la yel-paala dee sɛlsra yel-paala.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ isge ze'ele Arewopaazɩ la tẽŋasʋka yetɩ: «Atɛnɩ tẽŋa nẽrba, mam yẽ tɩ ya de la nẽrba n nã'asrɩ baga zozo'e sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Mam n gilge ya tẽŋa wã pʋam na, mam yẽ bõn-sɛba tɩ ya ẽŋɛ nã'asra mɛ, la mam yẽ la kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zuo tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Wẽn-sɛka tɩ tõma ka mi.› Wẽn-sɛka tɩ yãma nã'asrɩ dee ka mina ẽ na, tɩ mam mõolɩ bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋa n nãam dũnia la sɛla wuu n boe ba pʋam, ẽŋa n soe saazuum la tẽŋa. A ka kẽ'erɩ deego tɩ nẽrsaalba mẽ pʋam.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ nẽrsaalba tõm bɔ ẽ. Ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla woo yõvore, la vo'osʋm, la sɛla woo.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: A nãam la nẽra ayɩla botɩ nẽrba buuri wuu n boe dũnia zuo ze'ele la ẽŋa zẽ'am. Ẽŋa n makɛ wakat-sɛba la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ ba wʋn bɔna.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ ba ɛ ẽ mɛ, ba sãn ɛɛra babla, tã tɩ ba wʋn yẽ ẽ. La a ka zãagɛ la tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Ẽŋa n botɩ tɩ bɔna, sẽnna dee tara vɔm, wʋ yãma Atɛnɩ so'olʋm nẽrba basɛba n daan yetɩ: ‹Tõma mẽ de la a kɔma.›.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Tõma n de a kɔma la, tɩ da tẽ'esɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wõn na bõn-nãanɩsɩ tɩ nẽrsaalba dɩkɛ sãlma bɩɩ zũuro bɩɩ kuga tẽ'esɛ mãalɛ la nu'usi.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka geele wakat-sɛka tɩ tõma daan ka mi la. La lɛɛlɛ wã, a bɔ nõorɛ zẽ'a woo yetɩ, nẽrba wuu teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a looge la daarɛ tɩ a di dũnia nẽrba sarɩya, a dẽŋɛ looge la nẽra, ẽŋa n wʋn di nẽrba la wuu sarɩya la sɩra. A vo'oge nẽr-ẽŋa pa'alɛ tɩ la de la sɩra.».

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Ban wõm tɩ Apolɩ tɔgrɩ kũm vo'ore yelle la, tɩ sɛba la'ara ẽ, dee tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Tõma wʋn le selse fʋ yetɔg-bãna la da-se'ere.».

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele ba zẽ'am fõrgɛ.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: La nẽrba basɛba daan doose ẽ mɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra. Nẽr-sɛba n daan bɔ sɩra la de la Adenisi, ẽŋa de la Arewopaazɩ sarɩyaditba nẽra ayɩla, la pɔka ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Adamarisi, la nẽrba basɛba.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Apolɩ isge Atɛnɩ sẽŋɛ la Korẽntɩ.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Ẽn paagɛ bilam na, a yẽ la Zifdõma bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akila tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ Põ, tɩ ẽŋa la a pɔga Aprisili ze'ele Rom n boe Itali so'olʋm sẽŋɛ paam, se'ere n soe la, na-keko Akloorɩ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ Zifdõma wuu n boe Rom tẽŋam fõrgɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: La bãma la Apolɩ tõoma daan de la bʋyɩla, ba pɛ'ɛrɩ la futo tɩ ba da'ara tĩ'ira fuu-deto, bɛla ĩyã tɩ Apolɩ kẽ'era bilam, tɩ ba lagɩm tõnna.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: La Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ woo Apolɩ sẽnnɩ ba wẽndeem mɛ tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ'ɔra ba, mõrgra tɩ a botɩ Zifdõma, la Grɛkɩdõma sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: La Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ n daan ze'ele Masedʋanɩ paagɛ la, Apolɩ daan basɛ a tõoma la mɛ, dee mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la pa'ala Zifdõma tɩ Azezi n de Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Tɩ ba sɩ'ɩsɛ dee tɔgra ẽ tɔg-be'ero. Tɩ a pĩsũm a futo pa'alɛ tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn yẽ nãmsgɔ, ya yelle boe la ya mẽŋa zuo, la dagɩ mam yelle. La zĩna tɛka mam wʋn sẽŋɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la zẽ'am.».

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Apolɩ daan yele bɛla, dee yese mɛ, sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Atitiyusi-zustusi yire. A de la bu-zãŋka dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a yire lɛm Zifdõma wẽndeego la.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: La Akrispusi n de wẽndeego la nẽŋadãana, la a yir-dõma wuu daan bɔ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra mɛ. La Korẽntɩ nẽrba zozo'e n daan selsrɩ kõ-yẽlga la, mẽ bɔ sɩra mɛ, tɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Tɩ daarɛ ayɩla yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ Apolɩ yẽ wʋ zãasŋɔ la, tɩ Zuudãana yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem sĩna, ken tɔgra.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: tɩ mam boe la fʋ mɛ, nẽra nẽra kãn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ fʋ be'em. Se'ere n soe la, tẽŋa wã pʋam nẽrba zozo'e de la mam nẽrba.».

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Apolɩ zĩ'ire bilam na yʋʋnɛ la wõr-sɩyoobɩ zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: La wakat-sɛka tɩ Agaliyõ daan de Akayi so'olʋm gɔmna la, Zifdõma daan isge naagɛ la taaba dõrɛ Apolɩ tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ sarɩyadia zẽ'am.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: ka yetɩ: «Ẽna wã n mõrgrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ se'em na sɩ'ɩsrɩ la tõma lɔɔ.».

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ daan bɔna mĩ tɩ a tɔgɛ, tɩ Agaliyõ yele Zifdõma la yetɩ: «La sãn de nɩ na sɛla n ka masɛ bɩɩ tõon-be'ero tɩ a ẽŋɛ, mam wʋn mõrgɛ selse yãma Zifdõma.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: La, la sãn dɛna ya wẽ'erɩ la nõke'ene la yetɔga, la yʋ'ʋra, la ya mẽŋa lɔɔ yɛla, ya mẽŋa bɩsɛ bõn-bãma yelle. Tɩ mam kãn di bõn-ẽŋa sarɩya.».

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: A yele bɛla, dee dige ba sarɩya zẽ'am na mɛ tɩ ba yese.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Tɩ ba wuu yõkɛ Asostɛnɩ n de Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana wẽ ẽ sarɩyadia la zẽ'am. La Agaliyõ toogo ka boe la ban ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: La Apolɩ ken zĩ'ire Korẽntɩ la dabsa zozo'e la karẽnbiisi la lɛ, dee keese ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Siiri. Tɩ Aprisili la Akila doose ẽ. La ban daan paagɛ Sãnkre la, Apolɩ dẽŋɛ siige la a zuugo, se'ere n soe la, a bĩŋe la nõorɛ la Nawẽnnɛ. Dee tɩ ba yãŋa kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: ka paagɛ Efɛɛzɩ, tɩ Apolɩ basɛ Aprisili la Akila bilam. A sẽŋɛ ka kẽ la Zifdõma wẽndeem tɔgɛ la ba.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Tɩ ba sose ẽ yetɩ, a weege ba zẽ'am yuuge. Tɩ a zagsɛ.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: dee keese ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn sakɛ, m wʋn leme ya zẽ'am.» La a daan kẽ la õorŋɔ Efɛɛzɩ.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: sẽŋɛ Sezaare ka sige bilam, sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Azezi nẽr-sɛba n boe bilam na, dee sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: La a yuuge bilam mɛ fẽe, dee yese ɛ Galatɩ la Frizi pa'asɛ karẽnbiisi la wuu pãŋa.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: La Zifu bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Apolɔsɩ tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ Alɛgsãndri n wa'am Efɛɛzɩ, a de la tɔg-mita dee mina Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ sõŋa.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Ba daan pa'alɛ ẽ Zuudãana Azezi sore yɛla mɛ, tɩ a daan zãsna nẽrba so-ẽŋa yɛla sõŋa la a sũure wuu. La a daan mi la Azã ko'om misga la mã'a.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Tɩ a daan tɔgra Zifdõma wẽndeem la bʋraanɛ. La Aprisili la Akila n wõm ẽn tɔgrɩ la, tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka pa'alɛ ẽ Nawẽnnɛ sore la yɛla sõŋa pa'asɛ.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a sẽŋɛ Akayi, tɩ Azezi karẽnbi-sɛba n boe Efɛɛzɩ la gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ bɔ Azezi karẽnbiisi la n boe Akayi la yetɩ, ba to'oge ẽ sõŋa. La ẽn paagɛ bilam na, a sõŋɛ sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan bɔ ba a yel-sõnnɛ tɩ ba bɔ ẽ sɩra la mɛ zozo'e.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: La a daan dɩkɛ la Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ tɔgɛ yãŋɛ Zifdõma nẽrba wuu nẽŋam, pa'alɛ ba tɩ Azezi de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: La Apolɔsɩ n daan nãn boe Korẽntɩ la, Apolɩ doose la tãnsɩ zuo tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam tole, sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Efɛɛzɩ yẽ karẽnbiisi basɛba bilam.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma n bɔ Azezi sɩra la, yãma to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mɛ bɩɩ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, tõma nãn ka wõm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe.».

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ soke ba yetɩ: «Ko'om misga kãna tɩ yãma to'oge?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Tõma to'oge la Azã ko'om misga.».

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Azã daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam pa'ala tɩ ba teege la yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. A daan yetɩ nẽrba la, tɩ nẽra n weege ẽŋa poorʋm sẽna, ba doose ẽŋa. Nẽr-ẽŋa de la Azezi.».

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: La ban wõm bɛla la, ba botɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam mɛ Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wa'am ba zuo, tɩ ba tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ dee to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Ba daan de wʋ nẽrba pia la ayi.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Apolɩ daan kẽ Efɛɛzɩ la wõr-sɩtã, daan kẽ'era Zifdõma wẽndeem mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bʋraanɛ bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee mõrgɛ pa'alɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yɛla tɩ ba sakɛ.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Tɩ basɛba daan kɛ'ɛm ba sũure ka sakɛ, dee tɔgra Zuudãana sore la tɔg-be'ero nẽrba nẽŋam. Tɩ Apolɩ basɛ ba dee welge karẽnbiisi la fõrgɛ. Tɩ daarɛ woo tɩ a zãsna ba Atiranusi karẽndeego pʋam.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla la yʋʋma ayi. La nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe Azi tẽnsʋm na wõm Zuudãana yetɔga mɛ, Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ wuu.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ daan basɛ tɩ Apolɩ tõm na tõon-yãlm-kãra.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: La ba daan sãn ẽn dɩkɛ ãnkɩra bɩɩ futo n kalʋm a ĩyã tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka kalʋm bã'adõma, ba yẽtɩ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ, tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ yɛsra dee basra ba.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma basɛba n daan gilgri digra kʋlkã'arsɩ nẽrba zẽ'am, daan makɛ tɩ ba dige kʋlkã'arsɩ la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la Azezi sɛka tɩ Apolɩ mõolɩ la yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ ya yese.».

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Ba daan de la Aseeva n de Zifdõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana dayɔɔsɩ bayopɔɩ n ɩtɩ bɛla.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam mi Azezi mɛ, dee mina Apolɩ n de sɛka, la yãma de la ãndõma?».

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la n tarɩ sɛka la isge zɛbɛ ba la pãŋa yãŋɛ ba wuu, tɩ ba ze'ele yire la zoe yese bɛɛla la pɔglʋŋɔ.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ la wuu n boe Efɛɛzɩ la wõm yel-ẽna wã. Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba wuu, tɩ ba pẽgra Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e bɔ Azezi sɩra wa'am wa pelge ba pʋa tɔgɛ ba tõon-be'ero nẽrba nẽŋam.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Tɩ ba zozo'e n tarɩ ba gõnnɔ tõnna tɩɩm tõoma la, lagsɛ ba yõ la bugum nẽrba la wuu nifum. Tɩ ba geele gõnnɔ na wuu ligri, tɩ ba de la lig-kõba tʋspinuu.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: La Zuudãana n pa'alɛ a pãŋa bɛla la, tɩ a yetɔga la saara zẽ'a wuu dee yẽta pãŋa.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Apolɩ tẽ'esɛ a sũurum yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn sẽŋɛ la Masedʋanɩ, la Akayi ka ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm, dee yetɩ: «Mam sãn paagɛ bɔna Zerizalɛm, dẽnɩ tɩ mam paagɛ Rom.».

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: La a tõm na Atimõtɩ, la A'erastɩ n de a sõŋrɩba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Masedʋanɩ, dee weege a mẽŋa Azi yuuge fẽe.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: La wakat-ẽŋa dãmŋɔ zozo'e n isge Efɛɛzɩ Zuudãana Azezi sore la doosgo yelle.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: La bʋraa n daan de kuta, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ademetrisi. A daan tarɩ la zũuro kuta bõndõma tɩ ba wõna la ba wẽnnɛ Artemisi deego la. Tɩ zũuro tõntõnɩba la daan yẽ yõorɔ zozo'e tõon-ẽŋa pʋam.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Tɩ Ademetrisi lagsɛ ba, la sɛba wuu n tõnnɩ tõon-ẽŋa buuri, yele ba yetɩ: «Bʋraasɩ wã, ya mi tɩ tõon-ẽna wã pʋam tɩ tɩ yẽtɩ bõntarsõm.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Ya mẽŋa wõm dee yẽ Apolɩ n ɩtɩ se'em. A pa'alɩ nẽrba tɩ wẽn-sɛba tɩ nẽrsaalba ẽŋɛ la ba mẽŋa nu'usi la dagɩ wẽna sɩra sɩra. A ẽŋɛ bɛla mõrgɛ teege nẽrba zozo'e pʋtẽ'era mɛ Efɛɛzɩ kalam, la, la dee la fẽe tɩ a paagɛ Azi tẽnsɩ wã wuu pʋam.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Bõn-ẽna wʋn botɩ tõma tõonɛ na sã'am mɛ. La sɛla n pa'asɛ, wẽn-kãtɛ Artemisi deego la wʋn lebge la zãŋa, la Artemisi mẽŋa tɩ Azi nẽrba, la dũnia nẽrba wuu nã'asrɩ la, pãŋa wʋn sã'am mɛ.».

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: La ban wõm yetɔg-bãna la, ba sũa yĩige mɛ tɩ ba tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Efɛɛzɩ dõma wẽnnɛ Artemisi n tarɩ pãŋa.».

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la wuu dãmɛ. Tɩ nẽrba la yõkɛ Agayusi la Aristarkɩ tarɩ ba zoe sẽŋɛ lagsgɔ zẽ'am, bãma bayi de la Masedʋanɩ nẽrba n dolɩ Apolɩ.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la zẽ'am, tɩ karẽnbiisi la ka sakɛ.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Tɩ Azi tẽŋa nẽŋadõma basɛba n de a sɩrdõma tõm nẽrba yetɩ, ba wa'am a zẽ'am wa belem ẽ yetɩ, a da sẽŋɛ lagsgɔ zẽ'am na.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lagɩm vɔɔla zozo'e, tɩ nẽra woo tɔgra ẽŋa n tẽ'esɛ se'em, tɩ ba zozo'e ka mina bõn-sɛka n botɩ ba lagsɛ.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma looge Alɛgsãndrɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽrba la nẽŋam ka tɔgɛ, tɩ nẽrba la basɛba pa'alɛ ẽ yelle la võorɛ. Tɩ Alɛgsãndrɩ yɩbsɛ la nu'usi tɩ a tɔgɛ yelle la pa'alɛ nẽrba la.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: La ban bãŋɛ tɩ a de la Zifu la, tɩ ba wuu lagɩm taaba tĩi kaasɛ yetɩ: «Efɛɛzɩ dõma wẽnnɛ Artemisi n tarɩ pãŋa.» Ba kaasɛ bɛla paagɛ karf-ayi.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la gõn-gʋlsa botɩ ba go'oge, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Efɛɛzɩ nẽrba, dũnia nẽrba wuu mi mɛ tɩ wẽn-kãtɛ Artemisi pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego la boe la Efɛɛzɩ kalam, la tõma n de a pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego la gu'urba, la kugre la n wõn na Artemisi ze'ele saazuum lui la gu'urba.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Nẽra nẽra kãn yãŋɛ sɩ'ɩsɛ yel-ẽna. Bɛla ĩyã, go'oge-ya voole, da lɔgɛ-ya ẽŋɛ sɛla n ka masɛ.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Yan tarɩ nẽr-bãna wa'am kalam wã, ba ka yele tɩ wẽnnɛ na yel-be'ego, la ba ka kẽ a pʋ'ʋsgɔ deem na ẽŋɛ sɛla n de be'em.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: La Ademetrisi la a tõntõnɩba la sãn tara yelle la nẽr-sɛka, sarɩyadia daarɛ boe mɛ, la sarɩyaditba mẽ boe mɛ. A sãn bɔɔra, a tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ bilam ka sãm.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: La ya sãn yẽ la yel-sɛka tɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya malgɛ, tẽŋa la nẽrba lagsgɔ daarɛ, tɩ wʋn malgɛ.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Yãma n ẽŋɛ sɛla zĩna wã wʋn wa'am na yelle, tɩ ba wʋn dõrɛ tɩ yetɩ, tɩ gire la tẽŋa. La tɩ kãn yãŋɛ pa'alɛ sɛla n botɩ tɩ lagsɛ gire girga la. La tɩ kãn yãŋɛ tɔgɛ la võorɛ sõŋa.».

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: Ẽŋa n yele bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, dee yele ba tɩ ba wɩrgɛ.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Lan daan gire mã'agɛ la poorʋm, tɩ Apolɩ botɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la lagsɛ, tɩ a tɔgɛ bɔ ba bʋraanɛ, keese ba dee wɛ'ɛsa Masedʋanɩ.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: La ẽn daan dolɩ so'olʋm ẽŋa tẽn-sɛba pʋam tɔlla la, a tɔgɛ yetɔga zozo'e mɛ bɔ karẽnbiisi la bʋraanɛ. Dee yãŋa tole ka paagɛ Grɛsɩ.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: La ẽn paagɛ bilam na, a zĩ'ire mĩ na wõr-sɩtã, mãasʋm tɩ a kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Siiri, wõm tɩ Zifdõma dolɩ la a nãma. Tɩ a tẽ'esɛ yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn lebe doose la Masedʋanɩ.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Sɛba n daan dolɩ ẽ na de la: Apirusi bia Asopatɛɛrɩ n ze'ele Beere, la Aristarkɩ la Asekõndusi n ze'ele Tesaloniki, la Agayusi n ze'ele Dɛrbɩ, la Atisiki la Atrofim n ze'ele Azi, pa'asɛ la Atimõtɩ.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Nẽr-bãna wã daan dẽŋɛ tole la nẽŋa ka gu'ura tõma Trʋasɩ. Dee tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ Filipi tẽŋam.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: La borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la poorʋm, tɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ Filipi, tɩ dabsa anuu daarɛ tɩ tõma paagɛ sɛba n boe Trʋasɩ gu'ura tõma la zẽ'am, zĩ'ire bilam dabsa ayopɔɩ.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: La vo'osgo daarɛ ayɩla yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ tõma lagsɛ tɩ tɩ di, dee fiise borborɩ. Tɩ Apolɩ daan isge tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la nẽrba la halɩ ka paagɛ sõnsʋa, se'ere n soe la, a boorɩ tɩ beere sãn wiige, a fõrgɛ mɛ.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: La fɩtladõma zozo'e n daan boe saazuum deto batã dãana la tɩ tõma lagsɛ a pʋam na.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Tɩ budib-polle yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'etikusi zẽa deego la takolle zuo. La Apolɩ n tɔgɛ yuuge la, tɩ gẽem yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a gĩse. La a gĩse la gẽ-bergo ze'ele saazuum deego la tɩ tõma boe mĩ na lui tẽŋa. Tɩ ba zẽkɛ ẽ, tɩ a ki mɛ.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ sige yigle õbgɛ ẽ zãla a nu'usum, dee yetɩ: «Da fabla-ya, a vɔa mɛ.».

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: A yele bɛla dee lebe zom ka fiise la borborɩ obe, dee tɔgɛ la ba yuuge halɩ ka paagɛ bewiine, dee yãŋa fõrgɛ.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: La budibla la vɔa mɛ, tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ kule. Tɩ la ẽŋɛ nẽrba la sũure yẽlʋm halɩ zozo'e.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
