local: La tõma kẽ la õorŋɔ dẽŋɛ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ Asɔsɩ, a daan yetɩ a sẽŋɛ la nãma wa yakɛ tõma bilam.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: La ẽn yakɛ tõma Asɔsɩ la, a kẽ la õorŋɔ, tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ Mitilɛnɩ.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: La tõma ze'ele la bilam doose ko'om na pʋam, tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Kiyo zanõ-bɔba. Tɩ beere le wiige, tɩ tõma doose Samõsɩ. Tɩ beere le wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Milɛtɩ.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: La Apolɩ daan looge yɛm tɩ a ka gõlgɛ sẽŋɛ la Efɛɛzɩ, se'ere n soe la, a daan ka boorɩ tɩ a gʋ'ʋm a mẽŋa Azi so'olʋm na pʋam, a daan kãblɩ mɛ tɩ a paagɛ Zerizalɛm dee tɩ Pãntekɔtɩ daarɛ la paagɛ.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele Milɛtɩ soolʋm Efɛɛzɩ yetɩ, ba wi Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la kẽma tɩ ba wa'am.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: La ban paagɛ a zẽ'am na, a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma mi mam daan ãn se'em, wakat-sɛka wuu tɩ mam daan boe la yãma la mɛ, lan pĩ'ilum na daar-sɛka tɩ mam wa'am Azi so'olʋm pʋam na.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: Mam sike m mẽŋa mɛ tõm Nawẽnnɛ tõoma la nintãm, baa nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam yẽ Zifdõma n dolɩ mam nãma la pʋam.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Mam mõolɛ bõn-sɛba n wʋn sõŋɛ ya mɛ pa'alɛ ya nẽrba sʋkam la ya yiɛ pʋam, ka sʋgɛ sɛla sɛla.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: Mam tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ mɛ yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, sakɛ doose Nawẽnnɛ, dee bɔ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa nõorɛ mɛ yetɩ m sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm, la mam ka mi bõn-sɛka n wʋn ka ẽŋɛ mam mĩ.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: La mam n mi se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ mam mɛ tẽn-sɛba wuu pʋam tɩ mam sẽŋɛ la, tɩ ba wʋn nãmsɛ mam mɛ dee kẽ'esɛ mam yʋ'a deem.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: La mam ka geele m mẽŋa yõvore la tɩ a tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ mam, sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ mam de la mam yãŋɛ tõm m tõonɛ na tɩ Zuudãana Azezi bɔ mam na ba'asɛ. Tõon-ẽŋa de la mam mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na kõ-yẽlga.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam mi tɩ ya kãn le yẽ mam, yãma sɛba wuu tɩ mam mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yɛla ya zẽ'am na.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Bɛla ĩyã mam yetɩ ya mɛ zĩna, tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka sãn koŋe vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a taalɛ ka boe mam zuo.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na wuu bɔ ya mɛ, ka sʋgɛ sɛla sɛla.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Tara-ya ya mẽŋa, la nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa gu'ule ya la sõŋa. Bɩsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la tɩ a fãagɛ la a Bia zɩɩm tɩ ba dɛna a mẽŋa nẽrba la sõŋa.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: Mam mi tɩ mam sãn fõrgɛ deege, nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ n wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa sã'am ya, wʋ saasaadõma n yõgrɩ dũnsi se'em na.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: La nẽrba basɛba wʋn wa isge ya pʋam tɔgra põmpɔrŋɔ yetɔga, pã'asɛ karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba doose ba.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, guuse-ya ya mẽŋa, dee tẽra tɩ yʋʋma atã, mam kã'an ya nẽra woo wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ mɛ, la nintãm ka basɛ.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam dɩkɛ ya ẽŋɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum, la a yel-sõnnɛ yetɔga la pʋam. Ẽŋa n tã wʋn sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya yẽ pãŋa pa'asɛ, dee wʋn botɩ ya po lagɩm to'oge bõn-sõma tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: Mam daan ka tarɩ yɛmleego la nẽra nẽra ligri, la a sãlma, bɩɩ a futo.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Ya mẽŋa mi tɩ mam dɩkɛ la m nu'usi tõm bɩsɛ m mẽŋa la sɛba n dolɩ mam na.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Mam ẽŋɛ bɛla wuu pa'alɛ ya tɩ dẽnɩ ya tõm sõŋɛ tarɩmdõma. La ya tẽra Zuudãana Azezi n yele se'em na, a daan yetɩ: ‹Sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ tarɩ sũ-yẽlga zozo'e gãnna sɛka n to'orɩ.› ».

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: La Apolɩ n daan tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, ẽŋa la ba wuu ka la dũma tẽŋa, tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Tɩ ba wuu tĩi kɛlla, dee kɔkɛ Apolɩ mõbgɛ ẽ.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Ba sũure n daan sã'am zozo'e, ẽn yetɩ ba kãn le yẽ ẽ na ĩyã. La ba kɔlgɛ ẽ mɛ sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ õorŋɔ la zẽ'am.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Tɩ tõma keese ba, dee kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ tẽntẽa ka paagɛ Kɔsɩ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Rɔdɩ ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Patara.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: La bilam tɩ tõma yẽ õorŋɔ ayẽma n we Fenisi. Tɩ tõma kẽ sẽŋɛ.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: La tõma daan sẽŋɛ mɛ ka tũule yẽta Sipri, la tõma daan basɛ Sipri tẽŋa la gɔbga bɔba, dee tole ka paagɛ Siiri so'olʋm sige Tiiri. Bilam tɩ ba yetɩ ba sike õorŋɔ la lɔgrɔ.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Tɩ tõma sige sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Azezi karẽnbiisi bilam, zĩ'ire la ba lɛ dabsa ayopɔɩ. Tɩ ba yele Apolɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa yetɩ, a da sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: La dabsa ayopɔɩ la daarɛ, tɩ tõma isge tɩ tɩ tole, tɩ bãma la ba pɔgba, la ba kɔma wuu kɔlgɛ tõma yese tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa. Tɩ tõma wuu ka dũma ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: dee keese taaba. Tɩ tõma yãŋa kẽ õorŋɔ, dee tɩ ba lebe kulla.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: La tõma ze'ele Tiiri sẽŋɛ la Tolemasɩ ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Azezi karẽnbi-sɛba n boe bilam na, dee ẽŋɛ daarɛ ayɩla ba zẽ'am.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Sezaare ka sige Afilipi yire kẽ'era bilam. Afilipi daan de la nẽrba bayopɔɩ la tɩ ba daan looge Zerizalɛm na nẽra ayɩla, a daan mõolɩ la Azezi kõ-yẽlga.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: A daan tarɩ la pɔyɔɔsɩ banaasɩ n ka mi bʋraasɩ, ba daan to'osrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Tõma daan ẽŋɛ bilam na dabsa faa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agabisi ze'ele Zude wa'am.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: wa paam tõma zẽ'am wa fooge Apolɩ sagãnnɛ lʋ a mẽŋa nãma la a nu'usi, dee yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele la wãna yetɩ: ‹Zifdõma wʋn lʋ sagãn-ẽna wã dãana la wãna Zerizalɛm, dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum.› ».

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: La tõma n wõm bɛla la, tɩ tõma la nẽr-sɛba n boe bilam na, daan bɛlna Apolɩ yetɩ, a da sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Tɩ a lerge tõma yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya kaasrɩ sã'ana m sũure? Mam sakɛ mɛ tɩ ba lʋ ma, la dagɩ bɛla mã'a, baa mam sãn wʋn ki Zerizalɛm Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, mam ken sakɛ mɛ.».

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: La tõma n kãn yãŋɛ botɩ a sakɛ bɔ tõma la ĩyã, tɩ tõma koŋe yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana n boorɩ se'em ẽŋɛ.».

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ tõma daan yãŋa mãasʋm wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Tɩ Sezaare tẽŋa karẽnbiisi basɛba kɔlgɛ tõma sẽŋɛ Aminasõ yire, bilam tɩ tõma wʋn ka gã'arɛ. Aminasõ de la Sipri nẽra, a de la Azezi karẽnbia halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: La tõma n paagɛ Zerizalɛm na, tɩ sɔbiisi la to'oge tõma la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma doose Apolɩ sẽŋɛ Azakɩ yire. Tɩ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ kẽma la wuu wa'am mĩ.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ ba, dee tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bu-zãŋsɩ la tẽŋasʋka ẽŋa tõonɛ na pʋam.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: La ban wõm bɛla la, ba pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, dee yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Tɩ sɔbia, fõn yẽ Zifdõma tʋsa tʋsa n bɔ Azezi sɩra se'em dee mõrgɛ sakra Amoyisi lɔɔ sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Ba wõm tɩ ba yetɩ, fõn zãsnɩ Zifdõma sɛba wuu n boe bu-zãŋsɩ tẽnsʋm na, yɛta tɩ ba basɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ, la ba da wãta ba kɔma, la ba da dɔla Zifdõma dɔgmike.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Ba wʋn wõm tɩ fõn wa'am mɛ, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Ẽŋɛ tõma n wʋn yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ se'em na. Nẽrba banaasɩ n boe tõma zẽ'am kalam bĩŋe nõorɛ la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka lagɩm na ba malgɛ fʋ mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dee yɔ, ba wʋn yɔ se'em na bɔ ba tɩ ba siige ba zuto. Bɛla wʋn botɩ nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ tɩ fõn mẽŋa dolɩ la Amoyisi lɔɔ la, la ban tɔgɛ fʋ yelle se'em na wuu dagɩ sɩra.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: La sãn dɛna bu-zãŋsɩ la n bɔ Azezi sɩra la yelle, tõma daan looge yɛm gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ba da dita di-sɛba tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ baga, la ba da di zɩɩm, la ba da di sɛla tɩ ba tẽgɛ kʋ. La ba da tõnna yalsɩ tõoma.› ».

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Ban yele bɛla la, tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Apolɩ tarɩ nẽrba banaasɩ la sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ka lagɩm malgɛ ba mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dee tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɔgɛ pa'alɛ dabsa se'em n deege tɩ ba wʋn malgɛ ba mẽŋa kɩ'ɩlɩm, dee tɩ ba yãŋa kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽra woo ĩyã.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: La dabsa ayopɔɩ la n dee fẽe la a ba'asɛ la, tɩ Zifdõma ze'ele Azi so'olʋm wa'am wa yẽ Apolɩ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, botɩ nẽrba la sũnsõa yĩige la Apolɩ, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: dee tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, sõŋɛ-ya tɔ! Bʋra-ẽna wã gilgri la tẽnsɩ wuu zãsna nẽrba tɩ ba sɩ'ɩsra Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma ka dɔla Amoyisi lɔɔ, dee tɔgra Wẽnde-kãtɛ la tɔg-be'ero. A tarɩ la nẽrba n dagɩ Zifdõma wa'am wa kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam dẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la.».

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba daan yẽ la Efɛɛzɩ nẽra Atrofim n dolɩ Apolɩ tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a doose ẽ kẽ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la daan gire, tɩ nẽrba wuu zoe lagsɛ taaba yõkɛ Apolɩ ve'ege ẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yese. Dee yʋ zanõa la tɔtɔ.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Tɩ ba daan zɩgra tɩ ba kʋ Apolɩ. Tɩ Rom sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma wõm tɩ Zerizalɛm tẽŋa wuu gire mɛ.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Tɩ a kɔ'ɔn doose sʋdaasɩ, la ba kɛ'ɛndõma tɩ ba zoe sẽŋɛ nẽrba la zẽ'am. La ban yẽ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma, la sʋdaasɩ la, tɩ ba basɛ ka le wẽ'era Apolɩ.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la paagɛ yõkɛ Apolɩ, dee bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba ba ẽ na bãnsɩ bayi. Dee tɩ a soke bãŋɛ ẽn de nẽr-sɛka, la ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ sɛla.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tãsra yẽma yẽma, halɩ tĩi vɔɔla tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la ka tã'agɛ wõm sɛla n ẽŋɛ, tɩ a bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ bãma yi-kãtɛ la pʋam.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: La ban tarɩ ẽ paagɛ bĩmbĩna la n zonnɩ kẽ'era deego la pʋam na, ba buke ẽ mɛ, nẽrba la n ba gita la ĩyã.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Tɩ ba wuu dɔla tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Kʋ-ya ẽ.».

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: La ban yetɩ ba kẽ'esɛ Apolɩ sʋdaasɩ la yire la, tɩ Apolɩ soke sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la yetɩ: «Mam tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ m yele fʋ sɛla mɛ bɩɩ?» Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wõnnɩ Grɛkɩ yetɔga mɛ?.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Bɛla fõn dagɩ Ezipti nẽr-sɛka n daan sa'asɛ nẽrba la gɔmna, dee daan doose nẽr-kʋʋrba tʋsanaasɩ sẽŋɛ weem na?».

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, mam de la Zifu, mam ze'ele la Tarsɩ n boe Silisi so'olʋm. Mam de la tẽn-ẽŋa n tarɩ yõorɔ la nẽra. Mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ, bɔ mam sore tɩ m tɔgɛ la nẽrba la.».

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la bɔ ẽ nõorɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele bĩmbĩna la zuo zẽkɛ a nu'ugo. Tɩ nẽrba la ãna sĩm. Tɩ a tɔgɛ Ebre n de ba mẽŋa yetɔga bɔ ba.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: A yele ba yetɩ: «M sɔdõma la m sɔbiisi, selse-ya mam n yetɩ m tɔgɛ se'em to'oge m mẽŋa ya nẽŋam.».

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Ban wõm tɩ a tɔgrɩ la Ebre yetɔga bɔ'ɔra ba la, tɩ ba go'oge malɩn sɛlsra ẽ. Tɩ a yetɩ:.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: «Mam de la Zifu, ba dɔgɛ mam na Tarsɩ n boe Silisi so'olʋm pʋam na, uge mam Zerizalɛm kalam. Tɩ Agamalɩyɛlɩ zãsʋm mam tɩ yaabdõma lɔɔ la sõŋa sõŋa. Mam daan wɩsgɛ m mẽŋa mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ, wʋ yãma n wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa se'em zĩna wã.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: La mam daan nãmsɛ sɛba n dolɩ Azezi sore la mɛ zozo'e, yõgɛ bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ botɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deem, dee kʋ basɛba.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la kẽma la wuu n tã wʋn yetɩ, mam n tɔgɛ se'em na de la sɩra. Mam daan to'oge gõnnɔ mɛ ba zẽ'am wɛ'ɛsa tɩ m ka bɔ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Damaasɩ la, dee yõgɛ so-ẽŋa karẽnbiisi la n boe mĩ na, tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm ka nãmsɛ ba.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: La mam daan boe la sore wɛ'ɛsa ka lɛm Damaasɩ, la daan masɛ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa wẽnnɛ zuu-tẽŋasʋka, tɩ peelem daan ze'ele saazuum darɛ wa nẽegɛ zozo'e gilge mam.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Tɩ mam lui tẽŋa, wõm kõa n yele mam yetɩ: ‹Asolɩ, Asolɩ, bẽm tɩ fʋ nãmsra mam?›.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Tɩ mam lerge yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, fõn de la ãne?› Tɩ a lerge mam yetɩ: ‹Mam de la Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la tɩ fʋ nãmsrɩ.›.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ mam na yẽ peelem na, dee ka wõm kõa la n tɔgrɩ la mam na.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Tɩ mam soke yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, fõn yetɩ m ẽŋɛ la wãne?› Tɩ Zuudãana la yele mam yetɩ: ‹Isge sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ, tɩ ba wʋn ka yele fo sɛla wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ.›.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: La peelem na yãlgɛ mam nini mɛ tɩ mam ka yẽta. Tɩ sɛba n daan dolɩ mam na ve'ege mam tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: La bʋraa n daan boe mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ananiyasɩ. A daan sakrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Damaasɩ la wuu tɔgra a yelle sõŋa.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Tɩ a wa'am wa ze'ele lɛm mam zẽ'a yele mam yetɩ: ‹M sɔbia Asolɩ, le yẽta.› Tɩ mam nini le yẽta wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa, tɩ mam yẽ ẽ.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la n looge fo tɩ fʋ bãŋɛ ẽn boorɩ se'em, la fʋ yẽ sɩra dãana la, dee tɩ a tɔgɛ la a mẽŋa nõorɛ bɔ fɔ.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: tɩ fʋ wʋn dɛna a kaset-dãana yele nẽrba wuu, fõn yẽ dee wõm se'em na.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, bẽm tɩ fõn gʋ'ʋna? Isge pʋ'ʋsɛ la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, botɩ ba mise fʋ ko'om pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ.›.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: La bɛla poorʋm mam daan lebe la Zerizalɛm ka bɔna Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam pʋ'ʋsra, yẽ Zuudãana wʋ zãasŋɔ la.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: ‹Ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ yese Zerizalɛm, se'ere n soe la, tẽŋa la nẽrba kãn sakɛ fõn wʋn tɔgɛ mam yelle bɔ ba la.›.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Tɩ mam lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹M Zuudãana, nẽrba wã mi mɛ tɩ mam n daan sẽnnɩ ba wẽndeto pʋam yõgra nẽr-sɛba n bɔ fõn sɩra la, botɩ ba wẽ'era ba, dee kẽ'esra ba yʋ'a deto pʋam.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: La wakat-sɛka tɩ ba daan kʋ A'etɩɛnɩ n tɔgɛ fõn yɛla bɔ nẽrba la, mam daan boe mĩ mɛ sakɛ tɩ a kũm na masɛ mɛ, dee daan gu'ura sɛba n kʋ ẽ na futo.›.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele mam yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ, mam yetɩ m tõm fʋ la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am zãazãarɛ.› ».

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: La nẽrba la daan selsrɩ Apolɩ yetɔga la mɛ, la ẽn wa tɔgɛ bu-zãŋsɩ yelle la, tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Kʋ-ya ẽ, nẽr-ẽna ka narɩ a bɔna dũnia zuo.».

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Tɩ ba kaasra tagursi yɛɛsra ba futo lɔbra, dee vaara tẽntõnnɔ lɔbra saazuum.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba botɩ Apolɩ kẽ bãma yi-kãtɛ la pʋam tɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa, dee soke ẽ bãŋɛ, bẽm n soe tɩ ba tĩi kaasra ẽŋa ĩyã bɛla.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Tɩ ba lʋ ẽ yetɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa, tɩ a soke sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n ze mĩ na yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ ba wẽ Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra dee nãn ka di a sarɩya?».

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: La sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n wõm bɛla la, tɩ a sẽŋɛ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Bʋraa wã de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra.».

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la sẽŋɛ Apolɩ zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Yele ma: Fõn sɩrɩ de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe!».

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la yetɩ: «Mam mẽŋa yɔ la ligri zozo'e dee lebge Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra.» Tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ: «Ba kɔ'ɔn dɔgɛ mam tɩ mam dɛna la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra.».

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Tɩ sɛba n yetɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa la vuta lɛbra poorʋm. Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la, ẽŋa n bãŋɛ tɩ Apolɩ de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra dee tɩ ẽŋa botɩ ba ba ẽ na bãnsɩ la ĩyã.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: La sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la daan boorɩ tɩ a bãŋɛ la sɩra sɩra yel-sɛka n botɩ Zifdõma la dõrɛ ẽ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a ba'agɛ Apolɩ basɛ. Dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma n po dita sarɩya la wuu lagsɛ. Tɩ ba lagsɛ. Tɩ a tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka ze'ele ba nẽŋam.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ bɩsra sarɩyaditba la zĩi, dee yele yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam n tõm m tõoma la wuu Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam wa paam zĩna, mam bãŋɛ m sũure pʋam mɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa.».

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Ananiyasɩ bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n ze lɛm Apolɩ la wẽ a nõorɛ.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wẽ fɔ. Fʋ ãn wʋ dagoone tɩ ba saasɛ, tɩ a zuum mã'a ãna sõŋa la, fõn zĩ tɩ fʋ di mam sarɩya wʋ lɔɔ n yele se'em na, la fõn sã'am lɔɔ la mɛ, fõn bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba wẽ mam na.».

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Tɩ sɛba n ze lɛm Apolɩ la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn tʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana?».

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam ka mi tɩ a de la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Da tɔgɛ fʋ buuri nẽŋadãana be'em.› ».

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ bãŋɛ tɩ sarɩyaditba la basɛba de la Saduse dõma, dee tɩ basɛba mẽ dɛna Farisi dõma, tɩ a tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele ba wuu yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam de la Farisi nẽra, mam sɔ de la Farisi mẽŋa mẽŋa. Mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ sɛba n kiiri la wʋn le vo'oge mɛ na, bɛla ĩyã tɩ ba sãm mam.».

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Ẽn yele bɛla la, tɩ Farisi dõma la Saduse dõma wẽ'era nõkɛ'ɛma la taaba, tɩ nẽrba la welse.

eng: This is because Sadducees say that theres no resurrection, angel, or spirit, but Pharisees affirm them all.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Saduse dõma la ka sakɛ tɩ kũm wʋn vo'oge, la ba ka sakɛ tɩ malɛkadõma la sɩɩsɩ bɔna, la Farisi dõma sakɛ bɛla wuu mɛ.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba n de Farisi dõma la isge yãbra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Tõma ka yẽ bʋra-ẽna wã tõon-be'ego. Tã tɩ la dɛna la sɩa, bɩɩ malɛka n tɔgɛ bɔ ẽ.».

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Tɩ nõke'ene na wẽ'a daan zo'oge gãŋɛ. Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la zɔta dabeem yetɩ, ba na'an wʋn ãasʋm Apolɩ. Bɛla tɩ a bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka to'oge ẽ nẽrba la zẽ'am, tarɩ ẽ wa'am wa yʋ sʋdaasɩ la yire.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Tɩ daar-ẽŋa yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi wa'am Apolɩ zẽ'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Keŋe fʋ sũure, tɩ fõn tɔgɛ mam yɛla Zerizalɛm kalam se'em na, dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ ken tɔgɛ mam yɛla Rom tẽŋam bɛla.».

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Zifdõma basɛba lagsɛ taaba ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla pɔɩ yetɩ, bãma kãn di dia bɩɩ yũ ko'om, sãn dagna bãma kʋ la Apolɩ.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Sɛba n daan lagsɛ ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla bɛla la, daan gãnnɩ nẽrba pinaasɩ mɛ.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: La ba daan sẽŋɛ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma la zẽ'am ka yetɩ: «Tõma lagsɛ mɛ pɔɩ yetɩ, tõma kãn di sɛla, sãn dagna tɩ tõma kʋ la Apolɩ.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, yãma sarɩyaditba la wuu, tõm-ya tɩ ba ka yele sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la tɩ a tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am ya zẽ'am, dee ẽŋɛ-ya wʋ ya boorɩ tɩ ya soke bãŋɛ la a yelle la pa'asɛ, tɩ tõma mãasʋm mɛ tɩ kʋ ẽ tɩ a nãn ka paam.».

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ tã dayʋa bãŋɛ ban bãm yele se'em na, sẽŋɛ sʋdaasɩ la yire ka yele Apolɩ.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ wi sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma la ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tarɩ budib-ẽna wã sẽŋɛ ya kɛ'ɛma la zẽ'am, tɩ a tarɩ la sɛla tɩ a yele ẽ.».

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ba kɛ'ɛma la zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Apolɩ tɩ ba yʋ la n wi mam yetɩ, m tarɩ budib-ẽna wã wa'am fʋ zẽ'am, tɩ a tarɩ la sɛla tɩ a yele fo.».

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la gʋrɛ a nu'ugo tarɩ ẽ yese kẽnkɛrŋa ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn tarɩ la bẽm yelle tɩ fʋ yele mam?».

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zifdõma n bãm yetɩ, bãma wʋn sose fõn beesã tɩ fʋ tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am, wʋ bãma boorɩ tɩ ba bɩsɛ la a yelle la pa'asɛ.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: La, da sakɛ ban wʋn yele se'em na, tɩ ba nẽrba n gãŋɛ pinaasɩ n sʋgɛ gu'ura Apolɩ. Ba wuu pɔɩ mɛ yetɩ, bãma kãn di dia bɩɩ yũ ko'om, sãn dagna tɩ bãma kʋ la Apolɩ. Ba mãasʋm gu'ura la fõn wʋn lerge se'em.».

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la yele budibla la yetɩ: «Da yele nẽra tɩ fõn yele mam sɛla.» Dee botɩ a lebe.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la wi a sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma bayi yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɔbsyi, la wi-bã'arba pisyopɔɩ, la kãn-lɔbrɩba kɔbsyi mãasʋm. Tɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ẽna wã karf-awɛɩ ya sẽŋɛ Sezaare.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Mãasʋm-ya wiiri bɔ Apolɩ tɩ a bã tɩ ya tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ gɔmna Afeligsi zẽ'am la ĩmã'asʋm.».

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Dee gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ yetɩ:.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: «Mam Akloorɩ-lisiasɩ n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra fõn gɔmna sõŋɔ Afeligsi. Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Zifdõma n daan yõkɛ bʋra-ẽna wã bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ. Tɩ mam wõm tɩ a de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra, tɩ mam sẽŋɛ la sʋdaasɩ ka fãagɛ ẽ.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: dee bɔɔra tɩ m bãŋɛ bõn-sɛka n soe tɩ ba dõrɛ ẽ tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: La mam daan yẽ tɩ la de la Zifdõma lɔɔ yɛla ĩyã, dee a ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n narɩ ba kʋ ẽ, bɩɩ ba yʋ ẽ yʋ'a deem.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Tɩ ba yele mam yetɩ, Zifdõma bãm mɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ tõm tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am fõn zẽ'am, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n sãm ẽ na wa'am fõn zẽ'am wa tɔgɛ ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em.».

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la ẽŋɛ ẽn yele ba se'em na, tarɩ Apolɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ẽŋa sẽŋɛ Antipatrisi.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ sʋda-sɛba n sẽnnɩ nãma la lebe ba yire la, dee tɩ sɛba n bã wiiri la tarɩ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: La ban paagɛ Sezaare la, ba dɩkɛ gõŋɔ la mɛ bɔ gɔmna la dee dɩkɛ Apolɩ ẽŋɛ a nu'usum.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Tɩ a karẽŋɛ gõŋɔ la, dee soke yetɩ, ẽŋa yese la so'olʋm kãna? Tɩ Apolɩ pa'alɛ ẽ tɩ ẽŋa yese la Silisi so'olʋm.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: Tɩ gɔmna la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛba n tarɩ fʋ wa'am sãm na sãn paam, mam wʋn soke bãŋɛ lan ẽŋɛ se'em.» A yele bɛla dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba kẽ'esɛ ẽ naba A'erɔdɩ yire tɩ ba gu'ura ẽ.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: La dabsa anuu daarɛ, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Ananiyasɩ doose la kẽma basɛba, la yetɔg-mita yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Atɛrtuli wa'am Sezaare. Tɩ ba dɩkɛ Apolɩ yelle la tɩ ba tɔgɛ bɔ gɔmna la.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Tɩ ba wi Apolɩ tɩ a wa'am. Tɩ Atɛrtuli yãŋa tɔgra dõta Apolɩ yetɩ: «Gɔmna sõŋɔ Afeligsi. La de la fõn bɩsrɩ tõma sõŋa la ĩyã tɩ tẽŋa la tara laafɛ, tɩ yɛla zozo'e teege tɩ tẽŋa la malgɛ.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Fõn ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu zẽ'esɩ wuu bɔ tõma la, tõma to'oge mɛ pʋ'ʋsra fʋ zozo'e.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: La mam ka boorɩ tɩ m gʋ'ʋm fɔ, bɛla mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ fɔ ẽŋɛ sugri dee selse m yetɔga wã fẽfẽe.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Tõma yẽ nẽr-ẽna tɩ a de la tẽn-wã'ara, ẽŋa n sa'asrɩ Zifdõma dũnia wuu zuo tɩ ba gita tẽnsɩ. A de la Nazarɛtɩ dõma põmpɔrŋɔ sore la nẽŋadãana.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: A zɩgrɩ tɩ a sã'am la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la sisgo, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ. La tõma daan boorɩ tɩ tɩ di a sarɩya la tõma mẽŋa lɔɔ.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n sãm ẽ na wa'am fõn zẽ'am. Fʋ sãn soke ẽ fʋ mẽŋa, fʋ wʋn wõm bõn-sɛba wuu n botɩ tõma sãm ẽ.».

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Atɛrtuli n tɔgɛ ba'asɛ la, tɩ Zifdõma la lagɩm sakɛ yetɩ, la de la bɛla.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Zifdõma la n tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ gɔmna la bɔ Apolɩ nõorɛ tɩ a tɔgɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ: «Mam mi tɩ fõn de la sarɩyadita so'olʋm ẽna wã pʋam halɩ yʋʋma n zo'oge. Bɛla mam sũure ãn yẽlʋm tɩ m tɔgɛ to'oge m mẽŋa.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Fõn tã wʋn bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la ka gãŋɛ dabsa pia la ayi tɩ mam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm tɩ m ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: La Zifdõma la ka yẽ mam tɩ mam wẽ'erɩ nõke'ene la nẽra Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, la ba ken ka yẽ tɩ mam sa'asrɩ nẽrba ba wẽndeto la pʋam bɩɩ tẽŋa la zẽ'e-sɛka.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: La ba kãn yãŋɛ yẽ sɛla pa'alɛ tɩ bãma n yetɩ mam ẽŋɛ se'em na de sɩra.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: La mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ mam sɩrɩ dolɩ la so-sɛka tɩ ba yetɩ põmpɔrŋɔ sore la. La mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la, dee sakra sɛla wuu n gʋlsɛ bɔna Amoyisi lɔɔ pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la Nawẽnnɛ wʋ bãma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ se'em na, tɩ nẽrba wuu, nẽr-sõma la nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ wʋn vo'oge mɛ.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam mõrgra daarɛ woo tɩ m bãŋɛ m sũure pʋam tɩ mam ka ẽŋɛ be'em Nawẽnnɛ la nẽrba nifum.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: La de la yʋʋma zozo'e tɩ mam daan ka boe Zerizalɛm, dee yãŋa tarɩ ligri leme tɩ m sõŋɛ m tẽŋa nẽrba, dee bɔ bɔ'a tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: Mam daan boe mĩ ɩta la bɛla, tɩ ba yẽ mam Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ mam daan põn malgɛ m mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am kɩ'ɩlɩm mɛ, la nẽrba daan ka boe la mam mĩ zo'oge, la ba mẽ daan ka gire mĩ.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: La Zifdõma basɛba n ze'ele Azi so'olʋm n daan boe mĩ, ba sãn tara nɩ na yelle la mam, ba mẽŋa n wʋn wa'am nɩ wa yele fʋ mam n ẽŋɛ se'em.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: La nẽr-bãna wã sãn yẽ mam tõon-be'ego, wakat-sɛka tɩ mam daan boe ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am na, ba yele.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: La ba kãn yẽ sɛla, sãn dagna mam daan ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka tɔgɛ ba ke'enke'em yetɩ: ‹La de la mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge mɛ na ĩyã, tɩ mam zɛ ya nẽŋam tɩ ya dita mam sarɩya zĩna wã.› ».

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: La Afeligsi n daan põn mina kõ-yẽlga la sore yelle la, tɩ a viige yetɔga la, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Sʋda-kɛ'ɛma Alisiasɩ sãn paam, mam wʋn kʋ'ʋgɛ yetɔga la bɔ ya.».

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Dee bɔ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n gu'uri Apolɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, a bɩsra ẽ, la a bɔ ẽ yolsgo, la a sɩrdõma sãn tara sɛla bɔɔra tɩ ba bɔ ẽ, a basɛ tɩ ba bɔ ẽ.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: La dabsa fẽe n tole, tɩ Afeligsi lagɩm na a pɔga Adrisili n de Zifu botɩ ba wi Apolɩ, tɩ a wa'am wa tɔgra nẽra n wʋn bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra se'em yelle, tɩ ba sɛlsra.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: La Apolɩ n yãŋa wa tɔgra ɩrgɔ n masɛ, la yõk-m-mẽŋa, la sarɩyadia n sẽm yelle, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ Afeligsi, tɩ a yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Basɛ bɛla, mam sãn wa yẽ nẽŋa, mam wʋn le wi fo.».

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: A daan tẽ'esrɩ tɩ Apolɩ na'an wʋn bɔ ẽ na ligri, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a ẽn wi'ira ẽ sõsra la ẽ lɛ.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Tɩ yʋʋma ayi tole, tɩ Apɔrsusi-fɛstusi yãŋa sɔɔgɛ Afeligsi zẽ'am. La Afeligsi daan boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ãn Zifdõma sũure yẽlʋm, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a basɛ tɩ Apolɩ ken bɔna yʋ'a deem.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Afɛstusi n paagɛ to'oge na'am a so'olʋm na pʋam na, a ẽŋɛ la dabsa atã Sezaare, dee ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Zifdõma nẽŋadõma wa'am a zẽ'am wa dõrɛ Apolɩ. Dee sose ẽ mõrgra.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: yetɩ, a sõŋɛ ba botɩ ba tarɩ Apolɩ leme Zerizalɛm, se'ere n soe la, ba daan bãm bɔɔra tɩ ba sʋgɛ kʋ ẽ na sore.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Tɩ Afɛstusi lerge yetɩ: «Apolɩ boe la yʋ'a deem Sezaare, la mam mẽŋa wʋn lebe la bilam lɛɛlɛ wã.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: La basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽŋadõma doose mam sẽŋɛ Sezaare, tɩ a sãn tõm na tõon-be'ego, ya tɔgɛ bilam.».

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Afɛstusi daan ka ẽŋɛ gãŋɛ dabsa anii bɩɩ dabsa pia ba zẽ'am dee lebe Sezaare. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a kẽ zĩ'ire sarɩyadia zẽ'am, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ kẽ, tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n ze'ele Zerizalɛm wa'am na isge gilge ẽ, dõta ẽ yetɩ, a tõm na tõon-be'ero zozo'e, la ba ka tã'agɛ pa'alɛ tɩ bãma n yele se'em na de la sɩra.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ tɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa yetɩ: «Mam ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Zifdõma lɔɔ, bɩɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, la mam mẽ ka ẽŋɛ Rom na-keko la be'em.».

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Tɩ Afɛstusi bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn Zifdõma sũure yẽlʋm, soke Apolɩ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ fʋ sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm tɩ m ka di fʋ yelle wã sarɩya bilam mɛ bɩɩ?».

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ lerge yetɩ: «Mam põn bɔna la nẽr-sɛka tɩ Rom na-keko bɔ ẽ nõorɛ la sarɩyadia zẽ'am, bɛla la masɛ tɩ ba di mam sarɩya la Rom. Fõn mẽŋa mi tɩ mam ka ẽŋɛ Zifdõma be'em.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Mam sãn ẽŋɛ nɩ na be'em bɩɩ sɛla n masɛ tɩ ba kʋ mam, mam kãn zagsɛ. La ban dõrɛ mam se'em na dagɩ sɩra, nẽra ka tã wʋn dɩkɛ mam ẽŋɛ ba nu'usum. Mam boorɩ tɩ Rom na-keko la n di m sarɩya.».

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Tɩ Afɛstusi la a poorʋm dõma yẽ taaba, dee tɩ a lerge Apolɩ yetɩ: «Fõn yetɩ, fõn boorɩ tɩ Rom na-keko la n di fʋ sarɩya. Bɛla, fʋ wʋn sẽŋɛ la na-keko la zẽ'am.».

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: La dabsa fẽe n tole, tɩ naba Agripa la a tã Abɛrnisi sẽŋɛ Sezaare tɩ ba ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Afɛstusi.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: La ba yuuge mĩ na fẽe, tɩ Afɛstusi tɔgɛ Apolɩ yelle la bɔ naba la yetɩ: «Bʋraa n boe kalam tɩ Afeligsi basɛ tɩ a bɔna yʋ'a deem.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: La mam daan sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm, tɩ Zifdõma kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma tarɩ a yelle wa'am wa tɔgɛ bɔ mam, dee sose mam yetɩ, m di a sarɩya botɩ ba sɩbgɛ ẽ.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Tɩ mam lerge ba yetɩ, tõma Rom dõma ka dɩkrɩ nẽra ɩta nẽrba nu'usum tɩ ba sɩbgɛ ẽ, dee tɩ a nãn ka ze'ele sɛba n sãm ẽ na nẽŋam tɔgɛ a nõorɛ.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: La ba doose mam mɛ wa'am kalam, la mam ka gʋ'ʋm, beere n wiige, tɩ mam kẽ zĩ'ire sarɩyadia zẽ'am, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ bʋraa la wa'am.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: La sɛba n sãm ẽ na ka pa'alɛ tõon-be'ero sɛba tɩ a ẽŋɛ, wʋ mam n tẽ'esɛ se'em na.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: La de la nõke'ene tɩ ba wẽ'erɩ la ẽ, ba pʋ'ʋsgɔ sore yɛla ĩyã, la bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azezi n ki, tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ a vo'oge mɛ ĩyã.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Tɩ mam ka tã'agɛ bãŋɛ mam wʋn ẽŋɛ yel-ẽna wã se'em, tɩ mam soke Apolɩ yetɩ, ẽŋa boorɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka di a sarɩya la bilam?.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ zagsɛ dee yetɩ, ba basɛ tɩ Rom na-keko la tɔgɛ a yetɔga la. Tɩ mam bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba gu'ura ẽ tɩ m wa botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ na-keko la zẽ'am.».

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Tɩ naba Agripa yele Afɛstusi yetɩ: «Mam mẽ boorɩ tɩ m wõm bʋra-ẽna wã yetɔga.» Tɩ Afɛstusi lerge yetɩ: «Beere sãn wiige, fʋ wʋn wõm a yetɔga.».

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Agripa la Abɛrnisi yɛ fõŋɔ lɔgrɔ wa'am wa kẽ sarɩyadia deem na, la sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma, la tẽŋa la nẽŋadõma. Tɩ Afɛstusi bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Dee yetɩ: «Naba Agripa, la sɛba wuu n lagsɛ wã, bɩsɛ-ya bʋra-ẽna wã tɩ Zifdõma wuu wa'am wa tɔgɛ a yelle la mam, Zerizalɛm la kalam, tĩi tɔgra yetɩ, la ka masɛ tɩ a ken vɔa.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: La mam yẽ tɩ a ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ a ki. La a mẽŋa yetɩ, ẽŋa boorɩ tɩ ba tarɩ a yetɔga la sẽŋɛ la na-keko zẽ'am. Tɩ mam tɔgɛ kʋ yetɩ, mam wʋn botɩ a sẽŋɛ.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: La mam ka tarɩ a yelle la zuo wʋn gʋlsɛ bɔ na-keko la. Bɛla tɩ mam botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am ya zẽ'am, la fõn naba Agripa mẽŋa nẽŋam, tɩ ya sãn sogse ẽ kɩ'ɩlɩm, mam wʋn yẽ yelle gʋlsɛ.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ka ãn sõŋa tɩ mam tarɩ nẽra ka bɔ na-keko la tɩ a di a sarɩya, dee ka pa'alɛ yel-sɛka ĩyã tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.».

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Tɩ Agripa yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ fʋ tɔgɛ.» Tɩ Apolɩ zẽkɛ a nu'ugo tɩ a tɔgɛ to'oge a mẽŋa, yetɩ:.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: «Naba Agripa, mam sũure ãn yẽlʋm, mam n boe fʋ nẽŋam zĩna wã tɩ m tɔgɛ to'oge m mẽŋa la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ Zifdõma dõrɛ mam na.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Lan gãnnɩ wuu, mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga, fõn mi Zifdõma dɔgmike, la ba nõkɛ'ɛma la yelle sõŋa sõŋa la ĩyã. Bɛla mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ fʋ mã'agɛ fʋ mẽŋa selse m yetɔga wã.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Zifdõma wuu mi mam vɔm n ãn se'em mɛ halɩ lan pɔsɛ mam bõnbɩ'ɩlʋm, la ba mi mam n vɔa se'em mam mẽŋa tẽŋam, la Zerizalɛm mɛ.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Ban mi mam la yuuge mɛ, ba sãn bɔɔra, ba tã wʋn ze'ele bɔ mam kaseto mɛ yetɩ, mam de la Farisi nẽra, bãma n sakrɩ tɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ yɛla sõŋa gãnna.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ mam ze sarɩyadia zẽ'am kalam wã, la de la mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma la ĩyã.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Tõma buuri pia la ayi la tõnnɩ mɛ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ, dee tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ ẽn bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na wʋn ẽŋɛ mɛ. Naba Agripa, la de la pʋtẽ'e-ẽna wã ĩyã tɩ Zifdõma dõrɛ mam.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Bẽm tɩ la dɛna toogo bɔ ya, tɩ ya sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'orɩ kũm?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Mam mẽŋa daan tẽ'esɛ mɛ, tɩ dẽnɩ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em wuu sã'am Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Mam tõm na bɛla Zerizalɛm, la mam daan to'oge nõorɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am mɛ, yõgɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zozo'e botɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deem, di ba sarɩya yetɩ ba kʋ ba, tɩ mam daan sakɛ.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: La wakatɛ zozo'e tɩ mam daan nãmsɛ ba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, mõrgra tɩ ba basɛ ba sore la. Tɩ mam sũure daan yĩige la ba halɩ tɩ mam doose tẽnsɩ basɛba nãmsɛ ba.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam daan to'oge nõorɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am tɩ m sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Naa, mam daan boe la sore wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka wɛ'ɛsa, yẽ peelem n ze'ele saazuum yẽkɛ nẽegɛ gãnna wʋntɛɛŋa gilge mam, la sɛba n dolɩ mam na.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Tɩ tõma wuu lui tẽŋa, tɩ mam wõm kõa n yele mam la Ebre yetɔga yetɩ: ‹Asolɩ, Asolɩ, bẽm tɩ fʋ nãmsra mam? Fʋ sãn ka sakɛ mam, la wʋn ãna toogo bɔ fʋ, wʋ dũŋa n ka nannɩ a dãana ka'asrɛ.›.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Tɩ mam soke yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, fõn de la ãne?› Tɩ Zuudãana la yele mam yetɩ: ‹Mam de la Azezi, sɛka tɩ fʋ nãmsrɩ la.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: La isge ze'ele, mam dɩkɛ m mẽŋa pa'alɛ fɔ tɩ m botɩ fʋ lebge la mam tõntõnna. Fʋ wʋn dɛna la mam kaset-dãana yele nẽrba fõn yẽ mam se'em, dee ken pa'alɛ ba bõn-sɛba tɩ mam wʋn botɩ fʋ yẽ la.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: La mam wʋn ken tõm fʋ la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am, tɩ fʋ ka pike ba nini yese ba lika pʋam tɩ ba vɔa peelem pʋam, dee yese ba Asũtãana nu'usum lebse ba Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum, tɩ ba bɔ mam sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ, tɩ ba lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sõma yẽ pʋrɛ. La mam wʋn fãagɛ fʋ Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum basɛ.› ».

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Naba Agripa, mam n daan yẽ Azezi wʋ zãasŋɔ, tɩ a ze'ele saazuum tɔgɛ bɔ mam na, mam ka zagsɛ.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam pɔsɛ Damaasɩ, la Zerizalɛm, la Zude so'olʋm wuu, la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am mõolɛ yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee lebe sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee ẽŋɛ sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ ba teege yɛm.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Bɛla tɩ Zifdõma daan yõkɛ mam Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yetɩ ba kʋ mam.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ sõŋɛ mam mɛ wa paam zĩna, bɛla tɩ mam zɛ kalam tɔgra mam n yẽ sɛla bɔ'ɔra yãma, kɛ'ɛndõma la tarɩmdõma wuu. Mam n yele sɛla wuu la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la Amoyisi n yʋʋn yetɩ sɛla wʋn ẽŋɛ la.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: Ba yʋʋn yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Krisi nãmsɛ ki, la ẽŋa n wʋn dɛna pɔspɔsɩ nẽra n wʋn vo'oge, dee wʋn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga n de peelem bɔ Zifdõma la bu-zãŋsɩ.».

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: La Apolɩ n tɔgrɩ bɛla tɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa la, tɩ Afɛstusi tãsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Apolɩ, fõn zallɩ mɛ, fõn zãsʋm mɛ zo'oge tɩ la botɩ fʋ yirsra.».

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Afɛstusi sõŋɔ, mam ka zallɩ. Mam tɔgrɩ la yel-sɩra, la yɛm yetɔga.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Naba Agripa mi yel-bãna mɛ, mam tã wʋn tɔgɛ mɛ la bʋraanɛ a nẽŋam. Mam mi tɩ a mi bõn-bãna wuu mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba ka ẽŋɛ lika pʋam.».

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: A yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Naba Agripa, fõn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yele se'em na mɛ? Mam mi tɩ fõn sakɛ mɛ.».

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Tɩ Agripa yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fʋ botɩ mam lebge la Krisi nẽra wakatɛ fẽe wãna pʋam?».

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ: «La sãn yuuge, bɩɩ la sãn ka yuuge, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ tɩ fõn la sɛba wuu n selsrɩ mam zĩna wã wʋn lebge ãna wʋ mam ãn se'em na, sãn dagna bãnsɩ la mã'a tɩ mam ka boorɩ tɩ ba ba ya.».

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Tɩ naba la, la gɔmna Afɛstusi, la Abɛrnisi, la nẽrba la wuu isge yese.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: ka yele taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ a ki, bɩɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ẽ yʋ'a deem.».

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Tɩ Agripa yele Afɛstusi yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã sãn ka yele nɩ tɩ ẽŋa boorɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ la na-keko la zẽ'am, tɩ tã wʋn basɛ nɩ ẽ.».

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: La tõma n daan põsɛ paagɛ doone na, tɩ tõma bãŋɛ tɩ tẽŋa la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Maltɩ.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba to'oge tõma sõŋa sõŋa. Tɩ saaga daan niira tɩ ɔɔrɔ bɔna, tɩ ba tʋʋrɛ bugum bɔ tõma, tɩ tõma ɔɔsra.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ vaagɛ dɔɔrɔ wa'am wa ẽŋɛ bugum pʋam, tɩ tʋʋlgɔ wõŋɛ bõnsɛla, tɩ a yese vile Apolɩ nu'ugo.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba yẽ bõnsɛla n vile a nu'ugo la, tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã sɩrɩ de la nẽr-kʋʋra. Baa la ẽn yãŋɛ põsɛ ko'om pʋam na, wẽnnɛ ka sakɛ tɩ a vɔa.».

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ mẽsɛ a nu'ugo, tɩ bõnsɛla la lui bugum pʋam, dee tɩ sɛla ka ẽŋɛ ẽ.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la gu'ura tɩ ba wʋn yẽ tɩ a ĩyã fʋʋsʋm ya, bɩɩ a wʋn kɔ'ɔn lui tẽŋa ki. Tɩ la yuuge, tɩ ba ka yẽ tɩ sɛla ẽŋɛ ẽ, tɩ ba teege pʋtẽ'era yetɩ, a de la wẽnnɛ.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ tõma daan boe mĩ na lɛm na tẽŋa la nẽŋadãana va'am. A yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Apubulsi, a daan to'oge tõma mɛ la sũ-yẽlga. Tɩ tõma bɔna a yire dabsa atã.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Tɩ a sɔ bẽ'era gã yẽ'era-waafɔ, dee tɩ ɔɔrɔ tara ẽ. Tɩ Apolɩ kẽ a deem dɩkɛ nu'usi paglɛ a zuo, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ, tɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Lan ẽŋɛ bɛla la, tɩ tẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n de bã'adõma wuu sẽnna, tɩ Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba, tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Tɩ ba daan gõŋɛ tõma sõŋa sõŋa, la tõma n daan fõrgrɩ la, ba bɔ tõma la bɔ'a n seke tõma sore la sẽnnɛ.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: La wõr-sɩtã n tole, tɩ tõma yẽ õorŋɔ n ze'ele Alɛgsãndri wa'am bɔna Maltɩ tẽŋam, tɩ ɔɔrɔ la kʋseb-kãtɛ la wakatɛ tole. Õorŋɔ la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la «Diyɔskuri». Tɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ la wɛ'ɛsa.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: La tõma sẽŋɛ paagɛ la Sɩrakusi zĩ'ire bilam dabsa atã.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: le ze'ele bilam kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ paagɛ Regio. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ kʋsebgo ze'ele tõma poorʋm wa vugra zã'ara tõma. Tɩ tõma paagɛ Puzɔllɩ dabsa ayi daarɛ sige.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Bilam tɩ tõma yẽ Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba. Tɩ ba sose tõma, tɩ tõma zĩ'ire ba zẽ'am dabsa ayopɔɩ, dee yãŋa wɛ'ɛsa Rom.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Tɩ karẽnbi-sɛba n boe Rom na wõm tõma yelle. Tɩ basɛba tʋ'ʋsɛ tõma halɩ «Apiyusi da'am», dee tɩ basɛba mẽ tʋ'ʋsɛ tõma zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de «sãama deto batã» la. La Apolɩ n yẽ ba la, tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, le yẽ bʋraanɛ.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Tɩ tõma paagɛ Rom, tɩ ba bɔ Apolɩ nõorɛ tɩ a kẽ'era ẽn boorɩ zẽ'e-sɛka, dee botɩ sʋdaaga gu'ura ẽ.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Tɩ dabsa atã poorʋm, tɩ Apolɩ wi Zifdõma nẽŋadõma sɛba n boe tẽŋa la pʋam lagsɛ, yele ba yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam daan ka ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrba la be'em, la mam ka sã'am lɔɔ sɛba tɩ tɩ to'oge tɩ yaabdõma zẽ'am na, la baa bɛla ba daan yõkɛ mam mɛ Zerizalɛm, tarɩ mam ka bɔ Rom sʋdaasɩ tɩ ba yʋ mam.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Tɩ ba sogse mam, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba basɛ mam, ban daan ka yẽ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ kũm na ĩyã.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Tɩ Zifdõma zagsɛ tɩ ba da basɛ mam, bɛla n pẽrgɛ mam tɩ mam sose nõorɛ yetɩ, mam boorɩ tɩ m sẽŋɛ la Rom na-keko la zẽ'am tɩ a di m sarɩya, la dagɩ mam n tarɩ yelle la m nẽrba la.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam wi ya tɩ ya wa'am tɩ sõsɛ la taaba. Ban lʋ mam na bãnsɩ wã, la de la sɛka tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma tarɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura ẽ na ĩyã.».

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka to'oge gõnnɔ Zude tẽŋam n gʋlsɛ fʋ yelle. Zifdõma sɛba n sẽnnɩ kalam na, ba nẽra nẽra mẽ ka tɔgɛ fʋ tõon-be'ero yelle.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: La tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ wõm na fõn tẽ'esrɩ se'em, la sãn dɛna sore la tɩ fʋ dolɩ la, tõma mi tɩ zẽ'a woo ba sɩ'ɩsrɩ ẽ mɛ.».

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ Apolɩ daarɛ. Tɩ daarɛ la paagɛ, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e lagsɛ Apolɩ zẽ'am. Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle, la Azezi yelle n gʋlsɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ gõnnɔ pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na, pa'alɛ ba bulika halɩ ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ, mõrgra tɩ a botɩ ba sakɛ.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Tɩ basɛba sakɛ, dee tɩ basɛba ka sakɛ.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Tɩ nẽrba la ka wõm taaba bɔɔra tɩ ba fõrgɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ ba yetɔga ayɩla yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi tɔgɛ la ya yaabdõma la de la sɩra.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: A yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ bu-bãna wã zẽ'am ka yele ba yetɩ: Ya wʋn selse, la ya kãn wõm a võorɛ. Ya wʋn bɩsɛ, la ya kãn yẽ sɛla sɛla.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, bu-bãna wã pʋtẽ'era ka like, la ba tʋba lɩ mɛ, dee tɩ ba mũm ba nini. Tɩ ba nini da yẽ, tɩ ba tʋba da wõm, tɩ ba pʋtẽ'era da like. Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm, tɩ mam bɔ ba laafɛ.› ».

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Apolɩ daan tɔgɛ bɛla dee yetɩ: «Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm fãarɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ la bu-zãŋsɩ. Bãma wʋn selse.» .

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Apolɩ daan kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a boe mĩ na yʋʋma ayi, daan yɔɔra deego la ligri, dee tɔ'ɔsra sɛba wuu n sẽnnɩ a zẽ'am na.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle, dee zãsna ba Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yɛla. A daan tɔgrɩ la bʋraanɛ, tɩ nẽra ka gu'ura ẽ tɩ a da tɔgɛ.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Mam Apolɩ n de Azezi-krisi yamŋa n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã. Nawẽnnɛ n wi mam tɩ m dɛna Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna, welge mam to'ore tɩ m mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Nawẽnnɛ daan bĩŋe la nõorɛ halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ a bɔ tɔ a kõ-yẽlga la, botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gʋlsɛ a gõŋɔ pʋam.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi la yelle. Ẽŋa n lebge nẽrsaala la, a de la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la boe a zẽ'am mɛ la pãŋa, pa'alɛ tɩ a de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, ẽŋa n ki dee vo'oge la.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ, botɩ tõma dɛna a Tõntõnɩba tɩ tɩ botɩ so'olʋm wuu nẽrba bɔ ẽ sɩra, sakɛ a nõorɛ a yʋ'ʋrɛ nã'asgɔ ĩyã.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Yãma mẽ po bɔna la nẽr-bãma pʋam, yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi tɩ ya dɛna Azezi-krisi nẽrba la.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mam gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra la yãma sɛba wuu n boe Rom, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ ya, wi ya tɩ ya welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba la. Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Tɩ ba daan looge yɛm tɩ ba botɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Itali, tɩ ba dɩkɛ Apolɩ la nẽrba basɛba tɩ ba yʋ la, ẽŋɛ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azuliyusi nu'usum, a daan de la sʋda-sɛba tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ na-keko sʋdaasɩ la kɛ'ɛma ayɩla.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Pɔspɔsɩ, Azezi-krisi ĩyã mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ mam Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ yãma wuu ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, ba tɔgrɩ yãma n bɔ Azezi sɩra se'em mɛ dũnia wã wuu.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: La õorŋɔ n ze'ele Adramiti tẽŋam daan bɔna bilam yetɩ a sẽŋɛ tẽnsɩ basɛba n boe Azi ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm. Tɩ tõma kẽ wɛ'ɛsa, tɩ Aristarkɩ n ze'ele Tesaloniki n boe Masedʋanɩ so'olʋm, wa'am wa doose tõma.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Mam tẽrɩ ya yelle m pʋ'ʋsgɔ pʋam mɛ wakatɛ wuu. Nawẽnnɛ tɩ mam tõnnɩ la m sũure wuu bɔ'ɔra ẽ, mõola a Dayʋa kõ-yẽlga la, mi tɩ sɛla tɩ mam yele la de la sɩra.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Sidõ tẽŋam, tɩ Azuliyusi ẽŋɛ Apolɩ sõŋa bɔ ẽ nõorɛ tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ a sɩrdõma, dee tɩ ba sõŋɛ ẽ.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: Wakat-sɛka woo tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ, mam sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ tɩ la sãn dɛna ẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em, a botɩ m yãŋɛ wa'am ya zẽ'am wa kaagɛ ya yãŋa.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Tɩ tõma le kẽ õorŋɔ bilam wɛ'ɛsa. Tɩ kʋsebgo vugra tʋ'ʋsra tõma, tɩ tõma wẽrgɛ bẽglɛ Sipri sẽŋɛ.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam boorɩ la m sũure wuu tɩ m yẽ ya, tɩ m wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a bɔ ya tɩ ya yẽ pãŋa.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: La tõma doose la ko-kãtɛ la n lɛm Silisi la Pãnfili la, sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Miira n boe Lisi so'olʋm sige.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: La de la mam na yãma wʋn lagɩm yẽ bʋraanɛ pa'asɛ, tɩ yãma n bɔ Azezi sɩra se'em na wʋn bɔ mam bʋraanɛ, tɩ mam mẽ n bɔ Azezi sɩra se'em na wʋn bɔ yãma bʋraanɛ.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Bilam tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la yẽ õorŋɔ n ze'ele Alɛgsãndri tɩ a sẽŋɛ Itali. Tɩ a botɩ tõma kẽ.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ nõorɛ faa tɩ mam makɛ tɩ m wa'am ya zẽ'am. La gu'a n gu mam halɩ wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã. Mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ m wa'am tɩ m tõonɛ na tara yõorɔ yãma zẽ'am, wʋ ẽn tarɩ yõorɔ so'olʋm basɛba pʋam se'em na.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: La dabsa faa tɩ tõma nãmsɛ la õorŋɔ la sẽnna fẽfẽ halɩ ka lɛm Kinidi tẽŋam. La kʋsebgo la ka botɩ tõma yãŋɛ sẽŋɛ bilam bɔba, tɩ tõma lɔkɛ sẽŋɛ lɛm Salmõnɩ bẽglɛ Krɛtɩ sẽŋɛ.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Mam tõonɛ de la m sẽŋɛ nẽr-sɛba yɛm n like, la nẽr-sɛba yɛm n ka like, nẽr-sɛba n zãsʋm tara bãŋrɛ, la nẽr-sɛba n ka mi sɛla zẽ'am ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: La tõma daan bẽglɛ la ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm, daan nãmsɛ halɩ, dee yãŋa paagɛ zẽ'a yʋ'ʋrɛ n de õorŋɔ ze'elgo zẽ'e-sõŋɔ n lɛm Laze.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam bɔɔra tɩ m mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la mẽ bɔ yãma sɛba n boe Rom la.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: La dabsa zozo'e daan tole mɛ, tɩ Zifdõma nõorɛ lʋa daarɛ tole. La wakat-ẽŋa la daan de la toogo bɔ nẽrba tɩ ba dɩkɛ õorŋɔ doose ko'om na pʋam. Bɛla tɩ Apolɩ yele ba yetɩ:.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Yãnnɛ ka tarɩ mam la kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ. A de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa n botɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔ ẽ sɩra la yẽta fãarɛ, pɔspɔsɩ de la Zifdõma, pa'asɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: «Bʋraasɩ wã, mam mi tɩ tõma sẽnnɛ wã wʋn wa dɛna la toogo. La dagɩ lɔgrɔ, la õorŋɔ la mã'a n wʋn sã'am, la de la tõma mẽŋa yõorɛ põsga.».

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Kõ-yẽlga la n pukri pa'ala tõma Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am. La de la bɔ sɩra, tarɩ ka ba'asɛ la bɔ sɩra, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Nẽra n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra masɛ a zẽ'am wʋn vɔa, ẽn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.».

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la ka selse Apolɩ n yele se'em na, a selse la sɛka n bɩsrɩ õorŋɔ la sẽnnɛ yelle, la õorŋɔ la dãana yetɔga.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa sũure yĩige mɛ la nẽrsaalba n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, la sɛla wuu tɩ ba ɩtɩ tɩ la ka dɔla ẽŋa sore, tɩ ba tõon-be'ero la gu'ura nẽrba tɩ ba ka mina sɩra la, tɩ ẽŋa wʋn sɩbgɛ ba.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: La õorŋɔ ze'elgo zẽ'a na ka ãn sõŋa tɩ ba wʋn gã'arɛ tɩ kʋsebgo, la ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ la tole. Bɛla tɩ nẽrba la zozo'e yetɩ, tɩ basɛ bilam dee wĩbe sẽŋɛ paagɛ Fenigsi n mẽ boe Krɛtɩ so'olʋm. Zẽ'e-ẽŋa õorŋɔ ze'elgo zẽ'am la tɔglɛ la zanõrɛ gɔbga, la zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo bɔba. Bilam tɩ ba tã wʋn zĩ'ire tɩ kʋsebgo, la ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ la tole.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sore tɩ nẽrsaalba wʋn doose bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yelle de la vẽelga bɔ ba, Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n puke ẽ pa'alɛ ba.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Tɩ kʋsebgo ze'ele gɔbga bɔba wa'am wa vugra fẽfẽ. Tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ban boorɩ se'em, bɛla tɩ ba mãasʋm õorŋɔ la basɛ zẽ'e-ẽŋa, dee bẽgla Krɛtɩ ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm wɛ'ɛsa.
