eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Halɩ lan pɔsɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ, Nawẽnnɛ yɛla tɩ nẽrsaalba ka yẽtɩ n de a ke'eŋo n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ, la ẽn de Nawẽnnɛ na, nẽrba yẽ ba mɛ a tõoma la pʋam. Bɛla tɩ nẽrsaalba ka tãna wʋn yetɩ bãma ka mi Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Tɩ la ka yuuge, tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «Erakilõ» ze'ele Krɛtɩ tẽŋa bɔba, wa'am wa vugra ke'enke'em wẽ'era õorŋɔ la.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Ba mi Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, la ba ka nã'asrɩ ẽ, la ba ka pʋ'ʋsrɩ ẽ a yel-sõnnɛ wʋ lan narɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na. Ba tẽ'esgɔ la lebge la tẽ'es-yooro, tɩ ba pʋtẽ'e-yooro la kẽ'esɛ ba lika pʋam.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: Tɩ tõma ka yãŋɛ botɩ õorŋɔ la sẽŋɛ nẽŋa bɔba, tɩ tõma basɛ õorŋɔ la tɩ kʋsebgo la tarɩ ẽ wɛ'ɛsa.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ba tẽ'esɛ yetɩ bãma de la yɛm dõma, la ba de la gɔɔnɔ.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ ka lɛm tẽn-pɩka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Koda tɩ ko'om gilge ẽ, bẽgla ẽ tɩ kʋsebgo la ka le vugra ke'enke'em tʋ'ʋsra tõma. Tɩ tõma keŋe tɩ mẽŋa õbgɛ õorŋ-pɩka la zoole õorŋ-kãtɛ la zuo, la, la ka ẽŋɛ nãana.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: Lan de ba nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ n ka kiiri n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, ba tõn nã'asra la bõna tɩ nẽrba mãalɛ n ãn wʋ nẽrsaalba n kiiri, la bõn-ẽgla, la sɛla n tarɩ nãma anaasɩ, la bõn-vuula.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ mĩ'isi lʋ õorŋ-kãtɛ la kãŋkãŋɛ tɩ a tara pãŋa. Ba zotɩ mɛ tɩ, tɩ sãn paagɛ zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Sirti n dagɩ kʋlko'om tɩ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo bɔna mĩ na, a wʋn fɩrɛ. Bɛla tɩ ba sike õorŋɔ la fu-kãtɛ, dee tɩ kʋsebgo la zã'ara ẽ sẽnna fẽfẽ.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba, tɩ ba tõnna dẽgrɔ tõoma, wʋ ba yɛm n boorɩ se'em, tõnna yãnnɛ tõoma la taaba.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: La kʋsebgo la ken vugra la ke'enke'em nãmsra tõma, tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ba yese lɔg-sɛba n boe õorŋɔ la pʋam na, lobe basɛ ko'om na pʋam.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ba dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ yelle n de sɩra la basɛ mɛ, dee dɔla põmpɔrŋɔ. Ba nã'asrɩ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nãam na, tõnna bɔ'ɔra ba, dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n de sɛka n nãam sɛla woo la. Ẽŋa n masɛ pẽka wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: Tɩ beere le wiige, tɩ ba mẽŋa yese õorŋɔ la mẽŋa lɔgrɔ lobe basɛ ko'om na pʋam.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba, tɩ ba ɩta ba mẽŋa yɛmleego n de yãnnɛ. Tɩ ba pɔgsɩ basɛ lan de ba gã se'em na, dee gã la taaba.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ dabsa se'em, tɩ tõma ka yẽ wẽntulle la wõrbiɛ, tɩ kʋsebgo la ken vugra ke'enke'em nãmsra tõma. Tɩ tõma kɔ'ɔgɛ vɔm pʋtẽ'erɛ.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Tɩ bʋraasɩ mẽ basɛ lan de ba gã la pɔgsɩ la. Tɩ ba bʋra-taaba yɛmleego tara ba. Tɩ bʋraasɩ ɩta sɛla n de yãnnɛ gã la taaba. Bɛla tɩ ba yẽta sɩbgrɛ ba mẽŋa, n masɛ la ba tuure la.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Tɩ la daan yuuge tɩ nẽrba la ka di. Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka yetɩ: «Ya daan sãn sakɛ nɩ mam nõorɛ la, daan ka fõrgɛ Krɛtɩ, tɩ kãn yẽ nɩ nãmsgɔ la yel-bãna wã wuu.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Ban zagsɛ ka bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ na, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba ba tẽ'es-yooro la pʋam, tɩ ba ɩta sɛla n ka masɛ tɩ ba ɩta.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam sosrɩ ya tɩ ya keŋe ya sũure. Tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla kãn ki ya pʋam, sãn dagna õorŋɔ la mã'a n wʋn sã'am.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Sɛla n ka masɛ wuu n boe ba sũa pʋam. La de la be'em, la nõŋɛ bõnɔ zozo'e, la pʋtʋʋlgɔ, la ɩta sũure la tadãana bõnɔ, la nẽr-kʋa, la zɛbrɛ, la yɛm be'ero, la sũ-toogo. Ba wã'arɩ la taaba.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Zaam yʋ'ʋŋɔ Nawẽnnɛ n soe mam tɩ mam dɛna a tõntõnna la, tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa ze'ele mam zẽ'am.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: tɔgra be'em zɛrgra taaba, ba de la wẽn-sɩ'ɩsrɩba, ka nanna taaba, zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma, wẽ'er-m-yõ'ogɔ dõma, nẽrba n mi sɔa n wʋn doose tõm be'em, nẽrba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ ba madõma, la ba sɔdõma nõorɛ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: yetɩ: ‹Apolɩ, da zoe dabeem. Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ ka ze'ele na-keko la nẽŋam. La Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ botɩ sɛba n boe õorŋɔ pʋam na wuu vɔa.›.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Ba yɛm ka like, ba de la nõgõla dõma, ba ka tarɩ nõŋlʋm, ba ka zotɩ ninbãalga.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Bɛla, bʋraasɩ wã, keŋe-ya ya sũure, tɩ mam bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra mɛ tɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ malɛka la n yele mam se'em na.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ba mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla la masɛ la kũm, la baa bɛla ba ken ɩta la bõn-bãma, dee yetɩ nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla la ɩtɩ sõŋa mɛ.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: La kʋsebgo la wʋn pe'ege tɩ ka basɛ la tẽŋa tɩ ko'om gilge.».

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: La daan de la dabsa pia la anaasɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ kʋsebgo la vugra pɛ'ɛra tõma ko-kãtɛ la yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Adriyatiki la zuo. Tɩ sõnsʋa tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ õorŋɔ la tõoma la tẽ'esɛ tɩ tɩ lɛm na tẽŋa.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ mĩ'a lʋ bõn-tɩbsgɔ basɛ ko'om na pʋam tɩ ba bɩsɛ kʋlga la n kɔ zõna se'em, makɛ tɩ la dɛna wʋ mẽtrɩ pitã la ayopɔɩ. Tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ ba le makɛ bɩsɛ tɩ la dɛna wʋ mẽtrɩ pisyi la anii.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Tɩ ba zɔta dabeem tɩ õorŋɔ la wʋn wẽ tãmpĩisi, tɩ ba dɩkɛ kuto n botɩ õorŋɔ zɛ'ɛta banaasɩ basɛ ko'om pʋam õorŋɔ la poorʋm, dee kã'ara Nawẽnnɛ tɩ beere wiige.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ õorŋɔ la tõoma la daan bɔɔra tɩ ba zoe dee basɛ õorŋɔ la, bɛla tɩ ba sike õorŋ-pɩka la ko'om pʋam, ẽŋɛ wʋ ba boorɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ la kuto n botɩ õorŋɔ zɛ'ɛta basɛba basɛ õorŋɔ la nẽŋam.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Tɩ Apolɩ yele sʋda-kɛ'ɛma, la sʋdaasɩ la yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã sãn ka bɔna õorŋɔ wã pʋam, yãma wuu kãn yãŋɛ põsɛ.».

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la wã mĩ'i-sɛba n lʋ õorŋ-pɩka la, dee basɛ ẽ tɩ ko'om tarɩ ẽ fõrgɛ.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Tɩ beere bɔna mĩ wiira, tɩ Apolɩ belem ba wuu tɩ ba di dia, yele ba yetɩ: «Zĩna de la dabsa pia la anaasɩ n wãna, tɩ ya tẽ'esrɩ kũm la vɔm, dee ka di sɛla.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Bɛla mam belnɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya di dia yẽ pãŋa vɔa, tɩ ya zomto baa ayɩla kãn bɔɩ.».

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Ẽn yele bɛla la, dee dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ ba wuu nẽŋam, dee fiise borborɩ la obe.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Tɩ ba wuu yẽ bʋraanɛ mẽ di dia la.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: La tõma wuu daan de la nẽrba kɔbsyi la pisyopɔɩ la ayoobɩ õorŋɔ la pʋam.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Tɩ ba di tɩgɛ, dee dɩkɛ bõnkɔɔla n boe õorŋɔ la pʋam na, lobe basɛ ko'om na pʋam, ba boorɩ tɩ õorŋɔ la n fakɛ.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ba ka bãŋɛ tẽŋa la. La ba yẽ la zẽ'a n tã wʋn dooge, tɩ ba yetɩ, ba sãn tãna, ba botɩ õorŋɔ la tɔglɛ bilam.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Tɩ ba lorge kuto la n botɩ õorŋɔ zɛ'ɛta la basɛ ko-kãtɛ la pʋam, dee lorge mĩ'isi la n lʋ dɔ-sɛba n wʋn botɩ õorŋɔ la sẽŋɛ ban boorɩ zẽ'e-sɛka la basɛ, dee zẽkɛ fuugo la n boe õorŋɔ la nẽŋam na, tɩ kʋsebgo wẽ'era fuugo la, tɩ õorŋɔ la wɛ'ɛsa doone.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Tɩ õorŋɔ la sẽŋɛ zẽ'a tɩ ko'om ka zo'oge mĩ ka zom tẽntõnbĩ'isgo zuo, tɩ a nẽŋam fɩrɛ, tɩ a koŋe sẽnnɛ. Tɩ ko-mĩ'isi wẽ'era a poorʋm la pãŋa, tɩ a sã'am.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba yõgɛ la, tɩ sɛka da lui ko'om pʋam pãm dooge zoe fõrgɛ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la bɔɔra tɩ a fãagɛ Apolɩ basɛ, ka sakɛ tɩ ba kʋ nẽrba la, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n mi ko'om bũŋa la ẽgɛ lui ko'om pʋam yiɛn pãm dooge doone.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: Dee tɩ sɛba n ka mi bũŋa la dɩkɛ dɔ-palagsɩ, la õorŋɔ la bõn-sɛka n sã'am woo bũn dooge. Bɛla tɩ tõma wuu daan põsɛ dooge doone.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Fõn nẽr-sɛka n zɛrgrɩ nẽrba basɛba la, fʋ kãn yẽ bʋʋrɔ, se'ere n soe la, fõn zɛrgrɩ nẽrba basɛba dee ɩta bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na, fʋ mõŋrɩ la fʋ mẽŋa bʋʋrɔ.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Tɩ mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ diti nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero bãma sarɩya dɔla la sɩra.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn zɛrgrɩ fʋ taaba, dee ɩta fʋ mẽŋa bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na, fõn wʋn yãŋɛ põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n diti tõon-be'ero sarɩya la mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Bɩɩ fõn ka nannɩ la Nawẽnnɛ ɩr-sõŋɔ, la a sũ-bʋgsʋm, la a sũ-mã'arɛ n zo'oge la? Fʋ mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ɩr-sõŋɔ la boorɩ tɩ fʋ teege la yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: La fõn sũure kɛ'ɛm mɛ tɩ fʋ ka bɔɔra tɩ fʋ teege yɛm, bɛla tɩ fʋ lagsra sɩbgrɛ bĩŋra fʋ mẽŋa zuo gu'ura daar-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn pa'alɛ a sũ-isgre di nẽrba sarɩya la sɩra la.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ nẽra woo yɔɔrɔ masɛ wʋ ẽn tõm se'em.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n mõrgɛ ɩta sõŋa ɛɛra nã'asgɔ la pẽka, la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, a wʋn bɔ ba la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n ka sakɛ bɔ, sɩ'ɩsra sɩra, dee ɩta sɛla n ka masɛ la, wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre, la sɩbgrɛ.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: A wʋn botɩ toogo la sũ-sã'aŋɔ n paagɛ nẽr-sɛba wuu n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero la. Pɔspɔsɩ de la Zifdõma, pa'asɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: La nẽr-sɛba wuu n ɩtɩ sõŋa wʋn yẽ la na'am pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ, la sũ-mã'asʋm. Pɔspɔsɩ de la Zifdõma, pa'asɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ ka bɔkrɩ nẽrba.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n ka bãŋɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ la dee tõnna tõon-be'ero, ba wʋn sã'am ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã tɩ la ka doose la lɔɔ la. La Zifdõma n tarɩ Amoyisi lɔɔ la dee tõnna tõon-be'ero la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ la lɔɔ la di ba sarɩya.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: La dagɩ sɛba n selsrɩ lɔɔ la n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la de la sɛba n sakɛ ɩtɩ wʋ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na n masɛ.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma n ka tarɩ Amoyisi lɔɔ la, ba sãn ɩta ba mẽŋa wʋ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, la pa'alɛ tɩ ba mẽŋa mi sɛla n masɛ mɛ, baa la ban ka tarɩ lɔɔ la.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Bɛla ba pa'alɩ tɩ sɛla tɩ lɔɔ la yetɩ ba ɩta la boe ba sũurum mɛ, tɩ ba pʋtẽ'erɛ n mi se'em na mẽ pa'ala tɩ bɛla de la sɩra. Bɛla tɩ ba pʋtẽ'era ẽn yɛta ba tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, bɩɩ ba ẽŋɛ sõŋa.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Bõn-bãna wʋn puke la daar-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ Azezi-krisi di nẽrba yɛla n sʋgɛ sarɩya, masɛ wʋ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam mõolɛ n yetɩ se'em na.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Fõn yetɩ fõn de la Zifu la, fõn te'ege la Amoyisi lɔɔ la yẽsna tɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽra.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: fõn mi Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em mɛ. Lɔɔ la zãsʋm fõn mɛ tɩ fõn mina sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la sɛla n ka ãn sõŋa bɔkɛ.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: Fʋ tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn mi mɛ tãna wʋn pa'alɛ fɔɔsɩ sore. Fʋ tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn de la peelem bɔ nẽr-sɛba n boe lika pʋam na.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: Fʋ tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn zãsnɩ na sɛba n ka mi, dee dɛna kɔma karẽnsãama, se'ere n soe la, fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ lɔɔ la pʋam, fõn tarɩ bãŋrɛ zõnzõnnɔ, la sɩra mɛ.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Fõn zãsnɩ fʋ taaba la, bẽm tɩ fʋ ka zãsna fʋ mẽŋa? Fõn mõolɩ yɛta nẽrba tɩ ba da zũura, dee tɩ fõn mẽŋa zũura.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Fõn yetɩ nẽrba tɩ ba da ɩta yalsɩ, dee ɩta yalsɩ fʋ mẽŋa. Fõn nẽ'esrɩ baga tɩ ba mãalɛ nã'asra la tɩ ba de la bõn-põ'osa, dee kẽ'era ba deto pʋam zũura ba yõorɔ dita.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Fõn yẽsnɩ mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la, dee sɩ'ɩsra lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, ɩta Nawẽnnɛ yãnnɛ.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Yãma Zifdõma ĩyã tɩ buuri basɛba nẽrba tɔgra be'em pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ.».

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Fʋ sãn sakra lɔɔ la, wãaga la tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ. La fʋ sãn sɩ'ɩsɛ lɔɔ la, la de la zãŋa, la ãn wʋ fʋ ka wã mɛ na.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: La buuri sɛka n ka wãtɩ, nẽra sãn sakɛ ɩta lɔɔ la n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn geele ẽ wʋ nẽra n wã.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ka wã, dee sakɛ ɩta lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, wʋn zɛrgɛ fʋ mɛ fõn tarɩ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la, wã dee sɩ'ɩsra lɔɔ la.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: La dagɩ sɛka n ɩtɩ Zifdõma ɩrgɔ tɩ nẽrba yẽta n de Zifu. La dagɩ wãaga tɩ nẽrba yẽtɩ la n botɩ fʋ dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽra.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Zifu sɩra sɩra de la sɛka n de Zifu a sũure pʋam. Wãaga sɩra sɩra de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n ɩtɩ sũure pʋam, la dagɩ wãaga wʋ lɔɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na. Nawẽnnɛ n pẽgrɩ nẽr-ẽŋa, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, Zifdõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga la bẽm pʋam gãnna sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, la wãaga la yõorɔ de la bẽm?.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Yõorɔ la zo'oge mɛ. Pɔspɔsɩ, Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ a yetɔga ẽŋɛ la Zifdõma nu'usum.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: La ba nẽrba basɛba sãn dagna sɩra dõma, bɛla wʋn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ kãn dɛna sɩra dãana mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. Baa nẽrba wuu sãn dɛna põmpɔrndõma, Nawẽnnɛ ken dɛna la sɩra dãana wʋ lan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Fʋ yetɔga pʋam, nẽrba mi tɩ fõn masɛ mɛ dɛna sɩra dãana. Nẽrba sãn zɛrgɛ fɔ, fõn tarɩ bʋʋrɔ mɛ.».

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: La tõma n ɩtɩ sɛla n ka masɛ la sãn botɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Tɩ wʋn yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma ka masɛ, ẽn sɩbgrɩ tɩ la ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. Mam tɔgrɩ wʋ nẽrsaalba n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sãn dagna sɩra dãana, a kãn yãŋɛ nɩ dita dũnia nẽrba sarɩya.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: La mam põmpɔrŋɔ sãn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra pukra pa'asra, tɩ la dɛna nã'asgɔ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, bẽm tɩ ba wʋn di mam sarɩya tɩ mam de la tõon-be'ero dãana?.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, bẽm tɩ tɩ kãn yetɩ: «Tɩ ɩta be'em tɩ la botɩ sɛla n de sõŋa wa'am?» Nẽrba basɛba dõtɩ mam tɩ mam n yele bɛla. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di nẽr-bãma sarɩya wʋ lan masɛ.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Bɛla, tõma Zifdõma gãnnɩ nẽr-sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ! La dagɩ bɛla. Mam dẽŋɛ põn yele tɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, tõma wuu boe la tõon-be'ero yamnɛ pʋam.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽra ka boe n masɛ, baa ayɩla.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Nẽra ka boe n tarɩ yɛm ɛɛra tɩ a bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, baa ayɩla.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Nẽrba wuu bure la poore bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, ba wuu tuuge mɛ. Nẽra ka boe n ɩtɩ sõŋa, baa ayɩla.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Ba nõa ãn wʋ yɔɔgɔ n vuke. Ba tarɩ ba zɩlɩsɩ la pã'asra la nẽrba. Ba nõorɛ yetɔga ãn wʋ bõnsɛla zẽlõm na.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Tɩ wʋn yele Abraham n de tɩ yaaba la yelle yetɩ bo? A yʋʋn yẽ la bẽm?.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Ba nõa pɩrɛ la kã'a-be'ero, la yetɔg-be'ero.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: La sãn de nɩ Abraham tõoma ĩyã n botɩ a dɛna nẽra n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a yʋʋn tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ mɛ. La a ka tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Ba ka yagsnɩ la nẽrba kʋa.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Abraham bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a de la nẽra n masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.».

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Ban doose zẽ'e-sɛka woo tole, la de la sã'aŋɔ, la toogo tɩ ba basrɩ mĩ.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Nẽra sãn tõnna tõoma, a to'orɩ la yɔɔrɔ. Yɔɔrɔ la dagɩ bɔ'a tɩ ba bɔ ẽ, la de la a bõnɔ.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Ba ka mi sũ-mã'asʋm sore.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: La nẽra sãn ka tõm tõonɛ, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ tõon-be'ero dõma masra a zẽ'am na sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a lebge la nẽra n masɛ a zẽ'am ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Ba nẽnsɩ ka boe tɩ ba zoe Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Bɛla tɩ naba Adavidi mẽ tɔgɛ yetɩ, nẽra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ la dagna a tõoma ĩyã, tarɩ sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Tõma mi tɩ sɛla wuu tɩ lɔɔ la yetɩ la, a yetɩ la sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na. Bɛla tɩ nẽrba wuu nõa wʋn mũm, tɩ ba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ ba koŋe Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: A yele yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba ba tuure sugri, sɛba tɩ a basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka boe n tã wʋn sakɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Lɔɔ la wen botɩ nẽra bãŋra mɛ tɩ a de la tõon-be'ero dãana.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ ka geele a tõon-be'ero la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ ẽŋa n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ lɔɔ la nẽŋa ka bɔna mĩ. Lɔɔ gõnnɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba dẽŋɛ põn tɔgɛ a yelle mɛ.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Zu-yẽlga ẽŋa tɩ ba tɔgɛ a yelle la, de la nẽr-sɛba n wã la mã'a bõnɔ? Ayɛɩ, la po dɛna la nẽr-sɛba n ka wã la bõnɔ. Tõma dẽŋɛ yetɩ: «Abraham n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.».

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: La de la nẽra n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am. A ɩtɩ bɛla bɔ'ɔra la nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la, bɔkrɛ ka boe.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Wakat-kãna tɩ bõn-ẽŋa ẽŋɛ? La de la Abraham n yʋʋn wã la poorʋm bɩɩ, lan dẽŋɛ a wãaga la? La de la ẽn yʋʋn nãn ka wã la.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrsaalba wuu tõm na tõon-be'ero koŋe Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Abraham yãŋa yʋʋn wã, tɩ la dɛna dãalgɔ pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a de la nẽra n masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am, ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, wakatɛ sɛka tɩ a nãn ka wã la. Bɛla tɩ Abraham dɛna sɛba wuu n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra dee ka wã la sɔ-yaaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ ba de la nẽrba n masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã, a botɩ ba masɛ a zẽ'am na zãŋa, Azezi-krisi n fãarɩ ba tõon-be'ero pʋam na ĩyã.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: La Abraham ken dɛna la nẽr-sɛba n wã la sɔ-yaaba, la dagɩ nẽr-sɛba n wen wã la mã'a, la de la sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra wʋ Abraham n yʋʋn bɔ sɩra se'em, dee yʋʋn nãn ka wã la.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n looge Azezi tɩ a ki, tɩ a zɩɩm yese la ĩyã, a wʋn basɛ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la tõon-be'ero taalɛ. Bɛla Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa masɛ mɛ dɛna sɩra dãana. Kʋrʋm, Nawẽnnɛ mã'agɛ la a sũure ka sɩbgɛ nẽrsaalba ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la Abraham, la a yɩɩsɩ yetɩ ba wʋn wa sɔna dũnia. La dagɩ Abraham n sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ la, la de la ẽn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am na ĩyã.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ a pa'alɛ lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ ẽŋa de la sɩra dãana, ẽŋa mẽŋa masɛ mɛ, dee botɩ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: La sãn de nɩ nẽrba n sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã tɩ ba tɔ'ɔra bõn-sõma sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la, bɛla bɔ sɩra la ka tarɩ yõorɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõor-bĩŋre la ka tarɩ võorɛ.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Bɛla, bẽm pʋam tɩ nẽra tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ? Sɛla ka boe. Bẽm ĩyã? La dagɩ nẽra n sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã tɩ a masɛ, la de la ẽn bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra ĩyã.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Lɔɔ la sɩ'ɩsgɔ botɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure n isgra. La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ lɔɔ la ka boe, sɩ'ɩsgɔ mẽ ka boe.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Tõma yẽ tɩ la de la nẽra n bɔ Krisi sɩra ĩyã tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la dagɩ ẽn sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Bɛla, la de la bɔ sɩra ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõor-bĩŋre la wʋn dɛna yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a n tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ Abraham yɩɩsɩ la wuu. La, la ka de a yɩɩsɩ sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la mã'a ĩyã, la de la nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra wʋ Abraham n yʋʋn bɔ sɩra se'em na. Abraham n de tɩ wuu sɔ-yaaba.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ de la Zifdõma mã'a Nawẽnnɛ? A ka po dɛna nẽr-sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la Nawẽnnɛ? A po dɛna la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam wʋn botɩ fʋ lebge so'olʋm buuri zozo'e sɔ-yaaba.» A de la tõma sɔ-yaaba Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ sɩra la zẽ'am. Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ kũm vɔ'ɔra, dee botɩ sɛla n ka boe bɔna.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Nawẽnnɛ de la ayɩla, a wʋn botɩ Zifdõma masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ sɩra ĩyã, dee botɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la mẽ masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ sɩra ĩyã.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Abraham yʋʋn bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ a ka tarɩ nɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ la tã wʋn ẽŋɛ. Bɛla tɩ a lebge so'olʋm buuri zozo'e sɔ-yaaba, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn yetɩ a yɩɩsɩ wʋn zo'oge se'em na.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Tõma n yetɩ nẽra n bɔ sɩra ĩyã n botɩ a masɛ la, bɛla boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ bɔ sɩra ĩyã lɔɔ la ka le tara võorɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. Tõma pa'alɛ tɩ lɔɔ la tarɩ võorɛ mɛ.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Abraham yʋʋn kʋrgɛ mɛ, tara wʋ yʋʋma kɔbga, dee la kũm, dee tɩ a pɔga Asaara yʋʋn dɛna kõndɔgrɛ, dee kʋrgɛ, la baa bɛla Abraham n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ka bo'oge.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ ẽ na, a yɛm yʋʋn ka yɔɔrɩ, a bɔ sɩra mɛ. La ẽn yʋʋn bɔ sɩra la basɛ mɛ tɩ a yẽ pãŋa nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: A sũure yʋʋn kɛ'ɛm mɛ tɩ a mina sõŋa sõŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽn bĩŋe a nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na mɛ.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ mɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am, ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: La dagɩ ẽŋa mã'a ĩyã tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.».

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: La po dɛna la tõma mẽ ĩyã. Tõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ tɩ masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Nawẽnnɛ daan basɛ tɩ ba kʋ Azezi la tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, dee vo'oge ẽ tɩ a botɩ tõma dɛna nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ tõma dɛna sɛba n masɛ a zẽ'am na, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. La tõma tarɩ sũ-mã'asʋm mɛ naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Azezi-krisi n botɩ tɩ tãna wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am yẽ a yel-sõnnɛ, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. Yel-sõnnɛ ẽŋa tɩ tõma te'ege zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ, dee ɩta sũ-yẽlga tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ na'am pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: La tõma ken ɩta la sũ-yẽlga nãmsgɔ pʋam, tõma mi tɩ nãmsgɔ botɩ tɩ mõrgra sẽnna la nẽŋa.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: Tɩ sãn mõrgra sẽnna nẽŋa, la botɩ tɩ ɩta la sɩra yãŋra. Tɩ sãn ɩta sɩra yãŋra, la botɩ tɩ tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura la sɛla tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Pʋtẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ tɩ tarɩ la kãn ẽŋɛ tɩ yãnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bɔ tõma la ẽŋɛ la a nõŋlʋm tɩ sũa pʋam.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Se'ere n soe la, tõma n daan ka tã wʋn looge yelle la, Nawẽnnɛ n looge yɛm wakat-sɛka la n paagɛ la, Krisi ki mɛ tõon-be'ero dõma ĩyã.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: La de la toogo tɩ nẽra ki nẽra n masɛ ĩyã. La dase'ere, tã tɩ nẽr-sɛka wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ sakɛ ki nẽr-sõŋɔ ĩyã.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ ẽŋa n nõŋɛ tõma zozo'e se'em mɛ, tɩ tõma n daan ken dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma la, tɩ Krisi ki tõma ĩyã.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Krisi n ki tɩ a zɩɩm yese la ĩyã n botɩ tõma masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, bɛla, la de la sɩra tɩ Krisi n wʋn fãagɛ tõma, tɩ tɩ põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Tõma daan de la Nawẽnnɛ bɛ'ɛba, la a Dayʋa la kũm ĩyã tɩ tõma naagɛ la ẽ. Bɛla, tõma n naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ na, la de la sɩra tɩ Krisi n vɔa la ĩyã, tɩ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Sɛla n pa'asɛ, tõma ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ, tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã, ẽŋa n botɩ tõma naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ na.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: La de la nẽra ayɩla n yʋʋn tõm tõon-be'ero ĩyã n botɩ tõon-be'ero wa'am dũnia. Tɩ tõon-be'ero la tarɩ kũm wa'am. Bɛla tɩ kũm kʋʋra nẽrsaalba wuu, ban tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero la ĩyã.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Halɩ lan dẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ Amoyisi a lɔɔ la, tõon-be'ero yʋʋn boe dũnia zuo mɛ. La lɔɔ la n yʋʋn nãn ka boe la, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka geelɩ tõon-be'ero.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Halɩ lan pɔsɛ Adam wakatɛ tarɩ ka paagɛ Amoyisi wakatɛ la, kũm põn bɔna mɛ tãna nẽrba. Baa la nẽr-sɛba tõon-be'ero n ka ãn wʋ Adam n yʋʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em na wuu ki mɛ. Adam yʋʋn de la Azezi n yetɩ a wa'am na wõnnɛ.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: La ba dagɩ bʋyɩla. Adam tuure la, la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a la ka tã wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ taaba. Nẽra ayɩla tuure ĩyã n botɩ nẽrba zozo'e kiira. La bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n ãn sõŋa gãnna, la de la nẽra ayɩla n de Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ nẽrba zozo'e yel-sõnnɛ zo'oge gãŋɛ.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a la n ɩtɩ sɛla la, de la to'ore la nẽra ayɩla tõon-be'ero n ẽŋɛ sɛla la. Nẽra ayɩla tõon-be'ero la tarɩ la sarɩyadia wa'am, tɩ la sẽna la sɩbgrɛ. La nẽrba n tõm tõon-be'ero zozo'e la poorʋm, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ nẽrba la botɩ ba masɛ mɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Lan de nẽra ayɩla tuure ĩyã tɩ kũm tãna nẽrba wuu la, bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽra ayɩla n de Azezi-krisi mã'a ĩyã, nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ ba yel-sõnnɛ zo'oge, botɩ ba masɛ a zẽ'am na, wʋn vɔa mɛ, dita na'am Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Bɛla, nẽra ayɩla tuure n botɩ Nawẽnnɛ dita nẽrba wuu sarɩya sɩbgra ba la, bɛla mẽ, nẽra ayɩla mã'a tõonɛ n masɛ la ĩyã n botɩ nẽrba wuu masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tara vɔm.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Nẽra ayɩla n sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ ĩyã n botɩ nẽrba zozo'e dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma la, bɛla mẽ, nẽra ayɩla mã'a n sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ nẽrba zozo'e dɛna nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: La sãn dɛna lɔɔ la, a wa'am mɛ tɩ tuure wʋn zo'oge pãɩ. La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ tõon-be'ero zo'orɩ pa'asra, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ zo'orɩ mɛ pa'asra gãnna bɛla.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Bɛla, tõon-be'ero n tarɩ pãŋa botɩ nẽrba kiira la, bɛla mẽ, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n tarɩ pãŋa botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Tɩ wʋn ken tõnna la tõon-be'ero tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ pa'asɛ zo'oge bɩɩ?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La sãn dɛna tõon-be'ero ɩrgɔ yelle, tõma de la kũm, tõon-be'ero ka le sɔna tõma. Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne le tõnna tõon-be'ero?.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ tõma wuu n mise ko'om lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, tõma lagɩm na ẽ mɛ dɛna ayɩla a kũm na pʋam?.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Bɛla, ko'om misga la pʋam tõma po lagɩm na ẽ mɛ ki, tɩ ba laagɛ tõma la ẽ lɛ. Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ na'am pãŋa n botɩ Krisi vo'oge la, tõma mẽ vɔa la vɔm paalga.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: La de la sɩra tɩ tõma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla a kũm pʋam na, bɛla, tõma mẽ wʋn lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla, vo'oge wʋ ẽŋa n vo'oge se'em na.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Tɩ mina-ya tɩ tõma n de se'em kʋrʋm na lagɩm na Krisi mɛ ki dɔ-puurŋa la zuo, tɩ tõma tõon-be'ero yɛmleego pãŋa wʋn sã'am ba'asɛ, tɩ tõma kãn le dɛna tõon-be'ero yamsɩ.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra sãn ki, tõon-be'ero ka le tara pãŋa la ẽ.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: La tõma sãn lagɩm na Krisi ki, tõma mi sɩra sɩra tɩ tõma mẽ wʋn lagɩm na ẽ vɔa.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Tõma mi tɩ Krisi ki mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ, tɩ a kãn le ki. Kũm ka le tara pãŋa la ẽ.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Krisi n ki la, a ki la nõorɛ ayɩla sã'am tõon-be'ero pãŋa wakatɛ wuu ĩyã. La lɛɛlɛ tɩ a vɔa wã, a vɔa bɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Bɛla mẽ, geele-ya ya mẽŋa wʋ yãma ki mɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero ka le sɔna ya, dee vɔa bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ, yãma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Bɛla, da basɛ-ya tɩ tõon-be'ero le sɔna ya vɔm wã n ba'asrɩ la, botɩ ya sakɛ a yɛmleego n de be'em na.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Da dɩkɛ-ya ya ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore tɩ ba dɛna tõon-be'ero tõoma lɔgrɔ tɩ ya tara ba tõnna be'em. Dɩkɛ-ya ya mẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ wʋ nẽrba n ki dee vo'oge. Dɩkɛ-ya ya ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la wuu bɔ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a tara ba tõnna sɛla n masɛ.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Tõon-be'ero kãn le sɔna ya, se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ la ka le sɔna ya, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n soe ya.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bo? Tɩ wʋn tõnna la tõon-be'ero, lɔɔ la n ka soe tõma, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ sɔna tõma la ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Ya mi tɩ ya sãn dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa bɔ nẽra dɛna a yamŋa sakɛ a nõorɛ, ya sɩrɩ de la sɛka tɩ ya sakrɩ la yamsɩ. Bɛla, ya sãn sakɛ tõon-be'ero, ya de la tõon-be'ero yamsɩ, tɩ la sẽna la kũm. La ya sãn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, ya de la a yamsɩ, tɩ a botɩ ya dɛna nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ: Kʋrʋm, yãma daan de la tõon-be'ero yamsɩ, la yãma to'oge sɩra zãsŋɔ la mɛ wʋ ban zãsʋm ya se'em na, sakɛ ẽ la ya sũure wuu.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yese ya tõon-be'ero yamnɛ pʋam mɛ, botɩ ya dɛna sɛla n masɛ yamsɩ.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Mam dɩkɛ la nẽrsaalba yelle makɛ bɔ ya, yãma n ka wõnnɩ sɛla võorɛ tɔtɔ la ĩyã. Yãma n daan dɩkɛ ya ĩyã zẽ'esɩ wuu bɔ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la tõon-be'ero tɩ ba tara ba tõnna tõon-be'ero la, bɛla mẽ, lɛɛlɛ wã, dɩkɛ-ya ya mẽŋa wuu bɔ, tõnna sɛla n masɛ, welge to'ore dɛna pʋpeelem dõma.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Yãma n daan de tõon-be'ero yamsɩ la, sɛla n masɛ daan ka soe ya.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Tõon-sɛba tɩ ya daan tõm na, ya yẽ la bẽm yõorɔ ba pʋam? Lɛɛlɛ wã, la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya, tõon-bãma ba'asgɔ de la kũm.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ yese ya tõon-be'ero pʋam mɛ, tɩ ya lebge Nawẽnnɛ yamsɩ. Ya yõorɔ de la ya welge to'ore malgra ya mẽŋa dɛna pʋpeelem dõma. Bõn-ẽŋa ba'asgɔ de la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Asɩra, tõon-be'ero yɔɔrɔ de la kũm. La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a de la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, tɩn lagɩm na tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Bɛla, sɩbgrɛ ka le bɔna nẽr-sɛba n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la zuto.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm Azezi-krisi ĩyã la n fãagɛ tɔ tõon-be'ero, la kũm pãŋa pʋam.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Lɔɔ la ka tã wʋn fãagɛ nẽrsaalba basɛ, se'ere n soe la, nẽrsaalba ɩtɩ la ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em botɩ a ka tarɩ pãŋa. La sɛla tɩ lɔɔ la ka tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ la, Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ mɛ. A tõm na a mẽŋa Dayʋa, tɩ a lebge nẽrsaala ãna wʋ nẽrsaalba n de tõon-be'ero dõma, tɩ a ki yese tõon-be'ero. A kũm na pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ di tõon-be'ero sarɩya sã'am a pãŋa nẽrsaalba pʋam.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ tɩ masɛ ɩta wʋ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na, tõma n vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em, dee ka vɔa wʋ nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n vɔa wʋ nẽrsaalba n boorɩ se'em na, nẽrsaalba mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em na n pakɛ ba. La nẽr-sɛba n vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na n pakɛ ba.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Nẽrsaala mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em, sẽm na kũm. La a sãn dɔla Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, la bɔ'ɔrɩ la vɔm, la sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Nẽra sãn dɔla nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, la de la be'em la Nawẽnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, nẽrsaala mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na ka sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la, la a põn ka tãna wʋn sakɛ ẽ.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na ka tã wʋn paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: La yãma ka le vɔa wʋ nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, yãma vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe la yãma la ĩyã. Nẽr-sɛka woo n ka tarɩ Krisi Sɩa la, a ka de Krisi nẽra.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: La Krisi sãn bɔna la ya, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ ya la vɔm, Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ ya masɛ a zẽ'am na ĩyã. La ya ĩyã wʋn ki mɛ tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge Azezi la, a Sɩa la sãn bɔna la ya lɛ, ẽŋa n vo'oge Azezi-krisi n ki la, wʋn botɩ a Sɩa n boe la ya lɛ la, mẽ bɔ ya ĩyã n kiiri la vɔm paalga.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, la de la pẽrgrɛ tɩ tɩ vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, la dagɩ tɩ vɔa wʋ tɩ mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Ya sãn ɩta nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, ya wʋn ki mɛ. La ya sãn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa kʋ tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrsaala mẽŋa boorɩ la, ya wʋn vɔa.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Nẽr-sɛba wuu n dolɩ sore tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɩ ba la, de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bɔ ya la ka botɩ ya dɛna yamsɩ, le zɔta dabeem. A botɩ ya dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma, tɩ a bɔ sore tɩ ya wi'ira Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Abba», n boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ «tɩ Sɔ».

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la mẽŋa tɔgrɩ la tõma sɩa pa'ala yetɩ tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Tõma n de a kɔma la, tɩ wʋn po to'oge bõn-sõma sɛba tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a kɔma la, tɩ wʋn lagɩm na Krisi po to'oge bõn-sɛba tɩ a wʋn to'oge la. Tõma n nãmsrɩ la Krisi lɛ la, tɩ mẽ wʋn po lagɩm na ẽ yẽ nã'asgɔ.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: Mam mi mɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ sɛba tɩ tõma tarɩ zĩna wã de la fẽe, ka tã wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ la nã'asgɔ sɛka tɩ tõma wʋn wa yẽ la.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ sɛla wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nãam na gu'ura bɔɔra tɩ ba yẽ Nawẽnnɛ kɔma nã'asgɔ n wʋn puke la tɔtɔ.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n nãam bõn-sɛba la wuu lebge la sɛla n ka le tara võorɛ. La dagɩ ba mẽŋa n boorɩ bɛla, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ ba lebge bɛla, dee botɩ ba ken tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn fãagɛ ba sã'aŋɔ tɩ ba boe a pʋam na mɛ, tɩ ba to'oge ba mẽŋa, lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ kɔma po yẽ nã'asgɔ.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Tɩ mi tɩ halɩ wa paam zĩna, bõn-nãanɩsɩ la nãmsrɩ mɛ õosra la toogo, wʋ pɔka n virgri la.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: La, la dagɩ bõn-nãanɩsɩ la mã'a, tõma mẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dẽŋɛ bɔ tɩ a Sɩa pa'alɛ tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ nã'asgɔ la, nãmsrɩ mɛ tɩ sũure pʋam gu'ura wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn fãagɛ tɩ ba'asɛ tɩ tɩ dɛna a kɔma.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Se'ere n soe la, tõma yẽ fãarɛ mɛ, la tõma ken tara la pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura. La fʋ sãn yẽ sɛla tɩ fʋ tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura la, fʋ ka le tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura. Ãne n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ a yẽ?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: La tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ tõma nãn ka yẽ la, tõma mã'agɛ la tɩ mẽŋa gu'ura ẽ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam tɔgrɩ la yãma n mi lɔɔ la. Ya mi tɩ lɔɔ la soe nẽra la wakat-sɛka tɩ a vɔa la.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Bɛla mẽ, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sẽnnɩ sõŋra tõma mɛ tɩ tɔrgɔ pʋam. Se'ere n soe la, tõma ka mi tõma n wʋn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ se'em tɩ la masɛ. La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mẽŋa sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra tõma mɛ, tɔgra yetɔga tɩ nẽra ka tã wʋn tɔgɛ.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ: Pɔka sãn ele, lɔɔ la lʋ ẽ mɛ lagɩm na a sɩra halɩ ka paagɛ a sɩra la vɔm tɛka. La a sɩra la sãn ki, pɔka la yese elle lɔɔ la n lʋ ẽ na a sɩra la pʋam mɛ.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n mi nẽrba sũure la, mi sɛla tɩ Sɩa la boorɩ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Sɩa la sosrɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Bɛla, a sãn ele bʋraaga ayẽma, dee tɩ a sɩra la ken vɔa, a ẽŋɛ la yalsɩ. La a sɩra la sãn ki, ɛlga lɔɔ la ka le sɔna ẽ. Bɛla, a sãn ele bʋraaga ayẽma, a ka ẽŋɛ yalsɩ.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Tɩ mi tɩ sɛla woo pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ sõŋa bɔ'ɔra la nẽr-sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ, sɛba tɩ a looge ba, masɛ wʋ ẽn looge yɛm se'em na.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: M sɔbiisi, yãma zẽ'am la mẽ de la bɛla. La sãn dɛna la Amoyisi lɔɔ la yelle, yãma de la kũm, tɩ lɔɔ la ka le sɔna ya, yãma n lagɩm na Krisi ki la ĩyã. Bɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma yãŋa de la nẽra ayẽma n soe ya, la de la Krisi n ki tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ na n soe ya, tɩ tɩ wʋn tõnna tõon-sõma bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ põn dẽŋɛ looge la, a põn dẽŋɛ looge la yɛm tɩ ẽŋa wʋn botɩ ba ãna wʋ a Dayʋa la, tɩ a Dayʋa la wʋn dɛna kẽema a sɔbiisi zozo'e zẽ'am.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Kʋrʋm, tõma n daan vɔa wʋ tõma mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, lɔɔ la botɩ tõma yɛmleego bɔɔra la be'em, basɛ tɩ tõma ĩyã zẽ'esɩ wuu ɩta sɛla n sẽm na kũm.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: La nẽr-sɛba ĩyã tɩ a põn dẽŋɛ looge yɛm na, a wi ba mɛ. La sɛba tɩ a wi ba la, a botɩ ba masɛ mɛ a zẽ'am. Ẽn botɩ sɛba masɛ a zẽ'am na, a bɔ ba la nã'asgɔ.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, tõma to'oge tɩ mẽŋa lɔɔ la zẽ'am mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, tõma lebge la kũm lɔɔ la zẽ'am, a daan tarɩ tõma wʋ tõma boe la yʋ'a deego pʋam na. Bɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, tõma dolɩ la so-paalɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ tɔ, tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, la ka le dɛna lɔɔ kɛka n gʋlsɛ la tɩ tõma dolɩ.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Bɛla tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Nawẽnnɛ sãn bɔna la tõma, ãne n tã wʋn zɛbɛ la tõma?.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Tɩ wʋn yetɩ lɔɔ la de la tõon-be'ero bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La lɔɔ la daan sãn ka boe nɩ, mam kãn bãŋɛ tõon-be'ero n de sɛla. Lɔɔ la daan sãn ka yele yetɩ: «Da ɩta sũure», mam kãn bãŋɛ nɩ sũure n de sɛla.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka basɛ a mẽŋa Dayʋa la tɩ a põsɛ kũm. A basɛ mɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ tõma wuu ĩyã. A wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne kãn bɔ tõma sɛla wuu la a Dayʋa la?.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Lɔɔ la n bɔ tõon-be'ero sore tɩ a botɩ mam ɩta sɛla n de sũure buuri wuu. Se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ la sãn ka boe nɩ, tõon-be'ero de la bõn-kɩ'ɩŋa.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ dõrɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la? La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ ba masɛ.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Kʋrʋm, mam n daan ka bãŋɛ lɔɔ la, mam daan vɔa mɛ, tɩ yelle kana. La mam n yãŋa bãŋɛ lɔɔ la, tɩ tõon-be'ero tara pãŋa tõnna, dee tɩ mam ki.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Ãne n wʋn di ba sarɩya sɩbgɛ ba? Nẽra ka boe. Azezi-krisi ki mɛ, dee vo'oge, sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ, sɔsra tõma ĩyã.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: Lɔɔ la n de nɩ a tarɩ nẽrba sẽŋɛ vɔm zẽ'am na, a tarɩ mam sẽŋɛ la kũm zẽ'am.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Ãne n wʋn welge tõma la Krisi nõŋlʋm na? Toogo bɩɩ, sũ-sã'aŋɔ bɩɩ, nãmsgɔ bɩɩ, kɔm bɩɩ, nõŋɔ bɩɩ, yel-pakrɛ bɩɩ, bɩɩ kũm n wʋn yãŋɛ welge tõma? Sɛla ka boe.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Tõon-be'ero la n yẽ folle la, a dɩkɛ la lɔɔ la pã'asɛ mam yãŋɛ, botɩ mam ki.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ, fõn doosgo ĩyã tɩ tõma põsra tɩ yõorɛ daarɛ woo. Ba ɩtɩ tõma wʋ piisi tɩ ba tarɩ sẽnna kɔɔrna.».

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Bɛla, lɔɔ la de la bõn-sõŋɔ. Nõorɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ la mẽ de la bõn-sõŋɔ, masɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: La yel-bãma wuu pʋam, sɛka n nõŋɛ tõma la n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma pãŋa tɩ tõma yãŋra.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Bɛla, sɛla n ãn sõŋa la n botɩ mam lebge kũm bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La de la tõon-be'ero la n soe bɛla, a dɩkɛ la lɔɔ la n ãn sõŋa la botɩ mam ki, tɩ la puke pa'alɛ tɩ a sɩrɩ de la tõon-be'ero. Bɛla tɩ lɔɔ la botɩ tõon-be'ero yãŋa lebge sɛla n de tõon-be'e-kãtɛ halɩ tɩ la yõsgɛ.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Mam mi sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛla sɛla ka boe n wʋn yãŋɛ welge tõma la Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm. La sãn dɛna kũm, bɩɩ vɔm, bɩɩ malɛkadõma, bɩɩ pãnsɩ dõma, bɩɩ ke'eŋo dõma, bɩɩ sɛla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, bɩɩ sɛla n sẽm.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Tɩ mi tɩ lɔɔ la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yelle. La mam de la nẽrsaala tɩ tõon-be'ero tara mam ɩta ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: bɩɩ bõn-sɛba n tarɩ pãŋa saazuum, bɩɩ sɛla n tarɩ pãŋa tẽŋa, bõn-nãanɩŋa baa ayɩla ka tã wʋn welge tõma la Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm tɩ a nõŋɛ tõma tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã la.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: Mam ka wõnnɩ mam n ɩtɩ sɛla la võorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka ɩtɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla la, mam ɩtɩ la sɛla tɩ mam ka boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Mam sãn ɩta sɛla tɩ mam ka boorɩ la, la pa'alɛ tɩ mam sakɛ tɩ lɔɔ la ãn sõŋa mɛ.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Bɛla, la dagɩ mam n ɩtɩ bɛla, la de la tõon-be'ero n boe mam pʋam tõnna.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: La de se'em na, mam mi tɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa ka boe mam pʋam. Mam boorɩ tɩ m yetɩ, sɛla n ãn sõŋa ka boe mam n de nẽrsaala mẽŋa n vɔa se'em na pʋam. Mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa mɛ, la mam ka tarɩ pãŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Bɛla, mam ka ɩtɩ sõŋa la tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la, dee ɩta be'em na tɩ mam ka boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Mam sãn ɩta sɛla tɩ mam ka boorɩ la, la dagɩ mam n ɩtɩ bɛla, la de la tõon-be'ero la n boe mam pʋam tõnna.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: Bɛla, mam bãŋɛ bõn-ẽŋa mɛ: Mam n boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa la, mam tã ɩta la sɛla n de be'em mã'a.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Mam sũure pʋam, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: La mam yẽ la bõnɔ ayẽma n boe mam pʋam zɛbra la mam pʋtẽ'e-sõŋɔ la, botɩ mam dɛna tõon-be'ero la n boe mam pʋam tõnna la yamŋa.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: La de la ninbãalga bɔ mam. Ãne n wʋn fãagɛ mam be'em n boe mam pʋam tara mam wɛ'ɛsa kũm zẽ'am wã pʋam?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, ẽŋa n fãagɛ tɔ, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Bɛla, mam pʋtẽ'erɛ pʋam mam sakrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la. La mam mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na n botɩ mam tõnna tõon-be'ero.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mam yetɩ la sɩra, mam de la Krisi nẽra, mam ka parnɩ. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ mam mina m sũure pʋam tɩ mam n yetɩ m yele se'em na, de la sɩra.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Mam tarɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ zozo'e, la sũ-dõoma daarɛ woo m buuri la ĩyã.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: La sãn tã nɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã be'em bɔ mam, tɩ mam welge la Krisi, tɩ la sõŋɛ mam sɔbiisi la n de mam buuri la, mam boorɩ mɛ.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Ba de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n looge ba, botɩ ba lebge a kɔma, bɔ ba nã'asgɔ, bĩŋe nõa la ba lɛ, bɔ ba lɔɔ, la sore tɩ ba nã'asra ẽ, dee yele ba ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛba bɔ ba.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ba buuri la ze'ele la tɩ sɔ-yaaba Abraham, la a yɩɩsɩ la pʋam. La Krisi n lebge nẽrsaala la, de la bãma buuri nẽra. Ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla woo la n soe pẽka wakatɛ n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ. Amina.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Mam n yele se'em na, la ka boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka ẽŋɛ ẽn yetɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na. La, la dagɩ sɛba wuu n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma mẽŋa mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: La dagɩ Abraham yɩɩsɩ la wuu n de a kɔma mẽŋa mẽŋa. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yele Abraham yetɩ: «La de la A'izakɩ kɔma buuri n wʋn dɛna fʋ yɩɩsɩ tɩ mam bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ m bɔ fʋ la.».

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ, la dagɩ kɔm-sɛba tɩ Abraham dɔgɛ la wuu n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma. La de la kɔm-sɛba n dɔgɛ tɩ la doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na, n de Abraham yɩɩsɩ mẽŋa mẽŋa, bãma n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la wãna yetɩ: «Wakat-ẽna sãn wa paagɛ, mam wʋn leme wa'am, la Asaara wʋn tara dayʋa.».

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: La dagɩ bɛla mã'a, tõma yaaba A'izakɩ pɔga Arabɛka yʋʋn dɔgɛ la dayɔɔsɩ bayi, tɩ ba dɛna lɩɩba.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Wakat-sɛka tɩ a yʋʋn nãn ka dɔgɛ ba la, ba nãn ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n de sõŋa bɩɩ be'em, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele yetɩ: «Kẽema la n wʋn lebge a yɩbga la tõntõnna, tõnna bɔ'ɔra ẽ.» La a yele bɛla tɩ ẽn looge yɛm se'em na n sẽŋɛ nẽŋa. Nawẽnnɛ n looge nẽr-sɛka la, la dagɩ a tõoma ĩyã, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n looge yɛm wi ẽ.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam nõŋɛ la Azakɔbɩ, dee basɛ A'eso.».

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bo? Tɩ wʋn yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em na ka masɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele Amoyisi yetɩ: «Mam wʋn zoe la nẽr-sɛba tɩ mam boorɩ la ninbãalga. La mam wʋn tara la nẽr-sɛka tɩ mam boorɩ la zĩile.».

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Bɛla, la dagɩ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em, bɩɩ ẽn mõrgrɩ se'em, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n zotɩ ninbãalga.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: La Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam la gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele naba Afaarõ yetɩ: «Mam n bɔ fʋ na'am tɩ m dɩkɛ fʋ pa'alɛ m pãŋa, tɩ m yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn yese dũnia wuu.».

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ zotɩ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a boorɩ la ninbãalga, la ẽn boorɩ tɩ sɛka sũure kɛ'ɛm na, a botɩ a sũure kɛ'ɛm mɛ.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Dase'ere fʋ wʋn soke mam yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, bẽm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zɛrgra nẽrba tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa? Ãne n tã wʋn zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na?».

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Nẽrsaala, fõn de la ãne wẽ'era nõke'ene la Nawẽnnɛ? Yɔgrɔ lɔkɔ wʋn soke yagba n yagbɛ ẽ na yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fʋ yagbɛ mam wãna?».

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Yagba la tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em la yɔgrɔ la mɛ. A tã wʋn dɩkɛ yɔgrɔ bʋyɩla mɛ, dɩkɛ ba pʋsʋka yagbɛ lɔkɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ dɛna tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ, dee dɩkɛ pʋsʋka yagbɛ lɔkɔ n dagɩ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ. Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ a pa'alɛ la a sũ-isgre, dee botɩ nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ a pãŋa. La a yõkɛ a mẽŋa mɛ, mã'agɛ a sũure la sɛba n masɛ tɩ a sũ-isgre sige ba zuto, dee nara tɩ a sã'am ba la.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: La a ken bɔɔra tɩ a pa'alɛ la a nã'asgɔ n zo'oge se'em nẽr-sɛba tɩ a zotɩ ba ninbãalga la zẽ'am. Bãma de la sɛba tɩ a põn mãasʋm tɩ ba po yẽ a nã'asgɔ.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: La de la tõma sɛba tɩ a looge la, la dagɩ Zifdõma la mã'a, la po dɛna la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: La de wʋ A'oze gõŋɔ la pʋam tɩ la põn gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n yʋʋn dagɩ mam nẽrba la, mam wʋn wi ba tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba. La buuri sɛka tɩ mam yʋʋn ka nõŋɛ ba la, mam wʋn yetɩ mam nõŋɛ ba mɛ.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba yʋʋn yetɩ ba tɩ ba dagɩ mam nẽrba la, zẽ'e-ẽŋa ba wʋn wi'ira ba tɩ mam n de Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa la kɔma.».

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: La Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi yʋʋn tɔgɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle yetɩ: «Baa Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sõorɛ sãn zo'oge wʋ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm na, ba nẽrba fẽfẽe mã'a n wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em na, a wʋn ẽŋɛ a wuu mɛ tɔtɔ tẽŋa zuo.».

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: La ken dɛna wʋ A'ezayi n yʋʋn põn yele se'em yetɩ: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la yʋʋn sãn ka deege nɩ yɩɩsɩ fẽe bɔ tõma, tõma wʋn lebge nɩ wʋ Sodom dõma, dee ãna wʋ Gomõorɩ dõma.».

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bo? Bu-zãŋsɩ n daan ka eerɩ tɩ ba dɛna nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ ba dɛna la nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Dee tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n daan eerɩ tɩ lɔɔ la botɩ ba dɛna nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, ka yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Bẽm ĩyã? Ba ka eerɩ tɩ ba masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am bɔ sɩra ĩyã, ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma tõoma mã'a ĩyã tɩ bãma wʋn masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ba wẽ ba nãma la kugre tɩ nẽrba wẽ'erɩ nãma luta.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ ya! Mam bĩŋri la kugre n wẽ'erɩ nãma Siyõ, a de la kugre n botɩ nẽrba wẽ'era nãma luta. La nẽr-sɛka n bɔ ẽ sɩra, yãnnɛ kãn di ẽ.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam n boorɩ se'em na m sũure wuu, dee sɔsra Nawẽnnɛ se'em de la mam buuri la yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Mam yetɩ mɛ tɩ ba wɩsgɛ ba mẽŋa mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo, la ba ka mi la so-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn doose.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Ba ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masra a zẽ'am, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba ẽŋɛ sɔa ba mẽŋa masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Bɛla, ba ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masra a zẽ'am na.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Krisi n de lɔɔ la kʋkɩ'ɩ, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra wʋn masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ tɔgɛ nẽra n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ lɔɔ la botɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am yelle, yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n sakɛ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na, wʋn vɔa, ẽn sakɛ lɔɔ la ĩyã.».

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: La sãn dɛna nẽra n wʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra tɩ a botɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am na yelle, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Da tẽ'esɛ fʋ sũure pʋam yetɩ, ãne n wʋn zom Nawẽnnɛ yire?» La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ, ãne n wʋn zom Nawẽnnɛ yire botɩ Krisi sige.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: «Dee da yetɩ, ãne n wʋn sige kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa?» La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ, ãne n wʋn sige botɩ Krisi vo'oge.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Bɛla, la ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ bẽm? La yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la lɛm fʋ mɛ, a boe la fʋ nõorʋm, la fʋ sũurum.» La de la yetɔg-ẽŋa n bɛla tɩ tõma mõolɩ yɛta nẽrba tɩ ba bɔ sɩra.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Fʋ sãn tɔgɛ la fʋ nõorɛ nẽrba zẽ'am tɩ Azezi de la Zuudãana, la fʋ sãn bɔ sɩra fʋ sũurum tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ mɛ, fʋ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: La de la sũure pʋam tɩ nẽra bɔ'ɔrɩ Azezi sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am, nẽra tɔgrɩ la a nõorɛ yetɔga yẽta fãarɛ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra, yãnnɛ kãn di ẽ.».

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Bɛla, welgre ka boe Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ tẽŋasʋka. Ba lagɩm tara la Zuudãana ayɩla n ɩtɩ bõn-sõma bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba wuu n sosrɩ ẽ na.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n sosrɩ Zuudãana sõŋrɛ, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.».
