eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne sose ẽ sõŋrɛ, ba sãn ka bɔ ẽ sɩra? La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ ẽ sɩra, ba sãn ka wõm a kõ-yẽlga la? La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm kõ-yẽlga la, tɩ nẽra sãn ka mõolɛ?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: La kõ-yẽlga la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne mõolɛ, Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka tõm nẽrba? Wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na: «La de la sũ-yẽlga, fʋ sãn yẽ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ kõ-yẽlga sẽna la.».

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: La, la dagɩ nẽrba wuu n sakɛ kõ-yẽlga la. Bɛla tɩ A'ezayi gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Zuudãana, ãne n bɔ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ tõma mõolɛ la sɩra?».

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Bɛla, la de la kõ-yẽlga la selsgo n sẽm na bɔ sɩra, la kõ-yẽlga la de la Azezi-krisi yelle tɩ ba tɔgrɩ.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Bɛla, mam sokrɩ ya yetɩ, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba ka wõm kõ-yẽlga la mɛ bɩɩ? Ba wõm mɛ. La de wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na: «Ba kõa la paagɛ tẽŋa wã wuu mɛ, ba yetɔga la saagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu mɛ.».

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Mam wʋn ken soke ya yetɩ, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma ka wõm a võorɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ba wõm mɛ. Amoyisi n yʋʋn de pɔspɔsɩ dãana n yele tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn botɩ ya tara sũure la so'olʋm nẽrba n dagɩ mam nẽrba, mam wʋn botɩ ya sũure isge la so'olʋm sɛka n ka mi sɛla võorɛ.».

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: La A'ezayi yẽ bʋraanɛ mɛ yele tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n daan ka eerɩ mam na, ba yẽ mam mɛ, mam puke m mẽŋa mɛ pa'alɛ nẽr-sɛba n daan ka sokrɩ mam yelle la.».

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Dee yele Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle yetɩ: «Mam tẽegɛ m nu'usi mɛ daarɛ la wuu teese nẽrba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ, dee ka sakra.».

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Bɛla mam sokrɩ mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ zagsɛ a nẽrba la mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla, mam mẽ de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽra, mam de la Abraham yɩɩŋa, Abẽnzamẽ buuri.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka zagsɛ a nẽrba la tɩ a põn dẽŋɛ looge la. Ya mi Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la pʋam zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ A'eli yʋʋn yõnnɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle, yetɩ:.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: «Zuudãana, ba kʋ fʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la mɛ, ba sirge fʋ kãabgɔ zẽ'esɩ la mɛ. Mam m yɩla mã'a n weege, tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba mẽ kʋ mam.».

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn lerge ẽ yetɩ bẽm? A lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam botɩ nẽrba tʋsa ayopɔɩ n weege dɛna mam nẽrba, bãma ka ka dũma Baalɩ n de bagrɛ la nẽŋam.».

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Bɛla mẽ, lɛɛlɛ wã, nẽrba boe mɛ fẽe, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge ba a yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: La de la a yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã tɩ a looge ba, la dagɩ ba tõoma ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ loore la sãn de nɩ na nẽrba tõoma ĩyã, a yel-sõnnɛ na ka le dɛna yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Bɛla, bẽm ẽŋɛ? Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba ka yẽ bãma n eerɩ sɛla la. La de la sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge fẽe ba pʋam na mã'a n yẽ. La nẽrba basɛba la lebge la nẽrba n ka wõnnɩ sɛla võorɛ.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: La ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em yetɩ: «Halɩ wa paam zĩna, Nawẽnnɛ n bʋrsɛ ba yɛm tɩ ba ka wõnna sɛla võorɛ, botɩ ba nini ka yẽta, tɩ ba tʋba ka wõnna.».

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Adavidi mẽ yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Botɩ ba dia la dɛna bẽrgɔ bɔ ba, yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba wẽ nãma lui yẽ nãmsgɔ n de ba yɔɔrɔ.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Botɩ ba nini ãna lim, tɩ ba da yẽta, botɩ ba nãmsra wakatɛ wuu wʋ nẽra n tʋ zeero gõ'osra.».

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Bɛla mam sokrɩ ya yetɩ: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n wẽ nãma lui la, la ba'asɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La ban tuuge la ĩyã, tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ. Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ la botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n ɩta sũure.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma tuure la n tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ dũnia, botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ tara Nawẽnnɛ bõntarsõm na. Ba sãn yẽ fãarɛ ba zõnzõnnɔ, la wʋn tara yõorɔ zo'oge gãnna.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Mam yãŋa yetɩ m tɔgɛ la yãma sɛba n de bu-zãŋsɩ la: Mam n de Krisi Tõntõnna tõnna bɔ'ɔra yãma n de bu-zãŋsɩ la, mam pẽgrɩ m tõonɛ na mɛ.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: Mam ɩtɩ bɛla tɩ m botɩ m buuri la n ɩta sũure, tɩ la botɩ ba nẽrba basɛba yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n daan basɛ ba kẽnkɛrŋa la, a botɩ dũnia nẽrba wuu n naagɛ la ẽŋa. La Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn wa le to'oge ba la, la wʋn ãna la wãne? La wʋn ãna wʋ nẽra n ki dee vo'oge.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Fʋ sãn dɩkɛ pɔspɔsɩ borborɩ la bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, borbor-sɛba n weege la wuu mẽ de la a bõnɔ. Tɩa yẽga sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ, tɩa la wila mẽ de la a bõnɔ.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la ãn wʋ Oliivi tɩa tɩ ba sele, tɩ ba kʋ'ʋsɛ a wila basɛ. La fõn de bu-zãŋka la ãn wʋ Oliivi tɩa n boe weem wille tɩ ba kʋ'ʋgɛ, dɩkɛ toŋe la tɩa tɩ ba sele la wila. Fõn ãn wʋ wille la n to'orɩ sõŋrɛ tɩa tɩ ba sele la yẽga zẽ'am na.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Bɛla, da pɔ'ɔra wila tɩ ba kʋ'ʋsɛ basɛ la. Da wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ, tɩ la dagɩ fõn tarɩ yẽga la, yẽga la n tarɩ fõn.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Tã tɩ fʋ wʋn yele yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ kʋ'ʋsɛ wila la basɛ, tɩ a dɩkɛ la mam toŋe.».

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: La de la sɩra, la de la ban ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ a kʋ'ʋsɛ ba basɛ. La fõn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ fʋ toŋe mĩ. Bɛla, da ɩta tɩta'alʋm, zɔta dabeem.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n ka basɛ tɩa mẽŋa wila la, bãŋɛ tɩ a ken kãn basɛ fõn mẽ, fʋ sãn ka bɔ ẽ sɩra.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Bɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ sõŋa se'em, dee tɔɩ se'em. A tɔɩ mɛ la sɛba n ka sakrɩ ẽ na, dee ɩta fʋ sõŋa, fʋ sãn ken bɔna a sõŋa la pʋam. Sãn dagna bɛla, a mẽ wʋn kʋ'ʋgɛ fʋ basɛ mɛ.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: La Zifdõma la sãn basɛ ba sɩ'ɩsgɔ la, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ ba mɛ lebe toŋe la tɩa la. Nawẽnnɛ tarɩ pãŋa mɛ wʋn dɩkɛ ba lebe toŋe.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Fõn ãn wʋ Oliivi tɩa n boe weem wille, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kʋ'ʋgɛ toŋe Oliivi tɩa tɩ ba sele n dagɩ fʋ zẽ'am. La bãma n de tɩa tɩ ba sele la mẽŋa wila la, la de la nãana tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ ba lebe toŋe la ba mẽŋa tɩa la lɛ.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ yelle n sʋgɛ la, tɩ ya da bɩsɛ ya mẽŋa tɩ yãma de la yɛm dõma: Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba basɛba n kɛ'ɛm ba sũure ka bɔ sɩra la, la wʋn yuuge mɛ halɩ tarɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Bɛla tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri wuu wʋn yẽ fãarɛ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam la gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Fãagra la wʋn ze'ele la Siyõ wa'am. A wʋn botɩ Azakɔbɩ yɩɩsɩ da le sɩ'ɩsra.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Mam nõorɛ n bɛla tɩ mam wʋn bĩŋe la ba, wakat-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn yese ba tõon-be'ero basɛ la.».

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Bãma n zagsɛ kõ-yẽlga la, ba lebge la Nawẽnnɛ bɛ'ɛba, tɩ la dɛna yãma sõŋrɛ. La, la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ n looge ba la, ba de la nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ ba, ba yaabdõma ĩyã.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka teerɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a looge, ẽŋɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ ba la.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Kʋrʋm, yãma bu-zãŋsɩ la zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, a zoe yãma ninbãalga mɛ, Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n zagsɛ ẽ na ĩyã.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Bɛla, bãma n zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, tɩ a zoe yãma ninbãalga la, la de la tɩ a mẽ wʋn zoe ba ninbãalga.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ nẽrba wuu tɩ ba sɩ'ɩsɛ ẽ, tɩ a lagɩm zoe la ba wuu ninbãalga.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, la a yɛm, la a bãŋrɛ la zo'oge mɛ. Nẽra ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ a pʋtẽ'erɛ. La nẽra ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ a sorɔɔrɔ tɩ a dolɩ la võorɛ.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: La de wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Ãne n mi Zuudãana pʋtẽ'erɛ? Bɩɩ ãne n tã wʋn kã'an ẽ?.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Bɩɩ ãne n bɔ ẽ sɛla, dee tɩ a wa lebse ẽ?».

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Sɛla woo ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ sɛla woo bɔna, dee dɛna a bõnɔ. Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ wakatɛ wuu. Amina.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: M sɔbiisi, Nawẽnnɛ n zoe tõma ninbãalga bɛla la, mam kã'anɩ ya tɩ ya dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa bɔ ẽ tɩ la ãna wʋ kãabgɔ n vɔɩ, tɩ ya welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba n paarɩ a yɛm. Bɛla n de pʋ'ʋsgɔ sore n masɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Da ãna-ya wʋ dũnia wã dõma n ãn se'em na. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya yɛm like dɛna paalga, tɩ ya teege, tã'agɛ bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em: Sɛla n ãn sõŋa, sɛla n paarɩ a yɛm, sɛla n masɛ sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ mam na ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya wuu mɛ, tɩ ya da tẽ'esɛ tẽ'esgɔ n gãnnɩ ya mẽŋa. La tara ya tẽ'esgɔ n masɛ, tɩ la doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a tɩ a bɔ ya nẽra woo, yãma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Tõma ĩyã de la ayɩla, dee tara zẽ'esɩ zozo'e, tɩ zẽ'a woo tõoma dɛna to'oto'ore.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Bɛla mẽ, baa la tõma n zo'oge la, tõma lebge la ĩyã ayɩla, tõma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la. La tõma lagɩm na taaba ãna wʋ ĩyã ayɩla zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Tɩ to'oge la bɔ'a to'oto'ore, masɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ nẽra woo se'em na. Bɛla, sɛka n tarɩ bɔ'a n de a tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, a tɔ'ɔsra tɔgra tɩ la masɛ wʋ bɔ sɩra n de se'em na.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de sõŋrɛ tõonɛ, a sõŋra. Nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de a zãsna nẽrba, a zãsna.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de a kã'ana nẽrba, a kã'ana. La sɛka bɔ'a n de a bɔ'ɔra, a bɔ'ɔra la a sũure wuu. La sɛka bɔ'a n de a dɛna nẽŋadãana, a wɩsgɛ a mẽŋa tõnna sõŋa. Tɩ nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de a zɔta nẽrba ninbãalga, a ɩta la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya nõŋlʋm dɛna sɩra nõŋlʋm, sise-ya be'em, la ya gʋrɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Nõŋɛ-ya taaba sɔbiisi nõŋlʋm. La ya nanna taaba, tẽ'esra taaba yelle.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Wɩsgɛ-ya ya mẽŋa, da zɔta-ya zẽyãgrɛ. Tõnna-ya Nawẽnnɛ tõoma la ya sũure wuu.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Ɩta-ya sũ-yẽlga yãma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la ĩyã. Mã'agɛ-ya ya sũure toogo pʋam, mõrgɛ-ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ wakatɛ wuu.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Sõŋɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ sõŋrɛ la, la ya ɩta sãama sãanɔ.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n nãmsrɩ ya la. Pʋ'ʋsra-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ ba, la ya da kã'ara kã'a-be'ero bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Ɩta-ya sũ-yẽlga la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ sũ-yẽlga la. La ya kɛlla la sɛba n kellɩ.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Tara-ya pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla la taaba. Da dɛna-ya pa'al-m-mẽŋa dõma, sakra-ya lagna la tarɩmdõma. Da bɩsra ya mẽŋa tɩ yãma de la yɛm dõma.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Da lebse nẽra be'em, be'em ĩyã. Nargɛ-ya pãŋa ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa nẽrba wuu zẽ'am.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: La sãn dɛna la yãma, ya sãn tãna, ya botɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna yãma la nẽrba wuu tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, da lebse-ya lerge be'em ya mẽŋa. Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure isge. Tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam n de m lebse be'em, la de la mam n wʋn yɔ.».

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Fʋ bɛ kɔm sãn dõnna, bɔ ẽ dia tɩ a di, koyũuro sãn tarɩ ẽ, bɔ ẽ ko'om tɩ a yũ. Fʋ sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, la ãn wʋ bugsãana tɩ fʋ dɩkɛ dɔglɛ a zuugo la.».

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Da basɛ tɩ be'em yãŋɛ fɔ, ẽŋɛ sõŋa yãŋɛ be'em.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Nẽra woo sakɛ tẽŋa la sɔ'ɔnɩba n soe ke'eŋo la. Se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ la ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ tẽŋa la sɔ'ɔnɩba ke'eŋo.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka sãn sɩ'ɩsɛ tẽŋa la sɔ'ɔnɩba nõorɛ, a sɩ'ɩsɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ la dɛna se'em na. Nẽr-sɛba wuu n sɩ'ɩsrɩ la, eerɩ la sɩbgrɛ bɔ'ɔra ba mẽŋa.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ sõŋa la ka zotɩ dabeem la sɛba n bɩsrɩ tẽŋa la, la de la nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ be'em na n zotɩ dabeem. Fõn boorɩ tɩ fʋ da zɔta nẽr-sɛba n soe ke'eŋo la mɛ bɩɩ? Ɩta sõŋa tɩ ba pẽgɛ fɔ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sɛka n soe ke'eŋo la tõnnɩ la Nawẽnnɛ tõoma fʋ sõŋrɛ ĩyã. La fʋ sãn ẽŋɛ be'em, zoe a dabeem, tɩ la dagɩ zãŋa tɩ a tara ke'eŋo wʋn sɩbgɛ. A tõnnɩ la Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ sɩbgra nẽr-sɛka n ẽŋɛ be'em pa'ala Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ la nara tɩ nẽrba sakra nõorɛ, la dagɩ ban wʋn sɩbgɛ ya la mã'a ĩyã, la de la yan mi ya sũure pʋam tɩ la ãn sõŋa la ĩyã.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: La ken dɛna la bõn-ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ ya yɔɔra lampo. Sɛba n to'osrɩ lampo la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ba tõon-ẽŋa tɩ ba tõnna sõŋa.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Bɔ-ya nẽra woo sɛla n de ya bɔ ẽ. Yɔ-ya lampo bɔ sɛka n de a to'oge lampo, yɔ-ya sɛla woo n de ya yɔ bɔ sɛka n de a to'oge. Zɔta-ya sɛka n de ya zɔta, la ya nã'asra sɛka n de ya nã'asra.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Da tara-ya nẽra sãnnɛ. Sãnnɛ sɛka tɩ ya wʋn tara de la ya nõŋɛ taaba. Nẽr-sɛka woo n nõŋɛ a tadãana sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la wuu mɛ.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Da ɩta yalsɩ, da kʋ nẽra, da zũura, da ɩta sũure la nẽra bõnɔ.» Nawẽnnɛ nõa bãna, la basɛba la wuu lagsɛ taaba la yetɔg-ẽna ayɩla pʋam n yetɩ: «Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa la.».

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Nõŋlʋm basɛ mɛ tɩ nẽra ka ɩta a tadãana be'em. Bɛla, nẽra n nõŋɛ a taaba sakɛ la lɔɔ la wuu.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Ẽŋɛ-ya bɛla, yãma n mi wakat-sɛka tɩ tɩ boe wã la ĩyã, wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ dɛna ya nẽegɛ dee basɛ gẽem. Fãarɛ la lɛm tõma mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã gãnna wakat-sɛka tɩ tõma daan pɔsɛ bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Tẽŋa sʋtʋm mɛ tɩ beere deege wiire. Bɛla, basɛ-ya lika tõoma, dee dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ tõnna peelem tõoma.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ tõnna la pʋpeelem wʋ nẽrba n sẽnnɩ wʋntɛɛŋa. Tɩ basɛ-ya pʋyã'an-yooro, la dãam buka, la tũulgo, la yalsɩ, la zɛbrɛ, dee da ɩta sũure la taaba.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Ɩta-ya tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n ẽŋɛ se'em na, da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em yelle pakɛ ya, tɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya ɩta yãma mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ tõma sɛba n tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam na, sõŋɛ nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ pãŋa la, ba tɔrgɔ pʋam. La tɩ da ɛɛra sɛla n ãn tõma yẽlʋm.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ tõma nẽra woo ɛɛra sɛla n wʋn ẽŋɛ a tadãana yẽlʋm, tɩ la sõŋɛ ẽ tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Azezi-krisi ka ɛ sɛla n ãn a mẽŋa sũure yẽlʋm, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Tʋʋrɛ sɛba tɩ ba tʋʋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na, sige la mam zuo.».

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Sɛla wuu tɩ ba yʋʋn gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na, gʋlsɛ la tõma zãsŋɔ ĩyã, tɩ la wʋn botɩ tɩ mã'agɛ sũure sẽnna nẽŋa, dee bɔ'ɔra tɩ bʋraanɛ, tɩ tɩ wʋn tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a bɔ tɩ la.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ sũ-mã'arɛ, la bʋraanɛ la wʋn botɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla la taaba wʋ Azezi-krisi n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: Tɩ ya wuu wʋn tara nõorɛ ayɩla, la yɛm ayɩla nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Bɛla, tɔ'ɔsra-ya taaba wʋ Krisi n to'oge yãma se'em na, tɩ la wʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ Krisi lebge la Zifdõma tõntõnna, tɩ a ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma se'em na, pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: A ken wa'am mɛ tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ la wʋn nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ, ẽn zotɩ ba ninbãalga la ĩyã, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bɛla tɩ mam wʋn nã'asra fõn so'olʋm buuri wuu tẽŋasʋka, la mam wʋn ken yõom nã'asɛ fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ.».

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «So'olʋm wuu nẽrba, lagɩm-ya la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba ɩta sũ-yẽlga.».

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: La le gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «So'olʋm wuu nẽrba, nã'asɛ-ya Zuudãana, buuri wuu nẽrba, pẽgɛ-ya ẽ.».

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: A'ezayi mẽ gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Azese yɩɩŋa n wʋn wa'am, a wʋn sɔna la so'olʋm buuri wuu. La so'olʋm buuri wuu wʋn tara pʋtẽ'erɛ la ẽ lɛ.».

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ tõma tara pʋtẽ'erɛ la ẽ na wʋn bɔ ya sũ-yẽlga, la sũ-mã'asʋm zozo'e, yãma n bɔ Krisi sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a Sɩa pãŋa wʋn botɩ yan tara pʋtẽ'erɛ a zẽ'am na zo'oge gãŋɛ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam mi mɛ sɩra sɩra tɩ yãma ɩtɩ sõŋa mɛ zozo'e, la ya tarɩ bãŋrɛ wuu zo'oge mɛ tãna wʋn kã'an taaba.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: La baa bɛla, mam gʋlsɛ yãma mɛ zẽ'esɩ basɛba la sũ-ke'eŋo tɩ m tẽegɛ ya yãma n põn zãsʋm mina sɛla la. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: botɩ mam dɛna Azezi-krisi tõntõnna tɩ m tõnna a tõoma bɔ'ɔra bu-zãŋsɩ la ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ tõoma tɩ mam tõnnɩ na de la m mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ ba welge to'ore dɛna bɔ'a n paarɩ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Mam n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ mam n tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Mam kãn saagɛ tɔgɛ sɛla yelle, sãn dagna Krisi n botɩ mam ẽŋɛ sɛla botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ na mã'a. La de la mam yetɔga, la mam tõoma pʋam.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa botɩ mam ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ n tarɩ pãŋa. Bɛla tɩ mam ze'ele Zerizalɛm mõolɛ Azezi-krisi kõ-yẽlga la wuu zẽ'esɩ wuu halɩ ka paagɛ Iliri so'olʋm.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: La mam n boorɩ se'em de la m mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ ba nãn ka wõm a yelle la, mam ka boorɩ tɩ m mõolɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽra ayẽma dẽŋɛ mõolɛ la, tɩ m da mẽ nẽra ayẽma ẽbrɛ zuo.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba ka mõolɛ ba a yelle la, wʋn bãŋɛ. La nẽr-sɛba n nãn ka wõm a yelle la, wʋn wõm a võorɛ.».

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Bɛla n gu mam nõorɛ faa, tɩ mam ka yãŋɛ wa'am ya zẽ'am.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam tõm m tõoma zẽ'e-bãma ba'asɛ mɛ. La de la yʋʋma zozo'e tɩ mam boorɩ la m sũure wuu tɩ m wa'am wa bɩsɛ ya.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ mam sãn wa wɛ'ɛsa Ɛspayẽ, mam wʋn doose yẽ ya, dee bɔna ya zẽ'am wakatɛ fẽe tɩ m pʋtẽ'erɛ kɔ'ɔgɛ, dee tɩ ya yãŋa sõŋɛ mam tɩ m tole.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm tɩ m sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe mĩ na.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Masedʋanɩ, la Akayi so'olʋm na looge la yɛm lagsɛ ligri tɩ ba sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Zerizalɛm na tarɩmdõma.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: La de la ba mẽŋa n looge yɛm, la, la de la ba ẽŋɛ bɛla. Zifdõma la n botɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma po lagɩm to'oge bõn-sõma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ba la. Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ ba mẽ sõŋɛ Zifdõma, la ban tarɩ sɛla.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Mam sãn tarɩ ligri la tɩ ba lagsɛ la sẽŋɛ ka bɔ ba kɩ'ɩlɩm, mam wʋn wa'am wa kaagɛ ya, dee tole Ɛspayẽ.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: Mam mi tɩ mam sãn wa wa'am ya zẽ'am, tɩ wʋn yẽ Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ zo'oge.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: M sɔbiisi, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nõŋlʋm na ĩyã, mam sosrɩ ya mɛ, tɩ ya lagɩm na mam mõrgɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ mam ĩyã.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ m yãŋɛ põsɛ la sɛba n boe Zude so'olʋm ka bɔ Azezi sɩra la zẽ'am, la tɩ mam n tarɩ sõŋrɛ la wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm na, Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la wʋn to'oge ba la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ sãn bɔɔra, mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am la sũ-yẽlga, la mam wʋn yẽ vo'osgo yãma zẽ'am.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n de sũ-mã'asʋm dãana wʋn bɔna la ya wuu lɛ. Amina.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Mam Apolɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi, botɩ mam dɛna Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna wʋ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, la tɩ sɔbia Asostɛnɩ n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Korẽntɩ la. Nawẽnnɛ wi yãma tɩ ya welge la to'ore dɛna a nẽrba Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Gõŋɔ la ken dɛna la sɛba wuu n boe zẽ'a woo pʋ'ʋsra Azezi-krisi n de bãma la tõma Zuudãana la ĩyã.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ wakatɛ wuu ya ĩyã, ẽn bɔ ya a yel-sõnnɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã la.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Yãma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ya bɔ'a wuu n de mi'ilum buuri wuu, la yetɔga mi'ilum wuu.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Krisi kõ-yẽlga la tɩ ba tɔgɛ bɔ ya la, yãma gʋrɛ ẽ mɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Bɛla, yãma ka koŋe bɔ'a baa ayɩla, yãma n gu'uri tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wa'am wa puke a mẽŋa la.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ẽŋa n wʋn basɛ tɩ ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ dũnia ba'asgɔ. Tɩ ba kãn tã'agɛ dõrɛ ya tɩ ya ẽŋɛ be'em tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi lemŋo daarɛ.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya tɩ ya lagɩm na a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana la dɛna ayɩla la, de la sɩra dãana.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam sosrɩ ya la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ ya wuu sakra taaba nõorɛ, tɩ welgre da bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka. Ẽŋɛ-ya nõorɛ ayɩla, tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla la taaba.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: M sɔbiisi, se'ere n soe tɩ mam yele bɛla la, Akolowe yir-dõma basɛba n wa'am wa tɔgɛ pa'alɛ mam tɩ ka-wõm-taaba n boe ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Mam wõm tɩ yãma nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Apolɩ, tɩ basɛba yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Apolɔsɩ, tɩ basɛba yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, tɩ basɛba mẽ yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Krisi.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Krisi welse mɛ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Apolɩ n daan ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo yãma ĩyã, bɩɩ ba mise yãma ko'om pʋam la Apolɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, mam n ka mise ya nẽra baa ayɩla ko'om pʋam sãn dagna Akrispusi la Agayusi la.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Bɛla, nẽra kãn tã'agɛ yele yetɩ, ba daan mise ẽŋa ko'om pʋam la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: Oh, I baptized the house of Stephanas too. Otherwise, I dont know if I baptized anyone else.
local: Ẽhẽe! Mam daan ken mise la Astefanasɩ la a yir-dõma ko'om pʋam. La mam ka le mina mam sãn ken mise nẽr-sɛka.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Krisi ka tõm mam tɩ m mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam, a tõm mam tɩ m mõolɛ la Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga bɔ nẽrba, la, la dagɩ la nẽrsaalba yɛm, tɩ la da basɛ tɩ Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo la pãŋa la lebge zãŋa.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo yetɔga la de la yalnɛ bɔ nẽr-sɛba n ka yẽ fãarɛ la. La tõma sɛba n yẽ fãarɛ la mi tɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn sã'am yɛm dõma yɛm, dee yese bãŋrɩba bãŋrɛ.».

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Yɛm dõma yɛm võorɛ de la bẽm? Gõn-mitba mi'ilum võorɛ de la bẽm? Dũnia wã nõke'en-dõma n wẽ'erɩ nõkɛ'ɛma tɩ bãma mi yɛla zozo'e la võorɛ de la bẽm? Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ tɩ dũnia wã yɛm de la yalnɛ.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ sɛla la a yɛm na, nẽrsaalba ka tã'agɛ bãŋɛ ẽ la ba mẽŋa yɛm. Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ tɩ nẽrsaalba yetɩ la de la yalnɛ la, fãagɛ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Zifdõma boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ la bõn-yãlma n pa'alɛ tɩ la de la sɩra, la Grɛkɩdõma eerɩ la yɛm.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Tõma nõo mõolɩ la Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo la kõ-yẽlga, kõ-yẽlga ẽŋa tɩ Zifdõma dɩkɛ tɩ a dɛna pɔ'ɔrɛ yetɔga, tɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la dɩkɛ tɩ la dɛna yalnɛ.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n looge sɛba la, sɛba n de Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la, mi tɩ Krisi de la pãŋa dãana la yɛm dãana n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ, tɩ nẽrsaalba dɩkɛ tɩ a dɛna yalnɛ na, tarɩ yɛm mɛ gãnna nẽrsaalba yɛm. La sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrsaalba tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ka tarɩ pãŋa la, tarɩ pãŋa mɛ gãnna nẽrsaalba pãŋa.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: M sɔbiisi, lɛɛlɛ wã, tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi la n ãn se'em. Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ dũnia yɛm, la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ pãŋa, la nẽrzuto ka zo'oge ya pʋam.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ looge sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ la de la yalnɛ, tɩ a ẽŋɛ la yɛm dõma yãnnɛ, la a ken looge la sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ a ka tarɩ pãŋa la, tɩ a botɩ pãnsɩ dõma n yẽ yãnnɛ.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: La a looge la sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma ka bɩsrɩ, la sɛla tɩ ba nẽ'esrɩ, la sɛla tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a de la zãŋa la, tɩ a sã'am sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ a de la ninmõ'orɛ bõnɔ.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ nẽra n da yãŋɛ zẽkɛ a mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ ya lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ tɩ tara Nawẽnnɛ yɛm, dee masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, welge to'ore lebge a nẽrba n yese tõon-be'ero pʋam.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Bɛla tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a wẽ yõ'ogɔ, a wẽ yõ'ogɔ la sɛla tɩ Zuudãana ẽŋɛ la.».

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam n daan wa'am ya zẽ'am wa mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga n sʋgɛ bɔ ya la, la dagɩ la yetɔg-mi'ilum, bɩɩ nẽrsaalba yɛm tɩ mam mõolɛ bɔ ya.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Se'ere n soe la, mam daan looge yɛm tɩ mam kãn mõolɛ sɛla sɛla yelle, sãn dagna Azezi-krisi yelle, ẽŋa n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la yelle.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: Mam n daan boe ya zẽ'am na, mam daan ka tarɩ pãŋa, tɩ dabeem daan tara mam, tɩ mam gõgra.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Mam n daan mõolɛ dee zãsʋm kõ-yẽlga la, la daan dagɩ la nẽrsaalba yɛm yetɔga tɩ mam daan tɔgɛ bɔ ya tɩ ya sakɛ, mam daan tɔgɛ mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ a pãŋa.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: tɩ yãma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba yɛm ĩyã, la de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa ĩyã.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: La baa bɛla, tõma tɔgrɩ la yɛm yetɔga bɔ'ɔra sɛba n bɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la doosgo pʋam. La dagɩ dũnia wã yɛm, la mẽ ka de sɛba n soe dũnia wã yɛm. Bãma wʋn lebge la zãŋa.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Tõma mõolɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n daan sʋgɛ tɩ nẽrba ka mina. Nawẽnnɛ daan põn looge yel-bãma mɛ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo tɩ a botɩ tɩ yẽ nã'asgɔ a zẽ'am.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Sɛba n soe dũnia wã baa ayɩla ka mi yɛm ẽna wã. Ba daan sãn mi nɩ ẽ, ba kãn ka nɩ Zuudãana n tarɩ na'am pãŋa wuu la dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm tɩ a bɔ nẽr-sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ na, gãnnɩ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽra nãn ka yẽ, bɩɩ wõm, bɩɩ tẽ'esɛ a pʋtẽ'erʋm.».

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a Sɩa la puke bõn-bãma pa'alɛ la tõma. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la mi sɛla woo mɛ, baa la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sɛba n sʋgɛ.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Ãne n mi nẽrsaala pʋtẽ'erɛ? La de la nẽrsaala mẽŋa sɩa mã'a. Bɛla, nẽra mẽ ka mi Nawẽnnɛ pʋtẽ'erɛ, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la mã'a.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma ka to'oge dũnia wã sɩa, tõma to'oge la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n ze'ele a zẽ'am, tɩ tɩ yãŋɛ bãŋɛ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a bɔ tɔ.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: La tõma n tɔgrɩ yel-bãna wã, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba yɛm yetɔga, tõma tɔgrɩ la yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ tõma, tõma tɔgrɩ la yel-sɛba n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la kãn tã'agɛ to'oge sɛla n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am, la de la yalnɛ bɔ ẽ, a kãn tã'agɛ bãŋɛ a võorɛ. Nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ẽ lɛ mã'a n mi Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la yelle.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ẽ lɛ n tã wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla woo n ãn se'em bɔkɛ. La nẽra ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ ẽŋa dãana yelle bɔkɛ.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Ãne n mi Zuudãana pʋtẽ'erɛ? Ãne n wʋn yãŋɛ kã'an ẽ?» La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma tarɩ la Krisi pʋtẽ'erɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam daan ka tã'agɛ tɔgɛ ya wʋ nẽrba n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa. Mam daan tɔgɛ ya wʋ nẽrba n vɔa wʋ ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, wʋ ya de la bilɛɛsɩ Krisi doosgo la pʋam.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Yãma n daan ka tã wʋn to'oge zãsŋɔ ke'eŋo n ãn wʋ di-ke'ene la ĩyã, mam daan tɔgɛ ya wʋ bilɛɛsɩ n mõgrɩ bĩ'isum dee ka paagɛ di-ke'ene dia. La baa lɛɛlɛ wã, ya ken ka paagɛ ya to'oge zãsŋɔ ke'eŋo.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: yãma n ken vɔa wʋ ya mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na ĩyã. Ya sãn ɩta sũure zɛbra la taaba, bɛla ka pa'alɛ tɩ ya vɔa wʋ ya mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee ɩta wʋ dũnia nẽrba n ɩtɩ se'em na sɩra?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Ya nẽra ayɩla sãn yetɩ: «Mam dolɩ la Apolɩ», tɩ ayɩla yetɩ: «Mam dolɩ la Apolɔsɩ», ya ka ɩtɩ wʋ dũnia nẽrba la?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Apolɔsɩ de la ãne? Apolɩ de la ãne? Tõma wen dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba n daan botɩ ya bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra. Tõma nẽra woo tõnnɩ la tõon-sɛka tɩ Zuudãana la bɔ tɔ tɩ tɩ tõm.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Tõma ãn wʋ kaarba la, mam sele mɛ, tɩ Apolɔsɩ basɛ ko'om. La Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ ba bɩta.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Sɛka n sellɩ, la sɛka n basrɩ ko'om na de la zãŋa. Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n botɩ ba bɩta.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Sɛka n sellɩ, la sɛka n basrɩ ko'om na de la ayɩla. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn yɔ nẽra woo wʋ ẽn tõm se'em na.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Tõma lagɩm taaba tõnna la Nawẽnnɛ tõoma. La yãma ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ va'am, la ya ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ deego.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã, mam tõm wʋ tõnmẽta n mi mẽa sõŋa n ẽebɛ ẽbrɛ, tɩ nẽra ayẽma mẽ dɔglɛ a zuo. La nẽra woo guuse a mẽŋa la ẽn mẽtɩ se'em na.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ la Azezi-krisi tɩ a dɛna ẽbrɛ la. Ẽbrɛ ayẽma ka le bɔna tɩ nẽra wʋn ẽebɛ.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n mẽtɩ dɔgla ẽbrɛ la zuo la, basɛba dɩkrɩ la sãlma, bɩɩ zũuro, bɩɩ kug-sõma ligri n zo'oge mẽta dɔgla ẽbrɛ la zuo. Tɩ basɛba dɩkra dɔɔrɔ, bɩɩ mõorɔ, bɩɩ kẽnkã mẽta dɔgla.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: La Azezi-krisi lemŋo daarɛ, nẽra woo n tõm se'em wʋn puke mɛ peelem pʋam. La de la bugum n wʋn makɛ tõoma la, pa'alɛ nẽra woo tõoma n ãn se'em.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Bugum na sãn ka di nẽra tõoma, a dãana wʋn to'oge yɔɔrɔ.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: La bugum na sãn di nẽra tõoma, a dãana wʋn koŋe yɔɔrɔ. La nẽra la mẽŋa wʋn yẽ fãarɛ, la a wʋn ãna wʋ a doose la bugum pʋam põsɛ.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Ya ka mi tɩ ya de la Nawẽnnɛ deego tɩ a Sɩa bɔna ya pʋam?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Nẽra sãn sã'am Nawẽnnɛ deego la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sã'am a dãana. Nawẽnnɛ deego de la a mẽŋa mã'a bõnɔ, la yãma n de a deego la.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Nẽra da pã'asɛ a mẽŋa. Nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa tarɩ dũnia wã yɛm, la masɛ mɛ tɩ a lebge gɔɔŋɔ dũnia wã pʋam, bɛla n wʋn basɛ tɩ a lebge yɛm dãana.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ la de la yɛm na, de la yalnɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ bẽtɩ yɛm dõma mɛ yõgra ba ba yɛmkẽgsɩga la pʋam.».

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana mi yɛm dõma tẽ'esgɔ mɛ, a mi tɩ ba de la zãŋa.».

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka da wẽ yõ'ogɔ nẽrsaala ĩyã. Sɛla woo de la yãma bõnɔ.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Mam Apolɩ, bɩɩ Apolɔsɩ, bɩɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, bɩɩ dũnia wã, bɩɩ yõvore, bɩɩ kũm, bɩɩ sɛla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, bɩɩ sɛla n wʋn wa wa'am, yãma n soe ba wuu.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: La yãma, Krisi n soe ya, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɔna Krisi.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya to'oge tɩ sɔbi-pɔka Afeebe ya zẽ'am. A de la Sãnkre Wẽndeego yel-bɩsra.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: To'oge-ya ẽ sõŋa Zuudãana doosgo la pʋam, wʋ lan masɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba ɩta se'em na. La sɛla sãn wa bɔna tɩ a bɔɔra ya sõŋrɛ, ya sõŋɛ ẽ. Ẽŋa mẽŋa sõŋɛ nẽrba zo'oge mɛ, la mam mẽŋa mẽ.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Apriska, la Akila n de mam tõntõn-taaba Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam na.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Ba daan põsɛ ba yõorɛ mɛ mam ĩyã fãagɛ mam. La dagɩ mam m yɩla mã'a n pʋ'ʋsrɩ ba ba yel-sõnnɛ, bu-zãŋsɩ Wẽndeto la wuu nẽrba mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ba.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Azezi nẽrba la wuu n lagsrɩ ba yire la. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya m sɩr-nõŋrɛ A'epayinɛtɩ bɔ mam. Ẽŋa n de pɔspɔsɩ nẽra n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra Azi so'olʋm.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Amaari n daan tõm nãmsɛ zo'oge bɔ ya la.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ A'ãndronikusi, la Azuniasɩ n de mam buuri n daan po lagɩm na mam bɔna yʋ'a deem na. Ba de la nẽrba tɩ ba nannɩ Krisi Tõntõnɩba la pʋam, la ba põn dẽŋɛ mam mɛ lebge Krisi nẽrba.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Ampiliyatusi n de mam sɩrɛ tɩ mam nõŋɛ Zuudãana doosgo pʋam na.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya A'urbẽ n de tɩ tõntõn-tadãana Krisi doosgo pʋam na, la m sɩr-nõŋrɛ A'ɛstakisi.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Apelɛsɩ n mõrgɛ Krisi doosgo pʋam na. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Aristobuli yir-dõma.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya A'erodiyõ n de m buuri nẽra la. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya nẽr-sɛba n boe Anarsisi yire dɔla Zuudãana la.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Atirfɛnɩ, la Atirfozɩ tɩ ba de la pɔgsɩ n mõrgrɩ tõnna Zuudãana doosgo pʋam. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya m sõma Apɛrsidi n mõrgɛ tõm zozo'e bɔ Zuudãana la.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Arufusi tɩ nẽrba zozo'e bɩsrɩ Zuudãana doosgo pʋam na, la a ma n lebge wʋ mam ma la.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Asẽnkriti, la Afelgõ, la A'ɛrmẽsɩ, la Apatrobasɩ, la A'ɛrmasɩ, la sɔbiisi sɛba wuu n boe la ba lɛ la.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Afilolɔgɩ, la Azuli, la Aneere la a tã, la A'olẽmpasɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sɛba n boe la ba lɛ la.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm. Krisi Wẽndeto nẽrba la wuu pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam kã'anɩ ya tɩ ya guusra ya mẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ welgre sẽnna ya zẽ'am, dee sɩ'ɩsra zãsŋɔ sɛka tɩ ya to'oge, botɩ nẽrba basra Nawẽnnɛ sore la. Zãagɛ ya mẽŋa la ba lɛ.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãma buuri ka tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi, ba tõnnɩ na ba mẽŋa sõŋrɛ ĩyã. Ba tɔgrɩ la nõ-yẽlʋm yetɔga, la yetɔg-yẽlsɩ pã'asra nẽr-sɛba yɛm n ka zo'oge la.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ yãma n sakrɩ Zuudãana se'em mɛ. Bɛla, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ yãma ĩyã. La mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya tara yɛm mina sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la ya dɛna pʋpeelem dõma, da ɩta sɛla n de be'em.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n de sũ-mã'asʋm dãana la kãn yuuge dee sã'am Asũtãana pãŋa botɩ a bɔna ya nãma tẽŋa. Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya lɛ.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Mam tõntõn-tadãana Atimõtɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. Alusiyusi, la Azasõ, la Asozipatɛɛrɩ n de mam buuri la mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Mam Atɛrtiyusi n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã la, mam mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya la Zuudãana yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Agayusi tɩ mam sẽnnɩ sigra a yire la pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya, la de la ẽŋa yire tɩ Azezi nẽrba la wuu lagsrɩ. A'erastɩ n de tẽn-ẽŋa lig-gu'ura, la tɩ sɔbia Akartusi pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. .

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Pẽgɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ, ẽŋa n tã wʋn botɩ ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, wʋ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam mõolɛ pa'alɛ ya Azezi-krisi yelle, botɩ ya bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yelle n sʋgɛ la. Yel-ẽŋa sʋgɛ mɛ halɩ yʋʋma yʋʋma.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ na yãŋa botɩ a puke mɛ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na. A botɩ so'olʋm buuri wuu nẽrba bãŋɛ ẽ mɛ, tɩ ba wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra, sakɛ ẽ.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n de yɛm dãana la sɔna nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ, Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã. Amina.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: To'oge-ya nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam na sõŋa, la ya da wẽ'era nõke'ene la ẽ lɛ a tẽ'esgɔ pʋam.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Nẽr-sɛka tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ ẽŋa tã wʋn di sɛla woo mɛ, tɩ sɛka pãŋa n pɔ'ɔrɩ la dita bõnkɔɔla la zẽerɔ mã'a.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Bɛla, sɛka n diti sɛla woo la, da pɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛka n ka diti sɛla woo la. La sɛka n ka dita sɛla woo la, da zɛrgɛ sɛka n diti sɛla woo la, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ to'oge ẽ mɛ.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Fõn de la ãne zɛrgra nẽra ayẽma tõntõnna? A sãn zɛ saazuo, bɩɩ a sãn lui mɛ, bɛla pakɛ la a zuudãana. La a wʋn zɛ la saazuo, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana la tarɩ pãŋa mɛ wʋn botɩ a zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Nẽr-sɛka tẽ'esrɩ mɛ tɩ dabsa basɛba ãn sõŋa gãnna dabsa basɛba mɛ. Tɩ ayẽma yetɩ ba wuu de la ayɩla. Dẽnɩ tɩ nẽra woo bãŋɛ tɩ ẽŋa n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na masɛ mɛ.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n tẽ'esrɩ dabsa n ãn sõŋa yelle la, ɩtɩ bɛla nã'asra la Zuudãana. La nẽr-sɛka n diti sɛla woo la, mẽ bɔ'ɔrɩ la Zuudãana nã'asgɔ, ẽn pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ dia la ĩyã la. La sɛka n ka diti dia basɛba la, ɩtɩ bɛla mẽ bɔ'ɔra la Zuudãana nã'asgɔ, ẽŋa mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: La de se'em na, tõma nẽr-sɛka ka vɔa a mẽŋa ĩyã, la nẽr-sɛka ka kiiri a mẽŋa ĩyã.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Tõma sãn vɔa, tɩ vɔa la Zuudãana ĩyã. La tɩ sãn ki, tɩ ki la Zuudãana ĩyã. Bɛla, tɩ sãn vɔa mɛ, bɩɩ tɩ sãn ki mɛ, tɩ de la Zuudãana la nẽrba.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Krisi ki mɛ dee vo'oge, tɩ a dɛna sɛba n ki, la sɛba n vɔa Zuudãana.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: La fõn, bẽm tɩ fõn zɛrgra fʋ sɔbia? La fõn mẽ, bẽm tɩ fõn pɔ'ɔra fʋ sɔbia? Tɩ wuu wʋn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne nẽŋam tɩ a di tɩ sarɩya.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam n de Zuudãana la vɔa mɛ, nẽrsaalba wuu wʋn ka dũma mam nẽŋam, tɩ nẽra woo wʋn nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Bɛla, tõma nẽra woo wʋn tɔgɛ a mẽŋa yelle bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ da le zɛrgra taaba. La de la tɩ looge yɛm, da ẽŋɛ sɛla n wʋn botɩ tɩ sɔbia tuuge, bɩɩ n wʋn botɩ a basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Mam n lagɩm na Zuudãana Azezi dɛna ayɩla la, mam mi sɩra sɩra tɩ sɛla ka boe dɛna dẽgrɔ a mẽŋa. La nẽra sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ sɛla de la dẽgrɔ dee di, la de la dẽgrɔ bɔ ẽ.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Bõn-sɛka tɩ fõn diti la sãn botɩ fʋ sɔbia sũure sã'am, fʋ ka le ɩta wʋ nõŋlʋm n de se'em na. Da sakɛ tɩ dia tɩ fʋ diti la botɩ fʋ sɔbia tɩ Krisi ki a ĩyã la sã'am.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Da basɛ tɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa bɔ fõn na, lebge sɛla n wʋn dɛna yʋ'ʋrɛ sã'aŋɔ.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Bẽm ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na dagɩ dia, la bõn-yũula yelle, la de la sɛla n masɛ, la sũ-mã'asʋm, la sũ-yẽlga tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n dolɩ Krisi wãna, ẽŋa dãana n paarɩ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm, la nẽrba mẽ yetɩ mɛ tɩ ẽn ɩtɩ se'em na ãn sõŋa.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Bɛla, tɩ ɩta-ya bõn-sɛba wuu n sẽm na sũ-mã'asʋm, dee sõŋra nẽrba basɛba tɩ tɩ wuu lagɩm sẽnna nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Da sã'am-ya Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ dia ĩyã. Dia wuu ãn sõŋa mɛ tɩ ba di. La nẽra sãn di sɛla n wʋn botɩ a tadãana tuuge, la ka ãn sõŋa.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: La ãn sõŋa tɩ fʋ da ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛba wuu n wʋn botɩ fʋ sɔbia tuuge Nawẽnnɛ sore la zuo, la sãn dɛna nẽnnɔ ɔbga, bɩɩ dãam yũa, bɩɩ sɛla woo n wʋn botɩ a tuuge.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Fõn mi sɛla la bõn-ẽŋa yelle la, tara ẽ fõn mẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ mã'a zẽ'am. Nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ a bɩsɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa, dee ka zɛrgra a mẽŋa, ẽŋa dãana n tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n zɛrgrɩ a mẽŋa la sɛla tɩ a diti yelle, a koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, a ka ɩtɩ ẽŋa n bɔ sɩra mina se'em na. La bõn-sɛka woo n ka dolɩ la bɔ sɩra de la tõon-be'ero.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Ba tɔgrɩ tɩ ya ɩtɩ la yalsɩ, halɩ yalsɩ sɛka buuri tɩ baa sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka sakɛ ɩta. Mam wõm tɩ ba yetɩ nẽra ayɩla n boe ya pʋam gã la a sɔ pɔga.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Dee tɩ yãma zẽkra ya mẽŋa. La de nɩ na yãma sũure sã'am, tɩ ya yese bʋra-sɛka n ẽŋɛ yel-be'e-ẽŋa la ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Baa mam n ka boe yãma zẽ'am, mam pʋtẽ'erɛ boe la ya lɛ mɛ. La mam põn di bʋra-sɛka n ẽŋɛ yel-be'e-ẽŋa la sarɩya mɛ wʋ mam boe la yãma lɛ.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: yele la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ yetɩ: Yãma n lagsɛ la taaba, tɩ mam pʋtẽ'erɛ bɔna la yãma, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi pãŋa bɔna la ya lɛ la.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: ya yese nẽr-ẽŋa ya tẽŋasʋka basɛ Asũtãana nu'usum tɩ a sã'am a ĩyã, dee tɩ a sɩa wʋn yẽ fãarɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lemŋo daarɛ.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Yãma n zẽkrɩ ya mẽnsɩ la, la ka ãn sõŋa. Yãma ka mi tɩ dãbɩllɛ fẽe n botɩ borborɩ zom wuu ukra?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Yese-ya tõon-be'e-ẽŋa n ãn wʋ dãbɩllɛ la ya tẽŋasʋka, bɛla tɩ ya wʋn ãna sõŋa wʋ zom paalga tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ dãbɩllɛ ba pʋam. Ya põn ãna sõŋa wʋ borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ la. Se'ere n soe la, Krisi daan sakɛ mɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ tõma ĩyã wʋ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa pebila la.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ yese pʋtẽ'e-be'ero, la tõon-be'ero wuu tɩ vɔm pʋam, dee dɛna pʋpeelem dõma, la sɩra dõma wʋ ba diti Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ la.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: Mam n daan gʋlsɛ gõn-sɛka la, mam daan yele ya tɩ ya da lagɩm na sɛba n ɩtɩ yalsɩ.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Mam ka tɔgrɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe dũnia pʋam ɩta yalsɩ, bɩɩ nõŋɛ bõnɔ, bɩɩ dɛna nayigba, bɩɩ bag-kãabrɩba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na yelle. Ya sãn yetɩ ya welge ya mẽŋa la bãma, dẽnɩ tɩ ya yese dũnia wã pʋam.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Mam daan gʋlsɛ bɔ ya yetɩ, ya da dɔla la nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ a mẽŋa tɩ ẽŋa dolɩ la Nawẽnnɛ dee ɩta yalsɩ, bɩɩ nõŋɛ bõnɔ, bɩɩ kãabra baga, bɩɩ tʋʋra nẽrba, bɩɩ yũura dãam bugra, bɩɩ zũura. Da põn lagɩm-ya la nẽr-ẽŋa dãana di.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: La dagɩ mam n wʋn di sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na sarɩya, Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn di ba sarɩya. La de la yãma sɛba n boe la taaba la n wʋn di taaba sarɩya, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Dige-ya tõon-be'ero dãana la ya pʋam.».

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Ya nẽra ayɩla sãn tara yelle la a tadãana, bẽm tɩ a tarɩ ẽ wɛ'ɛsa sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɩ ba ka mãalɛ yelle la, dee ka tarɩ ẽ wɛ'ɛsa Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zẽ'am?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n wʋn wa di dũnia dõma sarɩya? La yãma sãn wʋn wa di dũnia dõma sarɩya, ya ka tã wʋn di yel-pɩg-bãna wã sarɩya?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ tõma n wʋn wa di malɛkadõma sarɩya? Lan de bɛla la, bẽm tɩ yãma ka tãna wʋn di dũnia wã yɛla sarɩya?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: La yãma sãn tara yel-bãna buuri la taaba, yãma tarɩ sẽnna la sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla la zẽ'am.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya. Bɛla, nẽra baa ayɩla ka boe yãma pʋam n tarɩ yɛm, wʋn tã'agɛ mãalɛ yel-sɛba tɩ yãma tarɩ la taaba la?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: La ka masɛ tɩ yãma n de sɔbiisi sãn tara yelle la taaba, ya tara taaba sẽnna sarɩyaditba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Yãma n tarɩ taaba sẽnna sarɩyadia zẽ'am na, la põn pa'alɛ tɩ ya tuuge mɛ zozo'e. Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ya be'em, bɩɩ zũ ya, bẽm tɩ yãma ka ɩta sugri, dee tara taaba sẽnna sarɩyadia zẽ'am?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Yãma malɩn ɩta taaba la be'em, zũura taaba, yãma n de sɔbiisi Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam na.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ tõon-be'ero dõma kãn yẽ zẽ'a Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam? Da pã'asɛ ya mẽŋa. Nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, bɩɩ sɛba n kãabrɩ baga, bɩɩ sɛba n gã la taaba pɔgba la taaba sɩrba, bɩɩ bʋra-sɛba n gã la taaba.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: bɩɩ nayigba, bɩɩ sɛba n nõŋɛ bõnɔ, bɩɩ sɛba n yũuri dãam bugra, bɩɩ sɛba n tʋʋrɩ nẽrba, bɩɩ fãarba, bãma kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Yãma nẽrba basɛba daan ãn na bɛla. La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa peege yãma tõon-be'ero mɛ botɩ ya lebge Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, masɛ a zẽ'am Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Yãma nẽrba basɛba yetɩ: «Mam tarɩ sore wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla woo mɛ.» La mam yetɩ ya tɩ dagɩ sɛla woo n tarɩ yõorɔ. Mam mẽ tarɩ sore wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla woo mɛ. La mam kãn sakɛ lebge sɛla baa ayɩla yamŋa.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Yãma yetɩ, dia boe la pʋʋrɛ ĩyã, tɩ pʋʋrɛ mẽ bɔna dia ĩyã. La de la sɩra, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sã'am bãma bayi la wuu mɛ. La nẽrsaala ĩyã ka boe yalsɩ ĩyã, a boe la Zuudãana ĩyã. Tɩ Zuudãana mẽ sɔna ĩyã la.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, ẽŋa n wʋn ken vo'oge tõma la a pãŋa.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ ya ĩyã la de la Azezi-krisi ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla? Bɛla, la ãn sõŋa tɩ nẽra dɩkɛ Krisi ĩyã zẽ'a ayɩla la sẽŋɛ ka lagɩm na pɔg-yalsɩ dõma mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la põn ka masɛ.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Ya ka mi tɩ sɛka n lagɩm a mẽŋa la pɔg-sɛka n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, ẽŋa la pɔka la lebge la ĩyã ayɩla? La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ, bʋraaga la pɔka sãn lagɩm taaba, bãma bayi lebge la ayɩla.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: La sɛka n lagɩm a mẽŋa la Zuudãana la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ a lebge la ayɩla la Zuudãana.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Zoe-ya yalsɩ. Tõon-be'e-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽrsaala ɩtɩ la ka boe a ĩyã pʋam. La tõon-be'e-sɛka n de yalsɩ la boe la nẽrsaala ĩyã pʋam.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Ya ka mi tɩ ya ĩyã la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa deego? Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bɔ ya la boe ya pʋam mɛ, ya ka soe ya mẽŋa.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Nawẽnnɛ da ya la da'aga n de toogo, bɛla, dɩkɛ-ya ya ĩyã, nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ tõma de la Krisi tõntõnɩba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ a yɛla n daan sʋgɛ ẽŋɛ tõma nu'usum tɩ tɩ tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Ba sãn dɩkɛ tõonɛ ẽŋɛ nẽra nu'usum tɩ a bɩsra, ba boorɩ tɩ a dɛna la sɩra dãana.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: La sãn dɛna mam, yãma bɩɩ dũnia wã sarɩyaditba sãn tɔgra mam yelle se'em, la ka ɩtɩ mam se'ere. Mam mẽ ka diti m mẽŋa sarɩya.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: Mam mi m sũure pʋam tɩ mam ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n ka ãn sõŋa, la, la dagɩ bɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ mam masɛ ya. La de la Zuudãana n diti mam sarɩya.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Bɛla, da dẽŋɛ-ya di nẽra sarɩya dee tɩ Zuudãana la nãn ka leme. A sãn wa leme, a wʋn dɩkɛ sɛla n sʋgɛ lika pʋam yese peelem pʋam, dee botɩ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽrsaalba tẽ'esrɩ ba sũure pʋam puke. Wakat-ẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn pẽgɛ nẽra woo wʋ lan masɛ tɩ a pẽgɛ ẽ se'em.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam n dɩkɛ m mẽŋa la Apolɔsɩ makɛ bɛla wuu la, la de la yãma ĩyã, tɩ tõma n ẽŋɛ se'em na wʋn dɛna zãsŋɔ bɔ ya. Tɩ ya «da ẽŋɛ yõsɛ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.» Bɛla, ya nẽra nẽra da zẽkɛ a mẽŋa wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ la nẽra ayɩla dee pɔ'ɔra ayẽma.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Bẽm soe tɩ fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn gãnnɩ fʋ taaba? Bẽm tɩ fõn tarɩ tɩ la dagna Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ fɔ? La fʋ sãn to'oge, wãne tɩ fʋ pa'ala fʋ mẽŋa wʋ fʋ ka to'oge mɛ na?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ yãma põn tara Nawẽnnɛ bõntarsõm na wuu tɩ la seke mɛ. La yãma lebge la na'adõma dee tɩ tõma ka pa'asɛ. Mam boorɩ nɩ tɩ ya sɩrɩ dɛna la na'adõma, tɩ tõma mẽ po di na'am na.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tõma sɛba n de a Tõntõnɩba la, tɩ tɩ bɔna la nẽrba la wuu poorʋm, ãna wʋ nẽrba tɩ ba di ba sarɩya yetɩ ba kʋ ba, tɩ dũnia dõma wuu bɩsra dee la'ara, nẽrsaalba, la malɛkadõma wuu.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Tõma ãn wʋ zɔllɔ Krisi ĩyã, la yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ yãma de la yɛm dõma Krisi doosgo pʋam. Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma ka tarɩ pãŋa, la yãma tarɩ pãŋa. Nẽrba pɔ'ɔrɩ tõma mɛ, la ba nã'asrɩ yãma mɛ.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Kɔm la koyũuro tarɩ tõma mɛ, tɩ tõma ka tara fu-sõma, tɩ ba nãmsra tõma, tɩ tõma ɛɛra yɛ'ɛna halɩ wa paam wakat-ẽna.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: La tõma tõnnɩ la tɩ yõ'ogɔ pãŋa nãmsra yẽta tɩ nõorɛ dia. Ba sãn tʋ tõma, tõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra ba. Ba sãn nãmsra tõma, tõma wĩbri mɛ.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Ba sãn tɔgɛ tõma yetɔg-be'ero, tõma lebsrɩ la yetɔg-sõma. Dũnia dõma bɩsrɩ tõma, tɩ tõma ãna wʋ sɔgrɔ la, ka tara võorɛ ba zẽ'am, halɩ wa paam zĩna.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: Mam ka gʋlsɛ wãna bɔ ya tɩ m botɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ ya, mam yetɩ m kã'an ya mɛ wʋ m kɔma tɩ m nõŋɛ la.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Baa ya sãn tara nẽrba tʋspia tɩ ba bɩsra ya Krisi doosgo la pʋam, ya tarɩ la sɔ ayɩla mã'a. Mam n de ya sɔ Azezi-krisi doosgo la pʋam, mam n wa'am wa mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ya, tɩ ya yẽ vɔm na ĩyã.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Bɛla, mam belnɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya ɩta mam n ɩtɩ se'em na.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam tõm Atimõtɩ ya zẽ'am. A de la mam bi-nõŋrɛ n de sɩra dãana Zuudãana doosgo la pʋam. Ẽŋa n wʋn tẽegɛ ya mam n vɔa se'em Krisi doosgo la pʋam, la de la bõn-bãma tɩ mam zãsnɩ Azezi nẽrba n lagsrɩ zẽ'a woo la.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Ya nẽrba basɛba tẽ'esrɩ tɩ mam kãn leme wa'am ya zẽ'am, zẽkra ba mẽnsɩ.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: La Zuudãana la sãn bɔɔra, la kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am ya zẽ'am, la mam wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla tɩ zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma la wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ, tɩ la sãn dagna ba yetɔga la mã'a.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm dagɩ nõorɛ yetɔga, la de la pãŋa.
