eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Yãma boorɩ la bẽm? Yãma boorɩ tɩ m wa'am na kafõbga bɩɩ? Bɩɩ m wa'am na nõŋlʋm la sũ-bʋgsʋm?.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Mam yetɩ m lerge la yãma n daan gʋlsɛ soke mam se'em na: «La ãn sõŋa tɩ bʋraaga da di pɔga.».

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: La, lan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ya da ẽŋɛ yalsɩ, bʋraaga woo di a pɔga, tɩ pɔka woo mẽ ele a sɩra.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: La bʋraaga bɔ a pɔga lan masɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ sɛla la, tɩ pɔka mẽ bɔ a sɩra lan masɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ sɛla la.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Se'ere n soe la, pɔka ka soe a mẽŋa ĩyã. La de la a sɩra n soe ẽ. La bʋraaga mẽ ka soe a mẽŋa ĩyã. La de la a pɔga n soe ẽ.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Pɔka la a sɩra da zagsɛ tɩ bãma kãn gã la taaba, sãn dagna tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la nõorɛ ayɩla looge wakatɛ fẽe tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, bɛla poorʋm ya le lagɩm na taaba tɩ Asũtãana da botɩ ya ẽŋɛ be'em, ya sãn ka tã'agɛ yõkɛ ya mẽŋa.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Mam n yele ya se'em wã, la dagɩ pẽrgrɛ, la de la sore tɩ mam pa'alɛ ya.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: La sãn dɛna mam tẽ'esgɔ, nẽra woo sãn dɛna dakõorɛ wʋ mam n ãn se'em wã, la ãn sõŋa. La nẽrsaala woo tarɩ la a bɔ'a tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ. Ayɩla tarɩ la bɔ'a ayẽma, tɩ ayẽma mẽ tara bɔ'a ayẽma.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Mam yetɩ sɛba n ka tara pɔgba bɩɩ n ka tara sɩrba, la pɔkõpa yetɩ, ba sãn ken bɔna bãma bayɩla mã'a wʋ mam n ãn se'em na, la ãn sõŋa.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: La ba sãn ka tãna wʋn yõkɛ ba mẽŋa, ba di pɔgba, bɩɩ ba ele sɩrba. La ãn sõŋa tɩ ya di pɔgba, bɩɩ ya ele sɩrba, sõna yãma n zĩ tɩ a yɛmleego tara ya nãmsra.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: La sãn dɛna sɛba n tarɩ pɔgba bɩɩ n tarɩ sɩrba la, mam yetɩ ya la wãna: La, la dagɩ mam mẽŋa nõorɛ, la de la Zuudãana Azezi nõorɛ: Pɔka da basɛ a sɩra.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: La a sãn basɛ a sɩra, a zẽa, a da le bɔta bʋraasɩ, bɩɩ ẽŋa la a sɩra la mãalɛ ba yelle la le naagɛ taaba. Bʋraaga mẽ da lebse a pɔga.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: La sãn dɛna nẽrba basɛba la, mam yetɩ la wãna: La de la mam mẽŋa n yele, la dagɩ Zuudãana nõorɛ: Bʋraaga n dolɩ Azezi sore sãn tara pɔga n ka dolɩ Azezi sore, dee sakɛ tɩ a bɔna la ẽ, a da lebse ẽ.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: La pɔka n dolɩ Azezi sore sãn tara sɩra n ka dolɩ Azezi sore, dee sakɛ tɩ a bɔna la ẽ, a da basɛ ẽ.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Sɩra n ka dolɩ Azezi sore ãn sõŋa mɛ, a pɔga n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽra la ĩyã. La pɔka n ka dolɩ Azezi sore ãn sõŋa mɛ, a sɩra n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽra la ĩyã. Bɛla ba kɔma wʋn ãna sõŋa mɛ, sãn dagna bɛla, ba kɔma wʋn ãna wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: La sɛka n ka dolɩ Azezi sore la, sãn bɔɔra tɩ a basɛ sɛka n dolɩ Azezi sore la, a basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ. La sãn dɛna bɛla, la ka de pẽrgrɛ tɩ pɔka bɩɩ bʋraaga n dolɩ Azezi sore bɔna la sɛka n ka dolɩ Azezi sore. Nawẽnnɛ wi to tɩ tɩ vɔa la sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Pɔka, fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ fõn sãn wʋn tã'agɛ botɩ fʋ sɩra la yẽ fãarɛ? Bɩɩ bʋraaga, fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ fõn sãn wʋn tã'agɛ botɩ fʋ pɔga la yẽ fãarɛ?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la nẽra woo vɔa wʋ ẽn daan ãn se'em tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ na, wʋ Zuudãana n bɔ ẽ tɩ a vɔa se'em na. La de la bɛla tɩ mam pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu tɩ ba ɩta.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Nẽr-sɛka sãn wã, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ, a da ɛɛra tɩ a sʋgɛ wãaga la. Nẽr-sɛka n mẽ ka wã, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ, a da wã.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Sɛka n wã, bɩɩ sɛka n ka wã, ba wuu ka tarɩ yõorɔ. Sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ sakrɛ.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Nẽra woo ken ãna wʋ ẽn daan ãn se'em tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ na.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: La sãn dɛna bõn-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ bɔ baga la nẽnnɔ yelle, tɩ mi tɩ tɩ wuu tarɩ bãŋrɛ mɛ, wʋ yãma n yele se'em na. La bãŋrɛ botɩ nẽra zẽkra la a mẽŋa. La nõŋlʋm sõŋrɩ nẽrba mɛ.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: Fʋ daan sãn dɛna la yamŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi fo, da basɛ tɩ fʋ yɛm yɔɔra. La sore sãn wa bɔna tɩ fʋ wʋn to'oge fʋ mẽŋa, fʋ doose bilam to'oge fʋ mẽŋa.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Nẽr-sɛka sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa mi sɛla, a ken ka mina wʋ lan masɛ tɩ a mina se'em.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n de yamŋa tɩ Zuudãana la wi ẽ, a de la nẽra tɩ Zuudãana botɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa. Bɛla mẽ, nẽr-sɛka n dagɩ yamŋa tɩ Zuudãana Krisi wi ẽ, a de la Krisi yamŋa.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: La nẽra sãn nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, Nawẽnnɛ mi ẽ mɛ.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Nawẽnnɛ da ya la toogo, bɛla da lebge-ya nẽrba yamsɩ.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Bɛla, la sãn dɛna baga nẽnnɔ na yelle, tɩ mi tɩ baga de la sɛla n de zãŋa dũnia wã zuo, dee ken mina tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a n boe.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, m sɔbiisi, nẽra woo vɔa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam wʋ ẽn daan ãn se'em tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ na.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Bõndõma n boe saazuum bɩɩ tẽŋa zuo, tɩ nẽrsaalba wi'iri tɩ wẽna. Asɩra, bõndõma zozo'e n boe tɩ nẽrsaalba wi'iri tɩ wẽna, bɩɩ zuudãandõma.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Mam yetɩ m tɔgɛ la sɛba n ka tarɩ sɩrba bɩɩ pɔgba la yelle, mam ka to'oge nõorɛ Zuudãana Azezi zẽ'am. La de la mam mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ, la ya wʋn bɔ mam sɩra Zuudãana la n zoe mam ninbãalga la ĩyã.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: La tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n boe dɛna tɩ Sɔ n nãam sɛla woo, la ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ tɩ vɔa. La tõma ken mina tɩ Zuudãana ayɩla mã'a n boe, a de la Azezi-krisi, la doose la ẽŋa tɩ sɛla woo bɔna, la ken doose la ẽŋa tɩ tɩ tara vɔm.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Yel-sɛba n boe lɛɛlɛ wã ĩyã, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ nẽra woo ken ãna wʋ ẽn ãn se'em na.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: La, la dagɩ nẽra woo n mi wãna. Nẽrba basɛba malʋm baga mɛ, tɩ halɩ la zĩna ba sãn obe baga nẽnnɔ, ba ken tẽ'esra a yelle mɛ tɩ la sɩrɩ de la baga dia. La ban ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sõŋa la, ba tẽ'esrɩ ba sũure pʋam tɩ ba ẽŋɛ la be'em.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Fʋ sãn tara pɔga, da ɛɛra tɩ fʋ lebse ẽ. La fʋ sãn ka tara pɔga, da ɛɛra tɩ fʋ di pɔga.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: La dagɩ dia n wʋn botɩ tɩ lɛm Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ sãn ka di, sɛla ka pɔ'ɔrɩ tɔ, la tɩ sãn di, a ka pa'asrɩ tɩ sɛla.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: La fʋ sãn di pɔga, la dagɩ be'em. La pug-sɛka n mẽ ele sɩra, la dagɩ be'em. Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ pɔgba, bɩɩ sɩrba la wʋn yẽ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ dũnia wã pʋam, bɛla tɩ mam ka bɔɔra tɩ yel-bãma paagɛ ya.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: La guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ yan tarɩ nõorɛ dita sɛla woo la, da basɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam na tõm tõon-be'ero.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam n yetɩ ya se'em n wãna: Wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ tɔ la ka le zo'oge. Lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, la ãn sõŋa tɩ sɛba n tarɩ pɔgba la, ãna wʋ ba ka tarɩ pɔgba.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sõŋa la, sãn yẽ fõn tarɩ bãŋrɛ la n zĩ baga kãabgɔ zẽ'am ɔbra nẽnnɔ, la wʋn pã'asɛ ẽ tɩ a mẽ obe la baga nẽnnɔ.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: tɩ sɛba n kellɩ, ãna wʋ ba ka kellɩ, tɩ sɛba n ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga, ãna wʋ ba ka ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga, tɩ sɛba n da'arɩ, ãna wʋ ba ka tarɩ sɛla tɩ ba da'arɩ la.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Bɛla, fõn bãŋrɛ la wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽr-ẽŋa n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam na sã'am mɛ, a de la fʋ sɔbia tɩ Krisi ki a ĩyã.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: Tɩ sɛba n yẽtɩ dũnia bõntarsõm, ãna wʋ ba ka yẽtɩ bõntarsõm. Se'ere n soe la, dũnia wã n ãn se'em wã tollɩ mɛ.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Fʋ sãn tõm tõon-be'e-ẽna sã'am fʋ sɔbia n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sõŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ, fʋ ẽŋɛ la Krisi be'em.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ ya yɛm yɔɔra la sɛla sɛla. Sɛka n ka tarɩ pɔga dɩkrɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ wuu tõnna la Zuudãana la tõoma, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ la ãna Zuudãana la sũure yẽlʋm.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Bɛla, la sãn dɛna dia n wʋn basɛ tɩ mam sɔbia tõm tõon-be'ero, mam kãn le malɩn di ẽ, tɩ m da basɛ tɩ m sɔbia tõm tõon-be'ero.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: La sɛka n di pɔga, dũnia yɛla n pakɛ ẽ, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ la ãna a pɔga sũure yẽlʋm.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: Bɛla, a pʋtẽ'erɛ n boorɩ se'em na pʋɩ mɛ. La pɔka bɩɩ pugla n mẽ ka tarɩ sɩra dɩkrɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ wuu tõnna la Zuudãana la tõoma, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ a ĩyã, la a sɩa wʋn wa dɛna Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ. La sɛka n tarɩ sɩra, dũnia yɛla n pakɛ ẽ, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ la ãna a sɩra sũure yẽlʋm.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: La de la yãma sõŋrɛ ĩyã tɩ mam yele bɛla. Mam ka boorɩ tɩ m mõrgɛ ya dɩkɛ yelle dɔglɛ ya zuto. Mam boorɩ tɩ ya tõm na sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la ya dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa wuu bɔ Zuudãana la, tõnna a tõoma.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: La sãn dɛna sɛba n lɛgrɩ pugunto tɩ ba di la, sɛka sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa sãn basɛ a zɛba la ka di ẽ, ẽŋa ẽŋɛ ẽ na be'em, dee tɩ a yɛmleego tara ẽ, a di ẽ wʋ ẽn tẽ'esɛ se'em na, tɩ la dagɩ be'em.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: La nẽr-sɛka sãn tẽ'esɛ a pʋtẽ'erʋm sõŋa sõŋa tɩ ẽŋa kãn di ẽ, tɩ pẽrgrɛ ka bɔna mĩ, a sãn wʋn tã'agɛ yõkɛ a mẽŋa, a ẽŋɛ wʋ ẽn tẽ'esɛ se'em na, tɩ la ãn sõŋa.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n di pɔga, a ẽŋɛ sõŋa. La nẽr-sɛka n mẽ ka di pɔga, a ẽŋɛ sõŋa gãnna.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Pɔg-sɛka n ele, dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔna la a sɩra lɛ, ẽn ken vɔa wakatɛ la. La a sɩra la sãn ki, sore boe mɛ tɩ a le ele nẽr-sɛka tɩ a boorɩ, la dẽnɩ tɩ a dɛna nẽra n dolɩ Zuudãana Azezi sore.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: La mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ a sãn ka le ele sɩra, a sũure wʋn ãna yẽlʋm gãnna ẽn ele. Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'ala ya.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Mam ka soe m mẽŋa tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em mɛ bɩɩ? Mam dagɩ la Krisi Tõntõnna bɩɩ? Mam daan ka yẽ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi mɛ bɩɩ? La dagɩ tõon-sɛba tɩ mam tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra Zuudãana la n basɛ tɩ yãma bɔ ẽ sɩra?.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Baa nẽrba basɛba sãn ka sakɛ tɩ mam de Krisi Tõntõnna, yãma masɛ mɛ tɩ ya sakɛ. Yãma n dolɩ Krisi sore la, n de sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ mam de Krisi Tõntõnna.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Sɛba n zɛrgrɩ mam na, mam tɔgrɩ la wãna lɛrgra ba.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Tõma ka tarɩ sore tɩ ba bɔ tɩ dia la ko'om tɩ tõoma la ĩyã?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Tõma ka tarɩ sore wʋn di pɔgba n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore tɩ ba dɔla tɩ ɛɛra wʋ tɩ Tõntõn-taaba, la Zuudãana yɩbsɩ, la Apɩyɛɛrɩ n ɩtɩ se'em na bɩɩ?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam na Abarnabasɩ mã'a n de tɩ tõnna tɩ mẽŋa dita bɩɩ?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Ãne n de sʋdaaga dee bɩsra a mẽŋa dia yelle? Ãne n sele tɩɩsɩ dee ka dita ba biɛ? La ãne n gu'uri dũnsi dee ka dita ba ɩɩlʋm?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: Mam ka yele bɛla la nẽrsaala pʋtẽ'erɛ mã'a. Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la mẽ yele la bɛla.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: La gʋlsɛ lɔɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bɔ Amoyisi gõnnɔ la pʋam yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ-ya naafɔ n kɔɔrɩ nõorɛ yʋka.» La de la niigi yelle mã'a tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn tɔgɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Ayɛɩ, la de la tõma yelle tɩ la gʋlsɛ bɛla. Bɛla nẽr-sɛka n kɔɔrɩ tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a wʋn yẽ mɛ, tɩ sɛka n wẽ'erɩ bõnkɔɔla mẽ tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a wʋn yẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ ba wuu yẽ ba pʋrɛ.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Tõma n mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bɔ ya la, la ãn wʋ bõnbuuri tɩ tõma bʋrɛ. Bɛla, la masɛ mɛ tɩ ya looge ya bõntarsõm na sõŋɛ tõma wʋ bõnkɔɔla pʋrɛ tɩ ya bɔ tõma.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Nẽrba basɛba sãn tara nõorɛ tɩ ba to'oge yãma bõntarsõm, tõma n tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ tɩ to'oge yãma bõntarsõm gãnna. La tõma ka to'oge ya sɛla wʋ tõma n tarɩ nõorɛ se'em na, tõma sakɛ nãmsgɔ mɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tõma ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ gu Krisi kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Yãma ka mi tɩ sɛba n tõnnɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam, diti la bilam? La tɩ sɛba n kãabrɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ na, to'orɩ ba pʋrɛ la kãabgɔ zẽ'a na?.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Bɛla, Zuudãana la bɔ nõorɛ mɛ tɩ sɛba n mõolɩ kõ-yẽlga la, mẽ tɔ'ɔra ba dia bilam.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: La mam ka to'oge ya sɛla baa ayɩla wʋ mam n tarɩ nõorɛ se'em na. La mam ka gʋlsɛ bɛla mẽ yetɩ ya bɔ mam sɛla. Baa mam sãn wʋn ki sõɩ, tɩ nẽra da wa basɛ tɩ mam ka tara yẽsõm.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Mam n mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, mam ka tã wʋn wẽ m yõ'ogɔ a ĩyã. La de la pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam mõolɛ. Mam sãn ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la, mam wʋn yẽ la toogo.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: La sãn de nɩ la mam mẽŋa n looge yɛm mõola kõ-yẽlga la, mam wʋn to'oge nɩ la yɔɔrɔ. La, lan de pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam mõolɛ la, la de la tõonɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ dɔglɛ mam zuo.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Bɛla, bẽm n de mam yɔɔrɔ? La de la mam n mõolɩ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ'ɔra nẽrba zãŋa, dee ka tɔ'ɔra ba sɛla wʋ mam tarɩ nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: Mam soe m mẽŋa mɛ, mam dagɩ nẽra yamŋa. La baa bɛla, mam dɩkɛ m mẽŋa lebge la nẽrba wuu yamŋa, tɩ m tã'agɛ botɩ ba nẽrba zozo'e dɔla Krisi.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Mam sãn bɔna Zifdõma zẽ'am, mam vɔa wʋ Zifdõma la, tɩ m tã'agɛ botɩ ba doose Azezi. Amoyisi lɔɔ la ka le tara nõorɛ la mam, la mam sãn bɔna la nẽr-bãma n dolɩ lɔɔ la, mam sakrɩ lɔɔ wʋ bãma la, tɩ m botɩ sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la doose Azezi.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: La mam sãn bɔna nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ lɔɔ la zẽ'am, mam vɔa wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ lɔɔ la, tɩ m botɩ ba doose Azezi. La bɛla ka pa'alɛ tɩ mam ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la. Mam dolɩ la Krisi lɔɔ.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Mam sãn bɔna la nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam, mam vɔa wʋ bãma, tɩ m sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba da basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore. Mam ɩtɩ sɛla woo la bɛla bɔ'ɔra nẽrba buuri wuu, ɩta mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ m tã'agɛ fãagɛ ba nẽrba basɛba.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ĩyã tɩ mam ɩta bɛla wuu tɩ m wa po to'oge a pʋrɛ.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Yãma mi tɩ zʋa pʋam, zɔtba la wuu lagɩm zɔta mɛ, la ba nẽra ayɩla mã'a n wʋn zoe yãŋɛ to'oge bɔ'a. Bɛla, mõrgɛ-ya wʋ zɔtba n zotɩ se'em na, tɩ ya yãŋɛ to'oge bɔ'a Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Zɔtba wuu yõkrɩ ba mẽŋa mɛ sɛla woo pʋam tɩ ba to'oge bɔ'a n kãn yuuge dee sã'am. La tõma yõkrɩ tɩ mẽŋa tɩ tɩ wa to'oge la bɔ'a n kãn malɩn sã'am.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam ka zɔta yoo yoo, la mam ka zɛbrɩ yoo yoo wʋ nẽra n wẽ'erɩ kʋsebgo.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Mam mõrgrɩ m ĩyã mɛ tara m mẽŋa sõŋa sõŋa. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka boorɩ tɩ mam n mõolɩ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la, mam mẽŋa wa koŋe yɔɔrɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ tɩ ya tẽ'esɛ la tɩ yaabdõma n yʋʋn dolɩ Amoyisi la yelle. Ba wuu yʋʋn boe la sawatɛ la tẽŋa, la ba wuu yʋʋn doose la ko-kãtɛ la pʋam tole.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: La ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn mise ba mɛ sawatɛ la pʋam, la ko-kãtɛ la pʋam tɩ ba dɛna ayɩla la Amoyisi.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Ba wuu yʋʋn di la dia ayɩla, dee yũ ko'om ayɩla n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: Ba yʋʋn yũ la ko'om n yesrɩ tãmpĩa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba pʋam. Tãmpĩ-ẽŋa yʋʋn de la Krisi n dolɩ ba.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: La baa bɛla, ba nẽrba zozo'e yʋʋn ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm, tɩ a basɛ tɩ ba ki weego la pʋam.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Bõn-bãna wuu ẽŋɛ tɩ la pa'alɛ la tõma, tɩ tɩ da bɔɔra tɩ ẽŋɛ be'em wʋ bãma la.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Da dɔla-ya baga wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em na. La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽrba la zĩ'ire mɛ di dee yũ, dee isge dɛ'ɛna.».

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Tɩ da ɩta yalsɩ wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ ba nẽrba tʋspisyi la atã ki daarɛ ayɩla la.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Tɩ da makɛ-ya tɩ Zuudãana la bɩsɛ wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma kʋ ba la.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Tɩ da yõnna-ya la Nawẽnnɛ wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn yõm se'em, tɩ kũm malɛka kʋ ba la.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Bõn-bãma wuu yʋʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ yaabdõma tɩ la dɛna la mãmsgɔ bɔ nẽrba basɛba. La ba gʋlsɛ bɛla, tɩ la dɛna la zãsŋɔ bɔ tõma sɛba n boe wakat-ẽna tɩ dũnia lɛm ba'asgɔ wã.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa ɩtɩ sõŋa dee zɛ sõŋa, a guuse da lui.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Yel-sɛba n paarɩ ya la, la de la yel-bãma n mẽ paarɩ nẽrba basɛba. La Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana, a kãn basɛ tɩ yel-sɛka n gãnnɩ ya pãŋa paagɛ ya. La yelle pʋam, a wʋn bɔ ya pãŋa tɩ ya wĩbe, dee ẽŋɛ sore tɩ ya yese.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, yese-ya ya mẽŋa baga kãabgɔ pʋam.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: Mam tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra ya wʋ ya de la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ yɛm. Ya mẽŋa tẽ'esɛ mam n yetɩ ya sɛla la võorɛ.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Bõn-yũula la tɩ tõma ẽn dɩkɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dee yũ la, pa'alɛ tɩ tõma lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla, a zɩɩm n yese bɔ tõma la ĩyã. La borborɩ la tɩ tõma ẽn fiise pʋɩ taaba obe la, pa'alɛ tɩ tõma lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ĩyã ayɩla.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Borborɩ la n de ayɩla la, tõma mẽ de la ĩyã ayɩla, baa tõma n zo'oge la. Se'ere n soe la, tɩ wuu lagɩm ɔbra la borbor-ẽŋa ayɩla.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Tẽ'esɛ-ya Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle. Sɛba n diti di-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zuo la, ba lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Bẽm tɩ mam yɛta wãna? Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam yetɩ bagrɛ, bɩɩ di-sɛba tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ bagrɛ la, de la sɛla bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ sɛla tɩ bag-kãabrɩba kãabrɩ la, ba kãabrɩ bɔ'ɔra la kʋlkã'arsɩ, dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ. Mam ka boorɩ tɩ ya lagɩm na kʋlkã'arsɩ.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Ya ka tã wʋn yũ Zuudãana Azezi bõn-yũula, dee le yũ kʋlkã'arsɩ bõn-yũula, ya ken ka tãna wʋn di Zuudãana kũm tẽerɛ dia, dee le di kʋlkã'arsɩ dia.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Tɩ sãn ɩta bɛla, tɩ wʋn botɩ Zuudãana sũure n isge. Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma tarɩ pãŋa gãnna ẽ mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Yãma nẽrba basɛba yetɩ: «Sore boe mɛ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla woo.» Asɩra, la, la dagɩ sɛla woo n tarɩ yõorɔ. Ba yetɩ: «Sore boe mɛ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla woo.» La, la dagɩ sɛla woo n sõŋrɩ.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Nẽra nẽra da ɛɛra a mẽŋa sõŋrɛ mã'a, ɛɛra-ya taaba sõŋrɛ.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Ya tarɩ sore tɩ ya da nẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ ba koosrɩ da'asɩ pʋam obe mɛ. Da basɛ-ya tɩ la nãmsra ya pʋtẽ'era tɩ ya soke nẽnnɔ na n ze'ele zẽ'a.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Dũnia, la bõn-sɛba wuu n boe a pʋam de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ.».

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Nẽra n ka bɔ Azezi sɩra sãn wi ya tɩ ya wa'am a yire wa di, tɩ ya sakɛ sẽŋɛ, di-ya di-sɛba wuu tɩ a tarɩ wa bɔ ya la, da basɛ-ya tɩ la nãmsra ya pʋtẽ'era, tɩ ya soke nẽnnɔ na n ze'ele zẽ'a.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: La nẽra sãn yele ya yetɩ: «Di-bãna wã de la bagrɛ dia», da di-ya, sɛka n yele ya bɛla la ĩyã, tɩ la da nãmsɛ a pʋtẽ'erɛ.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: La dagɩ yãma mẽŋa pʋtẽ'era yelle tɩ mam yele, la de la nẽr-sɛka n yele ya la pʋtẽ'erɛ. La yãma mẽ tã soke mam yetɩ: «Bẽm soe tɩ nẽra ayẽma pʋtẽ'erɛ wʋn gu mam, tɩ mam kãn di sɛla tɩ mam tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ m di?.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Mam sãn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dia ĩyã dee di, bẽm tɩ nẽra wʋn zɛrgɛ mam la bõn-sɛka tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a ĩyã?».

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Lan de se'em na, ya sãn dita mɛ, bɩɩ ya yũuri mɛ, bɩɩ sɛla woo tɩ ya ɩtɩ, ɩta-ya ba wuu nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Da ẽŋɛ-ya sɛla Zifdõma, bɩɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zẽ'am tɩ la gu ba Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Ɩta-ya wʋ mam n ɩtɩ se'em na. Mam mõrgrɩ mɛ tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn nẽra woo sũure yẽlʋm sɛla woo pʋam, mam ka eerɩ m mẽŋa sõŋrɛ, mam eerɩ la nẽrba zozo'e sõŋrɛ, tɩ ba tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ɩta-ya mam n ɩtɩ se'em na, wʋ mam mẽ n ɩtɩ wʋ Krisi n ɩtɩ se'em na.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: Mam pẽgrɩ ya, yãma n tẽrɩ mam yelle sɛla woo pʋam, dee dɔla bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam pa'alɛ ya la.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: La mam boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Krisi de la nẽra woo zuudãana, tɩ sɩra mẽ dɛna a pɔga zuudãana, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɛna Krisi zuudãana.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Bɛla, bʋra-sɛka n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, bɩɩ n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee vuge a zuugo, a pɔ'ɔrɩ la a zuudãana.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: La pɔg-sɛka n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, bɩɩ n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee ka vuge a zuugo, a pɔ'ɔrɩ la a zuudãana. La ãn wʋ ẽn põn a zuugo la.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Pɔka sãn ka bɔɔra tɩ a vuge a zuugo, a yi sɛ a zomto. La tɩ mi tɩ la de la yãnnɛ bɔ pɔka tɩ a põn a zuugo, bɩɩ a sɛ a zomto. Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ a vuge a zuugo.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Bʋraaga ka de a vuge a zuugo, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nãam ẽ tɩ a wõna la a mẽŋa, dɛna nã'asgɔ bɔ ẽ. La pɔka de la nã'asgɔ bɔ bʋraaga.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka dɩkɛ pɔka kõbrɛ nãam bʋraaga, a daan dɩkɛ la bʋraaga kõbrɛ nãam pɔka.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ ka nãam bʋraaga pɔka ĩyã, la a nãam pɔka la bʋraaga ĩyã.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, la malɛkadõma ĩyã, la masɛ tɩ pɔka vugra a zuugo tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ a tarɩ nõorɛ.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: La Zuudãana doosgo la pʋam, pɔka boe la bʋraaga nu'usum, tɩ bʋraaga mẽ bɔna pɔka nu'usum.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Nawẽnnɛ daan dɩkɛ la bʋraaga nãam pɔka. Tɩ pɔgsɩ mẽ dɔgra bʋraasɩ. Bɛla wuu ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Tẽ'esɛ-ya ya mẽŋa bɩsɛ. La masɛ tɩ pɔka pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ pʋam dee ka vuge a zuugo?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Nẽrba wuu mi mɛ tɩ bʋraaga sãn basɛ a zomto tɩ ba wɔgɛ, la de la yãnnɛ.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: La pɔka sãn basɛ a zomto tɩ ba wɔgɛ, la de la pẽka bɔ ẽ. A zomto la n wɔgɛ la, de la sɛla bɔ ẽ tɩ a dɩkɛ vuge a zuugo.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: La nẽr-sɛka sãn bɔɔra tɩ a wẽ nõke'ene bõn-ẽna wã yelle, dẽnɩ tɩ a bãŋɛ tɩ tõma, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu la, ka tarɩ ɩrgɔ ayẽma.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Mam n yetɩ m yele ya se'em wã, mam ka yetɩ m pẽgɛ ya, bẽm ĩyã, ya ẽn lagsɛ taaba la, ya ka ɩtɩ sõŋa, ya ɩtɩ la be'em.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Pɔspɔsɩ, mam wõm tɩ ya sãn ẽn lagsɛ taaba tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, welgre n boe ya tẽŋasʋka. La mam sakɛ tɩ yel-sɛba tɩ ba yele la, basɛba de la sɩra.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ welgre bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka, tɩ ba bãŋɛ sɛba n sɩrɩ dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔkɛ.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Yãma ẽn lagsɛ taaba la, ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya diti la Zuudãana dia, la dagɩ bɛla.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn lagsɛ, nẽra woo dẽŋɛ ka dita la a mẽŋa dia. Tɩ sɛba kɔm dõnna, dee tɩ basɛba yũ buge.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, yãma ka tarɩ yiɛ wʋn di dee yũ bilam? Bɩɩ yãma ka nannɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la, dee ɩta nasdõma yãnnɛ? Mam wʋn yele ya tɩ bo? M pẽgɛ ya bɛla ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, mam kãn pẽgɛ ya.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Zãsŋɔ sɛka tɩ mam daan zãsʋm ya la, mam to'oge ẽ na Zuudãana la zẽ'am. La de la wãna: Yʋ'ʋŋ-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn yõkɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, a dɩkɛ la borborɩ.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ dee fiise bɔ ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam ĩyã, tɩ mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ya. Ɩta-ya wãna tẽra mam yelle.».

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Ban daan di dia kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a dɩkɛ la bõn-yũula yetɩ: «Bõn-yũul-bãna pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la nõor-paalga la nẽrsaalba mam zɩɩm n wʋn yese la ĩyã. Wakat-sɛka tɩ ya ẽn yetɩ ya yũ ba, ɩta-ya wãna tẽra mam yelle.».

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Wakat-sɛka woo tɩ ya obrɩ borborɩ wã, dee yũura bõn-yũula wã, ya pa'alɩ la Zuudãana la kũm yelle, halɩ tɩ a wʋn wa leme wa'am.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka woo n obrɩ borborɩ wã, dee yũura bõn-yũula wã, dee ka masɛ tɩ a obe dee yũ, a tõnnɩ la tõon-be'ero pɔ'ɔra Zuudãana ĩyã, la a zɩɩm na.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Bɛla, nẽra woo tẽ'esɛ bɩsɛ a sãn masɛ, deege obe borborɩ la, dee yũ bõn-yũula la.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ka nannɩ Zuudãana la ĩyã, dee obe borborɩ la, dee yũ bõn-yũula la, a eerɩ la sɩbgrɛ bɔ'ɔra a mẽŋa.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ ya pʋam, nẽrba zozo'e dɛna bã'adõma, tɩ sɛba ka tara pãŋa, tɩ basɛba ki.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Tɩ sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ mẽŋa bɩsɛ tɩ sãn masɛ, Nawẽnnɛ kãn sɩbgɛ tɔ.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: La Zuudãana la sãn sɩbgra tɔ, a pa'alɩ tɩ mɛ tɩ tɩ ɩta sõŋa, tɩ a da wa lagɩm tɩ la dũnia nẽrba di tɩ sarɩya sɩbgɛ tɔ.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, ya sãn lagsɛ taaba tɩ ya di Zuudãana kũm tẽerɛ dia la, ya gu'ura taaba.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Nẽra kɔm sãn dõnna, a di a yire dee wa'am, tɩ ya da kãn lagsɛ taaba ɛ sɩbgrɛ bɔ ya mẽŋa. La yel-sɛba n weege la, mam sãn wa wa'am, mam wʋn mãalɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, mam yetɩ m tɔgɛ ya la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a yelle. M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la bõn-ẽŋa yelle sõŋa.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Ya mi tɩ yan daan nãn ka bɔ Azezi sɩra la, ya daan taagɛ la baga n de mugro la poore dɔla, ka tãna wʋn basɛ.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam bɔɔra tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sãn bɔna la nẽra, a ka tã wʋn tɔgɛ Azezi yelle be'em. La nẽra mẽ ka tã wʋn yetɩ, Azezi de la Zuudãana, tɩ la sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ a yele.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a la de la to'oto'ore, la, la de la Sɩa ayɩla mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Tõoma la de la to'oto'ore, la, la de la Zuudãana ayɩla tɩ tɩ tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Tõoma la n tõnnɩ se'em na de la to'oto'ore, la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n tõnnɩ ba wuu nẽrba wuu pʋam.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra woo la bɔ'a nẽrba la wuu sõŋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla la bɔ'a tɩ a tɔgra yɛm yetɔga, la Sɩa ẽŋa ayɩla n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayẽma bɔ'a tɩ a tɔgra bãŋrɛ yetɔga.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: La Sɩa ẽŋa mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla bɔ'a tɩ a bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ sɩra zozo'e, dee bɔ'ɔra ayẽma pãŋa tɩ a botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Sɩa la n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla pãŋa tɩ a tãna tõnna tõon-yãlma, dee bɔ'ɔra ayẽma bɔ'a tɩ a tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla yɛm tɩ a tãna wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'a, la sɛla n ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'a bɔkɛ. Sɩa ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla bɔ'a tɩ a tãna tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, dee bɔ'ɔra ayẽma bɔ'a tɩ a tãna pa'ala yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la võorɛ.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: La de la Sɩa ẽŋa ayɩla n ɩtɩ bɛla wuu. Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra woo la bɔ'a to'oto'ore wʋ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Azezi-krisi nẽrba ãn wʋ ĩyã ayɩla n tarɩ zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore. La baa la zẽ'esɩ la n de to'oto'ore la, ba wuu de la ĩyã ayɩla.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Tõma Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n de yamsɩ, la sɛba n dagɩ yamsɩ, tõma wuu n daan mise ko'om pʋam na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ tɩ lebge la ĩyã ayɩla. Nawẽnnɛ tõm na Sɩa ayɩla bɔ tɩ wuu.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Nẽra ĩyã ka tarɩ zẽ'a ayɩla mã'a. A tarɩ la zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Nã'arɛ sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa n dagɩ nu'ugo la, ẽŋa ka po dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla, bɛla kãn tã'agɛ basɛ tɩ a kãn dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: La tʋbrɛ mẽ sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa n ka de nifo la, ẽŋa ka po dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla, bɛla kãn tã'agɛ basɛ tɩ a kãn dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Tɩ ĩyã la wuu sãn de nɩ nifo, a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm yetɔga? La a wuu sãn de nɩ tʋbrɛ, a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm yũuŋo?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ ĩyã zẽ'a woo wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em, lagɩm ba taaba.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Ba wuu sãn de nɩ la zẽ'a ayɩla mã'a, ĩyã kãn boe nɩ.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Bɛla tɩ zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore bɔna, la ba de la ĩyã ayɩla.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Bɛla nifo ka tã wʋn yele nu'ugo yetɩ, ẽŋa ka boorɩ a sõŋrɛ. La zuugo mẽ ka tã wʋn yele nãma yetɩ, ẽŋa ka boorɩ ba sõŋrɛ.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Lan pa'asɛ, ĩyã la zẽ'e-sɛba n ãn wʋ ba ka tarɩ pãŋa la, tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ tõma bɩsɛ tɩ ba ka tarɩ yõorɔ zo'oge la, tɩ bɩsrɩ la bãma yelle sõŋa sõŋa. La zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ tɩ tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba ka masɛ tɩ nẽrba yẽ ba la, bãma tɩ tɩ malgrɩ sõŋa gãnna.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: La ka de tɩ dɩkɛ sɛla pĩ zẽ'e-sõma la. Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n ẽŋɛ tɩ ĩyã la, basɛ tɩ tɩ bɩsra zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ tɩ tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba ka tarɩ yõorɔ zo'oge la yelle sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: tɩ welgre da bɔna ĩyã la pʋam. La tɩ ĩyã la zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la wʋn sõŋra taaba.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Bɛla, zẽ'a ayɩla sãn nãmsra, zẽ'esɩ la wuu mẽ nãmsrɩ mɛ. Zẽ'a ayɩla sãn yẽ nã'asgɔ, zẽ'esɩ la wuu tarɩ la sũ-yẽlga.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Ya wuu de la Krisi ĩyã la. La nẽra woo de la ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ nẽra woo a zẽ'am a nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam. Pɔspɔsɩ de la sɛba tɩ Azezi mẽŋa daan looge tõm ba la. Bʋyi dãana de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba. Bʋtã de la karẽnsãandõma, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-yãlma, la sɛba tɩ a bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm, la sɛba tɩ a looge tɩ ba sõŋra nẽrba, la sɛba n wʋn bɔna nẽrba nẽŋa, la sɛba n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: La dagɩ tɩ wuu n de sɛba tɩ Azezi mẽŋa daan looge tõm na, dagɩ tɩ wuu n de nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, dagɩ tɩ wuu n de karẽnsãandõma. La dagɩ tɩ wuu n tarɩ pãŋa tõnna tõon-yãlma.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: la dagɩ tɩ wuu n botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm, dagɩ tɩ wuu n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, dagɩ tɩ wuu n tã wʋn pa'alɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la võorɛ.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Ɛɛra-ya bɔ'a sɛba n gãnnɩ wuu. La mam wʋn pa'alɛ ya so-sɛka n gãnnɩ ba wuu.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Mam sãn tɔgra nẽrsaalba buuri wuu yetɔga, la malɛkadõma yetɔga, dee ka tara nõŋlʋm, mam ãn wʋ dẽnlɔŋɔ bɩɩ pɩrga n wẽ'erɩ zãŋa.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: La mam sãn tara bɔ'a tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee tãna wʋn bãŋɛ yɛla n sʋgɛ wuu võorɛ, dee tara mi'ilum wuu, la mam sãn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra tãna wʋn botɩ tãŋa vurge, dee ka tara nõŋlʋm, mam de la zãŋa.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: La mam sãn dɩkɛ mam n tarɩ sɛla wuu pʋɩ nasdõma, la baa mam sãn sakɛ tɩ ba yõ mam ĩyã bugum, dee ka tara nõŋlʋm, bɛla ka tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ mam.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm mã'arɩ la a mẽŋa, dee sõŋra nẽrba, a ka ɩtɩ sũure, a ka wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ, la a ka zẽkrɩ a mẽŋa.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka ɩtɩ sɛla n ka masɛ, a ka eerɩ a mẽŋa sõŋrɛ mã'a, a sũure ka isgri, nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ẽ be'em, a ka tẽrɩ ẽ a sũure pʋam.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga la tõon-be'ero, a ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga la sɩra.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ɩtɩ sugri mɛ sɛla woo pʋam. A bɔ sɩra mɛ sɛla woo pʋam, a tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ gu'ura sɛla woo tɩ a bɔ sɩra la, a tã wĩbra mɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Nõŋlʋm ka malɩn ba'asra. Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ tɩ to'osrɩ tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, la yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, la mi'ilum wʋn tole.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Tõma n mi se'em, la tõma n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la, de la fẽe.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: La wakatɛ sãn wa paagɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malgɛ sɛla woo tɩ la masɛ sõŋa sõŋa, sɛla fẽe tɩ tõma mi la wʋn tole.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Mam n daan de bia la, mam daan tɔgrɩ wʋ bia la, mam daan tẽ'esra wʋ bia la, la mam daan wõnnɩ yɛla võorɛ wʋ bia la. La mam yãŋa n bɩ la, mam basɛ biyã'atɛ mɛ.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Tõma n mi sɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, ãn wʋ tõma bɩsrɩ la bɩsga n ka yẽtɩ sõŋa pʋam. La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ wʋn yẽ sɛla woo sõŋa. Mam n mi sɛla la, nãn de la fẽe. La mam wʋn wa bãŋɛ ẽ sõŋa sõŋa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n mi mam sõŋa se'em na.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Bɛla, bõn-bãna batã n boe wakatɛ wuu: La de la bɔ sɩra, la tɩ tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la, la nõŋlʋm. La bõn-bãna batã pʋam, nõŋlʋm n gãnnɩ ba wuu.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Ɛ-ya nõŋlʋm, la ya ken ɛɛra Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a la, lan gãnnɩ, bɔ'a sɛka tɩ ya wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Sɛka n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ ka tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, a tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ. Se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka wõnnɩ a yetɔga la võorɛ, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ a tɔgra la yɛla n sʋgɛ.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: La sɛka n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, ẽŋa tɔgrɩ sõŋra la nẽrba, botɩ ba sẽnna nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo la pʋam, dee kã'ana ba, bɔ'ɔra ba bʋraanɛ.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Sɛka n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ sõŋrɩ la a mẽŋa. La sɛka n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba sõŋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Mam boorɩ nɩ tɩ ya wuu tɔgra la yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, la mam boorɩ tɩ ya tɔ'ɔsra la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba gãnna. Nẽra sãn tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ tɩ nẽra ka bɔna wʋn to'oge yetɔga la tɔgɛ a võorɛ pa'alɛ nẽrba la tɩ la sõŋɛ ba, sɛka n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la n gãnnɩ.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne sõŋɛ ya? A ka tarɩ võorɛ bɔ ya. Sɛla n wʋn sõŋɛ ya, de la mam sãn pa'alɛ ya sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa puke pa'alɛ mam, bɩɩ m botɩ ya bãŋɛ sɛla, bɩɩ m to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ ya, bɩɩ m zãsʋm ya.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: La ãn wʋ de'eŋo lɔgrɔ la: Wɩa sãn pɛɛbra bʋra bʋra, bɩɩ kõlŋɔ sãn wẽ'era bʋra bʋra, fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ wɩa la peebgo võorɛ, bɩɩ kõlŋɔ la wẽ'a võorɛ?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Benkonne sãn peebe bʋra bʋra, ka peebe zɛbrɛ peebgo, ãne n wʋn mãasʋm yese zɛbrɛ?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Bɛla mẽ, ya sãn tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋka tɩ nẽra ka wõnnɩ a võorɛ, nẽra wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ yãma n boorɩ tɩ ya yele se'em? Ya yetɔga la de la zãŋa.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Yetɔga buuri zozo'e n boe dũnia zuo, la ayɩla ka boe ka tara võorɛ.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: La mam sãn ka wõnna yetɔga, mam na sɛka n tɔgrɩ la de la sãama la taaba.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Bɛla, yãma n mõrgɛ ɛɛra Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a la, ɛɛra-ya bɔ'a sɛba n wʋn sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, sɛka n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋka, a sose Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a botɩ a yãŋɛ tɔgɛ a võorɛ.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Mam sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ la yetɔg-zãŋka, mam sɩa n pʋ'ʋsrɩ, la mam ka wõnnɩ a võorɛ la m mẽŋa yɛm.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Bɛla, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Mam wʋn pʋ'ʋsra la m sɩa, dee ken pʋ'ʋsra la m yɛm, la m yõona la m sɩa, dee ken yõona la m yɛm.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Fʋ sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ la yetɔg-zãŋka, nẽra wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne ze'ele nẽrba la n lagsɛ la pʋam yetɩ: «Amina», dee ka wõm fõn yele se'em na võorɛ?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Baa fõn pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ se'em na sãn ãna sõŋa, la ka tarɩ sõŋrɛ bɔ nẽr-ẽŋa.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, mam n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ gãnna yãma wuu la.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am, mam boorɩ tɩ m tɔgɛ la yetɔga banuu la m yɛm tɩ nẽrba wõm ba võorɛ tɩ la zãsʋm ba, sõna mam wʋn tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ tʋspia.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: M sɔbiisi, da ãna-ya wʋ kɔma n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na. La, la sãn dɛna be'em ɩrgɔ yelle, ãna-ya wʋ kɔm-pɩgsɩ n ka ɩtɩ be'em na. La ya tẽ'esgɔ pʋam, ãna-ya wʋ bõnkɛgsɩ la.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn basɛ tɩ sãama n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ mõolɛ m kõ-yẽlga bɔ m nẽrba. Baa la wãna wuu, ba kãn selse mam.».

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, yetɔg-zãŋsɩ de la sɛla n pa'alɩ sɛba n ka bɔ sɩra Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, la dagɩ sɛba n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã. La nẽra n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba la, n de sɛla n pa'alɩ sɛba n bɔ sɩra Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, la dagɩ sɛba n ka bɔ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Sɛba n bɔ sɩra la sãn lagsɛ taaba zẽ'a ayɩla, tɩ nẽra woo tɔgra la yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, tɩ sɛba n ka mi, dee ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra kẽ, ba kãn yetɩ ya zallɩ mɛ?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: La sɛba n bɔ sɩra la sãn lagsɛ taaba, tɩ nẽra woo tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, tɩ nẽra n ka mi, dee ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra kẽ, ẽn wõm sɛla woo la, wʋn basɛ tɩ a bãŋɛ a tõon-be'ero, dee pa'alɛ ẽ a tuure.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Yel-sɛba n sʋgɛ a sũure pʋam wʋn puke mɛ peelem pʋam, tɩ a wʋn ka dũma dɩkɛ a nẽŋa võglɛ tẽŋa nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sɩrɩ boe la ya lɛ.».

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Ya sãn lagsɛ taaba tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, ya nẽra ayɩla tã wʋn pʋrɛ yõonɛ, tɩ ayẽma zãsʋm, tɩ ayẽma pa'alɛ bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ ẽ, tɩ ayẽma tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋka, tɩ ayẽma tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋka la võorɛ pa'alɛ nẽrba. Ɩta-ya bɛla wuu tɩ la sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: La sãn dɛna yetɔg-zãŋsɩ tɩ ya yetɩ ya tɔgɛ, nẽrba bayi bɩɩ nẽrba batã mã'a tɔgɛ ayɩla ayɩla, tɩ nẽra ayɩla tɔgɛ ba võorɛ pa'alɛ.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: La nẽra sãn ka bɔna wʋn tã'agɛ tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la võorɛ pa'alɛ, sɛba n yetɩ ba tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la sĩna da tɔgɛ. Ba tɔgɛ ba sũure pʋam bɔ ba mẽŋa, la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Sɛba n de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la, bayi bɩɩ batã tɔgɛ. Tɩ sɛba n weege la bɩsɛ ba yetɔga la sãn masɛ bɩɩ ka masɛ.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: La nẽra ayɩla sãn tɔgra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa puke sɛla pa'alɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ la zẽ'am, nẽr-sɛka n dẽŋɛ tɔgra la go'oge.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Ya wuu tã wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba mɛ, la ya tɔgra ayɩla ayɩla, tɩ la wʋn zãsʋm nẽrba wuu, dee bɔ ba bʋraanɛ.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tɔ'ɔsra yetɔga bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la tã wʋn yõkɛ ba mẽŋa mɛ.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ sɛla woo dɔla la sore la sũ-mã'asʋm, a ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ tõnna yooro yooro.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: Pɔgsɩ sĩna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am, wʋ ba ɩtɩ se'em zẽ'esɩ wuu la. Sore ka boe tɩ ba darɛ tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am. La ba sakra nõorɛ wʋ Zifdõma lɔɔ n yele se'em na.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Sɛla sãn bɔna tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ, ba soke ba sɩrba ba yiɛ. La ka narɩ tɩ pɔgsɩ tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ la de la yãma zẽ'am tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la pɔsɛ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yãma mã'a n to'oge yetɔga la?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Sɛka n tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, bɩɩ ẽŋa de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ẽ lɛ, a sakɛ tɩ sɛla tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya wã de la Zuudãana la nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: La nẽra sãn ka selse yel-ẽna, yãma mẽ da selse ẽ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, mõrgɛ-ya tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee da gu-ya nẽrba tɩ ba da tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: La ɩta-ya sɛla woo tɩ la ãna sõŋa, dee dɔla sore.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: M sɔbiisi, lɛɛlɛ wã mam boorɩ tɩ m tẽegɛ ya la kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam daan mõolɛ bɔ ya tɩ ya bɔ sɩra zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ la yelle.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Ya sãn gʋrɛ ẽ kãŋkãŋɛ wʋ mam n mõolɛ ya se'em na, ya wʋn yẽ fãarɛ. La sãn dagna bɛla, yan bɔ sɩra la de la zãŋa.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Zãsŋɔ sɛka n gãnnɩ tɩ mam daan to'oge n bɛla tɩ mam dẽŋɛ zãsʋm ya: Krisi ki la tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Tɩ ba daan laagɛ ẽ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a vo'oge dabsa atã daarɛ, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: La a daan dẽŋɛ dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, dee yãŋa dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi n gãnnɩ kɔbsnuu wakatɛ ayɩla. Ba nẽrba basɛba zozo'e ken vɔa mɛ, la basɛba ki mɛ.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Azakɩ, dee yãŋa dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Tõntõnɩba la wuu.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Bɛla wuu poorʋm, a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ la mam n ãn wʋ bia tɩ ba dɔgɛ tɩ a wakatɛ ka paagɛ la.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Mam de la pɩka Tõntõnɩba la wuu zẽ'am, dee ka nara tɩ ba wi'ira mam tɩ Tõntõnna, mam n daan nãmsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la ĩyã.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã tɩ mam dɛna mam n de se'em lɛɛlɛ wã. Yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ mam na dagɩ zãŋa. Mam tõm gãnna Tõntõnɩba basɛba la wuu mɛ. La, la dagɩ mam mẽŋa n tõnnɩ bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã tɩ mam yãŋɛ tõnna bɛla.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Bɛla, la sãn dɛna la mam n mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la, bɩɩ bãma, tɩ wuu mõolɛ la kõ-yẽlga sɛka tɩ mam tẽegɛ ya a yelle la, sɛka tɩ ya daan sakɛ la.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Tõma n mõolɩ tɩ Krisi ki mɛ dee vo'oge la, wãne tɩ ya nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, sɛba n ki la kãn vo'oge?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: La sãn dɛna sɩra tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ki la kãn vo'oge, bɛla Krisi yi ka vo'oge.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: La Krisi sãn ka vo'oge, kõ-yẽlga la tɩ tõma mõolɩ la de la zãŋa, tɩ yãma bɔ sɩra la mẽ dɛna zãŋa.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, tõma yi parnɩ mɛ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, tõma n yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ Krisi vo'oge mɛ, dee tɩ kũm kãn vo'oge la ĩyã.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Sɛba n ki la sãn kãn vo'oge, bɛla, Krisi yi ka vo'oge.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: La Krisi sãn ka vo'oge, yan bɔ sɩra la de la zãŋa, la ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ ken bɔna la ya zuto.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: La sɛba n bɔ Krisi sɩra dee ki la mẽ yi bɔɩ mɛ.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la Krisi la sãn dɛna la tõma n vɔa dũnia wã zuo mã'a ĩyã, tõma n yi de ninbãal-dõma gãnna nẽrsaalba wuu.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: La, la dagɩ bɛla, Krisi ki dee vo'oge mɛ, ẽŋa n de pɔspɔsɩ dãana n vo'oge, pa'alɛ tɩ nẽrba basɛba n ki la mẽ wʋn vo'oge.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: La de la nẽra ayɩla ĩyã tɩ nẽrba kiira, bɛla, la mẽ de la nẽra ayɩla ĩyã tɩ kũm wʋn vo'oge.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Nẽrsaalba wuu de la Adam yɩɩsɩ, bɛla tɩ ba kiira. Bɛla tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n mẽ de Krisi nẽrba wʋn vo'oge.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: La nẽra woo tarɩ la a wakatɛ, pɔspɔsɩ de la Krisi n vo'oge, la a lemŋo daarɛ, sɛba n de a nẽrba la wʋn vo'oge mɛ.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, Krisi wʋn sã'am sɛba wuu n soe ke'eŋo, la na'am, la pãŋa wuu, dee yãŋa dɩkɛ so'olʋm na lebse bɔ a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, dee tɩ sɛla woo ba'asɛ.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ Krisi di na'am halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a bɛ'ɛba wuu bɔna a nãma tẽŋa.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Kũm n de bɛ-sɛka tɩ a wʋn wa sã'am poorʋm.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ la a nãma tẽŋa.» Lan yetɩ Krisi n soe sɛla woo la, la ka tã tɩ a sɔna Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ a sɔna sɛla woo la.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ sãn dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ Krisi nu'usum, ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn dɩkɛ a mẽŋa ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɛna nẽŋadãana sɔna sɛla woo.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Sɛba n ki la sãn kãn malɩn vo'oge, nẽr-sɛba n botɩ ba misra ba ko'om pʋam sɛba n ki ĩyã la, wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Kũm sãn kãn malɩn vo'oge, bẽm tɩ ba ɩta bɛla?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: La bẽm tɩ tõma põsra tɩ yõorɛ wakatɛ wuu?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam põsrɩ m yõorɛ mɛ daarɛ woo, mam n yetɩ ya se'em na de la sɩra. La de la sɩra wʋ mam n ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga ya yelle yãma n dolɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã se'em na.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: La sãn dɛna la dũnia zuo vɔm mã'a ĩyã, mam n daan yẽ yɛla Efɛɛzɩ tẽŋa wʋ mam zɛbrɩ la we-dũnsi la, a yõorɔ de la bẽm? La sãn dɛna tɩ kũm kãn vo'oge, tɩ wʋn yele nɩ wʋ nẽrba basɛba n yetɩ se'em na: «Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ dita dee yũura, tɩ beere tɩ wʋn ki mɛ.».

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Da pã'asɛ-ya ya mẽŋa. Fʋ sãn dɔla nẽr-be'ego, fʋ wʋn lebge la nẽr-be'ego.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Basɛ-ya tẽ'esɛ yooro, dee da le tõnna tõon-be'ero. Mam yetɩ ya tɩ la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya: Ya nẽrba basɛba ka mi Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: La nẽr-sɛka wʋn soke yetɩ: «Sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge la wãne wãne? Ba wʋn wa tara la ĩyã bẽm buuri?».

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Sokre ẽna de la gɔɔn-sokre! Fõn ka mi tɩ fʋ sãn bʋrɛ sɛla biire, a yɛm n wʋn bule yese vɔa, dee tɩ a pɔkɔ põ'ogɛ?.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: La sãn dɛna la ke-biire bɩɩ sɛla ayẽma biire tɩ fʋ dɩkɛ bʋrɛ, a de la biire mã'a. La a sãn yese, a ka le ãna wʋ sɛka tɩ fʋ bʋrɛ la.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Nawẽnnɛ botɩ bõnbuuri la yɛsra ãna wʋ ẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ ba ãna se'em na. Bõnbuuri woo n yesrɩ ãna se'em de la to'oto'ore.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Bõn-vɔɔsɩ mẽ ka tarɩ ĩyã ayɩla. Nẽrsaalba ĩyã de la to'ore, dũnsi ĩyã de la to'ore, niinto ĩyã de la to'ore, tɩ zũma ĩyã mẽ dɛna to'ore.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum tarɩ la ba ĩyã, tɩ sɛba n boe tẽŋa zuo mẽ tara ba ĩyã, la ba sõŋa dagɩ ayɩla.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Wẽntulle peelem boe la a to'ore, tɩ wõrga peelem bɔna a to'ore, tɩ wõrbiɛ peelem mẽ bɔna ba to'ore. La wõrbiɛ la peelem mẽ le bɔna la to'oto'ore.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Kũm vo'ore la mẽ wʋn wa ãna la bɛla. Nẽra sãn ki tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ, a ĩyã põ'orɩ mɛ. La Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa vo'oge ẽ, a kãn le põ'ogɛ.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Ba sãn laagɛ kũm, a lɔrɛ mɛ dee dɛna gille. La a sãn vo'oge, a ãn na sõŋa, dee tara pãŋa.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Ban laagɛ ẽ na, a tarɩ la dũnia zuo ĩyã. La Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa vo'oge ẽ, a tarɩ la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ẽ. Dũnia zuo ĩyã boe mɛ, la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ mẽ boe mɛ.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Adam n de pɔspɔsɩ nẽra la, Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ vɔm.» La Krisi tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ poorʋm Adam na, tarɩ la Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: La dagɩ ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ la tɩ tɩ to'orɩ yiɛn. Tɩ to'orɩ la dũnia wã ĩyã yiɛn, dee yãŋa wʋn to'oge ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn dɩkɛ la tẽŋa tẽntõnnɔ nãam pɔspɔsɩ nẽra la. La poorʋm nẽra la ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Dũnia nẽrba wuu ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn dɩkɛ tẽŋa tẽntõnnɔ nãam na. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire nẽrba mẽ ãna wʋ nẽr-sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am na.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Bɛla, tõma n ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ tẽntõnnɔ nãam na, tɩ mẽ wʋn wa ãna wʋ nẽr-sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am na.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: M sɔbiisi, bɛla tɩ mam gʋlsɛ yele ya tɩ, nẽrsaala ĩyã wã kãn tã'agɛ kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam. Ĩyã wã n põ'orɩ la kãn tã'agɛ yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Selse-ya tɩ m yele ya yelle n sʋgɛ. Tõma nẽrsaalba wuu kãn ki dee tɩ dũnia ba'asɛ, la tɩ wuu ĩisi wʋn teege mɛ.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ, benkonne n wʋn peebe, tɩ tɩ wuu teege tɔtɔ nini kãbsɩgɔ pʋam. Benkonne na sãn peebe, sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge la ĩyã n kãn malɩn ki, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn teege tõma ĩyã mɛ.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrsaala ĩyã wã n põ'orɩ la, lebge ĩyã n ka põ'orɩ, tɩ ĩyã n kiiri la, lebge ĩyã n ka kiiri.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: La ĩyã n põ'orɩ la sãn lebge ĩyã n ka põ'orɩ, tɩ ĩyã n kiiri la lebge ĩyã n ka kiiri, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na wʋn ẽŋɛ. La gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ yãŋɛ kũm, tɩ kũm ka le bɔna.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Kũm, fʋ pãŋa boe la bɛ? Fʋ toogo boe la bɛ?».

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: La de la tõon-be'ero n botɩ kũm tara pãŋa kʋʋra nẽrba. Tɩ lɔɔ botɩ tõon-be'ero yẽta pãŋa.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, ẽŋa n botɩ tõma yãŋɛ tõon-be'ero, la kũm, Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã la.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, mõrgɛ-ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ. Tõnna-ya Zuudãana tõoma la wakatɛ wuu da basɛ, yãma n mi tɩ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ ya nãmsrɩ Zuudãana la ĩyã dagɩ zãŋa la.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, mam boorɩ tɩ m tɔgɛ la ligri la tɩ ya lagsrɩ tɩ ya sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la yelle. Doose-ya pa'alg-sɛka tɩ mam pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la n boe Galatɩ so'olʋm na.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Lasrɩ daarɛ woo, nẽra woo looge lig-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ la bĩŋe, da zɛ-ya gu'ura tɩ mam sãn wa paam, tɩ ya yãŋa lagsɛ.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Tɩ mam sãn wa paam, mam wʋn gʋlsɛ la gõŋɔ tõm nẽr-sɛba tɩ ya mẽŋa wʋn looge la, tɩ ba tarɩ bɔ'a la sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: La, la sãn le nara tɩ mam mẽŋa sẽŋɛ, bãma wʋn doose la mam tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: Mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am, la mam wʋn doose la Masedʋanɩ so'olʋm wa'am.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: La mam sãn wa'am, mam wʋn yuuge ya zẽ'am fẽe, bɩɩ tã tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la ya tɩ ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ wa tole, tɩ ya sõŋɛ ma tɩ m sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn sẽŋɛ la.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ m wen doose bɩsɛ ya dee tole, mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ mam wʋn bɔna ya zẽ'am yuuge, Zuudãana la sãn bɔ mam sore.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: La mam wʋn weege bɔna la Efɛɛzɩ tẽŋa wã halɩ ka paagɛ Pãntekɔtɩ kibsa daarɛ.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yo'oge la sore bɔ mam tɩ m tõm a tõoma zozo'e, baa bɛ'ɛba n zo'oge la.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
