local: Atimõtɩ sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am, ya to'oge ẽ sõŋa, dee ẽŋɛ-ya yan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ a bʋraanɛ da bo'oge. Se'ere n soe la, a tõnnɩ Zuudãana tõoma la wʋ mam n tõnnɩ se'em na.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Nẽra nẽra da pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ, sõŋɛ-ya ẽ tɩ a leme wa'am mam zẽ'am la sũ-mã'asʋm. Mam, la sɔbiisi la gu'uri ẽ mɛ.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔbia Apolɔsɩ, mam yele ẽ mɛ nõorɛ faa tɩ a doose la sɔbiisi basɛba la wa'am wa bɩsɛ ya. La a ka boorɩ tɩ a wa'am lɛɛlɛ wã. A sãn wa yẽ nẽŋa, a wʋn wa'am.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo la pʋam. Keŋe ya mẽŋa, tara-ya bʋraanɛ.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Ɩta-ya sɛla woo la nõŋlʋm.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: M sɔbiisi, ya mi Astefanasɩ la a yir-dõma yelle, bãma n dẽŋɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra Akayi so'olʋm pʋam. Ba tõm mɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Mam belnɩ ya mɛ.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: tɩ ya nanna nẽr-bãma, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n tõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma wʋ bãma la.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ Astefanasɩ la Afɔrtunatusi la Akayikusi n wa'am na. Se'ere n soe la, ba sõŋɛ mam wʋ yãma boe nɩ la mam zẽ'am na.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Ba keŋe mam, la yãma sũure mɛ. Pẽgra-ya nẽr-bãma buuri.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Azi so'olʋm pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. Akila, la Aprisili, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sɛba n lagsrɩ ba yire la pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya sõŋa Zuudãana Azezi doosgo pʋam.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Sɔbiisi la wuu pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Mam Apolɩ n gʋlsɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ wã la m mẽŋa nu'ugo, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ka nõŋɛ Zuudãana la, kã'a-be'ego wʋn bɔna a dãana zuo. Maranata.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: Mam nõŋɛ ya wuu mɛ Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Mam Apolɩ n de Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, la tɩ sɔbia Atimõtɩ n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu n boe Korẽntɩ, la Akayi so'olʋm wuu pʋam na.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Tɩ pẽgɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la. Ẽŋa n de Sɔ n zotɩ ninbãalga, la ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ n sõŋrɩ nẽrba bɔ'ɔra ba bʋraanɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma bʋraanɛ tɩ tooro wuu pʋam, tɩ tõma n yẽtɩ bʋraanɛ na, tõma mẽ wʋn yãŋɛ sõŋra nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe tooro buuri wuu pʋam bɔ ba bʋraanɛ.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Tõma n yẽtɩ nãmsgɔ zo'oge wʋ Krisi la, bɛla, tõma mẽ wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ zo'oge Krisi ĩyã.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Tõma sãn bɔna toogo pʋam, la de la tɩ yãma wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ, la fãarɛ. La tõma sãn yẽta bʋraanɛ, la de la tɩ yãma wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ tɩ la sõŋɛ ya tɩ yãma mẽ wʋn yãŋɛ wĩbra nãmsgɔ pʋam wʋ tõma n mẽ wĩbri nãmsgɔ pʋam na.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Tõma tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la yãma mɛ, mina sõŋa sõŋa tɩ yãma n po nãmsra la tõma la, yãma mẽ wʋn po yẽ bʋraanɛ la tõma.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Tɩ sɔbiisi, tõma boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la tooro sɛba n paagɛ tõma Azi so'olʋm pʋam na. Tooro la daan nãmsɛ tõma mɛ zo'oge gãŋɛ tõma pãŋa, tɩ tõma daan põn ka le tara pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ tõma wʋn vɔa.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Tõma daan kɔ'ɔgɛ tɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ yetɩ tõma vɔm tɛka n bɛla. La bɛla paagɛ tõma tɩ tɩ da te'ege la tɩ mẽŋa, la tɩ te'ege Nawẽnnɛ n vo'orɩ kũm na.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: La de la ẽŋa n fãagɛ tõma kũm ẽŋa pʋam, la a wʋn ken fãagɛ tõma mɛ. Asɩra, tõma tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la ẽ lɛ mɛ tɩ a wʋn le fãagɛ tõma mɛ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: Yãma n sõŋrɩ tõma pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra tõma la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn lerge nẽrba pãɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ sõŋɛ tõma, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ tõma ĩyã.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Sɛla n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma sũ-yẽlga de la tõma mi tɩ sũa pʋam tɩ tõma yel-ɩrɛ n ãn se'em dũnia wã pʋam, la, lan gãnnɩ wuu yãma zẽ'am, de la pʋpeelem, la sɩra n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. La dagɩ la nẽrsaala yɛm tɩ tõma ɩtɩ bɛla, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Tõma n gʋlsrɩ yãma sɛla wã, la dagɩ sɛla ayẽma, la de la yãma n karẽŋrɩ sɛla wõnna a võorɛ la. La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ ya wʋn wõm a wuu võorɛ mɛ.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: wʋ yãma n dẽŋɛ bãŋɛ a võorɛ fẽe, tɩ Zuudãana lemŋo daarɛ tõma n de yãma zu-zẽkrɛ, tɩ yãma mẽ dɛna tõma zu-zẽkrɛ la.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'e-ẽŋa la, mam daan boorɩ mɛ tɩ m dẽŋɛ wa'am yãma zẽ'am, tɩ ya wʋn to'oge yel-sõnnɛ nõorɛ bʋyi.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: Mam daan boorɩ la m dẽŋɛ wa'am yãma zẽ'am, yãŋa ze'ele yãma zẽ'am tole Masedʋanɩ so'olʋm, le ze'ele bilam leme wa'am yãma zẽ'am, tɩ ya sõŋɛ mam tɩ m sẽŋɛ Zude so'olʋm.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Mam n looge yɛm tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam de la nõgõla dãana bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam n looge yɛm se'em na de la nẽrsaala yɛm loore tɩ mam tãna wʋn yetɩ «ẽe», le yetɩ «ayɛɩ»?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n de mam sɩra dãana, tɩ mam n tɔgɛ se'em bɔ yãma la dagɩ «ẽe», la «ayɛɩ».

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Azezi-krisi n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa tɩ mam, la Atimõtɩ, la Asilvẽ daan mõolɛ a kõa yãma zẽ'am na, dagɩ «ẽe», la «ayɛɩ», ẽŋa zẽ'am la de la «ẽe» wakatɛ wuu.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu la, Azezi-krisi n yetɩ «ẽe», ẽŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ. Bɛla, la de la Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ tõma yɛta tɩ: «Amina!» tɩ la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ tõma la yãma lagɩm zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ Krisi doosgo pʋam, la ẽŋa n looge to, welge tɩ to'ore.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: Ẽŋa n dãalɛ tõma pa'alɩ tɩ ẽŋa n soe to, bɔ tõma a Sɩa n de sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ sɛla wuu tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ tɩ la.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n de mam kaset-dãana, mam sãn parna, a botɩ m ki. La de la, la wʋn sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya sũure da sã'am ĩyã n basɛ tɩ mam ka le wa'am Korẽntɩ.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: La dagɩ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ mõrgɛ ya mɛ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam. Tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na ya lɛ mɛ tõm tɩ ya yẽ sũ-yẽlga. Se'ere n soe la, yãma ze mɛ kãŋkãŋɛ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Bɛla, mam looge la yɛm yetɩ mam kãn le wa'am ya zẽ'am, tɩ m da botɩ ya sũure le sã'am.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Mam sãn botɩ yãma sũure sã'am, ãne n wʋn bɔ mam sũ-yẽlga, sãn dagna yãma sɛba tɩ mam wʋn botɩ nɩ ya sũure sã'am na?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam daan gʋlsɛ bɔ ya, mam ka boorɩ tɩ m sãn wa wa'am ya zẽ'am, m tara sũ-sã'aŋɔ la yãma n de sɛba n wʋn bɔ mam sũ-yẽlga la. Mam mi sɩra sɩra tɩ mam sãn tara sũ-yẽlga, yãma mẽ tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: La daan de la toogo, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la nintãm pʋam, tɩ mam daan gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ la bɔ ya. La dagɩ tɩ mam daan boorɩ tɩ m botɩ ya sũure n sã'am, la de la tɩ ya bãŋɛ mam n nõŋɛ ya zo'oge paagɛ se'em.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Nẽra sãn botɩ sũ-sã'aŋɔ wa'am, la dagɩ mam mã'a sũure tɩ a sã'am, la de la yãma wuu sũure tɩ a sã'am. La tɩ m da tɔgɛ tole, m yetɩ a sã'am na ya nẽrba zozo'e sũure.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ yãma nẽrba zozo'e bɔ nẽr-ẽŋa dãana la, la seke mɛ.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la ya bɔ ẽ sugri, keŋe a sũure, tɩ la da kẽ'esɛ ẽ sũ-sã'aŋɔ zozo'e pʋam tɩ a sã'am.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: Bɛla, mam kã'anɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sɛla n wʋn botɩ a bãŋɛ tɩ yãma nõŋɛ ẽ mɛ.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Mam n daan gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ la bɔ yãma la, mam daan boorɩ tɩ m bɩsɛ la ya sãn sakra mam sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Ya sãn bɔ nẽra sugri, mam mẽ bɔ'ɔrɩ a dãana sugri mɛ. La sãn dɛna mam, mam bɔ sugri mɛ, la bõn-sɛka sãn bɔna dɛna mam bɔ sugri, mam ɩtɩ bɛla la yãma ĩyã Krisi nifum.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: tɩ la da botɩ Asũtãana yãŋɛ tɔ. Se'ere n soe la, tõma mi ẽn boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em mɛ.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Mam n daan paagɛ Trʋasɩ tɩ m mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la, mam daan yẽ tɩ Zuudãana la yo'oge sore bɔ mam mɛ.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: la mam pʋtẽ'erɛ daan ka gã zẽ'a ayɩla, mam n ka yẽ m sɔbia Atiti bilam na ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ mam daan keese ba, dee tole Masedʋanɩ.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ tõma dɔla Krisi poore daarɛ woo a tã'arɛ la pʋam, dee botɩ tõma ĩyã nẽrba bãŋra Krisi zẽ'a woo, wʋ yũuŋ-yẽlga n taagɛ zẽ'a wuu la.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Asɩra, tõma ãn wʋ yũuŋ-yẽlga bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, tõma n mõolɩ Krisi kõa bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba n yẽtɩ fãarɛ, la nẽr-sɛba n sã'anɩ la.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: La sãn dɛna la nẽrba basɛba, la de la kũm yũuŋo n sẽm na kũm, la nẽrba basɛba zẽ'am, la de la vɔm yũuŋo n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm. Ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ tõm tõon-ẽŋa?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Tõma ka ãn wʋ nẽrba basɛba zozo'e n dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la lɛɛbra ɛɛra yõorɔ la. Ayɛɩ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm tõma, tɩ tõma pelge tɩ pʋʋrɛ tɔgra a nẽŋam Krisi doosgo pʋam.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ pẽgɛ la tɩ mẽŋa bɩɩ? Bɩɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ bɔ ya la gõnnɔ n pa'alɩ tõma n ãn sõŋa se'em, bɩɩ tõma boorɩ la ya gʋlsɛ gõnnɔ n pa'alɩ tõma n ãn se'em bɔ tɔ, wʋ nẽrba basɛba n ɩtɩ se'em na?.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Yãma mẽŋa n de tõma gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ tõma sũure pʋam, tɩ nẽrba wuu mina dee karẽŋra ẽ.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: La de la vẽelga tɩ yãma de la Krisi gõŋɔ tɩ a doose tõma tõoma pʋam gʋlsɛ. La dagɩ gʋlsgɔ ko'om n gʋlsɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa la Sɩa n gʋlsɛ. La ka gʋlsɛ kug-palagsɩ zuto, la gʋlsɛ la nẽrsaalba sũa pʋam.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Tõma n tɔgrɩ bɛla la, tõma mi sõŋa sõŋa tɩ bõn-ẽŋa de la sɩra Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: La ka boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ tõma tã wʋn tõm tõon-ẽŋa la tõma mẽŋa pãŋa, tõma pãŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: La de la ẽŋa n botɩ tõma tã'agɛ dɛna a nõor-bĩŋre paalga la tõntõnɩba. La dagɩ lɔɔ n gʋlsɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa. Lan de se'em na, lɔɔ n gʋlsɛ la sẽm na kũm, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ la vɔm.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Lɔɔ la yʋʋn gʋlsɛ la kug-palagsɩ zuto, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ wakatɛ ẽŋa, tɩ Amoyisi nẽŋa nẽegɛ yẽgra, tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma ka tãna wʋn bɩsɛ ẽ. La na'am pãŋa la yʋʋn tollɩ mɛ. Lɔɔ la n sẽm na kũm na n yʋʋn puke la na'am pãŋa bɛla la.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n tõnnɩ tõon-sɛka la, wʋn tara na'am pãŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ zozo'e.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Tõon-sɛka n sẽm na sɩbgrɛ la n yʋʋn tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, bɛla, tõon-sɛka n botɩ nẽrba masra Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na tarɩ na'am pãŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ zozo'e.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Tõma tã wʋn põn yetɩ na'am pãn-sɛka n yʋʋn boe la lebge la zãŋa, se'ere n soe la, sɛka n boe lɛɛlɛ wã gãnnɩ ẽ mɛ zozo'e.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Bɛla, sɛla n yʋʋn tollɩ la n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, sɛla n boe wakatɛ wuu la n tarɩ na'am pãŋa zo'oge gãnna bɛla.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'e-ẽŋa gu'ura la ĩyã, tõma tarɩ bʋraanɛ zo'oge mɛ.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Tõma ka ɩtɩ wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn dɩkɛ tãnnɛ pĩ a nẽŋa tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma da yẽ na'am pãŋa n nẽegɛ a nẽŋa n tollɩ la.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: La Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la yɛm yʋʋn ka like. Halɩ wakat-ẽŋa wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã, ba sãn karẽŋra nõor-bĩŋre kɛka gõŋɔ la, tãnnɛ na ken pĩ ba nẽnsɩ mɛ, tɩ ba ka bɔkra a võorɛ. Tãn-ẽŋa ka pilgri, sãn dagna Krisi peelem pʋam mã'a.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Asɩra, wakat-ẽŋa tarɩ wa paam zĩna, ba sãn karẽŋra Amoyisi gõnnɔ la, tãnnɛ na ken pĩ ba yɛm mɛ.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: La nẽra sãn teege yɛm lebe Zuudãana la zẽ'am, tãnnɛ na pilgri mɛ.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Ban yetɩ Zuudãana la, la de la Sɩa la. La Zuudãana Sɩa la n boe zẽ'a la, to'og-m-mẽŋa boe la bilam.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Tõma wuu n dolɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, tãnnɛ na pilge mɛ, tɩ tõma nẽnsɩ yẽgra la Zuudãana na'am pãŋa. Tɩ Zuudãana Sɩa tɛɛra tõma, tɩ tõma ãna wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na, tɩ na'am pãŋa la pa'asra zɔ'ɔra.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Tõma ĩyã tɩ tɩ tarɩ dũnia wã zuo ãn wʋ fuu-deego. Tõma mi tɩ fuu-deego ẽna sãn wa sã'am, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ kẽ'a zẽ'a saazuum bɔ tɔ. A de la kẽ'a zẽ'a n boe wakatɛ wuu, a dagɩ nẽrba n ẽŋɛ ẽ.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: La tõma nãmsrɩ mɛ bɔɔra zozo'e tɩ tɩ kẽ saazuum kẽ'a zẽ'a na.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Se'ere n soe la, tɩ sãn kẽ kẽ'a zẽ'e-ẽŋa, la ãn wʋ tɩ yɛ la fuugo la, tɩ kãn dɛna bɛɛla.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Tõma n ken bɔna dũnia zuo ĩyã wã pʋam na, tõma nãmsrɩ mɛ tara toogo. La dagɩ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ basɛ la dũnia wã zuo ĩyã la, tõma boorɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n dɩkɛ saazuum ĩyã la yɛ tɔ, tɩ sɛla n de vɔm wʋn pĩ sɛla n kiiri la.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n ẽŋɛ tõma tɩ tõma ãna bɛla, dee bɔ tõma a Sɩa n de sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tõma wʋn yẽ sɛla wuu tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ tɩ la.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Bɛla tɩ tõma tara bʋraanɛ zozo'e daarɛ woo, dee mina tɩ tõma n ken bɔna dũnia ĩyã wã pʋam na, tõma zãagɛ la Zuudãana kẽ'a zẽ'a na mɛ.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: La tõma dolɩ la sɛla tɩ tõma bɔ sɩra dee nãn ka yẽ.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Tõma tarɩ bʋraanɛ mɛ zo'oge, dee boorɩ nɩ tɩ tɩ basɛ dũnia ĩyã wã, dee sẽŋɛ ka bɔna Zuudãana la zẽ'am.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Bɛla tɩ tõma mõrgra bɔɔra tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ tɩ la paagɛ a yɛm, tõma sãn bɔna dũnia ĩyã wã pʋam, bɩɩ tɩ sãn sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Se'ere n soe la, tõma wuu wʋn ze'ele la Krisi nẽŋam tɩ a di tõma sarɩya, dee bɔ nẽra woo yɔɔrɔ tɩ la masɛ wʋ ẽn tõm se'em dũnia wã, a sãn tõm sõŋa, bɩɩ a sãn tõm be'em.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Bɛla tõma n mi tɩ zɔta Zuudãana n de se'em na, tɩ tõma mõrgra tɩ tɩ tɔgɛ yãŋɛ nẽrba tɩ ba sakɛ ẽ. Wẽnnaam mi tõma sõŋa mɛ. La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma sũure pʋam ya mẽ mi mam mɛ.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Tõma ken ka bɔɔra tɩ tɩ pa'alɛ ya tõma n ãn sõŋa se'em, tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ bɔ ya la folle tɩ ya tara yẽsõm tõma ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ tɔgɛ lerge nẽr-sɛba n wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ la sɛla n yẽtɩ la nini, dee ka bɩsra ba sũure pʋam na.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Tõma sãn dɛna zɔllɔ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã. La tõma sãn ka zalla, la de la yãma sõŋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: La de la Krisi nõŋlʋm n soe tõma la ĩyã. Tõma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ nẽra ayɩla n ki nẽrba wuu ĩyã, tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ nẽrba wuu po lagɩm na ẽ ki mɛ.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: A ki nẽrba wuu ĩyã tɩ nẽr-sɛba n vɔa la, da le vɔa ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, ba vɔa wʋ sɛka n ki ba ĩyã dee vo'oge la n boorɩ se'em.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ lɛɛlɛ wã, tõma ka le bɩsra nẽra wʋ nẽrsaala n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na. La baa tõma daan sãn bɩsra Krisi wʋ nẽrsaalba n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, lɛɛlɛ wã tõma ka le bɩsra ẽ bɛla.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Nẽra sãn lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla, a lebge la nẽr-paalga, yel-kɛgsɩ la tole mɛ, tɩ sɛla woo lebge paalsɩ.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ bɛla wuu, ẽŋa n botɩ Krisi naagɛ tõma la ẽŋa, dee bɔ tõma tõon-ẽna tɩ tɩ botɩ nẽrba naagɛ la ẽŋa.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Tõma n yetɩ se'em na, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ Krisi botɩ dũnia dõma la ẽŋa naagɛ taaba, dee ka geele ba tõon-be'ero. Dee bɔ tõma yetɔga tɩ tɩ tɔgɛ naarɛ yelle bɔ nẽrba.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Bɛla, Krisi n tõm tõma tɩ tõma sɔɔgɛ a zẽ'am tɔgra la yãma wʋ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n tɔgrɩ kã'ana ya tõma yetɔga la pʋam. Krisi ĩyã tõma sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Krisi ka tõm tõon-be'ero. La Nawẽnnɛ basɛ mɛ tɩ a lebge wʋ tõon-be'ero dãana tõma ĩyã, tɩ tõma wʋn lebge nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am ẽŋa ĩyã.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n zoe tõma ninbãalga bɔ tõma tõon-ẽŋa la ĩyã n soe tɩ tõma bʋraanɛ ka bɔ'ɔra.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Tõma n lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ tõnna la ĩyã, tõma sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya da basɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ya to'oge Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na dɛna zãŋa.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Tõma zagsɛ mɛ ka tõnna tõon-sɛba wuu n sʋgɛ n de yãnnɛ tõoma la, tõma ka ɩtɩ yɛm pã'asra nẽrba. La tõma ka teerɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. Tõma tɔgrɩ pa'ala la sɩra vẽelga vẽelga, tɩ nẽrba sũure n mi se'em na wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ tõma ɩtɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ nifum.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yele a gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam lerge ya pʋ'ʋsgɔ la wakat-sɛka tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ na. Fãarɛ la daarɛ, mam sõŋɛ ya mɛ.» La selse-ya, lɛɛlɛ wã de la wakatɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ yel-sõnnɛ, lɛɛlɛ wã de la fãarɛ daarɛ la.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: La kõ-yẽlga la tɩ tõma mõolɩ la sãn ken sʋgɛ mɛ, a sʋgɛ la nẽr-sɛba n wʋn sã'am na.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Tõma ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla n wʋn botɩ nẽra tuuge, tɩ nẽra da zɛrgɛ tɔ tɩ tõoma la pʋam.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: La de la sɛba n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la. Asũtãana n de dũnia wã naba la n pĩ ba yɛm tɩ ba da bãŋɛ Krisi n de Nawẽnnɛ wõnnɛ na kõ-yẽlga na'am pãŋa.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: La sɛla woo pʋam tõma pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ tõma de la nẽrba n tõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma. Tõma mã'arɩ tɩ sũure mɛ, wĩbra nãmsgɔ pʋam, la toogo, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ pʋam.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: La dagɩ tõma mẽŋa yelle tɩ tõma mõolɩ, la de la Azezi-krisi n de Zuudãana la kõa tɩ tõma mõolɩ. La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma de la yãma tõntõnɩba Azezi ĩyã.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Ba wẽ tõma mɛ, dee yʋ tõma yʋ'a deto pʋam. Ba botɩ nẽrba zozo'e gire mɛ zɛbɛ la tõma, tõma tõnnɩ tõon-ke'eno mɛ targɛ ka gĩsra, ka dita.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn yetɩ: «Peelem nẽegɛ lika pʋam» na, la de la ẽŋa n botɩ peelem nẽegɛ tõma sũa pʋam, botɩ tõma bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa n nẽegɛ Krisi nẽŋa zuo.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: La tõma tõnnɩ na pʋpeelem, la bãŋrɛ, la sũ-mã'arɛ, la ɩr-sõŋɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: La tõma n tarɩ bõntarsõm ẽŋa la, tõma ãn wʋ yɔgrɔ lɔgrɔ tɩ ba dɩkɛ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ ẽŋɛ mĩ. Tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ pãn-kãtɛ ẽŋa ka ze'ele tõma zẽ'am, a ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: Tõma tɔgrɩ la sɩra yetɔga la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa. Tõma n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ la n de tõma zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ tɩ tõma tarɩ zɛbra dee gu'ura tɩ mẽŋa.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Tõma yẽtɩ nãmsgɔ buuri to'oto'ore mɛ sɛla woo pʋam, la a ka yãŋɛ tõma. Tõma fablɩ mɛ ka mina tõma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, la tõma bʋraanɛ ka bo'oge.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Nẽrba basɛba nã'asrɩ tõma mɛ, tɩ basɛba pɔ'ɔra tõma, basɛba sã'anɩ tõma yʋ'ʋrɛ mɛ, tɩ basɛba pẽgra tõma. Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ tõma de la põmpɔrndõma, baa la tõma n tɔgrɩ sɩra la.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Nẽrba nãmsrɩ tõma mɛ, la Nawẽnnɛ ka basɛ tõma, ba wẽ'erɩ tõma lʋbra mɛ, la tõma ka ki.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ tõma de la sãama, baa la ban mi tõma sõŋa la, ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ nẽrba n kiiri, dee tɩ tõma vɔa. Ba sɩbgrɩ tõma mɛ, dee tɩ tõma ka kiira.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Tõma tarɩ nãmsgɔ mɛ tɩ ĩyã pʋam wʋ Azezi kũm nãmsgɔ la daarɛ woo ka gɔ'ɔra, tɩ nẽrba wʋn yẽ Azezi vɔm tõma pʋam.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ tõma tarɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la tõma tarɩ la sũ-yẽlga daarɛ woo. Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ nasdõma, dee tɩ tõma botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tara bõntarsõm. Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ nẽrba n ka tarɩ sɛla, dee tɩ tõma tara sɛla woo.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Tõma n vɔa wã, tõma põsrɩ tɩ yõorɛ mɛ wakatɛ wuu Azezi ĩyã, tɩ nẽrba wʋn yẽ a vɔm tõma ĩyã n kiiri la pʋam.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Korẽntɩ dõma, tõma tɔgɛ ya la vẽelga, tõma pelge la tɩ pʋʋrɛ tɔgɛ la ya.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Bɛla, kũm zɩgrɩ la a kʋ tõma, tɩ yãma yẽta vɔm.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: La dagɩ tõma n ka nõŋɛ yãma, la de la yãma n ka pa'alɛ ya nõŋlʋm.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam bɔ sɩra mɛ n soe tɩ mam tɔgɛ.» Tõma mẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra mɛ, bɛla n soe tɩ tõma tɔgra.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Mam tɔgrɩ la ya wʋ m kɔma la, ya mẽ nõŋɛ tõma, pelge-ya ya pʋʋrɛ la tõma.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Tõma mi mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, a mẽ wʋn po vo'oge tõma mɛ la Azezi, la a wʋn lagɩm tarɩ tõma la yãma sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Da dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa lagɩm na nẽr-sɛba n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la. Se'ere n soe la, sõŋa la be'em kãn yãŋɛ lagɩm taaba. Bẽm lagŋɔ n boe peelem, la lika tẽŋasʋka?.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Bõn-bãma wuu n ɩtɩ la de la yãma ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ wʋn paara nẽrba zozo'e, tɩ nẽrba wʋn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ zɔ'ɔra pa'asra, tɩ la dɛna a nã'asgɔ.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Bẽm naa-nõorɛ n boe Krisi, la Asũtãana tẽŋasʋka? Bɩɩ sɛka n bɔ Krisi sɩra, la sɛka n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra tarɩ la bẽm pʋrɛ la taaba?.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Bɛla tɩ tõma bʋraanɛ ka bɔ'ɔra. La baa tõma ĩyã n sã'anɩ na, tõma sɩa lebgrɩ la paalga daarɛ woo.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Bẽm lagŋɔ n boe Wẽnnaam deego, la baga tɩ ba mãalɛ tẽŋasʋka? La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa la deego wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yele yetɩ: «Mam wʋn bɔna la ba, kẽ'era la ba. Mam wʋn dɛna ba Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba.».

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ tõma yẽtɩ wakatɛ fẽfẽe wã de la pɩka ka tãna wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ la nã'asgɔ zozo'e n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ tɩ a wʋn botɩ tõma yẽ la.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Yese-ya ba pʋam, welge-ya ya mẽŋa dee basɛ ba. La ya da kalʋm sɛla n de dẽgrɔ, tɩ mam wʋn to'oge ya.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Tõma pʋtẽ'erɛ n boe la sɛla la, dagɩ sɛla tɩ nini yẽtɩ. A de la sɛla tɩ nini ka yẽtɩ. Bõn-sɛka tɩ nini yẽtɩ tollɩ mɛ. La sɛla tɩ nini ka yẽtɩ la boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: Mam wʋn dɛna ya Sɔ, tɩ ya dɛna mam dayɔɔsɩ, la m pɔyɔɔsɩ.» Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la n yele.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: Bɛla m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, nõor-bĩŋre bãma wuu n de tõma bõnɔ na, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ malgɛ tɩ mẽŋa la dẽgrɔ wuu n dẽgrɩ ĩyã, la sɩa, dee ãna sõŋa, vɔa pʋpeelem pʋam masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, zɔta Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ tõma nõŋlʋm bɔna ya sũure pʋam. Tõma ka ẽŋɛ nẽra baa ayɩla be'em, tõma ka sã'am nẽra yelle, la tõma ka zãm nẽra to'oge a sɛla.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Mam n tɔgrɩ bɛla la, dagɩ tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m mõŋɛ ya la bʋʋrɔ. Mam daan põn dẽŋɛ yele ya tõma n nõŋɛ ya zo'oge se'em mɛ, tõma sãn ki, bɩɩ tõma sãn vɔa mɛ.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: Mam bɔ ya sɩra mɛ, la mam yẽsnɩ mɛ yãma ĩyã. Baa tõma toogo la wuu pʋam, mam tarɩ bʋraanɛ, la sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Asɩra, tõma n daan paagɛ Masedʋanɩ na, tõma daan ka yẽ vo'osgo baa fẽfẽe. Tõma yẽ toogo buuri wuu mɛ, nẽrba n zɛbɛ la tõma, dee tɩ dabeem tara tõma.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ sũ-sã'aŋɔ dõma bʋraanɛ na, daan botɩ Atiti n wa'am tɩ tõma yẽ bʋraanɛ.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: La dagɩ a wa'aŋɔ la mã'a ĩyã, la de la ẽn wa'am wa yele tõma yãma n bɔ ẽŋa bʋraanɛ se'em na ĩyã. A yele tõma la yãma n boorɩ tɩ ya le yẽ mam zo'oge se'em, dee yele mam yãma sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la yãma n wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa mam ĩyã se'em. Bɛla n soe tɩ mam sũ-yẽlga le zo'oge pa'asɛ.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Mam gõŋɔ la tɩ mam daan gʋlsɛ la sãn sã'am ya sũure, mam ka ɩtɩ m sãn bãŋɛ nɩ. Mam sãn wʋn yetɩ m sãn bãŋɛ nɩ, la de la mam n daan bãŋɛ tɩ gõŋɔ la sã'am yãma sũure la. La bɛla de la wakatɛ fẽfẽe pʋam.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: La mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, la dagɩ mam n botɩ ya sũure sã'am na ĩyã, la de la ya sũ-sã'aŋɔ la n botɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Yãma sũ-sã'aŋɔ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em. Bɛla tõma ka ẽŋɛ ya sɛla n de be'em.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Asɩra, sũ-sã'aŋɔ sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na botɩ nẽra tɛɛra la yɛm basra tõon-be'ero, yẽta fãarɛ. Nẽra nẽra ka ɩtɩ a sãn bãŋɛ nɩ bɛla pʋam. La dũnia sũ-sã'aŋɔ sẽm na kũm.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Sũ-sã'aŋɔ sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na n botɩ ya ɩta se'em n wãna: A botɩ ya mõ'ora la nini, a botɩ ya pa'ala tɩ ya ka tarɩ taalɛ yel-ẽŋa pʋam, a botɩ ya sũure n ka ãna yẽlʋm, a botɩ ya zɔta la dabeem, a botɩ ya bɔɔra mɛ la ya sũure wuu tɩ ya yẽ mam, a botɩ ya tara la yɛmleego tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa, dee wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa tɩ ya sɩbgɛ sɛka n tõm be'em na. Yãma pa'alɛ mɛ sɛla woo pʋam tɩ yãma ka tarɩ taalɛ yel-ẽŋa pʋam.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Bɛla, mam n daan gʋlsɛ ya la, la dagɩ sɛka n tõm tõon-be'ero la ĩyã, la dagɩ nẽr-sɛka tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽ be'em na mẽ ĩyã. La de la, la botɩ ya bãŋɛ sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam yãma n nõŋɛ tõma zo'oge se'em.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: La de la bɛla n soe tɩ tõma yẽ bʋraanɛ, la dagɩ bʋraanɛ na mã'a tɩ tõma yẽ, tõma ken yẽ sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ, tõma n yẽ tɩ Atiti tarɩ sũ-yẽlga, yãma n keŋe a sũure se'em na.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: La sãn dɛna mam n pẽgɛ yãma Atiti zẽ'am na, yãma ka ẽŋɛ mam yãnnɛ. Tõma n ẽn tɔgra sɩra bɔ'ɔra ya daarɛ woo la, bɛla tõma n pẽgɛ yãma Atiti zẽ'am na mẽ de la sɩra.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Atiti sãn tẽegɛ yãma sakrɛ, la yãma n to'oge ẽ na dabeem na, a nõŋɛ yãma zo'oge pa'asɛ mɛ.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: Mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ, mam n tã wʋn bɔ yãma sɩra sɛla woo pʋam na ĩyã.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: M sɔbiisi, tõma boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ Masedʋanɩ Wẽndeto la nẽrba yel-sõnnɛ se'em.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Ba yẽ toogo mɛ nãmsɛ zozo'e, la ba tarɩ sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ, tɩ baa la ba nõŋɔ la n zo'oge la, ba bɔ sõŋrɛ zo'oge mɛ la ba sũure wuu.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ba bɔ masɛ wʋ ban tã se'em, la ba bɔ halɩ gãŋɛ la ba pãŋa.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: Ba mẽŋa n sose tõma tɩ tɩ bɔ ba sore tɩ ba po sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Zerizalɛm na.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Ba ẽŋɛ gãŋɛ tõma n daan tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ se'em na mɛ. Ba dẽŋɛ dɩkɛ la ba mẽŋa bɔ Zuudãana la, dee dɩkɛ ba mẽŋa bɔ tõma doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Bɛla tɩ tõma sose Atiti n pɔsɛ sõŋrɛ tõon-ẽŋa yãma zẽ'am na, yetɩ a tõm ẽ ba'asɛ.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Yãma mõrgɛ sɛla woo pʋam mɛ: Yãma bɔ Krisi sɩra zo'oge mɛ, tãna tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yelle sõŋa, tara Nawẽnnɛ mi'ilum zo'oge, wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa sɛla woo pʋam, la yãma tarɩ tõma nõŋlʋm zo'oge mɛ. Bɛla, mõrgɛ-ya la sõŋrɛ tõon-ẽŋa mẽ tõonɛ.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Mam n yetɩ bɛla la, la dagɩ pãŋa tɩ mam ɩtɩ ya. Mam dɩkɛ la nẽrba basɛba n ẽŋɛ se'em makɛ bɔ ya, tɩ ya pa'alɛ yãma nõŋlʋm n de nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra se'em.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Yãma mi tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ na mɛ: Baa la ẽŋa n daan de bõntarsõm dãana la, a daan ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa la nasa yãma ĩyã. Tɩ ẽn ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa nasa la, wʋn botɩ yãma lebge bõntatba Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: La de la mam pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ mam yetɩ m bɔ ya yel-ẽna wã pʋam: La ãn sõŋa bɔ ya tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa la tõon-ẽna wã. Yãma n de pɔspɔsɩ dõma n looge yɛm yʋʋnɛ n tole tɩ ya tõm tõon-ẽna. La dagɩ bɛla mã'a, yãma n ken dɛna pɔspɔsɩ dõma n pɔsɛ lig-lobre la.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Bɛla, lɛɛlɛ wã, tõm-ya ẽ la yɛmleego ba'asɛ, wʋ yãma n yetɩ yãma boorɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ se'em na. La ya bɔ masɛ wʋ yãma n tarɩ se'em.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra sãn bɔ la yɛmleego masɛ wʋ ẽn tarɩ se'em, Nawẽnnɛ to'orɩ a bɔ'a la mɛ, a ka bɩsrɩ sɛla tɩ fʋ ka tarɩ la.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ yãma n sõŋrɩ nẽrba basɛba la kẽ'esɛ ya nõŋɔ pʋam. La de la tɩ ya zõna taaba.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: La de la yãma sɛba n tarɩ zo'oge lɛɛlɛ wã la, sõŋɛ sɛba n de nasdõma la. Tɩ bãma mẽ sãn wa tara zo'oge, tɩ ya mẽ sãn bɔɔra sõŋrɛ, tɩ ba sõŋɛ ya, bɛla tɩ ya wuu wʋn zõna taaba.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: tɩ la doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ la yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n vaagɛ zo'oge la, ka tarɩ sɛla tɩ la zo'oge gãŋɛ. La sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ sɛka n vaagɛ fẽe la.».

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ yɛmloore ẽŋa Atiti sũure pʋam tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a sõŋɛ ya wʋ tõma n boorɩ tɩ sõŋɛ yãma se'em na.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Atiti sakɛ tõma sosgo mɛ, ken pa'asɛ, a looge la yɛm a mẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ a wa'am yãma zẽ'am wa sõŋɛ ya.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Tõma tõm na tɩ sɔbia ayɩla tɩ Wẽndeto nẽrba la wuu pẽgrɩ ẽ, ẽn tõnnɩ kõ-yẽlga la tõoma ĩyã, tɩ ẽŋa la Atiti wa'am.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Sɛla n pa'asɛ, Wẽndeto nẽrba la n looge ẽ tɩ a lagɩm la tõma ɛɛra tõnna sõŋrɛ tõon-ẽna wã. Tõma tõnnɩ tõon-ẽna nã'asra la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa, dee pa'ala tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ sõŋɛ nẽrba.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Tõma guusri la tɩ mẽŋa tɩ nẽr-sɛka da wa tã'agɛ zɛrgɛ tõma, tõma n bɩsrɩ ligri la n zo'oge yelle se'em na.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ãn sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la nẽrsaalba mẽ zẽ'am.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Tõma tõm na tɩ sɔbia ayẽma la bãma. Tõma makɛ ẽ mɛ nõorɛ faa bɩsɛ, la a wɩsgɛ a mẽŋa mɛ tõnna. Lɛɛlɛ wã a wɩsgɛ a mẽŋa gãnna kʋrʋm mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, a bɔ yãma sɩra zo'oge mɛ.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔbia Atiti yelle, a de la mam tõntõn-tadãana n lagɩm na mam tõnna yãma ĩyã. La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔbi-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na, ba de la nẽrba tɩ Wẽndeto nẽrba la tõm, tɩ la dɛna Krisi nã'asgɔ.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Bɛla, ẽŋɛ-ya sɛla n pa'alɩ ba, la Wẽndeto nẽrba la tɩ ya nõŋɛ ba, tɩ ba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ tõma n pẽgɛ yãma ba zẽ'am na, la de la sɩra.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: La ka le dɛna tɩ mam gʋlsɛ yele ya sõŋrɛ tɩ ya yetɩ ya bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Zerizalɛm na yelle.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Mam mi tɩ ya boorɩ tɩ ya sõŋɛ mɛ, bɛla tɩ mam pẽgɛ ya Masedʋanɩ dõma zẽ'am, yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Akayi la põn mãasʋm na yʋʋnɛ n tole tɩ ba sõŋɛ.» La yãma n wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa la keŋe nẽrba zozo'e mɛ, tɩ ba mẽ bɔɔra tɩ ba ẽŋɛ.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: La mam tõm na tɩ sɔbiisi la tɩ ba wa'am ya zẽ'am, tɩ tõma n pẽgrɩ yãma se'em bõn-ẽŋa pʋam na, da kãn dɛna zãŋa. Mam boorɩ la ya mãasʋm ba'asɛ wʋ mam n yele se'em na.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: La sãn dagna bɛla, Masedʋanɩ dõma la sãn doose mam wa'am wa yẽ tɩ ya ka mãasʋm, tõma n daan bɔ yãma sɩra zo'oge se'em na, la wʋn dɛna la yãnnɛ zozo'e bɔ tõma, la, la mẽ wʋn dɛna la yãnnɛ bɔ yãma.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam bɩsɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ mam sose tɩ sɔbiisi la tɩ ba dẽŋɛ nẽŋa wa'am yãma zẽ'am wa bɩsɛ yɛmleego bɔ'a la tɩ ya yetɩ ya bɔ la yelle. Tɩ mam sãn wa paam, tɩ ya põn mãasʋm bɔ'a la ba'asɛ mɛ, tɩ la wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya bɔ la ya yɛmleego, la dagɩ mõrgrɛ.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka n bʋrɛ fẽe, wʋn lagsɛ la fẽe. La nẽr-sɛka n bʋrɛ zo'oge, wʋn lagsɛ zo'oge.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Bɛla, nẽra woo bɔ wʋ ẽn looge yɛm se'em na, tɩ la da dɛna nẽnzũŋre, bɩɩ mõrgrɛ. La de la nẽr-sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ la sũ-yẽlga tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ tarɩ ke'eŋo mɛ tãna wʋn bɔ ya bõn-sõma buuri wuu, tɩ ya wʋn tara yãma n boorɩ sɛla la wuu daarɛ woo tɩ la seke, dee tara sɛla wʋn tõm tõon-sõma wuu.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam nẽra n masɛ la yelle yetɩ: «A pʋtɩ a bõntarsõm mɛ bɔ'ɔra nasdõma, a tõoma n masɛ boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ ka-kɔɔra bõnbuuri, dee bɔ'ɔra ẽ dia tɩ a dita la, wʋn bɔ ya sɛla tɩ ya tãna wʋn tõm tõon-sõma zozo'e wʋ bõnbuuri n wɔm zo'oge se'em, tɩ ya tõoma n masɛ wʋn zɔ'ɔra pa'asra.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: A wʋn bɔ ya bõntarsõm tɩ la seke sɛla woo pʋam, tɩ ya wʋn bɔ'ɔra la ya sũure wuu, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, yãma bɔ'a la tɩ tõma dɩkɛ bɔ ba la ĩyã.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sõŋrɛ tõonɛ na tɩ ya tõnnɩ na, la dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la sõŋrɛ mã'a ĩyã, la ken botɩ nẽrba zozo'e n pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Ba sãn to'oge sõŋrɛ ẽŋa, ba wʋn pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ yãma n pa'alɛ tɩ yãma sakrɩ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la, la yãma n tarɩ yɛmleego, tara ya bõntarsõm pʋta la bãma, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu la ĩyã.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Ba wʋn nõŋɛ ya mɛ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ ya ĩyã, ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ zozo'e la ĩyã.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, a bɔ'a n ka tarɩ makrɛ la ĩyã.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Mam Apolɩ tɩ ya yetɩ, mam sãn bɔna yãma zẽ'am, mam de la nẽr-bʋgsgɔ, la mam sãn ka bɔna yãma zẽ'am, mam tarɩ la bʋraanɛ tɔgra la, mam belnɩ ya mɛ la Krisi sũ-bʋgsʋm, la a sõŋa.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: Mam sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya da ɩta, tɩ mam sãn wa'am wa bɔna ya zẽ'am, la dɛna pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam tara bʋraanɛ tɔgra, se'ere n soe la, mam mi tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ la bʋraanɛ la nẽr-sɛba n yetɩ tõma ɩtɩ la tõma nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Tõma de la nẽrsaalba, la tõma ka zɛbrɩ wʋ nẽrsaalba n ɩtɩ se'em na.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: Zɛbrɛ lɔg-sɛba tɩ tõma tarɩ zɛbra la dagɩ nẽrsaala zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ, ba de la pãŋa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, n tã sã'ana sɛla wuu n tarɩ ke'eŋo. Tõma tarɩ ba sã'ana la tẽ'esgɔ sɛba wuu n de põmpɔrŋɔ.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: la bõn-sɛba wuu n zẽkrɩ ba mẽŋa botɩ nẽrba ka bãŋra Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa. Dee yãŋra tẽ'esgɔ buuri wuu botɩ ba sakra Krisi.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Yãma sãn wa ẽŋɛ sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ ya sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ sõŋa, tõma mãasʋm mɛ tɩ tɩ sɩbgɛ sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ la wuu.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Yãma bɩsrɩ la sɛla tɩ yãma nini yẽtɩ la. Nẽra sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la Krisi nẽra, a dãana mẽ tẽ'esɛ wãna wã: Ẽŋa n de Krisi nẽra la, tõma mẽ de la Krisi nẽrba.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: La mam sãn wẽ m yõ'ogɔ zo'oge fẽe la ke'eŋo sɛka tɩ Zuudãana bɔ tõma la, yãnnɛ ka tarɩ mam, la de la Zuudãana n bɔ tõma ke'eŋo tɩ tɩ sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam, la dagɩ tɩ sã'am ya.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ la ãna wʋ mam yetɩ m kẽ'esɛ ya la dabeem la m gõnnɔ na.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba n yetɩ se'em n wãna: «Apolɩ gõnnɔ na yetɔga kɛ'ɛm mɛ, dee dɛna toogo. La a mẽŋa sãn bɔna tõma zẽ'am, a ka tarɩ pãŋa, tɩ a yetɔga la dɛna ninbãalga.».

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: Nẽr-sɛka dãana n tɔgrɩ bɛla la, a bãŋɛ sõŋa tɩ tõma n ka boe yãma zẽ'am dee tɔgra se'em gõnnɔ na pʋam na, tõma sãn wa'am wa bɔna ya zẽ'am, tõma ken ãna la bɛla.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Tõma kãn saagɛ zõsõm tɩ mẽŋa, bɩɩ dɩkɛ tɩ mẽŋa makɛ la sɛba n pa'alɛ ba mẽŋa tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma ãn sõŋa la. Ba dɩkrɩ la ba mẽŋa mãmsra, makra ba mẽŋa la taaba. Ban ɩtɩ se'em na, ba ka tarɩ yɛm.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma kãn wẽ yõ'ogɔ tɩ la yõsgɛ, tõma wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ masɛ la tõon-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ tõma la beene. Ẽŋa n bɔ tõma sore tɩ tõma paagɛ yãma zẽ'am.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Tõma ka yõsgɛ tɩ beene wʋ tõma ka wa'am na yãma zẽ'am na, tõma n de pɔspɔsɩ nẽrba n wa'am yãma zẽ'am wa mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Tõma ka wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ la tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrba basɛba tõm, bɛla tõma ka yõsgɛ. La tõma tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la wʋn pa'asra mɛ, tɩ tõma tõonɛ na zɔ'ɔra pa'asra yãma zẽ'am, tɩ la ken masɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ tõma se'em na beene.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: Tɩ tõma yãŋa wʋn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la so'olʋm sɛba n boe ya nẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ la kãn dɛna tɩ tõma wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ la tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrba basɛba dẽŋɛ tõm ba tõoma zẽ'am.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a wẽ yõ'ogɔ, a wẽ yõ'ogɔ la sɛla tɩ Zuudãana ẽŋɛ la.».

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ nẽr-sɛka n pa'alɩ a mẽŋa tɩ ẽŋa ãn sõŋa la n masɛ, la de la nẽr-sɛka tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a ãn sõŋa la.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: Mam sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya sakɛ m yalnɛ wã fẽe, asɩra, sakɛ-ya m yetɔga wã.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: Mam ɩtɩ la sũure yãma ĩyã, la sũure ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Se'ere n soe la, mam yetɩ m dɩkɛ ya bɔ la Krisi mã'a, mam boorɩ tɩ ya tara la ya mẽŋa sõŋa tɩ m dɩkɛ ya bɔ ẽ wʋ pugla n nãn ka mi bʋraaga tɩ ba dɩkɛ bɔ sɩra.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: La mam zotɩ la dabeem tɩ nẽr-sɛka wʋn pã'asɛ ya, tɩ ya wẽrgɛ ka le dɔla Krisi la sũure ayɩla, la pʋpeelem, wʋ Awa tɩ bõnsɛla la yʋʋn dɩkɛ yɛmkẽgsɩga pã'asɛ ẽ tɩ a tuuge la.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra woo sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa mõola Azezi ayẽma yelle, tɩ la ka dɛna Azezi sɛka tɩ tõma mõolɛ bɔ ya la, yãma to'orɩ mɛ tɔtɔ, la yãma to'orɩ la sɩa ayẽma n dagɩ sɛka tɩ ya põn to'oge la, la yãma to'orɩ la kõ-yẽlga ayẽma n dagɩ sɛka tɩ yãma põn to'oge la, sakra ba sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Mam ka tẽ'esɛ tɩ nẽr-bãma n pa'alɩ tɩ bãma de la Tõntõnɩba n gãnnɩ na, mam de poorʋm dãana ba zẽ'am.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Baa la mam sãn ka mina yetɔga wʋ yetɔg-mita, la dagɩ tɩ mam ka tarɩ bãŋrɛ. La tõma pa'alɛ ya bõn-ẽŋa mɛ vẽelga vẽelga sɛla woo pʋam, wakatɛ wuu.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Mam n daan mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ yãma la, mam ka botɩ ya bɔ sɛla. Mam daan sike m mẽŋa mɛ, dee zẽkɛ yãma. Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam n daan ẽŋɛ se'em na de la be'em bɩɩ?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: Mam n daan tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra yãma la, mam sakɛ mɛ tɩ Wẽndeto basɛba nẽrba yɔ mam. La ãn wʋ mam fã la bãma, dɩkɛ sõŋɛ yãma.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: La mam n daan boe ya zẽ'am bɔɔra sõŋrɛ sɛba wuu la, mam ka te'ege ya nẽra nẽra tɩ a sõŋɛ mam. La daan de la tɩ sɔbi-sɛba n ze'ele Masedʋanɩ wa'am na n daan bɔ mam sɛla wuu n pɔ'ɔgɛ mam na. Mam daan guusri m mẽŋa mɛ tɩ m da dɛna zeero bɔ ya, la mam wʋn ken ãna la bɛla.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Krisi sɩra n boe mam sũure pʋam na, mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ nẽra ka tã wʋn botɩ mam ka wẽ yõ'ogɔ la bõn-ẽŋa yelle Akayi so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Bẽm ĩyã tɩ mam yele wãna? La de la mam ka nõŋɛ ya mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ ya mɛ.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Mam n ɩtɩ se'em na, mam wʋn ken ɩta la bɛla, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ yetɩ bãma n tõnnɩ se'em, la tõma n tõnnɩ se'em na de la bʋyɩla la, da yẽ folle.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Nẽr-bãma de la põmpɔrŋɔ Tõntõnɩba, ba pã'asrɩ la nẽrba, ɩta ba mẽŋa ãna wʋ ba de la Krisi Tõntõnɩba.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: La ẽna wã dagɩ bõn-di'iŋa. Se'ere n soe la, Asũtãana mẽŋa teerɩ mɛ ãna wʋ peelem malɛka.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Bɛla, la dagɩ di'ire tɩ a tõntõnɩba ɩta ba mẽŋa ãna wʋ sɛla n masɛ tõntõnɩba. La ba ba'asgɔ wʋn ãna wʋ ba tõoma la n de se'em na.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: Mam le yɛta mɛ tɩ nẽra da bɩsɛ mam wʋ mam de la yalma la. La ya sãn bɩsra mam wʋ yalma, ya yi sakɛ tɩ m wẽ m yõ'ogɔ fẽe, wʋ mam de la yalma la.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Mam n tɔgrɩ wẽ'era m yõ'ogɔ wã, la dagɩ Zuudãana n yetɩ mam yele se'em na. Mam keŋe la m sũure tɔgra wʋ yalma la, wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Nẽrba zozo'e n wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ la dũnia yɛla la, mam mẽ wʋn wẽ m yõ'ogɔ mɛ.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Yãma de la yɛm dõma, dee sakɛ tɔ'ɔra yalmdõma la yɛmleego!.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Yãma sakrɩ mɛ tɩ ba kẽ'esra ya yamnɛ pʋam, sakɛ tɩ ba dita ya, fãara ya, ka nanna ya, sakɛ tɩ ba wẽ'era ya pɛgsɩ.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Tõma n daan ẽŋɛ tɩ mẽŋa tarɩmdõma ka ẽŋɛ ya wʋ bãma n ẽŋɛ ya se'em na, la de la yãnnɛ bɔ mam bɩɩ? Nẽra sãn tãna wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ sɛla yelle, mam mẽ tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ mɛ a yelle. Wãna de la yalnɛ tɩ mam tɔgrɩ.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Ba de la Ebre dõma, mam mẽ de la Ebre nẽra. Ba de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, mam mẽ de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽra. Ba de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ, mam mẽ de la Abraham yɩɩŋa.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Ba yetɩ bãma de la Krisi tõntõnɩba, mam wʋn tɔgɛ wʋ mam yɛm n bɔɩ la, mam n de Krisi tõntõnna gãnna ba. Mam tõm nãmsɛ gãnna bãma mɛ. Ba kẽ'esɛ mam yʋ'a deem gãnna bãma mɛ, ba wẽ mam zo'oge gãnna bãma mɛ, nõorɛ zozo'e mam deege la m ki.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Nõorɛ bʋnuu tɩ Zifdõma wẽ mam la ka'asa, ka'asrɛ ayɩla kaɩ pinaasɩ.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Rom dõma wẽ mam mɛ nõorɛ atã. Daarɛ ayɩla nẽrba lobe mam mɛ la kuga deege la ba kʋ. Nõorɛ bʋtã tɩ mam boe õorŋɔ pʋam, tɩ a sã'am ko-kãtɛ pʋam mise. Mam ẽŋɛ daarɛ ayɩla mɛ ko'om pʋam, wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: Mam sɔa la sẽnnɛ pʋam, mam yẽ yel-pakrɩsɩ mɛ zozo'e la kʋlsɩ ko'om n pɩrɛ, la fãarba. Mam yẽ yɛla mɛ la Zifdõma n de mam buuri la, la bu-zãŋsɩ. Mam yẽ yel-pakrɩsɩ mɛ tẽnsɩ pʋam, la weto pʋam, la ko-kãtɛ pʋam. Mam daan yẽ yɛla mɛ la sɛba n parɩm tɩ bãma de la tɩ sɔbiisi la.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: Mam daan tõm tõon-ke'eno nãmsɛ mɛ. La nõorɛ faa mam ka gĩse. Kɔm, la koyũuro yõkɛ mam mɛ. Mam gã'arɛ kɔm mɛ wakatɛ basɛba. Wakatɛ basɛba ɔɔrɔ yõkɛ mam mɛ, tɩ mam ka tara fuugo wʋn pile.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Tɩ mam ka tɔgra yel-sɛba n weege la. La sɛla n de pakrɛ bɔ mam daarɛ woo, de la Azezi nẽrba n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu la yelle.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Nẽra sãn bɔna tɔrgɔ pʋam, la ãn wʋ mam mẽ boe la tɔrgɔ pʋam na. La nẽra sãn basɛ Nawẽnnɛ doosgo, mam sũure n sã'anɩ zozo'e.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: La sãn dɛna yõ'ogɔ wẽ'a, mam wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ la m tɔrgɔ la.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi Sɔ n soe pẽka wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ la, mi mɛ tɩ mam ka parnɩ.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: Mam n daan boe Damaasɩ la, gɔmna tɩ naba Aretasɩ botɩ a sɔna Damaasɩ bɩsra la, daan botɩ ba gu'ura la tẽŋa la nõa tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Tɩ ba daan botɩ mam sige pɩ'ɩgɔ pʋam, tɩ ba dɩkɛ mam doose sawure sike lalga poorʋm, la de la bɛla tɩ mam yãŋɛ põsɛ.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ mam wẽ yõ'ogɔ, la yõ'ogɔ wẽ'a la ka tarɩ yõorɔ. La mam wʋn ba'asõm tɔgɛ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Zuudãana la puke pa'alɛ mam, tɩ mam yẽ wʋ zãasŋɔ la.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: Mam mi la Krisi nẽra ayɩla yʋʋma pia la anaasɩ n wãna n tole, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ zom saazuum a yire. Mam ka mi a dãana sãn sɩrɩ zẽkɛ mɛ zom sɩra sɩra, bɩɩ la sãn ãna wʋ zãasŋɔ la, Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n mi.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Mam mi tɩ bʋra-ẽŋa zẽkɛ mɛ zom Nawẽnnɛ yire, la mam n yele se'em na, mam ka mi a sãn sɩrɩ zẽkɛ zom, bɩɩ la sãn ãna wʋ zãasŋɔ la.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: la mam n mi sɛla de la a wõm na yetɔga mĩ tɩ nẽra ka tã wʋn tɔgɛ, la sɛla tɩ ba ka bɔ nẽrsaala nõorɛ tɩ a yele.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: Mam wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ la bʋra-ẽŋa n yẽ sɛla la yelle, la mam kãn wẽ yõ'ogɔ m yɛla basɛba yelle, tɩ la sãn dagna mam tɔrgɔ la yelle mã'a.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Mam sãn bɔɔra tɩ m wẽ yõ'ogɔ, mam kãn dɛna yalma, se'ere n soe la, mam wʋn tɔgɛ la sɩra. La mam kãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, mam ka boorɩ tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ mam yelle gãŋɛ ban yẽ mam n ɩtɩ se'em, bɩɩ ban wõnnɩ mam n yele se'em na.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: La, lan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ mam da tã'agɛ zẽkɛ m mẽŋa la bõn-yãlma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ mam na, a basɛ tɩ Asũtãana ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn wʋ gõ'a mam ĩyã pʋam nãmsra mam tɩ mam ka tãna wʋn zẽkɛ m mẽŋa.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Mam sose Zuudãana la nõorɛ bʋtã yetɩ a botɩ bõn-ẽŋa fõrgɛ dee basɛ mam.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: La a lerge mam yetɩ: «Mam yel-sõnnɛ seke fʋ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, la de la tɔrgɔ pʋam tɩ mam pãŋa tõnnɩ zozo'e.» Bɛla, mam wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ m tɔrgɔ la pʋam, tɩ Krisi pãŋa la wʋn bɔna la mam lɛ.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam ɩta sũ-yẽlga m tɔrgɔ la pʋam, la tʋʋrɛ pʋam, la tooro pʋam, la nãmsgɔ pʋam, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ pʋam, mam n sakɛ Krisi la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, mam tɔrgɔ la pʋam tɩ mam tarɩ pãŋa.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: Yãma n mõrgɛ mam tɩ mam tɔgra wʋ yalma la. La de la yãma n de nɩ ya tɔgɛ po'oge mam, pa'alɛ mam n ãn se'em. Baa mam sãn ka dɛna sɛla, nẽr-sɛba n pa'alɩ tɩ bãma n de Tõntõnɩba n gãnnɩ la, mam ka de poorʋm dãana ba zẽ'am.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Yel-sɛba wuu tɩ mam daan wĩbri tõnna ya zẽ'am na pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ mam de la Krisi Tõntõnna. Bõn-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa daan ẽŋɛ mɛ.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Bẽm yel-sõnnɛ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ bɔ Azezi nẽrba zẽ'esɩ basɛba, tɩ la pɔ'ɔgɛ yãma? Sãn dagna mam n ka to'oge sõŋrɛ ya zẽ'am na mã'a. Bɔ-ya mam sugri yel-ẽŋa pʋam.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Nõorɛ atã dãana n wãna tɩ mam mãasʋm tɩ m wa'am ya zẽ'am wa bɩsɛ ya. La, la dagɩ yãma n wʋn bɩsɛ mam yelle. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka eerɩ ya sɛla, la de la yãma mẽŋa tɩ mam eerɩ. Yãma ãn wʋ mam kɔma la. Asɩra, la dagɩ kɔma n wʋn lagsɛ sɛla bĩŋe ba sɔdõma, la de la sɔdõma n wʋn lagsɛ sɛla bĩŋe ba kɔma.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: La sãn dɛna mam, mam wʋn dɩkɛ sɛla wuu tɩ mam tarɩ, la m mẽŋa wuu bɔ la yɛmleego yãma sõŋrɛ ĩyã. Mam n nõŋɛ yãma zo'oge la ĩyã n basɛ tɩ yãma ka nõŋɛ mam bɩɩ?.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Ya mi mɛ tɩ mam ka to'oge sõŋrɛ ya zẽ'am, la ya nẽrba basɛba yetɩ mam ẽŋɛ la yɛmkẽgsɩga pã'asɛ ya.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Mam n daan tõm nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba wa'am yãma zẽ'am na, ba nẽr-kãna tɩ mam botɩ a to'oge sɛla ya zẽ'am bɔ mam?.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: Mam daan sose la Atiti tɩ a wa'am ya zẽ'am, dee tõm tɩ sɔbia la, tɩ a doose ẽ. Bɛla, Atiti pã'asɛ yãma to'oge sɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Mam la Atiti tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, la tõma doose la sore ayɩla.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma n tɔgrɩ bɛla wuu la, tõma eerɩ la bʋʋrɔ yãma zẽ'am bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ. Tõma tɔgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, Krisi doosgo pʋam. Tɩ sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, tõma tɔgrɩ bɛla wuu tɩ la sõŋɛ la yãma, tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: Mam zotɩ mɛ tɩ m wʋn wa paam ya zẽ'am yẽ tɩ ya ka ãn wʋ mam n boorɩ se'em na, tɩ yãma mẽ yẽ tɩ mam ka ãn wʋ yãma n boorɩ se'em na. Mam zotɩ mɛ tɩ zɛbrɛ, la ɩta sũure la taaba, la sũ-isgre, la gẽelgɔ, la pɔ'ɔg-taaba, la ya'anzũm, la zẽk-m-mẽŋa, la ɩr-yooro wʋn bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: Mam zotɩ mɛ tɩ mam sãn wa wa'am yãma zẽ'am, mam dãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ mam yãma ĩyã, tɩ m sũure sã'am la ya nẽr-sɛba n daan tõm tõon-be'ero, dee ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la yalsɩ, la tõon-yooro sɛba tɩ ba ẽŋɛ la.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Nõorɛ bʋtã dãana n wãna tɩ mam sẽm ya zẽ'am. La mam wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na yetɩ: «Yɛla wuu malgrɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrba bayi bɩɩ batã dɛna kaset-dõma.».

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: Mam n daan wa'am ya zẽ'am nõorɛ bʋyi dãana la, mam daan kã'an sɛba n tõm tõon-be'ero, la nẽrba basɛba la wuu mɛ. La lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ mam ka boe ya zẽ'am na, mam le yɛta ya mɛ tɩ mam sãn wa'am, mam kãn zoe nẽra ninbãalga.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Yãma boorɩ tɩ ya yẽ la sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ Krisi n botɩ mam tɔgra, ya wʋn yẽ. Krisi sãn ɩta sɛla ya zẽ'am, tɔrgɔ ka tarɩ ẽ, a tarɩ pãŋa zo'oge mɛ ya zẽ'am.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Asɩra, a ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa la tarma, tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo. La a vɔa mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa. Tõma n de ayɩla la Krisi la, tõma mẽ de la tarɩmdõma, la tõma vɔa la Krisi lɛ, wʋn tõnna yãma zẽ'am la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Vẽesɛ-ya ya mẽŋa bɩsɛ ya sãn bɔna Krisi sore la zuo. Ya mẽŋa geele bɩsɛ, yãma ka mi tɩ Krisi boe la yãma mɛ? Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ya koŋe mɛ.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma mi mɛ tɩ tõma mõrgɛ mɛ ka koŋe.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Tõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ ya ĩyã tɩ ya da ẽŋɛ sɛla n de be'em. La dagɩ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ pa'alɛ tɩ tõma yãŋɛ mɛ, la de la tɩ yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa, baa la tõma sãn ãna wʋ tõma koŋe mɛ.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Se'ere n soe la, tõma ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla n sɩ'ɩsrɩ sɩra, tõma dolɩ la sɩra.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Tõma sãn bɔna tɔrgɔ pʋam dee tɩ yãma tara pãŋa, tõma tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ. La tõma n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɛla tɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ pʋam, de la ya vɔm wuu wʋn masɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
