local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam gʋlsra ya bõn-bãma, mam n ka boe ya zẽ'am na, tɩ m sãn wa wa'am ya zẽ'am, m da dɩkɛ Zuudãana n bɔ mam nõor-sɛka la sɩbgɛ ya. A bɔ mam nõorɛ la tɩ m sõŋɛ ya mɛ tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam, la dagɩ tɩ m sã'am ya.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Sɛla n deege, m sɔbiisi, tara-ya sũ-yẽlga, mõrgɛ-ya tõm masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, bɔ'ɔra-ya taaba bʋraanɛ, tara-ya nõorɛ ayɩla la taaba, vɔa-ya la sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n de nõŋlʋm, la sũ-mã'asʋm dãana la wʋn bɔna la ya lɛ.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm. Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa lagɩmtaarɛ wʋn bɔna la ya wuu.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Mam Apolɩ n de Krisi Tõntõnna n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã. La dagɩ nẽrsaalba n tõm mam, la mẽ dagɩ nẽrsaalba n looge mam tɩ mam dɛna Krisi Tõntõnna. La de la Azezi-krisi, la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge Azezi la n looge mam.

eng: and from all the brothers and sisters with me. To the churches in Galatia.
local: Tɩ sɔbi-sɛba wuu n boe kalam na, n lagɩm na mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra yãma Azezi-krisi nẽrba n lagsrɩ Galatɩ tẽnsɩ pʋam na.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: Azezi-krisi n dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, tɩ a fãagɛ tɔ dũnia wã be'em pʋam basɛ, wʋ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: Mam ze la yɛrɩ la yãma n darɛ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya Krisi yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã, dee to'oge kõa ayẽma wʋ kõ-yẽlga la.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: Kõ-yẽlga ayẽma ka boe. La de la nẽrba n bʋrsrɩ ya pʋtẽ'era bɔɔra tɩ ba teege Krisi kõ-yẽlga la.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Nẽra sãn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga ayẽma bɔ ya, tɩ la dagna sɛka tɩ tõma mõolɛ bɔ ya la, baa la sãn dɛna tõma mẽŋa, bɩɩ malɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego bɔna a dãana zuo.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Tõma põn yele ya mɛ, la mam le yɛta ya mɛ, tɩ nẽra sãn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga ayẽma n de to'ore la sɛka tɩ ya to'oge la, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego bɔna a dãana zuo.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Bɛla, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ nẽrba n pẽgɛ mam bɩɩ, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ n pẽgɛ ma? Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ tɩ la paagɛ la nẽrsaalba yɛm bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, mam sãn boorɩ nɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ tɩ la paagɛ nẽrsaalba yɛm, mam ka de nɩ Krisi tõntõnna.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam mõolɩ la ka ze'ele nẽrsaalba zẽ'am.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: La mam mẽ ka to'oge ẽ nẽra zẽ'am, la mẽ dagɩ nẽra n zãsʋm mam. La de la Azezi-krisi mẽŋa n puke ẽ pa'alɛ mam.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Ya wõm mam daan ãn se'em Zifdõma sore la pʋam, daan nãmsra Azezi nẽrba la zozo'e, mõora tɩ m sã'am ba sore la.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: Tõma Zifdõma sore la pʋam, mam daan sẽŋɛ nẽŋa mɛ wɩsgɛ m mẽŋa la tɩ yaabdõma dɔgmike la yelle, ɩta lan yele se'em na gãnna m zuutaaba zozo'e.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ daan welge mam na to'ore lan dẽŋɛ mam dɔka, ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ, looge mam tɩ m dɛna a tõntõnna.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: Ẽŋa n daan wa bɩsɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa, tɩ a puke a Dayʋa la pa'alɛ mam tɩ m mõolɛ a Dayʋa la kõ-yẽlga bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ la, mam daan ka sẽŋɛ nẽr-sɛka zẽ'am tɩ a zãsʋm mam.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: La mam mẽ daan ka sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm sɛba n dẽŋɛ mam dɛna Azezi Tõntõnɩba la zẽ'am. Mam daan sẽŋɛ la Araabɩ so'olʋm, bɛla poorʋm tɩ mam lebe Damaasɩ.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Bɛla poorʋm yʋʋma atã n tole, tɩ mam yãŋa sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm tɩ m ka bãŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, daan ẽŋɛ dabsa pia la anuu a zẽ'am.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: La mam ka yẽ Azezi Tõntõnna ayẽma pa'asɛ, sãn dagna Azakɩ n de Zuudãana yɩbga la mã'a.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Mam n gʋlsɛ ya sɛla wã, mam yetɩ ya Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tɩ la dagɩ põmpɔrŋɔ.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Bɛla poorʋm mam ze'ele Zerizalɛm sẽŋɛ la Siiri, la Silisi so'olʋm.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: La Azezi-krisi nẽr-sɛba n boe Zude tẽnsɩ la daan nãn ka yẽ mam.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: Ba daan wen wõm mɛ tɩ ba tɔgrɩ mam yelle yetɩ: «Sɛka n daan nãmsrɩ tõma mõora tɩ a sã'am kõ-yẽlga tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la, yãŋa mõolɩ la kõ-yẽlga la.».

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Tɩ ba nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ mam ĩyã.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Bɛla poorʋm yʋʋma pia la anaasɩ n tole, tɩ mam la Abarnabasɩ lebe Zerizalɛm, la mam tarɩ la Atiti lagɩm mĩ.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n daan puke pa'alɛ mam, tɩ mam sẽŋɛ. Mam daan pa'alɛ ba la kõ-yẽlga tɩ mam mõolɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ la. La de la Azezi nẽrba nẽŋadõma la mã'a tɩ mam tɔgɛ bɔ ba. Mam daan ka boorɩ tɩ tõon-sɛka tɩ mam daan tõm, dee ken tõnna wã, wa dɛna zãŋa.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: La Atiti n de Grɛkɩ n dolɩ la mam na, ba põn ka pẽrgɛ yetɩ ba wã ẽ.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Nẽrba basɛba n daan pã'asɛ tɩ bãma de la Azezi karẽnbiisi, li'ise kẽ tõma tẽŋasʋka bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ tõma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, tõma n to'oge tɩ mẽŋa se'em, bãma daan boorɩ tɩ ba basɛ tɩ tõma le dɛna la Zifdõma lɔɔ yamsɩ.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Tõma daan ka sakɛ ban boorɩ se'em na baa fẽfẽe, tɩ kõ-yẽlga n de sɩra la wʋn ken bɔna yãma ĩyã.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba bɩsrɩ wʋ nẽŋadõma la, ba sãn dɛna nẽŋadõma bɩɩ ba dagɩ nẽŋadõma, la ka pakɛ mam, Nawẽnnɛ ka tũ'usri nẽrba. Nẽŋadõma bãma ka mõrgɛ mam tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla pa'asɛ mam zãsŋɔ la pʋam.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Ba tõn yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ kõ-yẽlga la ẽŋɛ la mam nu'usum tɩ m mõolɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, wʋ ẽn dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ nu'usum tɩ a mõolɛ bɔ Zifdõma la.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ Apɩyɛɛrɩ pãŋa, tõm ẽ Zifdõma zẽ'am na, ẽŋa n ken bɔ mam pãŋa tɩ mam tõnna bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: La Azakɩ, la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azã tɩ ba bɩsrɩ wʋ ba wuu kɛ'ɛndõma la, n bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ dɩkɛ tõonɛ na ẽŋɛ mam nu'usum na, ba yõgɛ mam, la Abarnabasɩ nu'usi mɛ, pa'alɛ tɩ tɩ de la ayɩla lagɩm tõnna Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, tɩ tõma tõnna bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am, tɩ bãma mẽ tõnna Zifdõma zẽ'am.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Sɛla tɩ ba daan sose tõma de la tɩ sõŋra ba Wẽndeego la nasdõma mã'a. La de la bɛla tɩ mam daan ɩtɩ zozo'e.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: Apɩyɛɛrɩ n daan wa'am Antɩyɔsɩ la, mam daan tɔgɛ ẽ mɛ vẽelga vẽelga ka sakɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a daan ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ka masɛ.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Ẽn daan boe Antɩyɔsɩ la, a daan lagnɩ na bu-zãŋsɩ la dita mɛ. La nẽrba n daan ze'ele Azakɩ zẽ'am wa'am, tɩ a welge ka le dita la bu-zãŋsɩ la, se'ere n soe la, a daan zotɩ la sɛba n yetɩ dẽnɩ tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore wãta la.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: Tɩ tɩ sɔbiisi basɛba n de Zifdõma daan po ɩta wʋ Apɩyɛɛrɩ n ɩtɩ se'em na, halɩ tɩ Abarnabasɩ mẽ po kẽ ba pʋlibsum ɩrgɔ la pʋam.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: La mam n daan yẽ tɩ ban ɩtɩ se'em na ka masɛ wʋ kõ-yẽlga la sɩra n de se'em na ĩyã, mam daan yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ ba wuu nẽŋam yetɩ: «Fõn de Zifu dee daan vɔa wʋ bu-zãŋsɩ n vɔa se'em na, fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne mõrgra bu-zãŋsɩ la tɩ ba vɔa wʋ Zifdõma?».

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma dɔgɛ dɛna la Zifdõma, tõma dagɩ bu-zãŋsɩ n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: La tõma mi mɛ tɩ la dagɩ nẽra n dolɩ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na ĩyã, n wʋn basɛ tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la de la nẽra n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra mã'a tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am. Tõma mẽ bɔ la Azezi-krisi sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tɩ tɩ masɛ a zẽ'am, tõma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã, dee dagɩ tõma n dolɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na ĩyã. Nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ẽn dolɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na ĩyã.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Tõma n te'ege Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a basɛ tɩ tɩ masɛ a zẽ'am tõma n bɔ Krisi sɩra ĩyã la, bõn-ẽŋa sãn basɛ tɩ tõma mẽ dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma wʋ bu-zãŋsɩ la, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Krisi botɩ tɩ tõnna la tõon-be'ero bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: Nẽra sãn basɛ Zifdõma lɔɔ tɩ a daan dolɩ la, dee le yetɩ ba dɔla ẽ, la de la a mẽŋa n sã'anɩ lɔɔ la.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: La sãn dɛna lɔɔ la, a botɩ mam lebge la kũm a ĩyã, mam ka le dɔla ẽ. Mam lagɩm na Krisi ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo, tɩ m vɔa Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: tɩ mam n vɔa wã, la ka le dɛna mam mẽŋa n vɔɩ, la de la Krisi n botɩ mam vɔa. La vɔm sɛka tɩ mam vɔa lɛɛlɛ wã, de la mam n bɔ ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa n nõŋɛ mam ki mam yelle sɩra la, n basɛ tɩ mam vɔa.

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: Mam ka sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na de la zãŋa. La sãn dɛna la nẽra n dolɩ lɔɔ ĩyã n basɛ tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, Krisi kũm na de nɩ na zãŋa.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Yãma Galatɩ dõma, ãne n botɩ yãma zuto lebge tɩ ya ka tara yɛm? Mam daan pa'alɛ ya Krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la yelle mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ la dɛna vẽelga bɔ ya.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Mam boorɩ tɩ m soke ya la sokre ayɩla mã'a tɩ ya lerge mam: Yãma n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la, la de la yãma n sakrɩ ɩta lɔɔ la n yele se'em na ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la de la yãma n wõm kõ-yẽlga la, bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Yãma yɛm na pɔ'ɔgɛ paagɛ la wãna? Yãma n pɔsɛ lebge Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la a Sɩa pãŋa la, yãma yãŋa boorɩ tɩ ya ba'asɛ la ya mẽŋa pãŋa?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Yãma n yẽ se'em wuu la lebge la zãŋa bɩɩ? Dẽnɩ tɩ la da kãn lebge zãŋa.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ya a Sɩa, dee ɩta bõn-yãlma ya tẽŋasʋka la, la dagɩ yãma n ɩtɩ lɔɔ n yele se'em na ĩyã, la de la yãma n wõm kõ-yẽlga la, bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Abraham zẽ'am la mẽ yʋʋn de la bɛla: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Abraham bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ ẽ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.».

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la n sɩrɩ de Abraham yɩɩsɩ.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la põn yele mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn põn tɔgɛ kõ-yẽlga ẽŋa bɔ Abraham mɛ yetɩ: «Fõn ĩyã tɩ dũnia buuri wuu wʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ.».

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: La de la bɛla, Abraham n yʋʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra yẽ yel-sõnnɛ na, nẽr-sɛba n mẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra wʋn lagɩm na Abraham yẽ yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Nẽr-sɛba n te'ege lɔɔ la, kã'a-be'ero boe ba zuto, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Kã'a-be'ego boe nẽr-sɛka woo n ka ɩtɩ wʋ lan gʋlsɛ se'em wuu Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ pʋam na zuugo.».

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: La de la vẽelga tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla ka tã wʋn yẽ bʋʋrɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am ẽn dolɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽra n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra masɛ a zẽ'am wʋn vɔa, ẽn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.».

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Nẽra n dolɩ lɔɔ la, la dagɩ tɩ a bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, bẽm ĩyã, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, a wʋn vɔa bɛla ĩyã.».

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: La tõma n ka ẽŋɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na wuu ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego n boe tõma zuto. La Krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la, a dɔkɛ kã'a-be'ego la tõma zuto mɛ, tɩ ẽŋa mẽŋa sɔɔgɛ tõma zẽ'am, tɩ kã'a-be'ego la bɔna a zuugo, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Kã'a-be'ego boe nẽr-sɛka tɩ ba yulse ẽ dɔɔgɔ zuo la zuugo.».

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: Krisi ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ Abraham na, mẽ wʋn dɛna bu-zãŋsɩ bõnɔ, bãma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, dee tɩ tõma mẽ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɔ, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam yetɩ m dɩkɛ la nẽrsaala yelle makɛ pa'alɛ ya. Nẽra sãn looge yɛm dee gʋlsɛ bĩŋe, tɩ la doose la tẽŋa la lɔɔ, nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn sã'am ẽ, bɩɩ pa'asɛ sɛla mĩ.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la Abraham, la a yɩɩŋa. La ka gʋlsɛ yetɩ, a bĩŋe a nõorɛ bɔ la a yɩɩsɩ, wʋ la de la nẽrba zozo'e. La yetɩ, a yɩɩŋa, la de la nẽra ayɩla mã'a. Nẽr-ẽŋa de la Krisi.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Mam n yele se'em na võorɛ n wãna: La de tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la, yʋʋma kɔbsnaasɩ la pitã n tole dee tɩ a yãŋa bɔ a lɔɔ la. Bɛla lɔɔ la kãn yãŋɛ sã'am nõor-bĩŋre la tɩ a lebge zãŋa.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Se'ere n soe la, la sãn de nɩ na lɔɔ la dɔlga ĩyã tɩ nẽra wʋn to'oge sɛla sɔna, la ka le dɛna nõor-bĩŋre ĩyã. La, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la ĩyã, tɩ a ẽŋɛ Abraham yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, bẽm ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bɔ lɔɔ la? A yʋʋn bɔ ẽ tɩ a botɩ nẽrba n bãŋɛ ba tõon-be'ero, halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Abraham yɩɩŋa la wʋn wa wa'am, sɛka ĩyã tɩ a yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la. Malɛkadõma n yʋʋn to'oge lɔɔ la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am bɔ la, la doose la nẽra ayɩla n boe Nawẽnnɛ, la nẽrba tẽŋasʋka wẽ'era nu'ugo.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Nẽra ka wẽ'erɩ nu'ugo la nẽra ayɩla mã'a. La Nawẽnnɛ de la a ayɩla mã'a, dee yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ, tɩ nu'u-wẽ'era ka bɔna.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Bɛla, lɔɔ la sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõor-bĩŋre la mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla baa fẽfẽe. Se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ sãn boe nɩ n tã wʋn botɩ nẽrba tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, bɛla a ken tã nɩ wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am mɛ, ban sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɩ, dũnia dõma wuu boe la tõon-be'ero nu'usum. La de la bɛla tɩ sɛba n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ba, ban bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Wakat-sɛka tɩ Azezi-krisi daan nãn ka wa'am tɩ nẽrba bɔ ẽ sɩra la, lɔɔ la lʋ tõma mɛ wʋ tõma boe la yʋ'a deem na, gu'ura halɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wa puke Azezi-krisi tɩ tɩ wʋn bɔ sɩra la peelem.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Bɛla lɔɔ la n yʋʋn de tõma gu'ura pa'ala tõma sore halɩ wa paam wakat-sɛka tɩ Krisi wa'am na, tɩ tõma wʋn dɛna nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ Azezi-krisi wa'am tɩ tɩ bɔ ẽ sɩra la, lɔɔ la ka le dɛna tõma gu'ura.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Yãma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la, ya wuu de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Yãma wuu tɩ ba mise ya ko'om pʋam na, ya lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla, ãna wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Yãma n lagɩm na Krisi la, bɔkrɛ ka le bɔna tɩ fʋ de la Zifu, bɩɩ bu-zãŋka, yamŋa bɩɩ nẽra n soe a mẽŋa, pɔka bɩɩ bʋraaga, ya wuu de la ayɩla.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Yãma n lagɩm na Krisi la, ya de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ, wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ la.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Mam n yetɩ m yele se'em n wãna: Bia n yetɩ a wa sɔɔgɛ sɔna a sɔ bõnɔ dee ken dɛna bõn-pɩka, a ãn wʋ a de la yamŋa la, baa la ẽŋa n wʋn wa sɔna sɛla woo la.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: Ẽn nãn de bia la, a boe la nẽrba nu'usum tɩ ba gu'ura ẽ, dee bɩsra a sɛla woo yelle bɔ'ɔra ẽ halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ a sɔ looge la, tɩ a yãŋa sɔna sɛla woo.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Tõma n mẽ daan ka bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, ãna wʋ kɔma la, tõma daan de la dũnia wã yɛla yamsɩ.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: La wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la n paagɛ la, a tõm na a mẽŋa Dayʋa, tɩ pɔka dɔgɛ ẽ, tɩ a dɔla Zifdõma lɔɔ la.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: tɩ a fãagɛ sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la, tɩ tɩ wʋn lebge Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: La yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma la, a tõm na a Dayʋa Sɩa tɩ a bɔna tɩ sũure pʋam, tɩ Sɩa la wi'ira Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Abba, m Sɔ.».

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Bɛla tɩ ka le dɛna yamsɩ, tɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma. Tõma n de a kɔma la ĩyã, a wʋn bɔ tɔ sɛla wuu tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a kɔma la.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: Kʋrʋm, yan daan ka mi Nawẽnnɛ na, ya daan dolɩ la dũnia wã bõn-yooro n dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ poore, dɛna ba yamsɩ.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: La nãnannɛ wã tɩ ya mi Nawẽnnɛ na, la, lan gãnnɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n mi ya la, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ yãma le dɔla bõn-yooro n ka tarɩ pãŋa dee ka tara võorɛ poore, le bɔɔra tɩ ya dɛna ba yamsɩ?.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Yãma looge la dabsa, la wõrsɩ, la wakatɛ basɛba yʋʋnɛ pʋam, la yʋʋma dɔla tɩ la dɛna sisgo.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: Yãma n ɩtɩ se'em na botɩ dabeem n tara mam, tɩ mam n tõm se'em yãma ĩyã la, tã wʋn dɛna zãŋa.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam belnɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya ãna wʋ mam na, tɩ mam lebge ãna wʋ yãma la. Yãma daan ka ẽŋɛ mam tuuge.

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Ya mi tɩ la daan de la mam bã'aga la ĩyã tɩ mam yẽ folle mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ya pɔspɔsɩ.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: Mam bã'aga la daan de la nãmsgɔ bɔ ya, la yãma daan ka nẽ'esɛ mam, la ya mẽ daan ka zagsɛ mam, ya daan to'oge mam wʋ mam de la Nawẽnnɛ malɛka, bɩɩ Azezi-krisi la.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Yãma sũure daan ãn yẽlʋm mɛ, tɩ mam tãna wʋn yetɩ, yãma daan sãn tã nɩ, ya wʋn dɩkɛ ya nini bɔ mam. Bẽm ẽŋɛ tɩ yãma ka le tara sũ-yẽlga?.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Mam n tɔgɛ ya sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ mam yãŋa lebge ya bɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ mam yetɩ la, ban mõrgrɩ se'em yãma ĩyã la, la dagɩ ya sõŋrɛ ĩyã. Ba boorɩ tɩ ba welge ya la mam, tɩ ya lagɩm na bãma.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: Nẽra sãn mõrgra tɩ la sãn dɛna sõŋa ĩyã, la ãn sõŋa. La ãn sõŋa tɩ ya ɩta bɛla wakatɛ wuu, la, la da dɛna mam n boe ya zẽ'am wakatɛ sɛka la mã'a.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: M kɔm-nõŋrɩsɩ, mam le tara la toogo ya ĩyã wʋ pɔka n virgri la, halɩ tɩ ya wʋn wa ãna wʋ Krisi n ãn se'em na.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: Mam boorɩ nɩ na m bɔna ya zẽ'am lɛɛlɛ wã bãŋɛ mam wʋn tɔgɛ se'em pa'alɛ ya. La mam n boe zẽ'e-sɛka wã, mam ka mi mam wʋn yele ya se'em.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Yele-ya ma, yãma sɛba n boorɩ tɩ lɔɔ la sɔna ya la, yãma ka wõnnɩ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na?.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ, Abraham tarɩ la dayɔɔsɩ bayi. Ayɩla de la yam-pɔka bia, tɩ ayẽma dɛna a pɔga bia.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: Yam-pɔka bia la dɔgɛ wʋ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em na. La a pɔga bia la dɔgɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Bõn-ẽna de la makrɛ bɔ tõma. Pɔgsɩ bayi la makrɩ la yɛla bayi. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nẽrba a lɔɔ, dee bĩŋe a nõorɛ la. Pɔspɔsɩ pɔka la yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agaarɩ la mãmsrɩ la lɔɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ Sinayi tãŋa zuo la. Nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la de la yamsɩ, wʋ Agaarɩ la a kɔma n de yamsɩ se'em na.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Agaarɩ ãn wʋ Sinayi tãŋa n boe Araabɩ tẽŋa la, dee ken ãna wʋ Zerizalɛm sɛka n boe lɛɛlɛ wã la, se'ere n soe la, Zerizalɛm la a nẽrba la boe la lɔɔ yamnɛ pʋam.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: La Zerizalɛm sɛka n boe saazuum na ka boe yamnɛ pʋam, tõma de la tẽn-ẽŋa nẽrba, wʋ Asaara kɔma n dagɩ yamsɩ la.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, kõndɔgrɛ, fõn ka dɔgɛ la. Ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga wa'ara kĩnkĩma sɩrna, fõn ka virge la. Se'ere n soe la, pɔg-sɛka tɩ a sɩra basɛ la kɔma n wʋn zo'oge gãnna pɔg-sɛka n tarɩ sɩra la.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɔbiisi, yãma de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma, ẽn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la ĩyã, wʋ A'izakɩ tɩ ba dɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ ĩyã la.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: La, la mẽ ãn wʋ bi-sɛka tɩ ba daan dɔgɛ ẽ wʋ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em na n nãmsɛ bi-sɛka tɩ ba dɔgɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, la ken dɛna la bɛla mẽ lɛɛlɛ wã.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɩ bẽm? A yetɩ: «Dige yam-pɔka, la a bia la, se'ere n soe la, yam-pɔka la bia kãn lagɩm na pɔg-sɛka n dagɩ yamŋa la bia po to'oge ba sɔ bõnɔ.».

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, tõma dagɩ yam-pɔka kɔma, tõma de la pɔg-sɛka n dagɩ yamŋa la kɔma.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Krisi fãagɛ tɩ basɛ tɩ tɩ sɔna la tɩ mẽŋa. Bɛla, zɛ-ya kãŋkãŋɛ, da sakɛ-ya le kẽ'esɛ ya mẽŋa yamnɛ pʋam.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Selse-ya, mam Apolɩ yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya sãn basɛ tɩ ba wã ya, Krisi ka le tara yõorɔ bɔ ya.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: La mam le yɛta ya mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n basɛ tɩ ba wã ẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na wuu.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Yãma sɛba n te'ege lɔɔ la ɛɛra tɩ ya masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam na, ya welge ya mẽŋa mɛ la Krisi, ya koŋe Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, la de la tõma n bɔ Krisi sɩra la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa basɛ tɩ tõma tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ a wa basɛ tɩ tɩ masɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Azezi-krisi doosgo la pʋam, fʋ sãn wã, bɩɩ fʋ sãn ka wã, la ka tarɩ võorɛ, sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la fʋ bɔ Azezi sɩra tõnna la nõŋlʋm.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Yãma daan dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, ãne n gu ya tɩ ya basɛ da le sakɛ sɩra la?.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Tẽ'esgɔ bãma n botɩ ya basɛ sɩra la ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya la zẽ'am.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Ba makɛ yetɩ: «Dãbɩllɛ fẽe n botɩ borborɩ wuu ukra.».

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: La sãn dɛna mam, Zuudãana la ĩyã mam bɔ ya sɩra mɛ tɩ yãma tẽ'esgɔ kãn bɔna ayẽma la mam n pa'alɛ ya se'em na. Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ ya pʋtẽ'era yuulna la, baa ẽn de nẽr-sɛka, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sɩbgɛ ẽ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: M sɔbiisi, la sãn dɛna sɩra tɩ mam mẽŋa ken mõola mɛ yɛta tɩ ba wãta, bẽm tɩ Zifdõma nãmsra mam? La sãn de nɩ na bɛla, mam n mõolɩ Krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo tɩ ba nãmsra mam na kãn le bɔna.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Mam boorɩ nɩ mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ ya pʋtẽ'era yuulna tɩ ya wãta la, sãn põn bɔɔra, ba fɔ ba mẽŋa pa'asɛ.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɔbiisi, Nawẽnnɛ wi ya tɩ ya sɔna la ya mẽŋa. La da basɛ-ya tɩ yan soe ya mẽŋa la basɛ tɩ ya ɩta ya mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em. Vɔa-ya la taaba la nõŋlʋm tõnna sõŋra taaba.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ la wuu boe la yetɔg-ẽna ayɩla pʋam n yetɩ: «Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa.».

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: La ya sãn zɛbra la taaba, yĩ'ira dee dõnna taaba wʋ dũnsi la, guuse-ya, tɩ ya wʋn sã'am taaba mɛ ba'asɛ.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: Selse-ya, mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya dɔla Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em, tɩ ya kãn ɩta nẽrsaalba yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrsaalba mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em na de la to'ore la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ sɛla la. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ sɛla la, mẽ dɛna to'ore la sɛla tɩ nẽrsaala mẽŋa boorɩ la. Bõn-bãma bayi sɩ'ɩsrɩ la taaba. La bɛla basɛ mɛ tɩ ya ka tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ yãma n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sãn sɔna ya, ya ka le bɔna lɔɔ la nu'usum.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: Nẽrsaala mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em na de la vẽelga vẽelga. La de la yalsɩ, la tõon-dẽgrɔ, la tõon-yooro.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: la baga kãabgɔ, la tɩɩm tõoma, la taaba sisga, la zɛbrɛ, la ɩta sũure la tadãana bõnɔ, la sũ-isgre, la gãŋɛ taaba, la ka-wõm-taaba, la welge taaba.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: La nõŋɛ bõnɔ, la dãam buka, la pʋyã'an-yooro, la bõnɔ basɛba n wõn na bõn-bãma. Mam dẽŋɛ kã'ana ya mɛ, wʋ mam n daan põn yele ya se'em na, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ bõn-bãma kãn yẽ zẽ'am Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tõoma de la nõŋlʋm, la sũ-yẽlga, la sũ-mã'asʋm, la sũ-mã'arɛ, la sõŋa, la ɩr-sõŋɔ, la ẽŋɛ sɩra.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: la sũ-bʋgsʋm, la yõk-m-mẽŋa. Lɔɔ ka boe n sɩ'ɩsrɩ bõn-bãma.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n de Azezi-krisi nẽrba la ka ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em n de be'em, la ba yɛmleego la mɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, tɩ ba ka le sɔna ba.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ tɩ vɔm na, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ dɔla ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Tɩ da dɛna-ya uk-m-mẽŋa dõma, tɩ da ɛɛra-ya taaba nõorɛ, bɩɩ ɩta sũure la taaba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: M sɔbiisi, ya sãn yẽ nẽra tɩ a tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, yãma sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ya lɛ la, pa'alɛ-ya ẽ la sũ-bʋgsʋm tarɩ ẽ leme so-sõŋɔ la zuo. La guuse fʋ mẽŋa, tɩ fʋ mẽ da po tõm a tõon-be'ero la.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Sõŋra-ya taaba malgra taaba yɛla, tɩ bɛla tɩ ya wʋn ẽŋɛ Krisi nõorɛ n yele se'em na.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Nẽra sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la sɛla dee ka dɛna sɛla sɛla, a pã'asrɩ la a mẽŋa.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Nẽra woo geele bɩsɛ a mẽŋa tõoma n ãn se'em, a sãn yẽ a tõoma la pʋam sɛla n de a ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, tɩ a ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa ĩyã. La a da magra a mẽŋa la nẽrba basɛba.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Nẽra woo n wʋn tara a mẽŋa zuugo yelle.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ nẽra zãsnɩ ẽ Krisi yetɔga la, a basɛ tɩ sɛka n zãsnɩ ẽ na yẽta pʋrɛ ẽn tarɩ sɛla woo la pʋam.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Da pã'asɛ-ya ya mẽŋa, ba ka pɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ. Nẽra n bʋrɛ bõn-sɛba la, bõn-bãma tɩ a wʋn lagsɛ.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ a mẽŋa yɛmleego boorɩ, a wʋn yẽ a yɛmleego yõorɔ n de kũm. La sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boorɩ la, a wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla n de vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Bɛla, tɩ da targɛ-ya la tõon-sõma tõoma. Tɩ sãn ɩta dee ka basɛ, wakatɛ la sãn wa paagɛ, tɩ wʋn yẽ a yõorɔ.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Bɛla, tɩn yẽ folle sɛka woo, tɩ tõm ẽŋɛ nẽrba wuu sõŋa, lan gãnnɩ wuu, tɩ ɩta sɛba n lagɩm na tõma bɔ Krisi sɩra la sõŋa.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya gʋlsgɔ biɛ kãr-bãna wã de la mam Apolɩ n gʋlsɛ la m mẽŋa nu'ugo.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: Sɛba n mõrgrɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya wã la, de la nẽrba n boorɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ nẽrsaalba zẽ'am. Ban ka boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ nãmsgɔ Krisi kũm dɔ-puurŋa la ĩyã mã'a yelle tɩ ba ɩta bɛla.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: Bãma sɛba n wãtɩ la mẽŋa ka ɩtɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, ba boorɩ la ya wã tɩ ba tã'agɛ wẽ yõ'ogɔ, bãma n botɩ ya wã la.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: La sãn dɛna mam, mam ka wẽ'erɩ m yõ'ogɔ la sɛla yelle, sãn dagna tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la mã'a yelle. Krisi kũm na ĩyã dũnia yɛla wuu ãn wʋ ba ki mɛ ka le sɔna mam, tɩ mam mẽ ãna wʋ mam ki mɛ, ka le bɔna dũnia yɛla pʋam.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: La dagɩ nẽra n wã, bɩɩ nẽra n ka wã n tarɩ yõorɔ, sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la tɩ tarɩ la vɔm paalga.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: Sɛba wuu n dolɩ nõor-ẽna wã, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ba sũ-mã'asʋm, dee zoe ba ninbãalga, bãma la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la wuu.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: Nẽra nẽra da le basɛ tɩ toogo paagɛ mam, se'ere n soe la, yisa sɛba tɩ mam tarɩ m ĩyã la pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ mam de la Azezi nẽra.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: M sɔbiisi, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya. Amina.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will. To the holy and faithful people in Christ Jesus in Ephesus.
local: Mam Apolɩ n de Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na, n gʋlsɛ gõn-ẽna wã bɔ'ɔra yãma Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Efɛɛzɩ dɛna sɩra dõma Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam na.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: Bless the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing that comes from heaven.
local: Tɩ pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la, ẽŋa n bɔ tɔ a Sɩa yel-sõnnɛ wuu n ze'ele saazuum, tõma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã.

eng: God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless in Gods presence before the creation of the world.
local: Nawẽnnɛ looge tõma la ẽŋa ĩyã, lan dẽŋɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ, tɩ tɩ welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba, ka tara yelle a nẽŋam.

eng: God destined us to be his adopted children through Jesus Christ because of his love. This was according to his goodwill and plan.
local: A nõŋlʋm na ĩyã tɩ a dẽŋɛ looge tõma tɩ tɩ lebge a kɔma Azezi-krisi ĩyã, wʋ a mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em na.

eng: and to honor his glorious grace that he has given to us freely through the Son whom he loves.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ tɩ pẽgɛ ẽ mɛ, a yel-sõnnɛ n zo'oge gãŋɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ zãŋa, tõma n de ayɩla la a Dayɔ-nõŋrɛ la ĩyã.

eng: We have been ransomed through his Sons blood, and we have forgiveness for our failures based on his overflowing grace.
local: La de la Krisi n ki tɩ a zɩɩm yese la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tõma tõon-be'ero taalɛ, tɩ tõma yẽ fãarɛ, tɩ la masɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n zo'oge pãɩ se'em na.

eng: which he poured over us with wisdom and understanding.
local: Yel-sõnnɛ ẽŋa n zo'oge la bɔ tõma la yɛm wuu, la bãŋrɛ wuu.

eng: God revealed his hidden design to us, which is according to his goodwill and the plan that he intended to accomplish through his Son.
local: Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tɩ tõma bãŋɛ ẽn boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em n daan sʋgɛ tɩ a looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ Krisi ĩyã la mɛ.

eng: This is what God planned for the climax of all times: to bring all things together in Christ, the things in heaven along with the things on earth.
local: Wakatɛ la sãn wa paagɛ, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na mɛ, a wʋn lagsɛ sɛla wuu n boe saazuum, la dũnia zuo, tɩ ba sakɛ Zuudãana ayɩla n de Krisi.

eng: We have also received an inheritance in Christ. We were destined by the plan of God, who accomplishes everything according to his design.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ sɛla woo ɩta wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em na, n dẽŋɛ looge tõma tɩ tɩ dɛna a nẽrba Krisi ĩyã.

eng: We are called to be an honor to Gods glory because we were the first to hope in Christ.
local: Tɩ tõma n dẽŋɛ tara pʋtẽ'erɛ la Krisi la, wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba pẽgɛ ẽ a na'am pãŋa la ĩyã.

eng: You too heard the word of truth in Christ, which is the good news of your salvation. You were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit because you believed in Christ.
local: Yãma n mẽ wõm sɩra kõ-yẽlga la, n basɛ tɩ ya bɔ Krisi sɩra yẽ fãarɛ, ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ya a Sɩa wʋ ẽn bĩŋe nõorɛ se'em na, Sɩa ẽŋa de la dãalgɔ pa'alɛ tɩ ya de la a nẽrba.

eng: The Holy Spirit is the down payment on our inheritance, which is applied toward our redemption as Gods own people, resulting in the honor of Gods glory.
local: A Sɩa la n de sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ sɛla woo tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɔ la, halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ a wʋn fãagɛ tõma wuu n de a nẽrba la ba'asɛ la, bõn-ẽŋa n wʋn basɛ tɩ tɩ pẽgɛ ẽ, a na'am pãŋa la ĩyã.

eng: Since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all Gods people, this is the reason that.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam n wõm yãma n bɔ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra, la yãma n tarɩ nõŋlʋm la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu se'em na.

eng: I dont stop giving thanks to God for you when I remember you in my prayers.
local: tɩ mam tẽra ya yelle m pʋ'ʋsgɔ pʋam, pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ ya ĩyã ka gɔ'ɔra.

eng: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, will give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation that makes God known to you.
local: Mam sosrɩ la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ n de Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, tɩ a bɔ ya a yɛm Sɩa tɩ a vẽlgɛ ya yɛm, tɩ ya bãŋɛ ẽn ãn se'em.

eng: I pray that the eyes of your heart will have enough light to see what is the hope of Gods call, what is the richness of Gods glorious inheritance among believers.
local: Mam ken sɔsra Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a botɩ ya pʋtẽ'era like tɩ ya bãŋɛ ẽn wi ya tɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla, dee bãŋɛ bõn-sõma sɛba tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la n ba ãna sõŋa, dee zo'oge se'em.

eng: and what is the overwhelming greatness of Gods power that is working among us believers. This power is conferred by the energy of Gods powerful strength.
local: Mam ken sɔsra ẽ tɩ a botɩ ya bãŋɛ a pãŋa la n ãn kãtɛ gãŋɛ se'em tɩ a tarɩ tõnna la tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la lɛ, wʋ a na'am pãn-kãtɛ n tõnnɩ tõon-kãra se'em na.

eng: Gods power was at work in Christ when God raised him from the dead and sat him at Gods right side in the heavens.
local: Pãn-kãtɛ ẽŋa n bɛla tɩ a dɩkɛ vo'oge Krisi botɩ a zĩ'ire a zʋʋgɔ bɔba saazuum Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: far above every ruler and authority and power and angelic power, any power that might be named not only now but in the future.
local: A botɩ Krisi n gãnna na'adõma wuu, la pãnsɩ dõma wuu, la kɛ'ɛndõma wuu, la ke'eŋo buuri wuu, ẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n gãnnɩ yʋ'ʋra wuu, la dagɩ dũnia ẽna pʋam mã'a, la ken dɛna la dũnia sɛka n wʋn wa'am na pʋam.

eng: God put everything under Christs feet and made him head of everything in the church.
local: Nawẽnnɛ basɛ mɛ tɩ Krisi sɔna sɛla woo, dee botɩ a dɛna a nẽrba wuu Zuudãana sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: which is his body. His body, the church, is the fullness of Christ, who fills everything in every way.
local: Krisi nẽrba la ãn wʋ a mẽŋa ĩyã la, ẽŋa n vɔa ba pʋam, la ẽŋa n boe zẽ'a woo, botɩ sɛla woo bɔna.

eng: At one time you were like a dead person because of the things you did wrong and your offenses against God.
local: Ya daan ãn wʋ ya ki mɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ya tõon-be'ero, la ya tuure la ĩyã.

eng: You used to live like people of this world. You followed the rule of a destructive spiritual power. This is the spirit of disobedience to Gods will that is now at work in persons whose lives are characterized by disobedience.
local: Yãma n daan ɩtɩ se'em na ãn wʋ dũnia nẽrba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em na, dɔla Asũtãana n de pãnsɩ dõma n boe saazuum naba la, ẽŋa n tõnnɩ na sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la.

eng: At one time you were like those persons. All of you used to do whatever felt good and whatever you thought you wanted so that you were children headed for punishment just like everyone else.
local: Tõma wuu daan lagɩm ãna wʋ bãma n ãn se'em na, tõma daan vɔa wʋ nẽrsaala yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em, ɩta tɩ ĩyã, la tɩ yɛm n boorɩ se'em n de be'em na. La tõma n daan ãn se'em na basɛ tɩ tɩ daan masɛ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ wʋ nẽrba basɛba la.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ tõma zo'oge mɛ, tɩ a zoe tõma ninbãalga zozo'e.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Tɩ baa tõma n daan ãn wʋ tɩ ki mɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero ĩyã la, a basɛ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na Krisi yẽ vɔm. La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã tɩ a fãagɛ ya.

eng: And God raised us up and seated us in the heavens with Christ Jesus.
local: La de la tõma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lagɩm tõma la Krisi vo'oge, basɛ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na ẽŋa zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: God did this to show future generations the greatness of his grace by the goodness that God has shown us in Christ Jesus.
local: A ẽŋɛ tõma sõŋa Krisi ĩyã, tɩ a pa'alɛ la nẽrba wakatɛ n sẽm a yel-sõnnɛ n zo'oge gãŋɛ se'em.

eng: You are saved by Gods grace because of your faith. This salvation is Gods gift. Its not something you possessed.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ la ĩyã tɩ ya yẽ fãarɛ, yan bɔ Krisi sɩra la ĩyã, la dagɩ ya mẽŋa pãŋa, la de la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a.

eng: Its not something you did that you can be proud of.
local: La dagɩ ya tõoma ĩyã, tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka da pẽgɛ a mẽŋa.

eng: Instead, we are Gods accomplishment, created in Christ Jesus to do good things. God planned for these good things to be the way that we live our lives.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n nãam tɔ, botɩ tɩ lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla, tõnna tõon-sõma tɩ a põn dẽŋɛ mãasʋm tɩ tɩ wa tõm na.

eng: So remember that once you were Gentiles by physical descent, who were called uncircumcised by Jews who are physically circumcised.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya yan daan ãn se'em na, ba ka dɔgɛ ya tɩ ya dɛna Zifdõma buuri, Zifdõma n wãtɩ la wi'iri ya tɩ a ka wãtɩ dõma. Ba yetɩ bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, bãma n wãtɩ la ĩyã, la bõn-ẽŋa de la sɛla tɩ nẽrba ɩtɩ ba ĩyã pʋam mã'a.

eng: At that time you were without Christ. You were aliens rather than citizens of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of Gods promise. In this world you had no hope and no God.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya tɩ wakat-sɛka tɩ ya ka boe la Krisi la, ya daan de la sãama la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, ka tara nõorɛ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la. Ya daan vɔa la dũnia wã zuo ka mina Nawẽnnɛ, dee ka tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla.

eng: But now, thanks to Christ Jesus, you who once were so far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
local: Ya daan zãagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, a zɩɩm na n yese la basɛ tɩ ya yãŋa lɛm ẽ mɛ.

eng: Christ is our peace. He made both Jews and Gentiles into one group. With his body, he broke down the barrier of hatred that divided us.
local: La de la Krisi n botɩ tɩ tara sũ-mã'asʋm, ẽŋa n basɛ tɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma lebge buuri ayɩla. Ẽŋa n ki yese be'em n ãn wʋ lalga.

eng: He canceled the detailed rules of the Law so that he could create one new person out of the two groups, making peace.
local: bɔna tɩ tẽŋasʋka welge to tɩ tɩ sisra taaba la, dee basɛ tɩ Amoyisi lɔɔ, la yel-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ ba ɩta la ka le tõnna, botɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma naagɛ taaba lagɩm na ẽŋa lebge buur-paalga ayɩla tara sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba.

eng: He reconciled them both as one body to God by the cross, which ended the hostility to God.
local: Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo la, a lagɩm ba tɩ ba lebge la buuri ayɩla naagɛ ba la Nawẽnnɛ botɩ ba ka le sisra taaba.

eng: When he came, he announced the good news of peace to you who were far away from God and to those who were near.
local: Krisi wa'am wa mõolɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm kõ-yẽlga bɔ yãma sɛba n daan zãagɛ, la tõma sɛba n lɛm na.

eng: We both have access to the Father through Christ by the one Spirit.
local: Krisi ĩyã tɩ tõma wuu tara sore wʋn lɛm tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ayɩla n boe la tɩ wuu lɛ la ĩyã.

eng: So now you are no longer strangers and aliens. Rather, you are fellow citizens with Gods people, and you belong to Gods household.
local: Bɛla, ya ka le dɛna sãama, bɩɩ nẽrba n ze'ele zẽ'a ayẽma, ya de la buuri ayɩla la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, ya de la a yire nẽrba.

eng: As Gods household, you are built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
local: Yãma po bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n ãn wʋ deego la mẽeŋa pʋam. Azezi Tõntõnɩba, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n de deego la ẽbrɛ. Tɩ Azezi-krisi mẽŋa dɛna kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.

eng: The whole building is joined together in him, and it grows up into a temple that is dedicated to the Lord.
local: Ẽŋa n basɛ tɩ deego la wuu zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ lɛbgra Wẽnde-kãtɛ ãna sõŋa bɔ Zuudãana.

eng: Christ is building you into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
local: Yãma n lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla la, ya mẽ po bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ deego la tɩ a boe mĩ mẽta la mẽeŋa pʋam, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa Sɩa kẽ'era mĩ.

eng: This is why I, Paul, am a prisoner of Christ for you Gentiles.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ. Mam boe la yʋ'a deem Azezi-krisi yelle, mam n mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga bɔ yãma bu-zãŋsɩ la ĩyã.

eng: Youve heard, of course, about the responsibility to distribute Gods grace, which God gave to me for you, right?.
local: Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya wõm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ a yel-sõnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ẽŋɛ la mam nu'usum tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ yãma.

eng: God showed me his secret plan in a revelation, as I mentioned briefly before .
local: Nawẽnnɛ puke a yelle n sʋgɛ la pa'alɛ mam mɛ, wʋ mam gʋlsɛ se'em fẽe bɔ ya la.

eng: when you read this, youll understand my insight into the secret plan about Christ.
local: Ya sãn karẽŋɛ mam n gʋlsɛ se'em na, ya wʋn yẽ mam n bãŋɛ Krisi yelle n daan sʋgɛ la võorɛ n ãn se'em.

eng: Earlier generations didnt know this hidden plan that God has now revealed to his holy apostles and prophets through the Spirit.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka puke ẽ pa'alɛ kʋrʋm dõma, la lɛɛlɛ wã a botɩ a Sɩa la puke ẽ pa'alɛ la sɛba tɩ a tõm, la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba.

eng: This plan is that the Gentiles would be coheirs and parts of the same body, and that they would share with the Jews in the promises of God in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Yel-ẽŋa n sʋgɛ la de la kõ-yẽlga mõolgɔ n botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ wuu lagɩm na Zifdõma tara pʋrɛ, lagɩm taaba dɛna ayɩla, dee lagɩm tɔ'ɔra sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

eng: I became a servant of the gospel because of the grace that God showed me through the exercise of his power.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a tɩ a botɩ mam dɛna a tõntõnna mõola a kõ-yẽlga la, la a pãŋa.

eng: God gave his grace to me, the least of all Gods people, to preach the good news about the immeasurable riches of Christ to the Gentiles.
local: Mam n de nẽra n pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la wuu pʋam, la bɛla wuu Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la mam yel-sõn-ẽŋa tɩ m mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ tɩ ba bãŋɛ bõn-sõma n zo'oge gãŋɛ tɩ nẽrba ka tãna wʋn bãŋɛ ba'asɛ la.

eng: God sent me to reveal the secret plan that had been hidden since the beginning of time by God, who created everything.
local: dee botɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em n daan sʋgɛ la. Ẽŋa n nãam sɛla woo la, sʋgɛ bõn-ẽŋa mɛ halɩ yʋʋma yʋʋma wa paam zĩna.

eng: Gods purpose is now to show the rulers and powers in the heavens the many different varieties of his wisdom through the church.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Azezi nẽrba la wʋn basɛ tɩ kɛ'ɛndõma, la pãnsɩ dõma n boe saazuum bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n de to'oto'ore se'em wuu.

eng: This was consistent with the plan he had from the beginning of time that he accomplished through Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: La doose la Zuudãana Azezi-krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla masɛ wʋ ẽn looge a yɛm se'em halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la.

eng: In Christ we have bold and confident access to God through faith in him.
local: Tõma n bɔ Krisi sɩra lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla la, sore boe mɛ tɩ tõma tãna wʋn paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɩ dabeem ka tara tõma.

eng: So then, I ask you not to become discouraged by what Im suffering for you, which is your glory.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam sɔsra ya tɩ ya da basɛ tɩ ya bʋraanɛ bo'oge mam nãmsrɩ ya yelle la ĩyã, mam nãmsgɔ la de la yãma zu-zẽkrɛ.

eng: This is why I kneel before the Father.
local: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam ka dũma tẽŋa tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam.

eng: Every ethnic group in heaven or on earth is recognized by him.
local: ẽŋa n botɩ sɛla wuu n boe saazuum la tẽŋa zuo vɔa wʋn dɛna sɔdõma la ba yir-dõma wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ se'em na.

eng: I ask that he will strengthen you in your inner selves from the riches of his glory through the Spirit.
local: Mam sosrɩ ẽ tɩ a Sɩa bɔ ya pãŋa ya sũure pʋam zozo'e wʋ a na'am pãn-kãtɛ la n zo'oge se'em na.

eng: I ask that Christ will live in your hearts through faith. As a result of having strong roots in love.
local: tɩ Krisi wʋn bɔna ya sũure pʋam, yan bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ya wʋn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ tara nõŋlʋm zozo'e.

eng: I ask that youll have the power to grasp loves width and length, height and depth, together with all believers.
local: Tɩ yãma wʋn naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu tã'agɛ bãŋɛ Krisi nõŋlʋm n zo'oge se'em, ẽn yalgɛ, la ẽn wɔgɛ, la ẽn ãn ko'ogo se'em.

eng: I ask that youll know the love of Christ that is beyond knowledge so that you will be filled entirely with the fullness of God.
local: la ya bãŋɛ a nõŋlʋm na n gãŋɛ tɩ nẽrba ka tãna wʋn bãŋɛ ba'asɛ la. Tɩ bõn-sɛba wuu n ze'etɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wʋn pɩrɛ ya.

eng: Glory to God, who is able to do far beyond all that we could ask or imagine by his power at work within us.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n tã ɩta sɛla woo zo'oge gãnna tõma n sosrɩ se'em, bɩɩ tõma n tẽ'esrɩ se'em, ẽŋa pãŋa n tõnnɩ la tõma lɛ la.

eng: glory to him in the church and in Christ Jesus for all generations, forever and always. Amen.
local: ẽŋa n masɛ tɩ a yẽta nã'asgɔ Krisi nẽrba la zẽ'am Azezi-krisi ĩyã zamãana woo zamãana wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: Therefore, as a prisoner for the Lord, I encourage you to live as people worthy of the call you received from God.
local: Mam n boe yʋ'a deem Zuudãana doosgo yelle la, mam sosrɩ ya tɩ ya dɔla sorɔɔgɔ la sõŋa, wʋ lan masɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya tɩ ya ãna se'em na.

eng: Conduct yourselves with all humility, gentleness, and patience. Accept each other with love.
local: La masɛ mɛ tɩ ya sikra ya mẽŋa sɛla woo pʋam, tara sũ-bʋgsʋm, la sũ-mã'arɛ, la ya tara nõŋlʋm da isgra sũure la taaba.

eng: and make an effort to preserve the unity of the Spirit with the peace that ties you together.
local: Mõrgɛ-ya tara nõ-yẽnnɛ n ze'etɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am, naagɛ-ya taaba tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: You are one body and one spirit, just as God also called you in one hope.
local: Azezi nẽrba de la ĩyã ayɩla, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ayɩla mã'a bɔna, la Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya tɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura bõn-sɛba la de la bõn-yẽnnɛ.

eng: There is one Lord, one faith, one baptism.
local: La Zuudãana ayɩla n boe, la tõma n bɔ sɩra se'em na de la ayɩla, la ko'om misga de la ayɩla.

eng: and one God and Father of all, who is over all, through all, and in all.
local: la Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n de tɩ wuu Sɔ n soe sɛla woo, tɩ a pãŋa tara sɛla woo tõnna, dee bɔna sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: God has given his grace to each one of us measured out by the gift that is given by Christ.
local: La tõma nẽra woo to'oge yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a masɛ wʋ Krisi n bɔ tɩ se'em.

eng: Thats why scripture says, When he climbed up to the heights, he captured prisoners, and he gave gifts to people .
local: Bɛla tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽn daan zom saazuum na, a daan yãŋɛ a bɛ'ɛba mɛ yõgɛ ba, dee bɔ nẽrba bɔ'a.».

eng: What does the phrase he climbed up mean if it doesnt mean that he had first gone down into the lower regions, the earth?.
local: Lan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «A zom saazuum na», a võorɛ de la bẽm? La võorɛ de tɩ a daan dẽŋɛ sige mɛ halɩ ka paagɛ tẽŋa wã pʋam pʋam.

eng: The one who went down is the same one who climbed up above all the heavens so that he might fill everything.
local: Sɛka n daan sige la, la de la ẽŋa n ken zom saazuum-zuum tɩ a sɔna sɛla woo.

eng: He gave some apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers.
local: La de la ẽŋa n bɔ tɩ bɔ'a, botɩ sɛba dɛna a Tõntõnɩba, tɩ basɛba dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, tɩ basɛba mõola a kõ-yẽlga la bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, tɩ basɛba sɔna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba bɩsra, bɩɩ dɛna karẽnsãandõma.

eng: His purpose was to equip Gods people for the work of serving and building up the body of Christ.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ ba mãasʋm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tɩ ba tõnna Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, tɩ Krisi nẽrba n de a ĩyã la bɩta dee pa'asra.

eng: until we all reach the unity of faith and knowledge of Gods Son. Gods goal is for us to become mature adults—to be fully grown, measured by the standard of the fullness of Christ.
local: halɩ tɩ tɩ wʋn wa lagɩm taaba bɔ sɩra dɛna ayɩla, bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, lebge nẽrba n bɩ ba'asɛ paagɛ wʋ Krisi n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam se'em na.

eng: As a result, we arent supposed to be infants any longer who can be tossed and blown around by every wind that comes from teaching with deceitful scheming and the tricks people play to deliberately mislead others.
local: Bɛla tɩ kãn le ãna wʋ kɔma tɩ nẽrba zãsnɩ zãsŋɔ buuri to'oto'ore pã'asra ba, ɩta yɛm tɩ ba botɩ ba tuuge, wʋ kʋsebgo, la ko-mĩ'isi n wẽ'erɩ õorŋɔ tɩ a yɛgna se'em na.

eng: Instead, by speaking the truth with love, lets grow in every way into Christ.
local: La de la tɩn tɔgrɩ sɩra, la nõŋlʋm na ĩyã, tɩ tɩ bɩta sɛla woo pʋam wʋ ka paagɛ Krisi n de Zuudãana la.

eng: who is the head. The whole body grows from him, as it is joined and held together by all the supporting ligaments. The body makes itself grow in that it builds itself up with love as each one does its part.
local: La de la ẽŋa n de zuugo la n botɩ a nẽrba n ãn wʋ ĩyã la lagɩm taaba tõnna, wʋ kõb-suglsi n botɩ ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore toŋe taaba, tɩ zẽ'a woo tõnna wʋ lan masɛ se'em, tɩ ĩyã la wuu bɩta sõŋa sõŋa la nõŋlʋm.

eng: So Im telling you this, and I insist on it in the Lord: you shouldnt live your life like the Gentiles anymore. They base their lives on pointless thinking.
local: Bɛla tɩ mam yɛta ya la ninmõ'orɛ la Zuudãana nõorɛ, tɩ ya da le ɩta wʋ sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em na, bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na, ãn wʋ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em n ka tarɩ võorɛ la.

eng: and they are in the dark in their reasoning. They are disconnected from Gods life because of their ignorance and their closed hearts.
local: Ba yɛm na boe la lika pʋam, la ba ka vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, ban kɛ'ɛm ba sũure ka bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ la ĩyã.

eng: They are people who lack all sense of right and wrong, and who have turned themselves over to doing whatever feels good and to practicing every sort of corruption along with greed.
local: Ba zuugo ka zɛɛrɩ la tõon-be'ero ɩrgɔ, ba tõnnɩ la yalsɩ, la be'em buuri wuu, ba boorɩ la tõon-dẽgrɔ mã'a.

eng: But you didnt learn that sort of thing from Christ.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, ba ka zãsʋm ya Krisi yelle bɛla.

eng: Since you really listened to him and you were taught how the truth is in Jesus.
local: Yãma n wõm Azezi-krisi yelle, dee tɩ ba zãsʋm yãma n de a nẽrba sɩra n ze'ele a zẽ'am na.

eng: change the former way of life that was part of the person you once were, corrupted by deceitful desires.
local: dẽnɩ tɩ ya basɛ yãma n daan vɔa se'em kʋrʋm na. Basɛ-ya vɔm kɛka, la a yɛmleego la n boorɩ sɛla n de be'em n wʋn pã'asɛ ya botɩ ya sã'am na.

eng: Instead, renew the thinking in your mind by the Spirit.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa teege ya pʋtẽ'era tɩ ya lebge paalsɩ.

eng: and clothe yourself with the new person created according to Gods image in justice and true holiness.
local: Vɔa-ya vɔm paalga wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam ya tɩ ya ãna wʋ a mẽŋa n ãn se'em na. Vɔm ẽŋa de la sɛla n masɛ, la pʋpeelem n ze'ele sɩra pʋam.

eng: Therefore, after you have gotten rid of lying, Each of you must tell the truth to your neighbor because we are parts of each other in the same body.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, basɛ-ya põmpɔrŋɔ, tɩ ya nẽra woo tɔgra sɩra bɔ'ɔra a tadãana, tɩ tɩ wuu lagɩm dɛna la ĩyã ayɩla la taaba.

eng: Be angry without sinning. Dont let the sun set on your anger.
local: Ya sũure sãn isge, da tõm-ya tõon-be'ero, la ya da basɛ tɩ wẽnnɛ lui dee tɩ ya sũure la ka sige.

eng: Dont provide an opportunity for the devil.
local: Da bɔ-ya Asũtãana folle.

eng: Thieves should no longer steal. Instead, they should go to work, using their hands to do good so that they will have something to share with whoever is in need.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n daan zũuri, a da le zũura, a mõrgɛ a mẽŋa tõnna sɛla n masɛ, tɩ a wʋn tara sɛla sõŋɛ sɛba n boorɩ sõŋrɛ.

eng: Dont let any foul words come out of your mouth. Only say what is helpful when it is needed for building up the community so that it benefits those who hear what you say.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ yetɔg-be'ero yese ya nõorʋm, tɔgra-ya yetɔg-sõma n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽrba bʋraanɛ, tɩ la sõŋra nẽr-sɛba n selsrɩ la, dee ɩta ba sõŋa.

eng: Dont make the Holy Spirit of God unhappy—you were sealed by him for the day of redemption.
local: Da ẽŋɛ-ya sɛla sã'am Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sũure, tɩ la de la ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ bɔ ya tɩ la dɛna dãalgɔ pa'alɛ tɩ daarɛ n sẽm tɩ ẽŋa wʋn fãagɛ ya fasɩ fasɩ.

eng: Put aside all bitterness, losing your temper, anger, shouting, and slander, along with every other evil.
local: Basɛ-ya sũ-toogo, la sũ-yĩire, la sũ-isgre, la nõke'ene, la taaba tʋʋrɛ, la pʋtʋʋlgɔ wuu.

eng: Be kind, compassionate, and forgiving to each other, in the same way God forgave you in Christ.
local: Ɩta-ya taaba sõŋa, la ya tara taaba zĩile, basra-ya taaba tõon-be'ero taalɛ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ Krisi ĩyã la.

eng: Therefore, imitate God like dearly loved children.
local: Yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma tɩ a nõŋɛ la, ãna-ya wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na.

eng: Live your life with love, following the example of Christ, who loved us and gave himself for us. He was a sacrificial offering that smelled sweet to God.
local: Ɩta-ya sɛla woo la nõŋlʋm wʋ Krisi n nõŋɛ tɩ se'em dɩkɛ a vɔm bɔ tõma ĩyã, tɩ la ãna wʋ kãabgɔ n tarɩ yũuŋ-sõŋɔ paara Nawẽnnɛ yɛm na.

eng: Sexual immorality, and any kind of impurity or greed, shouldnt even be mentioned among you, which is right for holy persons.
local: Yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la, la ka masɛ tɩ yalsɩ, la tõon-dẽgrɔ buuri wuu, la bõnɔ zozo'e yɛmleego yelle pʋrɛ ya tẽŋasʋka.

eng: Obscene language, silly talk, or vulgar jokes arent acceptable for believers. Instead, there should be thanksgiving.
local: La ken ka masɛ tɩ ya tɔgra yetɔg-yooro, la yetɔg-be'ero, la yetɔg-gãlsɩ, la de la ya pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: Because you know for sure that persons who are sexually immoral, impure, or greedy—which happens when things become gods—those persons wont inherit the kingdom of Christ and God.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya sõŋa, tɩ nẽra n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, bɩɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ dãana, bɩɩ nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ bõnɔ zozo'e kãn yẽ sɛla sɛla Krisi, la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam. La sɛka n nõŋɛ bõnɔ zozo'e ãn wʋ nẽra n kãabrɩ baga dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ la.

eng: Nobody should deceive you with stupid ideas. Gods anger comes down on those who are disobedient because of this kind of thing.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽra pã'asɛ ya la yetɔg-yooro, tɩ la de la bõn-bãma n isgri Nawẽnnɛ sũure tɩ a sɩbgra sɛba n zagsɛ a nõorɛ la.

eng: So you shouldnt have anything to do with them.
local: Da lagɩm-ya la nẽr-bãma.

eng: You were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord, so live your life as children of light.
local: Kʋrʋm, ya daan boe la lika pʋam, la lɛɛlɛ wã, yan lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, ya boe la peelem pʋam. Bɛla, ɩta-ya wʋ nẽrba n boe peelem pʋam n ɩtɩ se'em na.

eng: Light produces fruit that consists of every sort of goodness, justice, and truth.
local: Peelem tõoma de la sɛla woo n ãn sõŋa, la sɛla n masɛ, la sɩra.

eng: Therefore, test everything to see whats pleasing to the Lord.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya sɛla n paarɩ Zuudãana yɛm bɔkɛ.

eng: and dont participate in the unfruitful actions of darkness. Instead, you should reveal the truth about them.
local: Da po tõnna-ya tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrba tõnnɩ lika pʋam n ka tarɩ yõorɔ la, tõn pa'alɛ-ya tɩ ba ka ãn sõŋa.

eng: Its embarrassing to even talk about what certain persons do in secret.
local: Nẽr-bãma n sʋgɛ ɩta sɛla la de la yãnnɛ halɩ, tɩ nẽra kãn saagɛ tɔgɛ a yelle.

eng: But everything exposed to the light is revealed by the light.
local: Ya sãn pa'alɛ tɩ ba ka ãn sõŋa, nẽrba wʋn yẽ tɩ sɛla woo de la vẽelga.

eng: Everything that is revealed by the light is light. Therefore, it says, Wake up, sleeper! Get up from the dead, and Christ will shine on you. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛka woo n de vẽelga boe la peelem pʋam. Bɛla tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Fõn sɛka n gĩsri la, nẽegɛ, isge kũm na tẽŋasʋka tɩ Krisi peelem nẽegɛ fɔ, bɔ fʋ vɔm.».

eng: So be careful to live your life wisely, not foolishly.
local: Guuse ya mẽŋa sõŋa la yan vɔa se'em na. Da ɩta-ya wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ yɛm, ɩta-ya wʋ yɛm dõma la.

eng: Take advantage of every opportunity because these are evil times.
local: Dɩkɛ-ya folle tɩ ya tarɩ la wuu tõm tõon-sõma, tɩ wakatɛ la de la tõon-be'ero wakatɛ.

eng: Because of this, dont be ignorant, but understand the Lords will.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, da dɛna-ya geto, bãŋɛ-ya Zuudãana la n boorɩ se'em.

eng: Dont get drunk on wine, which produces depravity. Instead, be filled with the Spirit in the following ways.
local: Da yũura-ya dãam bugra, tɩ a sã'anɩ la nẽra. Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sɔna ya, tõnna la ya lɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: speak to each other with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; sing and make music to the Lord in your hearts.
local: Naagɛ-ya taaba yõona Nawẽnnɛ yõoma, yõona-ya yõom-sõma buuri wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ya, nã'asra Zuudãana la ya sũure wuu.

eng: always give thanks to God the Father for everything in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ sɛla woo pʋam wakatɛ wuu, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ.

eng: and submit to each other out of respect for Christ.
local: Sakra-ya taaba, yãma n nã'asrɩ Krisi la ĩyã.

eng: For example, wives should submit to their husbands as if to the Lord.
local: Pɔgsɩ, sakra-ya ya sɩrba wʋ yãma n sakrɩ Zuudãana se'em na.

eng: A husband is the head of his wife like Christ is head of the church, that is, the savior of the body.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sɩra n de pɔka la zuudãana, wʋ Krisi n de a nẽrba la Zuudãana la. Krisi nẽrba la de la a ĩyã, tɩ Krisi dɛna ba Fãagra.

eng: So wives submit to their husbands in everything like the church submits to Christ.
local: Krisi nẽrba n sakrɩ ẽ se'em na, pɔgsɩ mẽ sakra ba sɩrba bɛla sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: As for husbands, love your wives just like Christ loved the church and gave himself for her.
local: Sɩrba, nõŋɛ-ya ya pɔgba wʋ Krisi n nõŋɛ a nẽrba dɩkɛ a mẽŋa vɔm bɔ ba ĩyã la.

eng: He did this to make her holy by washing her in a bath of water with the word.
local: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ a malgɛ ba la a yetɔga, tɩ ba ãna sõŋa wʋ ban sɔɔrɩ ĩyã la ko'om se'em na, botɩ ba welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba.

eng: He did this to present himself with a splendid church, one without any sort of stain or wrinkle on her clothes, but rather one that is holy and blameless.
local: Tɩ ba da tara dẽgrɔ, la be'em, la sɛla n ka ãn sõŋa, ba tara pʋpeelem ka tara dõrga, tɩ a wʋn tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am la nã'asgɔ.

eng: Thats how husbands ought to love their wives—in the same way as they do their own bodies. Anyone who loves his wife loves himself.
local: Bɛla mẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ sɩrba nõŋɛ ba pɔgba wʋ ba mẽŋa ĩyã la. Sɛka n nõŋɛ a pɔga, nõŋɛ la a mẽŋa.

eng: No one ever hates his own body, but feeds it and takes care of it just like Christ does for the church.
local: Nẽra ka boe n sisri a mẽŋa ĩyã, a diisri ẽ mɛ, bɩsra a yelle sõŋa wʋ Krisi n ɩtɩ se'em bɔ'ɔra a nẽrba la.

eng: because we are parts of his body.
local: tõma n de a ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la ĩyã.

eng: This is why a man will leave his father and mother and be united with his wife, and the two of them will be one body .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bʋraaga wʋn basɛ a sɔ, la a ma, dee lagɩm na a pɔga, tɩ bãma bayi lebge ĩyã ayɩla.».

eng: Marriage is a significant allegory, and Im applying it to Christ and the church.
local: Ẽna de la yel-kãtɛ n sʋgɛ yetɔg-bãna pʋam, mam n tɔgrɩ la de la Krisi, la a nẽrba la yelle.

eng: In any case, as for you individually, each one of you should love his wife as himself, and wives should respect their husbands.
local: La, la sãn dɛna yãma, nẽra woo nõŋɛ a pɔga wʋ a mẽŋa. Tɩ pɔka woo mẽ nanna a sɩra.

eng: As for children, obey your parents in the Lord, because it is right.
local: Kɔma, sakra-ya ya dɔgrɩba nõorɛ Zuudãana la ĩyã, tɩ bɛla n masɛ.

eng: The commandment Honor your father and mother is the first one with a promise attached.
local: «Nanna fɔ sɔ la fʋ ma.» Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ a nõorɛ tɩ ba ɩta bõn-sɛba la wuu pʋam, ẽna n de pɔspɔsɩ bõnɔ n lagɩm na nõorɛ bĩŋre.

eng: so that things will go well for you, and you will live for a long time in the land .
local: a yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn ɩta bɛla, fʋ wʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ, la yõ-woko dũnia zuo.».

eng: As for parents, dont provoke your children to anger, but raise them with discipline and instruction about the Lord.
local: La yãma n de sɔdõma la, da ɩta ya kɔma sɛla n wʋn botɩ ba sũure yĩige, uge-ya ba, kã'an ba, dee zãsʋm ba wʋ Zuudãana n boorɩ se'em.

eng: As for slaves, obey your human masters with fear and trembling and with sincere devotion to Christ.
local: Yamsɩ, sakra-ya sɛba n soe ya dũnia zuo wã la ya sũure wuu, nanna-ya ba dee zɔta ba, wʋ yan sakrɩ Krisi la.

eng: Dont work to make yourself look good and try to flatter people, but act like slaves of Christ carrying out Gods will from the heart.
local: Da ɩta-ya bɛla ba nifum mã'a tɩ ba pẽgɛ ya. Yãma n de Krisi yamsɩ la, ɩta-ya Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em la ya sũure wuu.

eng: Serve your owners enthusiastically, as though you were serving the Lord and not human beings.
local: Tõnna-ya ba tõoma la bɔ'ɔra ba la yɛmleego, wʋ ya tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra la Zuudãana la, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba ĩyã.

eng: You know that the Lord will reward every person who does what is right, whether that person is a slave or a free person.
local: La ya mina tɩ nẽra woo wʋn yẽ a tõonɛ yõorɔ Zuudãana Krisi zẽ'am masɛ wʋ tõon-sõŋɔ sɛka tɩ a tõm na, a sãn dɛna la nẽra yamŋa bɩɩ a sãn dagna nẽra yamŋa.

eng: As for masters, treat your slaves in the same way. Stop threatening them, because you know that both you and your slaves have a master in heaven. He doesnt distinguish between people on the basis of status.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma n de zuudãandõma la, yãma mẽ de la ya ɩta sõŋa bɔ'ɔra ya yamsɩ la, da gi'ira-ya ba, mina-ya tɩ yãma, la bãma wuu lagɩm tarɩ la Zuudãana ayɩla n boe saazuum n ka bɔkrɩ nẽrba.

eng: Finally, be strengthened by the Lord and his powerful strength.
local: Sɛla n deege, keŋe ya mẽŋa yẽ Zuudãana pãŋa ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ a doosgo la pʋam, a pãn-kãtɛ la ĩyã.

eng: Put on Gods armor so that you can make a stand against the tricks of the devil.
local: Yɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ wuu, tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, tɩ Asũtãana da yãŋɛ pã'asɛ ya.

eng: We arent fighting against human enemies but against rulers, authorities, forces of cosmic darkness, and spiritual powers of evil in the heavens.
local: Tõma zɛbrɛ ka boe la nẽrsaalba, tõma zɛbrɩ la Asũtãana kɛ'ɛndõma, la a pãnsɩ dõma, la dũnia lika tõon-be'ero na'adõma, la kʋlkã'arsɩ wuu n boe saazuum.

eng: Therefore, pick up the full armor of God so that you can stand your ground on the evil day and after you have done everything possible to still stand.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, dɩkɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ la wuu yɛ, tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ ze'ele zɛbɛ be'em wakatɛ. La zɛbrɛ la sãn wa ba'asɛ, ya wʋn ken zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

eng: So stand with the belt of truth around your waist, justice as your breastplate.
local: Bɛla, mãasʋm-ya zɛ, tara-ya sɩra tɩ a ãna wʋ sagãnnɛ tɩ ya lʋ ya sɩa la, la ya ɩta sɛla n masɛ tɩ la ãna wʋ kuto fuugo tɩ ya yɛ pĩ ya yõ'ogɔ.

eng: and put shoes on your feet so that you are ready to spread the good news of peace.
local: tara-ya yɛmleego mõola sũ-mã'asʋm kõ-yẽlga la wʋ tagra tɩ ya piti sẽnna la.

eng: Above all, carry the shield of faith so that you can extinguish the flaming arrows of the evil one.
local: La ya bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra sɛla woo pʋam tɩ la gu'ura ya la Asũtãana pɛɛma n ãn wʋ bugum na, tɩ ya tã'agɛ kĩŋe ba.

eng: Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is Gods word.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ fãarɛ tɩ ya tarɩ la ãna wʋ kuto zuu-wannɛ gu'ura ya, dee dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n ãn wʋ sʋkɔbgɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ya la zɛbɛ.

eng: Offer prayers and petitions in the Spirit all the time. Stay alert by hanging in there and praying for all believers.
local: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɛla woo pʋam, pʋ'ʋsra-ya dee sɔsra ẽ wakatɛ wuu la a Sɩa pãŋa. Nargɛ-ya pãŋa pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu ĩyã da basɛ.

eng: As for me, pray that when I open my mouth, Ill get a message that confidently makes this secret plan of the gospel known.
local: La ya mẽ pʋ'ʋsra mam ĩyã tɩ m sãn yetɩ m tɔgɛ, a wʋn ẽŋɛ yetɔga m nõorʋm tɩ m tã'agɛ mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga n sʋgɛ la, la bʋraanɛ tɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ a võorɛ.

eng: Im an ambassador in chains for the sake of the gospel. Pray so that the Lord will give me the confidence to say what I have to say.
local: La de la kõ-yẽlga ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm mam tɩ m mõolɛ, bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam bɔna yʋ'a deem. Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ m wʋn tã'agɛ mõolɛ la bʋraanɛ wʋ lan masɛ se'em.

eng: Tychicus, my loved brother and faithful servant of the Lord, can inform you about my situation and what Im doing.
local: Mam boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la mam n ãn se'em, la mam n ɩtɩ se'em. Tɩ sɔbi-nõŋrɛ Atisiki n de Zuudãana tõntõnna n de sɩra dãana la, wʋn pa'alɛ ya mam yelle wuu.

eng: Ive sent him for this reason—so that you will know about us. He can reassure you.
local: Mam tõm ẽ ya zẽ'am na bɛla ĩyã, tɩ ya bãŋɛ tõma yɛla n ãn se'em, dee tɩ a wʋn keŋe ya sũure.

eng: May there be peace with the brothers and sisters as well as love with the faith that comes from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
