eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Bɛla, fʋ sãn dɛna mam sɩrɛ, fʋ to'oge ẽ wʋ fõn wʋn to'oge mam mẽŋa se'em na.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: La a sãn ẽŋɛ fʋ tuuge, bɩɩ a sãn tara fʋ sãnnɛ, basɛ tɩ la dɛna mam sãnnɛ.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: Mam Apolɩ n gʋlsɛ wãna la mam mẽŋa nu'ugo yetɩ: Mam wʋn yɔ fɔ. Mam kãn tẽegɛ fɔ tɩ fʋ mẽŋa tarɩ mam sãnnɛ, la de la mam n sõŋɛ fɔ tɩ fʋ yẽ vɔm.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Ẽe, m sɔbia, ẽŋɛ yel-ẽna bɔ mam, Zuudãana la ĩyã. Botɩ m sũure mã'agɛ Krisi ĩyã.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: Mam n mi tɩ fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em na ĩyã tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ fɔ, la mam mi tɩ fʋ wʋn põn ẽŋɛ gãŋɛ mam n yele fʋ se'em na mɛ.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: La mam sosrɩ tɩ fʋ ken mãasʋm gã'argɔ zẽ'a bɔ ma, tɩ mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã, ba wʋn basɛ mam tɩ m leme wa'am ya zẽ'am.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: A'epafrasɩ n lagɩm na mam bɔna yʋ'a deem Azezi-krisi yelle la pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: La mam tõntõn-taaba, Aluki la Amarkɩ, la Aristarkɩ, la Ademasɩ mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Kʋrʋm, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n tɔgɛ nõorɛ zozo'e bɔ tɩ yaabdõma, la yetɔga buuri to'oto'ore.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã n de ba'asgɔ wakatɛ la, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a Dayʋa la n tɔgɛ bɔ tɔ. La doose la ẽŋa tɩ a nãam dũnia la saazuum wuu, la a botɩ ẽŋa n sɔna sɛla woo.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: Ẽŋa n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa wa pa'alɛ tɔ, a ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n ãn se'em na. La a yetɔga la tarɩ pãŋa mɛ botɩ sɛla woo bɔna a zẽ'a. Ẽŋa n yese nẽrsaalba tõon-be'ero tɩ ba lebge sõŋa la poorʋm, a sẽŋɛ ka zẽa la Naba Wẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ saazuum-zuum.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ tɩ a gãnna malɛkadõma halɩ zozo'e dee bɔ ẽ yʋ'ʋrɛ n gãnnɩ malɛkadõma yʋ'ʋra.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka yele malɛka baa ayɩla yetɩ: «Fõn de mam Dayʋa, zĩna mam lebge la fʋ Sɔ.» La a ken ka yele malɛka yetɩ: «Mam n wʋn dɛna fʋ Sɔ, tɩ fʋ dɛna mam Dayʋa.».

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: La ẽn daan tõm a bi-kẽŋa la dũnia pʋam na, a yele yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ malɛkadõma wuu nã'asra ẽ.».

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: La sãn dɛna malɛkadõma la yelle, a tɔgɛ yetɩ, ba de la ẽŋa tõntõnɩba, ẽŋa basɛ tɩ ba ãna wʋ kʋsebgo la, dee basɛ tɩ ba ãna wʋ bug-zɩlɩsɩ la.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: La a yele a Dayʋa la yelle yetɩ: «Fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ, fʋ na'am na wʋn bɔna la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Fʋ wʋn sɔna fʋ so'olʋm na la sɩra.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: Fõn nõŋɛ la sɛla n masɛ, dee sisra be'em. Bɛla n soe tɩ fʋ Nawẽnnɛ welge fʋ to'ore, bɔ fɔ pẽka, la sũ-yẽlga gãnna basɛba wuu.».

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Dee ken yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn nãam tẽŋa halɩ pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ, la saazuum wuu de la fõn nu'usi tõoma.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: Ba wuu wʋn sã'am mɛ, la fõn wʋn weege bɔna. Ba wʋn kʋrgɛ wʋ fuugo n kʋrgrɩ se'em na.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: Fʋ wʋn lobe ba basɛ wʋ fuugo tɩ ba kilim lobe basɛ la. Bõnɔ wuu wʋn teege wʋ ban teerɩ fu-kɛka la. La fõn, fõn de la fõn de se'em na ka tɛɛra, fõn yʋʋma ka tarɩ tɛka.».

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Nawẽnnɛ ka yele malɛka baa ayɩla yetɩ: «Zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ, halɩ tɩ m wa basɛ tɩ fʋ bɛ'ɛba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.».

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Malɛkadõma de la bẽm? Ba de la sɩɩsɩ n tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõnna ba, tɩ ba sõŋra nẽr-sɛba n de ba yẽ fãarɛ la.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ la masɛ tɩ tõma gʋrɛ yetɔga la tɩ tɩ wõm na sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ tɩ da bʋrgɛ.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Malɛkadõma n yʋʋn tɔgɛ yetɔg-sɛba kʋrʋm na ẽŋɛ ba tõonɛ mɛ, nẽr-sɛba n sɩ'ɩsɛ ka sakɛ ba la, yẽ sɩbgrɛ sɛka n masɛ tɩ ba yẽ la mɛ.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, tõma sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ fãarɛ kãtɛ ẽna, tõma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne põsɛ sɩbgrɛ? Zuudãana mẽŋa n daan põn dẽŋɛ mõolɛ fãarɛ ẽŋa yelle, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n wõm na pa'alɛ tõma tɩ la de la sɩra.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa mẽ tõm tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ, la bõn-yãlma buuri wuu to'oto'ore, dee bɔ nẽrba a Sɩa pãŋa wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em, pa'alɛ tɩ la de la sɩra.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: La dagɩ malɛkadõma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ ba sɔna dũnia sɛka n sẽm tɩ tõma tɔgra a yelle la.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: La nẽra n tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la pʋam zẽ'a ayẽma yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala de la bẽm tɩ fõn tẽra a yelle? Nẽrsaala bia de la bẽm tɩ fõn bɩsra a yelle?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: Fõn basɛ mɛ tɩ a ka paagɛ malɛkadõma wakatɛ fẽe mã'a, dee yãŋa nã'asɛ ẽ, bɔ ẽ na'am pãŋa la pẽka.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: Fõn basɛ mɛ tɩ a sɔna sɛla woo.» Lan yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tɩ sɛla woo sakra nẽrsaala la, a ka basɛ tɩ sɛla weege ka sakra ẽ. La, lan de se'em na, tõma nãn ka yẽtɩ tɩ sɛla woo sakrɩ ẽ.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: La tõma yẽ tɩ la de la Azezi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sike tɩ a ka paagɛ malɛkadõma wakatɛ fẽfẽe, dee yãŋa nã'asɛ ẽ, bɔ ẽ na'am pãŋa la pẽka ẽn nãmsɛ ki la ĩyã. La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã, tɩ a ki nẽrsaalba wuu ĩyã.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n de sɛka n basɛ tɩ sɛla woo bɔna, dɛna a bõnɔ na, la masɛ la ẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em tɩ a botɩ Azezi nãmsɛ, ẽŋɛ sɛla woo ba'asɛ, fãagɛ nẽrba, tarɩ a kɔma zozo'e sẽŋɛ a na'am pãŋa zẽ'am.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Azezi n malgrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba dɛna pʋpeelem dõma, la sɛba tɩ a malgɛ tɩ ba dɛna pʋpeelem dõma la lagɩm tara la Sɔ ayɩla. Bɛla n soe tɩ a ka zɔta yãnnɛ tɩ a wi ba tɩ a sɔbiisi.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: Bɛla tɩ a yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn tɔgɛ fʋ yelle m sɔbiisi zẽ'am, mam wʋn nã'asɛ fɔ ba lagsgɔ zẽ'am.».

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Dee le yetɩ: «Mam wʋn dɩkɛ m sɩra wuu bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ.» Dee ken yetɩ: «Mam n wãna, mam na kɔm-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ mam na.».

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Kɔma la tɩ a tɔgrɩ la n de nẽrsaalba tara ĩyã la zɩɩm na, bɛla tɩ Azezi mẽ lebge nẽrsaala wʋ bãma la ki, tɩ a kũm na sã'am Asũtãana n de sɛka n basɛ tɩ nẽrba kiira la pãŋa.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ a ẽŋɛ fãagɛ sɛba tɩ kũm dabeem tarɩ ba botɩ ba ãna wʋ yamsɩ ba vɔm na wuu pʋam na basɛ.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: La dagɩ malɛkadõma tɩ a wa'am tɩ a sõŋɛ, la de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ la tɩ a wa'am tɩ a sõŋɛ.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ la daan masɛ tɩ a lebge nẽrsaala ãna wʋ a sɔbiisi la sɛla woo pʋam, tã'agɛ dɛna ba kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n de ninbãal-zɔta, dee dɛna sɩra dãana Nawẽnnɛ tõoma pʋam. A ẽŋɛ la bɛla yese nẽrba tõon-be'ero, naagɛ ba la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi tɩ ya welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba la, bɩsra-ya Azezi, ẽŋa n de sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm, la ẽŋa n de tõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo la pʋam.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Azezi mẽŋa n daan yẽ nãmsgɔ wakatɛ sɛba tɩ Asũtãana makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ la, a tã wʋn sõŋɛ nẽr-sɛba n yẽtɩ nãmsgɔ la mɛ, bɔ ba pãŋa.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: A de la sɩra dãana sɛka n looge ẽ na zẽ'am, wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn de sɩra dãana se'em Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la pʋam na.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: La masɛ mɛ tɩ Azezi yẽ nã'asgɔ gãnna Amoyisi halɩ zozo'e, wʋ de-mẽta n yẽtɩ pẽka gãnna deego la mẽŋa se'em na.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Asɩra, deego woo de la nẽra n mẽ ẽ, la sɛla woo mẽta de la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: La sãn dɛna Amoyisi, a yʋʋn de la tõntõnna n de sɩra dãana Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n ãn wʋ deego la pʋam, dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ sɛla n wʋn wa tɔgɛ la yelle.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: La Krisi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa n de sɩra dãana sɔna deego la. A deego la de la tõma, tõma sãn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ la bʋraanɛ, dee tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la, la sũ-yẽlga, ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Bɛla, sakɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yele se'em yetɩ: «Zĩna, ya sãn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: ya da kɛ'ɛm ya sũure, wʋ ya yaabdõma n yʋʋn kɛ'ɛm ba sũure sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em, makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ weem na.».

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Bilam, ya yaabdõma yʋʋn makɛ mam mɛ bɩsɛ, baa ban yʋʋn yẽ mam tõoma yʋʋma pinaasɩ la.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Bɛla tɩ mam sũure yʋʋn yĩige la zamãan-ẽŋa nẽrba. Tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Ba pʋtẽ'era la botɩ ba tuura mɛ wakatɛ wuu, tɩ ba ka dɔla mam sore.›.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Mam sũ-yĩire la pʋam, mam pɔɩ mɛ yetɩ: ‹Ba kãn malɩn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ m bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la.› ».

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: M sɔbiisi, guuse-ya ya mẽŋa tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka da tara pʋtẽ'e-be'ero n wʋn basɛ tɩ a ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ bõn-ẽŋa basɛ tɩ a welge la Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana la.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: La bɔ'ɔra-ya taaba bʋraanɛ daarɛ woo, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n tɔgrɩ la tõma zĩna se'em na, tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka da kɛ'ɛm a sũure tɩ tõon-be'ero basɛ tɩ a tuuge.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Tõma sãn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ wʋ tõma n bɔ sɩra se'em pɔspɔsɩ la ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, tɩ wʋn lagɩm na Krisi to'oge pʋrɛ.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Bɛla tɩ la yele Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Zĩna, ya sãn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa, ya da kɛ'ɛm ya sũure, wʋ ya yaabdõma n yʋʋn kɛ'ɛm ba sũure sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em na.».

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Sɛba n yʋʋn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa dee sɩ'ɩsɛ a nõorɛ la de la bãna? Ba de la nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn tarɩ Ezipti tẽŋa yese la.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure yʋʋn yĩige la ba yʋʋma pinaasɩ la de la bãna? Ba de la sɛba n yʋʋn tõm tõon-be'ero ki weego pʋam na.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Sɛba tɩ a yʋʋn pɔɩ yetɩ ba kãn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la de la bãna? Ba de la sɛba n sɩ'ɩsɛ la.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: La tõma yẽ tɩ ba yʋʋn ka yãŋɛ kẽ, ban ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ tɩ tɩ kẽ a vo'osgo zẽ'am na ken bɔna mɛ, bɛla tɩ guuse, tɩ ba'asgɔ ya nẽr-sɛka da wa yẽ tɩ a koŋe ka kẽ.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Se'ere n soe la, tõma mẽ wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la wʋ nẽr-bãma n wõm se'em na. La bãma zẽ'am, yetɔga la tɩ ba yʋʋn wõm na ka tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ ba, bẽm ĩyã, sɛba n wõm na ka bɔ sɩra.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: La tõma sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la, tõma wʋn kẽ vo'osgo la zẽ'am tɩ a yʋʋn yetɩ: «Mam sũ-yĩire la pʋam, mam pɔɩ mɛ yetɩ, ba kãn malɩn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ m bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la.» Nawẽnnɛ n yele bɛla la, tɩ a dẽŋɛ põn tõm a tõoma wuu ba'asɛ mɛ halɩ lan pɔsɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ wakatɛ.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla tɔgɛ dabsa ayopɔɩ daarɛ la yelle yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nãam sɛla woo ba'asɛ, dee vo'ose la dabsa ayopɔɩ daarɛ.».

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: La zẽ'e-ẽna wã la ken tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Ba kãn malɩn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ m bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la.».

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n yʋʋn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la pɔspɔsɩ la ka kẽ, ban ka sakɛ la ĩyã. La dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrba kẽ.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ le looge daarɛ ayẽma tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ zĩna. Tɩ yaabdõma n sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ na, yʋʋma zozo'e n tole tɩ a botɩ Adavidi tɔgɛ wʋ lan põn gʋlsɛ se'em na yetɩ: «Zĩna, ya sãn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa, ya da kɛ'ɛm ya sũure.».

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Azoze yʋʋn sãn tarɩ nɩ ba paagɛ la vo'osgo asɩra sɩra zẽ'am, Nawẽnnɛ kãn le tɔgra vo'osgo daarɛ ayẽma yelle.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Bɛla, vo'osgo n ken bɔna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la ĩyã, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'ose dabsa ayopɔɩ daarɛ la.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka n kẽ Nawẽnnɛ vo'osgo la zẽ'am, mẽ basrɩ a tõoma mɛ dee vɔ'ɔsra, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ba'asɛ a tõoma dee vo'ose se'em na.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Bɛla, tɩ wɩsgɛ tɩ mẽŋa yẽ vo'osgo ẽna, tɩ guuse tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla da doose sɩ'ɩsgɔ la sore koŋe ka kẽ.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la vɔa mɛ, tara pãŋa, dee tara nõorɛ gãnna sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõa bayi. A kẽ'erɩ la nẽra pʋam mina a sɩa, la a sũure yɛla wuu bɔkra, dee mina a pʋtẽ'erɛ, la a yɛm wuu n ãn se'em, wʋ sʋ'a n welgrɩ kõb-suglsi paara kõb-sõom na.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Sɛla baa ayɩla ka boe n sʋgɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka yẽta. Bõn-sɛba tɩ a nãam na wuu de la vẽelsɩ a zẽ'am. La de la ẽŋa n de sɛka tɩ tɩ wʋn ze'ele a nẽŋam tɔgɛ tɩ yɛla wuu bɔ ẽ.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa Azezi n zom saazuum Nawẽnnɛ yire. Bɛla, tɩ ze'ele-ya kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore la doosgo pʋam.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Tɩ tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n tarɩ tɩ zĩile tɩ tɔrgɔ pʋam, se'ere n soe la, a yẽ nãmsgɔ mɛ sɛla woo pʋam wʋ tõma la, la a ka tõm tõon-be'ero.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ lɛm Nawẽnnɛ n de yel-sõnnɛ dãana la na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am la bʋraanɛ, tɩ a zoe tɩ ninbãalga ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ, dee sõŋɛ tɔ wakat-sɛka tɩ tɩ boorɩ.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana woo, ba loorɩ ẽ na nẽrsaalba pʋam tɩ a tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, nẽrba ĩyã. Ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a, dee kãabra bɔ'ɔra ẽ ba tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: A tã wʋn wõm sɛba n ka mi, la sɛba n tuuri la võorɛ mɛ zoe ba ninbãalga, ẽŋa mẽŋa n boe tɔrgɔ pʋam na ĩyã.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: La de la bɛla ĩyã, tɩ la dɛna a kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ a mẽŋa tõon-be'ero, la nẽrba la tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Nẽra ka tã wʋn nã'asɛ a mẽŋa dɩkɛ tõon-ẽna bɔ a mẽŋa dɛna kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n loorɩ, wʋ ẽn yʋʋn looge Aarõ se'em na.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Bɛla mẽ, la dagɩ Krisi mẽŋa n ɛ nã'asgɔ lebge kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n looge ẽ yele yetɩ: «Fõn de mam Dayʋa, zĩna mam lebge la fʋ Sɔ.».

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Dee le yele zẽ'a ayẽma yetɩ: «Fʋ de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.».

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Azezi-krisi n daan boe dũnia wã wakat-sɛka la, a pʋ'ʋsɛ mɛ sose Nawẽnnɛ n de sɛka n tã wʋn fãagɛ ẽ kũm pʋam basɛ la, belem ẽ na nintãm. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lerge a sosgo, a sik-m-mẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Baa la ẽn de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, nãmsgɔ la pʋam a bãŋɛ sakrɛ n de sɛla mɛ.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: La ẽn daan tõm Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em wuu ba'asɛ la, a lebge la sɛka tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n sakrɩ a nõorɛ la yẽtɩ fãarɛ n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ a zẽ'am.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele yetɩ, a de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Yɛla zozo'e tɩ tõma tarɩ wʋn tɔgɛ yel-ẽŋa yelle, la, la tɔ mɛ tɩ mam pa'alɛ ya a võorɛ tɩ ya wõm, se'ere n soe la, yãma lebge la mã'asga ka wõnna sɛla võorɛ tɔtɔ.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Lan yuuge paagɛ se'em wã, la seke nɩ mɛ tɩ yãma lebge karẽnsãandõma pa'ala nẽrba basɛba. La baa bɛla, yãma ken bɔɔra tɩ ba zãsna ya la Nawẽnnɛ pɔspɔsɩ yɛla n de nãana, wʋ kɔma n boorɩ bĩ'isum dee ka bɔɔra di-ke'ene na.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n ken ãna wʋ bia n mõgrɩ bĩ'isum na, a nãn ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla n masɛ, la sɛla n ka masɛ yelle bɔkɛ.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: La sɛba n de bõnkɛgsɩ la diti la di-ke'ene, bãma n de sɛba n zãsʋm mina sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la be'em bɔkra.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Bɛla, tɩ sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa zãsʋm bõnkɛgsɩ zãsŋɔ, dee basɛ Krisi pɔspɔsɩ yɛla la zãsŋɔ tɩ tɩ dẽŋɛ mina la. Tɩ kãn le zãsna pĩ'iluŋo yɛla la n de tɩ teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero n sẽm na kũm, la tɩ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: la ko'om misga zãsŋɔ, la ban paglɩ nu'usi nẽrba zuto pʋ'ʋsra yelle, la kũm vo'ore, la dũnia ba'asgɔ sarɩyadia yelle.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em n bɛla, tɩ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ sãn sakɛ.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra bɔna peelem pʋam, yẽ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n ãn sõŋa se'em, dee mina dũnia sɛka n sẽm na pãŋa n ãn se'em.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: bãma sãn lebe poorʋm dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, la ka tã tɩ ba le tarɩ ba leme tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãma ãn wʋ ba le ka'ara Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo, dee yaala ẽ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ: Saaga sãn niira zozo'e tɩ va'am yũura ko'om sõŋa, a bõnkɔɔla wʋn mãalɛ mɛ bɔ sɛba n kɔɔrɩ ẽ na, bɛla a yẽtɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: La va'am na sãn yɛsra gõ'osɩ, la mõ-yooro mã'a, a ka tarɩ yõorɔ, ba wʋn kã la be'em bɔ ẽ, la ba'asgɔ ba wʋn yõ ẽ na bugum.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, baa tõma n tɔgrɩ wãna la, tõma mi tɩ sũure pʋam sɩra sɩra tɩ yãma boe la so-sõŋɔ n wʋn botɩ ya yẽ fãarɛ la zuo.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana, a kãn yĩm ya tõoma, la yãma n pa'alɛ tɩ yãma nõŋɛ ẽ mɛ, tõm bɔ a nẽrba, dee ken tõnna bɔ'ɔra ba lɛɛlɛ wã.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Bɛla tõma n boorɩ se'em de la ya nẽra woo keŋe a mẽŋa bɛla, sẽnna nẽŋa ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, yẽ bõn-sɛba tɩ a tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura la.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Tõma ka boorɩ tɩ ya tara zẽyãgrɛ, ɩta-ya wʋ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, mõrgɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa yẽ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la Abraham na, a pɔɩ mɛ, la nẽra n ka boe gãnna a mẽŋa la ĩyã, a pɔɩ la a mẽŋa.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: yetɩ: «Asɩra, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ zozo'e, dee botɩ fʋ yɩɩsɩ yɩrgɛ zozo'e.».

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Tɩ Abraham mõrgɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa, yẽ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ la.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Nẽrsaala sãn yetɩ a pɔɩ, a pɔtɩ la sɛla n gãnnɩ a mẽŋa, tɩ pɔtɛ la dɛna sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽn yele se'em na de la sɩra, tɩ nõke'ene ba'asɛ.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ a pa'alɛ la sɛba tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ la ba la, tɩ ẽŋa n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na ka viiri, bɛla tɩ a bĩŋe a nõorɛ dee pɔɩ pa'asɛ.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n ka parnɩ na, n ẽŋɛ bõn-bãma bayi n ka viiri. Bɛla n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma bʋraanɛ zozo'e, tõma sɛba n basɛ sɛla woo, dee tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ la.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la botɩ tõma zɛ la kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, wʋ ka'aga n ka tẽŋa kẽ paagɛ pʋam pʋam yõkɛ kãŋkãŋɛ la. Bɛla tɩ tõma wʋn kẽ gãŋɛ fu-sɛka n beŋe Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la, paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: La de la zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi dẽŋɛ tõma kẽ, bɔna mĩ tõma ĩyã, dɛna kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Amelkisedɛkɩ ẽŋa yʋʋn de la Salɛm naba, la Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu kãabgɔ kẽema. Abraham n yʋʋn sẽŋɛ ka zɛbɛ yãŋɛ na'adõma dee lebe kulla la, Amelkisedɛkɩ n tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ẽ.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Tɩ Abraham looge lɔg-sɛba wuu tɩ a to'oge la pia pʋam ayɩla bɔ ẽ. Amelkisedɛkɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ la võorɛ de la: «Naba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ», sɛla n pa'asɛ, a de la Salɛm naba, n boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm naba».

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Ba ka mi a sɔ, la a ma, la a yaabdõma. Sɛla ka boe n pa'alɩ a vɔm pĩ'iluŋo, la a vɔm ba'asgɔ, a ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, a de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya Amelkisedɛkɩ n tarɩ ke'eŋo se'em. Ẽŋa tɩ tɩ yaaba Abraham yʋʋn looge lɔg-sɛba tɩ a zɛbɛ to'oge la pia pʋam ayɩla bɔ ẽ.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Alevi yɩɩsɩ sɛba n de kãabgɔ kẽma la, Amoyisi lɔɔ la bɔ ba nõorɛ tɩ ba tɔ'ɔsra pia pʋam ayɩla la Abraham yɩɩsɩ n de ba mẽŋa nẽrba la zẽ'am.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: La Amelkisedɛkɩ n dagɩ ba buuri la to'oge pia pʋam wuu ayɩla mɛ Abraham zẽ'am, dee kã kã'a-sõma bɔ Abraham n de sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ ẽ na.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: La de la sɩra, sẽ'eŋɔ ka boe, sɛka n kã kã'a-sõma bɔ nẽra n gãnnɩ sɛka tɩ a kã bɔ ẽ na.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: La sãn dɛna Alevi yɩɩsɩ la n to'osrɩ pia pʋam ayɩla la, ba de la nẽrsaalba n kiiri, la Amelkisedɛkɩ de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɩ, a vɔa mɛ.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Tɩ tã wʋn yetɩ, Abraham n yʋʋn looge sɛla tɩ a to'oge la pia pʋam ayɩla bɔ Amelkisedɛkɩ la, Alevi n de sɛka tɩ a buuri to'osrɩ pia pʋrɛ la mẽ bɔ ẽ mɛ.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Amelkisedɛkɩ n yʋʋn tʋ'ʋsɛ Abraham wakat-sɛka la, tɩ Alevi nãn boe la Abraham zɩɩm pʋam.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Lɔɔ la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bɔ a nẽrba la ẽebɛ la Leviti dõma kãabgɔ tõoma la zuo. Leviti dõma kãabgɔ tõoma la yʋʋn ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ sɛla woo masɛ ãna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na. A sãn tã nɩ, la ka le de nɩ tɩ kãabgɔ kẽema buuri ayẽma wa'am wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ la dagna Aarõ buuri.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Bɛla, kãabgɔ kẽma tõoma la sãn teege, dẽnɩ tɩ lɔɔ la mẽ teege.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yelle tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la, de la buuri ayẽma nẽra, la buur-ẽŋa nẽra nẽra nãn ka tõm kãabgɔ kẽema tõoma.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: La de la vẽelga tɩ tɩ Zuudãana la de la Azuda buuri nẽra, la Amoyisi n yʋʋn tɔgɛ kãabgɔ kẽma tõoma yelle la, a ka po tɔgɛ buur-ẽŋa yelle.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Kãabgɔ kẽema ayẽma n ãn wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n wa'am na n basɛ tɩ yetɔga la tɩ tõma tɔgrɩ la dɛna vẽelga.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: A ka lebge kãabgɔ kẽema wʋ nẽrsaala dɔgʋm lɔɔ n dolɩ se'em na, la de la vɔm n ka sã'anɩ pãŋa n botɩ a lebge kãabgɔ kẽema.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yele yetɩ: «Fʋ de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã, wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.».

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Bɛla tɩ kʋrʋm nõorɛ la n yele se'em na ka le tõnna, ẽn ka tarɩ pãŋa, dee ka tara yõorɔ la ĩyã.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Amoyisi lɔɔ la ka tã'agɛ basɛ tɩ sɛla sɛla masɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na. La lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la so-paalɛ, tɩ tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla n ãn sõŋɔ gãnna la basɛ tɩ tõma lɛnna Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: La sɛla n pa'asɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn pɔɩ mɛ. Leviti dõma n yʋʋn lebge kãabgɔ kẽma la, Nawẽnnɛ ka pɔɩ.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: La Azezi n lebge kãabgɔ kẽema la, Nawẽnnɛ pɔɩ mɛ yetɩ: «Mam n de Zuudãana la pɔɩ mɛ, mam kãn viige m nõorɛ. Fʋ de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.».

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ Azezi basɛ tɩ tɩ mina sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em la tõma la, n ãn sõŋa gãnna kʋrʋm nõorɛ la.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Sɛla n le pa'asɛ, kʋrʋm kãabgɔ kẽma la yʋʋn zo'oge mɛ sɔɔsna taaba, se'ere n soe la, kũm n yʋʋn gu'uri ba tɩ ba ka tãna tõnna wakatɛ wuu.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: La Azezi vɔa la wakatɛ wuu, nẽra ka sɔɔrɩ a kãabgɔ kẽema tõoma la tõnna.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ a tãna wʋn fãagɛ sɛba wuu n doose la ẽŋa lɛnna Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, wakatɛ wuu ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, a vɔa mɛ wakatɛ wuu yõkra Nawẽnnɛ sɩa bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: La de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n ãn wãna n masɛ tɩ tɩ tara: A de la pʋpeelem dãana, ka tara yelle, ka tara dõrga, a welge a mẽŋa mɛ la tõon-be'ero dõma, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ saazuum-zuum.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Ẽŋa ka ãn wʋ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma basɛba la, tɩ daarɛ woo la dɛna ba dẽŋɛ kãabɛ ba mẽŋa tõon-be'ero ĩyã, dee yãŋa kãabɛ nẽrba tõon-be'ero ĩyã la. Ẽŋa dɩkɛ la a mẽŋa kãabɛ nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a bɔ nẽrba tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Amoyisi lɔɔ la yʋʋn loorɩ la nẽrsaalba pãŋa n pɔ'ɔgɛ tɩ ba dɛna kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma. La Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ dee pɔɩ bɛla poorʋm na, a looge la a Dayʋa n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Tõma yetɔga la tɩ tõma tɔgrɩ la zuo n wãna: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, ẽŋa n zĩ saazuum Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo dãana la na'am bĩmbĩnne zʋʋgɔ.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: Ẽŋa n boe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la pʋam tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, zẽ'e-ẽŋa de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa yire, la dagɩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrsaalba tĩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Ba loorɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana woo tɩ a bɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a, la a kãabra bɔ'ɔra ẽ. Bɛla tɩ la mẽ masɛ tɩ ẽŋa tara sɛla bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: Azezi sãn boe nɩ na tẽŋa wã zuo, a ka de nɩ kãabgɔ kẽema. Se'ere n soe la, kãabgɔ kẽma põn bɔna mɛ bɔ'ɔra bɔ'a wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ la n de se'em na.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Bãma n tõnnɩ tõon-sɛka la de la sɛla n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire la wõnnɛ, la a mãmsgɔ. La ãn wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn yetɩ a tĩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ sõŋa sõŋa tɩ fʋ wʋn tĩ ẽ tɩ a ãna wʋ mam n pa'alɛ fʋ tãŋa zuo se'em na.».

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Azezi tarɩ la tõon-keko gãnna bãma. Nõorɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la tõma Azezi ĩyã la n ãn sõŋa gãnna, se'ere n soe la, sɛla tɩ a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ nõor-bĩŋre ẽna pʋam na n ãn sõŋa gãnna.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Pɔspɔsɩ nõor-bĩŋre la yʋʋn sãn ka gelge nɩ, la ka de nɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ bʋyi dãana.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: La Nawẽnnɛ zɛrgɛ ba mɛ, bɛla tɩ a yele yetɩ: «Dabsa n sẽm tɩ mam wʋn bĩŋe nõor-paalga la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, la Zuda dõma lɛ.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: La kãn ãna wʋ nõor-bĩŋre sɛka tɩ mam yʋʋn bĩŋe la ba yaabdõma wakat-sɛka tɩ mam gʋrɛ ba nu'usi tarɩ ba Ezipti yese la. Ba yʋʋn ka doose mam nõorɛ la tɩ mam bĩŋe yetɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na, bɛla n soe tɩ mam basɛ ba. Mam n de Zuudãana la n yele.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Mam n yetɩ m bĩŋe m nõorɛ se'em la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma dabsa bãma poorʋm n wãna: Mam wʋn dɩkɛ m lɔɔ la ẽŋɛ la ba pʋtẽ'era pʋam, mam wʋn gʋlsɛ ba la ba sũure pʋam. Mam wʋn dɛna ba Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Nẽra nẽra kãn le zãsʋm a tadãana bɩɩ a sɔbia yele ẽ tɩ a bãŋɛ Zuudãana la, se'ere n soe la, ba nẽrba wuu wʋn bãŋɛ mam mɛ, halɩ lan pɔsɛ la kɔm-pɩgsɩ ka paagɛ bõnkɛgsɩ.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: Lan de se'em na, mam wʋn basɛ ba koŋre la taalɛ mɛ, mam kãn le tẽra ba tõon-be'ero la yɛla.».

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n tɔgrɩ nõor-bĩŋre paalga yelle la, a botɩ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la dɛna la kɛka. La sɛla n lebge kɛka kʋrgɛ deege la a bɔɩ.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Pɔspɔsɩ nõor-bĩŋre la yʋʋn tarɩ la zẽ'e-sõŋɔ tẽŋa zuo, la sɔa tɩ ba dɔla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Ba yʋʋn tĩ la fuu-deego beŋe zẽ'esɩ bayi, wi'ira pɔspɔsɩ zẽ'a la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ. Bilam, fɩtladõma dɔglgɔ zẽ'a, la taablɩ tɩ ba dɔglɩ borborɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ n boe mĩ.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: La fuugo la n beŋe deego bʋyi dãana la, ba wi'iri bilam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne n de sãlma tɩ ba yõ'orɩ tɩdarɛ a zuo n boe mĩ, pa'asɛ la nõor-bĩŋre daka tɩ ba dɩkɛ sãlma yũlge a wuu. Sãlma lɔkɔ tɩ mannɩ boe a pʋam, la Aarõ dasaarɛ n puse võorɔ la, la kug-palagsɩ sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na gʋlsɛ ba zuto la n boe daka la pʋam.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Daka la zuo, ba ẽŋɛ la malɛkadõma tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ seribẽ n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa ẽŋɛ mĩ, tɩ ba kempagsɩ yɛrgɛ tɩ ba mã'asʋm pĩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba ɩtɩ zɩɩm mĩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basra nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ la. La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam kãn wɛgsɛ bõn-bãma yelle pa'alɛ ya.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Sɛla woo mãasʋm na bɛla bɔna a zẽ'a, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma la kẽ'era pɔspɔsɩ deego la pʋam daarɛ woo tõnna ba tõoma.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: La deego bʋyi dãana la pʋam, kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la mã'a n kẽ'erɩ mĩ nõorɛ ayɩla yʋʋnɛ pʋam. A sãn kẽ'era mĩ, dẽnɩ tɩ a tara dũnsi zɩɩm ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, a mẽŋa tuure, la nẽrba tuure ĩyã.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɩ tɩ pɔspɔsɩ deego la n daan ken bɔna la, sore ka pakɛ tɩ nẽra tãna wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Bɛla de la mãmsgɔ pa'ala tõma bõn-sɛba n boe lɛɛlɛ wã võorɛ. Bɔ'a, la dũnsi tɩ ba kãabrɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ na, ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na sũure masɛ a zẽ'am.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: Ba de la dia, la bõn-yũula, la ko'om peere buuri to'oto'ore yelle mã'a. Ɩrgɔ bãma de la ĩyã yɛla n de dũnia wã bõnɔ n yʋʋn boe tɩ nẽrba dɔla, halɩ wa paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɩn malgɛ sɛla woo sõŋa.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: La Krisi n wa'am na, a de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, ẽŋa n bɔ tõma bõn-sõma tɩ tɩ tarɩ wã, ẽŋa n kẽ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam n gãnnɩ na, la dagɩ fuugo deego tɩ nẽrsaalba tĩ, a dagɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: A daan kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la pʋam na nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a wakatɛ wuu ĩyã. La dagɩ bʋʋsɩ zɩɩm, la lɔl-pɔla zɩɩm tɩ a dɩkɛ kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, a dɩkɛ la a mẽŋa zɩɩm kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ tõma wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Ba yʋʋn tarɩ la bʋʋsɩ, la lɔlsɩ zɩɩm, la na-sa'arɛ tɩ ba yõ tãmpɛglʋm mĩisra nẽr-sɛba n sã'am lɔɔ la, tɩ ba le malgɛ ba tɩ ba ãna sõŋa masɛ la lɔɔ la.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Krisi zɩɩm na tarɩ pãŋa mɛ gãnna bɛla zozo'e. Ẽŋa ka tarɩ tuure, la doose la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe wakatɛ wuu tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, peege tɩ tõon-be'ero n sẽm na kũm na, tɩ tɩ sũure ãna sõŋa, tɩ tɩ tõnna bɔ'ɔra vɔm dãana Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõor-paalga ẽŋa ĩyã. Ẽn ki la ĩyã, a yɔ fãagɛ nẽr-sɛba n tuuge pɔspɔsɩ nõorɛ bĩŋre la pʋam mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ba la, ba wʋn to'oge bõn-sõma tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Nẽra sãn pa'alɛ ba wʋn pʋɩ ẽŋa bõntarsõm se'em ẽŋa kũm poorʋm, dẽnɩ tɩ a ki dee tɩ ba yãŋa pʋɩ ba.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Nẽra la sãn ken vɔa, a nõorɛ la tɩ a bɔ la de la yoogo. La de la a kũm poorʋm tɩ a tarɩ yõorɔ.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: La mẽ de la bɛla baa la Nawẽnnɛ pɔspɔsɩ nõor-bĩŋre la, la de la dũnsi zɩɩm n yese tɩ a pɔsɛ tõnna.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Amoyisi n yʋʋn tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ wuu pa'alɛ nẽrba wuu wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yãŋa dɩkɛ la lɔl-pɔla, la bʋʋsɩ zɩɩm, la ko'om, dɩkɛ pesgo kõbrɔ n ãn mõlga, vile izɔpɩ võorɔ lʋsɛ ba pʋam, mĩise nõor-bĩŋre gõŋɔ la, la nẽrba la wuu.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: dee yele yetɩ: «Zɩɩ-bãna wã n pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la ya lɛ tɩ ya sakɛ ẽ.».

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: Amoyisi yʋʋn ken dɩkɛ zɩɩm na mĩise fuu-deego la, la pʋ'ʋsgɔ lɔgrɔ la wuu mɛ.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: La sãn dɛna lɔɔ la sore, la ãn wʋ la de la sɛla woo tɩ zɩɩm malgrɩ tɩ ba le ãna sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Tɩ zɩɩm sãn ka yese, Nawẽnnɛ ka basrɩ nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Lan yʋʋn masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dũnsi zɩɩm malgɛ bõn-sɛba n mãmsrɩ sɛla n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la, tɩ ba le ãna sõŋa bɛla la, la sãn dɛna bõnɔ la mẽŋa n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la, dẽnɩ tɩ la dɛna kãabgɔ n ãn sõŋa gãnna bɛla.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Krisi n kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka la, dagɩ deego tɩ nẽrsaala ẽŋɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n mãmsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la, a kẽ la Nawẽnnɛ yire mẽŋa, bɔna Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tõma sõŋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Krisi ka kẽ nõorɛ faa wʋ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la n kẽ'erɩ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la yʋʋnɛ woo la zɩɩm n dagɩ a mẽŋa zɩɩm na, a kẽ la nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: La sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, a daan wʋn nãmsɛ nɩ mɛ nõorɛ faa, halɩ lan pɔsɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wa paam zĩna. La sɛla n de sɩra, a wa'am na nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ wã, tɩ a yese tõon-be'ero, ẽn dɩkɛ a mẽŋa kãabɛ la ĩyã.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Nẽra woo kiiri la bʋyɩla, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dita a sarɩya.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: Bɛla mẽ, Krisi dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ la nõorɛ ayɩla yese nẽrba zozo'e tõon-be'ero basɛ. A wʋn le leme wa'am mɛ nõorɛ bʋyi pʋam, la, la kãn dɛna tõon-be'ero yɛsga ĩyã, la de la nẽr-sɛba n gu'uri ẽ tɩ ba yẽ fãarɛ la ĩyã.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Amoyisi lɔɔ la yɛla yʋʋn mãmsrɩ la bõn-sõma n sẽm na mã'a, ba ka de sɛla n de sɩra la mẽŋa mẽŋa. A botɩ nẽrba kãabra la kãabgɔ bãma mẽŋa yʋʋnɛ woo bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ ka ba'asra. La a ka tã wʋn botɩ nẽr-sɛba n kãabrɩ bɛla la lɛm Nawẽnnɛ masɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na, sãn malgɛ nɩ mɛ ãna sõŋa wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, ba kãn le tẽ'esra tɩ tõon-be'ero boe bãma zuto, la ba wʋn basɛ mɛ kãn le kãabra.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: La dagɩ bɛla, kãabgɔ bãma tɩ ba kãabrɩ la, tẽerɩ ba la ba tõon-be'ero yelle yʋʋnɛ woo.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Se'ere n soe la, lɔlsɩ, la bʋʋsɩ zɩɩm ka tã wʋn yese tõon-be'ero basɛ.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ Krisi n daan sẽm dũnia zuo la, a daan yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Fõn ka boorɩ kãabgɔ, la bɔ'a, la fõn ẽŋɛ la ĩyã bɔ mam.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba yõ'orɩ bĩmbĩnne zuo, la kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba kãabrɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã la, ka ɩtɩ fõn sũure yẽlʋm.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Bɛla tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, mam n wãna n yetɩ m ẽŋɛ fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ mam yelle fʋ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.› ».

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: A dẽŋɛ yetɩ: «Fõn ka boorɩ kãabgɔ, la bɔ'a, la kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba yõ'orɩ bĩmbĩnne zuo, la kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba kãabrɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã, la ka ɩtɩ fõn sũure yẽlʋm.» La kãabgɔ bãma, ba kãabrɩ ba wʋ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Dee le yetɩ: «Mam n wãna n yetɩ m ẽŋɛ fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na.» A saam pɔspɔsɩ kãabgɔ la mɛ dee vigle bʋyi dãana.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Azezi-krisi ẽŋɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, a dɩkɛ la a mẽŋa kãabɛ bɔ ẽ nõorɛ ayɩla wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, tɩ tõma welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Kãabgɔ kẽema woo ze la a tõoma zẽ'am daarɛ woo tõnna, la a kãabrɩ la kãabgɔ bãma mẽŋa nõorɛ faa, dee tɩ la ka tãna tõon-be'ero yɛsra.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: La Krisi dɩkɛ a mẽŋa kãabɛ la nõorɛ ayɩla bɔ yese nẽrba tõon-be'ero wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, dee sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: gu'ura tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wa ẽŋɛ a bɛ'ɛba tɩ ba bɔna a nãma tẽŋa.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Bɛla, kãabgɔ bʋyɩla la mã'a tɩ a welge nẽrba to'ore tɩ ba dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba masɛ sɛla woo pʋam wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mẽ pa'alɛ tõma tɩ bõn-ẽŋa de la sɩra, se'ere n soe la, a yele yetɩ:.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: «Mam n yetɩ m bĩŋe m nõorɛ se'em la ba dabsa bãma poorʋm n wãna: Mam wʋn dɩkɛ m lɔɔ la ẽŋɛ la ba sũure pʋam, mam wʋn gʋlsɛ ba ba pʋtẽ'era pʋam. Mam n de Zuudãana n yele.».

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Dee ken yetɩ: «Mam kãn le tẽra ba tõon-be'ero, la ba tuure la yelle.».

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ la, la ka le nara tɩ ba kãabra tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: M sɔbiisi, tõma mi tɩ Azezi zɩɩm na ĩyã, tõma tarɩ nõorɛ mɛ tɩ tɩ kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: Azezi n dɩkɛ a mẽŋa ĩyã kãabɛ bɔ la, a ãagɛ fuugo la n beŋe tõma la Nawẽnnɛ na mɛ, yo'oge so-paalɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm bɔ tõma.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n bɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba yelle.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ lɛm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am la pʋpeelem, la sũure n bɔ sɩra zõnzõnnɔ, la sũure n peege tɩ tɩ mina tɩ tõon-be'ero ka le bɔna, la ĩyã n peege la ko-sõma.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Tɩ pa'alɛ-ya tɩn tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la da basɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana, a wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Tɩ bɩsra taaba yelle wʋn kɛŋra taaba, tɩ tɩ tara nõŋlʋm, dee tõnna tõon-sõma.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Tɩ da basɛ taaba lagsgɔ wʋ nẽrba basɛba n ɩtɩ se'em na, tɩ bɔ'ɔra-ya taaba bʋraanɛ, yãma n yẽ tɩ Azezi lemŋo daarɛ la lɛm mɛ na.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la, a sãn mina dee ken tõnna tõon-be'ero la yɛmleego, kãabgɔ ka le bɔna tõon-be'ero yɛsga ĩyã.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Sɛla n yãŋa deege de la nẽr-ẽŋa gu'ura Nawẽnnɛ sarɩya, la bugum n wʋn di sɛba n sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ na, la dabeem.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: La sãn dɛna la Amoyisi lɔɔ la, nẽra sãn sã'am lɔɔ la, tɩ nẽrba bayi, bɩɩ batã bɔna mĩ dɛna kaset-dõma, ninbãalga ka boe, dẽnɩ tɩ a dãana ki.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: La sãn dɛna bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n kɛsɛ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, kãn yẽ sɩbgrɛ n de be'em halɩ gãnna bɛla bɩɩ? Nẽr-ẽŋa bɩsɛ Azezi zɩɩm na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe a nõorɛ a ĩyã la zãŋa. La, la de la zɩɩm ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ botɩ nẽra la welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽra. Ẽŋa dãana ken pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n de yel-sõnnɛ dãana la mɛ.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Tõma mi Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ: «Mam n de m lebse be'em, la de la mam n wʋn yɔ.» La ken yetɩ: «Zuudãana la n wʋn di a nẽrba sarɩya.».

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: La de la yelle mẽŋa mẽŋa bɔ sɛka n lui Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana la nu'usum.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya yãma n daan pɔsɛ yẽ Nawẽnnɛ peelem n pa'alɛ ya sɩra la, lan daan ãn se'em bɔ ya, ya daan yẽ nãmsgɔ zo'oge mɛ, la ya wĩbe mɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Ba daan tʋʋrɩ dee nãmsra ya nẽrba basɛba mɛ nẽrba zẽ'am, la yãma naagɛ la sɛba tɩ ba nãmsrɩ la mɛ sõŋɛ ba.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: La yãma zoe sɛba n boe yʋ'a deem na ninbãalga mɛ. La ban fã yãma lɔgrɔ la, yãma sakɛ mɛ koŋe ba la sũ-yẽlga, yãma n mi tɩ yãma tarɩ la bõntarsõm sõma n gãnnɩ bɛla n kãn sã'am na ĩyã.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Bɛla, da basɛ-ya sũ-ke'eŋo sɛka tɩ ya tarɩ la, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ yɔɔrɔ zozo'e.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Yãma de la ya nargɛ pãŋa sẽŋɛ nẽŋa ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, tɩ ya wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ya la.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: La gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «La deege la fẽe, fẽfẽe mã'a, tɩ sɛka n sẽm na wʋn wa'am, a kãn yuuge.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Nẽr-sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra masɛ mam zẽ'am wʋn vɔa, ẽn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã. La sɛka n lebe poorʋm, mam sũure kãn ãna yẽlʋm la a dãana.».

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma dagɩ sɛba n lebrɩ poorʋm wʋn sã'am na, tõma de la sɛba n bɔ sɩra wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Bɛla, tõma n tarɩ nẽrba zozo'e n gilge tõma pa'ala tɩ sore la, tɩ mẽ basɛ bõn-sɛba n gu'uri tɩ sẽnnɛ, la tõon-be'ero tɩ tɩ ɩtɩ nãana nãana la, dee mõrgɛ zɔta zʋa n boe tɩ nẽŋa na.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Tɩ bɩsra-ya Azezi, ẽŋa n botɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra, dee wʋn tarɩ tɩ paagɛ a ba'asgɔ zẽ'am. A wĩbe mɛ ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo ka zoe yãnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, a yẽ sũ-yẽlga sɛka n gu'uri ẽ na mɛ, la a yãŋa zĩ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne zʋʋgɔ.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ, tõon-be'ero dõma n sɩ'ɩsɛ ẽ, nãmsɛ ẽ se'em, tɩ a wĩbe, tɩ ya da basɛ tɩ ya ĩyã ki tɩ ya bʋraanɛ bo'oge.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Yãma n zɛbrɩ la tõon-be'ero la, ya ken ka zɛbɛ ki.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Da yĩm-ya kã'aŋɔ sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã'an ya wʋ nẽra n kã'anɩ a kɔma se'em yetɩ: «M bia, Zuudãana sãn sɩbgɛ fɔ, da dɩkɛ tɩ la dɛna zãŋa, a sãn pa'alɛ fɔ fʋ tuure, da basɛ tɩ fʋ bʋraanɛ bo'oge.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana demsrɩ la sɛba tɩ a nõŋɛ, dee sɩbgra sɛka woo tɩ a dɩkɛ tɩ a dɛna a bia.».

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Wĩbra-ya, tɩ la de la ya demsgo, Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ ya la a kɔma ɩrgɔ. Bia ka boe tɩ a sɔ ka sɩbgra ẽ.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka demse ya wʋ ẽn ɩtɩ a kɔma wuu se'em na, bɛla ya yi de la tãmpĩiba, ya dagɩ a kɔma.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔdõma n dɔgɛ tɩ la, ba daan sɩbgrɩ tɩ mɛ, dee tɩ tɩ nanna ba. Bɛla, tɩ sakɛ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nõorɛ gãnna, tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ vɔm a zẽ'am.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Tɩ sɔdõma la daan sɩbgrɩ tɩ la wakatɛ fẽfẽe ĩyã wʋ bãma mẽŋa n tẽ'esɛ se'em na. La Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɩ tɩ la tõma mẽŋa sõŋrɛ ĩyã, tɩ a botɩ tɩ welge to'ore dɛna pʋpeelem dõma wʋ ẽŋa la.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Wakatɛ sɛka tɩ ba sɩbgrɩ tõma la, la de la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la ka de sũ-yẽlga. La bɛla poorʋm nẽr-sɛba n yẽ sɩbgrɛ ẽŋa la yẽtɩ a yõorɔ mɛ, n de sũ-mã'asʋm, la vɔm n masɛ ãna sõŋa.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Bɛla, keŋe-ya ya nu'usi n targɛ, la ya dũma n ki la.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya nãma la sẽnna sorɔɔrɔ n de tẽntẽesɩ zuto, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n dũ'usri la da lui, a wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Mõrgɛ-ya ɛ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna yãma la nẽrba wuu tẽŋasʋka, la ya ɛ pʋpeelem. Tɩ pʋpeelem sãn ka bɔna, nẽra ka tã wʋn yẽ Zuudãana la.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Guuse-ya tɩ ya nẽra nẽra da wa mõŋɛ a mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, ken guuse-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka da ãna wʋ yẽg-toogo n puse ya tẽŋasʋka, wʋn basɛ tɩ toogo la loŋe nẽrba zozo'e sã'am ba.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽra nẽra ɩta yalsɩ, bɩɩ nẽra n pɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla n de Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ, wʋ A'eso n yʋʋn dɩkɛ a kẽnnɔ bõnɔ teege dia laaga ayɩla la.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: La yãma mi tɩ bɛla poorʋm, ẽn wa bɔɔra tɩ a sɔ kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ẽ, ẽŋa n de kẽema ĩyã la, a ka tã'agɛ. A ka yẽ sore yãŋɛ teege yelle la tɩ a ẽŋɛ la, baa ẽn kellɩ la.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Yãma n lɛm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am se'em na, de la to'ore la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n yʋʋn lɛm sɛla tɩ ba tã wʋn kalʋm dee yẽ zẽ'am na. Bãma yʋʋn lɛm na tãŋa yẽ bugum n diti, la lika n ãn lim, la kʋsebgo kãtɛ.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: La ba wõm na benkonne peebgo, la kõa n tɔgɛ, tɩ nẽrba la n yʋʋn wõm na belem yetɩ, la da le tɔgɛ pa'asɛ.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba ka tã wʋn to'oge ẽn yele se'em yetɩ: «Sɛka sãn kalʋm tãŋa la, baa a sãn dɛna dũŋa, ba lobe ẽ na kuga kʋ» la.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Bõn-sɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ la bɔ'ɔrɩ la dabeem, halɩ tɩ Amoyisi yele yetɩ, ẽŋa zotɩ la dabeem, halɩ tɩ ẽŋa gõgra.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: La yãma lɛm na tãŋa tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Siyõ tãŋa n de vɔm dãana Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa, ẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm mẽŋa mẽŋa n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ malɛkadõma tʋsa tʋsa bɔna mĩ.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: La ya ken lɛm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ n de a kɔm-kẽendõma tɩ ba yʋ'ʋra gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire la. La ya lɛm Nawẽnnɛ n de nẽrba wuu sarɩyadita. Ya lɛm na nẽr-sɛba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dee masɛ sɛla woo pʋam na sɩɩsɩ zẽ'am.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Yãma lɛm na Azezi n de sɛka n boe Nawẽnnɛ la tõma tẽŋasʋka botɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõor-paalga la tõma lɛ. A zɩɩm na n yese bɔ tõma la pa'alɛ la sɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna Abɛlɩ zɩɩm na.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Ɩta-ya guusgo, da zagsɛ ka selse sɛka n tɔgrɩ la ya lɛ la. Sɛba n yʋʋn zagsɛ ka selse nẽr-sɛka n kã'anɩ ba tẽŋa zuo la ka põsɛ nãmsgɔ. Bɛla, tõma sãn zagsɛ ka selse sɛka n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la yetɔga, tõma kãn yãŋɛ põsɛ.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: A kõa la yʋʋn botɩ tẽŋa la mĩim mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, a bĩŋe la nõorɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn le mĩim dũnia mɛ nõorɛ ayɩla, la dagɩ dũnia mã'a, saazuum mẽ wʋn mĩim mɛ.».

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Ẽn yetɩ a wʋn le mĩim nõor-ayɩla la, pa'alɛ mɛ tɩ sɛla tɩ a nãam na wʋn mĩim mɛ sã'am bɔɩ, dee tɩ sɛla n kãn tã'agɛ mĩim sã'am na mã'a n wʋn weege.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Bɛla, tõma n wʋn to'oge so'olʋm n kãn sã'am na ĩyã, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, tõnna se'em n wʋn ãna a sũure yẽlʋm bɔ'ɔra ẽ, la tɩ nã'asra ẽ, dee zɔta ẽ.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: Lan de se'em na, tõma Nawẽnnɛ ãn wʋ bugum n diti.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Ken nõŋɛ-ya taaba wʋ sɔbiisi la.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Da basɛ-ya sãama sãanɔ ɩrgɔ, tɩ nẽrba basɛba ẽŋɛ la bɛla, ẽŋɛ malɛkadõma sãanɛ dee ka bãŋɛ.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Tẽra-ya sɛba n boe yʋ'a deem na yelle, wʋ yãma mẽ po bɔna la yʋ'a deem na. Tẽra-ya sɛba tɩ ba nãmsrɩ ba la yelle, wʋ yãma mẽŋa n nãmsrɩ la.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ pɔgdire tara nã'asgɔ, dee da basɛ tɩ dẽgrɔ tõoma bɔna mĩ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, la nẽr-sɛba n tũuli la sarɩya mɛ.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ ligri nõŋlʋm sɔna ya. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya sũure ãna yẽlʋm la yãma n tarɩ sɛla la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam kãn basɛ fɔ, mam kãn malɩn basɛ fɔ.».

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ tõma nẽra woo tãna wʋn yele la bʋraanɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana de la mam sõŋra, mam kãn zoe dabeem. Nẽrsaala tã wʋn ẽŋɛ mam na bẽm?».

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Tẽra-ya ya nẽŋadõma n mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ ya la yelle. Bɩsɛ-ya ba vɔm n daan ãn se'em ka paagɛ ba kũm, tɩ ya mẽ ɩta bãma n bɔ sɩra ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Azezi-krisi de la ẽn ãn se'em zaam, la zĩna, la wakatɛ wuu.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ zãsŋɔ sãasɩ buuri to'oto'ore botɩ ya tuuge. Sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la tɩ basɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a la keŋe tɩ sũure, la, la da dɛna ɩrgɔ sɔa n de dia yelle, sɛba n dolɩ ɩrgɔ bãma la ka yẽ yõorɔ.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ, tɩ Zifdõma n tõnnɩ ba kãabgɔ tõoma la ka tara sore tɩ ba naagɛ tõma kãabgɔ la pʋam.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Zifdõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tarɩ la dũnsi zɩɩm kẽ'era Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la kãabra tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã, dee tara ba nẽnnɔ na yɛsra tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa yõ'ora.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ Azezi mẽ yese tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa ka ki, tɩ a mẽŋa zɩɩm malgɛ nẽrba tɩ ba ãna sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Bɛla, tɩ yese-ya sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa ka po sakɛ pɔ'ɔrɛ la.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Tõma ka tarɩ tẽŋa dũnia wã zuo n boe wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, tõma eerɩ la tẽn-sɛka n sẽm na.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Azezi ĩyã, tɩ nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ wakatɛ wuu tɩ la ãna wʋ kãabgɔ la, la boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ tɩ nõorɛ yetɔga zẽkɛ a yʋ'ʋrɛ pa'alɛ ẽn de sɛka.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Da basɛ-ya ɩr-sõma ɩrgɔ, la taaba sõŋrɛ, tɩ tõon-bãma n ãn wʋ kãabgɔ n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure yẽlʋm.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Sakra-ya ya nẽŋadõma nõorɛ, dee nanna ba yetɔga. La de la bãma n bɩsrɩ yãma n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la yelle, ba wʋn ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ bãma n bɩsɛ ya yelle se'em mɛ. Ẽŋɛ-ya ya wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ba tã'agɛ tõm ba tõoma la sũ-yẽlga da yõnna, tɩ bɛla ka tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ ya.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra tɔ. Tõma mi tɩ sũure pʋam mɛ tɩ tõma ka ẽŋɛ be'em, tõma boorɩ mɛ tɩ tɩ yel-ɩrɛ ãna sõŋa sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: Mam sosrɩ ya mɛ zozo'e tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsra m ĩyã tɩ m yẽ sore leme wa'am ya zẽ'am tɔtɔ.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n de sũ-mã'asʋm dãana n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi, tɩ a bɩsra tõma yelle wʋ dũnsĩin-kɛ'ɛma, a zɩɩm na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la tõma wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ la ĩyã.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ ya tã'agɛ ɩta sɛla woo n ãn sõŋa, dɔla a yɛm n boorɩ se'em. A wʋn basɛ tɩ sɛla n ãn a sũure yẽlʋm ẽŋɛ tɩ pʋam, Azezi-krisi ĩyã, ẽŋa n de sɛka n masɛ nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: M sɔbiisi, mam sosrɩ ya tɩ ya sakɛ to'oge kã'aŋɔ yetɔg-bãna wã, tɩ mam n gʋlsɛ ya gõn-sɛka wã ka zo'oge.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ ba yo'oge tɩ sɔbia Atimõtɩ basɛ mɛ, a sãn wa'am tɔtɔ, mam na ẽŋa wʋn doose taaba wa'am wa kaagɛ ya.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya ya nẽŋadõma, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Itali dõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya wuu.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Mam Azakɩ n de Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi tõntõnna n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu n wɩrgɛ bɔna zẽ'a woo la.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: M sɔbiisi, nãmsgɔ to'oto'ore sãn paagɛ ya Azezi sore la pʋam, ya dɩkɛ ba tɩ ba dɛna ya sũ-yẽlga zõnzõnnɔ.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: se'ere n soe la, ya mi tɩ nãmsgɔ sãn paagɛ ya, ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam, tɩ ya yãŋɛ wĩbe, la wʋn botɩ ya mõrgɛ ze'ele la kãŋkãŋɛ.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: La mõrgɛ-ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ a tɛka, tɩ ya wʋn masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, dɛna nẽrba n bɩ, tɩ sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ ya.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Yɛm sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ ya nẽr-sɛka, a sose Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽrba wuu la yɛmleego dee ka mõŋra ba la, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ẽ.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: La a sãn sose ẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔ ẽ sɩra, da sẽ'ena baa fẽfẽe. Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n sẽ'enɩ, a ãn wʋ ko-kãtɛ ko-mĩ'isi tɩ kʋsebgo vugri wẽ'era ba tɩ ba yɛgna se'em na.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Nẽr-ẽŋa buuri da tara pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a wʋn to'oge sɛla Zuudãana la zẽ'am.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: A de la nẽra n de pʋtẽ'era bayi dãana, tɩ a yɛm yɔɔra ẽn ɩtɩ bõn-sɛba la wuu pʋam.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: La masɛ tɩ sɔbi-sɛka n de nasa ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkrɩ ẽ mɛ.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
