local: La bõntarsa mẽ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga Nawẽnnɛ n sikri ẽ na, se'ere n soe la, a bõntarsõm na wʋn tole wʋ mõ-puuro la.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Wʋntɛɛŋa sãn wõŋɛ mõogɔ, a puuro la zẽkrɩ mɛ sʋʋra kʋta, tɩ ba dasãnnɛ na bɔta. Bõntarsa mẽ ãn na bɛla, a wʋn ki mɛ a bõntarsõm na tõoma pʋam.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n wĩbri nãmsgɔ pʋam tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Se'ere n soe la, ẽn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ẽ bɔ'a n de vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ na.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Yelle sãn paagɛ nẽra botɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em, a da yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ ẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em. Sɛla ka tã wʋn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ be'em. La Nawẽnnɛ mẽ ka botɩ nẽra bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: La de la nẽra woo yɛmleego n de be'em n pã'asrɩ ẽ, botɩ a kẽ'era yɛla pʋam.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: La be'em yɛmleego sãn yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ, a dɔgrɩ la tõon-be'ero. La tõon-be'ero la sãn wa bɩ, a sẽm na kũm.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, da pã'asɛ-ya ya mẽŋa.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Bɔ'a wuu n ãn sõŋa, la bɔ'a n masɛ wuu ze'etɩ la saazuum, Nawẽnnɛ n de peelem dãana la zẽ'am. Ẽŋa n nãam bõn-sɛba wuu n boe saazuum nẽera peelem, tɩ ba tɛɛra, la ẽŋa ka teerɩ, a ka tarɩ lika.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: Ẽŋa mẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ a basɛ tɩ a sɩra yetɔga la bɔ tõma vɔm. A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ tõma dɛna la pɔspɔsɩ dõma bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a nãam na pʋam.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, bãŋɛ-ya bõn-ẽna wã sõŋa, nẽra woo mõrgɛ sɛlsra sõŋa, la a mã'agɛ a mẽŋa tɔgra, la a da isgra sũure tɔtɔ.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Nẽrsaala sũure sãn isge, a ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Bɛla, basɛ-ya sɛla n de tõon-dẽgrɔ buuri wuu, la sɛla n de pʋtʋʋlgɔ wuu, dee sike ya mẽŋa to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la tɩ a dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ ya sũure pʋam, n tã wʋn fãagɛ ya la.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Da pã'asɛ ya mẽŋa, dɛna-ya nẽrba n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, da dɛna-ya nẽrba n selsrɩ mã'a.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n selsrɩ yetɔga la, dee ka sakra ɩta lan yele se'em na, a dãana ãn wʋ nẽra n bɩsɛ bɩsga pʋam yẽ a mẽŋa n ãn se'em.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: la ẽn bɩsɛ a mẽŋa kɩ'ɩlɩm dee fõrgɛ la, a darɛ yĩm ẽŋa n ãn se'em na mɛ.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ n masɛ sõŋa n yolsrɩ nẽrba la, dee sakɛ ẽ sõŋa ɩta lan yele se'em na, ẽŋa dagɩ nẽra n selsrɩ dee yĩnna, ẽŋa dãana tarɩ la sũ-yẽlga ẽŋa n ɩtɩ sɛla la pʋam.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tẽ'esrɩ tɩ ẽŋa de la nẽra n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ, dee ka tãna a nõorɛ yõkra, a pã'asrɩ la a mẽŋa, nẽr-ẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo la de la zãŋa.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ tɩ dɔla ẽ se'em tɩ la ãna sõŋa ka tara dẽgrɔ n wãna: La de la fʋ bɩsɛ kɩɩbsɩ la pɔkõpa yelle ba nãmsgɔ pʋam, la fʋ guuse fʋ mẽŋa la dũnia wã tõon-dẽgrɔ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: M sɔbiisi, yãma n sakɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, da tũ'usra-ya nẽrba.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ, wʋ yãma sãn lagsɛ taaba tɩ nẽra n pire sãlma nutʋa dee yɛ fu-sõma wa'am wa kẽ ya lagsgɔ la zẽ'a, tɩ nasa n mẽ yɛ fu-zõra kẽ.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: tɩ yãma sãn bɩsɛ nẽr-sɛka n yɛ fu-sõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am wa zĩ'ire kalam n de zẽ'e-sõŋɔ», dee yele nasa la yetɩ: «Zɛ saazuo, bɩɩ zĩ'ire tẽŋa mam nãma zẽ'am wã.».

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: Yãma sãn ẽŋɛ wãna, yãma ka tũ'usri nẽrba sɩra? Bɛla, ya tarɩ la pʋtẽ'e-be'ero bɔkra nẽrba, yetɩ ayẽma n gãnnɩ ayẽma.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, selse-ya. Nawẽnnɛ looge la nẽr-sɛba tɩ dũnia dõma bɩsrɩ tɩ ba de la nasdõma la, botɩ ba bɔ ẽ sɩra dɛna bõntatba, tɩ ba wʋn wa bɔna a so'olʋm tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ na pʋam.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: La yãma pɔ'ɔrɩ la nasdõma. La dagɩ bõntatba n nãmsrɩ ya, dee yõgra ya sẽnna sarɩyaditba zẽ'am?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: La de la bãma n pɔ'ɔrɩ Krisi yʋ'ʋr-sõŋɔ sɛka tɩ ba bɔ ya la.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ya sãn sakɛ ɩta Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɔɔ la n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa la», ya ẽŋɛ sõŋa.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: La ya sãn tũ'usra nẽrba, ya tõm na tõon-be'ego, la lɔɔ la pa'alɛ ya tɩ ya tõm na tõon-be'ego.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Nẽr-sɛka sãn sakɛ lɔɔ la wuu, dee sɩ'ɩsɛ ayɩla tõm tõon-be'ego, a sɩ'ɩsɛ la ba wuu.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ yalsɩ» la, ẽŋa n ken yetɩ: «Da kʋ nẽra». La fʋ sãn ka ẽŋɛ yalsɩ, dee kʋ nẽra, fʋ de la nẽra n sɩ'ɩsɛ lɔɔ la.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Tɔgra-ya dee tõnna tɩ la masɛ la lɔɔ n yolsrɩ nẽrba la, mina tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ la ẽŋa di ya sarɩya.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa dita sarɩya, a kãn zoe ninbãalga la nẽr-sɛka n ka zotɩ a taaba ninbãalga. Fʋ sãn zoe nẽrba ninbãalga, Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn zoe fʋ ninbãalga.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: M sɔbiisi, nẽra sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, dee ka tõnna tõoma n pa'alɩ bɛla, a yõorɔ de la bẽm? Bɔ sɩra ẽŋa tã wʋn fãagɛ ẽ mɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ, wʋ sɔbia n ka tarɩ fuugo yɛɛra, dee ka tara dita.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: tɩ yãma nẽra ayɩla sãn yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm ka di tɩgɛ, dee yɛ fuugo tɩ ɔɔrɔ da tara fɔ» dee ka bɔ ẽ sõŋrɛ sɛka tɩ a boorɩ la, a yõorɔ de la bẽm?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: La mẽ de la bɛla la bɔ sɩra, ya sãn bɔ sɩra tɩ la ka lagɩm na tõoma, a de la zãŋa.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: La nẽr-sɛka tã mɛ yetɩ, ayẽma bɔ la sɩra, la ayẽma tõnnɩ la tõon-sõma. Mam wʋn yele ẽ yetɩ, pa'alɛ mam fʋ bɔ sɩra la n ka lagɩm na tõoma la, tɩ mam wʋn dɩkɛ m tõon-sõma la pa'alɛ tɩ mam bɔ sɩra.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Fõn sakɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a n boe. La ãn sõŋa. Kʋlkã'arsɩ mẽ sakɛ la bɛla, dee gõgra la dabeem.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: A ka tarɩ yɛm dãana, fõn boorɩ mɛ tɩ m pa'alɛ fɔ tɩ fʋ sãn bɔ sɩra tɩ la ka lagɩm na tõoma, a ka tarɩ yõorɔ?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: Tɩ yaaba Abraham tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ a masɛ ya la, la de la a tõoma ĩyã, ẽn yʋʋn dɩkɛ a dayʋa A'izakɩ tɩ a kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Fʋ yẽ mɛ tɩ a bɔ sɩra, la a tõoma la lagɩm na taaba. A tõoma la ĩyã n botɩ a bɔ sɩra la masɛ sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: «Abraham bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ a bɔ sɩra la, yetɩ a masɛ mɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.» Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ yetɩ a sɩrɛ.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Ya yẽ tɩ la de la nẽra tõoma n botɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ya, la dagɩ a bɔ sɩra mã'a ĩyã.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: La yʋʋn mẽ de la bɛla la Arahabɩ n de pɔka n ɩtɩ yalsɩ la. Zifdõma n yʋʋn tõm nẽrba la, tɩ a to'oge ba sʋgɛ a yire, botɩ ba doose sore ayẽma kule la, tõon-ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Nẽra sɩa sãn fõrgɛ deege ẽ, a ĩyã de la kũm. La mẽ de la bɛla, nẽra sãn bɔ sɩra tɩ la ka lagɩm na tõoma, la de la zãŋa.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: M sɔbiisi, da kãn bɔɔra-ya tɩ ya wuu dɛna karẽnsãandõma. Se'ere n soe la, ya mi tɩ tõma sɛba n de karẽnsãandõma la sarɩyadia n wʋn dɛna toogo gãnna.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Tõma wuu tuuri mɛ zozo'e. Nẽra sãn ka tuura a yetɔga pʋam, a de la nẽra n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tãna wʋn yõkra a mẽŋa sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Tõma sãn ẽŋɛ wiiri nõorɛ salba tɩ ba sakɛ ɩta tɩn boorɩ se'em, ba ĩyã la wuu ɩtɩ la tõma n boorɩ se'em.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Ken bɩsɛ-ya õornɔ n ãn se'em na, baa ban kãrgɛ se'em, dee tɩ kʋseb-kãra vugra zã'ara ba la, ba tarɩ la dɔ-pɩka wẽrgra ba, tɩ ba sẽnna wɛ'ɛsa zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ sɛka n tarɩ ba la boorɩ.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Nẽrsaala zɩlɩŋa mẽ ãn na bɛla, a de la ĩyã zẽ'a ayɩla n ãn pɩka pɩka, dee tɔgra yel-kãra. La de la bug-pɩka n tã wʋn di we-kãtɛ wuu.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Zɩlɩŋa mẽ ãn wʋ bugum na. A boe la tɩ ĩyã la pʋam tõnna tõon-be'ero tɩ la taara tɩ ĩyã la wuu. A bugum na ze'etɩ la bug-tẽŋa sã'ana tɩ vɔm wuu.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Nẽrsaala tã dũnsi buuri wuu, la bõn-ẽgla buuri wuu, la bõn-vuula buuri wuu, la sɛba n boe ko'om pʋam wuu mɛ, botɩ ba sakra ẽ.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: La nẽra ka boe n tã wʋn yãŋɛ a zɩlɩŋa. A pɩrɛ la yetɔg-be'ero tɔgra ka gɔ'ɔra, a tarɩ la yabga n kʋʋrɩ.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Tɩ tarɩ la ẽŋa pʋ'ʋsra Zuudãana n de tɩ Sɔ la a yel-sõnnɛ, dee ken tara ẽ kã'ara be'em bɔ'ɔra nẽrba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nãam tɩ ba ãna wʋ a mẽŋa la.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Kã'a-be'ero, la kã'a-sõma ze'etɩ la nõ-ẽŋa ayɩla pʋam yɛsra. M sɔbiisi, la ka masɛ tɩ la dɛna bɛla.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Ko-sõma, la ko-be'ero tã wʋn bulla bulnifo ayɩla pʋam? M sɔbiisi, kẽnkãŋa tã wʋn wɔnna tã'ama?.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: Bɩɩ sẽnsãabga tã wʋn wɔnna kẽnkãma? Bɛla, bulga n bulli ko-be'ero ka tã wʋn bulla ko-sõma.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Ãne n boe ya pʋam tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa tarɩ yɛm, la bãŋrɛ? A ẽŋɛ yel-ɩr-sõŋɔ, dee sike a mẽŋa pa'alɛ tɩ a tarɩ yɛm.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: La, la sãn dɛna tɩ ya ɩtɩ la sũure, dee gẽela taaba, da wẽ-ya yõ'ogɔ tɩ ya de la yɛm dõma parɩm sɩ'ɩsɛ sɩra la.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Yɛm ẽŋa dagɩ yɛm sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. A de la tẽŋa wã zuo bõnɔ, a de la nẽrsaala bõnɔ, a ze'ele la Asũtãana zẽ'am.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Sũure la gẽelgɔ n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, tõon-yooro, la tõon-be'ero buuri wuu boe la bilam.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: La yɛm n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am ãn na sõŋa ka tara dẽgrɔ, dee botɩ nẽra tara sũ-mã'asʋm la a taaba, la sũ-bʋgsʋm, ɩta sugri, zɔta nẽrba ninbãalga zozo'e, tõnna tõon-sõma zozo'e, ka bɔkra nẽrba, dee ka dɛna pʋlibsum dãana.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Sũ-mã'asʋm dõma n botɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna nẽrba tẽŋasʋka la tõoma de la tõon-sõma n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Bɔ sɩra de la fõn mi sɩra sɩra tɩ fõn tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la, fʋ wʋn yẽ, a botɩ fʋ mina la sɛla n ka yẽtɩ la nini.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: La de la kʋrʋm kʋrʋm dõma n yʋʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a tɔgɛ ba yelle sõŋa.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: La de la tõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ tõma bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ la a yetɔga nãam tẽŋa la saazuum, bɛla tɩ tõma mina tɩ sɛla n yẽtɩ wã ze'ele la sɛla n ka yẽtɩ pʋam.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Abɛlɩ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a yʋʋn kãabɛ kãabgɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna Akãɩ kãabgɔ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la bɔ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ to'oge a bɔ'a la, dee yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am. Abɛlɩ n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, baa ẽn ki la, a yelle ken tɔgra mɛ.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: La de la A'enõkɩ n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn zẽkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ a yire, tɩ a ka ki, tɩ ba yʋʋn ka le yẽ ẽ, Nawẽnnɛ n zẽkɛ ẽ na ĩyã. Lan dẽŋɛ a zẽkrɛ la, la gʋlsɛ a yelle yetɩ, a paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm mɛ.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Nẽra sãn ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, a ka tã wʋn paagɛ a yɛm. Nẽra sãn lɛnna Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔ sɩra tɩ a boe mɛ, la a yɔɔrɩ sɛba n eerɩ ẽ mɛ.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Anoe n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a zoe Nawẽnnɛ sakɛ a nõorɛ. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn pa'alɛ ẽ na bõn-sɛba n wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrba nãn ka yẽtɩ, tɩ a sakɛ mãalɛ õorŋɔ kãtɛ, tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma kẽ mĩ yẽ fãarɛ. Bɛla a pa'alɛ tɩ dũnia dõma n ka sakɛ la koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ, dee tɩ ẽŋa masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ẽn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Abraham n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a yʋʋn sakɛ a wi'a, yese wɛ'ɛsa tẽn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ na bɔbrɛ, dee ka mina ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'e-sɛka la.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Abraham n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, a yʋʋn sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era tẽn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ na pʋam wʋ sãana la, yʋʋn tĩ fuu-deego bɔna mĩ. A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ mẽ yʋʋn kẽ'erɩ la fuu-deto pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la bãma wuu.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Abraham yʋʋn tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tẽn-sɛka ẽbrɛ n ẽebɛ sõŋa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa dɛna sɛka n makɛ dee mẽ.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: La de la Asaara n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ baa ẽn yʋʋn de kõndɔgrɛ dee kʋrgɛ zo'oge la, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ mɛ tɩ a dɔgɛ bia, se'ere n soe la, a bɔ la sɩra tɩ sɛka n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la, wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽn yele se'em na mɛ.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Bɛla n soe, tɩ bʋraaga ayɩla n yʋʋn kʋrgɛ ka zãagɛ la kũm, dɔgɛ yɩɩsɩ tɩ ba zo'oge ãna wʋ wõrbiɛ n boe saazuum, bɩɩ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm tɩ nẽrba ka tã wʋn kãalɛ ba.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Nẽr-bãma wuu ki la ba bɔ sɩra la pʋam. Ba yʋʋn ka to'oge bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ba la, la ba yʋʋn tũule yẽ ba la halɩ zãazãarɛ, tɩ ba sũure ãna yẽlʋm. La ba bãŋɛ mɛ yele tɩ bãma de la sãama ka tara zẽ'a dũnia zuo.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Sɛba n tɔgrɩ wãna la pa'alɩ tɩ ba eerɩ la tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn wi'ira ba mẽŋa tẽŋa.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Ba yʋʋn sãn tẽ'esra tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba yese dee basɛ la yelle tɩ a sɩa tara ba, ba tarɩ nɩ sore wʋn lebe bilam mɛ.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Lan de se'em na, ba yʋʋn eerɩ la tẽn-sɛka n ãn sõŋa gãnna. Tẽn-ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ yire. Bɛla n soe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka zoe yãnnɛ tɩ ba wi'ira ẽ tɩ ba Nawẽnnɛ, la de la ẽŋa n mãasʋm tẽŋa bɔ ba.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: La de la Abraham n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a yʋʋn dɩkɛ A'izakɩ tɩ a kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ yetɩ a dɩkɛ a dayʋa la bɔ ẽ na. A'izakɩ n de a dayɔ-yẽŋa la tɩ a yʋʋn dɩkɛ tɩ a kãabɛ bɔ, dee tɩ la dɛna la ẽŋa yelle tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la ẽ lɛ.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ: «La wʋn doose la A'izakɩ tɩ fʋ yẽ yɩɩsɩ tɩ mam bĩŋe nõorɛ la.».

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abraham yʋʋn tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn vo'oge A'izakɩ mɛ. Bɛla n soe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lebse bɔ ẽ a dayʋa. La ãn wʋ a vo'oge ẽ mɛ na, yel-ẽna pʋam mãmsgɔ n boe mĩ.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: La de la A'izakɩ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a kã kã'a-sõma n de sɛla n sẽm bɔ Azakɔbɩ, la A'eso.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Azakɔbɩ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ẽn yʋʋn wa yɔna kũm na, tɩ a kã kã'a-sõma bɔ Azozɛfʋ dayɔɔsɩ bayi, tike a dasaarɛ ka dũma nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Azozɛfʋ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ẽn yʋʋn wa dee kũm na, tɩ a yele Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yetɩ, ba wʋn wa yese Ezipti tẽŋa mɛ, dee pa'alɛ ba wʋn wa ẽŋɛ ẽŋa kõba se'em.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Amoyisi dɔgrɩba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ba yʋʋn dɔgɛ ẽ, dɩkɛ ẽ sʋgɛ wõr-sɩtã. Ba yẽ mɛ tɩ a de la bia n ãn sõŋa, tɩ ba ka zoe dabeem la naba la n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Amoyisi n yʋʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ẽn wa lebge bõnkɛka la, tɩ a zagsɛ tɩ ba da wi'ira ẽŋa tɩ naba Afaarõ pɔyʋa la dayʋa.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: A yʋʋn boorɩ mɛ tɩ a lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la po nãmsra, gãnna ẽŋa n ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga la sɛla n de be'em wakatɛ fẽfẽe.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: A bɩsɛ mɛ tɩ ba sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽŋa wʋ ba wʋn pɔ'ɔgɛ Krisi la, n tarɩ yõorɔ zo'oge gãnna Ezipti tẽŋa bõntarsõm wuu. Se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura yɔɔrɔ n boe nẽŋa.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Ẽn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a ze'ele Ezipti fõrgɛ dee ka zoe naba la sũ-isgre, a yʋʋn mõrgɛ mɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, se'ere n soe la, a yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ ba ka yẽtɩ la nini.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: La ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma kʋ Zɔɔg-basɛ pebila ẽŋɛ zɩɩm mĩise deto nõa, tɩ malɛka sɛka n wʋn kʋ kɔm-kẽendõma la, da kʋ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma kɔm-kẽendõma.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ mõg-mõlga la ko'om yʋʋn welge dee basɛ tẽn-kɛ'ɛŋa tɩ ba doose tole, tɩ Ezipti dõma mẽ paagɛ doose tɩ ba tole, tɩ ko'om di ba.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ba yʋʋn sẽŋɛ kaagɛ Zeriko tẽŋa dabsa ayopɔɩ, tɩ a lalsɩ lui.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Arahabɩ n de pɔg-sɛka n yʋʋn ɩtɩ yalsɩ la n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a yʋʋn to'oge nẽr-sɛba n sẽŋɛ tɩ ba lalsɛ bɩsɛ Zeriko tẽŋa n ãn se'em na a yire sõŋa, bɛla tɩ a ka po naagɛ la sɩ'ɩsrɩba la ki.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Yɛla zozo'e boe mɛ tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ nɩ, la nẽŋa ka boe tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ Azedeyõ, la Abarakɩ, la Asãmsõ, la Azefte, la Adavidi, la Asãmwɛlɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la yelle.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: La de la ban bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ba yãŋɛ na'adõma to'ose ba so'olʋm, ba tõm sɛla n masɛ mɛ, ba yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe nõorɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ mɛ, ba mũm gɩgma nõa mɛ.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: La ba kĩŋe bugum n diti la pãŋa mɛ, ba põsɛ sʋkɔbgɔ kũm mɛ, ba yẽ pãŋa mɛ ba tɔrgɔ pʋam, dɛna gãndɔɔrɔ zɛbrɛ zẽ'a, zɛbɛ yãŋɛ satẽma zɛbzɛbrɩba dige ba.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Pɔgsɩ yẽ ba nẽrba n ki mɛ dee vo'oge. Sɛba yẽ nãmsgɔ mɛ zozo'e dee zagsɛ yolsgo, se'ere n soe la, ba boorɩ tɩ ba wa vo'oge mɛ yẽ vɔm n ãn sõŋa gãnna.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Ba la'arɩ sɛba mɛ, wẽ ba la kafõbsɩ, ba ba sɛba mɛ la bãnsɩ kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Ba lobe sɛba la kuga kʋ, wã sɛba la sɔya kʋ, sɛ basɛba la sʋkɔbrɔ kʋ. Tɩ sɛba ɛɛra yɛ'ɛna, yɛ piisi gãna bɩɩ bʋʋsɩ gãna, sakɛ ka tara sɛla sɛla, tɩ ba fɔ'ɔna ba, nãmsra ba.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Ba yʋʋn eerɩ la weem, la tãnsɩ zuto, gã tãnsɩ yogro, la tẽŋa yogro pʋam. Dũnia ka narɩ tɩ a tara nẽr-bãma.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Nẽr-bãma wuu, Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ ba yelle sõŋa mɛ ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã, la baa bɛla ba ka to'oge Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ba sɛla la.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Zɛbrɛ la nõkɛ'ɛma n boe ya tẽŋasʋka la ze'ele la bɛ? La de la yan boorɩ se'em n de be'em n boe ya pʋam botɩ ya ɩta bɛla.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna bɔ tõma, bɛla tɩ a ka botɩ ba masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tɩ tõma ka naagɛ la ba.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Ya boorɩ la sɛla dee ka yẽta, bɔɔra tɩ ya kʋʋra nẽrba. Ya ɩtɩ la sũure la ya taaba bõnɔ dee ken ka yẽta, bɛla tɩ ya zɛbra dee wẽ'era nõkɛ'ɛma la taaba. Yan ka tarɩ la, la de la yãma n ka sose Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: La ya sãn sɔsra mɛ dee ka yẽta, yãma n sosrɩ se'em na ka ãn sõŋa. Ya sosrɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la ya yɛm n boorɩ se'em.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Yãma ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ la sɩra. Yãma ka mi tɩ sɛka n nõŋɛ dũnia wã lɔgrɔ, la de la be'em la Nawẽnnɛ? Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a yõgɛ sɩtɔ la dũnia wã lɔgrɔ, a wʋn dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ bɛ.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Bɩɩ yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ lan gʋlsɛ se'em Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na de la zãŋa? La yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a ẽŋɛ tɩ pʋam na, boorɩ tɩ ẽŋa mã'a n sɔna tɔ.».

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: La a ɩtɩ la yel-sõnnɛ n ãn sõŋa gãnna bɔ'ɔra tɔ, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ zagsrɩ zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma mɛ, dee ɩta sik-m-mẽŋa dõma yel-sõnnɛ.».

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Bɛla, dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, dee sɩ'ɩsɛ Asũtãana, tɩ a wʋn zoe dee basɛ ya.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Lɛm-ya Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a mẽ wʋn lɛm ya. Tõon-be'ero dõma, peege ya nu'usi, pʋtẽ'era ayi dõma, peege ya sũure.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya ya ninbãalga, kaasɛ-ya fablɛ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya lɔ'ɔrɔ lebge kʋʋnɔ, tɩ ya sũ-yẽlga lebge sũ-sã'aŋɔ.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Sike-ya ya mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, tɩ a wʋn zẽkɛ ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: M sɔbiisi, da tɔgra-ya taaba yelle be'em. Nẽr-sɛka n tɔgɛ a tadãana yelle be'em, bɩɩ zɛrgra ẽ, a tɔgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la be'em dee zɛrgra ẽ. La fʋ sãn zɛrgra lɔɔ la, fʋ ka sakrɩ lɔɔ la, fʋ lebge la lɔɔ la sarɩyadita.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n bɔ'ɔrɩ lɔɔ, la ẽŋa mã'a n diti sarɩya, la ẽŋa mã'a n tã wʋn fãagɛ, bɩɩ sã'am. La fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn de la ãne, dita fʋ tadãana sarɩya?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Selse-ya! Yãma sɛba n yetɩ: «Zĩna bɩɩ beere, tõma wʋn sẽŋɛ la tẽn-ẽna ka ẽŋɛ yʋʋnɛ, leebe yẽ yõorɔ» la.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: ya ka mi ya vɔm n wʋn ãna se'em beere. Ya ãn wʋ pika n yʋ, tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe tɩ a pilge.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: La de nɩ na ya ẽn yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn bɔɔra, tɩ tõma vɔa, tõma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãna la wãna.».

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: La yãma pa'alɩ la ya mẽŋa dee wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ yãma n boorɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sɛla la pʋam. Pa'al-m-mẽŋa wãna buuri wuu de la be'em.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n mi sɛla n ãn sõŋa, dee ka ɩta, a tõm na tõon-be'ero.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Bõntatba, selse-ya! Kaasra-ya dee fabla, se'ere n soe la, toogo n sẽm ya zuto.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Ya bõntarsõm na wʋn põ'ogɛ mɛ. Bõn-mõgla n wʋn mõgɛ ya futo la.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Tẽŋanzẽem n wʋn kẽ ya sãlma, la ya zũuro la. Tẽŋanzẽem na n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, la a wʋn di ya wʋ bugum na. Yãma lagsrɩ la bõntarsõm bĩŋra dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Tõntõnɩba n tõnnɩ ya vatɔ pʋam tɩ ya mõŋɛ ba yɔɔrɔ la kellɩ mɛ. Nẽr-sɛba n lagsɛ ya bõnkɔɔla la kellɩ mɛ, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la wõm.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Dũnia zuo ya vɔa la na'am pʋam, ɩta ya yɛm n boorɩ se'em, dita tɩgra nõbra, ãna wʋ dũnsi la gu'ura kʋa daarɛ.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Ya tɔgɛ la sɩra dãana yelle kʋ, yetɩ ya kʋ ẽ, tɩ a ka tãna wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ ya tɩ ya kʋ ẽ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: M sɔbiisi, mã'ara ya sũure gu'ura Zuudãana la lemŋo. Bɩsɛ-ya kaara n mã'arɩ a sũure se'em gu'ura tɩ a va'am bõnkɔɔla biɛ wʋn wa bɩ sõŋa. A ẽn gu'ura mɛ tɩ sig-saaga wa ni, tɩ poorʋm saasɩ ni, tɩ bõnkɔɔla la biɛ bɩ sõŋa.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Bɛla, yãma mẽ mõrgɛ mã'ara ya sũure, tara bʋraanɛ, tɩ Zuudãana la lemŋo lɛm mɛ.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: M sɔbiisi, da yõnna-ya taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ da wa di ya sarɩya. Bɩsɛ-ya, sarɩyadita la ze la zanõrʋm.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: M sɔbiisi, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yetɔga, tɩ ba nãmsɛ ba, tɩ ba mã'agɛ ba sũure la, ya mẽ ẽŋɛ wʋ bãma n ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Tõma yetɩ mɛ tɩ sɛba n mõrgɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore pʋam na, de la zu-yẽlga dõma. Ya wõm ban tɔgɛ Azɔɔbɩ n ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ se'em yelle, la ya yẽ ba'asgɔ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ se'em bɔ ẽ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ de la ninbãal-zɔta, dee tara nẽrba zĩile.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: M sɔbiisi, sɛla n gãnnɩ wuu de la ya da pɔɩ. Da pɔɩ saazuum, bɩɩ tẽŋa, bɩɩ sɛla ayẽma. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya «ẽe» dɛna «ẽe», tɩ ya «ayɛɩ» dɛna «ayɛɩ», tɩ Nawẽnnɛ da di ya sarɩya.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam nãmsra, a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ. Nẽr-sɛka sũure n ãn yẽlʋm, a yõom yõom-sõma pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam bẽ'era, a wi Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba kẽma, tɩ ba ẽŋɛ kaam ẽŋɛ a zuugo la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Ban bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra pʋ'ʋsɛ la, la wʋn fãagɛ bã'ara la mɛ, Zuudãana la wʋn bɔ ẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ tɩ a isge. La a sãn tõm tõon-be'ero, a wʋn basɛ ẽ taalɛ.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Bɛla, pelge ya pʋa tɔgɛ ya tõon-be'ero pa'alɛ taaba, pʋ'ʋsɛ bɔ taaba, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Nẽra n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am pʋ'ʋsgɔ tõnnɩ mɛ la pãŋa zozo'e.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: A'eli yʋʋn de la nẽrsaala wʋ tõma la, la a pʋ'ʋsɛ mɛ sose Nawẽnnɛ la a sũure wuu tɩ saaga da ni, tɩ saaga ka tɔkɛ tẽŋa yʋʋma atã la wõr-sɩyoobɩ.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Tɩ a le pʋ'ʋsɛ, tɩ saaga ni, tɩ bõnkɔɔla wɔm biɛ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: M sɔbiisi, ya nẽra ayɩla sãn bʋrgɛ dee basɛ sɩra sore, tɩ nẽra tarɩ ẽ leme wa'am.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: ya bãŋɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n tarɩ tõon-be'ero dãana a tõon-be'ero sorɔɔgɔ zuo leme wa'am, a fãagɛ la tõon-be'ero dãana kũm pʋam, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ a tõon-be'ero zozo'e taalɛ.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Mam Apɩyɛɛrɩ n de Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tɩ ya dɛna a nẽrba wɩrgɛ bɔna Põ, la Galatɩ, la Kapadɔsɩ, la Azi, la Bitini so'olʋm pʋam dɛna sãama la.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: La de la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n looge ya wʋ ẽŋa n dẽŋɛ looge a yɛm se'em na, tɩ a Sɩa welge ya to'ore, tɩ Azezi-krisi zɩɩm peege ya tɩ ya ãna sõŋa, sakra a nõorɛ. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm zozo'e.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la a yel-sõnnɛ. A ninbãal-zʋa n zo'oge la ĩyã tɩ a botɩ tɩ yẽ vɔm paalga, Azezi-krisi n ki dee vo'oge la ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ tɩ tara pʋtẽ'erɛ wakatɛ wuu gu'ura sɛla tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: dee mina tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba. Bõn-bãma de la sɛla n ka sã'anɩ, n ka põ'orɩ, tɩ ba sõŋa ka bɔtɩ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe a yire tɩ a bɔ ya.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: Yãma n bɔ sɩra la, Nawẽnnɛ gu'uri ya mɛ la a pãŋa halɩ wʋn ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ wakatɛ fãagɛ ya ba'asɛ.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ ya tara sũ-yẽlga, baa la de ya sãn yẽta yɛla to'oto'ore n nãmsrɩ ya wakatɛ fẽe lɛɛlɛ wã.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Nãmsgɔ la de la sɛla n makrɩ bɩsra ya bɔ sɩra la n ãn se'em. Sãlma de la sɛla n sã'anɩ, la ba ẽn dɩkɛ ẽ makɛ la bugum bɩsɛ ẽn ãn se'em. La yãma bɔ sɩra la tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ zozo'e gãnna sãlma. Bɛla tɩ la makra ya bɔ sɩra la bɩsra, tɩ ya sãn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, ya wʋn yẽ pẽka, la nã'asgɔ Azezi-krisi lemŋo daarɛ.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Yãma nãn ka yẽ ẽ, dee nõŋɛ ẽ, ya nãn ka yẽtɩ ẽ, dee bɔ ẽ sɩra, bɛla tɩ ya ɩta sũ-yẽlga zozo'e n ka tã wʋn pa'alɛ.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ya yẽ fãarɛ mɛ n de ya bɔ sɩra la yõorɔ.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n yʋʋn tɔgɛ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wa ẽŋɛ bɔ ya la, yʋʋn ɛ mɛ vẽesɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ fãarɛ wã yelle.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Azezi-krisi Sɩa yʋʋn boe la ba mɛ, dẽŋɛ pa'alɛ ba yetɩ Krisi wʋn wa yẽ la nãmsgɔ dee yãŋa yẽ na'am pãŋa. Tɩ ba yʋʋn ɛɛra tɩ ba bãŋɛ wakatɛ sɛka tɩ bõn-bãma wʋn ẽŋɛ, la lan wʋn wa doose se'em tɩ la ẽŋɛ.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn pa'alɛ ba tɩ yel-sɛba tɩ ba mõolɩ la dagɩ bãma mẽŋa ĩyã, la de la yãma ĩyã. Yel-bãma n bɛla tɩ sɛba n mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la tɔgɛ bɔ ya. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm a Sɩa tɩ a wa'am wa bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba tɔgɛ. Tɩ baa malɛkadõma la mẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ ba wõm a võorɛ.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, yõkɛ ya tagsgɔ tara ya mẽŋa sõŋa, dee gu'ura la ya sũure wuu yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ya tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ ya Azezi-krisi lemŋo wakatɛ la.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Sakra-ya Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, da le vɔa-ya wʋ yãma n daan nãn ka bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ ya yɛmleego botɩ ya bɔɔra sɛla n de be'em na.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Dɛna-ya pʋpeelem dõma ya yel-ɩrɛ wuu pʋam, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya la n de pʋpeelem dãana se'em na.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Dɛna-ya pʋpeelem dõma, tɩ mam de la pʋpeelem dãana.».

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Yãma n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na, ya wi'iri ẽ tɩ ya Sɔ. Nawẽnnɛ ka tũ'usri nẽrba. A diti nẽrsaalba sarɩya la sɩra, wʋ nẽra woo tõoma n de se'em na. Bɛla, pa'alɛ-ya tɩ yãma nannɩ ẽ ya yel-ɩrɛ wuu pʋam, yãma n ken bɔna dũnia zuo wã.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ ya mɛ vɔm sɛka n ka tarɩ yõorɔ tɩ ya daan zãsʋm ya yaabdõma zẽ'am na pʋam. Ya mi tɩ la dagɩ bõn-sɛba n sã'anɩ wʋ sãlma, bɩɩ zũuro tɩ a dɩkɛ yɔ fãagɛ ya.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: La de la Krisi zɩɩm n tarɩ pãŋa la. Ba kʋ Krisi la tõma ĩyã wʋ pebila n ãn sõŋa dee ka tara zãnnɛ.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn põn looge ẽ mɛ halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ, yãŋa botɩ a wa'am dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ wã yãma ĩyã.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: Krisi ĩyã tɩ ya bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge Krisi, bɔ ẽ na'am pãŋa, bɛla tɩ ya dɩkɛ ya bɔ sɩra, la yan tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la te'ege Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Yãma n sakɛ sɩra la, ya lebge la pʋpeelem dõma, tɩ ya nõŋɛ ya sɔbiisi la nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra. Bɛla, nõŋɛ-ya taaba zozo'e la ya sũure wuu.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Ba dɔgɛ yãma la dɔg-paalga, la dagɩ nẽrsaala dɔka n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm sɛka n sã'anɩ la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n vɔa dee bɔna wakatɛ wuu la, n botɩ ya yẽ dɔg-paalga tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala woo ãn wʋ mõorɔ, la a sõŋa la wuu ãn wʋ mõ-puuro la. Mõorɔ zẽkrɩ mɛ, tɩ ba puuro la sʋʋra.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.» Yetɔg-ẽŋa n de kõ-yẽlga la tɩ ba mõolɛ bɔ ya la.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Bɛla, basɛ-ya pʋtʋʋlgɔ wuu, la põmpɔrŋɔ, la pʋlibsum, la sũure, la taaba pɔ'ɔrɛ.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Nõŋɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga wʋ bilɛɛsɩ n nõŋɛ ba ma bĩ'isum mã'a la. Tɩ a wʋn basɛ tɩ ya bɩ Azezi-krisi sore doosgo la pʋam, yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: Tɩ yãma bãŋɛ Zuudãana la n ãn sõŋa se'em mɛ.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Lɛm-ya Zuudãana n de vɔm dãana la zẽ'am, ẽŋa ãn wʋ kug-sɛka tɩ nẽrba zagsɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge ẽ tɩ a tara yõorɔ zozo'e.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Yãma mẽ vɔa mɛ. Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ ya tɩ ya ãna wʋ kuga mẽ deego tɩ a Sɩa bɔna mĩ, tɩ ya ãna wʋ kãabgɔ kẽma bɔna mĩ tõnna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em, tɩ la paara a yɛm, Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, mam looge la kugre n tarɩ yõorɔ zozo'e ẽebɛ m deego Siyõ, ẽŋa n de kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa. Nẽr-sɛka n bɔ ẽ sɩra, yãnnɛ kãn yõkɛ ẽ.».

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Yãma sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la, a tarɩ la yõorɔ zozo'e bɔ ya. La sɛba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la, la gʋlsɛ ba yelle yetɩ: «Kug-sɛka tɩ tõnmẽtba zagsɛ la n lebge kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.».

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «La de la kug-ẽŋa n wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba wẽ'era nãma, dee dɛna tãmpĩ-sɛka n wʋn basɛ tɩ ba lui.» Ban wẽ'erɩ nãma la, la de la ban ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga sɩra la ĩyã, wʋ lan masɛ tɩ la ẽŋɛ ba se'em na.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, yãma de la buuri tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge, ya de la ẽŋa n de naba la kãabgɔ kẽma, dee ken dɛna a nẽrba n welge to'ore tara pʋpeelem. A wi ya la lika pʋam, tarɩ yese a peelem n ãn sõŋa zozo'e pʋam, tɩ ya botɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ a tõon-kãra la.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Kʋrʋm, ya daan dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la lɛɛlɛ wã ya de la a nẽrba. A daan ka zotɩ ya ninbãalga, la a yãŋa zotɩ ya ninbãalga mɛ.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, yãma de la sãama la kɔɔs-weto dũnia zuo, bɛla mam kã'anɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya basɛ nẽrsaalba n boorɩ se'em n de be'em n sã'anɩ ya la.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ ãna sõŋa nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na tẽŋasʋka, tɩ baa ba sãn dõta ya yetɩ ya de la tõon-be'ero dõma, ba wʋn yẽ ya tõon-sõma la, nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ a wa'aŋɔ daarɛ.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Sakra-ya dũnia pãnsɩ dõma wuu Zuudãana Azezi ĩyã. Sakra-ya na-keko n soe ke'eŋo wuu.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: la ya sakra gɔmnadõma tɩ a tõm tɩ ba sɩbgrɩ sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, dee nã'asra nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-sõma la.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em de la ya ɩta sõŋa, basɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka mi sɛla dee tɔgra gɔɔn-yetɔga la go'oge.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Ɩta-ya wʋ nẽrba n soe ba mẽŋa, la da dɩkɛ-ya yan soe ya mẽŋa la tɩ la dɛna sɛla n botɩ ya tõnna be'em, tõnna-ya wʋ Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba n masɛ tɩ ba tõnna se'em na.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Nanna-ya nẽrba wuu, nõŋɛ ya sɔbiisi, zɔta-ya Nawẽnnɛ, nanna-ya na-keko.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Tõntõnɩba, sakra-ya ya zuudãandõma dee zɔta ba. La, la dagɩ sɛba n de nẽr-sõma, la sũ-bʋgsʋm dõma la mã'a, sakra-ya baa sɛba n de nẽr-tɔɔsɩ la.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Nẽra sãn sakra nãmsgɔ n ka masɛ nɩ tɩ a nãmsɛ, ẽn sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã, la de la yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Fʋ sãn tõm na tõon-be'ero tɩ ba nãmsra fɔ tɩ fʋ sakɛ wĩbra, a ka tarɩ yõorɔ. La fʋ sãn tõm na tõon-sõma tɩ ba nãmsra fɔ, tɩ fʋ sakɛ wĩbra, la de la yel-sõnnɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ya. Krisi mẽ daan nãmsɛ yãma ĩyã mɛ, basɛ mãmsgɔ tɩ ya dɔla a nabara ɩta wʋ ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ se'em na.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: A daan ka tõm tõon-be'ego, la põmpɔrŋɔ ka yese a nõorʋm.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
local: Ban daan tʋ ẽ na, a ka lebse tʋ, la ban daan nãmsɛ ẽ na, a daan ka zɛbɛ la ba, a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa basɛ la sɛka n diti sarɩya la sɩra la nu'usum.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: Ẽŋa n to'oge tõma zẽ'am ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, tɩ la sãn dɛna la tõon-be'ero tõoma, tõma ãn wʋ tɩ ki mɛ, dee vɔa ɩta sɛla n masɛ. A pɔglʋŋɔ la ĩyã tɩ ya yẽ laafɛ.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Ya daan ãn wʋ piisi n bɔɩ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, ya leme na ya dũnsĩina la zẽ'am, ẽŋa n bɩsrɩ ya.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Bɛla mẽ, pɔgsɩ, sakra-ya ya sɩrba, tɩ baa sɛba n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga sɩra la sãn yẽ ya yel-ɩrɛ, ba wʋn sakɛ tɩ la ka doose la yetɔga.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: ban yẽtɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ n ãn sõŋa dɛna nã'asgɔ la.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽnnõm dɛna yẽŋa sõŋa, n de zuto pã'aŋɔ, la sãlma lɔgrɔ, la fu-sõma yɛa.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya sõŋa la dɛna sũure pʋam sõŋa n de sũ-bʋgsʋm la sũ-mã'asʋm. Sõŋa ẽna n ka sã'anɩ dee tara yõorɔ zozo'e Nawẽnnɛ nifum.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, pɔg-sɛba n yʋʋn de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, sõŋa mẽ yʋʋn ãn na bɛla, sakra ba sɩrba.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: Asaara yʋʋn ãn na bɛla sakra a sɩra Abraham nõorɛ, wi'ira ẽ tɩ a zuudãana. Ya sãn tõnna tõon-sõma, dee ka basɛ tɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ya dabeem, ya de la a pɔyɔɔsɩ.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Bɛla mẽ, yãma sɛba n de sɩrba la, bɔna-ya la ya pɔgba la lɛ, la wõm taaba, la ya bãŋɛ tɩ ba ka tarɩ pãŋa wʋ yãma la, la ya nanna ba, tɩ bãma la yãma n wʋn lagɩm to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n de vɔm. Ɩta-ya bɛla tɩ sɛla da gu ya pʋ'ʋsgɔ.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Ba'asgɔ pʋam, mam yetɩ ya wuu tɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, la taaba zĩile, la sɔbiisi nõŋrɛ, la ninbãal-zʋa, la sik-m-mẽŋa.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ya be'em, ya da lebse ẽ be'em. Nẽra sãn tʋ ya, ya da tʋ lebse, tõn kã-ya kã'a-sõma bɔ ẽ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya la, la de la kã'a-sõma tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ya.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a vɔm ãna yẽlʋm, dee tɩ a yẽ sũ-yẽlga, dẽnɩ tɩ a yõkɛ a nõorɛ la yetɔg-be'ero, dee basɛ põmpɔrŋɔ.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: A zãagɛ a mẽŋa la tõon-be'ero, dee ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la a mõrgɛ ɛ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna nẽrba tẽŋasʋka da basɛ.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana bɩsrɩ la sɛba n de sɩra dõma, dee sɛlsra ba pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la a wẽrgrɩ a nẽŋa mɛ, dee basra nẽr-sɛba n de tõon-be'ero dõma.».

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: Yãma sãn mõrgɛ tõnna tõon-sõma, ãne n wʋn ẽŋɛ ya be'em?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
local: La baa nẽrba sãn nãmsra ya yan ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ ĩyã, ya wʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ. Bɛla, da zɔta-ya ba dabeem, la ya da basɛ tɩ la dãana ya.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: Nã'asra-ya Krisi ya sũure pʋam, basɛ-ya tɩ a dɛna ya Zuudãana. Mãasʋm-ya wakatɛ wuu lɛrgra nẽr-sɛka woo n sokrɩ ya, bɔɔra tɩ a bãŋɛ yãma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la yelle n ãn se'em na.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: La ya ɩta bɛla la sũ-bʋgsʋm, la wɔɔrɛ. Ɩta-ya sɛla tɩ ya mi ya sũure pʋam tɩ la ãn sõŋa, tɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ sɛba n tɔgrɩ ya be'em, yãma n tõnnɩ sõŋa Krisi ĩyã la.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa nãmsra tɩ la sãn dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ bɛla, bɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna yãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ be'em nãmsra.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Krisi nãmsɛ mɛ ki bʋyɩla tɩ a yese tõon-be'ero, ẽŋa n de sɛka n masɛ la, ki la sɛba n ka masɛ ĩyã, tɩ a tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ba kʋ ẽ mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa basɛ tɩ a vo'oge.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga bɔ sɩɩsɩ sɛba tɩ ba yʋ la.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: Sɩɩsɩ bãma de la nẽr-sɛba n yʋʋn ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ Anoe wakatɛ la. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn mã'agɛ la a sũure gu'ura, wakat-sɛka tɩ Anoe yʋʋn mãalɩ õorŋɔ la, õorŋɔ ẽŋa pʋam tɩ nẽrba fẽe, banii mã'a kẽ mĩ põsɛ ko'om na pʋam yẽ fãarɛ.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: Bɛla de la makrɛ pa'ala ko'om misga n fãarɩ ya lɛɛlɛ wã. Ko'om misga dagɩ ĩyã dẽgrɔ yɛsga yelle, la de la sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ nẽra looge yɛm tɩ a doose Nawẽnnɛ la pʋpeelem. Ko'om misga fãarɩ ya mɛ Azezi-krisi n ki dee vo'oge la ĩyã.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: Ẽŋa n zẽkɛ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire ka zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ malɛkadõma, la kɛ'ɛndõma, la pãnsɩ dõma wuu sakra ẽ.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Bɛla, Krisi n nãmsɛ a ĩyã pʋam na, ya mẽ keŋe ya mẽŋa tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ẽŋa mẽŋa. Nẽr-sɛka woo n nãmsɛ a ĩyã pʋam basɛ tõon-be'ero mɛ.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Tɩ ẽn ken vɔa dũnia zuo wakat-sɛka la, a kãn le vɔa wʋ nẽrsaala yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em, a wʋn vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Kʋrʋm, yãma n daan lagɩm na sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ ɩta bãma n boorɩ se'em na, da le ɩta-ya bɛla. Yãma daan lagɩm na ba mɛ, ba yalsɩ, la ba be'em yɛmleego, la ba dã-yũure, la ba pʋyã'an-yooro, la ba dã-buka, la ba baga kãabgɔ n de be'em na pʋam.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: La lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma n ka le lagɩm na ba lɛ tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ bãma la, ba ka bãŋɛ a võorɛ, tɩ ba tʋʋra ya.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: La ba wʋn wa tɔgɛ bãma n ẽŋɛ se'em bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n mãasʋm gu'ura tɩ a di sɛba n vɔa, la sɛba n ki sarɩya la.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ ba daan mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la mẽ bɔ sɛba n ki la, tɩ ban ki wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n di nẽrsaalba wuu sarɩya tɩ ba kiira la, ba tã'agɛ yẽ vɔm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: Sɛla woo ba'asgɔ lɛm mɛ. Bɛla ĩyã, tara-ya ya mẽŋa sõŋa, dee mõrgɛ la Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Sɛla n gãnnɩ wuu de la ya nõŋɛ taaba zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, nõŋlʋm n botɩ tɩ basra taaba tõon-be'ero zozo'e taalɛ.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: Ɩta-ya taaba sãanɔ, da yõnna.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Yãma nẽra woo to'oge la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a, dɩkɛ-ya ba sõŋɛ nẽrba basɛba. Bɛla tɩ ya wʋn dɛna nẽrba n bɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a to'oto'ore la yelle sõŋa.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n tɔgrɩ, a tɔgɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em, nẽr-sɛka n tõnnɩ sõŋrɛ tõoma, a sõŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ pãŋa se'em. Tɩ tõon-bãma wuu wʋn nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ, la pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, da basɛ-ya tɩ nãmsgɔ kãtɛ sɛka n paarɩ ya la ãna wʋ yel-di'iŋa bɔ ya.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: Tõn tara-ya sũ-yẽlga, tɩ yãma po nãmsra la Krisi nãmsgɔ, tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm wakatɛ sɛka tɩ a na'am pãŋa wʋn puke la.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Ba sãn tʋʋra ya yãma n de Krisi nẽrba la ĩyã, ya tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la n tarɩ nã'asgɔ zozo'e la boe la ya lɛ mɛ.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: La da basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka nãmsra ẽŋa n de nẽr-kʋʋra, bɩɩ nayiga, bɩɩ tõon-be'ero dãana, bɩɩ nẽra n kẽ'erɩ nẽrba yɛla n ka pakɛ ẽ pʋam.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: La nẽra sãn nãmsra ẽŋa n de Krisi nẽra ĩyã, a da zɔta yãnnɛ. A nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ, ban wi'iri ẽ Krisi nẽra la ĩyã.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di sarɩya, la a wʋn pɔsɛ la a mẽŋa nẽrba. La sãn dɛna tɩ a wʋn dẽŋɛ di la tõma sarɩya pɔspɔsɩ, sɛba n ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ba'asgɔ wʋn ãna la wãne?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna toogo bɔ tõon-sõma dãana dee tɩ a yẽ fãarɛ, tõon-be'ero dãana n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore yelle wʋn ãna la wãne?».

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛba n nãmsrɩ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na, ba ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa, dee dɩkɛ ba mẽŋa ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam ba dee dɛna sɩra dãana la nu'usum.

eng: Therefore, I have a request for the elders among you. I urge the elders.
local: Mam yetɩ m kã'an na sɛba n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba kẽma bɔna ya pʋam na. Mam mẽ de la kẽema, dee dɛna Krisi nãmsgɔ la kaset-dãana, la mam wʋn po yẽ a na'am pãŋa n wʋn wa puke la mɛ.

eng: Like shepherds, tend the flock of God among you. Watch over it. Dont shepherd because you must, but do it voluntarily for God. Dont shepherd greedily, but do it eagerly.
local: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya bɩsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la tɩ a gu'ule ya wʋ dũnsĩima la. La, la da kãn dɛna dɔk-m-zuugo yelle, la dɛna la yɛmleego wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na. Da ɩta-ya yan boorɩ bõntarsõm ĩyã, ɩta-ya ya yɛm n boe a pʋam ĩyã.

eng: Dont shepherd by ruling over those entrusted to your care, but become examples to the flock.
local: Da ɛɛra-ya tɩ ya sɔna sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ gu'ule ya la, la pãŋa. Dɛna-ya nẽrba tɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ wʋn pa'alɛ ba sɛla n ãn sõŋa, tɩ ba ãna wʋ yãma n ãn se'em na.

eng: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will receive an unfading crown of glory.
local: Tɩ dũnsĩin-kɛ'ɛma la sãn wa puke, ya wʋn to'oge yɔɔrɔ n ka sã'anɩ n tarɩ pẽka.

eng: In the same way, I urge you who are younger: accept the authority of the elders. And everyone, clothe yourselves with humility toward each other. God stands against the proud, but he gives favor to the humble.
local: Bɛla mẽ, kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ, sakra-ya kẽma la. La ya wuu tara sik-m-mẽŋa la taaba, tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sisri zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma mɛ, dee ɩta sik-m-mẽsɩ dõma yel-sõnnɛ.».

eng: Therefore, humble yourselves under Gods power so that he may raise you up in the last day.
local: Bɛla, sike ya mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo dãana la nẽŋam, tɩ wakatɛ sãn paagɛ, a wʋn zẽkɛ ya.

eng: Throw all your anxiety onto him, because he cares about you.
local: Basɛ-ya ya fablɩgɔ la wuu bɔ ẽ, tɩ a bɩsrɩ ya yelle mɛ.

eng: Be clearheaded. Keep alert. Your accuser, the devil, is on the prowl like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
local: Dɛna-ya nẽrba n yõkrɩ ya mẽŋa, dee guusra ya mẽŋa sõŋa, tɩ ya bɛ Asũtãana eerɩ ya mɛ, wʋ gɩgnɛ n kaarɩ ɛɛra sɛla tɩ a yõkɛ obe.

eng: Resist him, standing firm in the faith. Do so in the knowledge that your fellow believers are enduring the same suffering throughout the world.
local: Bɔ-ya Azezi-krisi sɩra ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, dee sɩ'ɩsɛ Asũtãana, la ya bãŋɛ tɩ ya sɔbiisi n boe dũnia zẽ'a woo mẽ nãmsrɩ la nãmsgɔ bãma mẽŋa buuri.

eng: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, the one who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, will himself restore, empower, strengthen, and establish you.
local: Ya wʋn ken nãmsɛ mɛ wakatɛ fẽe. La Nawẽnnɛ n soe yel-sõnnɛ wuu la wi ya mɛ tɩ ya po yẽ a na'am pãŋa n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ, yãma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã. Ẽŋa wʋn le malgɛ ya tɩ ya ãna sõŋa, bɔ ya pãŋa tɩ ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, tɩ sɛla kãn tã'agɛ botɩ ya bike.

eng: To him be power forever and always. Amen.
local: Ẽŋa n soe pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: I have written and sent these few lines to you by Silvanus. I consider him to be a faithful brother. In these lines I have urged and affirmed that this is the genuine grace of God. Stand firm in it.
local: Mam botɩ Asilvẽ n gʋlsɛ gõn-pɩka wã, ẽŋa n de sɔbia tɩ mam bɩsɛ tɩ a de sɩra dãana. Mam gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã tɩ m bɔ ya la bʋraanɛ, dee botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ya tarɩ la, de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ mẽŋa mẽŋa pʋam tɩ ya boe.

eng: The fellow-elect church in Babylon greets you, and so does my son Mark.
local: Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Babilɔnɩ tɩ a tũ'use ba wʋ yãma la pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya, Amarkɩ n lebge mam dayʋa la mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

eng: Greet each other with the kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ.
local: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm. Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la yãma sɛba wuu n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Mam Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ n de Azezi-krisi yamŋa la a Tõntõnna, n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba tɩ Azezi-krisi n de Nawẽnnɛ la tɩ Fãagra n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, botɩ ya bɔ ẽ sɩra sõŋa wʋ tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra se'em na.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Yãma n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm zozo'e.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: Azezi n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa la n botɩ tɩ bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi tõma tɩ tɩ po yẽ a na'am pãŋa, la a sõŋa. Bɛla tɩ a bɔ tɔ bõn-sɛba wuu n wʋn basɛ tɩ tɩ vɔa vɔm sɛka n dolɩ a sore.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ a bɔ tɔ bɔ'a sɛba n ãn sõŋa gãnna wuu tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɔ la. Tɩ bõn-bãma ĩyã ya wʋn tã'agɛ lɔkɛ be'em yɛmleego n boe dũnia zuo sã'ana nẽrba la, dee lebge ãna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ãn se'em na.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
local: Bɛla, nargɛ-ya pãŋa, tɩ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam, ɩr-sõŋɔ pa'asɛ, tɩ ɩr-sõŋɔ la pʋam, bãŋrɛ pa'asɛ.

eng: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, endurance; and to endurance, godliness.
local: tɩ bãŋrɛ la pʋam, yõk-m-mẽŋa pa'asɛ, tɩ yõk-m-mẽŋa la pʋam, wĩbga pa'asɛ, tɩ wĩbga la pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ doosgo sɩra sɩra pa'asɛ.

eng: and to godliness, affection for others; and to affection for others, love.
local: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ doosgo sɩra sɩra la pʋam, ya ɩta taaba sõŋa pa'asɛ, tɩ ya ɩta taaba sõŋa la pʋam, nõŋlʋm pa'asɛ.

eng: If all these are yours and they are growing in you, theyll keep you from becoming inactive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn ɩta bõn-bãna wã zozo'e, ba wʋn basɛ tɩ ya tõm Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, dee bãŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi pa'asɛ.

eng: Whoever lacks these things is shortsighted and blind, forgetting that they were cleansed from their past sins.
local: La sɛka n ka ɩtɩ bõn-bãna wã ãn wʋ fʋa la, a ka yẽtɩ, la a yĩm mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ a tõon-be'ero tɩ a ẽŋɛ kʋrʋm na taalɛ mɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be eager to confirm your call and election. Do this and you will never ever be lost.
local: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, mõrgɛ ya mẽŋa ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya, la ẽn looge ya la pʋam. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya kãn bʋrgɛ dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore.

eng: In this way you will receive a rich welcome into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ.
local: La de la bɛla tɩ ya wʋn yẽ sore sõŋa kẽ Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana, la tɩ Fãagra la so'olʋm n boe wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ la pʋam.

eng: So Ill keep reminding you about these things, although you already know them and stand secure in the truth you have.
local: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam tẽera ya bõn-bãna wakatɛ wuu, baa la yãma n põn mina ba, la yãma n ze kãŋkãŋɛ sɩra la tɩ ya to'oge la pʋam na.

eng: I think its right that I keep stirring up your memory, as long as Im alive.
local: Mam n nãn vɔa wã, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la masɛ mɛ tɩ mam tẽegɛ ya, tɩ ya da gĩse.

eng: After all, our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I am about to depart from this life.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n puke pa'alɛ mam, tɩ mam mina tɩ la deege la fẽe tɩ mam basɛ vɔm ẽna wã dee fõrgɛ.

eng: Im eager for you always to remember these things after my death.
local: Bɛla mam wʋn mõrgɛ mɛ, la mam fõrgrɛ poorʋm, ya wʋn tẽra bõn-bãna yelle sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: We didnt repeat crafty myths when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. Quite the contrary, we witnessed his majesty with our own eyes.
local: Tõma n tɔgɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi pãŋa, la a wa'aŋɔ la yelle la, la dagɩ sɔlma tɩ nẽrsaalba tẽ'esɛ nãam la ba mẽŋa yɛm. Tõma sɩrɩ yẽ a na'am pãŋa n zo'oge se'em mɛ la tɩ nini.

eng: He received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice came to him from the magnificent glory, saying, This is my dearly loved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la, n bɔ ẽ nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa. Ẽŋa n de na'am pãŋa wuu dãana la yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu, a paagɛ la mam yɛm.».

eng: We ourselves heard this voice from heaven while we were with him on the holy mountain.
local: Tõma n daan boe la ẽ lɛ tãŋa la zuo la, tɩ tõma wõm kõa la n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ.

eng: In addition, we have a most reliable prophetic word, and you would do well to pay attention to it, just as you would to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
local: Bɛla n basɛ tɩ tõma malɩn bãŋɛ sõŋa sõŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la de la sɩra. La ãn sõŋa tɩ ya tara yetɔg-bãma sõŋa, ba ãn wʋ fɩtla n nẽegɛ lika pʋam. Tara-ya ba halɩ ka paagɛ beere yɩlɩŋrɛ, tɩ Krisi nẽegɛ ya sũure pʋam wʋ bewã'arɛ la.

eng: Most important, you must know that no prophecy of scripture represents the prophets own understanding of things.
local: Sɛla n gãnnɩ de la ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na, nẽra ka tã wʋn pa'alɛ ba võorɛ la a mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ.

eng: because no prophecy ever came by human will. Instead, men and women led by the Holy Spirit spoke from God.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba ka de nẽrsaalba yɛm n boorɩ se'em, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ nẽrsaalba tɔgɛ yetɔga n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa zẽ'am.

eng: But false prophets also arose among the people. In the same way, false teachers will come among you. They will introduce destructive opinions and deny the master who bought them, bringing quick destruction on themselves.
local: Kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, nẽrba n yʋʋn boe Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la pʋam parna tɩ bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba. Bɛla, põmpɔrŋɔ karẽnsãandõma n mẽ wʋn bɔna ya pʋam. Ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la yɛm tarɩ zãsŋɔ n sẽm na sã'aŋɔ wa'am, dee wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Zuudãana n fãagɛ ba la, bɛla n wʋn basɛ tɩ sã'aŋɔ wa'am ba zuto tɔtɔ.

eng: Many will follow them in their unrestrained immorality, and because of these false teachers the way of truth will be slandered.
local: La nẽrba zozo'e n wʋn doose ba, ba tõon-yooro la poorʋm, tɩ la wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba tɔgɛ sɩra sore la yetɔg-be'ero.

eng: In their greed they will take advantage of you with lies. The judgment pronounced against them long ago hasnt fallen idle, nor is their destruction sleeping.
local: Ban boorɩ bõntarsõm zo'oge la ĩyã tɩ ba wʋn tɔgɛ nõ-yẽlʋm yetɔga pã'asɛ ya, yẽ yõorɔ ya zẽ'am. La halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ ba sɩbgrɛ põn mãasʋm gu'ura ba, ba sã'aŋɔ kãn viige.

eng: God didnt spare the angels when they sinned but cast them into the lowest level of the underworld and committed them to chains of darkness, keeping them there until the judgment.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka basɛ malɛkadõma sɛba n tõm tõon-be'ero la tɩ ba põsɛ, a lobe ba basɛ la bug-tẽŋa bog-ko'ogo n de lika pʋam, ba ba la bãnsɩ gu'ura sarɩyadia daarɛ.

eng: And he didnt spare the ancient world when he brought a flood on the world of ungodly people, even though he protected Noah, a preacher of righteousness, along with seven others.
local: La a yʋʋn ka basɛ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm dõma la tɩ ba põsɛ, sãn dagna nẽrba banii mã'a. La de la Anoe n mõolɛ sɛla n masɛ yelle, la a yir-dõma mã'a tɩ a yʋʋn fãagɛ, dee basɛ tɩ sa-kãtɛ ni, tɩ ko'om libe tẽŋa sã'am dũnia nẽrba wuu n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: God condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to total destruction, reducing them to ashes as a warning to ungodly people.
local: La a ken di Sodom la Gomõorɩ tẽnsɩ sarɩya mɛ sɩbgɛ ba, basɛ tɩ bugum di ba, tɩ ba lebge tãmpɛglʋm, tɩ la dɛna makrɛ bɔ sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la.

eng: And he rescued righteous Lot, who was made miserable by the unrestrained immorality of unruly people. .
local: La a yʋʋn fãagɛ la Alotɩ n de tõon-sõma dãana. Ẽŋa sũure n yʋʋn sã'am halɩ zozo'e la tõon-be'ero dõma tõon-yooro n zo'oge la.

eng: While that righteous man lived among them he felt deep distress every day on account of the immoral actions he saw and heard.
local: Alotɩ n yʋʋn de tõon-sõma dãana vɔa la ba lɛ, wõnna dee yẽta ba tõon-be'ero la, a sũure n sã'anɩ daarɛ woo, tɩ la nãmsra ẽ.

eng: These things show that the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from their trials, and how to keep the unrighteous for punishment on the Judgment Day.
local: Bɛla pa'alɩ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mi ẽŋa n ɩtɩ se'em fãara nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ a sore la nãmsgɔ pʋam mɛ, dee mina ẽŋa n ɩtɩ tõon-be'ero dõma se'em gu'ura sarɩyadia daarɛ tɩ ba wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ.

eng: This is especially true for those who follow after the corrupt cravings of the sinful nature and defy the Lords authority. These reckless, brash people arent afraid to insult the glorious ones.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ ba yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em n de tõon-dẽgrɔ, dee pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ nõorɛ la ba lɛ la, a wʋn sɩbgɛ la bãma gãnna. Põmpɔrŋɔ karẽnsãandõma la de la zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma, la tɩta'alʋm dõma, ba ka zotɩ dabeem tɩ ba tʋ saazuum pãnsɩ dõma.
