eng: yet angels, who are stronger and more powerful, dont use insults when pronouncing the Lords judgment on them.
local: La baa malɛkadõma n tarɩ pãŋa, la ke'eŋo gãnna ba la, ka tɔgrɩ ba yelle pɔ'ɔra ba Zuudãana la nẽŋam.

eng: These false teachers are like irrational animals, mere creatures of instinct, born to be captured and destroyed. They slander what they dont understand and, like animals, they will be destroyed.
local: Nẽr-bãma ɩtɩ la yooryooro, ba ãn wʋ dũnsi n ka tarɩ yɛm tɩ ba yõgrɩ kʋʋra la. Ba tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ ba ka mi be'em, ba mẽ wʋn sã'am wʋ dũnsi la.

eng: In this way, they will receive payment for their wrongdoing. They even enjoy unruly parties in broad daylight. They are blots and blemishes, taking delight in their seductive pleasures while feasting with you.
local: Ba wʋn yẽ tõon-be'ero yɔɔrɔ ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Ba tõnnɩ na ba yɛm n boorɩ se'em n de tõon-dẽgrɔ peelem pʋam, tɩ ba sũure ãna yẽlʋm. Ba sãn lagɩm na ya dita, ɩta sũ-yẽlga la ba põmpã'asʋm na, la de la dẽgrɔ, la yãnnɛ.

eng: They are always looking for someone with whom to commit adultery. They are always on the lookout for opportunities to sin. They ensnare people whose faith is weak. They have hearts trained in greed. They are under Gods curse.
local: Ba yɛm boorɩ la yalsɩ tõoma mã'a, ba ka tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero tɩgra. Ba pã'asrɩ la nẽr-sɛba pʋtẽ'era n ka zo'oge Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam na tɩ ba tõnna be'em. Ba nõŋɛ la bõnɔ zozo'e. Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ero boe ba zuto.

eng: Leaving the straight path, they have gone off course, following the way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved the payment of doing wrong.
local: Ba basɛ so-sõŋɔ la mɛ dee bʋrgɛ dɔla Abalaam n de Abozɔɔrɩ dayʋa la sore, ẽŋa nõŋɛ la ligri bɔɔra tɩ a tõm tõon-be'ero to'oge yɔɔrɔ.

eng: But Balaam was rebuked for his wrongdoing. A donkey, which has no voice, spoke with a human voice and put a stop to the prophets madness.
local: La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn gi Abalaam n de a nõtɔ'ɔsa la mɛ a tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. A yʋʋn botɩ Abalaam mẽŋa bõŋa n tɔgɛ la ẽ wʋ nẽrsaala la, gu ẽ tɩ a da tõm a yalnɛ tõoma la.

eng: These false teachers are springs without water, mists driven by the wind. The underworld has been reserved for them.
local: Nẽr-bãma ãn wʋ bulsi n ka tarɩ ko'om, dee ãna wʋ sawara tɩ kʋsebgo pe'ege la. Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm na zẽ'a n de lika tam gu'ura ba.

eng: With empty, self-important speech, they use sinful cravings and unrestrained immorality to ensnare people who have only just escaped life with those who have wandered from the truth.
local: Ba pa'alɩ la ba mẽŋa tɔgra yetɔg-kãra n ka tarɩ võorɛ, dee tara ba yɛm n boorɩ se'em n de tõon-dẽgrɔ la pã'asra nẽr-sɛba n nãn welge ba mẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n vɔa tuure pʋam na.

eng: These false teachers promise freedom, but they themselves are slaves of immorality; whatever overpowers you, enslaves you.
local: Ba yetɩ nẽrba la tɩ ba wʋn yẽ yolsgo, dee tɩ ba mẽŋa dɛna tõon-dẽgrɔ n wʋn sã'am ba la yamsɩ. Se'ere n soe la, nẽra woo de la sɛla n soe ẽ na yamŋa.

eng: If people escape the moral filth of this world through the knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ, then get tangled up in it again and are overcome by it, they are worse off than they were before.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n bãŋɛ Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana, la tɩ Fãagra la, ba basɛ dũnia tõon-dẽgrɔ mɛ. Ba sãn le kẽ ba pʋam basɛ tɩ ba sɔna ba, ba poorʋm toogo la wʋn gãnna pɔspɔsɩ la.

eng: It would be better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than, having come to know it, to turn back from the holy commandment entrusted to them.
local: Bɛla, ba sãn ka bãŋɛ nɩ sɛla n masɛ sore la sõɩ ban bãŋɛ ẽ dee wẽrgɛ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ tɩ ba to'oge tɔgɛ bɔ ba la.

eng: They demonstrate the truth of the proverb: A dog returns to its own vomit, and a washed sow wallows in the mud.
local: La ẽŋɛ ba bɛla pa'alɛ tɩ magsa wã de la sɩra, ba yetɩ: «Baaga lebe ka di la sɛla tɩ a tĩ la», dee le yetɩ: «Kurkuri tɩ ba peege ẽ sõŋa sõŋa la, lebe ka bilim na bɛglʋm pʋam.».

eng: My little children, Im writing these things to you so that you dont sin. But if you do sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous one.
local: M kɔma, mam gʋlsrɩ ya wãna tɩ ya da tõm na tõon-be'ero. La nẽra sãn tõm be'em, tõma tarɩ la sõŋra n boe tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la de la Azezi-krisi n de pʋpeelem dãana.

eng: He is Gods way of dealing with our sins, not only ours but the sins of the whole world.
local: La de la ẽŋa n dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ tɩ la dɛna kãabgɔ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tõma tõon-be'ero taalɛ. La, la dagɩ tõma mã'a tõon-be'ero taalɛ, la de la dũnia nẽrba wuu tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

eng: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.
local: Tɩ sãn sakra Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, bɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ sɩrɩ mi ẽ.

eng: The one who claims, I know him, while not keeping his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in this person.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa mi ẽ, dee ka sakra a nõorɛ, a de la põmpɔrma, la a ka tarɩ sɩra.

eng: But the love of God is truly perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know we are in him.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la, nẽr-ẽŋa n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra. Bɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla.

eng: The one who claims to remain in him ought to live in the same way as he lived.
local: Sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ a dãana vɔa wʋ Azezi-krisi n daan vɔa se'em na.

eng: Dear friends, Im not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the message you heard.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, la dagɩ nõor-paalga tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, a de la nõor-kɛka tɩ ya tarɩ halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la. Nõor-kɛka ẽŋa de la kõ-sɛka tɩ ya daan wõm na.

eng: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light already shines.
local: La a yɛrɩ dɛna la nõor-paalga tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. La de la Azezi-krisi zẽ'am, la yãma zẽ'am tɩ yel-ẽŋa de sɩra, se'ere n soe la, lika la tollɩ mɛ, dee tɩ peelem sɩra sɩra darɛ nẽera.

eng: The one who claims to be in the light while hating a brother or sister is in the darkness even now.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa boe la peelem pʋam, dee sisra a sɔbia, ken bɔna la lika pʋam.

eng: The person loving a brother and sister stays in the light, and there is nothing in the light that causes a person to stumble.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ a sɔbia, vɔa la peelem pʋam, bɛla ĩyã sɛla ka boe a zẽ'am n wʋn basɛ tɩ a tuuge.

eng: But the person who hates a brother or sister is in the darkness and lives in the darkness, and doesnt know where to go because the darkness blinds the eyes.
local: La sɛka n sisri a sɔbia, boe la lika pʋam, la a sẽnnɩ na lika pʋam, ka mina ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'a, se'ere n soe la, lika la fɔ a nini mɛ.

eng: Little children, Im writing to you because your sins have been forgiven through Jesus name.
local: Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔma, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ mɛ Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

eng: Parents, Im writing to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, Im writing to you because you have conquered the evil one.
local: Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m sɔdõma, se'ere n soe la, yãma mi sɛka n boe halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la mɛ. Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ, se'ere n soe la, yãma yãŋɛ be'em dãana Asũtãana la mɛ.

eng: My dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. I have written both letters to stir up your sincere understanding with a reminder.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, la de la gõŋɔ bʋyi dãana n wãna tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. Gõnnɔ bayi la pʋam, mam tẽegɛ ya la sɛla tɩ ya dẽŋɛ bãŋɛ la, tɩ m puke ya pʋtẽ'era sõŋa.

eng: Little children, I write to you because you know the Father. Parents, I write to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, I write to you because you are strong, the word of God remains in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
local: Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔma, se'ere n soe la, yãma mi tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ. Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m sɔdõma, se'ere n soe la, yãma mi sɛka n boe halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la mɛ. Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ, se'ere n soe la, yãma tarɩ pãŋa mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la boe ya pʋam mɛ, la yãma yãŋɛ be'em dãana Asũtãana la mɛ.

eng: I want you to recall what the holy prophets foretold as well as what the Lord and savior commanded through your apostles.
local: Mam boorɩ tɩ ya tẽra la yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ halɩ yʋʋma yʋʋma, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi n de tɩ Fãagra la nõorɛ tɩ Tõntõnɩba la tɩ a tõm ya zẽ'am na tɔgɛ bɔ ya la.

eng: Dont love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in them.
local: Da nõŋɛ-ya dũnia, la bõn-sɛba n boe a pʋam na. Nẽra sãn nõŋɛ dũnia wã, Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ la nõŋlʋm ka boe la a dãana lɛ.

eng: Most important, know this: in the last days scoffers will come, jeering, living by their own cravings.
local: Sɛla n gãnnɩ de la ya bãŋɛ tɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ, nẽrba n la'arɩ nẽrba n wʋn wa wa'am, wa vɔa vɔm sɛka n de be'em tɩ ba mẽŋa boorɩ. Ba wʋn la'ara ya.

eng: Everything that is in the world—the craving for whatever the body feels, the craving for whatever the eyes see and the arrogant pride in ones possessions—is not of the Father but is of the world.
local: Se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛba n de dũnia wã bõnɔ de la nẽrsaala mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, la nini n yẽ sɛla bɔɔra n de be'em, la nẽra n pa'alɩ a mẽŋa a bõntarsõm ĩyã. Bãma wuu ka ze'ele tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ba de la dũnia bõnɔ.

eng: and saying, Where is the promise of his coming? After all, nothing has changed—not since the beginning of creation, nor even since the ancestors died.
local: dee yetɩ: «Azezi n daan bĩŋe nõorɛ yetɩ a wʋn leme wa'am na, a bɛɛ? Halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ tɩ yaabdõma ki wa paam wakat-ẽna wã, sɛla woo ken ãna la bɛla wʋ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo n yʋʋn ãn se'em na.».

eng: And the world and its cravings are passing away, but the person who does the will of God remains forever.
local: Dũnia, la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽrba yɛmleego boorɩ la tollɩ mɛ. La nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em n wʋn bɔna wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: But they fail to notice that, by Gods word, heaven and earth were formed long ago out of water and by means of water.
local: Ban tɔgrɩ bɛla la, ba boorɩ tɩ ba yetɩ bãma yĩm mɛ tɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ la yetɔga tɩ saazuum la tẽŋa nãam, tɩ zẽ'a woo daan dɛna ko'om, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ tẽŋa ze'ele ko'om pʋam bɔna.

eng: Little children, it is the last hour. Just as you have heard that the antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. This is how we know it is the last hour.
local: M kɔma, lɛɛlɛ wã la de la dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ. Yãma wõm tɩ ba yetɩ Krisi sɩ'ɩsra n sẽm. La lɛɛlɛ wã, Krisi sɩ'ɩsrɩba zo'oge mɛ bɔna, bɛla tõma mi mɛ tɩ la de la dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ.

eng: And it was through these that the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
local: La ken dɛna la ko'om, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn botɩ ba libe dũnia ẽŋa sã'am ẽ.

eng: They went out from us, but they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But by going out from us, they showed they all are not part of us.
local: Nẽr-bãma ze'ele la tõma zẽ'am yese, la ba daan dagɩ tõma nẽrba sɩra sɩra. Ba daan sãn de nɩ tõma nẽrba, ba wʋn weege nɩ bɔna la tõma zẽ'am. La ban basɛ tõma la, dẽnɩ tɩ la ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ la pa'alɛ vẽelga tɩ ba nẽra baa ayɩla dagɩ tõma nẽra.

eng: But by the same word, heaven and earth are now held in reserve for fire, kept for the Judgment Day and destruction of ungodly people.
local: Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mẽŋa n ken botɩ dũni-ẽna wã, la saazuum bɔna gu'ura tɩ bugum wa sã'am ba. A basɛ tɩ ba bɔna gu'ura la daar-sɛka tɩ a wʋn di nẽrsaalba sarɩya, sã'am tõon-be'ero dõma la.

eng: But you have an anointing from the holy one, and all of you know the truth.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, Krisi bɔ ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mɛ, bɛla yãma wuu mi sɩra mɛ.

eng: Dont let it escape your notice, dear friends, that with the Lord a single day is like a thousand years and a thousand years are like a single day.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, sɛla ayɩla n boe tɩ yãma ka masɛ tɩ ya yĩm, la sãn dɛna la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, daarɛ ayɩla ãn wʋ yʋʋma tʋsrɛ, tɩ yʋʋma tʋsrɛ mẽ ãna wʋ daarɛ ayɩla.

eng: I dont write to you because you dont know the truth but because you know it. You know that no lie comes from the truth.
local: Mam n gʋlsɛ bɔ ya la, mam ka yetɩ ya ka mi sɩra la, la tõn dɛna la yan mi ẽ na ĩyã, la yãma mi tɩ sɛla n de põmpɔrŋɔ ka ze'etɩ sɩra pʋam.

eng: The Lord isnt slow to keep his promise, as some think of slowness, but he is patient toward you, not wanting anyone to perish but all to change their hearts and lives.
local: Zuudãana la n bĩŋe a nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla la, la ka yuuri tɩ a kãn ẽŋɛ wʋ nẽrba basɛba n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na. A mã'agɛ la a sũure la ya, a ka boorɩ tɩ nẽra nẽra sã'am, a boorɩ tɩ nẽra woo n teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: Who is the liar? Isnt it the person who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This person is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
local: Ãne n de põmpɔrma la? La de la sɛka n sɩ'ɩsrɩ yetɩ Azezi dagɩ Krisi la. Ẽŋa n de Krisi sɩ'ɩsra la. A sɩ'ɩsrɩ la Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa la.

eng: But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will pass away with a dreadful noise, the elements will be consumed by fire, and the earth and all the works done on it will be exposed.
local: Zuudãana la lemŋo daarɛ wʋn ãna wʋ nayiga n liŋri se'em na. Daar-ẽŋa, vool-kãtɛ n wʋn ẽŋɛ, tɩ bugum di bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum, tɩ saazuum bɔɩ. Tɩ tẽŋa, la bõn-sɛba n boe a pʋam na wuu mẽ ka le bɔna.

eng: Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father, but the one who confesses the Son has the Father also.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Dayʋa la, mẽ ka boe la Sɔ la. La nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa mi Dayʋa la, mẽ boe la Sɔ la mɛ.

eng: Since everything will be destroyed in this way, what sort of people ought you to be? You must live holy and godly lives.
local: Bõn-bãma wuu n wʋn sã'am bɛla la, dẽnɩ tɩ ya basɛ tɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ, la ya Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo ãna sõŋa, tɩ ya sakra ẽ sɛla woo pʋam.

eng: As for you, what you heard from the beginning must remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you will also remain in relationship to the Son and in the Father.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, mõrgɛ-ya tara yetɔg-sɛba tɩ ya daan wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la ya sũure pʋam. Ba sãn ken bɔna ya sũure pʋam, yãma mẽ wʋn ken bɔna la Sɔ, la Dayʋa la lɛ.

eng: waiting for and hastening the coming day of God. Because of that day, the heavens will be destroyed by fire and the elements will melt away in the flames.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ ya gu'ura Nawẽnnɛ daarɛ, tɩ Azezi wʋn wa'am na, la ya nargɛ pãŋa tɩ a wa'am tɔtɔ. Daar-ẽŋa, bugum n wʋn di saazuum, tɩ bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum wʋn yẽlgɛ sã'am.

eng: This is the promise that he himself gave us: eternal life.
local: Krisi n bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɩ sɛla n wãna: La de la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: But according to his promise we are waiting for a new heaven and a new earth, where righteousness is at home.
local: Tõma gu'uri la saazuum paalga, la dũnia paalga tɩ sɛla woo masɛ ãna sõŋa, wʋ ẽn bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: I write these things to you about those who are attempting to deceive you.
local: La de la nẽr-sɛba n zɩgrɩ tɩ ba pã'asɛ ya tɩ ya tuuge la yelle tɩ mam gʋlsɛ wãna bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, while you are waiting for these things to happen, make every effort to be found by him in peace—pure and faultless.
local: Bɛla, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, yãma n gu'uri bɛla la, nargɛ-ya pãŋa ãna sõŋa, tɩ ya da tara dõrga Nawẽnnɛ nifum, tɩ ya wʋn bɔna la ẽ la sũ-mã'asʋm.

eng: As for you, the anointing that you received from him remains on you, and you dont need anyone to teach you the truth. But since his anointing teaches you about all things , remain in relationship to him just as he taught you.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ ya to'oge Krisi zẽ'am na, boe la ya lɛ mɛ, yãma ka le ɛɛra tɩ ba zãsʋm ya. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la n boe la ya lɛ la, zãsnɩ ya sɛla woo mɛ, la de la sɩra, a ka parnɩ. Bɔna-ya la Krisi wʋ ẽn zãsnɩ ya se'em na.

eng: Consider the patience of our Lord to be salvation, just as our dear friend and brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given to him.
local: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Zuudãana la n mã'agɛ a sũure gu'ura la, a boorɩ tɩ ya yẽ la fãarɛ. La de la bɛla mẽ tɩ tɩ sɔbi-nõŋrɛ Apolɩ daan dɩkɛ yɛm sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ na gʋlsɛ bɔ ya.

eng: And now, little children, remain in relationship to Jesus, so that when he appears we can have confidence and not be ashamed in front of him when he comes.
local: Ẽe, m kɔma, bɔna-ya la ẽ lɛ, tɩ a sãn wa puke daarɛ tɩ tɩ tara sũ-ke'eŋo, tɩ yãnnɛ da yõkɛ tɔ a nẽŋam a lemŋo la daarɛ.

eng: speaking of these things in all his letters. Some of his remarks are hard to understand, and people who are ignorant and whose faith is weak twist them to their own destruction, just as they do the other scriptures.
local: A gõn-sɛba wuu pʋam tɩ a tɔgrɩ bɛla yelle la, a gʋlsɛ la bɛla. A gõnnɔ na pʋam, zẽ'esɩ basɛba n boe mĩ tɩ ba wõŋa tɔ, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka mi, dee ka vigle ban bɔ sɩra la pʋam na, tɛɛra ba võorɛ n de se'em, wʋ ban ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõnnɔ basɛba se'em na. Ban ɩtɩ bɛla la sẽm na ba mẽŋa sã'aŋɔ.

eng: If you know that he is righteous, you also know that every person who practices righteousness is born from him.
local: Yãma mi tɩ Krisi de la sɛka n masɛ, bɛla, bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka n mẽ ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ de la Nawẽnnɛ bia.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, since you have been warned in advance, be on guard so that you arent led off course into the error of sinful people, and lose your own safe position.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, yãma n dẽŋɛ tara tiire la, guuse-ya, da basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero dõma n tuuge la, tuuge ya. Da basɛ-ya tɩ ba botɩ ya basɛ yãma n bɔ sɩra zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ wã.

eng: Instead, grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ. To him belongs glory now and forever. Amen.
local: Sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa vɔa Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana la tɩ Fãagra la yel-sõnnɛ pʋam, bãŋra ẽ pa'asra. Ẽŋa n soe na'am pãŋa lɛɛlɛ wã, la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: See what kind of love the Father has given to us in that we should be called Gods children, and that is what we are! Because the world didnt recognize him, it doesnt recognize us.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la n nõŋɛ tɩ se'em. A nõŋlʋm na zo'oge gãŋɛ mɛ, tɩ a wi'ira tõma tɩ a kɔma. La tõma sɩrɩ de la a kɔma. Wãna n soe tɩ dũnia dõma ka mina tõma: La de la ban ka mi Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

eng: Dear friends, now we are Gods children, and it hasnt yet appeared what we will be. We know that when he appears we will be like him because well see him as he is.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma. La tɩn wʋn wa ãna se'em na nãn ka puke. La tɩ mi tɩ Krisi sãn wa puke, tɩ wʋn ãna wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na, tɩn wʋn wa yẽ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na ĩyã.

eng: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves even as he is pure.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura bõn-ẽŋa Krisi ĩyã, malgrɩ a mẽŋa mɛ dɛna pʋpeelem dãana, wʋ Azezi-krisi mẽŋa n de pʋpeelem dãana se'em na.

eng: Every person who practices sin commits an act of rebellion, and sin is rebellion.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, a sɩ'ɩsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, tõon-be'ero de la Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ sɩ'ɩsgɔ.

eng: You know that he appeared to take away sins, and there is no sin in him.
local: La yãma mi tɩ Azezi-krisi wa'am tɩ a yese la nẽrba tõon-be'ero basɛ, la a mẽŋa ka tarɩ tõon-be'ero.

eng: Every person who remains in relationship to him does not sin. Any person who sins has not seen him or known him.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n lagɩm na Krisi bɔna ka tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero. La sɛka n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero ka yẽ ẽ, la a ka bãŋɛ ẽ.

eng: Little children, make sure no one deceives you. The person who practices righteousness is righteous, in the same way that Jesus is righteous.
local: M kɔma, da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽra pã'asɛ ya. Sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, de la nẽra n masɛ, wʋ Azezi mẽŋa n masɛ se'em na.

eng: The person who practices sin belongs to the devil, because the devil has been sinning since the beginning. Gods Son appeared for this purpose: to destroy the works of the devil.
local: Sɛka n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero de la Asũtãana nẽra, se'ere n soe la, Asũtãana tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero la halɩ pĩ'iluŋo. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la wa'am tɩ a sã'am Asũtãana tõoma.

eng: Those born from God dont practice sin because Gods DNA remains in them. They cant sin because they are born from God.
local: Sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ bia ka le tõnna tõon-be'ero, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n boe a pʋam, a ka le tãna wʋn tõnna tõon-be'ero, Nawẽnnɛ n de a Sɔ la ĩyã.

eng: This is how Gods children and the devils children are apparent: everyone who doesnt practice righteousness is not from God, including the person who doesnt love a brother or sister.
local: Bɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ ba bãŋra Nawẽnnɛ kɔma, la sɛba n de Asũtãana kɔma bɔkra. Sɛka n ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, bɩɩ sɛka n ka nõŋɛ a sɔbia dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ bia.

eng: This is the message that you heard from the beginning: love each other.
local: Kõ-yẽlga tɩ ya wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la de la tɩ nõŋɛ-ya taaba.

eng: Dont behave like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he kill him? He killed him because his own works were evil, but the works of his brother were righteous.
local: La ka de tɩ ãna wʋ Akãɩ la, ẽŋa de la Asũtãana nẽra, yʋʋn kʋ a yɩbga. La bẽm n soe tɩ a yʋʋn kʋ ẽ? La de la a tõoma n yʋʋn de tõon-be'ero, dee tɩ a yɩbga tõoma masɛ la ĩyã.

eng: Dont be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
local: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, dũnia nẽrba sãn sisra ya, da kãn basɛ-ya tɩ la dɛna sɛla n di'ige ya.

eng: We know that we have transferred from death to life, because we love the brothers and sisters. The person who does not love remains in death.
local: Tõma mi mɛ tɩ tõma ze'ele la kũm pʋam yese bɔna vɔm pʋam, tõma n nõŋɛ tɩ sɔbiisi la ĩyã. Nẽr-sɛka n ka nõŋɛ a sɔbia ken bɔna la kũm pʋam.

eng: Everyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer, and you know that murderers dont have eternal life residing in them.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n sisri a sɔbia de la nẽr-kʋʋra. La ya mi tɩ nẽr-kʋʋra ka tarɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: This is how we know love: Jesus laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
local: La de la bõn-ẽna wã n botɩ tõma bãŋɛ nõŋlʋm n ãn se'em: Azezi ki mɛ bɔ tõma, bɛla, tõma mẽ de la tɩ dɩkɛ tɩ vɔm bɔ tɩ sɔbiisi ĩyã.

eng: But if someone has material possessions and sees a brother or sister in need but refuses to help—how can the love of God dwell in a person like that?.
local: Nẽra sãn tara dũnia wã bõntarsõm, dee yẽ a sɔbia n boorɩ sõŋrɛ, ka zoe a ninbãalga sõŋɛ ẽ, ẽŋa dãana ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm.

eng: Little children, lets not love with words or speech but with action and truth.
local: M kɔma, da basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ nõŋlʋm dɛna yetɔga, la nõ-yẽlʋm mã'a, tɩ basɛ tɩ la dɛna nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra n lagɩm na tõoma.

eng: This is how we will know that we belong to the truth and reassure our hearts in Gods presence.
local: Bɛla n wʋn basɛ tɩ tɩ bãŋɛ tɩ tõma de la sɩra dõma, la tɩ sũure wʋn ãna ke'eŋo Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam.

eng: Even if our hearts condemn us, God is greater than our hearts and knows all things.
local: Baa tɩ sũure sãn ka bɔ tɩ bʋʋrɔ, tɩ bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gãnnɩ tɩ sũure mɛ, la a mi sɛla woo bɔkɛ mɛ.

eng: Dear friends, if our hearts dont condemn us, we have confidence in relationship to God.
local: M kɔm-nõŋrɩsɩ, tɩ sũure sãn bɔ tɩ bʋʋrɔ, tɩ tarɩ sũ-ke'eŋo Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam mɛ.

eng: We receive whatever we ask from him because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
local: la bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ tɩ sosrɩ ẽ, a bɔ'ɔrɩ tɩ mɛ, tõma n sakrɩ a nõorɛ, dee ɩta sɛla n paarɩ a yɛm na ĩyã.

eng: This is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love each other as he commanded us.
local: Ẽŋa n bɔ a nõorɛ se'em n wãna: La de la tɩ bɔ a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi sɩra, dee nõŋɛ taaba wʋ ẽŋa n bɔ tɩ a nõorɛ se'em na.

eng: Those who keep his commandments dwell in God and God dwells in them. This is how we know that he dwells in us, because of the Spirit he has given us.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la, a boe la Nawẽnnɛ lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ bɔna la ẽ. Bõn-sɛka n basɛ tɩ tõma mina tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boe la tõma lɛ n wãna: La de la a Sɩa tɩ a bɔ tõma la.

eng: We announce to you what existed from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have seen and our hands handled, about the word of life.
local: Tõma gʋlsɛ la sɛka n boe halɩ pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ yelle bɔ'ɔra ya. A de la sɛka tɩ tõma wõm a yetɔga, yẽ ẽ la tɩ nini, bɩsɛ ẽ sõŋa sõŋa, kalʋm ẽ na tɩ nu'usi. Ẽŋa n de yetɔga n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.

eng: The life was revealed, and we have seen, and we testify and announce to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us.
local: Sɛka n de vɔm na puke a mẽŋa mɛ, tɩ tõma yẽ ẽ. Tɩ tõma tɔgra a yelle, mõola vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ la yelle bɔ'ɔra ya. A daan boe la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, tɩ a puke ẽ pa'alɛ tõma.

eng: What we have seen and heard, we also announce it to you so that you can have fellowship with us. Our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
local: Sɛla tɩ tõma yẽ dee wõm na, tõma tɔgrɩ la ẽŋa bɔ'ɔra yãma, tɩ yãma wʋn lagɩm na tõma, mẽ naagɛ la tõma tara lagɩmtaarɛ la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi.

eng: We are writing these things so that our joy can be complete.
local: Tõma gʋlsɛ ya la wãna tɩ tɩ wʋn tara sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

eng: This is the message that we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light and there is no darkness in him at all.
local: Kõa n wãna tɩ tõma wõm Azezi-krisi zẽ'am, tɔgra ẽ bɔ'ɔra ya: Nawẽnnɛ de la peelem, lika ka boe a zẽ'am baa fẽfẽe.

eng: If we claim, We have fellowship with him, and live in the darkness, we are lying and do not act truthfully.
local: Tɩ sãn yetɩ tõma tarɩ lagɩmtaarɛ la Nawẽnnɛ, dee ken vɔa lika pʋam tõnna be'em, tɩ parnɩ mɛ, tɩ ka ɩtɩ sɩra.

eng: But if we live in the light in the same way as he is in the light, we have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, cleanses us from every sin.
local: La tɩ sãn vɔa peelem pʋam wʋ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n boe peelem pʋam na, tɩ tarɩ lagɩmtaarɛ la taaba mɛ, la a Dayʋa Azezi zɩɩm peerɩ tɩ mɛ yɛsra tɩ tõon-be'ero wuu.

eng: If we claim, We dont have any sin, we deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us.
local: La tɩ sãn yetɩ, tõma ka tarɩ tõon-be'ero, tɩ pã'asɛ la tɩ mẽŋa, la sɩra ka boe tɩ zẽ'am.

eng: But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and cleanse us from everything weve done wrong.
local: Tɩ sãn pelge tɩ pʋʋrɛ tɔgɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ mɛ, dee peege to, yese sɛla wuu n ka masɛ, se'ere n soe la, a de la sɩra dãana dee ɩta sɛla n masɛ.

eng: If we claim, We have never sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not in us.
local: Tɩ sãn yetɩ, tõma ka tõm tõon-be'ero, tɩ yetɩ a de la põmpɔrma, la a yetɔga la ka boe la tɩ lɛ.

eng: Dear friends, dont believe every spirit. Test the spirits to see if they are from God because many false prophets have gone into the world.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, da bɔ-ya nẽr-sɛba wuu n yetɩ bãma tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sɩra, magɛ-ya ba bɩsɛ la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa. Se'ere n soe la, põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba zozo'e n boe dũnia wã zuo.

eng: This is how you know if a spirit comes from God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come as a human is from God.
local: Bõn-sɛka n wʋn basɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ Sɩa sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wãna: Nẽr-sɛka woo n yetɩ Azezi-krisi n wa'am dũnia zuo, la a tarɩ la nẽrsaala ĩyã, ẽŋa n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.

eng: and every spirit that doesnt confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and is now already in the world.
local: La sɛka n ka tɔgrɩ Azezi yelle bɛla, ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, a tarɩ la Krisi sɩ'ɩsra la sɩa. Sɩ'ɩsra ẽŋa tɩ ya daan wõm tɩ a sẽm na, la lɛɛlɛ wã, a darɛ bɔna dũnia zuo mɛ.

eng: You are from God, little children, and you have defeated these people because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
local: La yãma, m kɔma, yãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la yãma yãŋɛ põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe la yãma lɛ la, tarɩ pãŋa gãnna sɩ'ɩsra la sɩa n boe la dũnia dõma la mɛ.

eng: They are from the world. So they speak from the worlds point of view and the world listens to them.
local: Põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la de la dũnia wã bõnɔ, bɛla n soe tɩ ba tɔgra dũnia yɛla, tɩ dũnia dõma sɛlsra ba.

eng: We are from God. The person who knows God listens to us. Whoever is not from God doesnt listen to us. This is how we recognize the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n mi Nawẽnnɛ selsrɩ tõma mɛ. La sɛka n dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽra ka selsrɩ tõma. La de la bɛla tɩ tõma wʋn bãŋɛ sɩra Sɩa, la põmpɔrŋɔ sɩa la bɔkɛ.

eng: Dear friends, lets love each other, because love is from God, and everyone who loves is born from God and knows God.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, tɩ nõŋɛ-ya taaba, se'ere n soe la, nõŋlʋm ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm de la Nawẽnnɛ bia, la a mi Nawẽnnɛ mɛ.

eng: The person who doesnt love does not know God, because God is love.
local: La nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka mi Nawẽnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ de la nõŋlʋm dãana.

eng: This is how the love of God is revealed to us: God has sent his only Son into the world so that we can live through him.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n pa'alɛ ẽŋa n nõŋɛ tõma se'em n wãna: A tõm na a Dayɔ-yẽŋa dũnia zuo, tɩ tɩ tara vɔm ẽŋa ĩyã.

eng: This is love: it is not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son as the sacrifice that deals with our sins.
local: Nõŋlʋm na n doose se'em n wãna: La dagɩ tõma n dẽŋɛ nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n nõŋɛ tõma, tõm a Dayʋa, tɩ a dɛna kãabgɔ tõma tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã.

eng: Dear friends, if God loved us this way, we also ought to love each other.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n nõŋɛ tɩ bɛla la, dẽnɩ tɩ tõma mẽ nõŋɛ taaba.

eng: No one has ever seen God. If we love each other, God remains in us and his love is made perfect in us.
local: Nẽra nẽra nãn ka yẽ Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ sãn nõŋɛ taaba, Nawẽnnɛ boe la tɩ lɛ mɛ, la a nõŋlʋm n boe la tɩ lɛ la, de la nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ.

eng: This is how we know we remain in him and he remains in us, because he has given us a measure of his Spirit.
local: Bõn-sɛka n basɛ tɩ tõma bãŋɛ tɩ tõma boe la Nawẽnnɛ lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ bɔna la tõma lɛ n wãna: La de la a Sɩa tɩ a bɔ tõma la.

eng: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son to be the savior of the world.
local: La tõma yẽ mɛ, tɩ tõma tɔgra yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la tõm na a Dayʋa, tɩ a dɛna dũnia Fãagra.

eng: If any of us confess that Jesus is Gods Son, God remains in us and we remain in God.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n sakɛ yetɩ Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, Nawẽnnɛ boe la ẽ lɛ mɛ, tɩ a mẽ bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: We have known and have believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who remain in love remain in God and God remains in them.
local: La tõma mi mɛ, dee bɔ sɩra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ tõma mɛ. Nawẽnnɛ de la nõŋlʋm dãana. Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm boe la Nawẽnnɛ lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ bɔna la ẽ.

eng: This is how love has been perfected in us, so that we can have confidence on the Judgment Day, because we are exactly the same as God is in this world.
local: Tõma sãn tara Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ, tɩ wʋn ãna wʋ Azezi n ãn se'em na dũnia wã zuo. Bɛla tɩ wʋn tara sũ-ke'eŋo sarɩyadia daarɛ.

eng: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear, because fear expects punishment. The person who is afraid has not been made perfect in love.
local: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka zotɩ dabeem, nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ yesrɩ la dabeem, se'ere n soe la, dabeem zʋa pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ sɩbgrɛ n boe. Sɛka n zotɩ dabeem na ka tarɩ nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ.

eng: We love because God first loved us.
local: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma tarɩ nõŋlʋm mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ dẽŋɛ nõŋɛ tõma mɛ.

eng: Those who say, I love God and hate their brothers or sisters are liars. After all, those who dont love their brothers or sisters whom they have seen can hardly love God whom they have not seen!.
local: Nẽr-sɛka sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee sise a sɔbia, a de la põmpɔrma. Se'ere n soe la, a sãn ka nõŋɛ a sɔbia tɩ a yẽtɩ la, a ka tã wʋn nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a ka yẽtɩ la.

eng: This commandment we have from him: Those who claim to love God ought to love their brother and sister also.
local: Nõorɛ sɛka tɩ Krisi bɔ tõma la n wãna: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ a mẽ nõŋɛ a sɔbia.

eng: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born from God. Whoever loves someone who is a parent loves the child born to the parent.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ sɩra tɩ Azezi de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na, de la Nawẽnnɛ bia. Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ sɔ, mẽ nõŋɛ a kɔma mɛ.

eng: This is how we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and keep Gods commandments.
local: Sɛla n basɛ tɩ tõma mina tɩ tõma nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kɔma n wãna: La de la tõma n nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee sakra a nõorɛ wuu la.

eng: This is the love of God: we keep Gods commandments. Gods commandments are not difficult.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm de la tɩ sakra a nõorɛ. A nõorɛ la tɩ a bɔ tɩ la ka tɔɩ.

eng: because everyone who is born from God defeats the world. And this is the victory that has defeated the world: our faith.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n de Nawẽnnɛ bia n yãŋrɩ dũnia be'em. Tõma n yãŋɛ dũnia be'em na, de la tõma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la.

eng: Who defeats the world? Isnt it the one who believes that Jesus is Gods Son?.
local: Ãne n tã wʋn yãŋɛ dũnia? La de la nẽr-sɛka n bɔ sɩra tɩ Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la mã'a.

eng: This is the one who came by water and blood: Jesus Christ. Not by water only but by water and blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
local: Azezi-krisi n wa'am dũnia zuo la, la doose la ko'om, la zɩɩm. La dagɩ ko'om misga la mã'a, la ken dɛna la ẽn ki tɩ a zɩɩm yese la. La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ a kaseto, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n de sɩra dãana la ĩyã.

eng: The three are testifying.
local: La de la bõn-bãna batã n bɔ'ɔrɩ kaseto.

eng: the Spirit, the water, and the blood—and the three are united in agreement.
local: La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la ko'om, la zɩɩm. La bãma batã kaseto de la bʋyɩla.

eng: If we receive human testimony, Gods testimony is greater, because this is what God testified: he has testified about his Son.
local: Tõma n sakrɩ nẽrsaalba kaseto tɩ ba bɔ'ɔrɩ la, Nawẽnnɛ kaseto tarɩ pãŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ. La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n bɔ a Dayʋa la kaseto.

eng: The one who believes in Gods Son has the testimony within; the one who doesnt believe God has made God a liar, because that one has not believed the testimony that God gave about his Son.
local: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la sɩra, tarɩ kaseto ẽŋa mɛ a sũure pʋam. La nẽr-sɛka n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, basɛ tɩ a ãna wʋ põmpɔrma la, ẽŋa n ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ a Dayʋa la kaseto la ĩyã.

eng: And this is the testimony: God gave eternal life to us, and this life is in his Son.
local: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ kaseto la se'em n wãna: A bɔ tɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, la vɔm ẽŋa de la a Dayʋa la ĩyã.

eng: The one who has the Son has life. The one who doesnt have Gods Son does not have life.
local: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ a Dayʋa boe la ẽ na, tarɩ vɔm na mɛ. La nẽr-sɛka tɩ a Dayʋa ka boe la ẽ na, ka tarɩ vɔm.

eng: I write these things to you who believe in the name of Gods Son so that you can know that you have eternal life.
local: Mam gʋlsɛ wãna bɔ'ɔra ya tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ yãma sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa sɩra la, tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: This is the confidence that we have in our relationship with God: If we ask for anything in agreement with his will, he listens to us.
local: Tõma sũure ãn ke'eŋo mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ tõma mina tɩ tõma sãn sose sɛla tɩ la masɛ wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em, a selsrɩ tõma mɛ.

eng: If we know that he listens to whatever we ask, we know that we have received what we asked from him.
local: La tõma n mi tɩ a selsrɩ tõma n sosrɩ ẽ sɛla la, tõma mi tɩ a bɔ'ɔrɩ tɩ sɛla tɩ tɩ sosrɩ la mɛ.

eng: If anyone sees a brother or sister committing a sin that does not result in death, they should pray, and God will give life to them—that is, to those who commit sins that dont result in death. There is a sin that results in death—Im not saying that you should pray about that.
local: Nẽr-sɛka sãn yẽ a sɔbia n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ego n ka sẽm na kũm, a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ẽ vɔm. La de la tõon-be'ego sɛka n ka sẽm na kũm tɩ mam yetɩ. Tõon-be'ego n boe n sẽm na nẽra kũm, la dagɩ tõon-be'ego ẽŋa yelle tɩ mam yetɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ a ĩyã.

eng: Every unrighteous action is sin, but there is a sin that does not result in death.
local: Sɛla n ka masɛ wuu de la tõon-be'ero. La, la dagɩ tõon-be'ego woo n sẽm na kũm.

eng: We know that everyone born from God does not sin, but the ones born from God guard themselves, and the evil one cannot touch them.
local: Tõma mi tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n de Nawẽnnɛ bia ka le tõnna tõon-be'ero. La Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la gu'uri ẽ mɛ, tɩ be'em dãana Asũtãana ka tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ sɛla.

eng: We know we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
local: Tõma mi tɩ tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. La dũnia dõma wuu boe la be'em dãana Asũtãana nu'usum.

eng: We know that Gods Son has come and has given us understanding to know the one who is true. We are in the one who is true by being in his Son, Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
local: Tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa n wa'am wa bɔ tɩ yɛm, tɩ tɩ bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de sɩra dãana la. Tõma lagɩm na sɛka n de sɩra dãana la dɛna ayɩla, a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ sɩra sɩra, n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Little children, guard yourselves from idols!.
local: M kɔma, guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la baga.

eng: From the elder. To the chosen gentlewoman and her children, whom I truly love.
local: Mam n de kẽema la n gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra zuudãan-pɔka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la, la a kɔma tɩ mam nõŋɛ sɩra sɩra la. La, la dagɩ mam mã'a, nẽr-sɛba wuu n bãŋɛ sɩra la nõŋɛ ba mɛ.

eng: because of the truth that remains with us and will be with us forever.
local: Se'ere n soe la, sɩra la n boe la tɩ lɛ, dee wʋn ken bɔna la tɩ lɛ wakatɛ wuu.

eng: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, will be ours who live in truth and love.
local: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ, zoe tɩ ninbãalga, dee bɔ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm, tɩ tɩ wʋn vɔa la sɩra, dee tara nõŋlʋm.

eng: I was overjoyed to find some of your children living in the truth, just as we had been commanded by the Father.
local: Mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ halɩ zozo'e, mam n yẽ tɩ fʋ kɔma la basɛba dolɩ la sɩra sore wʋ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n yele tɩ se'em na.

eng: Now, dear friends, I am requesting that we love each other. Its not as though Im writing a new command to you, but its one we have had from the beginning.
local: Lɛɛlɛ wã, zuudãan-pɔka, mam n gʋlsɛ fʋ wã dagɩ nõor-paalga, la de la nõor-sɛka tɩ tɩ wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la, mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ tɩ nõŋɛ taaba.

eng: This is love: that we live according to his commands. This is the command that you heard from the beginning: live in love.
local: Nõŋlʋm n de sɛla n wãna: La de la tɩ ɩta wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ n yele se'em na. Nõor-sɛka tɩ ya wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la de la bɛla, la de la ya nõŋɛ taaba.

eng: Many deceivers have gone into the world who do not confess that Jesus Christ came as a human being. This kind of person is the deceiver and the antichrist.
local: Nẽrba zozo'e n saagɛ dũnia pã'asrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba tuura. Nẽr-bãma ka sakɛ tɩ Azezi lebge la nẽrsaala wa'am dũnia zuo. Nẽr-ẽŋa dãana n de põmpã'asʋm dãana, la Krisi sɩ'ɩsra.

eng: Watch yourselves so that you dont lose what weve worked for but instead receive a full reward.
local: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa, da kãn basɛ-ya tɩ tõma n tõm sɛla bɔ ya la bɔɩ, tɩ ya wʋn to'oge yɔɔrɔ wuu.

eng: Anyone who goes too far and does not continue in the teaching about Christ does not have God. Whoever continues in this teaching has both the Father and the Son.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yõsgɛ tole nẽŋa ka bɔna Krisi zãsŋɔ la pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ ka boe la ẽ. La sɛka woo n boe a zãsŋɔ la pʋam, Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa boe la ẽ mɛ.

eng: Whoever comes to you who does not affirm this teaching should neither be received nor welcomed into your home.
local: Nẽra sãn tarɩ zãsŋɔ ayẽma wa'am ya zẽ'am, ya da to'oge ẽ ya yire, la ya da pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.

eng: because welcoming people like that is the same thing as sharing in their evil actions.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ, lagɩm na ẽ mɛ a tõon-be'ero pʋam.

eng: I have a lot to tell you. I dont want to use paper and ink, but I hope to visit you and talk with you face-to-face, so that our joy can be complete.
local: Mam tarɩ yɛla zozo'e mɛ tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ ya, la mam ka boorɩ tɩ m gʋlsɛ ba gõŋɔ pʋam bɔ ya. La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ tɩ mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am, dee tɔgɛ m mẽŋa bɔ ya, tɩ tɩ sũ-yẽlga wʋn dɛna sũ-yẽlga zõnzõnnɔ.

eng: Your chosen sisters children greet you.
local: Fʋ sɔbi-pɔka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la kɔma pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ.

eng: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I truly love.
local: Mam n de kẽema la, n gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra m sɩrɛ Agayusi tɩ mam nõŋɛ sɩra sɩra.

eng: Dear friend, Im praying that all is well with you and that you enjoy good health in the same way that you prosper spiritually.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɛ, mam kã'arɩ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ sɛla woo ãna sõŋa bɔ fɔ, tɩ fʋ wʋn tara ĩmã'asʋm wʋ fõn dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sõŋa se'em na.

eng: I was overjoyed when the brothers and sisters arrived and spoke highly of your faithfulness to the truth, shown by how you live according to the truth.
local: Tɩ sɔbiisi n wa'am kalam wa yele fõn dolɩ sɩra sore se'em, la fõn vɔa la sɩra se'em. Tɩ mam ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

eng: I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are living according to the truth.
local: Sɛla ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sũ-yẽlga gãnna mam n wõm tɩ ba yetɩ mam kɔma dolɩ sɩra la.

eng: Dear friend, you act faithfully in whatever you do for our brothers and sisters, even though they are strangers.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɛ, fõn ɩtɩ sɛla bɔ'ɔra sɔbiisi la, baa la sɛba n de sãama la, fʋ ɩtɩ la sɩra.

eng: They spoke highly of your love in front of the church. You all would do well to provide for their journey in a way that honors God.
local: Ba tɔgɛ fʋ nõŋlʋm na yelle mɛ Azezi nẽrba zẽ'am. Fʋ sãn sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ba sore la wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, fʋ ẽŋɛ sõŋa.

eng: because they left on their journey for the sake of Jesus Christ without accepting any support from the Gentiles.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Azezi-krisi tõoma yelle tɩ ba wɛ'ɛsa sore la, dee ka to'oge sɛla nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Azezi la zẽ'am.

eng: Therefore, we ought to help people like this so that we can be coworkers with the truth.
local: Tõma de la tɩ sõŋɛ nẽr-bãma buuri pa'alɛ tɩ tõma naagɛ la ba mɛ dɛna sɩra kõ-yẽlga la tõntõnɩba.

eng: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, doesnt welcome us.
local: Mam daan gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ mɛ bɔ Azezi nẽrba, la Adiyotrɛfɩ n boorɩ tɩ a dɛna nẽŋadãana la ka to'oge mam n yele se'em na.

eng: Because of this, if I come, I will bring up what he has done—making unjustified and wicked accusations against us. And as if that were not enough, he not only refuses to welcome the brothers and sisters but stops those who want to do so and even throws them out of the church!.
local: Bɛla, mam sãn wa wa'am, mam wʋn pa'alɛ sɛla tɩ a ẽŋɛ, la ẽn parnɩ tɔgra tõma yetɔg-be'ero la. La, la dagɩ bɛla mã'a, a ken zagsra mɛ ka tɔ'ɔsra sɔbiisi, dee mẽ gu'ura nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba tɔ'ɔsra ba la, digra ba Azezi nẽrba la pʋam.

eng: Dear friend, dont imitate what is bad but what is good. Whoever practices what is good belongs to God. Whoever practices what is bad has not seen God.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɛ, da pagsɛ sɛla n de be'em, pagsra sɛla n ãn sõŋa. Nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽra. La nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ sɛla n de be'em ka mi Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: Everyone speaks highly of Demetrius, even the truth itself. We also speak highly of him, and you know that what we say is true.
local: La sãn dɛna Ademetrisi, nẽrba wuu tɔgrɩ a yelle sõŋa mɛ, tɩ ẽn dolɩ sɩra se'em na mẽ pa'ala a yelle sõŋa. Tõma mẽ tɔgrɩ a yelle sõŋa mɛ. La fʋ mi tɩ tõma kaseto de la sɩra.

eng: I have a lot to say to you, but I dont want to use pen and ink.
local: Mam tarɩ yɛla zozo'e mɛ tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ fɔ, la mam ka boorɩ tɩ m gʋlsɛ ba gõŋɔ pʋam.

eng: I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face-to-face.
local: Mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ tɩ mam wʋn wa yẽ fɔ tɩ tɩ sõsɛ la taaba.

eng: Peace be with you. Your friends here greet you. Greet our friends there by name.
local: Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la fɔ. Fʋ sɩrdõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ. Pʋ'ʋsɛ tɩ sɩrdõma la wuu ayɩla ayɩla bɔ tɔ.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the one who holds Gods seven spirits and the seven stars: I know your works. You have the reputation of being alive, and you are in fact dead.
local: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Sardɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ, la wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ la n yele se'em n wãna: ‹Mam mi ya tõoma mɛ, fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ yese mɛ tɩ ba yetɩ ya vɔa mɛ, la, ya ki mɛ.

eng: Wake up and strengthen whatever you have left, teetering on the brink of death, for Ive found that your works are far from complete in the eyes of my God.
local: Bɛla, guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ sɛla n deege la a ki la, yãagɛ da ki ba'asɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ ya tõoma la ka paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm.

eng: So remember what you received and heard. Hold on to it and change your hearts and lives. If you dont wake up, I will come like a thief, and you wont know what time I will come upon you.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya zãsŋɔ la tɩ ya daan wõm dee to'oge ẽ se'em na, sakɛ-ya ẽ teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Ya sãn ka guuse, mam wʋn wa'am wa di'ige ya wʋ nayiga la, la ya kãn bãŋɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am.

eng: But you do have a few people in Sardis who havent stained their clothing. They will walk with me clothed in white because they are worthy.
local: La nẽrba basɛba n boe yãma pʋam Sardɩ ka dẽgɛ ba futo. Bãma n wʋn yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ doose mam, bãma n masɛ la ĩyã.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will wear white clothing like this. I wont scratch out their names from the scroll of life, but will declare their names in the presence of my Father and his angels.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ wʋn wa yɛ la fu-pɛɛlsɩ bɛla, la mam kãn saam a yʋ'ʋrɛ vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam. La mam wʋn wi a yʋ'ʋrɛ mam Sɔ, la a malɛkadõma la nẽŋam yetɩ a de la mam nẽra.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› ».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the one who is holy and true, who has the key of David. Whatever he opens, no one will shut; and whatever he shuts, no one opens.
local: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Filadɛlfi Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n de pʋpeelem dãana, la sɩra dãana n tarɩ naba Adavidi kʋlɩnbiɛ, sɛka n yʋ'ʋrɩ tɩ nẽra ka tãna yɔ'ɔra, dee yɔ'ɔra tɩ nẽra ka tãna yʋ'ʋra la n yele se'em n wãna:.

eng: I know your works. Look! I have set in front of you an open door that no one can shut. You have so little power, and yet you have kept my word and havent denied my name.
local: ‹Mam mi ya tõoma mɛ, mam mi tɩ ya pãŋa ka zo'oge, la baa la bɛla, ya sakɛ mam yetɔga la mɛ, la ya ka sɩ'ɩsɛ tɩ yãma ka mi mam. Mam yo'oge la sore bɔ ya, la nẽra nẽra kãn yãŋɛ yʋ ẽ.

eng: Because of this I will make the people from Satans synagogue —I will make them come and bow down at your feet and realize that I have loved you.
local: Mam n wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ Asũtãana la se'em n wãna: Nẽr-bãma yetɩ bãma de la Zifdõma, la dagɩ bɛla, ba de la põmpɔrndõma. Mam wʋn basɛ mɛ tɩ ba wa'am wa ka dũma ya nẽŋam, la ba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam nõŋɛ ya.

eng: Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you safe through the time of testing that is about to come over the whole world, to test those who live on earth.
local: Yãma n sakɛ mam yetɔga la mõrgɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa na ĩyã, mam mẽ wʋn gu'ura ya sõŋa tɩ toogo sɛka n sẽm dũnia wuu zuo, tɩ a makɛ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe dũnia zuo la, da paagɛ ya.

eng: Im coming soon. Hold on to what you have so that no one takes your crown.
local: Mam sẽm na lɛɛlɛ, gʋrɛ-ya sɛla tɩ ya tarɩ la kãŋkãŋɛ, tɩ nẽra da to'oge ya yɔɔrɔ.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will make them pillars in the temple of my God, and they will never leave it. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the New Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my God. I will also write on them my own new name.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn botɩ a ãna wʋ zẽerɛ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yire pʋam, la ba kãn malɩn yese ẽ mĩ. La mam wʋn gʋlsɛ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ, la a tẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm paalga n ze'ele saazuum Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am signa la yʋ'ʋrɛ a zuo, la mam wʋn ken gʋlsɛ mam mẽŋa yʋ'ʋr-paalga la a zuo.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› ».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of Gods creation.
local: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Laodise Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n de Amina la, sɛka n de sɩra dãana, dee sɩrɩ tɔgra sɛla tɩ a nini yẽ, dee ken dɛna sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam sɛla wuu ze'ele a zẽ'am n yele se'em n wãna:.

eng: I know your works. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish that you were either cold or hot.
local: ‹Mam mi ya tõoma mɛ, mam mi tɩ ya dagɩ mã'asga, ya mẽ dagɩ tʋʋlga. Mam boorɩ nɩ tɩ ya dɛna la mã'asga bɩɩ tʋʋlga.

eng: So because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, Im about to spit you out of my mouth.
local: La yãma n ka de mã'asga bɩɩ tʋʋlga mam tõoma la pʋam, dee ãna wam na, mam wʋn ʋkɛ ya mɛ m nõorʋm basɛ.

eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Ya yetɩ yãma de la bõntatba, yãma tarɩ sɛla woo mɛ tɩ sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ yãma. La, ya ka mi tɩ ya de la ninbãal-dõma, la farɛ dõma. Ya de la nasdõma, la bɛɛla, la fɔɔsɩ.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: Bɛla ĩyã, mam kã'anɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya da sãlma mẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ ba zuge bugum pʋam yese ba mam zẽ'am, tɩ ya wʋn sɩrɩ dɛna bõntatba. La ya da fu-pɛɛlsɩ yɛ pĩ ya yãnnɛ na, dee da tɩɩm dõ ya nini yẽta.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Mam sɩbgrɩ la sɛba wuu tɩ mam nõŋɛ, dee dɛmsra ba. Bɛla, nargɛ-ya pãŋa teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya, mam ze la kʋlɩŋa nõorʋm wẽ'era, nẽr-sɛka n wõm mam kõa yo'oge kʋlɩŋa bɔ mam, mam wʋn kẽ a zẽ'am lagɩm na ẽ di, tɩ a mẽ lagɩm na mam di.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn botɩ a lagɩm na mam po zĩ'ire mam na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo, wʋ mam yãŋɛ po lagɩm na mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zĩ'ire a na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› ».

eng: Jude, a slave of Jesus Christ and brother of James. To those who are called, loved by God the Father and kept safe by Jesus Christ.
local: Mam Azudi n de Azezi-krisi tõntõnna, la Azakɩ yɩbga la n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba tɩ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ wi tɩ ya dɛna a nẽrba, nõŋɛ ya, tɩ Azezi-krisi gu'ura ya la.

eng: May you have more and more mercy, peace, and love.
local: Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zoe ya ninbãalga zozo'e, bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm, la nõŋlʋm zozo'e.

eng: Dear friends, I wanted very much to write to you concerning the salvation we share. Instead, I must write to urge you to fight for the faith delivered once and for all to Gods holy people.
local: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, mam daan boorɩ mɛ tɩ m gʋlsɛ ya la m sũure wuu tɔgɛ fãarɛ la tɩ tɩ wuu lagɩm yẽ la yelle. Mam bɩsɛ mɛ tɩ la de la pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam gʋlsɛ kã'an ya, tɩ ya mõrgɛ zɛbɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ yetɔga la tɩ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ yetɔg-bãma bɔ a nẽrba la nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a wakatɛ wuu ĩyã.

eng: Godless people have slipped in among you. They turn the grace of our God into unrestrained immorality and deny our only master and Lord, Jesus Christ. Judgment was passed against them a long time ago.
local: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba basɛba n li'ise kẽ ya pʋam. Nẽr-bãma de la sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, tɛɛra a yel-sõnnɛ yetɔga la, dɩkɛ tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ, dee sɩ'ɩsra Azezi-krisi n de tɩ kɛ'ɛma la tɩ Zuudãana ayɩla mã'a la. Halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ la põn gʋlsɛ sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ ba wʋn yẽ.

eng: I want to remind you of something you already know very well. The Lord, who once saved a people out of Egypt, later destroyed those who didnt maintain their faith.
local: Baa la yãma n mi bõn-bãma wuu sõŋa sõŋa la, mam boorɩ tɩ m tẽegɛ ya mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn fãagɛ a nẽrba Ezipti tẽŋa la, a yʋʋn yãŋa basɛ mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la ki.

eng: I remind you too of the angels who didnt keep their position of authority but deserted their own home. The Lord has kept them in eternal chains in the underworld until the judgment of the great day.
local: La malɛkadõma sɛba n yʋʋn ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ba ke'eŋo se'em basɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba la, Nawẽnnɛ ba ba mɛ la bãnsɩ, basɛ tɩ ba bɔna lika pʋam wakatɛ wuu gu'ura dabs-kãtɛ la n de sarɩyadia daarɛ la.

eng: In the same way, Sodom and Gomorrah and neighboring towns practiced immoral sexual relations and pursued other sexual urges. By undergoing the punishment of eternal fire, they serve as a warning.
local: La sãn dɛna Sodom la Gomõorɩ dõma, la tẽnsɩ sɛba n lɛm ba la, n mẽ yʋʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ bɛla, tõm yalsɩ tõoma, la, la dagɩ la nẽrsaalba mã'a. Ba yʋʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ mɛ n de bugum n ka kĩŋri, tɩ la dɛna kã'aŋɔ bɔ nẽrba basɛba.

eng: Yet, even knowing this, these dreamers in the same way pollute themselves, reject authority, and slander the angels.
local: La de la bõn-bãma buuri n bɛla tɩ nẽr-bãma mẽ ɩtɩ. Ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na botɩ ba dẽgra la ba mẽŋa, ba pɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ nõorɛ la ba lɛ la mɛ, dee tʋʋra saazuum pãnsɩ dõma.

eng: The archangel Michael, when he argued with the devil about Moses body, did not dare charge him with slander. Instead, he said, The Lord rebuke you.
local: La baa malɛkadõma kɛ'ɛma Amisɛlɩ n yʋʋn tɔgrɩ la Asũtãana wẽ'era nõke'ene Amoyisi kũm ĩyã la, a ka saagɛ tɔgɛ zɛrgɛ ẽ tʋ ẽ, a yʋʋn yetɩ: «Zuudãana la wʋn sɩbgɛ fɔ.».

eng: But these people slander whatever they dont understand. They are destroyed by what they know instinctively, as though they were irrational animals.
local: Nẽr-bãma tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ ba ka mi be'em, ba ɩtɩ la yooryooro, ba ãn wʋ dũnsi n ka tarɩ yɛm n ɩtɩ se'em na, bõn-ẽŋa n sã'anɩ ba.

eng: They are damned, for they follow in the footsteps of Cain. For profit they give themselves over to Balaams error. They are destroyed in the uprising of Korah.
local: Ba wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ba dolɩ la so-sɛka tɩ Akãɩ yʋʋn doose la, la ba kẽ'esɛ ba mẽŋa mɛ tuure pʋam yɔɔrɔ ĩyã, wʋ Abalaam n ẽŋɛ se'em ligri ĩyã la. Ban sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ wʋ Akoore n yʋʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em na, ba ki mɛ.

eng: These people are like jagged rocks just below the surface of the water waiting to snag you when they join your love feasts. They feast with you without reverence. They care only for themselves. They are waterless clouds carried along by the winds; fruitless autumn trees, twice dead, uprooted.
local: Ba sãn lagɩm na ya dita nõŋlʋm dia, la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya. Ba ka zotɩ yãnnɛ, ba kɔ'ɔn dita mɛ ɩta sũ-yẽlga ba mẽŋa ĩyã. Ba ãn wʋ sawara n ka tarɩ ko'om tɩ kʋsebgo pe'erɩ, ba ãn wʋ tɩɩsɩ n ka tarɩ biɛ ba wɔŋa wakatɛ, tɩ ba võ, basɛ tɩ ba võorɔ, la ba yẽga wuu kʋɩ.

eng: wild waves of the sea foaming up their own shame; wandering stars for whom the darkness of the underworld is reserved forever.
local: Ba ken ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ ko-mĩ'isi n wẽ'erɩ la pãŋa, ba yãnnɛ tõoma la ãn wʋ ko-kãtɛ fulfuka la, ba ãn wʋ wõrbiɛ sɛba n eerɩ. Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm na zẽ'a n de lika tam bĩŋe wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ gu'ura ba.

eng: Enoch, who lived seven generations after Adam, prophesied about these people when he said, See, the Lord comes with his countless holy ones.
local: La po dɛna la nẽr-bãma yelle tɩ A'enõkɩ n de tɩ yaaba bʋyopɔɩ dãana lan pɔsɛ la Adam na, yʋʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ sẽm mɛ la a malɛkadõma tʋsa tʋsa.

eng: to execute judgment on everyone and to convict everyone about every ungodly deed they have committed in their ungodliness as well as all the harsh things that sinful ungodly people have said against him.
local: tɩ a di nẽrsaalba wuu sarɩya, sɩbgɛ tõon-be'ero dõma wuu, ban sɩ'ɩsɛ ẽ tõnna tõon-be'ero la wuu ĩyã, la yetɔg-be'ero wuu tɩ ba tɔgɛ pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ na ĩyã.».

eng: These are faultfinding grumblers, living according to their own desires. They speak arrogant words and they show partiality to people when they want a favor in return.
local: Nẽr-bãma de la nẽrba n yõnnɩ wakatɛ wuu, ba sũure ka ãn yẽlʋm la ban ãn se'em na. Ba ɩtɩ la ba mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em, tɔgra pa'al-m-mẽŋa yetɔga, dee tɔgra nẽrba nõ-yẽlʋm yetɔga tɩ ba yẽ yõorɔ.

eng: But you, dear friends, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, tẽra-ya yetɔg-sɛba tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Tõntõnɩba la daan dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ bɔ ya la.

eng: They said to you, In the end time scoffers will come living according to their own ungodly desires.
local: Ba daan yele ya yetɩ: «Dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ, nẽrba n la'arɩ nẽrba n wʋn wa bɔna dɔla ba mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em, sɩ'ɩsra Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: These people create divisions. Since they dont have the Spirit, they are worldly.
local: La de la bãma n tarɩ welgre sẽna. Ba dolɩ la nẽrsaalba pʋtẽ'era n boorɩ se'em, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ka boe la ba lɛ.

eng: But you, dear friends: build each other up on the foundation of your most holy faith, pray in the Holy Spirit.
local: La yãma, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, sõŋra-ya taaba sẽnna nẽŋa yan bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la pʋam, pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ la a Sɩa pãŋa.

eng: keep each other in the love of God, wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, who will give you eternal life.
local: Bɔna-ya Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm na pʋam gu'ura tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n de ninbãal-zɔta la wʋn bɔ ya vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Have mercy on those who doubt.
local: Zɔta-ya sɛba yɛm n yɔɔrɩ la ninbãalga.

eng: Save some by snatching them from the fire. Fearing God, have mercy on some, hating even the clothing contaminated by their sinful urges.
local: Fãagɛ-ya sɛba n boe bugum pʋam na. La, la sãn dɛna basɛba la, zɔta-ya ba ninbãalga, la ya guuse ya mẽŋa da lagɩm na ba tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ tɩ ba mẽŋa boorɩ la baa fẽfẽe.

eng: To the one who is able to protect you from falling, and to present you blameless and rejoicing before his glorious presence.
local: Pẽgɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ, ẽŋa n tarɩ pãŋa wʋn gu ya tɩ ya kãn basɛ a sore, dee wʋn tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka ze'ele a nẽŋam tɩ ya ka tara dõrga, tɩ ya yẽ a na'am pãŋa, la sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

eng: to the only God our savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, belong glory, majesty, power, and authority, before all time, now and forever. Amen.
local: Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a n de tɩ Fãagra tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã, n soe pẽka, la nã'asgɔ, la pãŋa, la ke'eŋo, lan dẽŋɛ pĩ'iluŋo, la lɛɛlɛ wã, la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: A revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. Christ made it known by sending it through his angel to his servant John.
local: Gõŋɔ wã tarɩ la sɛla tɩ Azezi-krisi puke pa'alɛ. Nawẽnnɛ n daan bɔ ẽ bõn-bãma tɩ a pa'alɛ a tõntõnɩba sɛla n wʋn ẽŋɛ wakatɛ fẽfẽe pʋam. Azezi-krisi daan tõm na a malɛka a tõntõnna Azã zẽ'am tɩ a pa'alɛ ẽ bõn-bãma.

eng: who bore witness to the word of God and to the witness of Jesus Christ, including all that John saw.
local: Azã n gʋlsɛ sɛla wuu tɩ a yẽ la pa'alɛ tɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ Azezi-krisi puke pa'alɛ ẽŋa.

eng: Favored is the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and favored are those who listen to it being read, and keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n karẽŋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ a to'oge tɔgɛ wã, la sɛba n selsrɩ bõn-sɛba n gʋlsɛ gõn-ẽna wã pʋam dee sakra ba tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Wakatɛ la lɛm mɛ, tɩ bɛla wuu wʋn ẽŋɛ.

eng: John, to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace and peace to you from the one who is and was and is coming, and from the seven spirits that are before Gods throne.
local: Mam Azã n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ n boe Azi so'olʋm pʋam na Wẽndeto nẽrba. Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n daan boe, n ken bɔna, dee wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu la, la sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ n boe a na'am bĩmbĩnne nẽŋam, la Azezi-krisi n tɔgrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yelle sɩra, ẽŋa n ki dee dɛna pɔspɔsɩ dãana n vo'oge, dɛna dũnia na'adõma naba la, wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm. Azezi-krisi nõŋɛ tõma mɛ, ki tɩ a zɩɩm fãagɛ tõma tõon-be'ero pʋam.

eng: who made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be glory and power forever and always. Amen.
local: botɩ tõma dɛna a so'olʋm kãabgɔ kẽma tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ n de a Sɔ la. Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ, la pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

eng: Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, including those who pierced him, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of him. This is so. Amen.
local: Bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la sawara pʋam sẽna. Nẽrsaalba wuu wʋn yẽ ẽ, baa la nẽr-sɛba n daan lʋ ẽ na. Buuri wuu nẽrba n boe dũnia wʋn kɛlla mɛ fabla a yelle. Ẽe, la de la bɛla. Amina.

eng: I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, the one who is and was and is coming, the Almighty.
local: Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la yetɩ: «Mam de la pĩ'iluŋo la ba'asgɔ, mam n daan boe, n ken bɔna, dee wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu.».

eng: I, John, your brother who shares with you in the hardship, kingdom, and endurance that we have in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and my witness about Jesus.
local: Mam de la Azã n de ya sɔbia n po nãmsra, lagɩm na yãma po tara pʋrɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam, ken po lagɩm na yãma mõrgra sẽnna nẽŋa Azezi doosgo la pʋam. Mam daan tɔgɛ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, la Azezi-krisi yelle, tɩ ba tarɩ mam sẽŋɛ tẽŋa tɩ ko'om gilge yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Patmõsɩ.

eng: I was in a Spirit-inspired trance on the Lords day, and I heard behind me a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana daarɛ n de Lasrɩ daarɛ la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la mam, botɩ mam wõm kõa n tɔgɛ ke'enke'em mam poorʋm wʋ benkonne n peebe la.

eng: It said, Write down on a scroll whatever you see, and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
local: yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ sɛla tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ la ẽŋɛ gõŋɔ pʋam, soolʋm bɔ tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ la Wẽndeto nẽrba: Efɛɛzɩ, la Simirni, la Pɛrgamɩ, la Tiyatiiri, la Sardɩ, la Filadɛlfi, la Laodise.».

eng: I turned to see who was speaking to me, and when I turned, I saw seven oil lamps burning on top of seven gold stands.
local: Tɩ mam wẽrgɛ tɩ m bɩsɛ sɛka n tɔgrɩ la mam na, la mam n wẽrgɛ la, mam yẽ la sãlma fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ.

eng: In the middle of the lampstands I saw someone who looked like the Human One. He wore a robe that stretched down to his feet, and he had a gold sash around his chest.
local: La nẽra n boe fɩtladõma la tẽŋasʋka ãna wʋ nẽrsaala dayʋa la. A yɛ la fuugo n wɔgɛ sige paagɛ a nãma, dee dɩkɛ sãlma sẽbrɩga bobe a yõ'ogɔ.

eng: His head and hair were white as white wool—like snow—and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
local: A zomto daan pelge mɛ ãna yɩlɩ yɩlɩ wʋ lɔnnɔ na, tɩ a nini ãna wʋ bugwitum na.

eng: His feet were like fine brass that has been purified in a furnace, and his voice sounded like rushing water.
local: A nãma daan yẽgrɩ wʋ zũuro tɩ ba zuge ba bugum pʋam na, dee tɩ a kõa la ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ dãmŋɔ la.

eng: He held seven stars in his right hand, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword. His appearance was like the sun shining with all its power.
local: A daan zãlɩ la wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ la a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo. Tɩ sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõa ayi ze'ele a nõorʋm yɛsra. A nẽŋa daan yẽgrɩ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa n ãn se'em na.

eng: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he put his right hand on me and said, Dont be afraid. Im the first and the last.
local: Mam n daan yẽ ẽ na, mam lui la tẽŋa a nẽŋam ãna wʋ kũm na. Tɩ a dɩkɛ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo paglɛ mam zuo yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, tɩ mam n de pĩ'iluŋo, la ba'asgɔ.

eng: and the living one. I was dead, but look! Now Im alive forever and always. I have the keys of Death and the Grave.
local: Mam de la sɛka n vɔɩ. Mam daan ki mɛ, la mam n wãna, mam vɔa mɛ wakatɛ wuu. Mam n tarɩ ke'eŋo sɔna kũm, la kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa.

eng: So write down what you have seen, both the scene now before you and the things that are about to unfold after this.
local: Bɛla, gʋlsɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ yẽ, sɛla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, la sɛla n wʋn wa ẽŋɛ poorʋm na.

eng: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands, here is what they mean: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
local: Wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ tɩ fʋ yẽ mam zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo pʋam, la sãlma fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ la võorɛ n wãna: Wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ la de la tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ la Wẽndeto la nẽrba gu'urba, la fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ la de la tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ la Wẽndeto nẽrba.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands.
local: Sɛka tɩ mam yẽ la, a ken yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Efɛɛzɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n zãlɩ wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo pʋam, dee sẽnna sãlma fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ tẽŋasʋka la n yele se'em n wãna:.

eng: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance. I also know that you dont put up with those who are evil. You have tested those who say they are apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
local: ‹Mam mi yãma n tõnnɩ se'em, yãma n nãmsrɩ dee mõrgra sẽnna nẽŋa se'em mɛ. Mam mi tɩ ya ka sakɛ basɛ tɩ nẽr-wẽensɩ tõnna la ya. Yãma makɛ nẽr-sɛba n yetɩ bãma de la Krisi Tõntõnɩba dee dagna bɛla la bɩsɛ mɛ, la yãma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ ba de la põmpɔrndõma.

eng: You have shown endurance and put up with a lot for my names sake, and you havent gotten tired.
local: Yãma mõrgɛ sẽnna nẽŋa mɛ, ya nãmsrɩ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ la ĩyã mɛ, dee ka basɛ tɩ ya bʋraanɛ bo'oge.

eng: But I have this against you: you have let go of the love you had at first.
local: La yel-sɛka tɩ mam tarɩ la yãma n wãna: Ya ka le nõŋɛ mam wʋ yãma n daan nõŋɛ mam se'em pɔspɔsɩ la.

eng: So remember the high point from which you have fallen. Change your hearts and lives and do the things you did at first. If you dont, Im coming to you. I will move your lampstand from its place if you dont change your hearts and lives.
local: Tẽegɛ-ya yãma n daan ɩtɩ se'em dee basɛ la. Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee ɩta yãma n daan ɩtɩ se'em pɔspɔsɩ la. Sãn dagna bɛla, mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa dɩkɛ ya fɩtla la a dɔglgɔ zẽ'am mɛ, ya sãn ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

eng: But you have this in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, which I also hate.
local: La baa bɛla, ya ɩtɩ bõn-sõŋɔ mɛ. Yãma sise sɛla tɩ Nikolayiti nẽrba ɩtɩ la mɛ, wʋ mam n mẽ sise ba tõoma se'em na.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will allow those who emerge victorious to eat from the tree of life, which is in Gods paradise.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa. Sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn bɔ ẽ nõorɛ tɩ a di vɔm tɩa n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la biɛ.› ».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the one who is the first and the last, who died and came back to life.
local: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Simirni Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n de pĩ'iluŋo la ba'asgɔ, sɛka n daan ki dee vo'oge la n yele se'em n wãna:.

eng: I know your hardship and poverty . I also know the hurtful things that have been spoken about you by those who say they are Jews .
local: ‹Mam mi ya nãmsgɔ la ya nõŋɔ la yelle mɛ, la baa bɛla, ya de la bõntatba. Mam mi be'em yetɔg-sɛba tɩ nẽr-sɛba n yetɩ bãma de la Zifdõma dee ka dɛna la tɔgɛ ya. Ba de la Asũtãana nẽrba.

eng: Dont be afraid of what you are going to suffer. Look! The devil is going to throw some of you into prison in order to test you. You will suffer hardship for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
local: Da zoe-ya dabeem yãma n wʋn nãmsɛ la. Bɩsɛ-ya, Asũtãana wʋn makɛ ya bɩsɛ mɛ, botɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ya nẽrba basɛba yʋ'a deem, la ya wʋn yẽ nãmsgɔ mɛ dabsa pia. Bɔ-ya mam sɩra halɩ ka paagɛ ya kũm, tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ya bɔ'a n de vɔm.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Those who emerge victorious wont be hurt by the second death.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa. Sɛka n yãŋɛ, kũm bʋyi dãana kãn ẽŋɛ ẽ sɛla.› ».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the one who has the sharp, two-edged sword.
local: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Pɛrgamɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n tarɩ sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõa ayi la n yele se'em n wãna:.

eng: I know that you are living right where Satans throne is. You are holding on to my name, and you didnt break faith with me even at the time that Antipas, my faithful witness, was killed among you, where Satan lives.
local: ‹Mam mi zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ yãma boe la mɛ, zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ Asũtãana na'am bĩmbĩnne boe. La ya dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa lablɛ la mam mɛ, ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ bɔ mam sɩra ka basɛ, baa la wakatɛ sɛka tɩ ba daan kʋ A'ãntipasɩ n de sɩra dãana n tɔgɛ mam yelle ya zẽ'am, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Asũtãana boe la.

eng: But I have a few things against you, because you have some there who follow Balaams teaching. Balaam had taught Balak to trip up the Israelites so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
local: La baa bɛla, mam tarɩ la yelle fẽe la ya. Yãma tarɩ la nẽrba n boe ya pʋam dɔla Abalaam zãsŋɔ. Abalaam yʋʋn zãsʋm na Abalakɩ ẽn wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em bẽrɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma botɩ ba obe baga nẽnnɔ, dee tõm yalsɩ tõoma.

eng: In the same way, you have some who follow the Nicolaitans teaching.
local: Yãma mẽ tarɩ la nẽrba basɛba n boe ya zẽ'am n dolɩ Nikolayiti dõma zãsŋɔ.

eng: So change your hearts and lives. If you dont, I am coming to you soon, and I will make war on them with the sword that comes from my mouth.
local: Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Sãn dagna bɛla, mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am lɛɛlɛ wã, la mam wʋn zɛbɛ la nẽr-bãma la sʋkɔbgɔ n ze'ele m nõorʋm yɛsra la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will give those who emerge victorious some of the hidden manna to eat. I will also give to each of them a white stone with a new name written on it, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa. Sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn bɔ ẽ dia n sʋgɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de mannɩ n ze'ele saazuum tɩ a di, la mam wʋn bɔ ẽ kug-pɛɛlga. Kug-ẽŋa zuo yʋ'ʋr-paalɛ n gʋlsɛ mĩ, tɩ nẽra nẽra ka mina ẽ, sãn dagna sɛka n to'oge ẽ na mã'a.› ».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of Gods Son, whose eyes are like a fiery flame, and whose feet are like fine brass.
local: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Tiyatiiri Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la nini n ãn wʋ bugwitum, tɩ a nãma yẽgra wʋ zũuro tɩ ba zuge bugum pʋam na n yele se'em n wãna:.

eng: I know your works, your love and faithfulness, your service and endurance. I also know that the works you have done most recently are even greater than those you did at first.
local: ‹Mam bãŋɛ ya tõoma, la ya nõŋlʋm, la yan bɔ sɩra, la yan tõm bɔ nẽrba, la yan mõrgɛ sẽnna nẽŋa se'em mɛ. La mam mi tɩ ya tõnnɩ lɛɛlɛ wã zɔ'ɔra gãnna yan tõm se'em pɔspɔsɩ la mɛ.

eng: But I have this against you: you put up with that woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. You allow her to teach and to mislead my servants into committing sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.
local: La yel-sɛka tɩ mam tarɩ la yãma n wãna: La de la yan sakɛ basɛ Azezabɛlɩ n de pɔka n yetɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, tɩ a zãsna nẽrba, pã'asra mam tõntõnɩba tɩ ba ɩta yalsɩ, dee ɔbra baga nẽnnɔ na.

eng: I gave her time to change her heart and life, but she refuses to change her life of prostitution.
local: Mam bɔ ẽ na wakatɛ tɩ a teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, la a ka boorɩ tɩ a teege yɛm basɛ a yalsɩ tõoma la.

eng: Look! Im throwing her onto a sickbed. I am casting those who have committed adultery with her into terrible hardship—if they dont change their hearts from following her practices.
local: Bɛla, mam wʋn basɛ tɩ a gã'arɛ yẽ nãmsgɔ zozo'e. La sɛba n lagɩm na ẽ tõnna yalsɩ tõoma la wʋn yẽ toogo zozo'e, sãn dagna tɩ ba basɛ la tõon-be'e-sɛba tɩ a botɩ ba tõnna la.

eng: and I will even put her children to death with disease. Then all the churches will know that Im the one who examines minds and hearts, and that I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.
local: Mam wʋn ken basɛ tɩ a kɔma ki, tɩ Wẽndeto nẽrba la wuu wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam de la sɛka n mi nẽrba tẽ'esgɔ, la ban boorɩ se'em. La mam wʋn yɔ ya nẽra woo masɛ wʋ ẽn tõnnɩ se'em.

eng: As for the rest of you in Thyatira—those of you who dont follow this teaching and havent learned the so-called deep secrets of Satan—I wont burden you with anything else.
local: La yãma Tiyatiiri nẽr-sɛba n ka sakrɩ zãsŋɔ bãma, dee ka mina sɛla tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Asũtãana yɛla n sʋgɛ la, mam ka tarɩ sɛla wʋn yele ya pa'asɛ.

eng: Just hold on to what you have until I come.
local: La gʋrɛ-ya sɛla tɩ ya tarɩ la kãŋkãŋɛ halɩ tɩ mam wa wa'am.

eng: To those who emerge victorious, keeping my practices until the end, I will give authority over the nations.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, mam wʋn botɩ a sɔna so'olʋm buuri wuu.

eng: to rule the nations with an iron rod and smash them like pottery.
local: La de la pãn-sɛka tɩ mam to'oge m Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ẽ. A wʋn tara la kuto dasaarɛ sɔna ba wʋ ban wõtɩ lɔg-kɛgsɩ la. La mam wʋn ken bɔ ẽ bewã'arɛ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› ».

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: La mam yẽ la gõŋɔ n boe sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo pʋam, tɩ a nẽŋa la a poore wuu gʋlsɛ, tɩ ba dɩkɛ bõnɔ n tabrɩ tabɛ ẽ zẽ'esɩ bayopɔɩ.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛka n tarɩ pãŋa n tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ãne n masɛ wʋn lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ la, dee lakɛ gõŋɔ la?».

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: La nẽra nẽra daan ka boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, bɩɩ dũnia zuo, bɩɩ tẽŋa wã pʋam pʋam n tã wʋn lakɛ gõŋɔ la, dee bɩsɛ a pʋam.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Tɩ mam daan kɛlla zozo'e, ban ka yẽ nẽra n masɛ n wʋn lakɛ gõŋɔ la, dee bɩsɛ a pʋam na ĩyã.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Tɩ kẽma la nẽra ayɩla yele mam yetɩ: «Da kɛlla. Bɩsɛ, sɛka n de Azuda buuri gɩgnɛ n de naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa la yãŋɛ mɛ, ẽŋa n tã wʋn lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ zẽ'esɩ bayopɔɩ la, dee lakɛ gõŋɔ la.».

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ Pebila n ze na'am bĩmbĩnne na tẽŋasʋka, tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ, la kẽma la gilge ẽ basɛ tẽŋasʋka. Pebila la ãn wʋ ba daan kʋ ẽ mɛ na. A tarɩ la ɩɩla bayopɔɩ, la nini bayopɔɩ n de Nawẽnnɛ sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ la tɩ a tõm dũnia wuu pʋam na.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Tɩ Pebila la paagɛ to'oge gõŋɔ la sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la zʋʋgɔ pʋam.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: Ẽn to'oge gõŋɔ la, bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la, la kẽma pisyi la anaasɩ la, ka la dũma a nẽŋam. Ba nẽra woo zãlɩ la kõlŋɔ, la sãlma laasɩ n pɩrɛ la tɩdarɛ n tarɩ yũuŋo n ãn yẽlʋm, n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba pʋ'ʋsgɔ.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
