local: Ba daan yõonɩ la yõom-paalɛ yetɩ: «Fõn masɛ tɩ fʋ to'oge gõŋɔ la lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ la. Se'ere n soe la, ba daan kʋ fõn mɛ, tɩ fõn dɩkɛ fʋ zɩɩm na yɔ to'oge nẽrsaalba buuri wuu, la yetɔga buuri wuu, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: Fõn botɩ ba dɛna la fʋ so'olʋm kãabgɔ kẽma tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, dee wʋn di na'am dũnia zuo.».

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Tɩ mam le bɩsɛ, wõm malɛkadõma kʋʋŋɔ kõa, ba daan de la tʋsa nõorɛ tʋsa tʋsa. Ba daan gilge la na'am bĩmbĩnne na, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ, la kẽma la.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: Ba daan yõonɩ mɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Pebila tɩ ba daan kʋ la n masɛ tɩ a to'oge ke'eŋo, la bõntarsõm, la yɛm, la pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa, la pẽka.».

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Tɩ mam wõm bõn-nãanɩsɩ wuu n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, la tẽŋa zuo, la tẽŋa pʋam pʋam, la ko-kãtɛ pʋam, la bõn-nãanɩsɩ n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu n yetɩ: «Sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo, la Pebila la n soe pẽka, la nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa, la ke'eŋo wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la yetɩ: «Amina.» Tɩ kẽma la ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asra ẽ.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam bɩsɛ mɛ yẽ tɩ kʋlɩŋa n yo'oge Nawẽnnɛ yire. Tɩ kõa la tɩ mam wõm pɔspɔsɩ n tɔgɛ la mam wʋ benkonne n peebe la, yele mam yetɩ: «Zom wa'am kalam, tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fʋ sɛla n wʋn wa ẽŋɛ.».

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa darɛ bɔna la mam botɩ mam yẽ na'am bĩmbĩnne n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ nẽra zẽa a zuo.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Sɛka n zĩ bĩmbĩnne na zuo la nẽegɛ yẽgra wʋ kuga n tarɩ yõorɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de zaspɩ, la sardʋanɩ. La gõmatɩ-tãpɔ n yẽgrɩ ãna wʋ kugre yʋ'ʋrɛ n de emerɔdɩ n kaagɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne na.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: La na'am bĩmbĩna pisyi la anaasɩ n daan gilge na'am bĩmbĩn-ẽŋa, tɩ kẽma pisyi la anaasɩ n yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ vuge sãlma mũnsi zẽa ba zuto.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Tɩ saaga ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnnum na yẽgra, tãsra dee sũnna, la fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ n lasʋm dita na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam. Bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ la.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam, bõnɔ n gãɩ ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ yẽgra wʋ bɩsga. La bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ n gilge na'am bĩmbĩnne na n boe tẽŋasʋka la, tɩ nini pɩrɛ ba nẽŋa, la poore.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Pɔspɔsɩ bõn-vɔa la wõn na gɩgnɛ, tɩ bʋyi dãana la wõna lʋlla, tɩ bʋtã dãana la tara nẽŋa n wõn na nẽrsaala nẽŋa. La bõn-vɔa bʋnaasɩ dãana la wõn na tugfo n ẽgrɩ.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ayɩla woo tarɩ la kempagsɩ bayoobɩ tɩ nini pɩrɛ ba zẽ'a woo, tilum na zuum. Yʋ'ʋŋɔ la wʋntɛɛŋa, ba yõonɩ mɛ ka gɔ'ɔra yetɩ: «Sõŋa, sõŋa, sõŋa, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana n ãn sõŋa. Ẽŋa n daan boe, n ken bɔna, dee wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu.».

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ẽn nã'asra sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo n vɔa wakatɛ wuu la mɛ, pẽgra ẽ dee pʋ'ʋsra ẽ a yel-sõnnɛ.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: Ba sãn ẽn ẽŋɛ bɛla, kẽma pisyi la anaasɩ la ẽn ka la dũma sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo n vɔa wakatɛ wuu la nẽŋam nã'asra ẽ, dee vugse ba mũnsi bĩŋe na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam, dee yɛta tɩ:.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: «Tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, fõn masɛ fʋ to'oge na'am pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ, la ke'eŋo, la de la fõn nãam sɛla woo, la de la fõn boorɩ ĩyã, tɩ fõn nãam ba tɩ ba bɔna.».

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: La mam yẽ mɛ tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ zẽ'esɩ bayopɔɩ la ayɩla. Tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ayɩla n tɔgrɩ tɩ a kõa ãna wʋ saaga n tãsrɩ la yetɩ: «Wa'am!».

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ wemõa n de pɛɛlga. Tɩ nẽra bã ẽ zãla tãpɔ, tɩ ba bɔ ẽ mũŋa. Tɩ a dɛna nẽra n yãŋrɩ, sẽŋɛ tɩ a yãŋɛ.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋyi dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔa bʋyi dãana n yetɩ: «Wa'am!».

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Tɩ wemõa n de mõlga yese. Tɩ ba bɔ sɛka n bã ẽ na ke'eŋo tɩ a yese dũnia sũ-mã'asʋm tɩ nẽrba kʋʋra taaba, dee bɔ ẽ sʋkɔbgɔ n ãn kãtɛ.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋtã dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔa bʋtã dãana la n yetɩ: «Wa'am!» Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ wemõ-sabga. Tɩ sɛka n bã ẽ na zãla bõn-makrɩga a nu'ugo pʋam.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Tɩ mam wõm dãmnɛ n ãn wʋ kõa n ze'ele bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la tẽŋasʋka yetɩ: «Si ligri wʋn zo'oge mɛ, tɩ nẽra daarɛ ayɩla yɔɔrɔ wʋn da si wʋlla ayɩla, dee tɩ kamãana wʋlsɩ batã dɛna nẽra daarɛ ayɩla yɔɔrɔ. La, la sãn dɛna kaam la dãam, da pa'asɛ sɛla.».

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋnaasɩ dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔa bʋnaasɩ dãana n yetɩ: «Wa'am!».

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ wemõa n de puka. Sɛka n bã ẽ na yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akũm, tɩ kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa dɔla a poore. Tɩ ba bɔ ba ke'eŋo tɩ ba kʋ dũnia nẽrba banaasɩ woo pʋam nẽra ayɩla. Sʋkɔbgɔ, la kɔm, la bã'asɩ, la we-dũnsi n wʋn kʋ ba.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋnuu dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam yẽ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba daan kʋ ba, ban mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la, dee vɔa wʋ yetɔga la n yele se'em na ĩyã, sɩɩsɩ n boe kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na tilum.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Zuudãana n gãnnɩ wuu n ãn sõŋa, n de sɩra dãana, fõn wʋn yuuge paagɛ la wãne dee di dũnia nẽrba sarɩya sɩbgɛ ba, ban kʋ tõma la ĩyã?».

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ ba nẽra woo fu-pɛɛlga, dee yele ba yetɩ ba gu fẽe, halɩ tɩ ba kʋ ba sɔbiisi n de ba tõntõn-taaba, n de ba kʋ ba wʋ bãma la, tɩ ba sõorɛ paagɛ.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋyoobɩ dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim zozo'e, tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa lebge lika wʋ tãn-sabga, dee tɩ wõrga lebge mõlga wʋ zɩɩm na.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: Tɩ wõrbiɛ ze'ele saazuum sʋʋgɛ tẽŋa, wʋ kẽnkãŋa tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ mĩim tɩ kẽnkãma sʋʋgɛ la.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Tɩ sagõŋɔ kilim wʋ sõŋɔ la bɔɩ. Tɩ tãnsɩ wuu, la tẽn-sɛba tɩ ko'om gilge la wuu ka le bɔna ba zẽ'esɩ.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Tɩ dũnia na'adõma, la nẽr-kegro, la sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma, la bõntatba, la pãnsɩ dõma, la yamsɩ, la burkĩndõma wuu sʋgra tãnsɩ bogro pʋam, la tãmpĩisi lɛma.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Dee yɛta tãnsɩ, la tãmpĩisi la yetɩ: «Lui-ya tɩ zuto sʋgɛ tɔ, tɩ sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la da yẽta tɔ, tɩ Pebila la sũ-isgre da paagɛ tɔ.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba sũ-isgre daarɛ paagɛ mɛ, nẽra ka boe n wʋn yãŋɛ põsɛ.».

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Tɩ ba daan bɔ mam mẽelŋɔ n ãn wʋ dibga tɩ ba tarɩ makra, dee yele mam yetɩ: «Isge makɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, la kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na, dee kãalɛ nẽr-sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ a pʋam na.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: La basɛ a zẽnzaka la, da makɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, ba dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ la bu-zãŋsɩ la, ba wʋn nẽ Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa la mɛ wõrsɩ pinaasɩ la ayi sã'am.».

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: «Mam wʋn tõm na nẽrba bayi n wʋn ze'ele mam poorʋm tɔgɛ mam yelle. Ba wʋn yɛ la bɔra futo to'ose mam yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba dabsa tʋsrɛ la kɔbsyi la pisyoobɩ.».

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Nẽr-bãma bayi de la Oliivi tɩɩsɩ bayi, dee ken dɛna fɩtladõma bayi n ze dũnia wuu Zuudãana nẽŋam na.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Nẽra sãn ɛɛra tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba be'em, bugum n ze'etɩ ba nõa pʋam dita ba bɛ'ɛba la. Asɩra, nẽr-sɛka n eerɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba be'em, dẽnɩ tɩ a ki bɛla.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Ba tarɩ la ke'eŋo wʋn yõkɛ saaga tɩ a ka niira wakat-sɛba wuu tɩ ba to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la. La ba ken tara ke'eŋo mɛ wʋn botɩ ko'om lebge zɩɩm, dee ken tara ke'eŋo wʋn botɩ tooro buuri wuu paagɛ dũnia nẽrba wakat-sɛka wuu tɩ ba boorɩ.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Ba sãn wa tɔgɛ yetɔga la ba'asɛ, dũŋa n wʋn ze'ele bog-ko'ogo la pʋam yese zɛbɛ la ba, yãŋɛ kʋ ba.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Tɩ ba kũm na wʋn gã peelem nẽŋam tẽn-kãtɛ la pʋam. Bilam tɩ ba daan ka ba Zuudãana dɔ-puurŋa zuo. Tẽŋa la yʋ'ʋrɛ n pa'alɛ ẽn ãn se'em de la Sodom, la Ezipti.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la buuri wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn bɩsra ba kũm na la dabsa atã la pʋsʋka, dee kãn sakɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba kũm na laagɛ.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Dũnia nẽrba sũure wʋn ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ, ban yẽ tɩ nẽrba bayi la ki la ĩyã. Tɩ ba wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga de'em, bɔ taaba bɔ'a, se'ere n soe la, nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba bayi la nãmsɛ nẽrba dũnia mɛ.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: La dabsa atã la pʋsʋka la poorʋm, vo'osʋm n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n wʋn ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am wa kẽ ba pʋam, tɩ ba vo'oge isge. Tɩ dabeem zozo'e wʋn yõkɛ sɛba n yẽ ba la.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: La nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba bayi la wʋn wõm na kõa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yele ba yetɩ: «Zom-ya wa'am.» Tɩ ba kẽ sawara pʋam zɔnna wɛ'ɛsa Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ bɛ'ɛba la bɩsra ba.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Wakatɛ ẽŋa mẽŋa, tẽŋa n wʋn mĩim halɩ zozo'e. Tɩ Zerizalɛm tẽŋa la zẽ'esɩ pia pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla wʋn lui saam. Tɩ nẽrba tʋsa ayopɔɩ ki, tẽŋa la n mĩim na ĩyã. Tɩ dabeem wʋn yõkɛ nẽr-sɛba n weege la, tɩ ba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nã'asgɔ.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Toogo bʋyi dãana tole mɛ. La toogo bʋtã dãana la dee la fẽe la a wa'am.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyopɔɩ dãana peebe benkonne, tɩ kõosɩ ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Dũnia so'olʋm lebge la tõma Zuudãana, la Krisi tɩ a tõm na n soe, la a na'am na boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.».

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Tɩ kẽma pisyi la anaasɩ la n zĩ ba na'am bĩmbĩna zuto Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam na, võglɛ nẽnsɩ tẽŋa nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, n daan boe, n ken bɔna la, tõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la fõn fʋ yel-sõnnɛ, fõn dɩkɛ fʋ ke'eŋo n zo'oge vigle fʋ na'am na ĩyã.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Bu-zãŋsɩ wuu nẽrba sũure isge mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã fõn sũ-yĩire wakatɛ n paagɛ, tɩ fõn wʋn di sɛba n ki la sarɩya, la fõn wʋn yɔ fʋ tõntõnɩba la n de fʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la fʋ nẽrba la n nã'asrɩ fõn, kɛ'ɛndõma la tarɩmdõma wuu mɛ. La de la wakatɛ tɩ fõn wʋn sã'am nẽr-sɛba n sã'anɩ dũnia la.».

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la yo'oge, tɩ nõor-bĩŋre daka la yẽta. Tɩ saaga yãgra, sũnna, tãsra, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim, tɩ sakuga zozo'e lui.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam yẽ la malɛkadõma banaasɩ n ze dũnia sakʋgrɔ banaasɩ. Ba daan yõgɛ la dũnia kʋsebgo n ze'ele zẽ'esɩ banaasɩ la wuu, tɩ ba da vugra wẽ'erɩ tẽŋa, bɩɩ ko-kãtɛ, bɩɩ tɩa.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: La mam yẽ la malɛka ayẽma n ze'ele wẽntulle n doorɩ zẽ'a la zɔnna, a zãlɩ la vɔm dãana Nawẽnnɛ dãalgɔ bõnɔ. La a tɔgɛ la ke'enke'em yele malɛkadõma banaasɩ la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba nõorɛ tɩ ba sã'am tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ la yetɩ:.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: «Nãn da sã'am-ya tẽŋa, bɩɩ ko-kãtɛ, bɩɩ tɩɩsɩ, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba la dĩa dãalgɔ, dee tɩ ya sã'am.».

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Tɩ mam wõm sɛba tɩ ba ẽŋɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ dãalgɔ la sõorɛ. Ba de la nẽrba tʋskɔbga la tʋspinaasɩ la anaasɩ . Ba ze'ele la Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la wuu pʋam.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: Azuda buuri nẽrba tɩ ba dãalɛ de la tʋspia la ayi, Arubã buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, Agadɩ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: Azɛɛrɩ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, Aneftali buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, Amanase buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: Asimeyõ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, Alevi buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, A'isakaarɩ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: Azabilõ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, Azozɛfʋ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi, Abẽnzamẽ buuri nẽrba tʋspia la ayi.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Bɛla poorʋm mam bɩsrɩ mɛ, yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e tɩ nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn kãalɛ. Ba de la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba, la buuri wuu nẽrba, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba. Ba daan yɛ la fu-pɛɛlsɩ zãla karkɔŋa võorɔ ba nu'usum zɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne, la Pebila la nẽŋam.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Ba tɔgrɩ la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Fãarɛ ze'etɩ la tõma Nawẽnnɛ n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo, la Pebila la zẽ'am.».

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Malɛkadõma la wuu daan ze gilge la na'am bĩmbĩnne, la kẽma, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la. Ba daan ẽn ka la dũma dɩkɛ ba nẽnsɩ võglɛ tẽŋa na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: yetɩ: «Asɩra, Nawẽnnɛ n soe nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa, la yɛm, la yel-sõnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la pẽka, la ke'eŋo, la pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.».

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Tɩ kẽma la ayɩla soke mam yetɩ: «Ãndõma n de nẽr-bãma n yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ la, ba ze'ele la bɛ?».

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Tɩ mam lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M zuudãana, fõn mi.» Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «Ba de la nẽr-sɛba n ze'ele toog-kãtɛ pʋam na. Ba peege ba futo mɛ Pebila la zɩɩm pʋam tɩ ba pelge.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Bɛla n soe tɩ ba zɛ Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam, tõnna bɔ'ɔra ẽ wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ a yire la pʋam. Sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la n wʋn gu'ura ba.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Kɔm la koyũuro kãn le yõkɛ ba, wʋntɛɛŋa la a tʋʋlgɔ kãn le wõŋɛ ba.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Pebila la n boe na'am bĩmbĩnne tẽŋasʋka la n wʋn bɩsra ba yelle, ẽŋa n wʋn tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ ko'om n bulli n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm zẽ'am. La Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn duuse ba nintãm wuu.».

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Pebila la lakɛ la bõnɔ bayopɔɩ dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ la ãna yɩrɩrɩr Nawẽnnɛ yire wakatɛ fẽe paagɛ karf-ayɩla pʋsʋka.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ n ze Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, tɩ ba bɔ ba benkɔma bayopɔɩ.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: La malɛka ayẽma n wa'am wa ze'ele kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am. A daan tarɩ la sãlma laaga tɩ ba tarɩ yõ'ora tɩdarɛ. Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ tɩdarɛ pãɩ, tɩ a dɩkɛ ba lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu pʋ'ʋsgɔ yõ sãlma kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne n boe na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam na zuo bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Tɩ tɩdarɛ la tɩ a yõ la bug-zũ'usi lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la pʋ'ʋsgɔ, ze'ele malɛka la n ze Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam na nu'usum zɔnna saazuum.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Tɩ malɛka la dɩkɛ laaga la tɩ ba tarɩ yõ'ora tɩdarɛ la, pɩrɛ ẽ na tɩdarɛ bugum na n boe kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zuo la lobge ba tẽŋa zuo. Tɩ saaga tãsra, yãgra dee sũnna, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n zãlɩ benkɔma bayopɔɩ la, mãasʋm mɛ tɩ ba peebe ba.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Tɩ pɔspɔsɩ malɛka la peebe benkonne. Tɩ sakuga la bugum n gaam na zɩɩm ka'agɛ dũnia zuo. Tɩ dũnia zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, bugum di zẽ'a ayɩla, tɩ tɩɩsɩ zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, bugum di zẽ'a ayɩla, tɩ mõ-mã'asa wuu di bugum.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyi dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ ba lobge sɛla ko-kãtɛ pʋam n ãn wʋ tãn-kãtɛ tɩ bugum diti. Tɩ ko'om na zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla lebge zɩɩm.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: Tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ sɛba n boe ko'om pʋam na batã pʋam woo ayɩla ki. Tɩ õornɔ batã pʋam woo ayɩla sã'am.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋtã dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ wõrbi-kãtɛ n diti bug-zɩlɩŋa ze'ele saazuum lui mõga, la kʋlsɩ ko'om pʋam, a lui la ko'om na zẽ'esɩ batã ayɩla pʋam.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: Wõrbiire la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azẽlõm. Tɩ ko'om na zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla lebge zẽlõm. La nẽrba zozo'e n yũ ko'om na ki, ko'om na n de zẽlõm na ĩyã.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋnaasɩ dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ wẽntulle zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla, la wõrga zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla, la wõrbiɛ zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla pãŋa ba'asɛ, tɩ ba wuu zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla lebge lika. Tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam ayɩla ka le nẽera peelem, tɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ mẽ bɛla.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ sɩl-kãtɛ n ẽgrɩ saazuum-zuum, wõm ẽn tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Nẽr-sɛba n boe tẽŋa wã zuo wʋn yẽ la toogo, malɛkadõma batã n weege la n wʋn peebe ba benkɔma, tɩ ba yẽ toogo.».

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋnuu dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ mam yẽ wõrbiire n ze'ele saazuum lui dũnia zuo. Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ bog-ko'ogo saafɩbiire.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Tɩ wõrbiire la pakɛ bog-ko'ogo la, tɩ bug-zũ'usi ze'ele bog-ko'ogo la pʋam yɛsra wʋ bug-kãtɛ zũ'usi la, botɩ wʋntɛɛŋa, la sapebsʋm sobe.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Tɩ tẽnta'aba ze'ele zũ'usi la pʋam yese saagɛ dũnia zuo. Tɩ ba bɔ ba toogo wʋ nãnsɩ toogo la.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: La ba yele ba yetɩ ba da ẽŋɛ mõorɔ, la bõn-sɛba wuu n yesrɩ tẽŋa, la tɩɩsɩ sɛla, ba pɔglʋm nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ dãalgɔ ba dĩa zuto la mã'a.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Ba daan ka bɔ tẽnta'aba la nõorɛ tɩ ba kʋ nẽrba la, la de la ba nãmsɛ ba wõr-sɩnuu. Ban botɩ toog-sɛka nãmsra nẽrba la ãn wʋ nãŋa n ẽn dõn nẽra tɩ la dõnna se'em na.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: La dabsa bãma, nẽrsaalba wʋn ɛɛra kũm, la ba kãn yẽ ẽ. Ba wʋn bɔɔra tɩ ba ki, la kũm wʋn zoe dee basɛ ba.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Tẽnta'aba la ãn wʋ wiiri n mãasʋm gu'ura zɛbrɛ, la sɛla n boe ba zuto ãna wʋ sãlma mũnsi, dee tɩ ba nẽnsɩ ãna wʋ nẽrba nẽnsɩ la.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: Ba tarɩ la zomto n ãn wʋ pɔgsɩ zomto la. Dee tɩ ba yẽna ãna wʋ gɩgma yẽna la.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Ba dɩkɛ la bõnɔ n ãn wʋ kuto futo pĩ ba yõ'orɔ. La ba kempagsɩ la dãmnɛ ãn wʋ wiiri toto tɩ wiiri zozo'e ve'erɩ zɔta wɛ'ɛsa zɛbrɛ zẽ'a.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Ba tarɩ la zɔa wʋ nãnsɩ zɔa la dõnna. Ba zɔa bãma n bɔ ba ke'eŋo tɩ ba nãmsɛ nẽrba wõr-sɩnuu.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Ba daan tarɩ la naba, a de la malɛk-sɛka n gu'uri bog-ko'ogo la. A yʋ'ʋrɛ la Ebre yetɔga de la «Abadõ», la Grɛkɩ yetɔga de la «Apoliyõ», n boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ «Sã'ama».

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Pɔspɔsɩ toogo la tole mɛ. La toogo bʋyi n wʋn ken wa'am.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyoobɩ dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele sãlma kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne n boe Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam kʋgra banaasɩ la zẽ'am.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Kõa la yele malɛka bʋyoobɩ dãana n zãlɩ benkonne na yetɩ: «Lorge malɛkadõma banaasɩ tɩ ba lʋ ba, tɩ ba bɔna Efratɩ kʋl-kãtɛ zẽ'am na basɛ.».

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Tɩ a lorge malɛkadõma banaasɩ la basɛ. Ba daan mãasʋm malɛg-bãma gu'ura la wakatɛ ẽŋa, la dabsrɛ ẽŋa, la wõr-ẽŋa, la yʋʋn-ẽŋa, tɩ ba kʋ nẽrsaalba batã woo pʋam ayɩla.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Tɩ ba pa'alɛ mam ba sʋdaasɩ n bã'arɩ wiiri kãllɛ, ba de la tʋsa kɔbsyi nõorɛ tʋsrɛ.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ wiiri la, la ba wi-bã'arba n ãn se'em: Ba tarɩ la kuto futo n pĩ'iri yõ'orɔ, tɩ ba mõ'ogɛ ãna wʋ bugum, dee dɛna yõosnɛ, la dõbʋʋlʋm. Wiiri la zuto ãn wʋ gɩgma zuto la. Bugum, la bug-zũ'usi, la kidibre n ze'ele ba nõa pʋam yɛsra.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Tɩ tooro bãma batã n de bugum, la bug-zũ'usi, la kidibre n ze'ele wiiri la nõa pʋam yɛsra la, kʋ nẽrsaalba batã pʋam woo ayɩla.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Wiiri la ke'eŋo boe la ba nõa, la ba zɔa pʋam. Ba zɔa la ãn wʋ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma la, ba tarɩ la zuto bãma tɩ ba tarɩ ɩta nẽrba be'em sã'ana ba.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n weege tɩ tooro bãma ka kʋ ba la, ka teege yɛm basɛ ban ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ ba mẽŋa boorɩ la. Ba ken dɔla la kʋlkã'arsɩ, la baga tɩ ba dɩkɛ sãlma, la zũuro, la yõgõm, la kuga, la dɔɔrɔ mãalɛ, tɩ ba ka yẽta, ka wõnna, ka tãna sẽnna.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Ba ken ka teege yɛm basɛ ba nẽr-kʋa, la ba tɩɩm tõoma, la ba yalsɩ tõoma, la ba nayigum na.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Tɩ mam daan yẽ malɛka ayẽma n tarɩ pãŋa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra. Tɩ sawara pile ẽ wʋ fuugo tɩ a yɛ, dee tɩ gõmatɩ-tãpɔ kɔkɛ a zuugo. A nẽŋa ãn wʋ wẽntulle, dee tɩ a nãma ãna wʋ bug-zɩlɩsɩ la.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: A zãlɩ la gõn-pɩka n lakɛ a nu'usum. A dɩkɛ a zʋʋgɔ nã'arɛ taglɛ la ko-kãtɛ zuo, dee dɩkɛ a gɔbga nã'arɛ taglɛ tẽŋa zuo.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: A daan tɔgɛ mɛ ke'enke'em wʋ gɩgnɛ n kaasɛ la. Ẽn daan tɔgɛ la, saaga n tãsɛ nõorɛ bʋyopɔɩ.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Saaga la n tãsɛ tɔgɛ la, mam yetɩ m gʋlsɛ la ban yele se'em na. La mam wõm na kõa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ mam yetɩ: «Sʋgɛ sɛla tɩ saaga tãsɛ bʋyopɔɩ tɔgɛ la, da gʋlsɛ ba.».

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Tɩ malɛk-sɛka tɩ mam daan yẽ tɩ a ze ko-kãtɛ la tẽŋa zuo la, n zẽkɛ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo saazuum.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ n boe wakatɛ wuu, n nãam saazuum, la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la sɛla wuu n boe ba pʋam na, yetɩ: «La kãn le yuuge.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Malɛka bʋyopɔɩ dãana la sãn peebe benkonne, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽn looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em n sʋgɛ la mɛ, wʋ ẽn yʋʋn tɔgɛ la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n de a tõntõnɩba se'em na.».

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Tɩ kõa la n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ tɩ mam wõm na, le yele mam yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ ka to'oge gõn-pɩka la n lakɛ bɔna malɛka la n ze ko-kãtɛ la tẽŋa zuo la nu'um.».

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Tɩ mam sẽŋɛ malɛka la zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɔ mam gõn-pɩka la.» Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «To'oge ẽ obe vole. A wʋn lebge la toogo fʋ pʋʋrʋm. La fʋ nõorʋm, a wʋn ãna yẽlʋm wʋ seero la.».

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Tɩ mam to'oge gõn-pɩka la a nu'um obe vole. Tɩ a ãna yẽlʋm wʋ seero mam nõorʋm. La mam n vole ẽ na, a lebge la toogo mam pʋʋrʋm.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Tɩ ba yele mam yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ le tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga pa'alɛ la wʋn ẽŋɛ tẽnsɩ zozo'e nẽrba, la so'olʋm zozo'e nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri zozo'e nẽrba, la na'adõma zozo'e se'em.».

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam yẽ la dũŋa n ze'ele ko-kãtɛ pʋam dooge. A tarɩ la ɩɩla pia, la zuto bayopɔɩ, tɩ mũnsi pia vuge a ɩɩla la wuu. La yʋ'ʋra n pɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a zuto la zuo.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Dũŋa la tɩ mam yẽ la wõn na webaaga. Tɩ a nã'a-tala ãna kãra, tara nã'a-ẽesɩ wʋ tẽŋambõŋa la. Dee tɩ a nõorɛ ãna wʋ gɩgnɛ nõorɛ la. Tɩ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la dɩkɛ a pãŋa, la a na'am bĩmbĩnne, la a ke'eŋo bɔ dũŋa la.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Dũŋa la zuugo ayɩla daan pɔglʋm mɛ, ãna wʋ a ki mɛ na, la a pɔglʋŋɔ la daan bɔɩ mɛ. Tɩ la di'ige dũnia nẽrba wuu, tɩ ba dɔla dũŋa la.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Tɩ nẽrba wuu nã'asra pʋryõŋ-yãlma la, ẽn bɔ dũŋa la a ke'eŋo la ĩyã. Dee nã'asra dũŋa la yetɩ: «Ãne n tarɩ ke'eŋo wʋ dũŋa la? La ãne n tã wʋn zɛbɛ la ẽ lɛ?».

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ dũŋa la nõorɛ tɩ a tɔgɛ tɩta'alʋm yetɔga, dee tʋ Nawẽnnɛ. A to'oge ke'eŋo tɩ a tõm na wõrsɩ pinaasɩ la ayi.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Tɩ a pɔsɛ tɔgra pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ dee pɔ'ɔra a yʋ'ʋrɛ, la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a boe, la sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la wuu.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ nõorɛ tɩ a zɛbɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba yãŋɛ ba. Ba bɔ ẽ ke'eŋo tɩ a yãŋɛ la buuri wuu nẽrba, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Nẽrba wuu n boe dũnia zuo wʋn nã'asɛ dũŋa la mɛ, sãn dagna sɛba yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ Pebila tɩ ba daan kʋ la vɔm gõŋɔ pʋam na. Nẽr-bãma yʋ'ʋra daan põn gʋlsɛ vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam na halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n de ba yõkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ, ba wʋn yõkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ. Nẽr-sɛka n de ba kʋ ẽ na sʋ'a, ba wʋn kʋ ẽ na sʋ'a. Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tara sũ-ke'eŋo, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam yẽ la dũŋa ayẽma n ze'ele tẽŋa pʋam yese. A tarɩ la ɩɩla bayi n wõn na pebila ɩɩla, dee tɔgra wʋ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: A tarɩ la pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la ke'eŋo tõnna a nifum. A pẽrgɛ dũnia nẽrba wuu mɛ tɩ ba nã'asra pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la n daan pɔglʋm ãna wʋ a ki mɛ, dee yeege la.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Dũŋa bʋyi dãana la ẽŋɛ la bõn-yãlm-kãra, halɩ botɩ bugum zɛ'ɛta saazuum sigra dũnia, nẽrba nifum.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Bõn-yãlma la tɩ a ẽŋɛ pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la nifum na, botɩ a pã'asɛ dũnia nẽrba mɛ. A yele ba tɩ ba mãalɛ bõnɔ tɩ a ãna wʋ dũŋa la tɩ ba pɔglʋm na sʋ'a tɩ a vɔa la.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ dũŋa bʋyi dãana la ke'eŋo tɩ a bɔ bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ tɩ a ãna wʋ pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la vo'osʋm, tɩ a tãna tɔgra, dee kʋ sɛba wuu n ka nã'asrɩ ẽ na.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: La dũŋa la pẽrgɛ nẽrba wuu mɛ, tarɩmdõma la kɛ'ɛndõma, bõntatba la nasdõma, yamsɩ la burkĩndõma tɩ ba dãalɛ ba zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo bɩɩ ba dĩire.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka tãna wʋn da bɩɩ koose sɛla, a sãn ka tara dũŋa la dãalgɔ, a yʋ'ʋrɛ, bɩɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ la kãllɛ.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ tara yɛm: Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ, ẽŋa n wʋn tã'agɛ bãŋɛ dũŋa la kãllɛ võorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, kãllɛ la võorɛ de la nẽrsaala kãllɛ. Kãllɛ la biɛ de la .

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Bõn-bãn-kãtɛ n puke saazuum. Pɔka n dɩkɛ wẽntulle pile wʋ fuugo la, tɩ wõrga bɔna a nãma tẽŋa, dee tɩ a vuge mũŋa n tarɩ wõrbiɛ pia la ayi.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Pɔka la tarɩ la pʋʋrɛ virgra tɩ a dɔgɛ, wʋlsra kaasra, dɔka toogo n tarɩ ẽ na ĩyã.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Tɩ bõn-bãnnɛ ayẽma puke saazuum: A de la pʋryõŋ-yãlma n ãn kãtɛ dɛna mõlga, a tarɩ la zuto bayopɔɩ, la ɩɩla pia, tɩ mũnsi vuge a zuto la wuu.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: Tɩ a zʋʋrɛ kã'asɛ wõrbiɛ batã pʋam ayɩla saazuum lobe basɛ tẽŋa zuo. Pʋryõŋ-yãlma la ze la pɔka la n yetɩ a dɔgɛ la nẽŋam gu'ura tɩ pɔka la dɔgɛ tɩ a yõkɛ a bia la obe.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: La pɔka la dɔgɛ la budibla n wʋn tara kuto dasaarɛ sɔna so'olʋm buuri wuu. Tɩ ba zɛ bia la tarɩ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Tɩ pɔka la zoe sẽŋɛ weem, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm a ĩyã la, bilam tɩ ba diise ẽ dabsa tʋsrɛ la kɔbsyi la pisyoobɩ.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Tɩ zɛbrɛ isge Nawẽnnɛ yire. Tɩ malɛkadõma kɛ'ɛma Amisɛlɩ, la a malɛkadõma zɛbra la pʋryõŋ-yãlma la, tɩ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la, la a malɛgsɩ mẽ zɛbra la ba.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: La ba koŋe pãŋa mɛ, tɩ sore ka le bɔna tɩ ba bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Tɩ ba daan lobe pʋryõŋ-yãlma kãtɛ la basɛ. Ẽŋa n de bõnsɛla n yʋʋn boe, tɩ ba wi'ira ẽ tɩ «Dõta» bɩɩ «Asũtãana» n pã'asrɩ dũnia nẽrba wuu la. Ba lobe ẽ, la a malɛgsɩ basɛ la tẽŋa zuo.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Nãnannɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ fãarɛ daarɛ paagɛ mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yãŋa pa'alɛ a pãŋa, la a na'am mɛ, dee pa'alɛ Krisi tɩ a tõm na ke'eŋo, se'ere n soe la, ba dige sɛka n wã'arɩ tɩ sɔbiisi Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ la mɛ.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: La de la Pebila la zɩɩm ĩyã, la ban mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la ĩyã, tɩ tɩ sɔbiisi la yãŋɛ ẽ. Ba ka zoe tɩ ba wʋn kʋ ba.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ yire, la yãma sɛba wuu n boe mĩ na, ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga. La toogo n wʋn yẽ tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, se'ere n soe la, Asũtãana sige ya zẽ'am la sũ-yĩire, ẽn mi tɩ a wakatɛ deege la fẽe la ĩyã.».

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Pʋryõŋ-yãlma la n yẽ tɩ ba lobe ẽ basɛ la tẽŋa zuo la, a babsɛ la pɔka la n dɔgɛ bia la.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ pɔka la sɩl-kãtɛ kempagsɩ bayi tɩ a ẽkɛ sẽŋɛ weem, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn diisra ẽ yʋʋma atã la pʋsʋka la, zãagɛ la bõnsɛla la.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Tɩ bõnsɛla la ʋkɛ ko'om a nõorʋm wʋ mõgrɛ la pɔka la poorʋm tɩ ba pe'ege ẽ fõrgɛ.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: La tẽŋa sõŋɛ pɔka la mɛ, ya'agɛ nõorɛ fõogɛ ko'om na n ze'ele pʋryõŋ-yãlma la nõorʋm na.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Tɩ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la sũure yĩige la pɔka la, tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka zɛbra la pɔka la yɩɩsɩ sɛba n weege ken sakra Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, dee tɔgra Azezi yelle bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Pʋryõŋ-yãlma la sẽŋɛ ka zɛ la ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Tɩ mam le yẽ bõn-bãnnɛ ayẽma saazuum n de yel-kãtɛ n ẽgrɩ palẽŋa. La de la malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ n zãlɩ tooro bayopɔɩ n de ba'asgɔ tooro. Se'ere n soe la, bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-yĩire la ba'asɛ.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ sɛla n ãn wʋ ko-kãtɛ n yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga n gaam na bugum. La mam yẽ la nẽr-sɛba n zɛbɛ yãŋɛ dũŋa, la a bõnɔ tɩ ba mãalɛ, dee ka sakɛ to'oge a yʋ'ʋrɛ la kãllɛ dãalgɔ la, n ze ko-kãtɛ n yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga la nõorʋm. Ba zãlɩ la kõnnɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: Ba yõonɩ na Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnna Amoyisi yõonɛ, la Pebila la yõonɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, fõn tõoma de la tõon-kãra, la tõon-yãlma. So'olʋm buuri wuu Naba, fõn yɛla la masɛ mɛ, la ba de la sɩra.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: Zuudãana, ãne n kãn zoe fõn, nã'asɛ fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ la? Se'ere n soe la, fõn mã'a n de pʋpeelem dãana. So'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn wa'am wa ka dũma nã'asɛ fɔ, se'ere n soe la, fõn tõoma n masɛ la puke mɛ tɩ ba yẽ.».

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Bɛla poorʋm mam bɩsɛ mɛ, yẽ tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la pʋam deego la yo'oge mɛ.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: Tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n zãlɩ tooro bayopɔɩ la ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam yese, ba yɛ la fu-pɛɛlsɩ n yẽgrɩ, dee dɩkɛ sãlma sẽbrɩsɩ bobe ba yõ'orɔ.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ayɩla bɔ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la sãlma laasɩ bayopɔɩ n pɩrɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa wakatɛ wuu la sũ-isgre.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pɩrɛ la bug-zũ'usi n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa, la a ke'eŋo. Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka tãna wʋn kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam, sãn dagna tɩ tooro bayopɔɩ la tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la zãlɩ la n ba'asɛ.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Tɩ mam le bɩsɛ, yẽ Pebila la n ze Siyõ tãŋa zuo. Tɩ nẽrba tʋskɔbga la tʋspinaasɩ la anaasɩ zɛ a zẽ'am, ba tarɩ la Pebila la yʋ'ʋrɛ, la a Sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ ba dĩa zuto.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɩ a dãmnɛ ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ dãmnɛ, dee ãna wʋ saaga n tãsrɩ sũnna, dee ãna wʋ kõl-wẽ'erba n wẽ'erɩ ba kõnnɔ.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ bãma ze la na'am bĩmbĩnne, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ, la kẽma la nẽŋam yõona yõom-paalɛ. Nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn zãsʋm yõonɛ na, sãn dagna nẽrba tʋskɔbga la tʋspinaasɩ la anaasɩ la n yẽ fãarɛ dũnia zuo la mã'a.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: Nẽr-bãma ka dẽgɛ ba mẽŋa la pɔgsɩ, ba tarɩ ba mẽŋa sõŋa mɛ. Ba dolɩ la Pebila la zẽ'e-sɛka woo tɩ a we'esɩ. Ba yẽ fãarɛ nẽrsaalba pʋam tɩ ba dɛna la pɔspɔsɩ dõma bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la Pebila la.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Põmpɔrŋɔ yetɔga ka tole ze'ele ba nõorʋm, ba ka tarɩ tuure.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛka ayẽma n ẽgrɩ saazuum-zuum, a tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga n boe wakatɛ wuu tɩ a mõolɛ bɔ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe dũnia zuo, so'olʋm wuu nẽrba, la buuri wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: A daan tɔgrɩ mɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Zoe-ya Nawẽnnɛ, la ya nã'asɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ a wʋn di nẽrba sarɩya. Ka-ya dũma nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam saazuum, la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la kʋlsɩ wuu n bulli la.».

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyi dãana dɔla pɔspɔsɩ dãana la poore yɛta tɩ: «A lui mɛ, a lui mɛ, Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la lui sã'am mɛ. Ẽŋa n botɩ so'olʋm wuu nẽrba tõm a yalsɩ tõoma zozo'e wʋ dãam tɩ a yũuse ba la.».

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋtã dãana dɔla malɛkadõma bayi la tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn nã'asɛ dũŋa la, la a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la, la a sãn to'oge a dãalgɔ la a dĩire zuo bɩɩ a nu'ugo zuo.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: Nawẽnnɛ sũure wʋn yĩige la ẽ, tɩ a sɩbgɛ ẽ wʋ dã-ke'eŋo n ka pulum tɩ a pɩrɛ wannɛ bɔ ẽ tɩ a yũ. A wʋn nãmsɛ la kidibre n diti bugum pʋam, malɛg-sõma, la Pebila la nẽŋam.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: Nẽr-bãma nãmsgɔ bug-zũ'usi zonnɩ la saazuum wakatɛ wuu. Nẽr-sɛba wuu n nã'asrɩ dũŋa la, la a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la, dee to'oge a yʋ'ʋrɛ dãalgɔ la, kãn malɩn yẽ vo'osgo wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ.».

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n sakra a nõorɛ dee bɔ Azezi sɩra la, tara sũ-ke'eŋo.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele saazuum yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, nẽr-sɛba n kiiri Zuudãana doosgo pʋam na tarɩ zu-yẽlga.› Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yetɩ: ‹La de la sɩra, ba wʋn vo'ose la ba tõon-tooro, ba tõon-sõma la dolɩ ba mɛ.› ».

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ sawatɛ n de pɛɛlga, tɩ nẽra n wõn na nẽrsaala dayʋa zɔa a zuo. A vuge la sãlma mũŋa, dee tarɩ geego n saasɛ tara nõorɛ a nu'um.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Tɩ malɛka ayẽma ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam yese tɔgɛ la sɛka n zo sawatɛ zuo la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ geego la sɛɛra, tɩ lagsgɔ wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, tẽŋa zuo bõnkɔɔla bɩ mɛ seke lagsgɔ.».

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Tɩ sɛka n zĩ sawatɛ zuo la dɩkɛ a geego la doose tẽŋa zuo, sɛ tẽŋa bõnkɔɔla lagsɛ.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Tɩ malɛka ayẽma ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ n boe saazuum na pʋam yese, ẽŋa mẽ tarɩ la geego n saasɛ tara nõorɛ.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Tɩ malɛka ayẽma n tarɩ ke'eŋo sɔna bugum ze'ele kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'a yese tɔgɛ ke'enke'em la malɛk-sɛka n tarɩ geego n tarɩ nõorɛ la yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ fʋ geego la sɛ viinye tɩɩsɩ la biɛ n boe tẽŋa zuo la. Se'ere n soe la, ba biɛ la bɩ mɛ.».

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Tɩ malɛka la dɩkɛ a geego doose tẽŋa zuo sɛ viinye tɩɩsɩ la biɛ, dɩkɛ ba lobge bõn-kãtɛ tɩ ba wẽkrɩ viinye biɛ a pʋam n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-yĩire la pʋam.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Tɩ ba wẽkɛ biɛ la wẽkrɛ bõnɔ na pʋam tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa. Tɩ zɩɩm ze'ele wẽkrɛ bõnɔ na pʋam yese pɩrɛ tẽŋa zoe paagɛ kilomẽtrɩ kɔbstã. Tɩ wemõa sãn sige ba pʋam, la de ko'ogo paara la a garẽŋrɛ.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam le yẽ la malɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra. A daan tarɩ la ke'eŋo zozo'e, tɩ a na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ dũnia wuu.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: A tɔgɛ la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «A lui mɛ, a lui mɛ! Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la lui mɛ. A lebge la kʋlkã'arsɩ kẽ'a zẽ'a, a de la kʋlkã'ar-bɛ'ɛsɩ, la bõn-ẽgla n de bõn-dẽgrɔ tɩ nẽrba sisri sʋka zẽ'a.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: So'olʋm buuri wuu tõm na a yalsɩ tõoma wʋ dãam tɩ ba yũ la. Tɩ dũnia na'adõma lagɩm na ẽ tõm yalsɩ tõoma. Tɩ dũnia leebdõma doose a nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ n zo'oge la poorʋm yẽ bõntarsõm, bɛla tɩ a sã'am.».

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa ayẽma n ze'ele saazuum yetɩ: «M nẽrba, yese-ya a pʋam fõrgɛ, tɩ ya da kãn lagɩm na ẽ tõm a tõon-be'ero la, tɩ ya da lagɩm na ẽ yẽ tooro sɛba tɩ a wʋn yẽ la.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Se'ere n soe la, a tõon-be'ero la zo'oge mɛ halɩ paagɛ saazuum. Nawẽnnɛ tẽrɩ a tõon-be'ero la wuu yelle mɛ.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Ẽŋɛ-ya ẽ wʋ ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ basɛba se'em na, yɔ-ya ẽ ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em na nõorɛ ayi. Pɩrɛ-ya a wannɛ na la dã-ke'eŋo nõorɛ ayi bɔ ẽ, wʋ ẽn botɩ nẽrba basɛba yũ se'em na.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Nãmsɛ-ya ẽ tɩ a yẽ toogo la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, wʋ ẽŋa n nã'asrɩ a mẽŋa la a nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ se'em na: A yetɩ a mẽŋa tɩ: ‹Mam zĩ dɛna la pɔg-naba, mam dagɩ pɔkõorɛ, la mam kãn yẽ nãmsgɔ, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ.›.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: Bɛla ĩyã tooro wʋn wa'am a zuo la daarɛ ayɩla pʋam: La de la bã'asɩ n kʋʋrɩ, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la kɔm, la bugum n wʋn di ẽ, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n di a sarɩya la de la ke'eŋo dãana.».

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: Dũnia na'adõma n tõnnɩ yalsɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ la tẽn-ẽŋa lɛ, dee yẽta nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ a zẽ'am na, sãn wa yẽ bugum n laŋɛ tẽŋa la dita tɩ zũ'usi gulla, ba wʋn kɛlla mɛ fabla a yelle.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Ban zotɩ dabeem la a nãmsgɔ la, ba wʋn zɛ la zãazãarɛ dee yɛta yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Tẽn-kãtɛ la, Babilɔnɩ tẽŋa n tarɩ pãŋa la, karf-ayɩla pʋam tɩ fõn yẽ sɩbgrɛ wãna!».

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: Tɩ dũnia leebdõma mẽ wʋn kɛlla fabla a yelle, nẽra nẽra n ka le da'ara ba bõn-kɔɔsla la ĩyã.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: Ba bõn-kɔɔsla la de la sãlma, la zũuro, la kug-sõma buuri ligri n zo'oge, la tãn-sõma, la tãn-mõlsɩ n de gomusga, la tãn-saalsɩ n yẽgrɩ, la dɔɔrɔ buuri wuu n tarɩ yũuŋ-yẽlga, la lɔg-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ wɔbgɔ yẽna mãalɛ, la lɔg-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ dɔɔrɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ mãalɛ, la lɔg-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ yõgõm, la kuto, la sãlm-kuga mãalɛ.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: La tɩɩsɩ pɔgrɔ n tarɩ yũuŋo, la bõnɔ n ãn wʋ kakadule, la tɩdarɛ kaam, la miiri, la tɩdarɛ tɩ ba yõ'orɩ, la dãam, la kaam, la zom, la bõnkɔɔla, la niigi, la piisi, la wiiri, la toto, la halɩ baa nẽrsaalba.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Ba wʋn yele tẽŋa la yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ fʋ yɛm boorɩ la fõrgɛ dee basɛ fʋ mɛ. Fʋ bõntarsõm, la fʋ nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ la tole mɛ dee basɛ fɔ, tɩ nẽra nẽra kãn le malɩn yẽ ba.».

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Leebdõma n leebe lɔg-bãma yẽ bõntarsõm tẽn-ẽŋa pʋam na, wʋn zoe la dabeem la a nãmsgɔ la. Ba wʋn zɛ la zãazãarɛ kaasra dee fabla.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Tẽn-kãtɛ la n daan ãn wʋ pɔka n yɛɛrɩ fu-sõma, la fu-mõlsɩ n de gomusga, dee ɩta nẽnnõm la sãlma lɔgrɔ, la kug-sõma buuri ligri n zo'oge la.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: Karf-ayɩla pʋam tɩ a bõntarsõm na wuu darɛ sã'am.» Õornɔ kɛ'ɛndõma, la nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ õornɔ sẽnna sore, la sɛba n tõnnɩ õornɔ na pʋam, la nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ õornɔ na ɛɛra lɛɛbra la ze la zãazãarɛ.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: la ban yẽtɩ tɩ bugum diti tẽŋa la tɩ zũ'usi gulla la, ba kaasrɩ mɛ yɛta tɩ: «Tẽŋa nãn ka boe n de tẽn-kãtɛ wʋ ẽna.».

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: La ba vaarɩ la tẽntõnnɔ pukra ba zuto, kaasra, dee fabla yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Tẽn-kãtɛ ẽna bõntarsõm ĩyã tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ õornɔ ɛɛra ko-kãtɛ la pʋam lɛɛbra la lebge bõntatba. La karf-ayɩla pʋam tɩ a bõntarsõm na wuu darɛ sã'am.».

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: «Sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga sɛla n paagɛ ẽ na ĩyã. Yãma n welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, a Tõntõnɩba, la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ di tẽŋa la sarɩya sã'am ẽ mɛ, be'em sɛka tɩ a ẽŋɛ ya la ĩyã.».

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Tɩ malɛka n tarɩ pãŋa õbgɛ kug-kãtɛ n ãn wʋ nẽerɛ la lobge ko-kãtɛ la pʋam dee yetɩ: «Ba wʋn lobge Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la basɛ la bɛla la pãŋa, tɩ ba kãn le malɩn yẽ ẽ.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Ba kãn le wõm kõl-wẽ'erba wẽ'a, la yõonyõonɩba yõoma, la wɩɩsɩ, la benkɔma peebgo, la ba kãn le yẽ nẽra n tarɩ nu'ugo tõonɛ a pʋam, la ba kãn wõm nẽerɛ dãmnɛ mĩ.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Fɩtla bugum peelem kãn le nẽegɛ mĩ. Ba kãn le wõm pɔgdire kõa mĩ. Tẽŋa la leebdõma daan de la dũnia nẽrzuto. La tẽŋa la tɩɩm tõoma pã'asɛ so'olʋm buuri wuu nẽrba mɛ.».

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: La de la Babilɔnɩ tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ ba yẽ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba daan kʋ dũnia wuu la zɩɩm.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam tɔgra ke'enke'em la malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka ka'agɛ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre la n pɩrɛ laasɩ bayopɔɩ la basɛ dũnia zuo.».

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Tɩ pɔspɔsɩ malɛka sẽŋɛ ka ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ dũnia zuo. Tɩ nõrtãnkɛ'ɛ-tɔɔsɩ n dõnnɩ ĩyã yõgɛ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ dũŋa la dãalgɔ, dee nã'asra a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyi dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam. Tɩ ko-kãtɛ la lebge wʋ nẽra n ki zɩɩm, tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ sɛba wuu n boe ko-kãtɛ la pʋam ki.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋtã dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ mõga, la kʋlsɩ ko'om pʋam, tɩ ko'om na lebge zɩɩm.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Tɩ mam wõm malɛka n bɩsrɩ ko'om yelle la n yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Fõn de la pʋpeelem dãana, fõn daan boe mɛ, dee ken bɔna. Fõn di sarɩya se'em na masɛ mɛ.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Se'ere n soe la, ba kʋ la sɛba n welge to'ore dɛna fõn nẽrba, la fõn nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɩ ba zɩɩm yese, bɛla tɩ fõn bɔ ba zɩɩm tɩ ba yũ. Bɛla n masɛ la ba.».

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am yetɩ: «Asɩra, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, fõn sarɩyadia la masɛ mɛ, dɛna sɩra.».

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋnaasɩ dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ wẽntulle zuo tɩ a yõ'ogɛ nẽrba la a bugum.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Tɩ tʋʋlg-kãtɛ yõ'ogɛ nẽrba, tɩ ba tʋʋra Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana n tara nõorɛ la tooro bãma la. La ba ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋnuu dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ dũŋa la na'am bĩmbĩnne na zuo. Tɩ dũŋa la so'olʋm wuu lebge lika. Tɩ toogo basɛ tɩ nẽrba ɔbra ba zɩlɩsɩ, ba ĩyã n dõnnɩ ĩyã.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: Nõrtãnkɛ'ɛsɩ la tooro n tarɩ nẽrba la ĩyã, tɩ ba tʋʋra Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum na. La ba ka teege yɛm basɛ ba tõon-be'ero la.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyoobɩ dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ mõg-kãtɛ sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Efratɩ la pʋam. Tɩ a ko'om kẽesɛ, lebge sore bɔ na'adõma sɛba n ze'ele wẽntulle doore bɔba la.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ kʋlkã'arsɩ batã n ãn wʋ lonno n ze'ele pʋryõŋ-yãlma la nõorʋm, la dũŋa la nõorʋm, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la nõorʋm yɛsra.

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Ba de la Asũtãana kʋlkã'arsɩ n ɩtɩ bõn-yãlma. Ba we'esɩ la dũnia na'adõma wuu zẽ'am tɩ ba lagsɛ ba, tɩ ba zɛbɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, a dabs-kãtɛ la daarɛ.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam sẽm wʋ nayiga la. Nẽr-sɛka n ka gĩsri dee yɛ a futo tarɩ zu-yẽlga, bɛla tɩ a kãn sẽnna beele tɩ nẽrba yẽta a yãnnɛ.».

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la lagsɛ na'adõma la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri la Ebre yetɔga tɩ «Armagedõ».

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Tɩ malɛka bʋyopɔɩ dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ sapebsʋm pʋam. Tɩ kõa ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnne n boe Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam zẽ'am yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ.».

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Tɩ saaga yãgra, sũnna, tãsra, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim halɩ zozo'e. Nẽrsaalba n boe tẽŋa zuo tɛka, tẽŋa nãn ka mĩim be'em be'em wʋ bɛla.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Tẽŋa la n mĩim na, tẽn-kãtɛ la pãrsɛ la zẽ'esɩ batã. Tɩ dũnia so'olʋm wuu tẽnsɩ lui. Nawẽnnɛ tẽegɛ Babilɔnɩ tẽn-kãtɛ la yelle mɛ, botɩ a yũ a sũ-isgre kãtɛ n ãn wʋ dãam n boe a wannɛ pʋam na.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Tɩ tẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ ko'om gilge, la tãnsɩ wuu bɔɩ, tɩ nẽra ka le yẽta ba.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: Tɩ sakug-kãra ze'ele saazuum lui nẽrba zuto. Sakugre ayɩla tɩbsɩgɔ paagɛ wʋ kilo pinaasɩ. Tɩ nẽrba tʋʋra Nawẽnnɛ, sakuga la toogo ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, toogo la tarɩ la pãŋa tɩ la yõsgɛ gãŋɛ.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n daan zãlɩ laasɩ bayopɔɩ la, ayɩla wa'am mam zẽ'am wa yele mam yetɩ: «Wa'am, tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fɔ pɔg-yalsɩ dãana n de tẽn-kãtɛ n mẽ lɛm kʋlsɩ zozo'e nõorʋm na n wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ se'em.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: La de la ẽŋa tɩ dũnia na'adõma tõm yalsɩ tõoma la ẽ zozo'e. La dũnia nẽrba mẽ tõm na a yalsɩ tõoma wʋ dãam tɩ ba yũ buge la.».

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la mam, tɩ malɛka la tarɩ mam sẽŋɛ mõom. Tɩ mam yẽ pɔka n bã dũŋa n de mõlga tɩ yʋ'ʋra n tʋʋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ gʋlsɛ a ĩyã wuu. Dũŋa la daan tarɩ la zuto bayopɔɩ, la ɩɩla pia.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Pɔka la daan yɛ la fu-mõlga n de gomusga, dee yɛ nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ n de sãlma, la kug-sõma buuri ligri n zo'oge. La a zãlɩ la sãlma laaga n pɩrɛ la bõn-be'ero n de a yalsɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ la.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Yʋ'ʋrɛ n daan gʋlsɛ a dĩire zuo, tɩ ba ka mina a võorɛ. Yʋ'ʋrɛ la de la «Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la, a de la dũnia pɔg-yalsɩ dõma, la tõon-dẽgrɔ wuu ma».

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: La mam yẽ tɩ pɔg-ẽŋa yũ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zɩɩm buge, nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra tɩ ba kʋ ba la zɩɩm. Mam n yẽ ẽ na, la di'ige mam mɛ zozo'e.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Tɩ malɛka la soke mam yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ la dɛna di'ire bɔ fõn? Mam wʋn tɔgɛ pɔka la, la dũŋa la tɩ a bã n tarɩ zuto bayopɔɩ, la ɩɩla pia la võorɛ pa'alɛ fɔ.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Dũŋa la tɩ fʋ yẽ la, kʋrʋm a vɔa mɛ, la a ka le bɔna, la a wʋn ze'ele la bog-ko'ogo la pʋam dooge, sẽŋɛ sã'aŋɔ zẽ'am. La dũnia nẽr-sɛba yʋ'ʋra n ka gʋlsɛ bɔna vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ la sãn yẽ dũŋa la, la wʋn di'ige ba mɛ, dũŋa la n daan vɔa, ka le wa bɔna, dee le wa bɔna la ĩyã.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ tara yɛm, la pʋtẽ'erɛ. Zuto bayopɔɩ la de la tãnsɩ bayopɔɩ tɩ pɔka la zo ba zuto.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: La ba ken dɛna la na'adõma bayopɔɩ. Banuu bayopɔɩ la pʋam na'am ba'asɛ mɛ, la ayɩla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, dee tɩ ayẽma nãn ka wa'am. La a sãn wa wa'am, a wʋn bɔna la wakatɛ fẽe.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: La dũŋa la n daan vɔɩ dee ka le bɔna la, ẽŋa de la naba bʋnii dãana, la a ken dɛna la bayopɔɩ dõma la pʋam nẽra ayɩla. La a we'esɩ la sã'aŋɔ zẽ'a.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: Ɩɩla pia la tɩ fʋ yẽ la, de la na'adõma pia, la ba nãn ka to'oge na'am. La ba wʋn wa to'oge na'am lagɩm na dũŋa la di karf-ayɩla.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Bãma pia la pʋtẽ'erɛ de la ayɩla, ba dɩkɛ ba pãŋa, la ba ke'eŋo la wuu bɔ la dũŋa la.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Ba wʋn zɛbɛ la Pebila la mɛ, la Pebila la wʋn zɛbɛ yãŋɛ ba mɛ, ẽŋa n de zuudãandõma Zuudãana, la na'adõma Naba la ĩyã. Pebila la, la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a wi, looge ba tɩ ba dɛna sɩra dõma la, n wʋn zɛbɛ yãŋɛ ba.».

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Malɛka la le yele mam yetɩ: «Kʋlsɩ la tɩ fʋ yẽ, tɩ pɔg-yalsɩ dãana la zĩ ba nõorʋm na, de la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ, tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm to'oto'ore, la yetɔga buuri to'oto'ore nẽrba.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Ɩɩla pia la tɩ fʋ yẽ la, la dũŋa la wʋn sise pɔg-yalsɩ dãana la mɛ, wʋn to'ose sɛla wuu tɩ a tarɩ la, dee basɛ ẽ beele. La ba wʋn obe a nẽnnɔ mɛ, dee yõ ẽ na bugum.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ ba pʋtẽ'era tẽ'esra tɩ ba ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em. Tɩ ba wʋn lagɩm taaba la nõorɛ ayɩla dɩkɛ ba na'am ke'eŋo bɔ dũŋa la, halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n yele se'em na ba'asɛ.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: Pɔka la tɩ fʋ yẽ la de la tẽn-kãtɛ n soe dũnia na'adõma wuu.».

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam yẽ la malɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra. A zãlɩ la bog-ko'ogo la saafɩbiire, la zɔrgɔ n ãn kãtɛ.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: A yõkɛ la pʋryõŋ-yãlma la n de kʋrʋm kʋrʋm bõnsɛla, n de dõta, n de Asũtãana la lʋ ẽ yʋʋma tʋsrɛ ĩyã.

eng: He threw him into the abyss, then locked and sealed it over him. This was to keep him from continuing to deceive the nations until the thousand years were over. After this he must be released for a little while.
local: A lobe ẽ basɛ la bog-ko'ogo la pʋam yʋ, dee bɩ taasɛ, tɩ a da le yãŋɛ pã'asɛ dũnia so'olʋm nẽrba, gu'ura halɩ tɩ yʋʋma tʋsrɛ la wa paagɛ. Yʋʋma tʋsrɛ la poorʋm, dẽnɩ tɩ ba lorge ẽ basɛ wakatɛ fẽfẽe.

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ na'am bĩmbĩna, tɩ ba bɔ nẽr-sɛba n zĩ ba zuto la ke'eŋo tɩ ba di sarɩya. Tɩ mam yẽ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba daan wige ba zuto, ban bɔ Azezi sɩra, dee mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la sɩɩsɩ. Nẽr-bãma daan ka nã'asɛ dũŋa la, la a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la, la ba ka to'oge a dãalgɔ ba dĩa zuto bɩɩ ba nu'usi zuto. Ba vo'oge mɛ lagɩm na Krisi di na'am yʋʋma tʋsrɛ la pʋam.
