eng: The rest of the dead didnt come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.
local: Ẽna de la pɔspɔsɩ kũm vo'ore. La kũm basɛba la ka vo'oge, sãn dagna yʋʋma tʋsrɛ la n wʋn wa tole.

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Nẽr-sɛba n wʋn po vo'oge pɔspɔsɩ kũm vo'ore la, tarɩ zu-yẽlga, bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Kũm bʋyi dãana la ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ba sɛla. Bãma n wʋn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ la Krisi kãabgɔ kẽma, la ba wʋn lagɩm na Krisi di na'am yʋʋma tʋsrɛ.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Yʋʋma tʋsrɛ la sãn wa tole, ba wʋn yese Asũtãana yʋ'a zẽ'a na basɛ mɛ.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: Tɩ a yese pã'asɛ dũnia so'olʋm buuri wuu nẽrba, Gɔɔgɩ la Magɔɔgɩ. A wʋn lagsɛ ba mɛ tɩ ba zɛbɛ zɛbrɛ. Ba wʋn lagsɛ zo'oge wʋ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm se'em na.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Ba wʋn yese mɛ dũnia wuu lagsɛ kaagɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe zẽ'e-sɛka, tẽn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ la. La bugum n wʋn ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sige di ba.

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Tɩ ba wʋn yõkɛ Asũtãana n pã'asrɩ dũnia nẽrba la lobe basɛ bugum mõgrɛ n diti kidibre pʋam, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba daan põn lobe dũŋa, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la. Bilam ba wʋn nãmsra la wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Tɩ mam le yẽ na'am bĩmbĩnne kãtɛ n de pɛɛlga, la sɛka n zĩ a zuo. Tɩ tẽŋa la saazuum zoe fõrgɛ a nẽŋam, tɩ ba ka le yẽ ba.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ nẽr-sɛba n ki la, kɛ'ɛndõma, la tarɩmdõma n ze na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam. Tɩ ba lagsɛ gõnnɔ. La ba lakɛ la gõŋɔ ayẽma n de vɔm gõŋɔ. Tɩ ba di sɛba n ki la sarɩya masɛ wʋ ba tõoma n de se'em, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ gõnnɔ na pʋam se'em na.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Tɩ ko-kãtɛ basɛ sɛba n ki a pʋam na, tɩ kũm, la kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa mẽ basɛ nẽr-sɛba n ki bɔna ba zẽ'am na. La ba di ba wuu sarɩya masɛ wʋ ban tõm se'em na.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Tɩ ba lobe kũm, la kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa basɛ bugum mõgrɛ la pʋam. Bugum mõgrɛ la de la kũm bʋyi dãana.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: Sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n ka gʋlsɛ vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam, ba wʋn lobe ẽ basɛ la bugum mõgrɛ la pʋam.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Bɛla poorʋm, mam wõm na kõa n tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em Nawẽnnɛ yire wʋ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ kõosɩ la yetɩ: «Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ! Fãarɛ, la nã'asgɔ, la ke'eŋo de la tõma Wẽnnaam n soe.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: Ẽŋa sarɩyadia la masɛ mɛ, dɛna sɩra. Se'ere n soe la, a di pɔg-tutulga la n daan tõnnɩ yalsɩ tõoma zozo'e sã'ana dũnia la sarɩya mɛ sɩbgɛ ẽ, ẽn botɩ a tõntõnɩba ki la ĩyã.».

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Tɩ ba le yetɩ: «Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ. Tẽn-kãtɛ la tɩ bugum laŋɛ dita la bug-zũ'usi zonnɩ na saazuum wakatɛ wuu.».

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Tɩ kẽma pisyi la anaasɩ, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ n zĩ a na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la yetɩ: «Amina. Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Tɩ kõa ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am yetɩ: «Yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba n zotɩ ẽ na, tarɩmdõma, la kɛ'ɛndõma, nã'asɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ.».

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Tɩ mam le wõm kõa n ãn wʋ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e n tɔgrɩ, dee ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ dãmnɛ, dee ãna wʋ saaga n tãsrɩ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ. Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo dãana la n soe na'am.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, Pebila la pɔgdire wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, tɩ pɔka la mẽ mãasʋm a mẽŋa ba'asɛ.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ fu-sõŋɔ n yẽgrɩ ka tara dẽgrɔ, tɩ a yɛ.» Fu-sõŋɔ la de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tõon-sõma.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ yetɩ: Sɛba tɩ ba wi ba tɩ ba wa'am wa di Pebila la pɔgdire kibsa la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.» Dee le yetɩ: «Yetɔg-bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga sɩra sɩra.».

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Tɩ mam ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam tɩ m nã'asɛ ẽ, tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ bɛla! Mam mẽ de la Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnna wʋ fõn, la fʋ sɔbiisi la n sakɛ sɩra tɩ Azezi pa'alɛ la. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n de fʋ nã'asɛ.» Sɩra la tɩ Azezi puke pa'alɛ la n bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔ'ɔsra tɔgra.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: La mam yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ yire n yo'oge, tɩ wemõ-pɛɛlga bɔna mĩ, tɩ sɛka n bã ẽ na yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Atẽntẽa, la Sɩra Dãana. A diti la sarɩya tɩ la masɛ, dee zɛbra zɛbrɛ tɩ la masɛ.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: A nini ãn wʋ bugwitum na, dee vuge mũnsi zozo'e a zuum. A tarɩ la yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ tɩ nẽra ka mina ẽ, sãn dagna a mẽŋa mã'a.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: La a yɛ la fu-woko n lʋsɛ zɩɩm pʋam. A yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire zɛbzɛbrɩba yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ n ãn sõŋa ka tara dẽgrɔ, bã wemõ-pɛɛlsɩ dɔla ẽ.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Tɩ sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõorɛ ze'ele a nõorʋm yɛsra. A wʋn dɩkɛ la sʋ'a la zɛbɛ yãŋɛ so'olʋm buuri wuu. A wʋn tara la kuto dasaarɛ sɔna ba. A wʋn mẽrɛ ba la viinye biɛ wẽkrɛ bõnɔ n pa'alɩ ke'eŋo dãana Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre la pʋam.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a fuugo zuo, la a gere zuo yetɩ: «Na'adõma Naba, la zuudãandõma Zuudãana.».

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛka n ze wẽntulle pʋam n tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele bõn-ẽgla wuu n ẽgrɩ saazuum yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya wa lagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ di-kãtɛ la zẽ'am.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: Ya wʋn di la na'adõma nẽnnɔ, la kɛ'ɛndõma, la sʋdaasɩ nẽnnɔ, la wiiri, la wi-bã'arba nẽnnɔ, la nẽrba wuu, burkĩndõma, la yamsɩ, la kɛ'ɛndõma, la tarɩmdõma wuu nẽnnɔ.».

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ dũŋa la, la dũnia na'adõma, la ba zɛbzɛbrɩba n lagsɛ taaba tɩ ba zɛbɛ la sɛka n bã wemõa, la a zɛbzɛbrɩba la.

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Tɩ ba yõkɛ dũŋa la, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la n daan ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma dũŋa la nifum pã'asɛ sɛba n to'oge dũŋa la dãalgɔ, dee nã'asɛ a bõnɔ tɩ ba mãalɛ la. Ba lobe dũŋa, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la wuu la vɔɔsɩ basɛ bugum mõgrɛ n diti kidibre pʋam.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Tɩ sʋ'a la n ze'ele we-bã'ara la nõorʋm yɛsra la, kʋ sɛba n weege la. Tɩ bõn-ẽgla wuu di ba nẽnnɔ na tɩgɛ.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Tɩ mam yẽ saazuum paalga, la tẽn-paalga. Pɔspɔsɩ saazuum, la pɔspɔsɩ tẽŋa la bɔɩ mɛ, la ko-kãtɛ mẽ ka le bɔna.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: La mam yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm paalga n ze'ele saazuum Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am sigra. A malgɛ mɛ ãna sõŋa wʋ pɔka n yɛ nẽnnõm futo mãasʋm a mẽŋa tɩ a ele a sɩra.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ kẽ'a zẽ'am boe la nẽrsaalba tẽŋasʋka. A wʋn kẽ'era la ba lɛ, tɩ ba dɛna a nẽrba. Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n wʋn bɔna la ba lɛ.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: A wʋn duuse ba nintãm wuu. La kũm kãn le bɔna, sũ-sã'aŋɔ la kaasga, bɩɩ toogo kãn le bɔna. Yel-kɛgsɩ la wuu tole mɛ.».

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Tɩ sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la yele yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, mam ɩtɩ sɛla woo la paalsɩ.» Dee yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ wãna, se'ere n soe la, mam yetɔga wã de la sɩra dee masɛ tɩ ba bɔ sɩra.».

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Dee le yele mam yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ mɛ. Mam de la Alfa, la Omega, sɛla woo pĩ'iluŋo, la ba'asgɔ. Koyũuro n tarɩ sɛka, mam wʋn bɔ ẽ ko'om n bulli n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm zãŋa tɩ a yũ.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ, a wʋn to'oge la bõn-bãma mam zẽ'am. Mam wʋn dɛna a Wẽnnaam, tɩ a mẽ dɛna mam bia.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: La sãn dɛna dabeto, la sɛba n ka bɔ sɩra, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la nẽr-kʋʋrba, la yalsɩ dõma, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tɩɩm tõoma, la sɛba n nã'asrɩ bõndõma tɩ ba mãalɛ, la põmpɔrndõma la wuu, bãma zẽ'a de la bugum mõgrɛ n diti kidibre pʋam. Bɛla de la kũm bʋyi dãana.».

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n daan zãlɩ laasɩ bayopɔɩ n pɩrɛ la tooro bayopɔɩ n de ba'asgɔ tooro la, ayɩla wa'am wa yele mam yetɩ: «Wa'am tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fʋ pɔka la n yetɩ a ele la, Pebila la pɔga la.».

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la mam, tɩ malɛka la tarɩ mam zom tãn-kãtɛ n wɔgɛ zuo. La a pa'alɛ mam na Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm n ze'ele saazuum Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am sigra.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: n yẽgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa. A sõŋa la yẽgrɩ wʋ kug-sõŋɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ, ãna wʋ kugre tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ zaspɩ, dee yẽgra wʋ bɩsga tɩ ba wi'iri kristalɩ.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Tẽŋa la tarɩ la lal-kãtɛ n wɔgɛ gilge ẽ, tara nõa pia la ayi. Tɩ malɛkadõma pia la ayi gu'ura nõa la. Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri pia la ayi la yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ nõa la zuto.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Nõa batã n boe lalga la dapoore bɔba, tɩ batã bɔna zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ batã bɔna gɔbga bɔba, tɩ batã bɔna zanõa bɔba.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Tẽŋa la lalga ẽebɛ la kuga pia la ayi zuto. Tɩ Azezi n de Pebila la Tõntõnɩba pia la ayi la yʋ'ʋra gʋlsɛ kug-bãma zuto.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Malɛk-sɛka n daan tɔgɛ la mam na, tarɩ la sãlma dɔɔgɔ tɩ ba tarɩ makra, tɩ a makɛ tẽŋa la, la a nõa, la a lalga la.

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Tẽŋa la de la tẽn-wẽkɔ tɩ a lalsɩ banaasɩ la wuu zõna taaba. Tɩ malɛka la dɩkɛ dɔɔgɔ la makɛ tẽŋa la. Tɩ a yɔlŋɔ dɛna sɩtaadɩ tʋspia la ayi. A woglʋm la a yɔlŋɔ, la a saazuum woko zõn na taaba.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: La a makɛ lalga la mɛ, tɩ a woglʋm dɛna kãntɩɩsɩ kɔbga la pinaasɩ la anaasɩ. A kãntɩɩsɩ la de la bʋyɩla la nẽrsaala kãntɩɩsɩ.

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Ba dɩkɛ la kug-sõma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de zaspɩ mẽ lalga la. La tẽŋa la mẽŋa de la sãlma mẽŋa mẽŋa n yẽgrɩ ãna wʋ bɩsga la.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Lalga la, ba dɩkɛ la kug-sõma buuri to'oto'ore n tarɩ yõorɔ ẽebɛ ẽ, tɩ a ãna sõŋa. Ẽbrɛ la pɔspɔsɩ kugre la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la zaspɩ, tɩ bʋyi dãana yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna safɩɩrɩ, tɩ bʋtã dãana dɛna kalsedʋanɩ, tɩ bʋnaasɩ dãana dɛna emerɔdɩ.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: Tɩ bʋnuu dãana dɛna sadonigsi, tɩ bʋyoobɩ dãana dɛna sardʋanɩ, tɩ bʋyopɔɩ dãana dɛna krizoliti, tɩ bʋnii dãana dɛna berili, tɩ bʋwɛɩ dãana dɛna topaazɩ, tɩ pia dãana dɛna krizoprazɩ, tɩ pia la ayɩla dãana dɛna yasẽntɩ, tɩ pia la ayi dãana dɛna ametisti.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Nõa pia la ayi la kʋlɩnsɩ, ba dɩkɛ la kug-sõma buuri n tarɩ yõorɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de pɛrlɩ pia la ayi ẽŋɛ ba. Ba dɩkɛ la pɛrlɩ ayɩla ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla woo kʋlɩŋa. Tẽŋa la pallɛ de la sãlma mẽŋa mẽŋa n yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga la.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Mam ka yẽ Wẽndeego tẽŋa la pʋam, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, la Pebila la n boe mĩ.

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Tẽŋa la ka boorɩ wẽntulle, bɩɩ wõrga tɩ ba nẽegɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa peelem n nẽerɩ ẽ, tɩ Pebila la dɛna a fɩtla.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Dũnia so'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn sẽnna la a peelem na pʋam. La dũnia na'adõma wʋn tara ba bõntarsõm sẽŋɛ mĩ.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Tẽŋa la nõa kãn malɩn yʋ, se'ere n soe la, yʋ'ʋŋɔ ka boe mĩ.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Ba wʋn tarɩ dũnia so'olʋm wuu nã'asgɔ, la ba bõntarsõm sẽŋɛ mĩ.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Sɛla n de bõn-dẽgrɔ wuu, bɩɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la põmpɔrndõma kãn kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam. Nẽr-sɛba yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ Pebila la vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam mã'a n wʋn kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Tɩ malɛka la pa'alɛ mam ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm kʋlaaga. Kʋlaaga la yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga la. A ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ, la Pebila na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
local: zɔta tẽŋa la pʋam pallɛ tẽŋasʋka. Tɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n boe kʋlaaga la sakʋgrɔ bayi la wɔnna biɛ nõorɛ pia la ayi yʋʋnɛ pʋam. A wɔnnɩ na nõorɛ ayɩla wõrga woo pʋam. Tɩa la võorɔ bɔ'ɔrɩ la dũnia so'olʋm wuu ĩmã'asʋm.

eng: There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him.
local: Sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã be'em bɔ ẽ kãn le bɔna tẽŋa la pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ, la Pebila la na'am bĩmbĩnne n wʋn bɔna mĩ. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba wʋn nã'asra ẽ.

eng: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
local: La ba wʋn yẽ a nẽŋa, la a yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn gʋlsɛ ba dĩa zuto.

eng: Night will be no more. They wont need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will shine on them, and they will rule forever and always.
local: Yʋ'ʋŋɔ kãn le bɔna. Nẽrba kãn bɔɔra tɩ fɩtla, bɩɩ wẽntulle nẽegɛ peelem, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn nẽegɛ peelem bɔ ba. La ba wʋn di la na'am wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

eng: Then he said to me, These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon take place.
local: Tɩ malɛka la yele mam yetɩ: «Yetɔg-bãna wã wuu de la sɩra, masɛ tɩ ba bɔ sɩra. La de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔ'ɔsra a yetɔga tɔgra la, n tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa pa'alɛ a tõntõnɩba sɛla n kãn yuuge dee ẽŋɛ.».

eng: Look! Im coming soon. Favored is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll.
local: Azezi yetɩ: «Selse-ya! La kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am. Sɛka n sakrɩ gõŋɔ wã yetɔga tɩ ba to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɔgra la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.».

eng: I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
local: La de la mam Azã n wõm dee yẽ bõn-bãma. La mam n wõm dee yẽ ba la, mam ka la dũma tẽŋa malɛka la n pa'alɛ mam bõn-bãma la nẽŋam tɩ m nã'asɛ ẽ.

eng: But he said to me, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters, the prophets, and those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God.
local: Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ bɛla, tɩ mam mẽ de la tõntõnna wʋ fõn, la fʋ sɔbiisi n de nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n sakrɩ gõn-ẽna wã yetɔga la. Nawẽnnɛ n de fʋ nã'asɛ.».

eng: Then he said to me, Dont seal up the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll, because the time is near.
local: A yele bɛla dee yele mam yetɩ: «Da sʋgɛ gõŋɔ wã yetɔga tɩ Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ fʋ la, se'ere n soe la, wakat-sɛka tɩ bõn-bãna wʋn ẽŋɛ la lɛm mɛ.

eng: Let those who do wrong keep doing what is wrong. Let the filthy still be filthy. Let those who are righteous keep doing what is right. Let those who are holy still be holy.
local: Sɛka n de tõon-be'ero dãana, a ken tõnna be'em. Sɛka n de tõon-dẽgrɔ dãana, a ken tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ. La sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, a ken ɩta sɛla n masɛ. Tɩ sɛka n de pʋpeelem dãana, ken sẽŋɛ nẽŋa la pʋpeelem.».

eng: Look! Im coming soon. My reward is with me, to repay all people as their actions deserve.
local: Azezi yele yetɩ: «Selse-ya! La kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am. La mam tarɩ la yɔɔrɔ sẽna tɩ m yɔ nẽra woo masɛ wʋ ẽn tõm se'em.

eng: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
local: Mam de la Alfa, la Omega, pɔspɔsɩ dãana, la poorʋm dãana, sɛla woo pĩ'iluŋo, la ba'asgɔ.».

eng: Favored are those who wash their robes so that they may have the right of access to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
local: Sɛba n peege ba futo tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Bãma n wʋn tara nõorɛ tɩ ba di vɔm tɩa la biɛ, la bãma n wʋn doose tẽŋa la nõa kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: Outside are the dogs, the drug users and spell-casters, those who commit sexual immorality, the murderers, the idolaters, and all who love and practice deception.
local: La nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tɩɩm tõoma, la sɛba n tõnnɩ yalsɩ tõoma, la nẽr-kʋʋrba, la sɛba n nã'asrɩ bõndõma tɩ ba mãalɛ, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n nõŋɛ põmpɔrŋɔ dee parna la, wʋn weege la yẽŋa, ba kãn kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

eng: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to bear witness to all of you about these things for the churches. Im the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
local: «Mam Azezi n tõm m malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa tɔgɛ bõn-bãma wuu pa'alɛ m nẽrba. Mam de la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa, la bewã'arɛ n nẽerɩ.».

eng: The Spirit and the bride say, Come! Let the one who hears say, Come! And let the one who is thirsty come! Let the one who wishes receive life-giving water as a gift.
local: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la pɔka la n mãasʋm a mẽŋa tɩ a ele la yetɩ: «Wa'am!» Sɛka n wõm bɛla, a mẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am!» Koyũuro n tarɩ sɛka, a wa'am, sɛka n boorɩ ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm, a wa'am wa to'oge zãŋa.

eng: Now I bear witness to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll: If anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues that are written in this scroll.
local: Nẽr-sɛka woo n wõm Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga wã n boe gõŋɔ wã pʋam, mam Azã kã'anɩ ẽ yetɩ, nẽr-sɛka sãn pa'asɛ sɛla yetɔga wã pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a yẽ tooro sɛba n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã pʋam na.

eng: If anyone takes away from the words of this scroll of prophecy, God will take away that persons share in the tree of life and the holy city, which are described in this scroll.
local: La nẽr-sɛka sãn yese sɛla gõŋɔ wã yetɔga pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn yese a pʋrɛ tɩ a kãn po di vɔm tɩa la biɛ, la a kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa la pʋam wʋ lan pa'alɛ se'em gõŋɔ wã pʋam na.

eng: The one who bears witness to these things says, Yes, Im coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!.
local: Sɛka n tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna bɔ la yetɩ la de la sɩra, a yetɩ: «Asɩra, la kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am.» Amina! La dɛna bɛla. Zuudãana Azezi, wa'am.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
local: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yel-sõnnɛ bɔna la ya wuu.
